facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Anovion Technologies (Anovion), a supplier of premium synthetic graphite anode materials for lithium-ion batteries, will build a new manufacturing facility in Bainbridge.
The project will create more than 400 jobs and over $800 million in investment in Decatur County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Georgia is fast becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Governor Kemp noted job creators are recognizing that every corner of the state has the resources and workforce needed for success.
Governor Kemp mentioned meeting Anovion's team personally and looking forward to celebrating their partnership with Georgians in Decatur County.
Anovion is a climate tech-driven advanced materials company.
Anovion is one of the first qualified U.S. suppliers of synthetic graphite anode materials for use in e-mobility applications.
Graphite is the largest battery material used in electric vehicles by mass, more than copper, nickel, manganese, cobalt, and lithium.
Eric Stopka, Chief Executive Officer of Anovion, stated they are thrilled to be opening their first large-scale expansion manufacturing facility in Georgia.

Stopka noted the state’s strong technical workforce and top-tier schools will help advance research partnerships and contribute to a highly skilled talent pipeline for Anovion’s innovation and climate technology jobs.
Stopka mentioned encouragement by the region’s growing availability of clean energy, resulting in increased sustainability for Anovion’s manufacturing process.
Stopka expressed looking forward to providing solutions to secure the country’s electrified future with support from partners in Georgia and the Federal government.
Anovion’s new facility will be 1,500,000-square-foot.
The facility will use proprietary furnace technologies.
The facility is located at Downrange Industrial Park in Bainbridge.
It will be the first of its size and scale in North America.
The facility will produce synthetic graphite for use in batteries that power EVs, electric storage systems, electronics, military applications, and other products.
Operations are expected to begin in late 2025.
The company will be hiring for a variety of technical, production, and engineering roles, as well as administrative and management positions.
Bainbridge Mayor Edward Reynolds welcomed Anovion to Bainbridge.
Reynolds noted the $800 million investment and creation of over 400 high-paying, quality jobs as a huge win for the community.
Reynolds stated the local workforce is ready to embrace the opportunity and that everyone will grow and prosper together.
Pete Stephens, Chairman of the Decatur County Board of Commissioners, noted Decatur County leadership has focused on attracting quality companies and jobs for many years.
Stephens stated diversifying the industry mix is needed to remain competitive and grow.

Stephens described today’s announcement as a step in that direction, noting green industry jobs represent a growing segment of the economy and will provide quality employment opportunities for decades to come.
Stephens expressed pride in welcoming Anovion to Decatur County and its family of industries.
Project Manager Taylor Walden represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was done in partnership with the City of Bainbridge, Development Authority of Bainbridge and Decatur County, University System of Georgia, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated much of the critical supply chain for e-mobility and manufacturing is currently sourced outside the U.S.
Wilson described Anovion as an innovative industry leader that fills that need and delivers another critical component to Georgia.

Wilson noted a diverse mix of public-private partnerships are coming together to support the next revolution in the automotive industry, from working with utility partners and establishing infrastructure to attracting and retaining top-talent through the University System of Georgia.
Wilson expressed excitement for the hope and opportunity Anovion will create for Bainbridge and Decatur County.
Building on assets that make the automotive industry successful, Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry.
Since 2020, 40 EV-related projects have announced more than $21.9 billion in investment and 28,000 jobs in Georgia.
More than 10 of these projects are battery manufacturers, recyclers, and suppliers, representing approximately $8.5 billion in investment and 6,000 jobs.
Anovion Technologies is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.
Anovion Technologies is a U.S.-owned and U.S.-based advanced materials business.

It is currently America’s first commercial-scale manufacturer of premium, cleaner anode-grade synthetic graphite across its existing footprint near Niagara Falls, New York, and Clarksburg, West Virginia.
As a leader in innovation and production of lithium-ion battery materials, Anovion began commercial production in early 2021.
Anovion is among a limited number of graphite anode producers in America to have successfully gained qualification for EV applications.
source_title: Anovion Technologies to Create Over 400 Jobs in Bainbridge | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Mobis plans to invest $926 million in an Electric Vehicle (EV) Power Electric system plant in Bryan County.
The new plant will eventually employ at least 1,500 people.
This announcement marks the second supplier to locate in the Bryan County area in just two weeks following Hyundai’s EV and battery manufacturing facility groundbreaking.
The EV Power Electric system plant will expand Hyundai Mobis’ current footprint in Georgia.
Hyundai Mobis has operated a manufacturing facility with almost 1,200 employees in West Point since 2009.
The West Point facility serves as the largest Tier 1 supplier to Kia Georgia.
The West Point plant supplies automotive manufacturers with complete cockpit modules, chassis modules, and bumper assemblies.

H.S. Oh, Vice President of Hyundai Mobis Electric Powertrain Business Unit, stated the Bryan County investment reflects an acceleration in the development of the EV supply chain in Georgia’s auto industry.
H.S. Oh stated Hyundai Mobis will be a major production player in the EV market and expects to trigger more growth within the sector.
Hyundai Mobis plans to provide high-quality work opportunities to the growing local workforce.
Once at full production, the 1,200,000-square-foot facility will supply over 900,000 EV Power Electric systems and 450,000 Integrated Charging Control Units annually.
The annual output will be supplied to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Bryan County, Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA) in Montgomery, and the Kia Georgia plant.
Construction on the new facility is expected to begin as early as January 2023.
Production at the new facility is expected to begin in 2024.
The new facility is located at the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified Belfast Commerce Park in Richmond Hill.
Jon Seagraves, Chairman of the Development Authority of Bryan County, welcomed Hyundai Mobis to the community and noted the site was purchased about two years ago.
Belfast Commerce Park offers close proximity to available housing in nearby Heartwood, a top-performing public school system, interstate access, and rail.
Jon Seagraves cited nearly $1 billion of investment and the creation of over 1,500 good-paying jobs as key project benefits.
Jon Seagraves thanked Bryan County, the City of Richmond Hill, and Raydient Places for their support and partnership on the project.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the Development Authority of Bryan County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power on the project.
Pat Wilson, GDEcD Commissioner, stated suppliers like Hyundai Mobis are transitioning to the jobs of the future and becoming part of Georgia’s growing EV ecosystem.
In May, GDEcD predicted that non-affiliated HMGMA suppliers in connection with the project would invest an additional $1 billion into Georgia.
Pat Wilson noted that the May prediction was exceeded in about two weeks.
GDEcD plans to prepare Georgia’s workforce of tomorrow for the jobs being created now using support from partners like Georgia Quick Start and sister agencies.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry, building on assets that make the automotive industry successful.
Since 2020, more than 30 EV-related projects have contributed over $13.3 billion in investment and announced more than 18,100 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility in Bryan County.
Offsite suppliers connected to the HMGMA project are predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Second Global Automotive Supplier for Hyundai Metaplant To Create 1,500 Jobs | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is reshaping Georgia’s manufacturing landscape with its first dedicated mass-production EV and hybrid plant.
At full production, the cutting-edge Metaplant will be able to support the production of 500,000 units of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis electric and hybrid vehicles annually.
This historic $7.59 billion investment will create at least 8,500 new jobs on-site at the Metaplant.
The project has spurred nearly 6,900 additional new jobs through direct, offsite suppliers in Georgia.
The project has spurred $2.5 billion in investments through direct, offsite suppliers in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group’s total investments in Georgia are expected to create nearly 40,000 direct and indirect jobs.
These investments are expected to generate $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year.
The job and earnings projections are according to a report from the Center for Automotive Research at the time of HMGMA’s grand opening.

On May 20, 2022, Governor Brian P. Kemp and Hyundai Motor Group Chairman Euisun Chung announce Hyundai’s selection of Georgia for its dedicated mass-production EV and hybrid vehicle manufacturing plant.
The original commitment was $5.54 billion in investment and the creation of 8,100 new jobs.
On October 25, 2022, Hyundai breaks ground at the Bryan County Megasite, marking the start of construction on this state-of-the-art facility.
On May 26, 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution formalize their joint venture for battery production.
On August 31, 2023, Hyundai expands the battery facility’s scope, adding $2 billion in investment.
The August 31, 2023 expansion adds 400 more jobs.
On June 18, 2024, Hyundai announces the all-electric IONIQ 5 SUV as the first vehicle to be produced at HMGMA.
On October 7, 2024, HMGMA begins production on the first of its Georgia Made® EVs, the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5.
On December 4, 2024, Hyundai deploys the first XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks as part of its Clean Logistics Project to reduce internal logistics emissions.
As of March 26, 2025, at the time of its official opening, HMGMA has hired 1,200 Meta Pros.
On March 26, 2025, HMGMA announces plans to expand manufacturing capacity to 500,000 vehicles annually.
Hyundai’s investment solidifies Georgia’s position as a leader in sustainable innovation and advanced manufacturing.
Georgia is paving the way for a fully integrated, closed-loop EV supply chain with a growing network of suppliers and partners.
Doowon Climate Control Co., Ltd. is listed among the partners in Georgia's e-mobility ecosystem.
Hyundai Motor Company cited speed-to-market, workforce, and the state’s ability to meet energy requirements for choosing Georgia.
The close proximity to the Port of Savannah was central to the selection of the Bryan County Megasite.
Connections to the port are available by both rail and highway.
Hyundai will be able to access global markets quickly and efficiently through these connections.
Georgia Quick Start, the No. 1 workforce training program in the nation, is constructing a customized training center at the Metaplant site.
HMGMA has partnered with local technical colleges to prepare students for entry-level employment at the Metaplant.
Electrical Vehicle Professional Technical Certificate of Credit (EVP TCC) training is available at Coastal Pines Technical College’s Baxley and Waycross campuses.
EVP TCC training is also available at Southeastern Technical College’s Vidalia campus, Savannah Technical College, and Ogeechee Technical College.

Euisun Chung, Executive Chair of Hyundai Motor Group, stated: “Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America not only represents the Group’s advanced manufacturing capabilities and commitment to innovation, but also our investment in relationships with our partners and communities right here in Georgia.”

Euisun Chung added: “With the rich history of craftsmanship and manufacturing in this community, together with the talented workforce at HMGMA we are building the future of mobility with America, in America.”

links.context: Dedicated career site for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America
Hyundai Motor Group Economic Development Agreement Basics
First Amended and Restated Economic Development Agreement
Original Hyundai Motor Group Offer Letter

source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America | Georgia.org | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on February 19, 2025 that Duracell will establish its new Global Headquarters for Research and Development at Science Square in Atlanta.
The new R&D headquarters project involves an investment of approximately $56 million and will create 110 jobs.
Duracell currently operates a manufacturing facility in LaGrange, Georgia that has been in operation since 1980.
The LaGrange facility currently supports approximately 400 jobs.
Duracell also operates a logistics and distribution plant in Fairburn, Georgia that began operations in 2020.
The Fairburn plant supports an additional 275 jobs.
Duracell is an American manufacturer of alkaline, lithium coin, and hearing aid batteries.
Duracell is a leader in the primary battery market in North America.
The new Global Headquarters for Research and Development will be located at 101 Nerem Street NW in Atlanta.
Science Square is adjacent to Georgia Tech’s Midtown Atlanta campus.
Science Square is an 18-acre multi-phase development centered on innovation featuring more than 1.8 million square feet of lab and office space.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia has set itself apart as a leader in attracting innovative companies due to its research institutions, world-class logistics network, and pro-business environment.

Mayor Andre Dickens noted that Atlanta’s transportation infrastructure, diverse talent pool from top-tier universities, and thriving tech ecosystem make the city ideal for corporate innovation and growth.
Mayor Andre Dickens highlighted that the investment of more than 100 new jobs will provide opportunities for Atlanta residents to build promising careers.

Katie Kirkpatrick, President & CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated that Duracell's decision strengthens the region’s reputation as a hub for innovation and furthers Georgia's growing battery ecosystem.
Katie Kirkpatrick noted that Duracell is locating literally next door to world-class talent at Georgia Tech and in close proximity to tens of thousands of new graduates in the region.
Robb Pitts, Chairman of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners, stated that Duracell’s choice solidifies Fulton’s leadership in innovation and talent attraction.
Assistant Director of Statewide Projects John Soper represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with Invest Atlanta, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Power, and the University System of Georgia for this competitive project.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that locating the new Global Headquarters for Research and Development in Atlanta makes it clear that Duracell sees the State of Georgia as a long-term partner in its success strategy.
The Duracell brand and company was started in the 1920s.
Berkshire Hathaway Inc. acquired Duracell in 2016.
Berkshire Hathaway Inc. is a $250 billion holding company owning subsidiaries that engage in diverse business activities.
source_title: Duracell Selects Georgia for New R&D Headquarters | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on August 29, 2023, that Daesol Ausys Georgia will establish a new manufacturing facility in West Point.
The facility will create more than 140 new jobs and $72 million in investment in Harris County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right, and Daesol Ausys will join other innovators in Harris County.

Governor Brian P. Kemp noted that Georgia's automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for the jobs of the future.
Daesol Ausys was established in Korea in 2017.
The company specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
It is a key supplier for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
Min Ho Kwon, CEO of Daesol Ausys, stated the company is committed to delivering world-class services that exceed client requirements and expectations.
Min Ho Kwon expressed excitement about the project in the Northwest Harris Business Park and looking forward to continuing the partnership with the State of Georgia and Harris County.
The new facility will produce luggage boards and luggage covers.
Operations are expected to begin in December 2024.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.
Robert Grant, Chairman of the Harris County Commission, stated that Daesol Ausys’ decision to locate in the Northwest Harris Business Park represents a commitment to Harris County.
Robert Grant noted that West Point and Harris County have a wonderful relationship with Daesol Ausys’ sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials.
Craig Greenhaw, Chairman of the Harris County Development Authority, welcomed Daesol Ausys and noted the venture brings jobs and capital investment into the region.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Harris County Commission, Harris County Development Authority, City of West Point, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that economic development is driven by the local level and congratulated Harris County and community partners for bringing Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.
Pat Wilson noted that the automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state, and expressed excitement for opportunities Daesol Ausys will provide.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Daesol Ausys Georgia is a leading manufacturer of automotive components and a key supplier of luggage boards and luggage covers.
The company has a long history as one of the top suppliers to Kia and Hyundai worldwide.

links.context: Press Secretary for Governor Brian P. Kemp
Deputy Press Secretary for Governor Brian P. Kemp
Communications Manager at Georgia Department of Economic Development

source_title: Daesol Ausys Georgia to Invest $72 Million in Harris County | Georgia

facts: Automotive News published a special 100th anniversary edition chronicling the evolution of the automotive industry and Georgia's role over the last century.
The first hybrid 2027 Kia Telluride is scheduled to roll off the production line in West Point, Georgia.
White Star Automobile launched during Georgia's early automotive roots between 1909 and the 1920s.
Savannah Blowpipe (now Great Dane) launched during Georgia's early automotive roots between 1909 and the 1920s.
Blue Bird Corporation was established in Georgia in 1927.
Genuine Parts Company was established in Georgia in 1928.
Ford invested in Georgia by 1947.
General Motors invested in Georgia by 1947.
The former GM plant in Doraville was repurposed into Assembly Studios.
The Ford Hapeville facility was transformed into the Porsche Experience Center Atlanta.
Kia Georgia opened in 2006 as part of 21st-century expansion.
Kia Georgia and its suppliers have created over 14,000 jobs in Georgia.
Pirelli expanded its operations in Georgia.
Novelis expanded its operations in Georgia.
Honda Precision Parts expanded its operations in Georgia.
Kumho Tire USA expanded its operations in Georgia.
Toyo Tire expanded its operations in Georgia.
Cox Automotive expanded its operations in Georgia.
Porsche expanded its operations in Georgia.
Toyota Industries Corporation expanded its operations in Georgia.
Mercedes-Benz expanded its operations in Georgia.
Recent investments in Georgia focus on hybrid and electric vehicles, DC-to-DC production, and e-mobility innovation corridors including The Ray and Curiosity Lab.
Hyundai Motor Group announced a $12.6 billion Metaplant America facility in Georgia.
Misti Martin serves as Deputy Commissioner at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
GDEcD connects automakers with communities, utilities, education partners, and industry leaders to accelerate integrated supply chains in a stable, business-friendly environment.
Georgia Quick Start provides customized workforce training for rapid scaling.
The Technical College System of Georgia provides customized workforce training for rapid scaling.
Kia Georgia and Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America produce a mix of traditional electric, and hybrid vehicles backed by a robust supplier network.
The Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) program ensures industrial sites are prepped for major projects.
The Rural Site Development Initiative ensures industrial sites are prepped for major projects.
Hyundai's Metaplant America is valued at $12.6 billion.
GDEcD continuously aligns with evolving automotive trends to support innovation, growth, and global investment in Georgia.
Automotive investments in Georgia spark job creation, charge educational partnerships, drive infrastructure growth and cross-cultural exchange, and shift local economies.
source_title: A Century of Innovation and Counting: Georgia’s Automotive Edge  | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Battery Resourcers, a lithium-ion battery recycling and engineered materials startup, will invest $43 million to open its first commercial-scale battery recycling plant in Covington.
The processing facility will become the largest of its kind in North America when fully operational in August 2022.
The facility will create at least 150 jobs in Newton County.
Governor Kemp stated that Battery Resourcers is the latest job creator to move to Georgia due to the state's leadership position in the electric vehicle manufacturing space.
The strategically located facility will have the capacity to process 30,000 metric tons of discarded lithium-ion batteries and scrap annually.
The annual processing capacity is equivalent to 70,000 vehicle batteries per year.
The recycling process will return battery-grade lithium, cobalt, and nickel back into the battery supply chain.
Major brand investors in Battery Resourcers include Jaguar Land Rover, Orbia, TDK, TRUMPF Group, Doral Energy, and Hitachi.
Recycling batteries is one component of Battery Resourcers' technology and marks a vital step in the company's strategic expansion plans.
Battery Resourcers has engineered a process to turn recyclables back into critical battery materials, specifically nickel-manganese-cobalt cathodes.
The produced nickel-manganese-cobalt cathode materials are sold back to battery manufacturers.
Michael O’Kronley, CEO and Director of Battery Resourcers, stated the company is bringing 150 tech jobs to the Covington area.
O’Kronley noted that automotive manufacturers currently have very few options for responsible and cost-effective disposal of discarded batteries and scrap.
The company aims to provide a sustainable solution that minimizes the need for mining and returns valuable, battery-grade materials back into the lithium-ion supply chain.
Battery Resourcers will operate out of an existing 154,000-square-foot building located at 9172 Industrial Drive Northeast in Covington.
Operations are expected to begin in spring 2022.
The company has already begun hiring for the facility.
Lanier Sims, Chairman of the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, welcomed Battery Resourcers to the area's business mix.
The Newton County Industrial Development Authority is partnering with Battery Resourcers and the State of Georgia to make the I-20 corridor a business and technology-focused hub.
Mayor Steve Horton stated that Battery Resourcers is an outstanding addition to Covington's business ecosystem and will complement regional businesses in Georgia.
When fully operational, the facility will be located in the heart of Covington.

Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick stated that Metro Atlanta is an ideal location for EV and clean energy businesses due to local university and technical college engineering/manufacturing expertise, market proximity, and rapid industry growth.
Project Manager Virginia Sengewald represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce division on the competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that battery recycling has become a vital part of the supply chain as the EV industry grows.
Wilson emphasized the importance of cutting reliance on unstable areas of the globe for battery materials.
The announcement follows Rivian's recent announcement to make Georgia the location of its second EV manufacturing facility and campus.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Battery Resourcers to Open North America’s Largest Lithium-ion Battery Recycling Facility in Georgia, Create 150 Jobs | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Daechang Seat Corp. USA will invest $72.5 million in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center, in close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The investment will create over 500 jobs in Chatham County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant project alone reach at least nine counties.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA is adding to Georgia's growing manufacturing base centered around Chatham County and the Savannah region.
Daechang Seat Corp. (DSC) was established in 1979 in Kyungju, South Korea.
DSC is one of the largest seat frame producers in the world.
Globally, DSC employs more than 5,000 people across seven countries.
The company's production process includes fully automatic lines with arc welding and spot-welding machines.
CEO Jinsuk Lee expressed appreciation to the State of Georgia, local communities, and the City of Savannah for their support.
DSC's primary objective is to drive growth through facility expansions to transform into a global entity.
DSC currently holds a prominent partnership with Hyundai Transys and Hyundai Mobis at HMGMA.
DSC's new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The company will hire for assembly operators, equipment technicians, mechanical engineers, and electrical engineers through local job fairs and website postings.
The facility is located only minutes from the Port of Savannah to leverage Georgia's world-class connectivity.
Daechang Seat Corp. USA is the third Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America supplier to locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.

The project was facilitated by the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted that Chatham County has leveraged Savannah's status as an established global gateway to foster economic growth.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has created more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, automotive industry job creation in Georgia increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia's mobility original equipment manufacturers resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
DSC has recently ventured into the electric vehicle sector and continues to expand its scope.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Automotive Supplier Daechang Seat Corp. USA to Create 500 Jobs in Chatham County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: On July 20, 2021, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced a statewide initiative to strengthen Georgia's position as a national leader in the electric mobility industry.
The Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance (EMIA) is driven by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
EMIA includes partners from the private and public sectors ranging from government, industry, electric utilities, education, nonprofits, and other stakeholders.
The initiative focuses on advancing Georgia’s role in developing the electric mobility industry and its supply chain.
In conjunction with the initiative, GDEcD launched new website assets for the program.
Governor Kemp stated that Georgia has a proven record of investing early in the resources and infrastructure needed to connect it to the world and develop jobs of the future.
Governor Kemp noted that the Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance will ensure the state is positioned to continue leading the nation in the rapidly growing electric mobility industry.
The announcement cited nearly $2.6 billion in SK Innovation electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing facilities located in Jackson County as an existing investment.
Georgia has attracted numerous recent international investments, including Dutch e-mobility charging systems leader Heliox.
Georgia has attracted Turkish EV-parts manufacturer TEKLAS as a recent international investment.
Georgia has attracted German-owned lightweight automotive-body parts manufacturer GEDIA as a recent international investment.
Georgia has attracted SK-supplier EnChem of Korea as a recent international investment.
In 2018, Georgia Made school bus manufacturer Blue Bird introduced their all-electric buses.
Blue Bird recently reported its 500th delivery or sale of electric buses.
The Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance will offer policy recommendations intended to further enhance the state’s attractiveness to the electric mobility industry.
EMIA aims to foster innovation in related fields including drones, autonomous vehicles, connected vehicles, battery technology, and more.
EMIA consists of five committees that will develop a multi-step approach setting objectives for Supply Chain, Infrastructure, Workforce, Innovation, and Policy/Initiatives.
The Carl Vinson Institute at the University of Georgia will host EMIA’s committee meetings and integrated research forums.
According to a report by the Alliance for Automotive Innovation, automakers are planning to invest $250 billion in electrification by 2023.
IHS Markit predicts there will be 130 EV models available in the U.S. by 2026.
Georgia is positioned as the center of the southeast automotive corridor with close proximity to the region’s automotive original equipment manufacturers.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that expanding on Georgia’s growing e-mobility ecosystem, EMIA will give Georgia a greater, more comprehensive strategy for the wave of electrification across industry sectors.

Commissioner Wilson noted that by bringing together industry leaders and public and private sector partners, they are proactively developing a roadmap to keep Georgia at the forefront of electric mobility and innovation.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and the lead agency for attracting new business investment.

GDEcD's responsibilities include encouraging the expansion of existing industry and small businesses, locating new markets for Georgia products, attracting tourists, promoting the state for arts and entertainment projects, and planning and mobilizing state resources for economic development.
source_title: Gov. Kemp Announces Statewide Initiative to Accelerate Georgia’s Electric Mobility Industry | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Rivian Inc. will invest $5 billion in a carbon-conscious campus in Georgia for its electric adventure vehicles.
Across operations, Rivian will create approximately 7,500 jobs on just under 2,000 acres located at the site known as the East Atlanta Megasite.
The site is represented by the Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton counties.
Once manufacturing operations are fully ramped up, the Georgia facility will be capable of producing up to 400,000 vehicles per year.
Rivian’s manufacturing plant represents the single-largest economic development project in state history.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Georgia will now be home to Rivian's largest manufacturing facility.
The investment establishes Georgia's leading role in the booming automotive manufacturing industry for generations to come.
Georgia's long-term focus on logistics, talent, workforce, and responsive government significantly contributed to the announcement.
Governor Kemp's administration emphasized innovation and development in the electric mobility ecosystem to deliver wins for Georgians.
Georgia is considered the top state for business.

Georgia Quick Start workforce training resources, world-class higher education institutions, and statewide logistics infrastructure assets are prepared to meet Rivian's production and research and development needs.
Construction on the facility, located at Stanton Springs North, is expected to begin in summer 2022.
The start of production is slated for 2024.
Rivian’s technology centers at the new campus will support the company’s research and development programs.
The company is actively working to ensure that both the construction process and future facility meet Rivian’s high standards of conservation and sustainability for a cleaner, wilder future.
The entire project will support the expansion of the U.S. electric vehicle industry and will serve to decarbonize the transportation and energy sectors.
Rivian is the latest success in Georgia’s push to recruit the entire electric mobility ecosystem to the state.
In August, Governor Kemp announced the launch of the Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance.

The Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance is a statewide initiative between government, industries, electric utilities, nonprofits, and other relevant stakeholders focused on growing the electric mobility ecosystem in the state and strengthening Georgia’s position in electrification-related manufacturing and innovation.

Georgia is committed to ensuring the state’s existing industry and accompanying 55,000 automotive manufacturing jobs receive the support for retraining and expansions needed to transition to meet the new demands of electrified transportation.
Rivian Chief People Officer Helen Russell stated that Georgia will serve as Rivian's next manufacturing site to meet demand for Rivian products and scale its business rapidly.
Rivian's work in Georgia is rooted in collaboration, transparency, fairness, and a strong sense of belonging.
Helen Russell expressed confidence that alongside Illinois, Georgia is the place for Rivian to scale and thrive.

Rivian has developed and vertically integrated a connected electric platform that can be flexibly applied to a range of applications, including its adventure products as well as B2B products such as its last-mile delivery vans.
The last-mile delivery vans were launched in partnership with Amazon.
Rivian’s launch products are the R1T and R1S.
The R1T and R1S provide an unmatched combination of performance, off-road capability, and utility.
Earlier this week (relative to Dec 2021), the Rivian R1T all-electric truck earned the coveted 2022 truck of the year from MotorTrend.
MotorTrend called the R1T “the most remarkable pickup truck we’ve ever driven!”.
Joint Development Authority of Stanton Springs Chairman Jerry Silvio welcomed Rivian to the country’s premier joint economic development partnership.
Rivian will join two revolutionary leaders in their respective fields to make east Atlanta a beacon of technology and employment potential.
The Joint Development Authority of Stanton Springs began over 25 years ago with the goal of creating high-tech, regional-focused jobs for generations to come.
Economic development directors Shane Short and Serra P. Hall, and attorney Andrea Gray provided dedication and steadfast focus to ensure the vision is met and exceeded.
The four counties and utility partners have planned strategically to ensure minimal impact on the local environment.
Continued collaboration between the State of Georgia, the JDA, and Rivian will be unprecedented.
Rivian’s strategic location on the I-20 corridor will enable the company to access resources and speed its products to market.
Georgia offers 1,200 miles of highway and 5,000 miles of rail to move product quickly and efficiently.
Investments such as the newly opened Mason Mega Rail Terminal at the Port of Savannah will allow Rivian to efficiently tap into supply chain needs.
The Mason Mega Rail Terminal is the largest on-terminal rail facility in North America.
Griff Lynch, Executive Director for the Georgia Ports Authority, expressed excitement to welcome Rivian to the Peach State’s manufacturing community.
The Georgia Ports Authority is expanding facilities for processing containers and finished vehicles to provide supply chain solutions to Rivian.
Russell McMurry, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Transportation, stated that Georgia’s exceptional multimodal transportation network is foundational for the future of transportation.
This network has led to Georgia being recognized as a national leader in EV corridor readiness.
The Georgia Department of Transportation looks forward to providing the transportation infrastructure necessary to make this project successful.
Rivian’s just under 2,000-acre parcel will include abundant natural space.
Mike Hopkins, Executive Director of the Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority, expressed gratitude for partnering with Rivian and the Joint Development Authority of Stanton Springs.
The partnership is viewed as a once-in-a-lifetime moment where water must be managed to become the standard that all future projects aspire to achieve.
The State of Georgia is recognized as a beacon of preservation and steward for water protection.
The water system is uniquely designed to support citizens, businesses, and the natural environment in the Southeast.
Rivian’s focus on environmental excellence fits perfectly within the vision here.
Georgia’s strong and diverse talent pool is bolstered by one of the largest state university systems in the country.
Eighty-five accredited public and private universities in the state award more than 132,000 degrees and certificates to its graduates annually.
The Technical College System of Georgia consists of 22 colleges offering education and training in numerous industries that keep Georgia competitive in the global economy.
Metro Atlanta Chamber President and CEO Katie Kirkpatrick stated that Metro Atlanta is ideally suited for Rivian’s new operations.
The region offers a diverse and highly skilled workforce, access to innovative businesses, and collaborative higher education institutions.
To further assist with workforce requirements, Georgia Quick Start will build and operate a state-of-the-art manufacturing training center.
The training center will provide Rivian with a fully customized training program that meets the company’s start-up needs.

This initiative will also build capacity and curricula within the Technical College System of Georgia to maintain a long-term pipeline of highly skilled employees trained specifically in these operations.
The Georgia Department of Labor will assist the company in identifying and recruiting workers.

Hank Evans, Director of the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Aerospace, Defense, and Advanced Manufacturing team, represented GDEcD’s Global Commerce division on this competitive project.

Partners include the Joint Development Authority (JDA) of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton Counties; the Georgia Ports Authority; the Georgia Department of Transportation; Georgia Quick Start; the Metro Atlanta Chamber of Commerce; and Georgia Power.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Rivian’s decision to locate in Georgia will have a tremendous impact on the entire state.
Pat Wilson noted that more change in the automotive industry will occur in the next 10 years than in the past 100.
With this announcement, Georgia will be home to one of the main drivers of this transformation.
Rivian carefully plans construction efforts at the site to preserve and protect the beauty and uniqueness of their new home.
Rivian will be an outstanding corporate citizen and community partner, creating jobs and investment that will change lives and accelerate Georgia’s sustainable and electrified future.
Georgia is at the center of the rapidly expanding Southeast Automotive Alley.
Rivian’s investment accelerates the evolution of Georgia’s automotive ecosystem.
Within the last five years, 78 automotive-related companies have located or expanded operations in Georgia, creating thousands of new jobs.
Existing companies in Georgia have expanded their portfolios to include parts for EVs.
Companies from Korea, Germany, Turkey, and the Netherlands have also recently announced plans to build EV-related facilities in the state.

Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate has made it an attractive location for a diverse array of rapidly developing industries focused on creating a sustainable future.

The state is leading in the development and use of emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industries through fostering healthy industry practices and encouraging collaboration and innovation.
Rivian exists to create products and services that help the planet transition to carbon neutral energy and transportation.
Rivian designs, develops, and manufactures category-defining electric vehicles and accessories and sells them directly to customers in the consumer and commercial markets.
Rivian complements its vehicles with a full suite of proprietary, value-added services that address the entire lifecycle of the vehicle and deepen customer relationships.

links.context: Inquiries about project RFIs, RFPs listing, and supply chain outreach

source_title: Gov. Kemp Welcomes Rivian for Single-Largest Economic Development Project in State History | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Kia’s aim is to change the future for the better and be a respected company that people feel inspired by and want to work for.
Georgia Quick Start was a major draw for Kia's decision to locate in Georgia.

Former Chief Administrative Officer of Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia Randy Jackson stated that while other states typically provide a workforce training budget and cut a check, Georgia’s approach established the state as a true long-term partner in workforce development and training.
In 2006, Kia Motors Corp. announced plans to build Georgia’s first new auto manufacturing facility in decades.
The chosen location was West Point, a small town in West Georgia where textile manufacturing offshoring had decimated once-thriving communities.
Hand-made yard signs reading “God Bless Kia” appeared when the announcement was made.

Despite the Great Recession and hedge fund bankruptcies causing global economic uncertainty and skepticism, the community, company, Georgia Quick Start, and state economic development players continued forward.
The all-new Kia Sorento, manufactured in West Point, became one of the most popular models on the road.
Georgia Quick Start developed and delivered a pre-employment assessment program that allowed Kia to select the best candidates from an initial pool of 41,000 applicants.
In 2008, a landmark moment in Georgia manufacturing occurred at the Georgia Ports Authority.
Georgia Quick Start designed and built a training center to prepare Georgians for automotive manufacturing work, which remains operational.
source_title: Kia Led Georgia into the 21st Century - Georgia Quick Start

facts: On May 20, 2022, Hyundai Motor Group announced an agreement with the State of Georgia to build its first dedicated full electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facilities in the U.S.
The investment supports Hyundai's goal of becoming a leader in electric mobility in the U.S. market.

The official signing ceremony was held in Bryan County, attended by Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp, other Georgia officials, Hyundai Motor Company President and CEO Jaehoon Chang, Hyundai Motor President and Chief Operating Officer José Muñoz, and Executive Chair Euisun Chung (who joined virtually).
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp called the project the largest in the state's history, delivering high-quality jobs on the leading edge of mobility to hardworking Georgians.
Governor Kemp noted it is incredible that within a year of Georgia's investment in the Bryan County Megasite, they are locating a world-class project with a company with great Georgia history.

Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung expressed pride in sharing plans to open the first dedicated full EV and battery manufacturing facilities in the U.S., highlighting the U.S.'s importance to the Group's global strategy and partnership with Georgia for electrified mobility.
The new EV plant and battery manufacturing facilities represent an investment of approximately USD 5.54 billion.
The new facility will break ground in early 2023 and is expected to begin commercial production in the first half of 2025.
The facility will have an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
The battery manufacturing facility will be established through a strategic partnership, with details to be disclosed at a later stage.
The Group plans to produce a wide range of full electric vehicles for U.S. customers at the new Georgia EV plant, with production model details to be shared later.
Local EV production aims to increase U.S. consumer accessibility to the Group's innovative EVs.
Through the battery manufacturing facility, the Group aims to establish a stable supply chain and build a healthy EV ecosystem in the U.S.
The EV and battery manufacturing plant will be located on a dedicated 2,923-acre site in Bryan County, Georgia.
The site has immediate access to I-95 and I-16 highways, creating easy access to 250 major metro areas.

The site is less than 50 kilometers from the Port of Savannah, the single-largest and fastest-growing container terminal in the U.S., with two Class I rail facilities on-site provided by Georgia Central Railway.
West Point, Georgia is already home to the Group's Kia manufacturing hub in the US.
Hyundai Motor Group selected Georgia due to favorable business conditions including speed-to-market, a talented workforce, and an existing network of Group affiliates and suppliers.
Georgia is the Southeastern leader for EV registration per 1,000 registered automobiles and offers more publicly available EV charging outlets per capita than anywhere else in the Southeast.
The Group's new investment will help accelerate the state's sustainable shift to electrification.
The project is expected to create about 8,100 new jobs.

The EV plant investment is part of the Group's 2021 announcement to invest USD 7.4 billion by 2025 to foster future mobility in the U.S., including production of EVs and offering smart mobility solutions.
Hyundai Motor Group aims to lead the U.S. auto market's EV transition with the new facility.
The Group has a global target to sell 3.23 million full electric vehicles annually by 2030.
To realize this goal, the Group plans to establish a global EV production network to ensure a stable supply of EVs around the globe.

Hyundai Motor President and CEO Jaehoon Chang stated the decision to build the first dedicated EV plant in the U.S. was driven by America embracing change and innovation, and that the plant will meet growing demands for leading edge design, safe, zero-emissions vehicles.
With additional EV and battery production capabilities in the U.S., the Group aims to become one of the top three EV providers in the U.S. by 2026.
The Group plans to lead the EV market not just in sales, but also in terms of design, technologies, and mobility solutions optimized for the electric mobility era.
The investment aligns with the U.S. government's roadmap to accelerate electrification as global OEMs expand local EV production in the U.S.
Last year, the U.S. government announced an initiative under which sales of zero-emission vehicles should account for at least half of OEM's new vehicle sales by 2030.
The new plant will feature a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem to realize customer value.
The Group plans to implement advanced intelligent manufacturing technologies currently under test at the Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center in Singapore (HMGICS).
All production processes, including order collection, procurement, logistics, and production, will be optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system will create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
As part of its commitment to sustainability, the plant will mainly rely on renewable energy sources to power the facility and use emission-reduction technologies to meet RE100 requirements.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group to Establish First dedicated EV Plant and Battery Manufacturing Facility in the U.S.

facts: Seoyon E-HWA, a global auto parts supplier that manufactures both interior and exterior components, will create 740 new direct and indirect jobs.
Seoyon E-HWA will invest almost $76 million in a new manufacturing facility in Chatham County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the project on February 1, 2023.
Seoyon E-Hwa was established in 1972.
Seoyon E-Hwa operates 32 corporations in North America, South America, Europe, and Asia.
Seoyon E-Hwa supplies door trim, headlining, seats, C/PAD, bumpers, and other parts to Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, Mercedes-Benz, Ford, Volkswagen, and more.
Seoyon’s LaGrange location will continue to support 630 full time jobs.
The new facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified site.
The new manufacturing facility will produce automotive interior parts such as door trims, headlining, and tailgate trims.
Production is expected to begin in October 2024.
More than two-thirds, or approximately 500, of the announced jobs will be permanent roles in human resources, development, quality, and production personnel.

Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison stated the location ideally positions Seoyon in close proximity to the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, the Port of Savannah, Interstates I-16 and I-95.
Georgia Ports Authority Executive Director Griff Lynch stated Seoyon is a valued customer using the Port of Savannah to supply its manufacturing facility in West Georgia.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Seoyon is committing to nearly doubling the number of jobs it supports in the state with its second Georgia facility.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced over $21 billion in investment and 26,700 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Offsite suppliers for the Hyundai project have already exceeded the $1 billion in investment predicted from other locations connected to the project around the state by more than $600 million.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Seoyon E-HWA Joins Growing List of Hyundai Suppliers, Nearly Doubles Georgia Employment | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Georgia is home to some of SK’s most significant investments, including manufacturing sites for advanced batteries that are part of U.S. “battery belt.”
Absolics, a subsidiary of SKC, manufactures a glass substrate for semiconductor circuit boards that can significantly increase performance and energy efficiency of chipsets.
The Absolics facility is located at 1 SKC Drive, Covington, GA 30014.
SK Battery America is the first U.S. battery manufacturing site of SK On.
SK Battery America currently operates two EV battery plants in Commerce, Georgia.
The SK Battery America facility is located at 1760 SK Boulevard, Commerce, GA 30529.
SK Inc. C&C is an advanced IT company providing local support in Georgia for the SK Battery America EV battery site.
The SK Inc. C&C facility is located at 2915 Premiere Parkway, Duluth, GA 30097.
Hyundai Motor Group and SK On formed a joint venture to establish an EV battery cell plant in Bartow County, Georgia.
The joint venture plant is expected to be operational by the end of 2025.
The joint venture plant location is Cartersville, GA.
SK Tes, a subsidiary of SK Ecoplant, is an electronic waste management company and global leader in e-waste recycling, IT asset disposition, and battery recycling.

SK Tes has locations at 1100 Mary Crest Rd, Suite A, Henderson, NV 89074; 9601 Cosner Dr, Fredericksburg, VA 22408; 5238 Royal Woods Pkwy, Suite 110, Tucker, GA 30084; and 1208 Andover Park E, Tukwila, WA 98188.

A federal workplace safety investigation found an Augusta battery manufacturer exposed employees to unsafe levels of lead based on personal air monitoring and lead accumulation on respirators and counters in lunch break areas.

The U.S. Department of Labor’s Occupational Safety and Health Administration issued two repeat citations to U.S. Battery Mfg. Augusta Inc. after May 2023 inspections found the company failed to have engineering and work practice controls in place to reduce lead exposures.
Inspections found the company did not prevent lead from accumulating on surfaces.
OSHA issued two serious citations for exposing employees to lead concentrations greater than permissible limits.
The agency found the company did not ensure employees’ respirators were cleaned and disinfected.
OSHA proposed $160,727 in penalties against the company.

OSHA Area Office Director Josh Turner stated that elevated lead levels can cause debilitating and permanent health issues and that employers are legally responsible for minimizing worker exposure through engineering controls, safe work practices, and clean protective equipment.
Since 2018, federal safety inspections at U.S. Battery’s Augusta facility have identified numerous violations related to lead exposure.
In 2019, OSHA issued citations for one serious, one repeat, and one other-than-serious violation at the facility.
In 2018, OSHA found nine serious violations and one repeat and one other-than-serious violation at the facility.
Founded in 1926, U.S. Battery Mfg. Augusta Inc. designs and manufactures deep cycle batteries for scissor lifts, golf carts, boats, and other uses by numerous industries in the U.S. and abroad.
The company has locations in Corona, California, and in Evans and Augusta, Georgia.

The company has 15 business days from receipt of its citations and penalties to comply, request an informal conference with OSHA’s area director, or contest the findings before the independent Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission.
source_title: US Department of Labor finds Augusta battery manufacturer exposing employees to unsafe lead levels, other hazards | U.S. Department of Labor

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America moves up start of production to Q4 2024.
Georgia Governor Kemp, company, and state leaders officially marked Feb. 26, 2024 as Hyundai Day.
Hyundai will deploy its XCIENT Fuel Cell Truck between HMGMA and its suppliers.
Governor Brian P. Kemp, First Lady Marty A. Kemp, and José Muñoz celebrated Hyundai Motor Group’s positive impact on the State of Georgia at the event.
The official start of production has accelerated to the fourth quarter of 2024 with the grand opening celebration targeted for the first quarter of 2025.
Production was previously announced to begin in the first quarter of 2025.
The General Assembly passed a Resolution commending Hyundai Motor Group’s dedication to the State of Georgia and recognizing Feb. 26, 2024, as Hyundai Day at the State Capitol.

Hyundai and Genesis electric vehicles, including the IONIQ 5, IONIQ 6, GV60, and Electrified GV70, along with the hydrogen-powered XCIENT Fuel Cell Truck for logistics, were on display outside of the State Capitol.
José Muñoz, president and global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and president and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America, stated that Georgia is like a second home to Hyundai.
Hyundai’s investments in Georgia will create tens of thousands of great paying American jobs and bring tens of billions of dollars in economic impact for decades to come.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Hyundai has been an incredible partner to the state and will help recruit businesses, give back to communities, and work with colleges and universities to connect Georgians with opportunities.
Oscar Kwon, president and CEO of HMGMA, stated that 2024 is going to be a great year for the plant and called it the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history.
The Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Joint Development Authority provided continued support and cooperation for the project.
Lieutenant Governor Burt Jones, House Speaker Jon Burns, legislative leaders, Department of Economic Development Director Pat Wilson, and local officials joined the celebration.
Hyundai Motor Group is investing $7.59 billion to develop HMGMA, which will create 8,500 direct jobs.
Hyundai and SK On are building a $5 billion EV battery cell plant in Bartow County, Georgia that will employ 3,500 workers.
A recent Center for Automotive Research study expects Hyundai’s investments in Georgia to create nearly 40,000 jobs and generate $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year.
HMGMA continues hiring efforts and has developed educational partnerships with area colleges to create a pipeline for future employment.
The Electric Vehicle Professional Technical Certificate (EVP TCC) is being offered at four area technical colleges as part of this collaboration.
Students who successfully complete the EVP TCC program receive preferred status when applying for a position at HMGMA.
Hyundai announced it is actively developing a hydrogen mobility value chain at HMGMA at CES 2024.
Through the Clean Logistics Project in Georgia, the Metaplant will utilize XCIENT Fuel Cell tractors to manage logistics for the plant and its suppliers.
The XCIENT is Hyundai’s commercialized class 8 6x4 fuel cell electric model with more than 6.25 million accumulated miles of real-world application.
Hyundai is developing hydrogen refueling infrastructure needed to support this fuel cell activity.
This collaboration is anticipated to stimulate economic growth and create job opportunities within the state.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated Electric Vehicle mass-production plant located in Bryan County, Georgia.
The new plant will feature a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem.
The Georgia facility will operate as an intelligent manufacturing plant where order collection, procurement, logistics, and production are optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system will create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
source_title: Hyundai Announces New Production Start Date at Metaplant America During Hyundai Day in Georgia - Alexandria Hyundai

facts: Hyundai Motor Group unveiled its AI Robotics Strategy at CES 2026 under the theme ‘Partnering Human Progress’.
The strategy is built on three foundational partnerships: partnering humans with co-working robots, partnering the Group Value Network with Boston Dynamics, and partnering with AI leaders.
Physical AI refers to tangible technologies that collect data using hardware in real-world environments to make autonomous decisions, spanning robotics, smart factories, and autonomous driving.
Hyundai Motor Group plans to establish a Physical AI Application Center to advance the Physical AI ecosystem.
The Group plans to build a robot manufacturing and foundry plant based on customized robotics technologies developed through Physical AI.
Boston Dynamics robots have been deployed in industrial settings to enhance safety and improve efficiency.
Spot, an agile quadruped robot, is operational in more than 40 countries for data collection and safety monitoring in industrial sites.
Stretch, a warehouse robot, has unloaded more than 20 million boxes globally since its launch in 2023.
The Group aims to combine its manufacturing data and expertise with Boston Dynamics’ R&D leadership to establish safe human-robot collaboration starting in manufacturing environments.
The Atlas prototype was unveiled at CES 2026 as a general-purpose humanoid research model designed for industrial applications and built to integrate with existing facilities.
Atlas is equipped with advanced rotational joints and sensors, features 56 degrees of freedom (DoF) with most being fully rotational joints, and has human-scale hands with tactile sensing.
Atlas supports applications including material sequencing, assembly, and machine tending while prioritizing safety, reliability, and collaboration in shared workspaces.
Hyundai Motor Group expects humanoids to become the largest segment of the Physical AI market and aims to mass-produce the Atlas product model for deployment across industrial sites.
The Group plans to integrate Atlas across its global network, including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Savannah, Georgia, to perform real-world tasks on-site.
Atlas deployment will expand process-by-process through validation, beginning in 2028 with processes like parts sequencing that have proven safety and quality benefits.
By 2030, Atlas applications will extend to component assembly, and over time it will handle repetitive motions, heavy loads, and other complex operations.
The Group envisions a human-centered automation model where robots handle labor-intensive or high-risk tasks while humans focus on training robots and providing oversight.
Hyundai Motor Group is building a Group Value Network as its central AI Robotics Strategy leveraging Group capabilities.
At the core of this network is an End-to-End (E2E) AI Robotics Value Chain leveraging automotive production infrastructure, safety know-how, and affiliate technological capabilities.
Atlas robots will undergo extensive training in the Group’s manufacturing environment to ensure safe readiness for real-world applications.
The Group has a unique advantage in extending its electrification value chain and applying AI-based software from Software-Defined Vehicles (SDVs) to robots and other Physical AI products.
By 2028, the Group aims to establish a scalable production system capable of manufacturing 30,000 robot units annually.
Boston Dynamics announced a strategic partnership with Google DeepMind to accelerate technological development for next-generation humanoid robots.
This collaboration will integrate Boston Dynamics’ robotics expertise with Google DeepMind’s Gemini Robotics AI foundation models built on the Gemini multimodal generative AI model.
The partnership aims to accelerate research on AI models to control complex robots for high-impact tasks and scale robot adoptions safely and efficiently.

Zachary Jackowski, Vice President and General Manager of Atlas at Boston Dynamics, stated that combining capabilities with Google DeepMind is a significant step toward redefining the industry paradigm.

Carolina Parada, Senior Director of Robotics at Google DeepMind, expressed excitement to explore possibilities with the new Atlas robot while developing new models to expand robotics impact safely and efficiently.

From January 6–9, 2026, Hyundai Motor Group’s CES 2026 exhibition booth at the Las Vegas Convention Center featured live demonstrations of Spot, Stretch, Atlas, X-ble Shoulder, ACR, MobED, IONIQ 5 Robotaxi, AMR, Cobot, and Parking Robot.
MobED won the CES 2026 Best of Innovation Award.
Hyundai Motor Group unveiled an official brand video for CES 2026 showcasing human-centered AI Robotics technologies on its official YouTube channel.
Additional CES 2026 updates will be shared through the Group’s official social media channels.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with about 250,000 employees worldwide and mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Announces AI Robotics Strategy to Lead Human-Centered Robotics Era at CES 2026

facts: Federal agents executed a federal search warrant at HL-GA Battery Company, LLC on September 4, 2025.
The facility is located on the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant American Campus in Ellabell, Georgia.
The search was conducted to find evidence related to the unlawful employment of illegal aliens in violation of Title 8, United States Code, Section 1324.
Over 475 people were identified as unlawfully working at the HL-GA Battery Company location during the operation.
Law enforcement detained over 400 illegal workers found on the premises.
Several individuals attempted to flee the site by running into a sewage pond located on the property, requiring agents to use a boat to retrieve them.
One individual swam under the boat and attempted to flip it over before being captured.
The operation involved hundreds of federal, state, and local law enforcement personnel.
Over 400 agents participated in executing the search warrant.
U.S. Attorney Margaret E. Heap stated that over 400 illegal workers were identified and detained during the significant undertaking.
U.S. Attorney Margaret E. Heap stated the goal of the operation is to reduce illegal employment and prevent employers from gaining an unfair advantage by hiring unauthorized workers.
U.S. Attorney Margaret E. Heap also noted another goal is to protect unauthorized workers from exploitation.
The investigation was led by Homeland Security Investigations (HSI).

Assisting agencies included U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO), Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF), Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Internal Revenue Service (IRS) Criminal Investigations, U.S. Department of Labor Office of Inspector General, United States Marshal Service (USMS), and Georgia State Patrol.
Assistant United States Attorneys Tania Groover, Ryan Bondura, Kelsey Scanlon, and E. Greg Gilluly, Jr., are investigating the case.
The investigation remains ongoing as of the publication date.
The case is part of Operation Take Back America, a nationwide Department of Justice initiative targeting illegal immigration and transnational criminal organizations.

links.context: U.S. Attorney's Office contact phone number

source_title: Southern District of Georgia |  OPERATION LOW VOLTAGE- Homeland Security Investigations Executes Federal Search Warrant at the HL-GA Battery Company, LLC, located on the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant American Campus in Ellabell, Georgia | United States Department of Justice

facts: Anovion Technologies announced plans for an $800 million initial investment in a new manufacturing facility in Southwest Georgia.
The Decatur County facility will produce 40,000 metric tonnes per annum of synthetic graphite anode material for lithium-ion batteries.
The facility will create hundreds of clean energy jobs.
Anovion selected Decatur County in Southwest Georgia as the location for its first large-scale expansion of manufacturing capacity for premium synthetic graphite anode materials.
The initial facility will be 1,500,000 square feet.

Anovion chose the location based on proximity to existing and planned low- and carbon-free energy sources, short supply chains, access to existing rail infrastructure, a highly valuable skilled workforce, and a business-friendly environment.
Eric Stopka is the Chief Executive Officer of Anovion.
Anovion conducted a thorough search to identify a location addressing energy, transportation, logistics, and human capital needs.
The proximity to other battery and EV manufacturing plants will allow Anovion to further reduce its carbon footprint.
Governor Brian Kemp welcomed Anovion to Georgia, calling it the No. 1 state for business and fast becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
In October 2022, Anovion was awarded a grant totaling $117 million from the U.S. Department of Energy under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
The DOE grant aims to help secure the domestic battery manufacturing supply chain for the country’s electrified future.
This new expansion facility will be the first of this scale for Anovion and in North America.
The facility is a crucial first step to reshoring synthetic graphite anode production.
Once fully operational, the facility is expected to initially produce 40,000 metric tonnes per annum.
Bainbridge Mayor Edward Reynolds welcomed Anovion to Bainbridge.
Anovion's decision will create over 400 high-paying, quality jobs.
Anovion will use a sustainable and technologically advanced manufacturing process at the new facility.
The facility will rely on the region’s rapidly decarbonizing energy mix to power manufacturing.
Anovion will introduce proprietary furnace technologies that substantially reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
The company has laid out a clear roadmap to achieve a carbon-neutral footprint over time.
Chip Dunn is the Executive Chairman of Anovion.
Anovion's long-term strategic vision focuses on growth and innovation, positioning itself as a leader in the U.S. energy transition.
The goal includes securing a domestic source for critical materials required for U.S. lithium-ion battery production.
Anovion will continue research and development in electrochemistry and technology improvements in its production process.
In addition to Georgia, Anovion is expanding its Anovion Center of Excellence.
Anovion is also expanding a hydro-powered sister manufacturing facility near Niagara Falls, NY.
The Niagara Falls facility is one of the only qualified U.S. sources of battery-grade synthetic graphite that is commercially shipping product today.
Anovion Technologies is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.
Anovion is a U.S.-owned and U.S.-based advanced materials business.
Anovion is currently one of America’s first commercial-scale manufacturers of premium, cleaner anode-grade synthetic graphite.
Anovion's existing footprint includes facilities near Niagara Falls, NY and Clarksburg, WV.
As a leader in lithium-ion battery materials, Anovion began commercial production in early 2021.
Anovion is among a limited number of graphite anode producers in America to have successfully gained qualification for EV applications.
In 2022, Anovion was awarded the $117 million DOE grant to rapidly scale production capacity to meet growing demand.
The grant supports construction of a large-scale factory in the southeastern U.S. and investment in its relocated Anovion Center of Excellence (ACE) in Sanborn, NY.
Anovion is commencing a multi-year expansion program of 150,000 metric tonnes of annual capacity for anode-grade synthetic graphite.
source_title: Anovion Technologies Announces Plans for $800 Million Initial Investment in New Manufacturing Facility in Southwest Georgia

facts: On December 19, 2024, the Department of Energy’s Loan Programs Office (LPO) announced the closing of a $1.45 billion loan guarantee to Hanwha Q Cells Georgia, Inc. (Qcells).
The loan guarantee supports Qcells’ solar supply chain facility in Cartersville, Georgia.
The facility will produce ingots, wafers, cells, and finished solar panels.
The facility will be the largest ingot and wafer plant ever built in the United States.
The facility will reestablish critical parts of the domestic solar supply chain.
The factory will be the first fully integrated silicon-based solar manufacturing facility constructed in the United States in over a decade.
The Qcells facility will enable deployment of PV energy systems and domestic capability for critical parts of the solar supply chain.
The project is expected to create approximately 1,200 construction jobs.
Upon completion, the project will support approximately 1,650 full-time operations jobs in Cartersville, GA.
An economic review by the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development states the investment will indirectly create nearly 6,800 jobs in Georgia’s Bartow and Whitfield Counties.
The project has a potential sales output of more than $2 billion.
This investment boosts the regional economy and adds to the nearly 16 million jobs created since President Biden and Vice President Harris took office.
Once fully operational, the facility is expected to produce 3.3 GW of solar panels per year.
The annual production will be enough to supply panels to half a million American households.
The project will reduce emissions from power generation by more than 5 million tons of CO2e per year.
Hanwha Qcells USA, Inc previously opened another solar factory in 2019 in Dalton, GA.
The Dalton facility is located one hour north of the Cartersville facility.
The Dalton facility was recently expanded to produce a total of 5.1 gigawatts (GW) of solar panels per year.
The experience gained from operating the Dalton facility will benefit the new Cartersville project.
The Cartersville plant will be the first vertically integrated factory in the United States to produce ingots, wafers, cells, and panels in over a decade.
Qcells will make larger-format wafer sizes that lower costs and increase product performance.
The project will help build the U.S. solar industry while reshoring production capacity for solar components that are largely produced in China and Southeast Asia.
The project aims to onshore and reshore supply chains, reducing dependence on China and furthering U.S. leadership in the global clean energy transition.
Projects like this could benefit from the Section 45X Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credit created by President Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
The IRA incentivizes manufacturing at each step of the solar supply chain.
Solar panels produced at the Cartersville plant will help solar developers qualify for the IRA's domestic content bonus for clean electricity production and investment tax credits.
Since the IRA’s passage, over 335 GW of manufacturing capacity has been announced across the solar supply chain.
This represents more than 33,000 potential jobs and nearly $17 billion in announced investments across 118 new facilities or expansions.
The solar panels produced by the Cartersville factory will be used for distributed and utility-scale projects.
Hanwha Qcells USA is one of the ten largest utility-scale project developers for both solar and storage in the United States.
Hanwha Qcells USA has over 2 GW of projects developed or constructed and a project development pipeline of 10+ GW.
Qcells has entered into an 8-year, 12 GW solar and engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) agreement with Microsoft to be fulfilled with solar panels made in Cartersville.
The loan guarantee is offered through LPO’s Title 17 Clean Energy Financing Program.
This program includes financing opportunities for innovative energy and supply chain projects and projects that reinvest in existing energy infrastructure.
LPO borrowers are required to develop and ultimately implement a comprehensive Community Benefits Plan (CBP).
Qcells Georgia’s CBP outlines strong community and labor engagement, quality jobs, inclusion, and accessibility.
The project is located near disadvantaged communities with low-income populations and housing, transportation, and health burdens, according to the Climate and Economic Justice Screening Tool.
Qcells' new manufacturing plant will benefit residents of Cartersville, which is located about 43 miles from Atlanta.
Approximately 40 to 50 percent of the construction work has been awarded to local contractors, including contractors from Atlanta, Georgia and Chattanooga, Tennessee.
The construction contractor is partnering with Kennesaw State University in Georgia to hire recent construction management graduates.
Qcells Georgia plans to offer job training and apprenticeships to local Cartersville residents who face barriers to employment.
Across all LPO’s programs, DOE has attracted 210 applications for projects across the country totaling over $324.3 billion in requested loans and loan guarantees as of November 2024.
source_title: LPO Announces $1.45 Billion Loan Guarantee to Qcells to Finance a Solar Manufacturing Facility in Georgia | Department of Energy

facts: Hwashin is a supplier for Hyundai.
Georgia is leading the nation in anticipated electric vehicle manufacturing jobs.
Hyundai supplier announcements reach $2.2 billion.
Hyundai, Kia plants attract another electric vehicle supplier.
Georgia's largest electric vehicle plant is getting another $2B.
source_title: Hyundai supplier Hwashin to build Georgia manufacturing plant - Atlanta Business Chronicle

facts: Governor Brian Kemp announced that Hyundai Mobis plans to invest $926 million in an Electric Vehicle (EV) Power Electric system plant in Richmond Hill.
