Additional announcements regarding this partnership’s research projects, educational programs, and vehicle deployment are expected in the coming months.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and Georgia Tech Sign MOU to Further Collaborate on Hydrogen Mobility Development

facts: Hyundai Motor Group and Georgia Institute of Technology announced an expansion of their collaboration to advance hydrogen-powered transportation, deepen applied research and education, and accelerate zero-emissions vehicles in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor North America is donating four NEXO fuel cell electric SUVs exclusively for campus use.
Hyundai Motor Group is donating a hydrogen electrolyzer project exclusively for campus use.

The partnership brings NEXO hydrogen fuel cell SUVs and fueling infrastructure to the Georgia Tech campus, positioning it as one of the nation's most prominent campus-based examples for hydrogen mobility.
The hydrogen electrolyzer will be installed at Georgia Tech's North Avenue Research Area (NARA).
The donated vehicles and infrastructure will support campus operations and interdisciplinary research across engineering, sustainability, energy systems, and public policy.
Engineering research will focus on exploring hydrogen-based systems and mobility solutions.
Sustainability research will assess the environmental benefits of hydrogen technologies.
Energy systems research will understand the integration of hydrogen fuel cells into current infrastructure.
Public policy research will evaluate the regulatory and social implications of hydrogen adoption.
The university campus will serve as a living laboratory for clean transportation technologies.
Faculty and students will study the real-world performance of hydrogen technology, infrastructure requirements for large-scale deployment, and environmental impacts of hydrogen energy systems.
Insights from the initiative aim to inform and accelerate the widespread use of hydrogen technology in campuses, fleets, cities, and freight corridors.
The initiative supports Georgia Tech's strategic plan goal of expanding the use of zero-emissions vehicles powered by sustainable energy sources.
Beyond hydrogen energy, the partnership aims to advance innovation in autonomous driving, electric vehicle batteries, charging infrastructure, materials science, and cybersecurity.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) is located in Georgia.
HTWO Logistics operates as a clean logistics partnership running Hyundai XCIENT fuel cell heavy-duty trucks in logistics operations near Savannah.
The collaboration reinforces the link between education, research, and Hyundai's long-term goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2045.
The partnership aims to generate workforce talent and position Georgia as a national leader in hydrogen and zero-emissions transportation.
Additional announcements regarding the partnership's research projects, educational programs, and vehicle deployment are expected in the coming months.

Ken Ramírez, Executive Vice President and Head of Global Energy and Hydrogen Business at Hyundai Motor Group, stated that combining university research with Hyundai's global experience creates a foundation for real-world solutions to drive the energy transition.
Ken Ramírez is a Georgia Tech alumnus (class of 1991) and currently serves on the Georgia Tech Advisory Board.

Randy Parker, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor North America, stated that donating NEXO fuel cell SUVs will expand the campus clean mobility footprint and provide real-world experiences with zero-emissions transportation technology.

Ángel Cabrera, President of the Georgia Institute of Technology, stated that expanding work with Hyundai advances hydrogen research, reduces campus emissions, and strengthens Georgia's role in the future of clean mobility.
Georgia Tech has more than 57,000 undergraduate and graduate students representing 54 U.S. states and territories and more than 146 countries.
Georgia Tech conducts more than $1 billion in research annually for government, industry, and society.
Hyundai Motor Group has about 250,000 employees worldwide and its mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and Georgia Tech Sign MOU to Further Collaborate on Hydrogen Mobility Development

facts: Hyundai Motor Company announced its 2025 annual and fourth quarter business results on January 29, 2026.
In 2025, the company sold 4,138,389 vehicles worldwide, nearly unchanged from 2024.
In 2025, annual revenue reached a record-high of KRW 186.3 trillion, up 6.3 percent year over year.
In 2025, annual operating profit totaled KRW 11.47 trillion, down 19.5 percent year over year due to global trade uncertainties including tariff impacts.
In 2025, operating profit margin stood at 6.2 percent.
In 2025, annual net profit amounted to KRW 10.36 trillion, down 21.7 percent year over year.
Hyundai Motor achieved a historic milestone in the U.S., recording its first-ever one million units in annual wholesale.
The U.S. sales milestone was driven by a robust SUV portfolio and accelerating hybrid electric vehicle sales.
In Korea, full-year sales volume increased 1.1 percent year-over-year in 2025, driven by successful new model launches including the PALISADE SUV and its hybrid variant.
In Q4 2025, Hyundai Motor sold 1,033,043 units globally, a 3.1 percent decrease from the year earlier.
In Q4 2025, sales outside Korea totaled 855,547 units, down 2.4 percent year over year.
In Q4 2025, sales in Korea came to 177,496 units, down 6.3 percent year over year, reflecting a lower number of business days in October.
In Q4 2025, Hyundai Motor recorded its highest ever fourth-quarter revenue of KRW 46.84 trillion, up 0.5 percent year over year.
Q4 2025 revenue growth was driven by solid sales of high-value models such as hybrids and Genesis brand vehicles, as well as favorable exchange rates.
In Q4 2025, operating profit totaled KRW 1.7 trillion, down 39.9 percent year over year, reflecting the impact of U.S. tariffs and higher incentives amid intensified competition.
In Q4 2025, operating profit margin stood at 3.6 percent.
In Q4 2025, net profit including non-controlling interests amounted to KRW 1.18 trillion, down 52.1 percent year over year.
For full-year 2025, Hyundai Motor sold 961,812 electrified vehicles globally, up 27 percent year-over-year.
The 2025 global electrified vehicle sales comprised 275,669 EVs and 634,990 HEVs.
Electrified vehicles accounted for approximately 23 percent of total global sales in 2025.
In 2025, HEV sales grew 27.9 percent year-over-year, achieving a record-high share of 15.3 percent of total global sales.
In Q4 2025, global electrified vehicle sales increased 12.1 percent year over year to 234,957 units.
Q4 2025 electrified vehicle demand was supported by solid demand across major markets, including North America.
In Q4 2025, EV sales totaled 56,625 units globally.
In Q4 2025, HEV sales reached 168,002 units globally.
HEVs represented a record-high 16.3 percent of Hyundai Motor’s global quarterly sales in Q4 2025.
In the U.S. market in Q4 2025, HEVs reached an all-time quarterly high share of 22.6 percent.
Hyundai Motor achieved its 2025 annual guidance, with revenue growth exceeding targets and operating margin remaining within guided ranges.
The company delivered a 6.3 percent annual revenue increase in 2025, surpassing its 5 to 6 percent target range.
For 2026, Hyundai Motor is targeting consolidated revenue growth of between 1 to 2 percent.
For 2026, the company targets an annual operating profit margin of 6.3 to 7.3 percent.
For 2026, the company aims for total vehicle sales of more than 4.16 million units.
The company plans to expand its investment for 2026 total to KRW 17.8 trillion.
The 2026 investment plan includes KRW 9 trillion in capital expenditure, KRW 7.4 trillion in research and development, and KRW 1.4 trillion in strategic investments.
Hyundai Motor will pay a year-end dividend of KRW 2,500 per common share for full-year 2025.
The company's total payout per common share for full-year 2025 is KRW 10,000.
The company announced a share buyback plan of KRW 400.7 billion in line with its shareholder return policy.
Hyundai Motor expects challenging global market conditions to continue in 2026 due to stagnant industry growth and intensifying competition across emerging markets.
To navigate the market environment, the company is reinforcing its leadership in electrification and expanding localized production capabilities.

The company is advancing cost-optimization across global operations and maintaining a flexible powertrain strategy meeting diverse customer needs for EVs, HEVs, or highly efficient internal combustion models.
As new production bases ramp up operations, the company aims to accelerate sales growth and respond swiftly to regional market and supply-chain needs.
Hyundai Motor Company was established in 1967 and is present in over 200 countries with more than 120,000 employees.
The company invests in advanced technologies such as robotics and Advanced Air Mobility (AAM).
Hyundai continues efforts to introduce zero emission vehicles equipped with industry-leading hydrogen fuel cell and EV technologies.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Announces 2025 Annual and Q4 Business Results

facts: FCEVs are powered by hydrogen and produce no harmful tailpipe pollutants, emitting only water vapor and warm air.
FCEVs and the hydrogen infrastructure to fuel them are in the early stages of implementation.
The U.S. Department of Energy leads research efforts to make hydrogen-powered vehicles an affordable, environmentally friendly, and safe transportation option.
Hydrogen is considered an alternative fuel under the Energy Policy Act of 1992 and qualifies for alternative fuel vehicle tax credits.
FCEVs use a propulsion system similar to that of electric vehicles where energy stored as hydrogen is converted to electricity by the fuel cell.
Unlike conventional internal combustion engine vehicles, FCEVs produce no harmful tailpipe pollutants.
FCEVs increase U.S. energy security through diversity of energy sources and strengthen the economy.
FCEVs are fueled with pure hydrogen gas stored in a tank on the vehicle.
FCEVs can refuel in about 5 minutes and have a driving range of more than 300 miles.
FCEVs are equipped with advanced technologies to increase efficiency, such as regenerative braking systems that capture energy lost during braking and store it in a battery.
Major automobile manufacturers are offering a limited but growing number of production FCEVs to the public in certain markets, in sync with developing supporting infrastructure.
The most common type of fuel cell for vehicle applications is the polymer electrolyte membrane (PEM) fuel cell.
In a PEM fuel cell, an electrolyte membrane is sandwiched between a positive electrode (cathode) and a negative electrode (anode).
Hydrogen is introduced to the anode and oxygen from air is introduced to the cathode.
Hydrogen molecules break apart into protons and electrons due to an electrochemical reaction aided by a catalyst.
Protons travel through the membrane to the cathode while electrons are forced through an external circuit to provide power to the electric motor.
Electrons recombine with protons on the cathode side where they combine with oxygen molecules to form water.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Fuel Cell Electric Vehicles

facts: OSHA cited Hyundai Transys Georgia Powertrain Inc. – operating as Powertech America Inc. – for exposing employees to struck-by and fall hazards after a fatality at the company’s West Point, Georgia, facility.
The automobile transmission manufacturer faces $68,194 in penalties.
An employee suffered a fatal injury when a transmission turnover machine activated while performing maintenance.
OSHA cited the company for failing to train employees to control the release of hazardous energy and use energy isolation devices.
OSHA cited the company for failing to provide lockout procedures to employees in their native language.
OSHA cited the company for failing to provide fall protection.
OSHA cited the company for failing to implement a hearing conservation program.
The inspection was conducted in conjunction with OSHA’s Regional Emphasis Program for Safety Hazards in Auto Parts Industry.
OSHA Atlanta-West Area Director Jeffery Stawowy stated that failure to adequately control hazardous energy is a leading cause of serious injuries and fatalities in the auto parts industry.
Jeffery Stawowy emphasized that employers must provide workers with the tools and information needed to ensure safety for themselves and coworkers.

The company has 15 business days from receipt of the citations and proposed penalties to comply, request an informal conference with OSHA’s area director, or contest the findings before the independent Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission.
Under the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970, employers are responsible for providing safe and healthful workplaces for their employees.
source_title: U.S. Department of Labor Cites Georgia Transmissions Manufacturer For Exposing Employees to Safety Hazards After Fatality | Occupational Safety and Health Administration

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced an international mission to Germany and Poland on January 17, 2025, to strengthen economic ties and develop new relationships for Georgians.
Over the last six years prior to the announcement, international companies have invested over $38 billion in Georgia, creating more than 58,000 new opportunities for Georgians.
Communities outside of metro Atlanta attracted over 80% of those jobs created by international investments.
Germany is one of Georgia's largest trade partners and a major contributor to job creation and investment in the state.
The Governor and representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) will conduct meetings with firms already operating or under construction in Georgia.
Notable German companies on the itinerary include BASF, Gerresheimer, Carcoustics, KIRCHHOFF Automotive, and GEDIA Automotive Group.
German companies excel in industries like life sciences and automotive manufacturing, and Georgia serves as a strategic gateway for them and other European businesses entering the U.S. market.
Over the past two decades, German automotive firms alone have contributed approximately 2,200 jobs and $830 million in investments to Georgia communities.
The growth of life sciences companies like Gerresheimer demonstrates how international businesses become integral to Georgia's economy.
Exploring emerging markets such as Poland allows Georgia to stay ahead of global trends, build new partnerships, and support local companies seeking to expand internationally.
The State of Georgia has had continuous representation in Europe since 1973.
Germany is the fourth largest export and import market for the State of Georgia, with more than $12.8 billion in total trade moving between Georgia’s global gateways and Germany in 2023.
German companies have invested more than $3.9 billion in Georgia since 2010 through projects with state involvement.
An estimated 650 German-owned facilities support approximately 42,000 jobs for Georgians across the state.

Georgia-based companies such as Alimera Sciences, Bridgestone Golf, Delta Air Lines, Eversheds Sutherland, Graphic Packaging Holding, Novelis, Porex Corporation, and WestRock maintain operations in Germany.
BASF’s manufacturing presence in Georgia includes operations in Attapulgus, Edgar, and Savannah.
Gerresheimer first established a production facility in Peachtree City in 1993 and expanded in 2009 with the establishment of a Technical Competence Center (TCC).
Gerresheimer announced additional expansions in Georgia in December 2023 and May 2024.
In 2017, Carcoustics established Carcoustics USA South Inc. in Buford, Georgia, including a central research center and a manufacturing presence.
KIRCHHOFF Automotive opened an assembly facility in Lawrenceville in 2023.
In 2020, GEDIA Automotive Group announced an advanced manufacturing facility that will begin production with a focus on parts for electric vehicles in the greater Dalton area.

facts: Beginning January 1, 2025, the Treasury Department will offer tax credits for the production and sale of low emission transportation fuels, including sustainable aviation fuel (SAF).
The tax credit amount is $0.20 per gallon for non-aviation fuel and $0.35 per gallon for SAF.
For facilities that satisfy the prevailing wage and apprenticeship requirements, the credit amount is $1.00 per gallon for non-aviation fuel and $1.75 per gallon for SAF.
For any taxable year, the Clean Fuel Production Credit is equal to the applicable credit amount per gallon multiplied by the fuel’s carbon dioxide emissions factor.
Emissions factors will be published annually by the Secretary of the Treasury.
Beginning January 1, 2025, tax credits will be adjusted for inflation.
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) released guidance on registration requirements to claim the credit in May 2024.
The IRS released frequently asked questions about applying for registration in July 2024.
Further guidance is forthcoming.
The program references Public Law 117-169 and 26 U.S. Code 45Z.
Jurisdiction is Federal.
Type is Incentives.
Administering agency is the U.S. Internal Revenue Service.
Applicable technologies include Biodiesel, Ethanol, Natural Gas, and Renewable Diesel.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Clean Fuel Production Credit

facts: Georgia lawmakers recognized January 31 as "Kia Day," commending Kia's vehicle manufacturing and its impact to the state.
Kia made one of the biggest single foreign investments in Georgia's history in 2006 by establishing a state-of-the-art vehicle assembly facility in West Point.
Kia Georgia was Kia Corporation's first manufacturing plant in North America.
Kia Georgia assembles nearly 40-percent of all Kia vehicles sold in the U.S.
To date, Kia has invested more than $1.9 billion in the state.
Kia Georgia is responsible for more than 14,000 plant and supplier jobs in the region.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Kia chose Georgia because they saw something in them – their great workforce, quality of life, and pro-business approach.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia saw something in Kia – a shared vision for transformational growth and a commitment to the hardworking people of the state.
Kia is a dynamic part of the world's third-largest automaker and continues to invest in Georgia.
In October, it was revealed that assembly of Kia electric vehicles (EVs) is expected to begin in the first half of 2025 at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
HMGMA officially broke ground near Savannah, Georgia.
HMGMA will create more than 8,100 jobs over the next few years.

Sean Yoon, president and CEO of Kia North America and Kia America, stated that Kia was the first to open an auto manufacturing plant in Georgia because they saw the talented workforce, sense of community, respected pro-growth business climate and commitment to innovation that is prevalent there.
Sean Yoon stated that Kia is focused on becoming a leader in sustainable mobility by popularizing EVs and looks forward to working with and bringing new opportunities to Georgia.
Kia Georgia is home to the K5 midsize sedan and various models of the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs.
More than 90-percent of the vehicles assembled at Kia Georgia are destined for dealerships across America.
Kia Georgia's total production is anticipated to surpass 4 million vehicles in April of 2023.
The West Point facility has provided over $20 million in support of Georgia communities since 2009.
Over $6 million was provided to support educational programs such as the THINC College & Career Academy in LaGrange and SAE's "A World in Motion."

Stuart Countess, president and CEO of Kia Georgia, stated that Georgia's array of resources to promote economic growth and workforce development enabled Kia to focus on the key fundamentals of building a state-of-the-art facility and the necessary skill sets for hundreds of team members new to automotive manufacturing.
Stuart Countess stated that as Kia looks ahead with excitement, they are confident in their ability to adapt and lead the evolving automotive industry.
Stuart Countess stated that Kia's experience in the state of Georgia is a huge part of building that confidence.
Kia America is headquartered in Irvine, California.
Kia America continues to top automotive quality surveys and is recognized as one of the 100 Best Global Brands.
Kia serves as the "Official Automotive Partner" of the NBA.
Kia offers a range of gasoline, hybrid, plug-in hybrid and electrified vehicles sold through a network of over 750 dealers in the U.S.
Several cars and SUVs are proudly assembled in America.
The K5, Sportage, Sorento, and Telluride (excludes HEV and PHEV models) are assembled in the United States from U.S. and globally sourced parts.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
With an annual capacity of 340,000 units, Kia Georgia is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, GA.
Kia Georgia began mass vehicle assembly on Nov. 16, 2009.
Kia Georgia is home to the Telluride SUV, Sorento SUV, Sportage SUV (excluding HEV and PHEV models) and the K5 mid-size sedan.

canonical_name: Generation III+ SMR Pathway to Deployment Program
facts: U.S. Department of Energy announced the selection of eight companies to support the near-term deployment of advanced light-water small modular reactors in the United States on May 14, 2026.
Awardees will collectively receive more than $94 million in Federal cost-shared funding.
Funding aims to spur additional Gen III+ SMR deployments by addressing key gaps that have hindered the domestic nuclear industry in licensing, supply chain, and site preparation.
Projects will bolster the supply chain needed to deliver new nuclear generation in the 2030s.
Projects aim to strengthen the development of Gen III+ SMR orderbooks.
Awards advance President Trump’s Executive Orders to usher in a nuclear renaissance and expand America’s Energy Dominance agenda.
U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright stated that President Trump has made clear America is going to build more energy, not less, and nuclear is central to that mission.

Wright noted advanced light-water SMRs will provide reliable, round-the-clock power to fuel the President’s manufacturing boom, support data centers and AI growth, and reinforce a stronger, more secure electric grid.
Awards are administered under the Generation III+ SMR Pathway to Deployment Program.

Constellation SMR Development, LLC received $17,264,292 to pursue a U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission approved Early Site Permit for a location in New York to support future deployment of Gen III+ SMRs.
Nebraska Public Power District received $27,864,860 to obtain a U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission approved Early Site Permit for a location in Nebraska to support future deployment of Gen III+ SMRs.

BWXT Nuclear Energy, Inc. received $21,423,305 to procure equipment for an existing facility in Mount Vernon, Ind., required for final assembly of reactor pressure vessels and manufacture of other large nuclear reactor components.

Container Technologies Industries, LLC received $547,900 to expand the company’s nuclear quality assurance certifications for their facility in Helenwood, Tenn., to enable production of steel for Gen III+ SMR deployments.

Framatome U.S. Government Solutions, LLC received $8,800,000 to expand a fuel fabrication facility in Richland, Wash., by increasing ceramic pellet production lines and adding approximately 200 metric tons of uranium annual capacity.

Global Nuclear Fuel Americas, LLC received $3,000,000 to establish a second production line for fuel rod fabrication for boiling water reactors, acquire capital equipment to automate pellet inspection, and implement automated storage and handling for pellets at their facility in Wilmington, N.C.
American Forgemasters Company received $2,900,000 to procure a new furnace for their facility in New Castle, Pa., to facilitate domestic production of large component forgings for Gen III+ SMRs.

Scot Forge Company received $12,267,000 to procure and install a large vertical turning lathe and gantry style milling machine for a facility in Spring Grove, Ill., to facilitate domestic production and manufacturing of large components for Gen III+ SMRs.
Light-water SMRs offer flexible deployment, proven technology, and ability to leverage existing supply chains, making them the fastest and most scalable path to new nuclear capacity.
In March 2025, DOE issued a $900 million solicitation to de-risk the deployment of Gen III+ SMRs.
DOE announced $800 million in Tier 1 awards in December 2025 to the Tennessee Valley Authority and Holtec Government Services to advance initial projects in Tennessee and Michigan.
These initial projects aim to expand the country’s nuclear capacity while facilitating additional follow-on projects and associated supply chains.
Another round of awards under Tier 2 may be issued if additional funds become available.

links.context: Generation III+ Small Modular Reactor Pathway to Deployment Program webpage

source_title: Energy Department Awards $94 Million to American Companies to Help Expedite the Deployments of Small Modular Reactors in the United States | Department of Energy

facts: Tradeport West Business Center in Liberty County has earned “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) certification with “Select” status.
The site’s new “GRAD Select” status provides an additional seal of approval that makes it especially attractive for future industrial development.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that achieving GRAD Select status is a powerful signal to companies and site selectors that a location is ready for investment and growth.
Liberty County’s Tradeport West Business Center has received further support through state initiatives such as the Rural Site Development Initiative (RSDI).
The 1,224-acre industrial site in Liberty County, Georgia, is located south of Midway on US 17, about one mile south of its intersection with US 84, with I-95 approximately five miles east.
Planned main access to the site will be from US 17.
The site borders the Riceboro Southern Railway.
Portions of the property have been graded to accommodate access roads, while other areas remain undeveloped.
Tradeport West is a major expansion priority for the Liberty County Development Authority (LCDA), accelerated by a $2 million OneGeorgia Rural Site Development Initiative grant.
The $2 million grant will fund 7,000 feet of new access road, critical utility extensions, and site preparation across 170 acres of prime industrial property within the next year.

State Representative and LCDA Board Chairman Al Williams stated that earning ‘GRAD Select’ certification reflects years of deliberate investment and close partnership with GDEcD to ensure the highest standards for speed-to-market and positioning the park to deliver higher-wage jobs as it develops.

Donald Lovette, Chairman of the Liberty County Board of Commissioners and LCDA Board Member, stated that Liberty County is proud to have developed this asset and excited about its impact in strengthening the local economy.
The industrial park has already attracted strong interest from large-scale manufacturing prospects.

The designation reinforces Liberty County’s position as a premier destination, strategically located between the Ports of Savannah and Brunswick and supported by a strong workforce pipeline from Fort Stewart and across the region.
Completing the GRAD certification program with GDEcD is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development in their regions.

To qualify for GRAD status, available sites must meet the program’s due diligence standards, be reviewed by a third party, and earn the final approval of a board of advisors comprised of public and private sector economic development professionals.
The “GRAD Select” status is an indication that a site has met or exceeded more rigorous certification requirements.
The GRAD Program offers more than 70 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects, thanks to comprehensive due diligence.
Georgia is investing over $21 million to help rural communities prepare new industrial sites through the Rural Site Development Initiative.
These grants support site improvements and the addition of new GRAD-certified sites.

facts: TI Fluid Systems (LSE:TIFS), a leading global supplier of automotive fluid systems technology, has introduced an innovative approach for coolant fluid management on electrified vehicles.

The new technology is an Integrated Thermal Manifold assembly (ITMa) that provides a one-piece, light-weight, blow-molded plastic manifold to optimize complex line design and replaces multiple line bundle assemblies of thermal loops for heating and cooling on next generation of electric vehicles.
Current EVs typically use multiple complex rubber, aluminum or multi-layer plastic lines that are packaged tightly together under hood or chassis mounted.

TI Fluid Systems has developed the ability to evolve their Fuel Tank and Delivery Systems (FTDS) division fuel tank blow molding technology to produce a one piece integrated thermal 'manifold' (ITMa) approach.

This single piece blow molded plastic manifold provides significant OEM EV value including improved flow performance for efficiency gains, lower part count for sub and final vehicle assembly, lower weight, and reduced investment tooling costs.

TI Fluid Systems' FTDS division has demonstrated further integration potential for 'fluid storage' (coolant reservoir expansion tank), 'fluid temp control' (heat exchangers/sensors) and 'fluid delivery' (pumps) to create a very compact module solution.
These integrated thermal modular assemblies are bespoke to each OEM vehicle or application and can be engineered for various system types of multiple thermal loops.
Hans Dieltjens, COO stated that the company's innovations have captured the attention of OEMs as they move from 'build-to-print' components to 'innovative collaboration' of EV sub-systems.

Hans Dieltjens, COO also noted that TI Fluid Systems is leveraging its fluid know-how on new thermal EV challenges and successfully demonstrating the ability to pivot fluid competencies, capabilities and assets to provide a family of new break-through EV products with various levels of integration.
TI Fluid Systems has received their first ITMa global EV platform application release for model year 2023 introduction with a leading high-volume European OEM.
TI Fluid Systems is a leading global manufacturer of fluid storage, carrying and delivery systems primarily for the light duty automotive market.
The company possesses nearly 100 years of automotive fluid systems experience.
TI Fluid Systems operates manufacturing facilities in 107 locations across 28 countries serving all major global OEMs.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems Introduces New Integrated Thermal Manifold Assemblies For Electric Vehicles

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Qcells will invest more than $2.5 billion to expand its solar module manufacturing operations in Georgia.
Qcells will build a new facility in Cartersville and add a third facility to its Dalton location, creating more than 2,500 new jobs in northwest Georgia.
These investments are expected to bring Qcells' total solar panel production capacity in Georgia to 8.4 gigawatts by 2024.
In 2019, Qcells opened the largest solar panel manufacturing facility in the Western Hemisphere in Dalton, Georgia.
The 1.7-gigawatt Dalton facility has continued to expand since then and now supports more than 700 jobs in Whitfield County.
Qcells is expected to increase the number of employees in Georgia to more than 4,000 by the end of 2024.
Qcells will construct a new manufacturing facility located at Highland 75 Corporate / Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site in Bartow County.
The new Cartersville facility will manufacture 3.3 gigawatts of solar ingots, wafers, cells, and finished panels.
The new Cartersville facility will create approximately 2,000 new jobs for the region.
Qcells expects to break ground at the Cartersville site in the first quarter of 2023.
Due to rapid growth, Qcells will also add a new facility adjacent to its existing plant at 300 Nexus Drive in Dalton.
The third Whitfield County facility will create 510 new jobs and add another 2 gigawatts of production capability.
Jevin Jensen, Chairman of the Whitfield County Board of Commissioners, noted that optimizing recent land purchases will add 500 well-paying jobs and continue to diversify Whitfield County's economy.

Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development's (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on these projects in partnership with the Dalton-Whitfield Joint Development Authority, Bartow-Cartersville Joint Development Authority, Bartow-Cartersville Second Joint Development Authority, Georgia Institute of Technology, Georgia Electric Membership Corporation (Georgia EMC), and Georgia Quick Start.
The solar industry has experienced an average annual growth rate of 33 percent for the last decade, according to the Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA).
Supply chain challenges disrupted that growth in 2022, with a 23 percent decline in installations compared to 2021.
Qcells plans to reduce future supply chain barriers in the solar industry by building a comprehensive value chain in the United States.
Today's announcement positions Qcells as the only company in the U.S. to establish a fully-integrated, silicon-based solar supply chain from raw material to finished panel.
In Georgia, net electricity generation from renewable sources has increased by more than 93 percent since 2009.
The state's electricity mix currently consists of 13.6 percent renewable energy.
Over 4 percent of the state's electricity now comes from solar power.

Qcells is one of the world’s leading clean energy companies, recognized as a manufacturer of high-performance, high-quality solar cells and modules, portfolio of intelligent storage systems, and growing international pipeline of large-scale renewable energy projects.
Qcells also provides renewable electricity retail services and packages to end customers worldwide.

links.context: Job search for Qcells Dalton roles

source_title: Qcells to More than Double Production in Georgia, Create 2,500 Jobs | Georgia

facts: Volumetric energy density refers to the amount of energy that can be contained within a given volume.
Increasing the volumetric energy density of batteries allows electric vehicles (EVs) to travel further without increasing the size of the battery pack.
Higher volumetric energy density can allow an EV to travel the same distance with a smaller battery pack, thus saving space, weight, and manufacturing costs.
Funding in battery development by the Department of Energy and private industry has yielded impressive gains in energy density.
In 2008, lithium-ion batteries had a volumetric energy density of 55 watt-hours per liter.
By 2020, the volumetric energy density of lithium-ion batteries had increased to 450 watt-hours per liter.

The energy density data is sourced from Nitin Muralidharan, Ethan C. Self, Marm Dixit, Zhijia Du, Rachid Essehli, Ruhul Amin, Jagjit Nanda, and Ilias Belharouak in the January 2022 Advanced Energy Materials paper 'Next-Generation Cobalt-Free Cathodes – A Prospective Solution to the Battery Industry's Cobalt Problem'.
source_title: FOTW #1234, April 18, 2022: Volumetric Energy Density of Lithium-ion Batteries Increased by More than Eight Times Between 2008 and 2020 | Department of Energy

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy released the 45ZCF-GREET model.
This announcement follows guidance on the Clean Fuels Production Credit (section 45Z) released by the U.S. Department of the Treasury and Internal Revenue Service on January 10, 2025.
The Clean Fuels Production Credit provides a tax credit for the production of transportation fuels with life cycle emissions below certain levels.
The credit applies to eligible transportation fuel produced domestically after December 31, 2024, that is sold by December 31, 2027.
The 45ZCF-GREET model includes feedstock-specific fuel production pathways for synthetic aviation fuels (SAF) and non-SAF fuels.
The model is designed specifically to evaluate life cycle emissions to meet the requirements of section 45Z and includes features that make it easy to use for taxpayers.

DOE released the 45ZCF-GREET User Manual, which describes how the model characterizes life cycle emissions of transportation fuel production pathways and provides guidance for using the model to determine emissions rates for alternative fuels under section 45Z.
The 45ZCF-GREET model and user manual are available on the DOE GREET webpage.
source_title: U.S. Department of Energy Releases 45ZCF-GREET | Department of Energy

facts: National Grid is an investor-owned utility (IOU) that operates in multiple states.
The page provides a summary of the types of incentives provided by the utility related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
Residential fuel price incentives include an EV Time-of-Use (TOU) Rate.
Residential infrastructure incentives include an EV Level 2 Charging Station Purchase Rebate and a Make-Ready or Pre-Wiring Rebate for EV Chargers.
No residential vehicle incentives are currently offered.
Non-residential fuel prices have no incentives currently offered.

Non-residential infrastructure incentives include an EV Level 2 Charging Station Installation Rebate, an EV Direct Current (DC) Fast Charging Station Installation Rebate, and a Make-Ready or Pre-Wiring Rebate for EV Chargers.
A Fleet Electrification Assessment is offered as a non-residential vehicle incentive.
National Grid operates in the states of Massachusetts, New York, and Rhode Island.
National Grid serves the Boston-Cambridge-Newton, Buffalo-Cheektowaga, and Providence-Warwick Metropolitan Statistical Areas.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: National Grid

entity_name: DOE Mines & Metals Capacity Expansion Funding Program
canonical_name: Funding Notice: Mines & Metals Capacity Expansion – Piloting By-Product Critical Minerals and Materials Recovery at Domestic Industrial Facilities
facts: On November 14, 2025, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) announced up to $275 million in federal funding for American industrial facilities capable of producing valuable minerals from existing industrial and coal byproducts.
These critical materials are essential for developing technology needed to support U.S. energy production, manufacturing, and transport and for national defense.
The Department announced in August its intent to invest $1 billion to advance and scale mining, processing, and manufacturing technologies.
This investment aims to deliver on President Trump’s Executive Orders, Unleashing American Energy and Immediate Measures to Increase American Mineral Production.
These actions will secure America’s critical material supply chain, increase domestic mineral production, reduce reliance on foreign sources, and strengthen U.S. energy independence.
The pilots will demonstrate the feasibility of recovering critical minerals from existing American infrastructure and industrial activity.
The initiative aims to reduce waste while building new domestic supply chains for high-value materials.
Eligible applicants include institutions of higher education, for- and non-profit entities, state and local governments, and Indian Tribes.

This funding opportunity has two topic areas for the design, construction, and operation of large pilot-scale facilities to produce critical materials: Topic Area 1: Mines & Metals Pilots—Coal-Based Industry and Topic Area 2: Mines & Metals Pilots—All Industries.
AMMPT announced $75 million across five selected projects that use coal and coal-based feedstocks to produce rare earth elements and other critical materials.
The selected projects aim to derisk the technical and financial uncertainties for commercial deployment of critical materials byproduct recovery technologies.
Selected projects will reduce America’s dependence on foreign resources through large pilot-scale production under real-world conditions.
University of North Dakota (Headquarters: Grand Forks, North Dakota) is among the selected projects.
Valor Metals, Inc. (Headquarters: New York, New York) is among the selected projects.
CONSOL Innovations, LLC (Headquarters: Canonsburg, Pennsylvania) is among the selected projects.
American Resources Corporation (Headquarters: Fishers, Indiana) is among the selected projects.
Peabody Energy Corporation (Headquarters: St. Louis, Missouri) is among the selected projects.
The NOI Issue Date was 08/13/2025.
The Funding Opportunity Issue Date was 11/14/2025.
The Submission Deadline for Full Applications is 1/15/2026 at 5:00 PM ET.
The Expected Date for Selection Notifications is Summer 2026.
source_title: Funding Notice: Mines & Metals Capacity Expansion – Piloting By-Product Critical Minerals and Materials Recovery at Domestic Industrial Facilities | Department of Energy

canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Transportation Technologies Office
facts: Advanced materials are essential for boosting the fuel economy of modern automobiles while maintaining safety and performance.
A 10% reduction in vehicle weight can result in a 6%-8% fuel economy improvement.

Replacing cast iron and traditional steel components with lightweight materials such as high-strength steel, magnesium (Mg) alloys, aluminum (Al) alloys, carbon fiber, and polymer composites can directly reduce the weight of a vehicle's body and chassis by up to 50 percent.
Using lightweight components and high-efficiency engines enabled by advanced materials in one quarter of the U.S. fleet could save more than 5 billion gallons of fuel annually by 2030.
Lightweight structural materials allow cars to carry additional advanced emission control systems, safety devices, and integrated electronic systems without increasing overall vehicle weight.
Lightweight materials are especially important for hybrid electric, plug-in hybrid electric, and electric vehicles.
Using lightweight materials in these vehicles can offset the weight of power systems such as batteries and electric motors, improving efficiency and increasing all-electric range.
The use of lightweight materials could result in needing a smaller and lower cost battery while keeping the all-electric range of plug-in vehicles constant.

Research and development into lightweight materials is essential for lowering their cost, increasing their ability to be recycled, enabling their integration into vehicles, and maximizing fuel economy benefits.

The Transportation Technologies Office (TTO) works to improve these materials in four ways: increasing understanding through modeling and computational materials science; improving properties like strength, stiffness, and ductility; improving manufacturing regarding material cost, production rate, or yield; and developing alloys of advanced materials.
In the short term, replacing heavy steel components with high-strength steel, aluminum, or glass fiber-reinforced polymer composites can decrease component weight by 10-60 percent.

Scientists already understand the properties of these short-term materials and associated manufacturing processes, but researchers are working to lower their cost and improve processes for joining, modeling, and recycling.
In the longer term, advanced materials such as magnesium and carbon fiber reinforced composites could reduce the weight of some components by 50-75 percent.
The Office is working to increase knowledge of these long-term materials' chemical and physical properties and reduce their cost.
Further developing advanced materials requires increasing understanding of their composition and morphology, with computational materials science speeding up the process by simulating experiments.
Computational approaches can bring advanced materials like magnesium into the market much faster than past materials and help create vehicle designs that maximize their potential.

To improve research tools, TTO works with the Materials Genome Initiative, an interagency effort supporting reduced time to develop advanced materials through integrated computation, experimentation, and data.
Work supported by TTO has developed computational tools enabling improvements in joining methods, corrosion prevention, and predictive models.
TTO collaborates with DOE's National Laboratories on lightweight materials research.
TTO collaborates with CMEI's Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office on lightweight materials research.
TTO works through cross-cutting government/industry partnerships including the U.S. DRIVE Partnership focusing on light-duty vehicles.
TTO works through cross-cutting government/industry partnerships including the 21st Century Truck Partnership focusing on heavy-duty vehicles.
TTO works through cross-cutting government/industry partnerships including the US Automotive Materials Partnership (USAMP).
A key research and development goal is to validate by 2015 the ability to reduce the weight of a passenger vehicle body and chassis system by 50% compared to a 2002 vehicle.
This 50% weight reduction goal requires cost-effectiveness and recyclability of the materials.
Results of these R&D activities are described annually at the Annual Merit Review and Peer Evaluation Meeting and in the annual Progress Report.

These reports serve as a benchmark for current state-of-the-art technologies and technical goals in Light-Duty Vehicles Technical Requirements and Gaps for Lightweight and Propulsion Materials, and Trucks and Heavy-Duty Vehicles Technical Requirements and Gaps for Lightweight and Propulsion Materials.
Researchers present summaries of VTO-supported lightweight materials projects at the Annual Merit Review and Peer Evaluation.

Selected presentations from the 2014 Merit Review include Materials Technologies R&D Overview, Multi-Material Lightweight Vehicles, and High-Speed Joining of Dissimilar Alloy Aluminum Tailor Welded Blanks.
Magnesium alloys can achieve a mass reduction of 30-70%.
Carbon fiber composites can achieve a mass reduction of 50-70%.
Aluminum and Al matrix composites can achieve a mass reduction of 30-60%.
Titanium can achieve a mass reduction of 40-55%.
Glass fiber composites can achieve a mass reduction of 25-35%.
Advanced high strength steel can achieve a mass reduction of 15-25%.
High strength steel can achieve a mass reduction of 10-28%.
source_title: Lightweight Materials for Cars and Trucks | Department of Energy

canonical_name: Alternative Fuels Data Center Medium- and Heavy-Duty Vehicle Search
facts: The page provides a search tool for medium- and heavy-duty alternative fuel vehicles, engines, and power sources.
Autocar ACMD runs on LNG or CNG, uses an Allison 2500/3000/3500 RDS transmission, and features a Cummins L9N 8.9L Near Zero power source, available in 4x2 and 6x4 chassis configurations.

Autocar ACX runs on LNG or CNG, uses an Allison 3000/4000/4500 RDS transmission, features Cummins L9N 8.9L and ISX12N 11.9L Near Zero power sources, and is available in 4x2, 6x4, and 8x4 chassis configurations.

Autocar DC-64 runs on CNG or LNG, uses an Allison 3000/3500/4500 RDS transmission, features a Cummins ISX12N 11.9L Near Zero power source, and is available in Mixer, Dump, or Refuse/Rolloff configurations.
Battle Motors Raider is an electric vehicle with a 225-mile electric-only range, a 2-Speed Powershift Automatic transmission, and a BorgWarner Cascadia Motion IM435 power source.
According to the manufacturer, the Battle Motors Raider is available in Class 7 or 8, up to 72,000 GVWR, up to 570 HP, with a 240-400 kWh battery pack, and achieves an 80% recharge in 180 minutes.

Battle Motors Raider CNG runs on CNG, uses an Allison 4500 RDS transmission, features Cummins L9N 8.9L and ISX12N 11.9L Near Zero power sources, and is available in Class 7 or 8 with up to 66,000 GVWR.

Battle Motors Striker is an electric vehicle with a 130-150 mile electric-only range, a 420 kWh battery capacity, a Direct Drive or 2-Speed Powershift Automatic transmission, and a BorgWarner Cascadia Motion IM435 power source.
According to the manufacturer, the Battle Motors Striker has a 54,000 GVWR, produces 442-570 HP, and achieves an 80% recharge in 110 minutes.
BYD Class 6F Box Truck is an electric vehicle with a 250-mile electric-only range, BYD transmission, and a BYD 390 kW electric motor power source.

According to the manufacturer, the BYD Class 6F Box Truck has a 26,000 lbs GVWR, up to 523 HP, 2,360 lb-ft torque, offers 281 kWh or 343 kWh battery options, supports charging capacity up to 120 kW, achieves a 20%-80% charge in 2-3 hours, and includes an 8-year battery warranty.
BYD RC-S4 Stripped Chassis is an electric vehicle with a 165-mile electric-only range, BYD transmission, and a BYD e-Axle power source.

According to the manufacturer, the BYD RC-S4 Stripped Chassis has an 11,000 lbs or 14,330 lbs GVWR, up to 523 HP, 2,360 lb-ft torque, offers 80/90/100/132 kWh battery options, supports charging capacity up to 150 kW, achieves a 20%-80% charge in 2-3 hours, and includes an 8-year battery warranty.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Vehicle Search

facts: The New Clean Vehicle Credit is not available for vehicles acquired after Sept. 30, 2025.
A vehicle must be placed in service to claim the credit.
If a vehicle is placed in service after Sept. 30, 2025, acquisition must have occurred on or before Sept. 30, 2025 to remain eligible for the credit.
Acquisition can be demonstrated by entering into a binding written contract and making a payment on the vehicle on or before Sept. 30, 2025.
A vehicle is considered placed in service when the buyer takes possession of it.
The page covers changes to the credit under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill.
Buyers who acquire a new plug-in electric vehicle (EV) or fuel cell vehicle (FCV) on or before Sept. 30, 2025 may qualify for the clean vehicle tax credit upon placing the vehicle in service.
Sellers must provide buyers with information about the vehicle's qualifications when they take possession of the vehicle.
Sellers must register online and report vehicle qualification information to the IRS; failure to do so renders the vehicle ineligible for the credit.
Buyers may qualify for a tax credit of up to $7,500 under Internal Revenue Code Section 30D for purchasing a new, qualified plug-in EV or FCV.
The credit is available to both individuals and their businesses.
To qualify, the vehicle must be purchased for the buyer's own use rather than for resale.
To qualify, the vehicle must be used primarily within the United States.
Buyers' modified adjusted gross income (AGI) cannot exceed $300,000 for married couples filing jointly or a surviving spouse.
Buyers' modified AGI cannot exceed $225,000 for heads of households.
Buyers' modified AGI cannot exceed $150,000 for all other filers.
Buyers can use their modified AGI from the year they take delivery of the vehicle or the prior year, whichever is lower.
If a buyer's modified AGI falls below the threshold in either of the two relevant years, they remain eligible to claim the credit.
The credit is nonrefundable at tax filing if not transferred at the time of sale, meaning taxpayers cannot receive a refund exceeding their tax liability through this credit.
Any excess credit amount cannot be applied to future tax years.
The final credit amount depends on when the vehicle was placed in service (took delivery), regardless of the actual purchase date.
For vehicles placed in service between January 1 and April 17, 2023, the base credit is $2,500.
An additional $417 is added for vehicles with at least 7 kilowatt hours of battery capacity.
An additional $417 is added for each kilowatt hour of battery capacity beyond 5 kilowatt hours.
The maximum credit for the January 1 to April 17, 2023 period is up to $7,500 total.
In general, the minimum credit will be $3,751 ($2,500 plus three times $417) for a vehicle with the minimum required 7 kilowatt hours of battery capacity.
For vehicles placed in service on April 18, 2023, and after, buyers must meet new critical mineral and battery component requirements to qualify.
Vehicles meeting only the critical minerals requirement qualify for a credit up to $3,750.
Vehicles meeting only the battery components requirement qualify for a credit up to $3,750.
Vehicles meeting both the critical mineral and battery component requirements qualify for a credit up to $7,500.
A vehicle that fails to meet either the critical mineral or battery component requirement is ineligible for any credit.
Qualified vehicles must have a battery capacity of at least 7 kilowatt hours.
Qualified vehicles must have a gross vehicle weight rating of less than 14,000 pounds.
Qualified vehicles must be manufactured by a qualified manufacturer.
Qualified vehicles must undergo final assembly in North America.
Qualified vehicles must meet critical mineral and battery component requirements as of April 18, 2023.
The credit only applies to sales where the buyer purchases the vehicle new.
Sellers are required to report the buyer's name and taxpayer identification number to the IRS for the buyer to be eligible to claim the credit.
The vehicle's manufacturer suggested retail price (MSRP) cannot exceed $80,000 for vans, sport utility vehicles, and pickup trucks.
The vehicle's MSRP cannot exceed $55,000 for other vehicle types.
MSRP is defined as the manufacturer's suggested retail price including installed options, accessories, and trim but excluding destination fees, and may differ from the actual purchase price.
Buyers can find their vehicle's weight, battery capacity, final assembly location (listed as final assembly point), and VIN on the vehicle's window sticker.
To claim the credit, taxpayers must file Form 8936, Clean Vehicle Credits, with their tax return.
Claiming the credit requires providing the vehicle's VIN on the tax form.
Dealers are required to provide a paper copy of a time-of-sale report to buyers upon taking possession of the vehicle.
Buyers should keep the time-of-sale report copy for their records as it confirms the dealer reported the purchase date to the IRS.
Taxpayers must file Form 8936 in the tax year they take delivery of the vehicle, regardless of whether the credit was transferred at sale or deferred to filing.
source_title: Credits for new clean vehicles purchased in 2023 or after | Internal Revenue Service

facts: More than 1,600 industry professionals convened at AEE World 2023 in Orlando, Florida.
Better Plants was a gold-level sponsor of the event and had its own track dedicated to highlighting partner success and solutions.
Former Vice President Al Gore participates in a Q&A session during the opening plenary.
DOE’s Better Plants team hosted three technical sessions covering energy efficiency and energy management, industrial electrification, and low-carbon fuels, feedstocks, and energy sources.
Partners who shared strategies and experiences included Eastman Chemical Co., GE Aerospace, Pactiv Evergreen, Volvo Group North America, Whirlpool, RTX, Nestlé, and the City of Fort Wayne, Indiana.
DOE recognized Cleveland-Cliffs, Oshkosh Corporation, and Volvo Group North America for achieving their energy efficiency and GHG emissions reduction goals at the Better Plants luncheon.
Maria T. Vargas, director of the Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative, discussed critical pathways for reducing carbon emissions and advancing electrification.

Anne Hampson, acting deputy director of DOE’s Industrial Efficiency & Decarbonization Office, joined a panel discussion to share insights on public-private collaboration and how DOE builds partnerships to drive a clean energy future.
Better Plants is a voluntary partnership initiative that succeeds because partners eagerly step up to share solutions at events like AEE World.
Upcoming events include Technology Days on February 7-8, 2024 at Oak Ridge National Laboratory and the 2024 Better Buildings & Better Plants Summit on April 2-4 in Washington, D.C.
source_title: Highlights from AEE World 2023 | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 130 new jobs and an estimated $18 million investment in Dublin-Laurens County.
Woory Industrial Company, known as Woory, was established in 1989 and specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the global automotive industry.
The company is concentrating on the development of new products for electric vehicles (EV) and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, stated that the transition to electric vehicles is the future and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets.
Over the past 10 years, Woory has successfully grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation.
The new facility will be located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin and will be the company’s first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations are expected to begin in November 2023.
Unlike internal combustion engine vehicles, electric vehicles do not have a heating source, so heaters for EVs are required for cabin heating and maintaining battery temperature.
Woory’s pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has made it a leader in this growing market segment.
Woory will be hiring for positions in personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that Woory’s commitment to place an existing manufacturing building back into service helps promote infill development and creates new career paths for the community.

Dr. Fred Williams, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, noted that Dublin and Laurens County have a rapidly growing automotive sector due to their geographic location on Interstate 16 and access to a skilled workforce.
The partnership with area education systems and training providers will ensure training programs are in place to fill Woory’s talent pipeline for years to come.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project is a partnership with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia has invested for decades to match competitive projects with communities and sites that are prepared for development.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia’s pursuit of job creation opportunities along the entire EV supply chain has resulted in over $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of more than 28,400 jobs.
Woory began operating a small factory producing fuel senders in Suwon, South Korea almost 35 years ago.
The company now employs over 1,300 people across production plants around the world.
Woory has been nominated as a supplier for Tesla, Rivian, Lucid, and Canoo.
Woory is currently supplying the HV heater system used to heat the cabins of EVs.
source_title: Woory to open EV parts manufacturing, R&D facility in Laurens County – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that ArcelorMittal Building Solutions will establish its North American headquarters and a new manufacturing facility in Macon-Bibb County.
The initial investment will bring roughly $57 million to the community.
The initial investment will create up to 70 new jobs.
Plans include an additional $50 million in investment over the next several years.
Plans include as many as 70 more jobs over the next several years.
ArcelorMittal Building Solutions designs and manufactures high-performance building envelope systems used to insulate and protect warehouses, factories, data centers, and commercial buildings.
The company's insulated panels combine structural strength, thermal efficiency, and fast installation to help customers reduce construction time and energy consumption.
ArcelorMittal Building Solutions serves a broad range of construction markets through more than 50 manufacturing and office locations worldwide.

ArcelorMittal maintains a longstanding U.S. presence with steelmaking, finishing, manufacturing, research and development, and sales/support locations employing more than 3,200 people across nine states.
Jean Christophe Kennel serves as CEO of ArcelorMittal Building Solutions.
The new headquarters and manufacturing operation will include a new training facility.
The facility will be located at the Airport South site in Macon.
Construction is expected to begin in the second half of 2026.
Operations are targeted to commence in the second half of 2027, subject to customary permitting and approvals.
The company plans to hire for roles in production, logistics, management, and sales.
Macon Mayor Lester Miller stated that the combined 140 jobs will provide strong wages and opportunities for families.
Robby Fountain serves as Chairman of the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority.
Statewide Project Manager Kimberlyn Daniel represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development on this competitive project.
The project partnership includes the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson commented on the announcement, highlighting Georgia's skilled workforce and global connectivity.
ArcelorMittal Building Solutions provides integrated services for roofing, cladding, composite floors, light-gauge structures, and modular housing.
ArcelorMittal is a leading integrated steel and mining company with a presence in 60 countries and primary steelmaking operations in 14 countries.
ArcelorMittal sells its products to customers in the automotive, engineering, construction, and machinery industries.
source_title: ArcelorMittal Building Solutions Brings North American HQ to Macon-Bib | Georgia

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) operates a manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia.
YMMC produces golf cars, personal watercraft, Side-by-Side vehicles, and all-terrain vehicles (ATVs).
YMMC holds ISO9001 Quality Management System and ISO14001 Environmental Management System certifications for quality, safety, and environmental manufacturing.
Yamaha Motor Corp., USA (YMUS) maintains two corporate offices in Georgia, including one in Marietta.
YMUS has factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia, along with facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama.

YMUS product portfolio includes power assist bicycles, golf cars, ATVs, Side-by-Side vehicles, motorcycles, scooters, snowmobiles, WaveRunner personal watercraft, boats, outboard motors, outdoor power equipment, power assist wheelchair systems, surface mount technology (SMT), robotic machines, and unmanned helicopters.
Bob Brown serves as Vice President, Finance and Operations Support at YMMC.
YMUS operates additional U.S.-based subsidiaries including Yamaha Marine Systems Company (YMSC), Skeeter Boats, and Yamaha Precision Propeller.
source_title: Yamaha Funds Six All Kids Bike Programs in Georgia and

facts: Middle Georgia sits at the center of the state, where a rich cultural legacy meets manufacturing, agriculture, and military service.
The region’s diverse economy spans manufacturing, agriculture, and military operations anchored by Robins Air Force Base.
Communities like Warner Robins, Milledgeville, and Fort Valley support aerospace, agriculture, and food processing across the region.
Purdue Farms operates a food processing facility in Middle Georgia.

Irving Tissue has invested in Macon, with President Robert Irving stating: 'We greatly appreciate the State of Georgia’s commitment to economic prosperity, jobs, and growth. The team’s drive to promote the state’s advantages, while fostering a positive business environment, helped make this investment possible. We are proud to continue to invest in the State of Georgia and truly appreciate the commitment to helping us expand and grow in Macon.'
The Middle Georgia region comprises 11 counties: Baldwin, Bibb, Crawford, Houston, Jones, Monroe, Peach, Pulaski, Putnam, Twiggs, and Wilkinson.
Ocmulgee Mounds National Historical Park in the region has been proposed as Georgia’s first national park.
The region hosts cultural events including Macon’s International Cherry Blossom Festival and the Georgia National Fair in Perry.
source_title: Explore Middle Georgia | Georgia

facts: The Indiana Economic Development Corporation (IEDC) may designate an area as a certified technology park if criteria are met, including commitment from at least one business engaged in high technology activity creating significant jobs.

High technology activities for certified technology parks include advanced vehicles technology, which covers electric vehicles, hybrid electric vehicles, alternative fuel vehicles, or their components.

Each Indiana state entity must purchase or lease a clean energy vehicle unless the Department of Administration determines it is inappropriate or substantially more costly than a comparable non-clean vehicle.

A clean energy vehicle is defined as operating on one or more alternative energy sources, including rechargeable energy storage systems, electricity, ethanol, biodiesel, hydrogen, natural gas, and propane.
Before July 1, 2025, the Indiana Department of Administration must make recommendations to state entities regarding clean energy vehicle procurement.
Each Indiana state entity must annually submit information to the Department of Administration regarding its use of clean energy vehicles.
The EV Commission is established in Indiana to assess the EV market and labor force.
The EV Commission must evaluate EV production facilities and capabilities within the state.
The EV Commission must take inventory of skilled and non-skilled workers, training needs, and opportunities in the Indiana EV industry.
The EV Commission must identify and leverage manufacturing competencies within the automotive industry to increase EV production.
The EV Commission must identify opportunities for EV-related research and development industry in Indiana.
EV owners in Indiana are required to pay an additional registration fee of $230.
Plug-in hybrid and hybrid electric vehicle owners in Indiana are required to pay an additional registration fee of $77.
Indiana EV registration fees will be updated annually based on the special fuel tax index factor.
Indiana joined Michigan, Illinois, and Wisconsin in signing a memorandum of understanding (MOU) to create an EV charging corridor along the Lake Michigan coastline for seamless cross-state driving.
Signatory states of the Lake Michigan EV Circuit are committed to coordinating regional EV charger siting and deployment strategies.
Signatory states aim to achieve a consistent EV charger user experience across state lines.
The Lake Michigan EV Circuit MOU aims to encourage EV tourism around Lake Michigan.
The initiative focuses on removing barriers to EV adoption and reducing range anxiety.
Signatory states will identify and develop best practices for EV charging, infrastructure optimization, information sharing, and electricity demand management.
The corridor aims to provide reliable long-distance driving routes for EV drivers.
A working group composed of senior leadership from each signatory state will meet and report on Lake Michigan EV Circuit actions.
In Indiana, a person or joint agency that owns, operates, or leases EV chargers for public use is not defined as a public utility.
Indiana joined Illinois, Michigan, Minnesota, and Wisconsin in signing the REV Midwest MOU to accelerate vehicle electrification in the Midwest.
Signatory states of the REV Midwest plan are committed to accelerating medium- and heavy-duty fleet electrification.
The REV Midwest initiative involves collaborating on regional EV charger siting and deployment analyses with a focus on commercial routes.
States will standardize regulations, messaging, and customer experience related to EVs across state lines.
The REV Midwest plan includes evaluating opportunities for workforce development.
Signatory states will identify historically underserved communities for equitable EV charger development and EV adoption.
The initiative aims to educate consumers and fleet owners to raise EV awareness, reduce range anxiety, and increase EV adoption.
A coordination group composed of senior leadership from each REV Midwest signatory state will meet and report on actions.
Electric utilities in Indiana may request approval from the Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission to implement a pilot program evaluating large-scale EV charger deployment for public-use EVs.
Public-use EVs eligible under the utility pilot program include electric school buses, electric transit buses, and EVs used for public goods and services delivery.
Eligible utility pilot proposals must install, own, operate, or make-ready EV chargers for public-use EVs and provide incentives or rebates for EV purchases and charger installations.
Utility pilot program proposals must include plans to install EV chargers in underserved and diverse communities.

The Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission has authority to promote transportation sector electrification by designating specific EV planning issues utilities must include in their next integrated resource plan.
The Indiana Department of Environmental Management (IDEM) administers the DieselWise Indiana grant programs to reduce diesel emissions.
DieselWise grants range from $50,000 to $250,000 for diesel vehicle, equipment, and engine retrofits or replacements, as well as idle reduction technologies.
Private and public entities are eligible applicants for IDEM's DieselWise grant programs.

The U.S. Department of Transportation’s NEVI Formula Program required the Indiana Department of Transportation (INDOT) to submit an annually updated EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan for fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
INDOT's NEVI plans must describe how the state intends to distribute funds, outline physical and cybersecurity strategies, and include a Community Engagement Outcomes Report.
The Indiana 21st Century Research and Technology Fund provides grants and loans to support economic development in high technology industry clusters.
Eligible projects for the 21st Century Research and Technology Fund may include qualified alternative fuel technologies and fuel-efficient vehicle development and production.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced operating and financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026.
Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein issued a shareholder letter discussing highlights from the quarter.
US operations are expected to begin this year under the White House’s eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP) and in preparation for LA28 Olympic Games.

Archer expects Midnight operations in American cities to begin this year as part of the White House’s eIPP and in preparation to serve as the Official Air Taxi Provider of the LA28 Olympic Games, in coordination with the DOT and FAA.
Archer was recently selected as an air taxi partner in 3 winning applications encompassing 8 states, including Florida, Texas, and New York.
In April, Archer became the first eVTOL company to close Phase 3 of the FAA’s 4-phase Type Certification process for its Midnight aircraft.
Archer has been driving progress in Phase 4 of certification, where Midnight's compliance with FAA airworthiness requirements will be demonstrated through formal testing and analysis.
The flight test program expanded this quarter, with piloted VTOL and CTOL flights across its expanded fleet occurring nearly every day, often multiple times a day.
Archer took over operations of Hawthorne Airport in LA to develop the site into the airport of the future.

Located near LAX and several major LA sports and entertainment venues, Hawthorne Airport is expected to anchor Archer’s planned LA air taxi operations while serving as an innovation hub for next-generation aviation technologies.
Archer continues work alongside Anduril on a dual-use, hybrid, autonomous aircraft, aiming to deliver one of the most sophisticated vertical lift platforms in this category.
Archer anticipates beginning to win phased government awards for its dual-use aircraft program later this year.
NVIDIA is integrating its IGX Thor platform to power safety-capable onboard compute and autonomy-ready flight systems for Archer.
Starlink will deliver high-speed, low-latency LEO satellite connectivity for Midnight aircraft.
Partner Palantir was recently downselected as a finalist for the FAA's SMART AI project, which is instrumental to the DOT’s ~$20B air traffic control modernization effort.
Archer ended Q1 2026 with approximately $1.8 billion in liquidity and limited debt exposure.
Q1 2026 marked consistent financial execution, with Archer meeting its Adjusted EBITDA loss guidance for the quarter.
Archer ended Q1 2026 with $1,775.9 million of cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments on its balance sheet, plus an additional $7.3 million of restricted cash.

Q1 2026 cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments decreased by $188.8 million from Q4 2025, primarily due to $149.1 million used in operating activities and $32.6 million used for property and equipment purchases.
Q1 2026 revenue increased by $1.3 million from Q4 2025 to $1.6 million as operations expanded at Hawthorne Airport in LA.

Q1 2026 operating expenses increased by $21.5 million from Q4 2025 due to continued investment in flight testing, certification efforts, production activities for Midnight aircraft, and design/development of its hybrid aircraft.

Q1 2026 net loss increased by $28.8 million from Q4 2025, driven by a $21.5 million increase in operating expenses, a $7.3 million decrease in non-cash gain within other income, and a $1.2 million decrease in interest income, offset by a $1.3 million revenue increase.
Q1 2026 Adjusted EBITDA was a loss of $172.5 million, within the guidance range of $160 million to $180 million, representing a planned increase of $34.6 million over Q4 2025.
Archer’s financial estimates for Q2 2026 project an Adjusted EBITDA loss of $170 million to $200 million.
Archer builds aircraft and core technologies that will define the next era of flight for aerospace and defense.
source_title: Archer Announces First Quarter 2026 Results, Highlighting Record FAA Certification Progress With Initial US Operations Expected In 2026

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Aurubis will invest $340 million (€300 million) in a state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta.
The new facility will be the very first of its kind in the U.S.
The facility will create 125 jobs in the Augusta area.
Governor Kemp stated this marks the single-largest investment made by a German company in the State of Georgia.
Governor Kemp cited Georgia's ports system, highly-skilled workforce, and pro-business environment as factors that attracted the opportunity.
Aurubis will join more than 500 German companies with operations in Georgia.
Aurubis’ commitment to sustainable development and raw material security will play a role in building Georgia’s electric mobility value chain.
From Augusta, the company plans to produce materials found in lithium-ion batteries and other electronics.
CEO Roland Harings stated the investment sets an example of sustainable growth and positions Aurubis as a forerunner for multi-metal recycling in the U.S., matching its role in Europe.
Aurubis will build its facility on 150 acres at Augusta Corporate Park.
The site is a “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified” location.
Operations are expected to begin by the first half of 2024.
The company plans to begin hiring for positions as soon as summer 2022.
Steven Kendrick, Chairman of the Development Authority of Augusta, welcomed Aurubis as a new corporate tenant and industry leader building a world-class advanced manufacturing facility.
Kendrick noted the Augusta Corporate Park is proud to host the company and that the Augusta Economic Development Authority stands ready to support its success over the next several decades.
Griff Lynch, Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority (GPA), stated the GPA will help bring jobs and investment to Georgia.

Lynch noted exports from the Aurubis recycling operation will strengthen the state’s global position via Savannah, which is already one of the nation’s most balanced ports in terms of import-export trade.
Fernanda Kirchner, Senior Project Manager for GDEcD’s Global Commerce division, represented the department on this project.
The project involved partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Power.
Sérgio Domingues, Managing Director for the State of Georgia’s Europe Office, played a supporting role in attracting Aurubis to Georgia.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson celebrated the largest single investment by a German company in Georgia.
Wilson highlighted that Georgia is home to more than 500 German companies and that the longstanding relationship with Germany helps create jobs for green manufacturing.
Augusta Corporate Park completed due diligence and specific certification requirements prior to prospective business visits as a GRAD Certified park.
Receiving GRAD certification is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to catalyze economic growth and industrial development.
Thousands of Georgia jobs have been created on GRAD sites since the program’s inception in 2008.
Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
The company processes complex metal concentrates, scrap metals, organic and inorganic metal-bearing recycling materials, and industrial residues into high-quality metals.
Aurubis produces more than 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually.
From copper cathodes, it makes wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, and flat rolled products made of copper and copper alloys.
The company also produces precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, and zinc.
The portfolio includes additional products such as sulfuric acid and iron silicate.
Carter Chapman serves as Press Secretary for the Office of Governor Brian P. Kemp.
Annalise Morning serves as Deputy Press Secretary for the Office of Governor Brian P. Kemp.
Jessica Atwell serves as Communications Manager for the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
source_title: Aurubis to Open Recycling and Copper Smelting Facility in Augusta | Georgia
