Kenworth T880E is an electric-only truck with a range of 250 miles.
Kenworth T880E battery capacity ranges from 250 to 625 kWh.
Kenworth T880E uses an automatic transmission.
Kenworth T880E power sources include PACCAR ePowertrain 350 kW and PACCAR ePowertrain 450 kW.
According to the manufacturer, the Kenworth T880E has up to 82,000 GCWR and a 350 kW charge rate.
Mack Granite runs on LNG (Liquified Natural Gas) and CNG (Compressed Natural Gas).
Mack Granite uses an Allison transmission.
Mack Granite power source is the Cummins L9N 8.9L Near Zero engine.
Mack LR runs on LNG and CNG with an Allison 3000/4500 6-Speed transmission.
Mack LR power source is the Cummins L9N 8.9L Near Zero engine.
According to the manufacturer, the Mack LR is available in Front-, Side-, and Rear-Loader models.
Mack LR Electric has an electric-only range of 100 miles.
Mack LR Electric uses a Mack Powershift 2-speed transmission.
Mack LR Electric power source consists of two Mack AC motors providing 400 kW (536 hp) total peak power.
According to the manufacturer, the Mack LR Electric has a 66,000 GVWR.
The Mack LR Electric uses four NMC lithium-ion batteries at 600V.
The Mack LR Electric supports up to 150-kW DC Fast charging for approximately a 2-hour charge time.
Users can select up to 4 items to compare on the platform.

entity_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO)
canonical_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office
facts: AMMTO invested in five groundbreaking funding opportunities during its inaugural year.
AMMTO launched four prize competitions during its inaugural year.
AMMTO renewed three Clean Energy Manufacturing Innovation Institutes and the Critical Materials Innovation Hub during its inaugural year.
The Next Generation Materials and Processes team, led by Program Manager Huijuan Dai, invested in 14 projects across 10 states to receive a total of $45 million in federal funding.

AMMTO allocated $15 million to a team led by GE Research for developing, building, and refining manufacturing practices of large-scale machinery to manufacture wind and hydropower technology using 3D printing.

AMMTO allocated a combined $30 million for 13 projects committed to advancing innovative practices that will reshape the design, materials, and sustainability of manufacturing for wind turbine equipment.
AMMTO renewed the Clean Energy Smart Manufacturing Innovation Institute (CESMII) for an initial $6 million with potential additional funding across four subsequent fiscal years.
CESMII aims to advance widespread adoption of smart manufacturing to accelerate domestic competitiveness in clean energy manufacturing and the nation’s transition to a net-zero carbon economy.
AMMTO renewed the Institute for Advanced Composites Manufacturing Innovation (IACMI) for an initial $6 million and potential funding across four subsequent fiscal years.
IACMI aims to further technological R&D and accelerate commercialization in the domestic composites manufacturing sector.
AMMTO announced a $4.7 million request for proposals through the Cybersecurity Manufacturing Innovation Institute (CyManII) to fortify the cybersecurity landscape within American manufacturing.

The Energy Technology and Manufacturing and Workforce team, led by Acting Program Manager Tina Kaarsberg, renewed DOE’s first Clean Energy Manufacturing Innovation Institute, PowerAmerica, for an initial $8 million, with potential funding across four subsequent fiscal years.
PowerAmerica aims to advance domestic manufacturing of next-generation wide bandgap semiconductors for power electronics to aid economy-wide decarbonization and electrification.
AMMTO selected eight projects for a total of $2 million to drive innovation in lithium-ion battery rejuvenation, recycling, and reuse.
AMMTO awarded eight $75,000 prizes through Phase I of the Microbattery Design Prize to accelerate commercialization of innovative microbattery designs for clean energy manufacturing.
AMMTO secured 61 pledges across leading private industry, national labs, and academia to double semiconductor energy efficiency every two years.

This effort aims to increase economic competitiveness of American semiconductor manufacturers and strengthen domestic clean energy supply chains through the Energy Efficiency Scaling for 2 Decades (EES2) national initiative.
AMMTO is awarding funds through the Community Energy Innovation Prize, soliciting submissions for the prize’s Manufacturing Ecosystem Track.
AMMTO announced a 2023 cohort of 21 Lab-Embedded Entrepreneurship Program (LEEP) innovators to develop next-generation technologies for a clean energy future.
AMMTO opened applications for the coming year’s LEEP cohort.

The new LEEP cohort will spend two years embedded within four DOE national energy labs as fellows: West Gate at NREL, Cyclotron Road at LBNL, Chain Reaction Innovations at ANL, or Innovation Crossroads at ORNL.

Program Manager Kate Peretti and the Secure and Sustainable Materials team published a historic DOE Critical Materials Assessment and corresponding list of Critical Materials to inform government-wide approach to clean energy technology elements, including research into alternatives, substitutes, and recycling.
AMMTO launched a Critical Materials Collaborative to integrate critical materials RD&D across DOE, government, industry, and academia to accelerate domestic critical material supply chains.
AMMTO announced a FOA to fund up to $10 million to establish a Critical Materials Accelerator for early-stage science and technology solutions research toward commercialization.
These critical minerals and materials solutions will help the manufacturing sector deliver clean energy technologies needed to reach DOE and Biden-Harris Administration net-zero goals.

AMMTO rebranded and renewed the Critical Materials Innovation Hub (formerly the Critical Materials Institute) for $30 million in the first year with potential additional funding across subsequent four fiscal years.

The Critical Materials Innovation Hub aims to accelerate innovative scientific and technological solutions to develop resilient and secure supply chains for materials critical to clean energy technologies.

AMMTO launched the Re-X Before Recycling clean energy prize, which will award a total of $5.6 million to teams developing ways to extend product or parts lifetimes via re-using, repairing, refurbishing, remanufacturing, or repurposing before recycling.
AMMTO plans to launch a series of strategies and roadmaps in 2024 to lay out materials and manufacturing advances guiding the clean energy future.
source_title: Advancing Manufacturing for Our Clean Energy Future: 2023 in Review | Department of Energy

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on September 6, 2023 that DAS Corp. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The new facility will create 300 new jobs and generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
This initiative will also involve collaborative indirect investments.
DAS was established in Korea in 1987.
DAS specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.

Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS, stated the strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in the decision-making process.
Metter was deemed an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub thanks to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
The facility will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will be recruiting for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.

Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated the City of Metter is proud to welcome DAS and that the impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving socioeconomic wellbeing.
Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing Candler County as its new home and noted they will provide a boost to spur more economic development.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the authority is proud to support the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network as a result of years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.
Since 2018, Georgia has pursued its entire EV supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
DAS Corp. garnered a total revenue of approximately $900 million USD during the fiscal year of 2022.
Strategic collaborations with Hyundai Motor Group and Kia Motors continue to drive progress within the automotive industry for DAS.

source_title: DAS Corp. to Bring 300 Automotive Jobs to Metter | Georgia

facts: U.S Department of Energy’s Office of Nuclear Energy announced an ambitious new initiative to secure the nation’s nuclear fuel supply chain.

The initiative operates through the Defense Production Act (DPA) Nuclear Fuel Cycle Consortium, where the federal government will work with the domestic nuclear industry to ensure enough nuclear fuel for current reactors and future advanced reactors.
The Consortium is comprised of representatives from more than 90 companies spanning the nuclear industrial base.
The initiative addresses all facets of the nuclear fuel supply chain including milling, conversion, enrichment, deconversion, fabrication, recycling, and reprocessing.
The announcement took place at the Ronald Reagan Building and International Trade Center with partners and the public present.
Under the “Nuclear Dominance — 3 by 33” campaign, the Consortium aims to achieve three goals by 2033.
The first goal is to catalyze a secure and cost-competitive domestic fuel supply chain.
The second goal is to accelerate advanced reactor deployment and close the fuel cycle.
The third goal is to explore how the DPA framework can be activated to grow and align workforce, finance, innovation and collaboration in support of nuclear build out.
The initiative will begin a series of 60-day sprints designed to make rapid progress on the Consortium’s goals.

Assistant Secretary of Nuclear Energy Ted Garrish stated the work comes at a pivotal time for nuclear energy growth and expressed pleasure with the Committee's dedication, looking forward to rapid progress on near-term goals for a robust American-made supply of nuclear fuel.

The country’s need for secure and reliable energy is projected to rise in coming years, driven by growth in industrial manufacturing and power needs of data centers supporting artificial intelligence.
Nuclear energy currently provides the country with nearly 20 percent of its power.
Long-term success of efforts to put more power on the grid through nuclear plant uprates, restarts, and commercial deployment of advanced reactors depends on nuclear fuel availability.
In May of 2025, President Trump released four executive orders to catalyze a nuclear energy resurgence.

Within months, DOE announced the creation of the DPA Consortium to seek voluntary agreements with U.S. companies focused on increasing fuel availability, providing more access to reliable power, and ending reliance on foreign sources of enriched uranium and critical materials.

links.context: DPA Nuclear Fuel Cycle Consortium website

source_title: Department of Energy’s Defense Production Act Consortium Unveils New Initiative to Grow Nation’s Nuclear Fuel Cycle | Department of Energy

entity_name: DOE AMMTO-BTO and OE FY22 Multi-Topic FOA
canonical_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO) FY22 Multi-Topic Funding Program
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE) selected 31 projects led by national laboratories, industry, and academia.
More than $61 million in federal funding is available for the selectees to drive innovation and progress next generation materials and manufacturing related energy technologies.
The initiative aims to strengthen America’s economic competitiveness.
The FOA selections seek to advance development of next generation structural and functional materials for energy technology.
The selections also seek field validation and pilot demonstration of technologies for materials and manufacturing processes.
Award and cost share amounts are subject to change pending negotiations.
Topic 1: Next Generation Materials and Manufacturing includes 20 projects with an award amount of $27.6 million.

Topic 1 focuses on cost-effective manufacturing processes and development of novel materials with improved properties such as high strength, enhanced conductivity, or high performance under extreme conditions.
Topic 2: Secure and Sustainable Materials includes 4 projects with an award amount of $10.8 million.

Topic 2 focuses on materials RD&D to support the establishment of reuse and recycling systems with an emphasis on material and product design, recycling technology development, and reverse supply chain logistics.
Topic 3: Energy Technology Manufacturing includes 7 projects with an award amount of $22.6 million.
Topic 3 focuses on energy technology manufacturing innovation to improve performance and address technical barriers.
source_title: Funding Selections: AMMTO-BTO and OE FY22 Multi-Topic FOA | Department of Energy

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation announced a Notice of Funding Opportunity for up to $500 million to expand U.S. critical mineral and materials processing and derivative battery manufacturing and recycling.
Funding will expand domestic manufacturing of battery supply chains for defense, grid resilience, transportation, manufacturing and other industries.

Assistant Secretary of Energy (EERE) Audrey Robertson is currently in Japan meeting with regional allies at the Indo-Pacific Energy Security Ministerial and Business Forum to advance shared efforts on supply chain resilience and energy security issues.
Her engagements at the forum underscore the importance of close cooperation with partners as the United States strengthens its supply chain through this NOFO.

U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that for too long, the United States has relied on hostile foreign actors to supply and process the critical materials that are essential in battery manufacturing and materials processing.

Chris Wright credited President Trump’s leadership for the Department of Energy playing a leading role in strengthening domestic industries that will position the U.S. to win the AI race, meet rising energy demand, and achieve energy dominance.
Audrey Robertson stated she is delighted to be in Japan meeting with allies, underscoring the important connection between critical materials and energy security.
Robertson noted that critical minerals processing is a vital component of the nation’s critical minerals supply base.
Boosting domestic production, including through recycling, will bolster national security and ensure the United States and its partners are prepared to meet the energy challenges of the 21st century.
Funding awarded through this NOFO will support demonstration and/or commercial facilities for processing, recycling, or utilizing for manufacturing of critical materials.

Eligible critical materials may include traditional battery minerals such as lithium, graphite, nickel, copper, aluminum, as well as other minerals that are contained within commercially available batteries.
This is the third round of funding issued through DOE’s Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing and Recycling programs.
DOE is seeking projects in Domestic Critical Minerals Processing from Raw Feedstocks to increase U.S. processing capacity for critical minerals and materials for use in advanced batteries.

DOE is seeking projects in Domestic Critical Materials Recycling to increase recovery of battery critical minerals through recycling of manufacturing scrap and/or off-specification or end-of-life batteries.
DOE is seeking projects in Domestic Battery Materials and Component Manufacturing to increase domestic manufacturing capacity for strategic battery materials, components and technologies.
A webinar with additional information on this funding opportunity will be held at 1:00 PM ET on March 26, 2026.
Non-binding letters of intent are requested by 5:00 p.m. ET on March 27, 2026, to assist the Department in planning for the review process.
Applications must be submitted by 5:00 p.m. ET on April 24, 2026.
source_title: Energy Department Announces $500 Million to Strengthen Domestic Critical Materials Processing and Manufacturing | Department of Energy

This new commitment represents an additional USD 5 billion on top of a USD 21 billion allocation announced in March 2025.
The investment is dedicated to advancing strategic initiatives in automotive, steel, and robotics.
A new steel mill will be built in Louisiana to support strategic U.S. industries such as automotive manufacturing, strengthening local supply chains and industrial resilience.

The Group will establish a state-of-the-art robotics facility with an annual capacity of 30,000 units in the U.S., serving as a hub for design, manufacturing, testing, and deployment.

The Group is accelerating technology commercialization through its U.S.-based tech affiliates Boston Dynamics for robotics and Motional for autonomous driving.

facts: Georgia’s GDP exceeded 100 billion GEL in 2025 for the first time in its history.
Prime Minister Irakli Kobakhidze presented economic results showing a 7.5% growth rate in 2025.
The average economic growth rate over the past five years reached around 9.3%.
Economic growth stood at 8.2% in January–April 2026.
Georgia’s GDP was below 28 billion GEL in 2012 and had not reached 50 billion GEL in 2020.
Consumer prices in Georgia increased by 6.4% over the past three years.
Food and non-alcoholic beverage prices rose by 9.7% within the inflation structure.
Average salary increased by 47.9%, significantly outpacing price dynamics.
Goods exports increased by 11.2% in 2025, reaching a record $7.3 billion.
Foreign direct investment rose by 7.6% to around $1.7 billion.
The Ministry of Finance placed $500 million in Eurobonds with demand exceeding supply by 5.5 times.
Treasury bonds worth 400 million GEL were issued with demand exceeding supply by 4.5 times.
The government created an Efficiency Department to review state programs and procurement.
In 2025, the Efficiency Department reviewed procurement worth 115 million GEL, generating over 67 million GEL in savings.
An additional 67 million GEL in savings was achieved in 2026 through efficiency reforms.
Energy sector reforms eliminated the practice of reselling grid connection permits and introduced a 10-year development plan.
Optimization of state-owned energy companies reduced annual costs by nearly 25 million GEL.
The railway system cut around 1,100 positions and brought outsourced processes in-house, generating over 40 million GEL in annual savings.
Railway procurement optimization added 190.4 million GEL in savings.
Georgian Railway profit increased from 68 million to 145 million GEL, with operating margin rising from 25% to 36%.
Aviation procurement optimization resulted in 70 million GEL in savings and expanded international agreements.
Tbilisi Airport capacity increased by 53%, leading to growth in state revenues.
The IMF forecasts Georgia among the highest-growth economies in Europe and the region for 2026–2031.
Georgia maintains leading positions in international rankings on budget transparency.
Analysts note that accelerating GDP, moderate inflation, and declining debt burden create space for long-term planning.
Government programs set the development pace for transport, energy, construction, and logistics sectors.
Export growth and rising FDI indicate deeper integration into global value chains.
source_title: Georgia’s Economy Grows 7.5%: Investment Surge and Stronger Fiscal Stability

facts: Visteon Corporation announced an agreement to sell its 50 percent ownership stake in a Korean automotive interiors joint venture, Duckyang Industry Co., Ltd.
The sale supports Visteon's previously announced intentions to divest its interiors business.
Duckyang is based in Ulsan, South Korea.
Duckyang manufactures and develops original equipment interior components for vehicles.
The sale is to certain management shareholders of Duckyang and associated parties.
The sale price is the equivalent of about $24.1 million (25.9 billion Korean Won).
The transaction is expected to be completed in April after closing conditions are met.
In addition to the share sale proceeds, Visteon expects to receive approximately $5.9 million (6.3 billion Korean Won) in dividends.

Visteon president and CEO Timothy D. Leuliette stated they are pleased to have reached the agreement as another step in Visteon’s transformation and in support of their stated goal to exit their interiors business.
Leuliette noted they are working toward the interiors divestiture as part of a commitment to deliver customer and shareholder value by focusing on core growth climate and electronics businesses.
source_title: Visteon signs agreement to sell shares in Duckyang JV for $ 24.1 mn - Auto Components India

facts: Rivian is scaling production capacity to cut costs and support its lower-priced R2 strategy.
Federal policy changes and softer EV demand are shaping near-term planning.
Financial results show improving losses, but revenue mix pressures remain.
Rivian is set to increase initial production capacity at its Georgia plant by 50% to 300,000 units annually.
The automaker remains on track to begin vehicle production at the Georgia facility in late 2028.
Rivian is expanding capacity as it prepares to scale the R2, a more affordable mid-sized SUV.
The expanded Georgia footprint, combined with the existing 215,000-unit capacity at Normal, brings Rivian’s total planned manufacturing capacity to 515,000 units.
The company says this scale is needed to reach free cash flow positive.
To support the expansion, Rivian renegotiated its agreement with the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE), reducing its previously approved loan from $6.57 billion to $4.5 billion.
The original loan was structured to support two phases of production totaling 400,000 units.
The amended agreement covers one phase at 300,000 units.
Rivian expects to begin drawing on the funding in early 2027, a year ahead of the original schedule.
In the first quarter, Rivian reported a net loss of $416 million, or 33 cents per share, improving from a year-earlier loss of $541 million, or 48 cents per share.
Revenue rose 11% to $1.38 billion, exceeding analyst estimates of $1.36 billion.
Automotive revenue declined 2% to $908 million, driven by a $100 million decrease in regulatory credit sales.
Rivian produced 10,236 vehicles and delivered 10,365 vehicles during the quarter at its Normal, Illinois, facility.
The company maintained its full-year delivery forecast of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles.
Hyundai officially confirmed that its Metaplant's first production vehicle will be the 2025 IONIQ 5.
HMGMA is actively training and preparing its workforce to begin production in the fourth quarter of 2024.
The new facility will manufacture all trim lines of the IONIQ 5, with the exception of the IONIQ 5 N.
The Metaplant will eventually become the sole U.S. production site for the IONIQ 5 model.
Oscar Kwon, president and CEO of HMGMA, stated that the facility is in the final phase of construction and getting ready for the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5 to roll off the line.

The IONIQ 5 has received numerous awards this year, including Best Electric SUV, Best EV for the Money, One of the Best Cars for Families by U.S. News & World Report, and Best Electric Vehicle for the second consecutive year by Cars.com.
The IONIQ 5 previously won the 2022 World Car of the Year, 2023 MotorTrend SUV of the Year, and 2022 Car and Driver EV of the Year.
The IONIQ 5 earned the highest TOP SAFETY PICK+ award from the Insurance Institute For Highway Safety (IIHS).
The Hyundai IONIQ 5 remains Hyundai’s highest volume EV, with a 43% increase in sales in 2024 and an 82% surge in May alone.
Hyundai announced June incentives for the 2024 IONIQ 5, offering buyers a $7,500 cash rebate and lease customers a $7,500 federal tax credit.
Hyundai Motor Group is making a substantial investment of $7.59 billion to develop HMGMA, creating 8,500 direct jobs.
In partnership with SK On, Hyundai is constructing a $5 billion EV battery cell plant in Bartow County, Georgia, expected to employ 3,500 workers.
A recent study by the Center for Automotive Research estimates that Hyundai’s investments in Georgia will generate nearly 40,000 jobs and $4.6 billion in individual earnings annually.
Additional vehicles from Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia are planned for future production at the Metaplant.
source_title: Hyundai's Metaplant First Production Vehicle to Be 2025 IONIQ 5

facts: GF Casting Solutions will invest over $184 million in a new manufacturing facility in Augusta Corporate Park.
The new facility is slated to start operations in 2027.
The project will bring 350 additional jobs to Richmond County.
GF Casting Solutions is a division of Georg Fischer AG, headquartered in Schaffhausen, Switzerland.
The Swiss company produces aluminum, magnesium, iron, and super alloy parts for light vehicles, trucks, aerospace, energy, off-highway vehicles, and industrial applications.
The Augusta facility will be a LEED-certified, state-of-the-art High Pressure Die Casting Facility developing cast aluminum parts primarily for the automotive industry.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is proud to carry the title of the No. 1 state for business to companies across the globe.
Governor Brian Kemp welcomed GF Casting Solutions to the Peach State after meeting its team during a recent mission to Switzerland.
Carlos Vasto, President of GF Casting Solutions, called the project a very long-term investment that will complete the company's footprint to become truly global in the e-mobility market.
Carlos Vasto stated the company is looking forward to bringing leading technology to the US and creating advanced manufacturing jobs for several generations in Georgia.

Augusta Mayor Garnett Johnson noted that the city and Development Authority invested in water, sewer, roads, and workforce growth to establish the site as a premier industrial park in the Southeast United States.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that GF Casting Solutions was facilitated through Georgia's Europe Office connections with European companies.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson explained that European companies bring centuries of business experience combined with an intentional focus on innovation driving the market forward.
GF Casting Solutions will hire for manufacturing and automation positions at the Augusta facility over the next few years.
Since 2018, the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) has championed more than $27.3 billion of investments in the e-mobility industry.
The state aims to attract more investment to become the nation’s “capital of EV manufacturing.”

Companies with an existing e-mobility presence in Georgia include Kia, Hyundai Motor Group, Rivian, Caterpillar, SK Battery America, Yamaha Motor Manufacturing, Dongwon Tech, Duckyang America, Aspen Aerogels, Club Car, GEDIA, JCB, Heliox, Blue Bird Corporation, TEKLAS, and Textron.
source_title: Augusta Lands $184 Million Manufacturing Deal Bringing 350 New E-Mobility Jobs to Richmond County - Tennessee Star

Enchem America LLC is likely focused on scaling production capacity, enhancing supply chain resilience, and supporting the rapid growth of the electric vehicle and energy storage industries.

Enchem America LLC is making strategic efforts to expand its market presence and reach new customer segments.
The company is implementing advanced technologies to improve service delivery and customer experience.
Enchem America LLC is recognized for its expertise in producing high-performance electrolytes and additives essential for rechargeable battery technologies.
The company’s U.S. facility, located in Commerce, Georgia, is equipped to handle large-scale production and distribution, supporting both domestic and international clients.
Enchem America’s operations include shipping and receiving raw materials, chemical mixing, and rigorous safety protocols for hazardous materials.
As part of Enchem’s global network, the company leverages advanced R&D and manufacturing capabilities to serve the rapidly expanding electric vehicle and energy storage markets.
The company's U.S. facility is located in Commerce, Georgia, USA.
Enchem America LLC was founded in 2019.
Enchem America LLC is a private company.

facts: Enchem is a fast-moving Korean electrolyte leader establishing a localized strategy to dominate the battery electrolyte market amid trade policy disruptions and supply chain realignments.
The success of Korean conglomerates like Samsung, LG, and SK has provided growth momentum and core capabilities to domestic suppliers like Enchem.
Enchem has built competency to supply global giants like CATL and AESC through joint development with major Korean multinationals.
The implementation of the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) is gradually cutting Chinese competitors off from the American supply chain, creating a window of opportunity for Korean companies like Enchem.
Experts estimate electrolyte demand will reach around 2,864,000 tons by 2030, driven by pent up demand from batteries, electric vehicles (EVs), and energy storage systems (ESS).
Enchem anticipates electrolyte demand could exceed 10 million tons, potentially equaling a market size of 30 trillion KRW.
Chinese competitors are generally assumed to take 5-6 years from business plan to commercial production, with the earliest supply to customers appearing to be 2027.
Enchem stabilized its electrolyte production in North America since 2022 and is currently scaling up and enhancing its global capacity.
By 2025, Enchem will commence operations of electrolyte plants in Kentucky, Ontario, and Texas.
Enchem's primary goal is to preempt the U.S. and EU markets by 2027, achieving economies of scale and maximizing market share to gain a head start on Chinese contenders.
CEO Oh Jung-Kang stated that Enchem is focusing on targeting major battery companies in North America and Europe to achieve more than 50% market share in the global market.
Enchem's strategy involves opening production hubs within a 500-kilometer radius of clients' bases to maximize localization rates and dominate the market by 2025.
Enchem is securing price competitiveness and just-in-time delivery by internalizing its supply chain and minimizing supply distances between factories and clients.
Enchem is developing new technologies and products, including NMP recycling and CNT dispersion slurry, to distribute inventory and logistics costs across product categories.
Around 70% of electrolyte production costs currently come from raw material procurement.
Enchem has established joint ventures with globally leading solvent and lithium salt makers to internalize raw materials and reduce price fluctuations.
Enchem is establishing the Saemangeum plant to produce high-quality lithium salt, which will later be expanded to the U.S. mainland.
Enchem's operating margin in the electrolyte market is currently at 5%.
Internalizing lithium salt production can secure an additional 3 to 5% margin, while localizing raw materials and being within 500 kilometers of clients can save another 2% in operational costs.
Achieving economies of scale with one production site having a capacity of 100,000 tons (equivalent to about 1 trillion KRW) can lead to an additional 3% cost saving.
These measures aim to elevate Enchem's price competitiveness to around 15% from the current 5%.
Enchem expects to achieve 2.4 trillion KRW in revenue by 2026.
By the end of the year, Enchem will have orders from each member of the top 10 battery makers, increasing to the top 20 next year.
Enchem recently expanded its business portfolio into NMP recycling and CNT conductive slurry dispersion markets due to high entry barriers and growth potential.
Enchem collaborates with battery manufacturers to develop new additives tailored to specific requirements, conducting technological exchanges with the top 20 global battery makers.
Enchem's complete vertical integration spans from raw materials to electrolytes and additives, allowing it to provide one-stop solutions.
Enchem's stock price jumped from 60,000 KRW per share a year ago to a peak of more than 360,000 KRW in April 2024.
Since Q1 of this year, Enchem has been supplying electrolytes to Ultium Cells, Company “P”, and Company “T”.
The company operates a North American factory located in Georgia, USA.
Enchem plans to increase its U.S. production capacity to 650,000 tons, representing about 70% of the total U.S. capacity share.
Enchem is the only electrolyte company that will integrate raw material production with synthesis technology in the U.S.
The biggest risk for Enchem's North American business is market uncertainty regarding EV sales and domestic consumption, as EVs manufactured in the U.S. cannot be exported to China.
High interest rates have reduced the purchasing power of U.S. citizens, increasing market uncertainty.
U.S. consumers prefer higher-priced EVs ranging from 50 million KRW to over 100 million KRW, making the U.S. market five to ten times larger than the Chinese one in terms of potential size.
U.S. consumers favor high-quality, costly electrolytes and expect EV ranges of 600 to 1,000 km on a single charge.
Enchem has operational plants in Poland (established to supply LG) and Hungary (established to supply SK and Samsung SDI).
Enchem is considering establishing a factory in Spain in the future to strengthen European production capabilities.
In France, Enchem has local partnerships with ACC and Verkor.
Enchem considers collaborations with Chinese companies operating in Europe (AESC, CATL, EVE, Gotion) and explores partnerships with German and Swedish firms.
Enchem is revising its 10-year plan through 2035, aiming to evolve from a global electrolyte specialist to a comprehensive global chemical company.

The company plans to diversify into chemicals used in vehicles, semiconductors, and medicinal chemistry, as well as develop high molecular electrolytes for robots and solid-state or polymeric electrolytes.
source_title: The Worldfolio: ENCHEM: The Winning Plan to Dominate the Battery Electrolyte Market?

facts: Enchem America Inc. officials announced earlier this month the company will invest $152.5 million to establish electrolyte manufacturing operations in Brownsville.
The new facility is located at the I-40 Advantage Industrial Park, a Select Tennessee Certified Site.
The project will be Enchem’s second U.S. plant.
The project will create 190 new jobs in Tier 4, At-Risk Haywood County.
The Brownsville facility will operate similarly to Enchem’s U.S. headquarters in Commerce, Georgia.
The facility will manufacture electrolytes for electric vehicle (EV) batteries as well as other EV battery-related services.
Enchem America Inc. is a subsidiary of Enchem Co., Ltd., which is based in South Korea.
Enchem Co., Ltd. specializes in the development and manufacturing of electrolytes and other additives for rechargeable batteries.

Jung Kang Oh, CEO and founder of Enchem Co. Ltd., stated that the growth of the EV market in recent years has been truly remarkable, and all indications suggest this trend will continue at an accelerated pace over the coming decade.

Jung Kang Oh expressed confidence that Enchem is well positioned to further capitalize on the growth of the EV market and look forward to exploring new avenues of innovation and partnership opportunities with Haywood County and the City of Brownsville.
With the additional Tennessee operations, Enchem will be able to further support its EV partners across the Southeast.

TNECD Commissioner Stuart McWhorter stated that South Korea is one of Tennessee’s top 10 foreign direct investment partners, and they are proud that another global brand like Enchem has chosen to place its trust in their state.
Stuart McWhorter noted that Enchem is not only investing in one of Tennessee’s At-Risk counties, but the company is also boosting the EV supply chain across Tennessee.
source_title: Enchem America locating $152 million plant, 190 new jobs in Brownsville | Business | therogersvillereview.com

facts: South Korean battery chemicals manufacturer Enchem has abandoned its plans for a plant in the United States, according to a report by KED Global.
The company had planned to invest the equivalent of around 142 million US dollars in a production facility in Tennessee.
The plant would have manufactured electrolyte materials for BlueOval SK, a joint venture between SK On and Ford Motor.
According to the report, the project was canceled after the signing of the planned supply contracts was delayed several times.
KED Global reports that this decision is related to the ongoing weakness of the electric vehicle market in the United States.
Ford has recorded losses totaling approximately 12 billion dollars in its electric mobility division, including 2.2 billion dollars in the first half of 2025.
Additionally, the federal tax credit of up to 7,500 US dollars for electric car buyers expired in October 2025.
Due to these developments, BlueOval SK is not expected to utilize its full production capacity of 127 gigawatt-hours next year.
According to industry sources, companies in Fordʼs supply chain are currently revising their strategies.
Enchem now plans to focus on its existing sites in Georgia and Indiana, which supply electrolytes to LG Energy Solution and Samsung SDI, respectively.
source_title: Enchem Abandons Plans for Electrolyte Plant in USA - Battery-News

A listing ceremony for CATL took place in Hong Kong in May, where Robin Zeng shared a toast with guests.

AMPC is a system that provides tax credits for parts manufactured using advanced manufacturing technologies such as batteries, solar, and wind power in the United States.
Electrolytes produced at Enchem's Georgia plant are recognized by the U.S. Internal Revenue Service as "electrode active substances," an item subject to AMPC.
Enchem established a corporation in Commerce, Georgia, USA in 2019.
Enchem began supplying electrolytes to global customers in 2023.

Enchem expects to supply about 10,000 tons of electrolytes to a multinational battery manufacturer this year.
The multinational battery manufacturer's supply includes 7,500 tons of electrolytes supplied to China and 2,500 tons of electrolytes supplied to North America.

An Enchem official stated, "We have local response capabilities based on key production bases in China, North America, and Europe, so we are in an advantageous position to absorb the demand of customers seeking to diversify our supply chains."

entity_name: South Korean EV & Battery Investment in Tennessee & Georgia
canonical_name: South Korean Companies Investing in Tennessee & Georgia
facts: Michael Kofoed, an assistant professor of economics at The University of Tennessee Knoxville, stated that the relationship between South Korea and the U.S. will never be the same.
South Korea has opened an investigation into whether human rights violations were committed in the Sept. 4 raid at LG Energy Solution's joint battery plant with Hyundai Motor in Georgia.
The raid detained 475 workers, more than 300 of whom were South Korean nationals.
Korean employees at LG Energy Solution's Spring Hill, Tenn., operation left the U.S., citing growing concerns over visa status and legal uncertainty.
LG Energy Solution told the Tennessean that its plant is still operating as usual.
An LG Energy Solutions spokesperson stated the company remains committed to its U.S. projects and will continue to navigate circumstances with the aim to continue necessary investments and business.
The same spokesperson noted the U.S. is an important market and, in the mid- to long-term, plans to locate half of its total global battery cell-making capacity in North America.
South Korean President Lee Jae Myung stated at a Sept. 11 news conference that businesses will hesitate to make direct investments in the United States as things stand now.
Many Georgia detainees held Electronic System for Travel Authorization visa waivers or B-1 temporary business traveler visas.
Those visa documents prohibit foreign nationals from working in construction or equipment installation in the U.S.
The trade rift could impact Tennessee, where more than 20 South Korean companies have collectively poured nearly $6 billion into the state as of Oct. 2024.
The $6 billion investment figure comes from the Tennessee Department of Economic and Community Development.
Michael Kofoed stated that injecting fear and uncertainty around LG is equivalent to raising their taxes and has raised the cost of doing business in the United States.
U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem acknowledged the trade policy impact of the deportations.
Noem said the deportations signal policy consistency that could ultimately bolster investor confidence.
Much of South Korean investment in Tennessee revolves around auto industry second-tier suppliers supporting electric vehicle production for local OEMs like Ford and General Motors.
Wisarut Suwanprasert, Middle Tennessee State University Associate Professor of Economics, stated that Korean investment has created thousands of jobs.
Suwanprasert believes the dwindling Korean morale toward U.S. investment is a minor diplomatic issue.
Suwanprasert suggested the Trump administration should provide a formal apology to make things smoother.
Kofoed did not anticipate a U.S. apology nor suggest South Korea would try to restore the bond between the partners.
Kofoed stated that given the current trajectory, there is less willingness for foreign partners to do foreign direct investment in the U.S.
Amid shifting global dynamics and U.S. trade and immigration policies under President Donald Trump, countries like South Korea and Japan have increasingly explored economic ties with China.
Kofoed stated that because the U.S. is receding as a business partner, South Korea and Japan are being drawn into the Chinese sphere of influence.
Noem argued that tough actions mean there is no uncertainty about the Trump administration's policies.
Noem claimed investors prefer a stable, rule-bound environment where immigration laws are enforced evenly against allies' workers on grey zone visas to protect American jobs long-term.
Kofoed expects long-term trust problems between the U.S. and its foreign partners.
Kofoed described the situation as a conflict between trusting the U.S. for investment and feeling that trust was betrayed.
Ultium Cells is a joint venture between Korean-owned LG Energy Solutions and General Motors that manufactures low-cost electric vehicle battery cells known as lithium iron phosphate (LFP).
Ultium Cells announced a $2.3 billion investment in July, located onsite at the GM manufacturing plant in Spring Hill.

LG Chem is a global leading chemical company specializing in petrochemicals, advanced materials and life sciences, and is the parent company of LG Energy Solutions following a December 2020 diversification.
In Nov. 2022, LG Chem announced it would invest roughly $3.2 billion to build a cathode materials plant for electric vehicle batteries in Clarksville.
Dongwha Electrolyte USA is a subsidiary of Dongwha Enterprise Company that specializes in electrolytes used in lithium batteries.
In 2023, Dongwha Electrolyte USA announced a $70 million investment into creating a Clarksville production facility.

facts: Pratt & Whitney commits $200 million to add a seventh forging press in Columbus, Georgia.
The investment increases production for key commercial and military engines.
Pratt & Whitney is an RTX business.
An official company press release regarding the investment was issued on February 24, 2026.
The investment funds the addition of a seventh isothermal forging press at the Columbus Forge facility.
The expansion aims to increase production of critical engine components for commercial aircraft and military programs.
The new infrastructure aims to boost output of rotating compressor and turbine disks by 30%.
These components are essential for Geared Turbofan (GTF) engines used in commercial aviation.
These components are also essential for the F135 engine that powers the F-35 Lightning II fighter jet.
The company expects the new forging press to become operational by 2028.
The Columbus Forge facility plays a central role in Pratt & Whitney’s supply chain.
The facility produces high-performance parts that must withstand extreme temperatures and stresses.
Pratt & Whitney President Shane Eddy emphasized the strategic necessity of the expansion.

Shane Eddy stated: “This latest investment will increase output of critical parts for our growing military and commercial engine programs and underscores our commitment to supporting customers worldwide.”
The project is expected to initially create 15 new jobs.
Further hiring is anticipated as operations scale up.
Pratt & Whitney has invested more than $1 billion in its Columbus operations since 2008.
The Columbus campus currently employs approximately 2,600 workers.
RTX reports the facility recently completed a $70 million expansion of its GTF Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul (MRO) center.
The MRO expansion added 81,000 square feet to the facility.
The MRO expansion increased annual capacity by more than 25%.
Pratt & Whitney aims to streamline logistics by locating new manufacturing and aftermarket services on the same campus.
The expanded capabilities support the growing global fleet of GTF-powered aircraft and F-35 fighters.
source_title: Pratt & Whitney Invests $200M To Expand Georgia Manufacturing - AirPro News

facts: Rivian has increased the planned output of its upcoming Georgia manufacturing facility.
The company said its second U.S. plant will begin with an annual capacity of 300,000 units.
This represents a notable increase from the previously announced 200,000-unit capacity.
The update was shared alongside Rivian’s first-quarter earnings report.
The automaker revised its financing strategy to expect $4.5 billion in loans from the U.S. Department of Energy to support construction.
This loan expectation is down from an earlier estimate of $6.6 billion.
Rivian plans to begin drawing on those DOE funds sooner, starting in 2027.
Construction timelines remain unchanged, with production in Georgia still scheduled to begin by the end of 2028.
The facility will manufacture vehicles built on Rivian’s midsize platform.
Production will include the R2 crossover and a robotaxi variant tied to a 50,000-unit partnership with Uber.
Production of the R2 has already started at Rivian’s expanded plant in Normal, Illinois.
Customer deliveries from the Illinois facility are expected later this spring.
The Illinois facility currently has the capacity to produce up to 155,000 R2 vehicles annually.
With the addition of the Georgia plant, Rivian’s total production capacity is projected to reach 515,000 vehicles per year.
According to Chief Financial Officer Claire McDonough, that scale is expected to support the company’s path toward positive free cash flow.
In the first quarter, Rivian reported $1.38 billion in revenue and a net loss of $416 million.
The company is counting on the more affordable R2 lineup to deliver economies of scale that its higher-priced R1T pickup and R1S SUV have not yet achieved.
Looking ahead, Rivian expects continued growth driven by the R2, the upcoming R3, and future models.
Although the initial Georgia plan included two construction phases of 200,000 units each, the revised approach increases early capacity while still preserving room for future expansion at the site.

Rivian approached development of the R2 crossover with a clear objective to retain the appeal of the premium R1 lineup while significantly lowering both production costs and the eventual purchase price.
Based on early impressions including prototype evaluations, the company appears to have executed on that strategy.
According to Rivian, input costs for the R2 have been reduced by roughly 50% compared with the R1T and R1S.
That cost compression is expected to support a starting price around $45,000 over time.
The higher-spec R2 Performance variant will launch first at approximately $58,000 when deliveries begin in the near term.
Rivian provided deeper insight into the engineering decisions behind those savings in its latest earnings update.
A major example of cost reduction is the redesigned electrical architecture.
By adopting a more consolidated zonal computing system, the company reduced total wiring harness length by 2.3 miles and cut connector usage by 60%.
High-voltage cabling was also reduced by 70% after integrating multiple power conversion components into a single unit.
The new Maximus drive unit features 41% fewer parts than the Enduro units used in the R1 platform.
Rivian integrated the inverter directly into the drive unit, improving packaging and thermal management while reducing component count.
Additional cost reductions came from replacing ultrasonic sensors with corner radar systems, lowering sensing costs by about half.
Mechanical simplification played a major role in cost savings.
The front suspension switches from a double wishbone setup to a MacPherson strut design, delivering significant savings.
Large-scale die castings reduce underbody complexity by 90% in terms of part count.
Rear door assemblies have been simplified by cutting roughly 65% of components.
CEO RJ Scaringe said these changes reflect a broader focus on design for manufacturing and scaling efficiencies, enabling Rivian to deliver the R2 profitably at a more accessible price point.
The company also benefits from improved supplier leverage as it grows, reducing component costs such as the windshield, which is now roughly half the cost of the R1 equivalent.
Combined with its smaller size and lower material requirements, the R2 represents Rivian’s push toward true mass-market scale.
The remaining question for the project is execution as the vehicle moves into full production.
source_title: Rivian Boosts GA Plant Capacity To 300,000 Units, Reveals R2 Costs 50% Less to Build Than R1 - EVinfo.net

facts: Bryan County, Georgia, is emerging as a critical hub for the future of U.S. auto manufacturing.
Hyundai Motor Group has invested $7.6 billion into its Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The facility is a nearly 3,000-acre site located outside Savannah.
This is Hyundai’s first dedicated EV and battery plant in the U.S.
Initial capacity is 300,000 vehicles annually with the ability to scale to 500,000.
The plant uses advanced robotics and real-time production systems to improve quality and flexibility.
On-site battery production is handled through a joint venture with LG Energy Solution.
Hyundai expects to create more than 8,500 direct jobs.
Nearly 7,000 additional roles tied to suppliers and logistics are expected.
Hyundai entered the U.S. market in 1986 and has steadily expanded domestic production since then.
About 80 percent of Hyundai’s U.S. lineup is now built in America.
The MetaPlant will eventually support multiple brands, including Kia and Genesis.
The Hyundai Mobility Training Center provides hands-on training for technicians and engineers in EV systems, diagnostics, and advanced vehicle technologies.
The plant is already producing the 2026 Hyundai IONIQ 5.
The 2026 Hyundai IONIQ 5 offers up to 318 miles of range and access to the Tesla Supercharger network through its NACS port.
The plant will also produce the 2026 Hyundai IONIQ 9, a three-row electric SUV with up to 335 miles of range.
The 2026 Hyundai IONIQ 9 features ultra-fast charging capability from 10 to 80 percent in about 24 minutes.
Hyundai plans to expand production to include hybrid models and hydrogen fuel cell trucks.
Hyundai is investing in autonomy through partnerships with Waymo and Motional, with additional support from Magna International and deployment through Uber.
Rivian secured a $6.6 billion loan from the US Department of Energy (DOE).
The financing is part of the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing programme (ATVM).
The loan is earmarked for constructing a 9 million sq. ft manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, near Social Circle, Georgia.
The plant will focus on producing Rivian’s all-electric midsize platform, starting with the R2 SUV and R3 crossover.
When fully operational, the facility is expected to manufacture up to 400,000 vehicles annually.
Rivian founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated: “This additional capacity for our mass market products is key to US leadership in the electric vehicle industry.”
Georgia has aggressively pursued automotive investments with attractive tax incentives, workforce training programmes, and robust infrastructure.
The Stanton Springs site is located less than an hour from Atlanta.
The project is set to generate 7,500 direct jobs by 2030, along with thousands of indirect roles.

The project’s ripple effect is expected to boost the local economy while reinforcing the Biden-Harris administration’s focus on clean energy initiatives, all hinging, however, on the status of the controversial decisions expected to be taken by the Trump team.
Rivian is collaborating with Georgia’s QuickStart technical training programme to prepare a skilled EV-capable and digitally-ready workforce.
Twenty local fellows have already commenced training at Rivian’s Illinois facility and will return to Georgia upon completion.
A DOE spokesperson stated: “This investment will provide thousands of jobs and strengthen America’s leadership in clean transportation.”
The DOE’s ATVM programme previously aided Tesla in 2009.
The loan’s dual-tranche structure allows Rivian to draw $3.35 billion during the first phase of construction.
Rivian can draw $2.62 billion in the second tranche.
The facility will incorporate advanced automation and renewable energy, setting a benchmark for sustainable manufacturing.
The investment aligns with the Justice40 Initiative, which ensures 40% of certain climate-related federal investments benefit disadvantaged communities.
Rivian plans to hire at least a quarter of its employees from local communities.
The new facility will feature an R&D wing dedicated to advancing battery technology and refining manufacturing processes.
Rivian delayed construction on its Georgia facility in 2024 initially to cut costs, a move now viewed as a strategic recalibration.
Supply chain disruptions, inflation, and regulatory hurdles could complicate timelines.
The first R2 SUVs are expected to roll off the line in 2028, two years after construction begins in 2026.
The EVs manufactured in Georgia will reportedly save 146 million gallons of petroleum annually.
source_title: Rivian Gets $6.6B DOE Loan for New Georgia EV Factory | Automotive Manufacturing Solutions

CEO Jason Moss stated that Georgia manufacturers are positioned well for growth in a post-COVID-19 landscape and that strong relationships created by the organization are helping businesses emerge from shutdowns.
Since its founding in 2008, GMA has partnered with companies throughout Georgia to strengthen relationships and partnerships between manufacturers and suppliers.
The August 4 re-launch and membership drive was sponsored by Taylor English in Atlanta.
The new membership drive aims to bring manufacturing leaders together to strengthen ramp-up efforts as facilities reopen and employees return to the workplace.
The drive for new members continues through the end of 2022 as more companies ramp up to full-production schedules.
GMA provides monthly plant tours, educational sessions, tradeshows, and networking opportunities designed to help make profitable business connections for its members.
Additional resources produced by GMA include the Georgia Manufacturing Directory, Georgia Manufacturing Summit, Georgia Manufacturing Calendar, and Georgia Manufacturing Job Board.

facts: Jason Moss is the founder and CEO of the Georgia Manufacturing Alliance (GMA).
GMA is a membership-based organization that supports Georgia’s manufacturing community.
Moss founded GMA in 2008 to help grow his market while selling software to manufacturers, after realizing there was no existing place for manufacturers to network.
Before the COVID pandemic, GMA hosted monthly meetings and approximately 120 live events annually, culminating in the annual Georgia Manufacturing Summit.
Post-COVID, GMA has adopted a more selective approach and expects to host 50 or 55 events in the coming year.
Unlike most non-profit associations focused on lobbying or education, GMA operates as a for-profit industry association.
The organization's sole agenda is supporting initiatives that benefit manufacturing and Georgia, focusing on factory tours, networking events, and educational sessions rather than lobbying.
AI is identified as a major workforce challenge and trend within Georgia manufacturing.
GMA has partnered with Georgia AIM to integrate AI into advanced manufacturing, utilizing a massive grant to host events that provide AI access and education, particularly in rural Georgia.
Automation and robotics are noted as the primary direction for the industry's future.
GMA acts as a resource for new manufacturing entering the state by helping manufacturers identify vetted backups for their supply chains or service providers.
The alliance connects manufacturers with suppliers ranging from construction firms to component providers, facilitating mutual business opportunities.
Solidifying professional networks and supply chains, ensuring redundancies, and maintaining open communication are highlighted as the biggest current trends in manufacturing.
Confidence in suppliers is described as critical for manufacturers, alongside the necessity of support and redundancy.
source_title: Catching up with... Jason Moss - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: DAS Corp plans to establish a new Georgia manufacturing facility for automotive parts creating 300 new jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp. (DAS), an automotive parts company, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The facility will create 300 new jobs and generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
This initiative will also involve collaborative indirect investments.

Suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year, alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth, according to Governor Brian Kemp.

Georgia's logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners in all corners of the state and to markets across the world, according to Governor Brian Kemp.
DAS was established in Korea in 1987.
DAS specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.

The strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in the decision-making process, according to Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS.

Metter was deemed an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub thanks to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, according to Sean Kim.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
The facility will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will be recruiting for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.
The City of Metter is proud to welcome DAS to Metter and Candler County, according to Metter Mayor Ed Boyd.

The impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving the social economic wellbeing of citizens, according to Metter Mayor Ed Boyd.
On behalf of the citizens of Candler County, thanks are extended to DAS for choosing them as their new home, according to Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners.
DAS will provide a boost to spur more economic development in Candler County, according to Glyn Thrift.

Georgia Ports Authority supports the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state to drive economic activity and jobs, according to Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network as a result of years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity, according to GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson.
DAS is another exciting addition to Georgia’s automotive community, according to GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
DAS Corp. maintains a strong focus on innovation and quality, influencing all aspects of the business to consistently provide premier automotive seating solutions tailored to evolving customer needs.
Strategic collaborations, including partnerships with Hyundai Motor Group and Kia Motors, continue to drive progress within the constantly evolving automotive industry.
DAS Corp. garnered a total revenue of approximately $900 million USD during the fiscal year of 2022.
source_title: Auto parts manufacturer to establish Georgia facility

facts: GSC Steel Stamping, LLC is a minority and family-owned and operated automotive parts manufacturer.
The company provides Body in White frame assembly brackets to some of the world’s leading OEMs and tier one suppliers.
GSC Steel Stamping offers competitively priced metal stamping, engineering, welded assemblies, and tooling services.
In June 2023, GSC acquired Dixien, LLC’s assets.
The acquisition continues a 43-year history of automotive parts stamping at the company's Lake City, GA location.
The facility is located at 5286 Circle Drive, Lake City, Georgia, 30260, United States.
The Lake City, GA manufacturing site spans 200,000 sq. ft.
CEO Vincent Henderson purchased and led the turnaround of two award-winning automotive stamping operations in Michigan prior to GSC Steel Stamping.
Those Michigan operations were regularly recognized by customers for excellence in overall performance, quality, and on-time delivery.
The plant operations management team possesses over 200 years of combined stamping experience.
GSC continuously re-evaluates processes, invests in advanced technology, and hires staff to maintain excellence in overall performance, quality, and on-time delivery.
The company has between 100 and 249 employees.

facts: Clayton County has access to 19 exits and four major interstates (I-75, I-85, I-285 and I-675), providing logistical benefits for businesses.

Automotive parts producer GSC Steel Stamping added 75 jobs following a $150,000 million investment to acquire the assets of parts manufacturer Dixien.
GSC Steel Stamping operates a 200,000-square-foot facility in Lake City that it claims is the largest minority-owned automotive stamping plant globally.
TOTO invested $238 million and added more than 300 skilled workers to its production floor in Morrow.
Clayton County serves as a pipeline from Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport to the Georgia Ports Authority for moving products.

Victory Landing Logistics Center is a 90-acre, five-building industrial site just off I-285, representing a $400 million project offering 700,000 square feet of warehouse space with an additional 335,000 square feet under construction.
Construction on MARTA’s bus rapid-transit expansion in Clayton County is expected to begin this year to improve metro area mobility.

Clayton State University's Launchpad Leadership Academy provides four-year scholarships, pairs students with corporate partners for hands-on learning experiences, and certifies completers as workforce ready.
Workforce-friendly majors at Clayton State University include cyber technology and information technology, which are popular among incoming students.

BlueStar Studios cited state tax credits for film businesses locating to Georgia as an incentive for its location decision.
The Urban Redevelopment Authority facilitated the sale of the former Fort Gillem land to BlueStar Studios along with a 10-year property tax abatement.

facts: In February 2025, ESI Group introduced BM-Stamp, an advanced stamping simulation tool designed to simplify and accelerate the metal stamping process.

The BM-Stamp tool enables engineers at all expertise levels to conduct accurate, predictive simulations for standard and high-strength materials such as steel and aluminum without requiring specialized knowledge of FEM or meshing.
This innovation enhances production efficiency and reduces errors, supporting automotive manufacturers in cutting costs and shortening development cycles.
The tool is expected to positively influence demand for precision-stamped components across the market.
In January 2024, GSC Steel Stamping LLC announced the successful acquisition of Dixien, LLC's assets, strengthening its leadership in the sector.
GSC Steel Stamping has 43 years of experience in stamping automobile components.

In November 2023, Generational Growth Capital, a Milwaukee-based equity firm, acquired Federal Tool & Engineering, BP Metals, and Rockford Specialties, three U.S.-based metal stamping and structural steel manufacturers.
This strategic consolidation expands the new entity's manufacturing capacity and strengthens its logistics network to ensure consistent delivery to customers.
The acquisition is set to boost production efficiency and meet growing demand for high-quality stamped components across automotive, construction, and industrial sectors in North America.
In October 2023, Ryerson acquired Norlen Inc., a Wisconsin-based metal stamping fabricator serving the agricultural and defense industries.
This acquisition enhances Ryerson's product portfolio and strengthens its capabilities in delivering precision-stamped components for critical sectors.
The expansion is expected to drive growth opportunities within niche market segments and reinforce supply chains for complex, high-performance stamped parts.
In June 2023, General Motors announced a USD 500 million investment in its Arlington, Texas assembly plant to support production of its next-generation SUVs.
The GM investment includes the purchase of advanced metal stamping equipment and upgrades to the body shop.
This move highlights GM's focus on increasing production efficiency and vehicle quality.

The investment is expected to drive demand for high-precision stamped components, reinforcing the role of advanced stamping technologies in meeting evolving automotive design and performance requirements.
source_title: Comprehensive Global Stamped Component  Trends, Growth, Demand & Performance Analysis | 2024 Insights

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) is a department of the State of Georgia headquartered at 75 Fifth Street, N.W., Suite 1200, Atlanta, Georgia.
The agency was originally named the Georgia Department of Commerce and established by law in 1949.
Governor Herman Talmadge appointed the first five-member board under Official Code of Georgia Annotated sections 50-7-1 through 50-7-41.
George C. Gaines served as the first commissioner.
Pat Wilson was appointed Commissioner of GDEcD by Governor Nathan Deal in November 2016.

As Commissioner, Pat Wilson leads the state agency responsible for creating jobs and investment through business recruitment and expansion, international trade and tourism, and arts/film/music industries.
Christopher M. Carr served as Commissioner from November 2013 until his appointment as state Attorney General in November 2016.

GDEcD plans, manages, and mobilizes state resources to attract new business investment, drive industry expansion, locate markets for Georgia products, and promote the state for arts and entertainment projects.

The department is divided into seven distinct divisions: Global Commerce, International Trade, Workforce, Georgia Centers of Innovation, Tourism, Film/Music & Digital Entertainment, and Georgia Council for the Arts, supported by Administration and Marketing/Communications divisions.
The Global Commerce Division assists businesses with site location, employee training, market research, and export assistance.
In FY2012, the Global Commerce division led to 403 announced projects and nearly $6B in investment.
GDEcD supports small businesses (which make up 97% of all Georgia businesses) through direct company relationships, resource awareness, B-2-B opportunities, and local-level business development.
The International Trade Division maintains representation in 11 markets including Brazil, Canada, Chile, China, Colombia, Germany, Japan, Korea, Mexico, Israel, and the United Kingdom.
The Centers of Innovation (COI) program accelerates technology-based companies through six centers focused on agribusiness, aerospace, energy, life sciences, logistics, and manufacturing.
COI centers are located in Atlanta, Augusta, Gainesville, Savannah, Tifton, and Eastman.

Tangible services for COI include access to university-level research and development, product commercialization, industry-specific business counsel, matching research grants for qualified companies, networking opportunities, and connections to potential investor networks.
Georgia Allies was formed in 1997 as a partnership between state government and private corporations.

On May 12, 2008, Governor Sonny Perdue signed the Georgia Entertainment Industry Investment Act, increasing the state tax credit for qualified production and post-production expenditures by as much as 30%.
The tax credit is available to traditional motion picture projects as well as new industries such as game development and animation.

During FY2012, GDEcD led more than 100 feature films and television series produced in Georgia, ranking the state number one in the Southeast and among the top five states nationally for film and TV productions.
The Film, Music & Digital Entertainment Division has helped secure Georgia as a location for over 700 film and television productions.
Currently, more than 30 video game businesses operate in the state.
Georgia's video game workforce benefits from 15 of the state's colleges and universities offering video game development courses or curriculums, including some graduate degree programs.
source_title: Georgia Department of Economic Development - Wikipedia

facts: Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. is an innovator in automotive vision systems.
Foresight signed an agreement with Hitachi Astemo Americas to test and evaluate autonomous-driving vision systems (ADAS).
Hitachi Astemo is a global Tier One supplier of engine management, electric powertrain and integrated vehicle-control systems for the auto industry.
The project with Hitachi Astemo is a paid joint proof of concept (POC) that calls for the evaluation and testing of simulated and real-life autonomous driving scenarios.
The program will pair Foresight QuadSight and Mono2Stereo technologies with existing Hitachi Astemo camera systems to improve distance measurement and object detection.
The project with Hitachi Astemo follows the successful evaluation of a QuadSight vision system prototype reported by the company in March 2021.

Foresight’s proprietary Mono2Stereo software-based solution uses overlapping views of existing cameras with different fields of vision to create a three-dimensional stereo-vision system to improve object detection.
Haim Siboni, CEO of Foresight, stated the company is excited to be involved in another auto industry POC project.
Siboni noted that being chosen by Hitachi Astemo, a major supplier of automotive stereo vision systems, underlines the importance of stereoscopic technology in enhancing existing ADAS systems.
The technology can amplify performance, provide better distance accuracy, and offer more robust active safety features.
Foresight's solutions can be readily deployed in vehicles equipped with Level 2 and Level 2-plus autonomy systems without requiring additional hardware and design changes.
Siboni believes teaming up with Hitachi Astemo could potentially lead to further collaboration with other automotive OEMs.
source_title: Foresight Signs Agreement with Hitachi Astemo Americas

facts: Alan Kading has been named industrial and powersports aftermarket sales manager for Hitachi Astemo Americas Inc.
Kading has more than 25 years of automotive aftermarket experience and will be responsible for managing the expansion of Hitachi Astemo’s powersports product lines in the Americas.
He previously held sales management positions at Horizon Global and Nextel Partners.
Earlier in his career, he worked at General Motors as an OnStar product representative and as a GM accessories-channel sales representative.
Kading stated he is excited about the opportunity to lead Hitachi Astemo’s powersports and industrial products team in the Americas.
Hitachi Astemo will bring added value and innovation to current and future customers alike as it expands its catalog of racing, performance and maintenance parts.
Kading holds a bachelor’s degree in business administration from the University of Wisconsin-Stout in Menomonie, Wisconsin.
He resides in the Menomonie-Eau Claire area.

facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas has promoted Ryan Pelkey to director of sales for the Hitachi Astemo Americas Aftermarket Division.
Pelkey assumes the role vacated by Gary Plover, who recently became Hitachi Astemo’s vice president/Americas Aftermarket Business Division head.
Pelkey will report to Plover and lead the Americas aftermarket business unit’s sales activities for Hitachi.

As Director of Sales for Hitachi Astemo Americas Aftermarket Division, Pelkey will work closely with its sales managers and distributors in each territory to continue to provide high quality parts and service to Hitachi’s customers.

Pelkey stated he is excited to lead the Hitachi Astemo Americas Aftermarket Sales team and believes their strong team, coupled with exceptional fill rates and on-time delivery strategy, will propel them toward a very successful future as they expand coverage with the highest-quality parts.
Pelkey has been a member of the Hitachi Astemo Americas team since 2018.
Pelkey previously served Hitachi as the U.S. retail sales manager.

When Hitachi considered expanding its product line-up, Hitachi Astemo called on Pelkey’s 10-plus years of aftermarket product development experience to lead its U.S. product expansion initiatives as the U.S. marketing manager.

Pelkey stated that sticking to Hitachi values means quality is paramount in their offerings no matter the vehicle coverage, and customers can confidently reach for any Hitachi Astemo product knowing they are getting the same original equipment quality they have experienced for years.
Pelkey comes to his new position with 15 years of sales experience within the automotive aftermarket.
Throughout these years, Pelkey has been a successful leader managing key relationships with multiple retailers and national accounts.
During Pelkey’s tenure he has worked at K&N Engineering, ReadyLIFT suspension, and Gale Banks engineering.
At previous employers, Pelkey was instrumental in creating strategies that increased sales significantly.
Pelkey added that Hitachi Astemo will continue to focus on delivering more high-quality products and service to its customers.

Hitachi Astemo has been a trusted brand for over 90 years to a wide range of original equipment and aftermarket customers, including Advance Auto Parts, AutoZone, O’Reilly Auto Parts, as well as independent warehouse distributors.
