facts: Superior Essex Inc. announced the appointment of Daniel Choi as CEO effective March 1, 2022.
Choi will assume the role for Superior Essex Inc. and all its subsidiaries, including Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, Essex Brownell, Essex Energy Italy, IVA, and Hi-Wire.
Choi brings decades of experience in strategy, operations, and sales development to the company.
Choi previously worked at LS Cable and System, the parent company of Superior Essex Inc., most recently serving as Regional President, North and South America for LS Cable and System.
Prior to that role, Choi served as President, LS Cable and System USA for MV and LV power cable systems.
Choi was closely associated with Superior Essex as President of Cyprus Investments from 2008 to 2014 and Senior Vice President of PMO Europe and China in 2015.
Choi holds a PhD in Materials Science and Engineering from Seoul National University and an MBA from Helsinki Business School.
Choi lives and works in Atlanta, where he also chairs the Korean Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Georgia.
Daniel Choi stated his goal is to develop the business based on past results for future success while maintaining a high level of quality and customer service.
Brian Kim resigned as CEO of Superior Essex Inc. and its subsidiaries after nearly seven years leading the team.
During his tenure, Kim increased hybrid and PoE cable infrastructure capacity and prioritized innovation and R&D with the launch of the MagForceX Innovation Center.
Kim also launched the strategic joint venture Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire and committed the business to a sustainable future.
Superior Essex is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
Superior Essex has over 3,000 employees across 11 countries on three continents.
Superior Essex is a global leading supplier of magnet wire and is at the forefront of communication fiber and Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology application.
The company's technology is crucial for creating disruptive technological advances in the electrification of automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is pioneering smart building technology and setting the pace for sustainability.

On May 14, 2021, Superior Essex Inc. aligned its sustainability efforts with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
The company has committed to aligning with 12 of the 17 specific UN SDGs.
This alignment supports green manufacturing, sustainable products, and Essex Furukawa's 2030 Vision goals.
Superior Essex is the parent company of the brands Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.
Annie Bevan serves as Chief Sustainability Officer for Superior Essex Inc.
Annie Bevan stated that as a global manufacturer, the company is very excited to align with the UN SDG program.
She noted that while they have not reached the finish line, this commitment and subsequent actions show the company sees a greater good and the ability to reduce its impact to support the collective.
In markets not yet focused on sustainability or green manufacturing methods, Superior Essex and its divisions align with UN SDGs as well as customer and planetary environmental and social awareness.

facts: On May 15, 2023, Essex Brownell announced it will expand its operational footprint in the Southeast United States by moving and expanding its distribution center in McDonough, Georgia.
The new facility will be located within the same industrial complex as the previous location to ensure uninterrupted or accelerated service for customers.
Essex Brownell is expanding its facility size from 25,000 square feet to over 133,000 square feet.
The company will retain its current staff and create opportunities to double jobs at the new location.
Rebecca Hunnicutt, Vice President of Operations & Customer Service, stated that the expansion opportunity was an easy choice due to additional benefits.
Essex Brownell has been located in McDonough since 1999 and values its excellent staff and strong relationships with the local property management team.
The expansion aligns with Essex Brownell's business goals of 'continuous investment in this region' and 'better support for our customers.'
This marks the second operational expansion within one year, following the opening of a state-of-the-art facility in Reno, Nevada in the second half of 2022.
Current customers serviced outside this location will not experience service disruptions, while others can expect increased service speed.
The expansion avoids traditional growing pains like retraining or painful processes, making the company larger and closer to its customers.
The move allows Essex Brownell to reduce post-pandemic supply chain redundancies and overlaps, ensuring the right materials are delivered at the right time and place.
The expansion increases agility and speed in product delivery, which remains a long-term focus for the organization.
Essex Brownell is an international distributor of magnet wire and related products used to build and repair motors, generators, transformers, and electrical components.
In addition to discrete materials, Essex Brownell provides custom fabrication and rewinding services through its Essex Active conversion facility.
Essex Brownell operates under the parent company Superior Essex, headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Essex Brownell Announces Investment At McDonough, Georgia Distribution Center - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex is headquartered in Atlanta, United States.
Key people include Daniel Choi serving as CEO.
The company's products include Magnet Wire and Communication Cables.
Revenue reached $2.99 billion in 2007.
The company employs 3,800 people globally, with over 3,000 employees across 11 countries.
The parent company is LS I&D, a Korean entity.
Essex Solutions manufactures magnet wire for the automotive industry as well as energy, industrial, commercial, and residential sectors.
Superior Essex Communications was founded in 1998 and manufactures and supplies communications cable in North America.

Superior Essex Communications is active in developing power over Ethernet applications, intelligent buildings, smart cities, and 5G Fiber infrastructure as part of the Cisco Digital Ceiling ecosystem.
In 2021, Superior Essex Communications was appointed a corporate board member of the Continental Automated Buildings Association (CABA).
Essex Brownell operates as a distributor of lead wire, motors, drives, electrical and electronic insulation, tapes, repair parts, and shop and test equipment.
Essex Brownell operates 16 facilities in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.
Essex Wire Corporation was founded in 1930 as an original equipment manufacturer and operated 44 plants in North America by 1965 with sales of $355 million.
Superior Cable came into existence in 1954 and had sales passing $500 million in 1997.
In April 1999, Superior Essex was formed by a merger of Essex Wire Corporation and Superior Cable.
In 2008, the Korean LS Group acquired Superior Essex for roughly $900 million to extend its reach into the North American and European market.
Superior Essex's PowerWise cable won a 2018 Top Product of the Year Award for its 4PPoE innovation.

Superior Essex was the first cable manufacturer awarded the International Living Future Institute's Living Product Challenge certification in September 2017 for its Category 6+ low smoke, halogen free (LSHF) cable.
Manufacturing facilities are located across the Americas (United States, Canada, Mexico), Europe (France, Germany, Italy, Serbia, United Kingdom), and Asia (China, Malaysia, Japan).

facts: Superior Essex, a leading global manufacturer of wire and cable products, relocated to The Pavilion, a Class-A office property located at 5770 Powers Ferry Road, Sandy Springs, GA to open the month of March.
Its new location spans two full-floors and has 57,078 square feet of space, featuring an open floor plan designed to facilitate collaboration and spark creativity.

Having served a variety of industries for 85 years, this relocation allows the global company to continue optimizing productivity and efficiency while increasing talent acquisition by embracing modern workplace trends.

Anna Basista, Vice President of Global Human Resources for Superior Essex, said: 'We are excited for the years of opportunity this transition will provide for Superior Essex. We’re looking forward to aligning our culture of collaboration, agility, engagement, and respect with our workplace – this will be made possible with this move to The Pavilion’s tenant-focused site.'

Relocating from an office nearby, the move enabled Superior Essex to maintain its strategic position in Atlanta’s Northwest market, allowing employees to experience seamless access and connectivity to the clients they service and surrounding business centers.
Additionally, and coordinated with the move into its new corporate office space, the executive team at Superior Essex announced the opening of an employee library.

Each of the two library spaces are located in the social hub spaces of the facility and the pair offers a wide range of materials, including bilingual books, magazines, and other periodicals for employees to either read at their leisure or take off-site.

Superior Essex CEO Brian Kim put the plans for this project in motion concurrently with the move into The Pavilion building and believes that it will continue to enrich the work-life balance that is important to the culture of the company.

Kim said: 'The new library spaces will be a wonderful addition to the social hub as well as to the overall Superior Essex corporate vision,' adding, 'As a longtime advocate for access to educational resources, I consider a library to be a symbol of a company that places a high priority on learning and positive development. It is my hope that this addition will increase the quality work experience for everyone.'
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Solutions, Superior Essex Communications, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
It has over 3,000 employees in 11 countries, on three continents.
Superior Essex is the leading, global provider of magnet wire and is on the forefront of communications fiber application as well as Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
It is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while also setting the pace on sustainability.
source_title: Superior Essex Moves to New Location | Essex Solutions

facts: Hyundai expects production to begin at its new electric vehicle manufacturing plant in Bryan County by early 2025.
Chatham County leaders expect thousands of people to move to the area as a result of the Hyundai Metaplant and other similar projects.

Companies building charging networks or supplying components, such as ChargePoint and EVgo, are expected to see demand surge as Georgia's model replicates nationwide.

facts: Superior Essex Communications announced its membership in GeSI, an organization focused on enabling digital sustainability.
Membership aligns Superior Essex Communications with a growing number of large ICT companies worldwide pursuing social and ecological sustainability through technology.
Annie Bevan, Global Head of Sustainability at Superior Essex Communications, stated the GeSI membership reflects long-standing support for the company's global sustainability efforts.
Superior Essex Communications is the only cable manufacturer to have received Living Product Challenge certification.
It is the only company offering externally verified EPDs, HPDs, and Multi-Attribute Labels for over 55 product families.
Many of its products are manufactured in a certified Zero Waste to Landfill facility.
The company's products support customer sustainability goals by contributing to green building certifications such as Living Building Challenge, WELL, and LEED v2009 and v4.
Superior Essex is a leading cable provider supporting Power-over-Ethernet systems that lead to energy savings and higher occupant productivity and well-being.
Will Bryan, VP of Broadband Sales at Superior Essex Communications, stated the GeSI membership allows the company to participate in and facilitate global sustainability discussions.

GeSI is a globally recognized leader in ICT sustainability, focusing on climate change, energy/resource efficiency, e-waste disposal, responsible supply chain practices, labor rights, and public order.
Luis Neves, CEO of GeSI, welcomed Superior Essex Communications as a member, noting their commitment strengthens GeSI's work across the industry value chain.

Superior Essex International LP manufactures a broad portfolio of wire and cable products, including copper and fiber optic cables for outside plant applications, DAS cables, and accessories for commercial, industrial, and utility markets.
The company maintains high ecological sustainability in its manufacturing processes through safe, environmentally conscious operations and environmental performance measurement.
Superior Essex Communications is headquartered at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327 – USA.

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) became a 100% subsidiary of Suzuki Motor USA LLC (SMO) effective March 1, 2024.
The organizational change was announced on March 5, 2024, in Brea, California.
SMAC was founded in 2001 and is based in Rome, Georgia.
SMAC serves as Suzuki’s All-Terrain Vehicle (ATV) manufacturing facility.
For over two decades, all Suzuki adult KingQuad ATVs have been manufactured at the Rome, Georgia location.
Suzuki ATVs are sold in the United States, Canada, and nearly 25 countries worldwide.
SMO officials cited consolidating ATV sales and production arms to strengthen the overall ATV business in the United States and globally as a primary reason for the change.
Suzuki introduced the first four-wheel ATV in 1982 with the LT125 model, creating an all-new category of off-road recreational and utility vehicles.
Suzuki’s current flagship ATV is the KingQuad 750AXi.
Masami Haga, President of SMO, commented on the new organizational structure.
Haga stated that a primary goal of the change is to expand commitment to the growth of the entire powersports business.
The organizational evolution will enable building on relationships with Suzuki dealers and SMAC vendor partners.
Strengthening the supply chain and purchasing policies for locally sourced parts are objectives of the move.
By integrating production and sales functions, SMO and SMAC leaders expect to improve responsiveness to the ATV market by more accurately linking production to sales demand.
SMO aims to improve inventory management for both the company and its dealer body to protect sales margins, flooring costs, and profitability.
The move will result in better, more direct communication between SMO and SMAC, improving staff interaction between the two companies.
Leaders expect to create a more collaborative environment between the production and sales groups of Suzuki ATVs, fostering better connection between Rome- and Brea-based teams.
Suzuki’s United States distribution headquarters are located in Brea, California, where the company relocated in 1981 after first entering the U.S. market in 1963.
SMAC becoming a subsidiary of SMO sets the stage for new Suzuki ATV offerings and enhancements to existing products in the future.
source_title: Suzuki Cycles

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America is one of the companies contributing to the growing industry of technical metals and automotive parts in Rome.
The technical metals and automotive parts industry has largely replaced the textile and carpet industry that was prominent in Rome during much of the twentieth century.

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and GDEcD officially recognized Lockheed Martin’s 75-year history of aerospace production in Georgia on June 18, 2026.
The milestone celebration was attended by First Lady Marty Kemp and state economic officials.
Lockheed Martin represents a massive, permanent anchor of high-multiplier jobs supporting an ecosystem of smaller machine shops, logistics firms, and service providers across the state.
According to GDEcD, Lockheed’s operational footprint supports thousands of direct jobs and contributes significantly to Georgia’s GDP through aerospace exports and defense contracts.
Lockheed has helped Georgia transition from an agrarian and textile-heavy economy into a global hub for aviation and aerospace.
For every high-paying engineering role at a Lockheed facility, several indirect jobs are created in the local community.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “The enduring partnership between the state of Georgia and Lockheed Martin is a testament to our commitment to a pro-business climate that attracts and retains the world’s leading innovators.”
Lockheed-Georgia operations have shifted from mid-20th century raw production volume to software integration, stealth technology, and autonomous systems.
Georgia’s workforce has evolved from traditional assembly line skills to advanced robotics and systems engineering.
The state has invested in technical college programs and STEM initiatives specifically tailored to meet aerospace industry needs.
Economic analysts argue that heavy reliance on massive defense contractors creates a single-point-of-failure risk for Georgia’s economy due to federal budget volatility.

Proponents note that precision engineering and complex project management skills from the Lockheed ecosystem are highly transferable, with former employees often starting aerospace firms or moving to commercial aviation.
While Georgia's automotive sector has seen a recent surge with EV manufacturers, the defense sector's tenure is deeper and supply chain integration more entrenched.

Defense (Lockheed) in Georgia has a 75+ year investment horizon driven by federal contracts with high economic stability, whereas EV/Automotive has a recent (last decade) horizon driven by consumer demand with moderate volatility.
The U.S. Department of Defense emphasizes Joint All-Domain Command and Control (JADC2), driving skyrocketing demand for integrated data and AI in cockpits.
GDEcD continues to leverage tax credits and workforce grants to keep the cost of doing business in Georgia competitive relative to the available talent pool.
source_title: Governor Kemp and GDEcD Welcome New Investment to Georgia - News Usa Today

entity_name: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. Rome Plant
facts: Suzuki’s ATV manufacturing facility in Rome, Ga. celebrated the production of its 250,000th ATV on March 31.
The year 2008 marks the 25th anniversary of Suzuki's production of the industry’s first four-wheeled ATV, the 1983 Suzuki LT-125.
Anniversary events are scheduled throughout the summer to commemorate the landmark.
Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. (SMAC) opened its Rome plant in May of 2002 as the company’s only US-based manufacturing facility to produce the Eiger series of ATVs.
The facility employs 300 SMAC employees who build ATV frames, mold plastic, and assemble KingQuad 400s, 450s, and 750s.
More than 200 units are assembled in an eight-hour shift at the Rome plant.
Nearly 60,000 ATVs were produced at the facility in 2007.
Factory workers, local dignitaries, media, and the Suzuki factory ATV race team (Team Rockstar/MSR/Suzuki/Yoshimura/Pro Taper) attended a commemorative ceremony.
SMAC’s ATV sales manager Alan Horne stated that the milestone commemorates the success of Suzuki’s ATV production over the last six years and the cooperation with the community.

Rome mayor Wright Bagby stated that for the last ten years, local efforts have fought to save manufacturing jobs, and with help from Suzuki employees, they saved jobs and maintained a higher percentage of manufacturing jobs in the community than most places in the South or probably in the country.
Current AMA ATV Motocross Series points leader Dustin Wimmer rode the historic quad off the production line.
Team Suzuki personnel, factory staff, mayor Bagby, and other local government officials added their signatures to the red KingQuad 450 AXI 4X4.

Mel Harris, vice president of American Suzuki’s motorcycle/ATV division, congratulated the city of Rome, Ga., and SMAC employees for believing in the project, noting that producing a quarter of a million ATVs in six years demonstrates Suzuki’s commitment to the ATV market.
source_title: Suzuki Plant Celebrates Milestone | ATV.com

facts: Suzuki Motor USA, LLC is celebrating 20 years of manufacturing KingQuad ATVs at the SMAC facility.
KingQuad ATVs first rolled off the assembly line in Rome, Georgia in May of 2002.
KingQuad ATVs have continued to be built exclusively in the United States since 2002.
The SMAC facility is a multi-million dollar, 35-acre manufacturing site.
The facility is located in the center of the biggest ATV market in the world.
The Rome, Georgia location allows access to real-world rider feedback for product refinement and timely delivery of KingQuad ATVs to Suzuki dealers worldwide.
Suzuki was founded in 1909 and has been an innovator in engineering since its founding.
In the early 1980s, Suzuki led the industry with the creation of the first ever four-wheel ATV in 1983.
Kerry Graeber serves as Vice President, Division Manager MC/ATV Sales and Marketing at Suzuki.
The SMAC facility is a key to the continued success of Suzuki’s global ATV business.
Suzuki KingQuad ATVs built at the Rome, Georgia facility are shipped to U.S. Suzuki dealers and nearly 30 countries worldwide.

facts: E-Z-GO is headquartered in Augusta, Georgia.

From June 13-15, buyers will receive a $500 instant rebate off the purchase of any new E-Z-GO model in stock at local dealers.
The rebate applies to models including the Freedom® RXV personal golf car and the E-Z-GO Express™ L6 six-passenger vehicle.
The June 13-15 discount stacks with the ongoing "Red, White, & Built Right" savings event.
The "Red, White, & Built Right" event offers instant rebates ranging from $1,000-$4,000 on specific E-Z-GO models.
Customers could save up to $4,500 on a brand-new American-made golf cart when combining promotions.
June 13 marks the exact day in 1954 when brothers Billy and Bev Dolan started E-Z-GO in a garage in Augusta, Georgia.
E-Z-GO operates from a 54-acre campus in Augusta, Georgia.
The Augusta campus employs over 1,000 people who design, manufacture, and distribute golf cars and utility vehicles worldwide.
The region also has manufacturing jobs with the golf cart/utility vehicle company E-Z-Go Texxon.
E-Z-Go Texxon earned the Augusta metro area the nickname "Golf Cart Capital of the World."
Ninety percent of the world’s golf carts are made in Augusta, Georgia.
Nine out of every ten golf carts rolling around courses from California to Florida were built by American hands in Georgia.
E-Z-GO builds its vehicles with American engineering and craftsmanship rather than relying on cheap Chinese parts or foreign subsidies used by competitors.
Local competitors Club Car and E-Z-GO combine to employ thousands of local Augusta residents and make a lasting economic impact each year.
Close to 20 percent of Georgia’s workforce is dedicated to traditional and advanced manufacturing, including sterile environment technology.
Georgia has built one of the strongest manufacturing economies in the country through decades of smart policies that support manufacturing and American workers.
Adam Harris serves as Vice President and General Manager at E-Z-GO.
Adam Harris stated: "We are proud to celebrate National Golf Cart Day and our anniversary with our employees, our community, and our customers around the world."
Adam Harris added: "E-Z-GO is an iconic American brand, and it’s an honor to have this occasion to commemorate its unique place in the sport of golf and the world of transportation."
The company began operations in a cramped one-room machine shop in 1954 before expanding to its current campus.
The National Golf Cart Day promotion runs through June 15.

entity_name: Thin film lithium conducting powder material deposition from flux
canonical_name: US9362546B1 - Thin film lithium conducting powder material deposition from flux
facts: The invention is directed to battery technologies and processing techniques for solid-state lithium-ion batteries.
All solid state lithium ion batteries have applications in electric cars and energy storage for solar cells.
Ceramic electrolyte powder material is mixed with two or more flux materials at a temperature of less than 400° C. to form a fluxed powder material.
The fluxed powder material is shaped and heated again at a temperature less than 1100° C. to form a dense lithium conducting material.
The method deposits lithium conducting electrolyte materials with garnet, perovskite, NASICON, and LISICON structures.
The ceramic powder material has a median particle size of about 100 nm to 10 um.
The first flux material comprises lithium material and has a melting temperature of about 500-1000° C.
A second flux material is mixed with the first to form a eutectic mixture with a melting point of less than 1000° C.
The eutectic mixture is subjected to a temperature of about 100 to 1000° C.
The resulting dense lithium conducting material has a second density of at least 80% of the theoretical crystalline density.
The solid electrolyte can be deposited upon a substrate at relatively low temperatures, translating to low manufacturing cost and high efficiency.
Solid electrolytes according to the invention have high stability to water, air, lithium metal anodes, and cathode potentials greater than 5V.
Existing batteries with liquid electrolytes form SEI layers that limit cycle life and do not allow safe usage of Li metal anodes.
Batteries with polymer electrolytes cannot be reliably deposited at less than 50 μm thickness and lack sufficient Li+ conductivity at <60° C.
LiPON type batteries have low Li+ conductivity at reasonable temperatures and require expensive PVD processes.
Sulfide solid electrolytes are unstable to air, water, and/or Li anodes and typically cannot be deposited as thin films.
Many oxide solid electrolytes have low Li+ conductivity at reasonable temperatures.
Lithium Garnet materials are a notable exception for stability and conductivity.
Compositions similar to Li7La3Zr2O12 and doped compositions such as Li7-xLa3Zr2-xMxO12 (where M can be Nb or Ta) have high conductivity.
The invention discloses a method of depositing a thin film of lithium conducting garnet LiALaBM′CM″DZrEOF, where 4<A<8.5, 1.5<B<4, 0<C<2, 0<D<2, 0<E<2, 10<F<14.
M′ and M″ are selected from Al, Mo, W, Nb, Sb, Ca, Ba, Sr, Ce, Hf, Rb, and Ta.
Traditional solid state reaction processes require repeated calcination at high reaction temperatures typically greater than 800° C. for >6 hours.
Garnet calcined products usually require even higher temperatures (>1000° C.) for long times (>20 hrs) for sintering.
The flux growth method prepares a slurry of electrolyte and flux powders.
Viable powdered oxide components for garnet material include LiOH, La2O3, and ZrO2.
Flux component salts may include LiOH, Li2CO3, Li3BO3, LiCl, LiBr, and/or LiI.
In various embodiments, the flux comprises two types of flux powders: one from LiOH, LiCl, LiBr, LiNO3, LiSO4 and another from NaOH, NaCl, NaNO3, NaSO4, NaBr, Na2CO3.
A third flux material comprising KOH, KCl, KNO3, KSO4, KBr, and/or K2CO can be used.
Individual flux materials typically have a melting point of about 700° C.
Mixing two flux materials with a eutectic melting point can achieve a lower melting temperature of about 200˜400° C.
Example eutectic solvent mixtures include 0.3LiOH-0.7NaOH (m.p.=251° C.) and 0.29LiOH-0.71KOH (m.p.=227° C.).
The ratio of flux powder to electrolyte powders can be about 2:1, 3:1, 6:1, or other ratios.
Electrolyte and flux powders can be mixed using ball milling in an inert liquid such as ethanol, isopropanol, or toluene.
The mixture is cast onto a carrier substrate which can serve as the anode current collector.
The carrier substrate can be a copper, nickel, or stainless steel foil of <25 um thickness, or polymer material with nickel and/or copper coating.
Flux materials are heated above their melting point to form a liquid phase that fills space between ceramic electrolyte powders.
Dissolved ceramic electrolyte powers form a completely molten layer on the substrate.
Nucleation of desired electrolyte phases occurs, initiated by gently cooling the system.
Crystallites continue to grow and coalesce, forming a continuous layer while excess molten flux is pushed out.
The system is quenched to room temperature and excess flux is washed away using solvents like water, ethanol, isopropanol, acetone, or acetonitrile.
Flux may also be chemically etched by acids such as HCl or bases such as NH4OH.
The resulting continuous dense polycrystalline ceramic layer has a thickness between about 100 nm and 100 um, preferably 1-10 um.
The growth method may generate a structure with few grain boundaries in the plane of the film to avoid reducing total conductivity.
For flux sintering, pre-synthesized electrolyte powders can be used instead of raw components.
Pre-synthesized electrolyte ceramic powders can be prepared by conventional high temperature reaction followed by milling to reduce particle size to around 1 micron.

Example preparation: 100 gms of pre-synthesized Li7La3Zr2O12 electrolyte powder is prepared by mixing 31.03 gms of Li2CO3, 58.65 gms of La2O3, and 29.57 gms of ZrO2 by ball milling in isopropanol for 24 hrs.
The dried mixture is calcined at 900° C. for 12 hrs, and then 1100° C. for 12 hrs to form the garnet phase.
The calcined powder is re-milled in isopropanol to reduce average particle size to 1 um.
The synthesized electrolyte powder is mixed with flux components and liquid to form a slurry for film casting.
The slurry is cast onto a substrate to form a film of thickness between 200 nm-100 um.
The dried slurry can be heated at a rate of 1° C./min to a temperature of 400° C. and held for 6 hrs.
Liquid phase sintering occurs where ceramic electrolyte particles dissolve at high energy contact points and re-precipitate at lower energy vertices, resulting in densification and coarsening.
Densification can result in a reduction in volume of as much as 5-20%.
Excess flux material is expelled to the surface of the film and can be washed away or trapped at triple points within the ceramic structure.
A two-component battery structure has cathode particle elements coated on both sides of a cathode current collector.
Cathode material may comprise a mixture of active cathode material LiFePO4, conductive additive materials like carbon black, and presynthesized Li7La3Zr2O12 material.
Cathode layers thickness are between 1 um and 1000 um, preferably 100 um.
Cathode current collector element thickness is preferably less than 20 um.
Anode current collector is coated on both sides by electrolyte elements.
Garnet precursor material, flux material, and optionally solvent, dispersant, and/or binder may be coated onto a substrate like nickel, copper, or stainless steel foil.
Coating techniques include screen printing, slot-die, gravure, microgravure, doctor blade, knife-over-roll, comma coating, reverse comma coating.
The coating is dried, calendered, and sintered to produce a dense garnet film on one or both sides of the substrate.
A one-component battery structure positions the cathode current collector between cathode particle elements, with the cathode element interfacing with the electrolyte.
Molten mixture of flux and electrolyte powder can be deposited onto substrates transported by rollers for fast manufacturing compatible with existing tools.
Substrates can be dipped into a molten mixture to deposit material on both sides simultaneously.
A slot-casting process provides an electrolyte mixture within a cavity, comprising dense electrolyte material optionally including polymer material.
source_title:
US9362546B1 - Thin film lithium conducting powder material deposition from flux 
        - Google Patents

facts: Established in 1975.
Located in Thomson, GA, US.
Primary industry is Materials with sub-industries Rubber & Plastics and Manufacturing verticals.
Excels in large tonnage injection molding, providing customized plastic components for various industries, including automotive and industrial markets.
Specializes in large tonnage injection molding with capabilities extending up to 3,700 tons of clamping force.
Employs advanced machinery and robotics to manufacture high-quality plastic components.
Core product offerings include precise injection-molded parts tailored to meet specific customer needs.
Emphasizes integration of assembly expertise into manufacturing processes to provide added value and cost savings for clients.
Products are utilized extensively in the Southeastern United States, catering to both domestic and international clients.
Generates revenue primarily through business-to-business transactions for injection molding and assembly services on a contractual basis.
Revenue streams are driven by completed project volume alongside pricing agreements based on product complexity and quantity.
In March 2022, Advantage Capital invested USD 2. 75 mn in Thomson Plastics, Inc.
The company has raised a total of USD 2. 75 mn.

facts: Thomson Plastics, Inc. began business in Thomson, Georgia in 1995.
The company is an industry leader in large-tonnage plastic injection molding concentrating on power sports, lawn and garden, industrial, and automotive sectors.
Less than 4% of the roughly 8,500 enterprises in the US that can turn a plastic pellet into a part have large tonnage capability.
Plastics News defines large tonnage capability as having at least one large machine with an injection pressure of more than 1,200 tons.
More than 20% of Thomson’s fleet is larger than 1,200 tons.
Thomson can reach injection pressures of up to 4,400 tons.
The re-shoring or near-shoring of crucial components for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) remains one of the key developments in Thomson’s market today.
Many OEMs have been compelled to reassess long-held ideas on strategic sourcing because of the brutally difficult lessons learned during the most recent worldwide pandemic.
Given that large plastic parts are difficult to ship, Thomson’s proximity to all locations east of the Mississippi River makes it an excellent choice as a supply partner.
Thomson operates four locations: Thomson, Georgia; Lexington, North Carolina; Spartanburg, South Carolina; and Howell, Michigan.
The collecting and pumping of data as close to real-time as feasible gives Thomson’s operations teams the best chance to service their consumers.
Thomson relentlessly pursues continuous improvement across all parts of its business rather than just from the standpoint of the shop floor.
To ensure flawless quality, on time delivery, and within budget, Thomson’s specialists continuously improve all systems and processes.
Ms. Tina Williamson serves as Vice President of Finance at Thomson.
Under Ms. Williamson's team, the cycle time to close the accounting period was reduced by 92%.
The improved closing financial package is prepared and distributed to operations teams to utilize data for identifying areas for improvement and cost savings opportunities.
Thomson is a very metric-focused company guided by select few critical indicators that best position the team to satisfy consumers.
A “red” measure at Thomson indicates a need to focus rather than necessarily meaning something is bad.
The team approaches both internal and external issues with laser-like accuracy by focusing on rapidly pulsating, accurate data of crucial few metrics driving total performance.
Thomson serves as a one-stop shop for clients’ plastic injection molding requirements, offering features and services rivals do not.
Thomson stands out in the market due to its technical proficiency in tool design, process design, and manufacturing execution.
Finding innovative methods to employ data as a force multiplier for partners is a new initiative for Thomson.
To continue tightening the supply chain and ensure precise daily execution, Thomson is concentrating on constant improvement in the pulse of data and information provided to clients.
Tina Williamson emphasizes continual improvement and ensuring the company has the data and insight to fuel its next steps.

Per Tina Williamson, operating in the current business environment requires real-time data, complete transparency, the ability to pivot and focus on what is critical, identify process improvements, and keep the focal point on goals.
Working with a family-oriented team is described as a bonus by Tina Williamson.
With the work entailed by leadership and employees, Thomson is looking forward to continued profitability, new customers, new markets, and continued investment.

facts: Amara Industries announced the acquisition of Thomson Plastics on Dec. 9, 2025.
Amara Industries is a parent company to the Gracious Living group of companies.
Thomson Plastics is a recognized leader in large tonnage injection molding currently operating from two locations in Georgia and North Carolina.
The acquisition enhances Amara Industries' manufacturing footprint, automation capabilities, and technical depth to a total of five manufacturing facilities.
Thomson Plastics has clamping force capacities up to 3,700 tons, with 30% of its fleet exceeding 1,000 tons.
Thomson Plastics is supported by advanced robotics and automation to deliver consistent performance across high-volume production environments.

Thomson Plastics' technical portfolio includes injection molding, gas-assisted molding, over-molding, insert molding, in-mold labeling, robotic part picking, pad printing, and heat transfer decorating.

Vince Orlando, Co-President at Amara Industries, stated the addition represents a strategic milestone that will enhance their ability to serve customers with scale, speed, and precision across North America.
The unified company will meet rising demand for high-performance molded products in key sectors including automotive, consumer goods, industrial, and commercial markets.
The expansion into the Southeastern United States strengthens Amara Industries' ability to provide customers with faster delivery, greater flexibility, and improved supply chain efficiency.
Amara Industries employs over 500 people and offers end-to-end capabilities spanning design, product development, engineering, and materials science.
Amara Industries specializes in injection molding, assembly, profile extrusion, decorating, and material compounding.
Amara Industries holds ISO, IATF, and CTPAT certifications.

facts: Thomson Plastics, Inc. (“TPI”) formally announced the investment and installation of two new 3,700-ton injection molding machines for its southern locations.
The purchase fulfills TPI’s strategic plan to refresh the fleet of injection molding equipment across all facilities by the end of 2026.
TPI President and CEO Steve Dyer stated that long-term investments are critical to TPI’s ongoing commitment to exceeding customer expectations in quality, cost, and customer service.
The new presses are faster and significantly more efficient, using 40% less energy than older vintage machines.
According to Dyer, the upgrades produce a more consistent and higher-quality product that will continue to serve marquee customers.
TPI is working to step up its game as marquee customers grow and expand, with growing expectations from them.
TPI will also be looking for many new customers.
This effort follows a Year One investment in five new production machines during 2022.
Three of the 2022 machines ranged in capacities between 1,000 and 1,980 tons and were installed at Thomson headquarters.
Two of the 2022 machines were 400-ton units brought online at TPI’s Lexington, NC, facility.
TPI is one of only a handful of companies that can employ as much as 4,400 tons of clamping force.
TPI’s mission is to be the leader in large tonnage injection molding in the southeast.
source_title: Altus Capital Partners | News

facts: ABC Technologies in Toronto completed its acquisition of TI Fluid Systems (TIFS) in Auburn Hills.
The transaction has an enterprise value of just over $2 billion.
The combined business will be rebranded as TI Automotive.
The rebranding reflects both companies’ shared focus on world-class engineering, people, and customer service.
The acquisition brings together two complementary businesses to create a stronger, more diversified Tier 1 manufacturer.
TI Automotive will supply market-leading safety and performance critical products and highly engineered components.
The business is positioned to support a diverse range of customers with its propulsion agnostic portfolio.
Combined revenue for the new entity will be $5.4 billion.
TI Automotive will have 34,600 employees in 26 countries.
TI Automotive will have the global scale and local reach needed to serve the automotive industry.
TI Automotive will be led by ABC President and CEO Terry Campbell.
An executive leadership team combining the skills and expertise of both ABC and TIFS will support Campbell.
TI Automotive will be headquartered in Auburn Hills.
The company will be managed through a regional operating model to ensure customer proximity and agility to respond rapidly to local market dynamics.
ABC President and CEO Terry Campbell stated the business has exceptional people, differentiated products, a deep commitment to safety, and a relentless focus on delivering for customers.
Campbell noted the combination creates opportunities to drive innovation and manufacturing excellence while establishing foundations for future growth.
source_title: ABC Technologies Acquires TI Fluid Systems in Auburn Hills to Create TI Automotive - DBusiness Magazine

facts: Toronto-based manufacturer ABC Technologies is acquiring British TI Fluid for $1.32 billion.
ABC Technologies has more than 11,000 employees.
ABC Technologies manufactures and supplies custom, highly-engineered technical plastics to the North American light vehicle industry.
TI Fluid Systems is a London-listed, market-leading global manufacturer of thermal and fluid system solutions for current and developing vehicle architectures.
Shareholders of TI Fluid Systems will receive 200 pence per share under the transaction terms.
The transaction values TI Fluid Systems at an enterprise value of approximately £1,831 million.
TI Fluid Systems currently operates across 27 countries.
ABC Technologies is acquiring TI Fluid to expand both firms' global footprint and bolster its product portfolio.
The acquisition aims to attract a broader range of customers, including automotive OEMs and Tier One suppliers worldwide.

Terry Campbell, president and chief executive at ABC Technologies, stated the transaction represents a transformative strategic opportunity that will unlock value for stakeholders and accelerate growth.
A combined business will enable better customer service, according to Campbell.
ABC Technologies plans to look for further acquisitions in the future.
The company will persistently seek alignment with organizations that have proven capabilities to further its success.
The initial deadline for the sale offering was postponed to 22 November at ABC's request.
TI Fluid Systems shares rose 1.8 percent to 184.00 pence each on the last Friday afternoon in London.
ABC Technologies requested a further extension on Thursday of the previous week to finalize financing and other arrangements.
ABC Technologies stated it has substantially completed due diligence and is reconfirming the possible offer price.
The acquisition is expected to complete in the first half of 2025, subject to shareholder and relevant legal and regulatory approvals.
ABC Technologies is backed by Apollo Global Management, which acquired 51 percent of its shares in April 2021.
source_title: Canada’s ABC Technologies to acquire British TI Fluid for $1.32 bn - National Technology

facts: TI Fluid Systems is a global innovator of thermal and fluid system solutions for the full range of current and developing vehicle architectures.
TI Fluid Systems serves all major automotive manufacturers and has more than 100 years of automotive supply experience.
TI Fluid Systems operates across 28 countries with a commitment to improving efficiency, performance, and sustainability worldwide.
At IAA Mobility 2023, TI Fluid Systems is presenting an expanded product portfolio to reinforce its commitment to greener, smarter, highly efficient, and connected mobility.
The company's primary focus at IAA Mobility 2023 is a recently developed modular thermal management system engineered for future mobility and efficiency demands.
TI Fluid Systems will exhibit a holistic product portfolio ranging from component to system level at the event.
A demo vehicle will be exhibited to demonstrate how an efficient TI thermal management system can improve battery range.
Johannes Helmich states that while cybersecurity, connectivity, and autonomous driving are primary industry focuses, current customer needs should be prioritized.
End customers expect electromobility without restrictions regarding battery range, charging time, and available charging infrastructure.
TI Fluid Systems promotes the concept that "Efficiency is the New Currency," highlighting efficiency as critical for temperature control in battery electric vehicles (BEVs).
The company's modular thermal management system allows for tailor-made solutions with maximum flexibility.
Development focuses on system efficiency to increase overall vehicle efficiency, minimize energy losses, and maximize battery range by up to 20%.
Johannes Helmich identifies creating clean, sustainable, and innovative mobility as the greatest potential of the mobility transition.
Electrification is considered the first milestone of the mobility transition, with significant potential remaining in associated influencing factors.
Expanding fast-charging station infrastructure in rural areas and along highways is crucial for meeting customer flexibility demands and facilitating BEV adoption.
OEMs and legislators must ensure the generation and use of sustainable, CO2-neutral, and reusable materials, including limited raw materials used in batteries.
The entire BEV supply chain must make a significant contribution to product sustainability.
Energy sources must be renewable to make the entire vehicle life cycle more environmentally friendly.
Hydrogen and e-fuels are identified as potential energy sources with enormous potential but require significant effort to become competitive.
TI Fluid Systems is committed to strengthening its position as a leading provider of thermal management solutions for the automotive industry over the next 10 to 25 years.
The company plans to expand its product portfolio, develop new breakthrough technologies, and invest more in research, development, and its workforce.
source_title: Interview with: Johannes Helmich – TI Fluid Systems  | IAA MOBILITY

facts: TI Fluid Systems is actively transforming its operations and product offerings to support a more sustainable future as the automotive industry shifts toward electrification.
The company operates a company-wide transition strategy called “Taking-the-Turn,” which focuses on three pillars: Technology, Transformation, and Talent.
The “Taking-the-Turn” strategy aims to reduce emissions, foster innovation, and improve workplace safety and inclusivity.
TI Fluid Systems develops advanced products for electric and hybrid vehicles.

Through its global E-Mobility Innovation Centers (eMICs), TI Fluid Systems works with OEMs to design lightweight and modular systems that improve thermal management, reduce vehicle weight, and enhance energy efficiency.
The company is adopting more sustainable materials and advancing techniques like vertical integration to streamline production and lower environmental impact.
TI Fluid Systems is modernizing its facilities with renewable energy, digital monitoring systems, and smart manufacturing practices.
These efforts support the UN Sustainable Development Goals—particularly SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation & Infrastructure) and SDG 13 (Climate Action).
Brian David is the former Asia Pacific President and Managing Director of TI Fluid Systems.
Brian David played a pivotal role in evolving TI Fluid Systems’ product portfolio to support cleaner mobility, including electric and hybrid vehicle technologies.
His leadership included the development of lighter, more efficient systems and the integration of environmentally responsible practices across manufacturing sites.
Brian David is now retired and channels his passion for sustainable development and leadership through coaching businesses.

Brian David stated: “I lead with a couple of very simple dogmas in mind – ‘be the very best you can be’ and ‘always lead with integrity’. In this industry, to be the best and embody integrity means looking out for the environment. I am less focused on just the environment, but more on what can I do to better everyone and everything. Gone are the days when companies can sweep things under the rug and move on to the next environmentally damaging product. To innovate now is to build sustainable infrastructure and products – I don’t mean that’s the end-all, be-all, but a start to an industry-wide change. I lead by example, and I think what we have done here [TI Fluid Systems] is a pretty good example.”
TI Fluid Systems reduced its Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 15% year-on-year in 2024.
The company’s Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions are 28% lower than its 2021 baseline.
Progress stems from procuring over 120,000 MWh of renewable electricity.
The company implemented an energy efficiency program across global facilities.
TI Fluid Systems is investing in new product development, including an electric coolant pump and modular EV thermal systems.
TI Fluid Systems collaborates directly with original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) through its five e-Mobility Innovation Centers (eMICs).
The company is tracking toward 2030 targets: a 50% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions and a 30% reduction in Scope 3 emissions.
TI Fluid Systems secured €500 million in lifetime battery BEV bookings in 2024.
The company secured €600 million for HEVs in 2024 alone.
The electric coolant pump is set to enter production this year, completing the product offering for modular thermal systems.
Modular thermal systems will be a critical growth area given the increasing complexity and cooling needs of EV platforms.
Average training hours per salaried employee increased from 4 to 13 in 2024.
Lost Time Injury frequency was cut nearly in half in one year, dropping from 3.0 to 1.76.
The company's innovation alignment with global regulatory and consumer expectations has attracted positive ESG ratings and increased investor confidence.
Nylon-based multi-layer refrigerant lines offer a 30–60% weight reduction compared to traditional materials, contributing to greater EV efficiency and range.
The company diverted over 60,000 tons of CO2(e) emissions in 2024 by sourcing renewable energy through Energy Attribute Certificates.
In 2024, the company expanded its Women’s Empowerment Networks globally.
The company sponsored over 40 STEM scholarships for women in 2024.
TI Fluid Systems serves over 24,600 employees across 95 manufacturing sites in 26 countries.
TI Fluid Systems designs and produces essential vehicle components such as brake and fuel lines, fuel tanks, and thermal systems.
These components cater to all types of propulsion—from traditional internal combustion engines (ICE) to hybrid (HEV) and battery electric vehicles (BEV).
source_title: AIM2Flourish | Sustainable Development of TI Fluid Systems

The Company was established in 1919 as Tube Investments Ltd in Birmingham by consolidating four steel tube manufacturers.
The Company's name was changed to TI Group in 1982.
Bundy Corp became a subsidiary of the Company in 1988.
Walbro came under the control of the Company in April 1999 and was integrated with Bundy to form TI Automotive.
The Company merged into Smiths (then Smiths Industries) as its Automotive Systems Division in December 2000.
The Company was spun off from Smiths and became independent in July 2001.
The Company was acquired by Bain Capital in June 2015.
The Company acquired Millennium Industries Corporation, a U.S.-based producer of powertrain products for the automotive industry, in February 2016.
The Company successfully completed its IPO under the name TI Fluid Systems plc and listed on the London Stock Exchange in October 2017.
The Company opened a brake manufacturing facility in Monterrey, Mexico in April 2016.
The Company opened a third fuel tank systems production facility in Baoding, Hebei Province, China in June 2016.
The Company changed its corporate name from TI Automotive to TI Fluid Systems in May 2019.
The Company acquired 100% of Hanil Tube Corporation located in Korea in 2021.
The Company opened a new e-Mobility Innovation Centre in Rastatt, Germany in April 2022.
The Company opened new e-Mobility Innovation Centers in Nagoya, Japan and Incheon, South Korea in 2023.
The Company opened its fourth e-Mobility Innovation Center in Jiading, Shanghai, China in October 2023.
The Company acquired Hungarian-based Cascade Engineering Europe in November 2023.
The Company opened an all-new e-Mobility Innovation Center in Auburn Hills, Michigan in June 2024.
The Company was acquired by ABC Technologies Inc for an enterprise value of over GBP 1.8 billion in April 2025.

facts: TI Fluid Systems reiterated full-year targets on Thursday after third-quarter revenues sparked.
Group revenue in the nine months to 30 September jumped 10.4% to €2.41bn, or by 4.5% on a constant currency basis.
Sales rose in Europe and Africa, its biggest region, by 2.1% to €880.9m.
Sales rose in Asia Pacific by nearly 9% to €821.8m.
The company is making "excellent progress" on its organic electrification growth strategy.
€0.9bn of lifetime revenue for battery electric vehicle awards was booked in the year to date.
€300m of the BEV award revenue was booked in the third quarter.
Inflationary cost increases are expected to remain, and the firm is pursuing cost recoveries and price adjustments in response.
The company expects full-year results in line with current consensus market expectations.
Full-year revenue growth is expected to be consistent with, or slightly below, global light vehicle production.
Full-year adjusted earnings before interest and tax margin is likely to be around 6%.

facts: ABC Technologies is acquiring TI Fluid Systems.
The acquisition is tied to a $2.2 billion debt sale.
Banks are selling the debt associated with the acquisition.
TI Fluid Systems is a global manufacturer of thermal and fluid system solutions.
TI Fluid Systems operates across 27 countries.
TI Fluid Systems serves all major automotive manufacturers.
The combined entity promises an expanded global footprint and a more diversified product portfolio.
The success of the acquisition hinges on the ability of the combined entity to manage the $2.2 billion debt effectively.
Banks involved in holding and selling the debt include Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, and Barclays.
The banks held onto this debt for nearly two years before selling it.
The delay in selling the debt suggests initial concerns about the viability of the acquisition and the combined entity's ability to repay the debt.
The debt was sold at a fixed rate of 9.5% and priced at par.
The sale of the debt raises questions about transparency and fairness in high-stakes acquisitions.
The automotive industry is already facing challenges such as market volatility and economic uncertainty.
The addition of $2.2 billion in debt could exacerbate systemic risks and challenges for the automotive industry.
source_title: Banks Unload $2.2 Billion Debt for ABC's TI Fluid Acquisition

facts: TI Fluid Systems PLC is a United Kingdom-based company that manufactures engineered automotive fluid storage, carrying, delivery and thermal management systems for light vehicles.
The company operates through two segments: Fluid Carrying Systems (FCS) and Fuel Tank and Delivery Systems (FTDS).

The FCS segment manufactures brake and fuel lines and bundles, thermal management fluid systems including hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) and battery electric vehicle (BEV), heating and cooling lines, powertrain components and quick connectors.
The FTDS segment produces fuel tank systems and pumps and module fuel delivery systems, including HEV pressure-resistant fuel tanks.
FTDS products include plastic fuel tanks, filler pipes, pumps and modules and level sensors.
The company's geographic sub-divisions include North America, Europe, Asia Pacific and Latin America.
It has manufacturing facilities near original equipment manufacturer (OEM) assembly plants in over 104 locations across 29 countries.
Deutsche set a target price of 145 GBX for TI Fluid Systems PLC, indicating a potential upside of 17.9% compared to its opening share price of 123 GBX on 20/10/2023.
Trading has ranged between 89 (52 week low) and 162 (52 week high) with an average of 504,011 shares exchanging hands daily.
The market capitalisation at the time of writing is £616,333,830.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems PLC 17.9% Potential Upside Indicated By Deutsche

facts: The company provides automotive fluid storage, carrying and delivery systems and thermal management products and systems.

facts: TN Americas Holdings, Inc. is located at 2200 Pendley Road, Cumming, Georgia, United States ZIP: 30041.

The company specializes in the manufacture of precision and semi-precision balls to various industries including automotive, aerospace, anti-friction bearings, pumps, medical, pen, paints, oil, cosmetics, jewelry and furniture.
TN Americas Holdings, Inc. supplies a complete range of materials and has the ability to manufacture metal and ceramic balls to Grade 3 specifications.
Plastic can be manufactured to Grade 0 specification; using a different Grade scale than metals and ceramics.
The company offers high precision, precision, semi-precision, imported, specialty, and medical ball products that represent the very best in the industry.
The High-Precision Division offers superior performance through highly specialized materials supported by experienced engineers and knowledgeable customer service representatives.

The Semi-Precision Division & Distribution Center supplies custom orders generally suited for more standard applications; in addition to, stocking a wide selection of ISO-3290 and/or ABMA parts for immediate delivery.
TN Americas Holdings, Inc. balls are found in the majority of America's automotive, aircraft, marine, industrial, construction and scientific applications.
The company manufactures balls in sizes from 17-thousandths of an inch diameter for use in ball point pens, to 4 1/2 inches in diameter for use in heavy duty military and industrial applications.
Tolerances are held to within 25-millionths of an inch for bearing-quality lapped balls, and to within 3-millionths of absolute roundness for precision balls.
Surface finish is less than one RMS.
The production process combines operator skill with ongoing research and development program designed to infuse the manufacturing process with the latest scientific development and technology.
In 2002, the company established THT (Tsubaki Hoover Taicang) to produce precision Chrome balls.
In 2003, the company acquired Polish ball and roller manufacturer ZET to form THP (Tsubaki Hoover Poland).
In 2006, the company acquired Chongqing Steel Ball Company located in Chongqing, China.
In 2006, the Washington, Indiana Distribution Center was moved to Cumming, Georgia.
In 2007, Connecticut and Tennessee facilities were consolidated and transferred to the Georgia facility.
In 2010, the company established THI (Tsubaki Hoover India) to produce precision Chrome balls.
In 2013, the company acquired Spheric Trafalgar STE and STT in England and Thailand.
In 2013, Hoover celebrated 100 years of business.
In 2015, there was an announcement of listing on Tokyo Stock Exchange.
The company's parent company is located in Japan.
The company has a Specialty Division (Plastics/Medical) located in Sault Ste. Marie, MI.
source_title: TN Americas Holdings, Inc.. United States,Georgia,Cumming, Steel/Iron Company | ScrapMonster

facts: A series of thefts involving entire truckloads of Tesla batteries are ongoing, with authorities investigating.
The thefts threaten supply chains and raise security concerns for Tesla suppliers.
Authorities are investigating a series of thefts involving entire truckloads of Tesla batteries, which have occurred over the past three weeks.
The thefts are believed to be targeted and have raised concerns about security at Tesla supplier facilities and logistics operations.
The incidents have disrupted supply chains and prompted increased security measures.
At least five truckloads of Tesla batteries have been stolen from different locations across California and Nevada since early March.
The stolen batteries are believed to be destined for Tesla manufacturing plants or distribution centers.
No arrests have been made, and the suspects are still at large, with investigations ongoing.
Investigations are confirmed by the California Highway Patrol (CHP) and local police departments.
Tesla has not officially commented on the thefts but has reportedly increased security protocols at its supplier facilities.
Industry analysts note that the stolen batteries are valuable commodities, and their theft could impact Tesla’s production schedules if the supply chain disruptions continue.
The stolen cargo includes large lithium-ion battery packs, which are critical components for Tesla’s vehicle and energy storage products.
The ongoing thefts highlight vulnerabilities in Tesla’s logistics and supply chain security, especially concerning high-value components like batteries.
If such thefts continue, they could lead to delays in vehicle production and increased costs.
The incidents raise broader concerns about cargo security in the EV manufacturing sector, which relies heavily on secure transportation of sensitive materials.
Over the past year, there has been a rise in thefts targeting high-value EV components, including batteries and electric drivetrains.
Industry sources indicate that organized theft rings may be involved, motivated by the high resale value of lithium-ion batteries on black markets.
Tesla’s rapid expansion and increasing battery inventory make it a target for such criminal activities.

California Highway Patrol spokesperson stated: “We are actively investigating multiple thefts involving large quantities of Tesla batteries. No arrests have been made, but we are pursuing several leads.”
It is not yet confirmed who is responsible for the thefts or whether they are connected to organized crime.
The specific methods used by thieves and whether the incidents are coordinated remain unknown.
Authorities have not identified any suspects or disclosed whether the thefts are part of a larger pattern.
Law enforcement agencies are expected to continue investigations and may release further details about suspects or patterns.
Tesla is likely to implement additional security protocols at its supply chain points.
The industry will monitor whether these thefts escalate or are contained, and whether new security legislation or regulations are introduced to prevent similar incidents.
While no official figures have been released, industry sources suggest that the thefts could cause delays if the supply of batteries is disrupted.
Tesla is reportedly working to mitigate impact through increased security and inventory management.
It is currently unknown whether the thefts are organized or linked to broader criminal networks.
Tesla is reportedly increasing security at its supplier facilities, including surveillance and personnel checks, but specific measures have not been publicly detailed.
If thefts continue or escalate, there could be supply shortages or delays, potentially affecting production schedules and delivery timelines.
Recovery efforts are ongoing, but it is too early to determine if the stolen batteries will be recovered or if the perpetrators will be apprehended.
source_title: Someone Keeps Stealing Entire Truckloads Of Tesla Batteries - Commute Volt

facts: Kristal Lee and her husband bought a house in Gaston County, North Carolina two years ago, envisioning it as a 'forever home'.
A planned lithium mine is bringing Lee sleepless nights.
The nearby area is earmarked for a $1.2 billion project to produce battery grade lithium for US electric vehicle supply chains.
This site is one of only a few such sites in the country.
Kristal Lee expressed anxiety about the Piedmont Lithium project, noting residents do not have an option to move right now due to the economy and inflation.
President Joe Biden is working to build domestic EV and battery industries.
Companies are seeking lithium supplies within the United States, which holds major deposits in Nevada, North Carolina, and California.
Biden's aims include furthering the energy transition and lowering dependence on China, which supplies the bulk of global lithium-ion battery exports.
Lithium-ion battery demand is projected to grow nearly 30 percent annually from 2022-2030, according to McKinsey & Company.
Pushback against Piedmont and other mining companies underscores how officials' efforts leave some residents skeptical.
Aimee Boulanger, executive director at the Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance (IRMA), identified water pollution and reduced water supplies as top mining risks.
Boulanger stated that moving rock opens up land and risks soil and metals washing into streams, rivers, and drinking water.
IRMA certifies mines for major companies.
Lee, who is 41 and has five children, worries about dust and noise from the planned open pit mine where Piedmont intends to blast once or twice daily as needed.
Piedmont maintains that blasting would not cause structural damage to nearby properties.
Neighbors fear water contamination or supply disruption.
Piedmont plans to test and treat water draining into its pits before using or discharging it.
The company's backup water plans involve drilling new wells, providing municipal water access, or supplying bottled water, though these measures have failed to quell resident jitters.
Boulanger emphasized the need to ensure mines do not cause harm that outweighs or outlasts their benefits.
Resident Jim McMahan cited the mine's expected decade-long lifespan as a concern.
McMahan, a 65-year-old retiree and farmer, noted that while the project will provide temporary jobs, they will eventually disappear along with local farms.

Locke Bell, a 73-year-old retired county district attorney, acknowledged reducing emissions is a legitimate argument for new industries but warned against reckless destruction of earth and waters for minimal lithium production.
Piedmont Lithium received a North Carolina state mining permit this month but still requires further local permissions.
Chad Brown, chair of the Gaston County Board of Commissioners, stated officials must ensure the project is done correctly and assured residents before proceeding.
Brown indicated a local decision could be reached by November.
Lee learned about the mine a year after moving in when Piedmont mailed her family coffee and a note.
The Piedmont Lithium project is expected to produce 30,000 metric tons of lithium hydroxide annually, significantly boosting US capacity.
World's top lithium producer Albemarle is working to reopen a dormant mine in Kings Mountain, North Carolina.
Albemarle stated it benefited from policies like the Inflation Reduction Act and received nearly $150 million via a Department of Energy grant to support costs.
President Biden has struggled to win over Americans with his economic policies despite triggering a surge in clean energy investments.
Bell noted the situation leaves a 'negative' view of the government's clean energy push in Gaston County.
Lee supports clean energy but is less convinced of EVs due to a lack of charging infrastructure and high costs even with tax rebates.
McMahan described the lithium mining process as 'invasive'.
McMahan stated he does not feel the push for electric vehicles is being done in the way it should be.
source_title: Push for new US lithium mine leaves some Americans wary - Tuko.co.ke

facts: Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc. (TIESNA) will create more than 250 new jobs while expanding the Toyota Industries Corporation campus in Pendergrass with a new manufacturing facility valued at $69 million in investment.

TIESNA is a subsidiary of Toyota Industries, which is headquartered in Japan and develops and manufactures automobile-related products, including car air-conditioning compressors and car electronics components for worldwide carmakers.
The Group has operated in Pendergrass since 2004, starting with TD Automotive Compressor Georgia, LLC (TACG), followed by Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America, Co. (TICA) in 2012.
TACG and TICA currently support more than 400 jobs in Jackson County.
TIESNA’s new facility will be located at the 1700 Valentine Industrial Parkway campus in Pendergrass.
The new facility will produce DC-to-DC converters, which converts high voltage to low voltage to supply power to auxiliary equipment.
Operations are expected to begin in 2025.
Jackson County Commission Chair Tom Crow stated that TIESNA is Toyota Industries' third related company in Jackson County and praised its outstanding corporate citizenship.

Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, welcomed TIESNA’s expansion as part of Georgia’s growing automaking sector and noted significant investments from Toyota Industries and other automotive suppliers are delivering career opportunities.

Senior Regional Project Manager Kristen Miller represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce, Georgia Power, the Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that building long-term relationships is a strategic way to give Georgia a competitive advantage when companies are looking to expand.
Maintaining 50 years of representation in Japan has allowed GDEcD to keep direct communication lines open with corporate headquarters, including Toyota Industries’.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned itself as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
source_title: Toyota Industries Corporation Expands Georgia Presence

facts: The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) and Governor Brian P. Kemp thanked companies across the state for supplying personal protective equipment (PPE) and health care supplies to fight COVID-19.
Governor Kemp signed legislation encouraging additional PPE production in Georgia and aiding businesses whose job numbers were harmed during the pandemic.
Georgia reported roughly $574 million in new investments and 3,629 new jobs created in July prior to the immediate pandemic response.
On June 30, Governor Kemp signed legislation offering economic relief to companies that created new jobs while making PPE and other critical health care supplies.
The new legislation generated strong interest from industry.
Governor Kemp expressed gratitude for manufacturers' resilience during the crisis and commended the General Assembly for supporting measures to secure Georgia's future.
Georgia businesses adjusted operations, retooled machines, and pivoted to manufacture critical health care supplies during COVID-19.
Some companies tapped assistance from GDEcD’s Centers of Innovation and Global Commerce divisions and their networks.
The Atlanta Business Chronicle highlighted these manufacturing efforts in a recent article titled “Manufacturing companies create products to fight Covid-19”.

The Georgia legislature, with support from Governor Kemp, Lieutenant Governor Geoff Duncan, and House Speaker David Ralston, passed a tax credit to aid PPE manufacturers, including those who did not traditionally manufacture PPE or hand sanitizer.
The new credit aims to incentivize more PPE and hand sanitizer production in Georgia to enhance its position as a national leader in advanced manufacturing.
Georgia’s GDP was $64.7 billion in 2019 due to strength across multiple manufacturing sectors.
The Georgia Department of Labor estimated there were 387,400 manufacturing jobs in the state as of June 2020.
Changes were made to existing job tax credit programs to give relief to companies whose recently created jobs were impacted by the pandemic.
Existing Georgia companies that claimed Job Tax Credits or Quality Jobs Tax Credits in 2019 may carry over their 2019 job creation numbers to 2020 and/or 2021.
This change ensures impacted businesses can continue receiving tax credits at 2019 levels while returning employment to pre-COVID levels.
The credit expires on Jan. 1, 2025.
The Georgia Department of Community Affairs’ Board of Directors updated its policies to account for the new legislation.
GDEcD led efforts to support businesses, identify companies restructuring operations for PPE production, and coordinate with existing producers.
The Georgia Suppliers Interactive Map and List, compiled by GDEcD’s Centers of Innovation, includes more than 320 Georgia companies providing protective supplies.
The interactive map displays business locations and allows sorting supplies by category.
The free tool allows Georgia businesses to support each other while protecting employees and patrons, keeping valuable dollars in the state.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson appreciated Governor Kemp and the legislature for incentivizing PPE production and safeguarding job creators.
Wilson stated these pro-business measures will help ensure Georgia’s future is bright as they continue to create good jobs and prepare for public health challenges.
H.B. 846 covers the PPE credit and carryover option legislation.

links.context: Atlanta Business Chronicle article highlighting manufacturing efforts
Georgia Suppliers Interactive Map and List

source_title: GDEcD expresses gratitude to PPE providers during pandemic

facts: Trenton Pressing completed a $12 million expansion of its manufacturing operations in Dade County.
The expansion adds 100,000 square feet to the company's manufacturing and assembly operations.
State Representative Mike Cameron (R-Rossville) applauded the completion of the expansion.
Rep. Cameron stated that the Southeast United States has become a magnet for automotive brands and suppliers over the last two decades.
Rep. Cameron noted that Georgia leads by example in attracting automotive investment by investing in its workforce and making business conditions better.
The 100,000-square-foot expansion represents a milestone for Trenton Pressing's growth.
The expansion enhances Trenton Pressing's ability to supply high-quality components to its clients.
Trenton Pressing supplies components to brands including BMW, Club Car, and ZF.
Trenton Pressing is now one of the leading employers in Dade County.
The company's workforce exceeds 200 employees.
Trenton Pressing specializes in metal stamping, welding processes, and complex assemblies.
The company provides comprehensive turnkey contract manufacturing solutions.
Trenton Pressing offers warehousing and shipping services to its clients.
The company caters to both automotive and non-automotive clients.
Representative Mike Cameron represents District 1, which includes Dade County and portions of Walker County.
Rep. Cameron was elected to the Georgia House of Representatives in 2020.
He currently serves as Vice Chairman of the Human Relations & Aging committee.
He serves as Secretary of the Interstate Cooperation committee.
He serves on the Appropriations Subcommittee on Human Resources.
He serves on the Agriculture & Consumer Affairs committee.
He serves on the Budget and Fiscal Affairs Oversight committee.
He serves on the Industry and Labor committees.
