facts: Trenton Pressing broke ground on a new $10 million manufacturing facility in Trenton, Ga., on April 6, 2023.
The new facility will be 105,000 square feet and will allow for expanded manufacturing and assembly services, significantly increasing the organization’s manufacturing capacity.
Construction on the new facility is expected to be completed by December of 2023.

In just over two years prior to the announcement, Trenton Pressing purchased a struggling manufacturing company and completely turned it around through a reorganization of management, technology and equipment upgrades, and personnel training.
The expanded facility will allow the company to better service its growth and meet the manufacturing needs of its clients, which include BMW, Club Car, ZF, and more.
Trenton Pressing is currently one of the top five employers in Dade County with 220 employees.
The company plans to add 20 more jobs with the facility expansion.
Trenton Pressing began operations in Dade County in 2021.
Trenton Pressing provides metal stamping processes, welding and assembly processes, warehousing and shipping.
The company offers a wide range of products and services including automatic and hand metal stamping, welding, engineering, and product assembly.
Hans Pfunder, chief operating officer for Trenton Pressing, stated: “We are extremely grateful for Dade County’s support throughout this expansion process.”
Hans Pfunder noted: “It was exciting to see the tremendous growth over the past two years after the reorganization process. This achievement speaks to the dedication and commitment of our employees.”

Dade County Executive Ted Rumley stated: “Trenton Pressing has been a great community partner since it began operations in our county in 2021. They have not only provided great jobs, but they have also supported our schools and the environment.”

Trenton Mayor Alex Case stated: “Trenton Pressing has been a great asset to our city since they first came here just over two years ago. We are grateful that they are providing us with much needed jobs and also for their overall commitment to help improve the quality of life for our citizens.”

Jeff Mullis, president and CEO of the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Agency and retired state senator, stated: “I want to congratulate Trenton Pressing on their expansion efforts and commend them for adding jobs in the Northwest Georgia area.”
source_title: Trenton Pressing breaks ground on new $10 million manufacturing facility – Discover Dade

facts: The Kansas Factory aims for an annual capacity of 32 GWh.
Panasonic Energy has begun mass production at its new automotive lithium-ion battery factory in Kansas.
The Kansas Factory is built on a site spanning approximately 300 acres, equivalent to the size of more than 225 American football fields.
This facility represents Panasonic Energy’s second EV battery production site in North America, following the Nevada Factory.
The Nevada Factory has been operating since 2017 with a current annual capacity of approximately 41 GWh.
Once fully operational, the Kansas Factory will bring Panasonic Energy’s total U.S.-based production capacity to approximately 73 GWh.
The new plant aims to meet growing demand from automotive customers expanding their electric vehicle production.
Panasonic Energy focuses on producing high-performance, high-quality cylindrical lithium-ion batteries to meet North American EV range, capacity, and safety demands.
Starting with EV battery production in Japan, Panasonic Energy has expanded its advanced battery production to North America as an industry leader.
The company has approximately 30 years of experience in lithium-ion battery manufacturing.
Panasonic Energy has around 8 years of production expertise at its Nevada Factory in Sparks, Nevada.
The Nevada facility employs over 4,000 people and has delivered more than 11 billion cells to date.
By introducing labor-saving production lines, the Kansas Factory is expected to achieve approximately 20 percent higher productivity than the Nevada Factory.
In the near future, Panasonic Energy plans to introduce products using advanced materials that will increase cell capacity by around five percent.
The facility strengthens the domestic EV supply chain, enhances competitiveness in North American battery manufacturing, and supports sustainability commitments.
As the largest economic development project in Kansas state history, the factory will create up to 4,000 direct jobs.
The project will create approximately 8,000 total jobs, including those in supplier and related industries.
Panasonic Energy is collaborating with the University of Kansas to promote long-term industry-academia partnerships for technological advancement and specialized talent development.

The collaboration aims to contribute to the economic growth of De Soto and the surrounding Kansas region, the revitalization of the U.S. manufacturing sector, skilled workforce cultivation, and long-term economic strength.
Kazuo Tadanobu, CEO of Panasonic Energy, stated that the opening of the Kansas Factory marks a major milestone in scaling advanced battery production in the United States.
Tadanobu noted the achievement required strong support from local partners and the State of Kansas.
The company is using the facility to accelerate the shift to electrification, reinforce regional supply chains, and nurture the next generation of battery talent.
Tadanobu described the facility as a foundation for long-term collaboration and innovation in the U.S.
Panasonic Energy’s high-capacity lithium-ion cells feature an industry-leading volumetric energy density of 800 Wh/L.
The company's cells have powered approximately 3.7 million EVs, with around 19 billion cells supplied as of March 2025.
The batteries have set a standard for safety and reliability, having caused no vehicle recalls to date.
Panasonic Energy remains committed to supporting CO₂ emissions reduction through the widespread adoption of electric vehicles.
The Kansas Factory is operated by Panasonic Energy Corporation of North America.
The facility is located at 10301 Astra Parkway, De Soto, KS 66018.
The factory opening is scheduled for July 2025.
The plant will produce cylindrical lithium-ion batteries for automotive use, specifically 2170 cells.
The total site area is approximately 300 acres (about 1.2 million m²).
The building area is approximately 4.7 million ft² (about 440,000 m²).
The facility will employ up to approximately 4,000 personnel.
Executive leadership includes Chief Executive Officer Yasuaki Takamoto and President and Chief Operating Officer Allan Swan.
Panasonic Energy Co., Ltd. was established in April 2022 as part of the Panasonic Group's switch to an operating company system.
The company provides innovative battery technology-based products globally, including automotive lithium-ion batteries, storage battery systems, and dry batteries.
source_title: Panasonic Energy Begins Mass Production at New Automotive Lithium-ion Battery Factory in Kansas, Aiming for Annual Capacity of 32 GWh to Accelerate U.S. Local Production | Panasonic North America - United States

facts: GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson was named the 2023 “Georgian of the Year” by Georgia Trend.
The award recognizes Commissioner Wilson as the top salesperson for the state and commends his work promoting the advantages of Georgia to attract investments and create jobs.
Former Governor Nathan Deal appointed Wilson as economic development Commissioner six years prior to January 2023.
Georgia maintained its place as the No. 1 state in the nation to do business, earning the recognition for the ninth consecutive year in 2022.
Former Georgia Governor Sonny Perdue credited Wilson with helping make Georgia one of the best places to grow a business by communicating the actions of the governor and legislature.
In FY22, investments in expansions and new locations totaled more than $21.2 billion.
The FY22 investments would create 51,132 direct new jobs in the state, setting new records for Georgia.
GDEcD’s work providing solutions for companies and keeping communication open helped Georgia land two of its largest-ever investments in FY22.
Lee Thomas, Director of the Georgia Film Office, was recognized by Georgia Trend for his contributions to the state.
David Nuckolls, Executive Director of the Center of Innovation, was also recognized by Georgia Trend for his contributions to the state.
GDEcD is described as the “quarterback” that brings partners across the state together to make business happen.

facts: French automotive supplier Valeo SA presented its full-year 2025 results on February 26, 2026.
Total sales reached €20.9 billion with a 0.5% like-for-like growth.
Operating margin improved to 4.7% from 4.3% in 2024.
Free cash flow after net financial interest reached a record €371 million, marking a 50% increase year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA margin reached 14.7%, surpassing the guided range of 13.5-14.5%.
Operating margin of 4.7% landed within the 4.5-5.5% target range.
Operating margin increased from 1.7% in 2021 to 4.7% in 2025, driven by gross margin expansion of 1.2 percentage points versus 2024 and 2.3 percentage points versus 2023.
Selling, general and administrative expenses declined 5% compared to 2024 and 10% versus 2023.
Net attributable income reached €200 million, up 23% year-over-year.
The company faced a 43% effective tax rate partly due to cash repatriation taxes.
Free cash flow after net financial interest evolved from negative €146 million in 2021 to positive €371 million in 2025.

Improvement stems from higher profitability with adjusted EBITDA reaching 14.7% of sales, capital expenditure reduction of 30% or €348 million versus 2024 to 3.8% of sales, and gross R&D reduction of 7.3% or €193 million year-over-year.
The company generated €756 million in free cash flow before exceptional restructuring costs, comfortably within the €700-800 million guidance range.
Net debt declined to €4.0 billion at year-end from €4.2 billion at mid-year.
The company maintains a leverage ratio of 1.3x and gearing ratio of 121%.
Valeo holds €2.5 billion in cash and cash equivalents alongside €1.6 billion in undrawn credit lines.
Valeo secured €24.6 billion in order intake during 2025, with €11.8 billion in the first half and €12.8 billion in the second half.
The cumulative 2022-2025 order intake ratio reached 1.5x, with Power division representing 38%, Brain 37%, and Light 25% of the total.
China emerged as a strategic bright spot despite near-term sales challenges.
The company achieved a 2.8x order intake ratio with Chinese OEMs, with these manufacturers now representing 63% of China order intake compared to 54% of current OEM sales.
Major contract wins included an innovative dual-layer HVAC system, 5-in-1 power electronics module, pillar-to-pillar head-up display, and new generation dual inverter solution.
Valeo secured its first €225 million business in Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), marking entry into an adjacent growth market.
The Power division generated €10.5 billion in sales with 2% like-for-like OEM growth, underperforming the market by 2 percentage points.
Power division operating margin improved significantly to 4.2% from 2.9% in 2024.
The Brain division reported €5.0 billion in sales with a 6% like-for-like decline in OEM sales, underperforming by 10 percentage points.
Brain division operating margin compressed slightly to 5.4% from 5.8%, impacted by delayed production starts in North America.
The Brain division secured key positions in software-defined vehicles through large contracts.
The Light division posted €5.4 billion in sales with flat like-for-like OEM growth, underperforming by 4 percentage points.
Light division operating margin reached 5.0% versus 5.5% in 2024.
The Light division experienced successful product launches in Europe and China with momentum accelerating in the second half.
Valeo achieved its 2025 ESG objectives across multiple dimensions, earning an A rating from CDP for both climate change and water security.
CO2 emissions reached 39.4 MtCO2 equivalent with an intensity ratio of 1.88 ktCO2 per million euros.
The company achieved 27% women representation in management committees and a frequency rate of 0.69 for workplace safety incidents, both meeting 2025 targets.
Valeo's One site, One cause initiative proved successful, with 92% of suppliers assessed on sustainability practices.
Valeo maintains strong ESG ratings including MSCI AA, S&P Global 72/100, and Sustainalytics low risk at 17.3.
For 2026, Valeo projects sales of €20-21 billion with flat organic growth in OEM sales.
Operating margin is expected to range between 4.7-5.3% for 2026, maintained through price discipline and continued cost reduction.
Free cash flow after net financial interest is guided above €400 million for 2026 with potential further R&D efficiency gains.
The company remains focused on its Elevate 2028 strategic plan targeting €22-24 billion in sales, 6-7% operating margin, and over €500 million in free cash flow after net financial interest.
The Elevate 2028 plan aims to achieve a leverage ratio below 1.0x adjusted EBITDA, positioning Valeo for investment-grade credit metrics.
The restructuring program continues with confirmed savings of €300 million annual run rate by 2026.
Restructuring costs in P&L are expected to decline to approximately €20 million in 2026 from €110 million in 2025.
Cash outflows for restructuring will remain elevated at approximately €150 million before declining to €100 million in 2027.
Valeo proposed a dividend of €0.44 per share for 2025, continuing the progressive dividend policy that has seen payouts increase from €0.35 in 2021.
The dividend will be submitted for approval at the May 21, 2026 shareholders meeting.
Valeo stock declined 1.75% following the results presentation, closing at €13.13.
Market reaction likely reflects concerns about flat sales guidance for 2026 and ongoing challenges in China, where OEM sales declined 7% like-for-like despite 10% production growth.
The company faces continued headwinds including supply chain disruptions, particularly component shortages, and market volatility affecting production volumes.
Geographic market dynamics require careful strategic execution navigating the rapidly evolving Chinese automotive landscape dominated by local manufacturers.
source_title: Latest Company News - Investing.com

facts: Valeo breaks ground on a $225 million high-tech manufacturing facility in McAllen, Texas.
The project will produce next-generation vehicle computing systems for General Motors.
The facility will create up to 500 high-wage jobs in South Texas.
The new facility represents a major expansion of Valeo’s North American footprint.

The investment is aligned with the company’s Elevate 2028 plan, which focuses on accelerating innovation in electrification, advanced driver assistance systems, and software-defined vehicle architecture.
The McAllen plant will manufacture central compute systems described by the company as the “brain of the vehicle,” enabling safer, more connected, and more intelligent mobility.
Jeffrey Shay, Group President of Valeo North America, stated that the investment reflects a commitment to innovation and supporting customers in the transition toward software-defined vehicles.
Jeffrey Shay noted that McAllen offers the workforce, community, and readiness needed to deliver advanced technology at scale.
The project marks one of the largest industrial investments in the history of Hidalgo County.
Local leaders emphasized that the project was secured through coordinated execution, infrastructure readiness, and a business-friendly environment.
Ike Tawil, McAllen City Manager, called Valeo’s investment transformative for the community and the largest single industrial investment in Hidalgo County history.
The facility is being developed at the Nearshoring Industrial Park in McAllen by CiL Capital.
CiL Capital is led by President and CEO Joaquin Spamer.

The site was designed as a shovel-ready, build-to-suit environment capable of supporting advanced manufacturing requirements, including cleanroom production, specialized power infrastructure, and high-speed data connectivity.
Joaquin Spamer stated that the park was built to respond to the nearshoring movement and deliver at the speed of business.
The McAllen Economic Development Corporation worked closely with Valeo and partners to align workforce, infrastructure, and operational requirements.
Ralph Garcia, Chief Operating Officer of the McAllen Economic Development Corporation, called the project the largest industrial opportunity the region has ever seen.
McAllen Mayor Javier Villalobos noted the broader economic impact, highlighting the evolution of the local economy from agriculture and services to advanced manufacturing.
The facility is expected to strengthen North American automotive supply chains through its proximity to Mexico’s manufacturing base and integration into cross-border logistics networks.
Valeo already maintains a significant presence in the United States with multiple production sites and research and development centers, operating globally in nearly 30 countries.
The project will support workforce development through partnerships with regional institutions, including the University of Texas Rio Grande Valley and South Texas College.
These partnerships will create pathways for students into high-skill careers in advanced manufacturing and automotive technology.

Ralph Garcia later served as Chief Executive Officer of the McAllen Economic Development Corporation and emphasized that McAllen responded quickly to a highly competitive U.S. project, positioning the Rio Grande Valley as a serious contender for advanced manufacturing and nearshoring opportunities.
source_title: Valeo Breaks Ground on $225 Million High-Tech Manufacturing Facility in McAllen, Texas

facts: Valeo is a global tech company designing breakthrough solutions to reinvent mobility.
Valeo serves as an automotive supplier partner to automakers and new mobility actors worldwide.
The company's vision focuses on intuitive driving and reducing CO2 emissions.
Valeo Group is one of the world's largest Automotive suppliers, focused on innovative products, development, and customer satisfaction.
The Santena plant operates under Valeo's Brain Division, which leads the market in parking and driving assistance components.
Valeo Smart Cookpit Italy specializes in switches, Human Machine Interface (HMI) control panels, and displays.
A Purchasing trainee supports the Purchasing team in daily activities related to supplier management and component sourcing processes.
Trainees work closely with the Purchasing Manager and other team members to contribute to department objectives.
Key tasks include data analysis, market research, supporting negotiations with suppliers, and helping implement new purchasing strategies.
Responsibilities involve supporting project awards during the CAA stage by aligning competitive costs with segment strategy.
Trainees build up project supplier target panels from pre-SOCO alongside the commodity organization.
Employees must define and control communication flows between project teams and suppliers.
Daily operations must comply with internal audit rules, including Financial audit and V5000 audit.
Desired qualifications include enrollment in an Engineering degree course.
Candidates need strong communication skills, teamwork ability, English fluency, independence, initiative, attention to detail, and advantageous knowledge of procurement practices.
Valeo employs more than 20,000 engineers dedicated to Research & Development.
The company fosters a multi-cultural environment that values diversity and international collaboration.
Valeo has over 100,000 colleagues working across 31 countries.
Corporate Knights ranks Valeo as the number one company in the automotive sector for sustainable development.
source_title:
Purchasing Trainee	at 	Valeo - 
Italy | iAgora.com

facts: Valeo plans to invest more than €200 million in India under its global Elevate 2028 roadmap.
Valeo targets a jump in India revenues from roughly €220 million currently to €700 million by 2028.
Valeo has a longer-term ambition of crossing €1 billion revenue beyond the 2028 period.
A large share of the €200 million investment will flow into Valeo’s Power division.
Valeo’s Power division houses electrification technologies including electric motors, inverters, on-board chargers, DC-DC converters and battery cooling systems.
EV penetration in India is currently at around 4 per cent.
EV penetration in India is projected to rise sharply over the next decade.
Valeo expects electrified vehicles, including hybrids, to account for about 25% of the Indian market by 2032.
Around 25% of Valeo India’s Power division revenue already comes from electrified products.
Localisation levels in electrification stand at about 50%, compared with 80 to 90% in legacy ICE products.
Valeo is expanding capacity at its Pune and Chennai plants.
Valeo is deepening domestic sourcing as volumes build.
Valeo’s Brain division is focused on ADAS technologies such as parking sensors, cameras and radar.
India’s tightening safety regulations are driving higher demand for advanced safety systems.
Rising SUV penetration in India is now above 60% of new orders.
Valeo has installed electronic parking sensor lines locally.
Valeo is industrialising camera production, with manufacturing expected to begin in 2026.
Radar localisation is planned as market readiness improves.
Regulatory changes around 2028 are expected, including features such as automatic emergency braking and lane departure warning systems, to further accelerate ADAS adoption.
Valeo operates six production sites in India across Pune, Chennai and Sanand.
Valeo is supported by a major R&D centre in Chennai.
Valeo employs over 7,000 people in India.
Valeo’s Indian workforce includes more than 3,000 engineers and over 1,000 software specialists.
Under its local-for-local strategy, Valeo aims to manufacture in India everything it sells in India.
Valeo works with more than 200 Indian suppliers.
Valeo is expanding its footprint in two-wheelers.
Valeo has partnered with Hero to introduce advanced rider assistance systems, adapting ADAS technologies to enhance motorcycle safety.
India currently contributes around 1% to Valeo’s global revenue.
Under Elevate 2028, Valeo intends to significantly raise India's share of global revenue.
Christophe Périllat, CEO of Valeo, stated: “We are absolutely determined to grow in India,” underscoring confidence in the country’s accelerating technology shift.
source_title: Valeo plans €200 million India investment, targets €700 million revenue by 2028 - Business News | The Financial Express

facts: Volkswagen Group will work with Valeo and Mobileye to improve driving assistance systems on future models.
The collaboration applies to vehicles built on the MQB platform.
The affected models will utilize internal-combustion engines.
The companies announced the partnership on March 25.
source_title: VW teams with Valeo, Mobileye for Level 2 ADAS on new ICE models - Automotive News

facts: Georgia Power owns a network of electric generation plants, hydro dams, and multiple solar energy facilities spread across the state.
The network provides safe, clean, reliable, and affordable energy to 2.7 million customers.
Georgia Power owns two nuclear facilities named Hatch and Vogtle.
These nuclear facilities provide about 29 percent of the electricity used in Georgia.
The plants are managed by Southern Nuclear, a sister company owned by Southern Company that specializes in nuclear operations.
The Hatch facility is located in Waynesboro, Georgia.
The Hatch facility has a current total generating capacity of approximately 3,547 MW.
Hatch Unit 1 began commercial operation on June 1, 1987.
Hatch Unit 2 began commercial operation on May 20, 1989.
Hatch Unit 3 began commercial operation on July 31, 2023.
Hatch Unit 4 began commercial operation on April 29, 2024.
The Vogtle facility is located in Baxley, Georgia.
The Vogtle facility has a current total generating capacity of approximately 1,848 MW.
Vogtle Unit 1 began commercial operation in December 1975.
Vogtle Unit 2 began commercial operation in September 1979.
Georgia Power has constructed and is operating 16 solar farms and additional demo facilities across the state of Georgia.
Many of these solar facilities are built in partnership with military installations.
The largest solar facility, with a capacity of 128 MW, is located at Robins Air Force Base in Warner Robins, Georgia.
Other solar facilities add carbon-free energy to the grid and supply renewable energy in support of the Community Solar program.
The Comer solar facility has a capacity of 2,160 kW.
The Dalton solar facility has a capacity of 6,508 kW.
The Falcons Solar facility has a capacity of 1,019 kW.
The UGA solar facility has a capacity of 984 kW.
The Fort Benning solar facility has a capacity of 30,005 kW.
The Fort Valley solar facility has a capacity of 10,800 kW.
The LaGrange solar facility has a capacity of 800 kW.
The MCLB solar facility has a capacity of 31,161 kW.
The Moody AFB solar facility has a capacity of 49,500 kW.
The Robins AFB solar facility has a capacity of 128,000 kW.
The Tri-County EMC solar facility has a capacity of 1,008 kW.
The Fort Gordon solar facility has a capacity of 30,000 kW.
The Fort Stewart solar facility has a capacity of 30,000 kW.
The Guyton solar facility has a capacity of 3,600 kW.
The Kings Bay solar facility has a capacity of 30,161 kW.
The Waynesboro solar facility has a capacity of 2,400 kW.
Georgia Power's natural gas facilities have a combined generating capacity of over 5,000 MW.
The McDonough-Atkinson facility in Smyrna, GA has approximately 2,520 MW capacity using 3x Combined Cycle technology.
The McIntosh facility in Rincon, GA has approximately 1,376 MW capacity using 2x Combined Cycle and 8x Combustion Turbine / Simple Cycle technology.
The Yates facility in Newnan, GA has approximately 724 MW capacity using 2x Combustion Turbine / Simple Cycle technology.
The Robins facility at Robins Air Force Base, GA has approximately 184 MW capacity using 2x Combustion Turbine / Simple Cycle technology.
The McManus facility in Brunswick, GA has approximately 558 MW capacity using 2x Combustion Turbine / Simple Cycle technology.

Georgia Power operates 15 hydroelectric generating plants distributed across the state in three groups: the North Georgia Hydro Group, the Chattahoochee Hydro Group, and the Central Georgia Hydro Group.
The Burton hydro plant has a capacity of 8.1 MW.
The Nacoochee hydro plant has a capacity of 5.5 MW.
The Terrora hydro plant has a capacity of 20.8 MW.
The Tallulah Falls hydro plant has a capacity of 72 MW.
The Tugalo hydro plant has a capacity of 58 MW.
The Yonah hydro plant has a capacity of 22.5 MW.
The Morgan Falls hydro plant has a capacity of 16.8 MW.
The Bartletts Ferry hydro plant has a capacity of 187.1 MW.
The Flint River hydro plant has a capacity of 5.4 MW.
The Goat Rock hydro plant has a capacity of 40.5 MW.
The North Highlands hydro plant has a capacity of 29.6 MW.
The Oliver hydro plant has a capacity of 60.1 MW.
The Lloyd Shoals hydro plant has a capacity of 18 MW.
The Sinclair hydro plant has a capacity of 45 MW.
The Wallace hydro plant has a capacity of 321.3 MW.
The Rocky Mountain hydro plant has a capacity of 229.4 MW.
Georgia Power holds a 25.4% ownership stake in the hydroelectric plants, while Oglethorpe Power Corporation holds 74.6%.
Plant Scherer is located in Juliette, Georgia and provides for 18 percent of Georgia Power's capacity mix.
Plant Scherer has a current total generating capacity of approximately 3,720 MW.
Plant Scherer Unit 1 began commercial operation in 1982.
Plant Scherer Unit 2 began commercial operation in 1984.
Plant Scherer Unit 3 began commercial operation in 1987.
Plant Scherer Unit 4 operated from 1989 to 2022 before being decommissioned.
Plant Bowen is located in Bartow County, Georgia and provides for 18 percent of Georgia Power's capacity mix.
Plant Bowen has a current total generating capacity of approximately 3,376 MW.
Plant Bowen Unit 1 began commercial operation in 1971.
Plant Bowen Unit 2 began commercial operation in 1972.
Plant Bowen Unit 3 began commercial operation in 1974.
Plant Bowen Unit 4 began commercial operation in 1975.
source_title: Plants Owned or Operated by Georgia Power

facts: Valeo has been selected by a leading Chinese New Energy Vehicle (NEV) manufacturer to supply its new 5-in-1 Deep Integration Power Electronics Module for future electrified powertrains.
The compact and cost-optimised unit integrates five core power electronics functions into a single box.
Production of the module is set for 2026.
The module achieves around a 20% weight reduction.
The module has been fast-tracked from concept to production-ready in under a year.
The module combines an inverter, OBC, DC/DC converter, power distribution unit, and eFuse into a single compact module with a shared SoC architecture.
The module supports trends in electric vehicle development such as EE architecture consolidation, system integration, and platform-based engineering.
The module provides simplified wiring for engineers.
The module reduces system complexity.
The module improves thermal and energy efficiency.
The module lowers validation effort, which is key for next-gen EV design and scalability.
source_title: Major OEM Selects Valeo’s 5-in-1 Power Electronics Module

facts: Retrofitting transforms an I.C.E. vehicle into an electric one by replacing the petrol/diesel engine and fuel tank with an electric motor and battery.
Valeo's 'I Care for the Planet' program aims to reduce the environmental impact of the automotive aftermarket.

Regulations for emissions have become increasingly severe around the world, and major cities are implementing low-emission zones and sometimes outright diesel bans to combat air pollution and meet climate targets.
Most light commercial vehicles (LCVs) run on diesel, so city entry bans directly impact businesses, fleet owners, and small businesses that must urgently switch to electric vehicles.
Buying new electric vehicles is expensive, making retrofitting a perfect alternative.
Valeo, a leading automotive supplier in Electrification, offers a range of products and solutions for EVs that can be used for retrofitting conventional vehicles into EVs.
Products include e-motors, OBC DCDC, Inverters, HV heaters, and Electric driven compressors (EDC).
These components can all be integrated into retrofit projects to convert Internal Combustion Engine (I.C.E.) LCVs into electric ones.
The cost saving of converting existing vehicles into electric vehicles is estimated to be about 50% the cost of buying a new eLCV.
Retrofitting contributes to automotive decarbonization and saves at least 50% CO2 emissions over 10 years.
By not scrapping the vehicle, retrofitting also contributes to the circular economy.
Governments are increasingly offering incentives and support programs to facilitate retrofitting in recognition of its importance in transitioning to cleaner transportation.
Some governments, like France, are gradually reducing subsidies for buying new electric vehicles, which further enhances the economic appeal of retrofitting.
In Europe, there are more than 30 million active LCVs facing the challenge of entering urban spaces.

Valeo is well-placed to support the retrofit industry and help convert part of the LCV car parc into electric vehicles for the future due to its automotive electrification expertise and established relationships.
source_title: Electric Retrofit: The Valeo Way | Valeo Service

facts: The 2030 CO2 reduction targets are unreachable without automotive hybridization.

Valeo's 48-Volt powertrain system architecture is a hybridization solution suited to all types of vehicles, allowing significant gains compared to a 12 Volts hybridization system and a full electrification solution for light vehicles.
A 48V mild hybrid system is a hybrid electric vehicle technology that uses a 48-volt electrical system in addition to the conventional 12-volt system found in most cars.
The 48V system typically includes a 48V lithium-ion battery, a belt-driven integrated starter-generator (BISG), and a DC/DC converter.

Cars with 48V-volt technology promise weight reduction, small size that does not impact interior space, fuel efficiency reducing CO2 emissions, and affordability being less expensive than a full hybrid system.
Reaching the voltage level to 48V maximizes the hybrid system benefits in terms of CO2 reduction and fuel economy.

The powertrain system can be used in either 100% electric or hybrid vehicles and is on average 20% more affordable than high-voltage motors greater than 60V due to a simplified architecture that reduces safety equipment requirements.
Valeo offers two levels of 48V hybridization: Hybrid4all and a 48V hybrid system with electric propulsion.
Level 1 of the Hybrid4all system provides 5% fuel savings with increased comfort and power compared to a 12 Volts system.
Level 1 includes regenerative braking that stores energy in the 48V lithium-ion battery.
Level 1 uses torque assist where the 48V starter generator uses recovered energy to assist the internal combustion engine when accelerating.
Level 1 supplies electrical energy stored in the 48V lithium-ion battery through a 48V converter to power vehicle accessories like car radio and windscreen wipers.
Level 2 is a hybrid with 100% electric propulsion offering around 15% fuel savings with greater comfort and power.
Level 2 includes Level 1 features plus an added 48V electric propulsion engine.
Combined with a high-capacity battery, the vehicle can be driven in 100% electric mode and offers similar performance to a high-voltage hybrid system.
Valeo's range of 48V electric motors meets different needs for existing motor hybridization ranges.
Valeo’s 48V starter-alternator, from 4 to 6 kW, has already been sold in more than 1 million units and was developed using know-how acquired with the 12V starter-alternator.
The 48V 15 kW electric motor is oil-cooled, can be fitted to a hybrid dual-clutch gearbox, and will go into series production in 2021.
The 48V 25kW water-cooled electric motor can be positioned on the rear axle of the vehicle to provide an electric 4WD function and enhance driving experience.
Valeo is producing the 48V power converter that transfers energy stored in the battery to the vehicle’s 12 Volts on-board power supply.
Series production of the air-cooled DC/DC converter began in 2017 and meets cost-performance requirements.
A new generation of the DC/DC converter launched in 2021 is capable of meeting all energy needs thanks to adaptable air or water cooling.

Valeo's solutions include an additional 48 Volts lithium-ion battery, a 48V converter, a new 48V starter generator, and electric motors incorporating regenerative braking, torque assist, and electrical supply functions.
Valeo offers the very first 48V low-voltage rechargeable vehicle resulting from its pursuit of excellence in 48V system development.
The Four-Wheel drive electric architecture includes a 48 Volts starter-alternator and a 48V electric motor on the rear axle to significantly improve handling in harsh weather conditions such as snow.
Combining 48V hybridization with a larger 48V battery offers an electric range of up to 80 km for plug-in hybrid solutions, guaranteeing a drastic overall reduction in CO2 emissions.
The Valeo 48V plug-in hybrid solution enables all-electric travel for around 40 kilometers, with the thermal engine taking over for longer journeys.
Using geolocation, the 100% electric mode is automatically activated when the vehicle enters a low emission zone.
When a driver enters a route into the GPS, the vehicle can adapt the use of the electric drive system accordingly.
Compared to conventional high-voltage plug-in hybrid vehicles, the Valeo 48V plug-in hybrid system is more economical with an average cost around 20% lower.
Valeo proposes applying 48V technology initially developed for hybrid vehicles to new mobility applications such as light 2, 3, or 4 wheels.
Valeo supplies the engine for the Citroën AMI.
The Valeo eCityCar is a 100% electric prototype operating at a low voltage of 48V and is powered entirely by Valeo excluding batteries.
The two-seater eCityCar can reach a maximum speed of 100km/h and has a range of 150km.
Its small size and weight make it suitable for short journeys and city trips, and it can be recharged at any electric charging point.

The 48V 100% electric vehicle is more economical being 20% less expensive than a current all-electric solution because its low-voltage system does not require specific protection unlike high-voltage vehicles subject to restrictive regulations and accreditations.
The cost of a small 48V 100% electric vehicle could be around €7,500 after deduction of purchase subsidies.

This type of vehicle equipped with Valeo’s solution gave manufacturers the impulse for the new era of low-voltage urban mobility which arrived on the streets of French cities in 2020 with the very first full electric 48V Citroën AMI.
source_title: 48V Mild Hybrid System | Automotive electrification | Valeo

facts: Valeo is the global leader in driving assistance systems (ADAS).
Valeo presented its third generation scanning LiDAR on Nov. 23, 2021.
The third generation scanning LiDAR is set to make its market debut in 2024.
The new technology offers significantly enhanced performance, makes autonomous mobility a reality, and provides previously unseen levels of road safety.
Geoffrey Bouquot serves as Valeo's Senior Vice-President, R&D and Strategy.
Bouquot stated that Valeo's third generation LiDAR is a major technological advance toward the autonomous vehicle.
Bouquot noted the upgrade strengthens Valeo's technological and industrial leadership in the field.
At the time of the announcement, Valeo was already the only supplier on the market currently series-producing an automotive-grade LiDAR scanner.
Bouquot stated that Valeo's number one goal with the device is to save lives on the road.
The new LiDAR delivers unrivaled performance in range, resolution, and frame rate.
It reconstructs a 3D real-time image of the vehicle's surroundings at a rate of 4.5 million pixels and 25 frames per second.
Compared to the previous generation, the resolution has been increased 12-fold.
The range has been increased 3-fold compared to the previous generation.
The viewing angle has been increased 2.5-fold compared to the previous generation.
The LiDAR can see things that humans, cameras, and radars cannot.
This capability allows driving to be delegated to the vehicle in many situations (level 2 automation and above), including on the highway at speeds of up to 130km/h.
A vehicle fitted with the third-generation scanning LiDAR can manage emergency situations autonomously.
The scanning LiDAR detects, recognizes, and classifies all objects located around the car.
If objects are moving, the LiDAR measures their speed and direction.
The scanning LiDAR can adapt to all light conditions, including dazzlingly bright or pitch black environments.
It measures the density of raindrops to calculate the right braking distance.
It tracks nearby vehicles even when they are no longer in the driver's line of sight.
It uses algorithms to anticipate trajectories and trigger necessary safety maneuvers.
The scanning LiDAR protects people inside the car and those around it, including pedestrians, cyclists, and other road users.
Beyond the vehicles it equips, the LiDAR will alert other vehicles of road hazards via the Cloud.
Valeo designs and manufactures the entire system, including hardware, software, and associated artificial intelligence.
The AI acts as the "brain" that combines collected data and enables the vehicle to instantly make the right decision.
The software automatically adapts to the environment and improves performance over time through regular updates.
Valeo's LiDARs are produced in Germany at Valeo's Wemding plant in Bavaria.
Components at the Wemding plant are assembled with a micron level of precision.
The plant's production lines draw on Valeo's state-of-the-art expertise in optics, mechanics, and photonics.
300 engineers at Valeo are dedicated solely to this LiDAR technology.
Over 500 patents have already been filed for this technology.
Valeo was the first, and remains the only, company to produce a scanning LiDAR on an industrial scale.
Valeo has already produced over 150,000 units of its scanning LiDAR.
99% of cars equipped with a scanning LiDAR scanner worldwide are equipped with a Valeo scanning LiDAR.
Up to 30% of premium new vehicles are set to reach level 3 automation by 2030.
Vehicles reaching level 3 automation will need to be equipped with LiDAR technology.
Autonomous shuttles, robotaxis, delivery droids, autonomous trucks, and the agriculture, mining, and infrastructure sectors will also need to be equipped with one or more LiDARs.
The LiDAR market is expected to represent more than $50 billion by 2030.
Valeo's new third generation LiDAR will be a key contributor to this change in scale.
source_title: With its new generation LiDAR, Valeo makes autonomous mobility a reality

entity_name: State of Georgia / Georgia General Assembly
canonical_name: Georgia 2026 Construction Industry Licensing and Permitting Legislation
facts: During the 2026 legislative session, the Georgia General Assembly passed several measures affecting the construction industry.
Governor Brian Kemp signed the construction industry measures into law.
Principal changes to specialty contractor licensing and permit procedures take effect July 1, 2026.
A rewritten Chapter 14 of Title 43 will govern specialty contractor licensing starting July 1, 2026.
New procedures for private-provider inspections and land-disturbance permit review begin July 1, 2026.
A separate measure applies to certain public roofing contracts entered into since April 22, 2026.
Senate Bill 553 was signed into law as Act 472.
Act 472 extends revised licensing framework provisions to electrical, plumbing, conditioned air, low voltage, and utility contractors by repealing and reenacting Chapter 14 of Title 43.
Under the new rules, one year of “full-time experience” for contractor licensure requires 2,000 hours of documented qualifying work.
Contractor applicants must submit sworn affidavits verifying experience and consent to applicant-funded criminal background checks, including fingerprinting.

The revised statute updates experience, examination, and training requirements for electrical, plumbing, and low voltage contractor licenses, as well as utility manager and utility foreman certifications.
The rewritten chapter explicitly lists license lending, leasing, renting, assigning, or allowing an unlicensed person or entity to use a license as grounds for discipline.
Sanctions for license violations include reprimand, suspension, revocation, cancellation, or refusal to issue, renew, or restore a license or certificate.
Contractor licenses and certificates remain subject to biennial renewal with a 31-day late-renewal period.
Continuing-education hours established by each division cannot be carried forward if unused.
Senate Bill 447 was signed into law as Act 513.
Act 513 revises local permit-review procedures and mandates a new permit-transparency requirement.

Beginning January 1, 2028, counties, municipalities, or consolidated governments that issued more than 250 building permits in the preceding calendar year must provide real-time permit-status information via a publicly accessible website or application.
The transparency rule defines “building permit” to include land-disturbance permits.
The public permit system must display permit type, current status, date of most recent update, reviewing department, any missed approval or denial deadlines, and the statutory basis for any denial.
Permit information must be searchable by permit number, property address, or parcel identification number and available without charge, registration, or login.
Permit status updates generally must be posted when the status changes or within one business day.
For private-provider inspections, local governing authorities have two business days to identify deficiencies in an approved inspection report.
If a local authority fails to provide timely written notice of deficiencies for a private-provider inspection, the inspection is deemed approved as a matter of law.
Correcting a deficient inspection report may not prevent completion of work already approved by the private professional provider or delay the related project.
The existing 45-day initial review period for land-disturbance permits remains in effect.
Local issuing authorities have five days to determine if a land-disturbance application is complete and whether external review is required; without timely notice, the application is deemed complete.
Local authorities must act on the first resubmission of a land-disturbance permit within 20 days and on later resubmissions within 14 days.
New comments on resubmitted applications generally must relate to the original comments or the applicant’s revisions.
A denial or nonacceptance of a land-disturbance permit must state the reasons in writing.

If a local authority misses the applicable issuance or denial deadline for a land-disturbance permit, it must refund application fees and the applicant may seek mandamus relief with priority on the superior court’s docket.
Senate Bill 284 was signed into law as Act 377.
Act 377 changes the treatment of contracts entered into on or after April 22, 2026, for improvements to roofs of existing public structures or buildings.
Roof improvements under this measure are excluded from the definition of “public works construction,” though ancillary items placed on the roof are not excluded.

Notice and competitive-award requirements are deemed satisfied when a qualifying roofing contract is awarded to a supplier approved by a cooperative purchasing organization and designated by the Department of Administrative Services as an authorized source of supply.
Certain local school systems may receive related capital-outlay grants under this measure.
source_title: Contractor Licensing, Permit Review, and Public Roofing in Georgi

facts: French car parts maker Valeo expected to report a lower core profit margin in 2022 amid geopolitical tensions and inflationary pressures, after missing its target in 2021.
RBC analyst Tom Narayan noted that Valeo's H2/21 results were consistent with the Jan 26 pre-announce, but 2022 guidance was well below consensus expectations.
Analyst Tom Narayan stated this cautious outlook aligns with Renault, Michelin, Faurecia, and Stellantis regarding the global chip shortage.
Valeo shares dropped 10% in midday trade, heading for its worst day since March 2020.
Semiconductor suppliers, car parts producers, and automakers expect some improvement in component shortages this year as chipmakers boost production capacity.
Valeo specializes in the design, production, and sale of components and services for the automotive sector.
Valeo forecast its annual core profit (EBITDA) margin for 2022 to be between 11.8% and 12.3%, compared to 13.4% in the previous year.
Valeo projected 2022 sales between 19.2 billion and 20.0 billion euros, representing an 11-16% growth from the prior year.
The outlook excludes possible effects from the Ukraine-Russia crisis but includes a negative impact from integrating Valeo Siemens eAutomotive.
CEO Christophe Perillat stated the direct impact of the Ukraine-Russia crisis should be minimal, noting the company has no sales in Ukraine and less than 1% of total sales in Russia.
Valeo plans to increase original equipment sales by around 13% in the medium term to reach a revenue of 27.5 billion euros ($30.73 billion) in 2025.
The company is betting on recovery in car production volumes for its growth strategy.
Valeo expects further revenue growth to 40 billion euros by 2030.
source_title: Valeo gives cautious 2022 outlook amid global uncertainties | Euronews

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Vanguard National Trailer Corp., a leading semi-trailer manufacturer based in Monon, Ind., will invest $30 million in a new manufacturing facility in Dade County.
Once operating at full capacity, the new location will create 400 jobs.
Vanguard National Trailer Corp. will develop nearly 100 acres of land and build a new 350,000 square-foot facility near Trenton.
The newly-created jobs will include both office and manufacturing positions.
Vanguard National Trailer will take advantage of Georgia’s Quick Start program, the nation’s No. 1 workforce training program.

Vanguard president and CEO Charlie Mudd stated the company is excited to expand production capabilities and thanked the State of Georgia and the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their project support.
Vanguard National Trailer Corp. manufactures dry freight and refrigerated semi-trailer vans for the North American transportation industry.

Dade County Commission chairman and county executive Ted Rumley noted that the capital investment and 400 new jobs will have a significant impact on the community and phase II of a new industrial park.

GDEcD senior project manager Brittany Holtzclaw supported the company in partnership with the Dade County Industrial Development Authority, the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority, Georgia Power, and the Georgia Department of Transportation.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr highlighted Quick Start as one of the state’s greatest economic development assets and expressed confidence in Vanguard's long-term success in Georgia.
Vanguard National Trailer Corp. is a subsidiary of CIMC, the world’s leading manufacturer of dry and refrigerated container and trailer equipment.
CIMC operates over 100 subsidiaries with 47,000 employees across North America, China, Europe, Asia, and Australia.
CIMC is listed in Forbes Top 2000 World Leading Companies and Business Weekly’s Top 50 Asian Companies.
source_title: GA: Vanguard National Trailer Corp. to Invest $30M, Create 400 Jobs | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Vanguard Trailer is an Indiana-based manufacturer of dry freight and refrigerated semi-trailer vans for the North American transportation industry.
A subsidiary of CIMC (China International Marine Containers), Vanguard began considering building a second dry van manufacturing facility in 2014 to continue growth and address demand.
Vanguard identified an opportunity to build the first dry van manufacturing plant in the Southeast, initially considering locations in Tennessee, Alabama, or Georgia.
In 2015, Vanguard Trailer announced Dade County, Georgia as the home of its new $36 million, 350,000-square-foot manufacturing facility.
The facility will employ 400 workers and produce 10,000 semi-trailers annually once operating at full capacity.
Vanguard credits Dade County’s proximity to customers, availability of a talented workforce, and support of local and state governments for its decision to locate in Trenton, Georgia.
Denny Williams serves as chief operating officer at CIMC USA, the holding company for Vanguard Trailer.
Williams stated that Dade County fits well with their current and planned customer base, saving them a substantial amount on delivery costs for Southeast customers and dealers.
Before settling, Vanguard met with the Georgia Department of Economic Development and the Dade County Industrial Development Authority to learn about available support.
Available incentives included job-tax credits and Georgia’s state EDGE grant.
Georgia offers a comprehensive workforce training program called Quick Start, which partners businesses with technical colleges to provide customized workforce training for free.
The chosen site received the state’s GRAD certification, which handles due diligence such as environmental and geological reviews before property purchase.
Williams noted that both the state of Georgia and Dade County were excellent at providing resources and information for their location decision.
Ground broke on the project in September 2015, and plant operations began on August 15, 2016, meeting the original Q3 2016 start goal.
To-date, the company has hired 130 employees with plans to exceed the originally planned 400 workers.
Employees will fill roles in manufacturing, leadership, safety and human resources, materials, and accounting.
Williams expressed satisfaction with operations in Dade County, stating both the county and state delivered on all their promises.
Workers at Vanguard Trailer work four 10-hour shifts instead of five eight-hour days to allow more personal time outside of work.
source_title: How Dade County’s Workforce and Low Cost of Doing Business Wooed Vanguard Trailer - Greater Chattanooga Economic Partnership

facts: Optronics International’s new stop-tail-turn lamp integrates graphics within the lighting package, combining a GloLight Lens and a traditional LED pattern.
The lamps come in a four-inch round or six-inch oval configuration.
The traditional LED-style lens pattern surrounds the graphic in the middle of the lamp.
The GloLight logo illuminates when operating as a tail lamp, while all portions of the LED lamp brighten when turn signal or brake functions are operating.
The GloLight can be used in red, white, or a combination of both.
Different visual effects are achieved with lens striations.
The development process included Vanguard National Trailer Corporation and Miller Industries.
source_title: Lamps have a graphic appeal - Truck News

facts: Optronics International is a manufacturer and supplier of heavy-duty LED vehicle lighting.
The company created stop, tail, and turn lamps with integrated graphics.
The new lamps merge brand identities into the lighting design by juxtaposing a smooth GloLight lens appearance against a more traditional LED pattern.
GloLight technology allows Optronics to consolidate complex graphics into the functional operation of the lamps.
A traditional LED-style lens pattern surrounds the logo graphic in the middle of the lamp.
The GloLight logo portion of the lamp illuminates when operating in its standard function as a tail lamp.
When the lamp’s turn signal or brake functions are in operation, all portions of the LED lamp brighten.
Brett Johnson serves as president and CEO of Optronics International.
Brett Johnson stated that logos and branding have been widely incorporated into conspicuity tape for decades, so the migration to lighting is a logical one.
Professionals recognize that 4” round and 6” oval lighting formats are among the most noticeable features on any vehicle.
Optronics employed its state-of-the-art 3D printing capabilities in preparing prototypes for manufacturers.
The company worked with several OEMs during lamp development, including Vanguard National Trailer Corporation and Miller Industries.
Charlie Mudd serves as president of Vanguard National Trailer Corporation.
Charlie Mudd stated that Vanguard was among the first trailer manufacturers to make Optronics’ GloLight part of their standard lighting package.
The LED lamps offer a broad level of creative design flexibility for company branding, as GloLight technology can be used in both red and white or a combination of both.
Lens striations can be used to achieve unique and complementary visual effects.
Examples of the new 4” round and 6” oval stop, tail, and turn lamps will be displayed in booth 2547 at the North American Commercial Vehicle (NACV) Show 2019.
Optronics products are available in the U.S. and Canada through a distribution network of more than 20,000 convenient distribution locations.
source_title: Optronics Introduces Custom LED Lighting | Crane Hot Line

The facility initially features 10 advanced production presses.

Vernay plans to expand the facility by adding up to 45 presses in the near future.

Vernay specializes in liquid silicone (LSR) molding, targeting markets where demand for high-quality, reliable components is paramount.
Each state-of-the-art production cell can produce 64 parts every 32 seconds.
The capacity for these production cells delivers over 200 million micro-molded parts per year.
Part of the Kennesaw facility investment ensures ISO-13485 and Clean Room Class 8 compliance for the Medical market.
Vernay Laboratories has been a technology leader in the design, development, and manufacture of customized flow-control components since its founding in 1946.
The company formulates its own compounds to address each specific application.
Vernay’s products play a vital role in millions of medical devices, automobiles, and consumer products.

facts: The Development Authority of Polk County shared their 2024 Annual Report of economic progress to their Board and other community stakeholders at their regular monthly meeting on February 14, 2025.

On February 15, 2024, a statewide announcement confirmed that SOLARCYCLE, an advanced technology-based solar recycling company, will create 617 new full-time jobs in Polk County by investing an estimated $344 million in a solar glass manufacturing facility in Cedartown.
The Solarcycle facility will be the first-of-its-kind in the country to use recycled materials from retired solar panels to make new solar glass.
On October 31, 2024, a second statewide announcement confirmed that SOLARCYCE will accelerate its expansion into Polk County to meet increasing demand for solar panel recycling services.

As part of the expansion, the company is investing an additional $62 million to increase its panel recycling capacity to 10 million solar panels per year and create an additional 640 new full-time jobs.

On November 18, 2024, the Development Authority of Polk County was awarded the 2024 Mid-size Community Deal of the Year by the Georgia Economic Developers Association for its role in securing the deal to bring SOLARCYCLE to Cedartown.

Award winners were evaluated based on economic impact, the degree of creativity and expertise required to complete the deal, and the obstacles and challenges overcome during negotiations and completion.
F.A.M.E. Health Labs took ownership of an existing manufacturing facility located at 3520 Lees Chapel Road, Cedartown.

F.A.M.E. Health Labs manufactures private label supplement brands that include graphics, formulation, in-house and third-party testing, sampling, contract manufacturing, packaging, graphic design, and unparalleled customer service.
F.A.M.E Health Labs currently employs approximately 140 employees, offering competitive wage and benefits.
Millennium Freight Solutions purchased and relocated to 2649 Cartersville Hwy, Rockmart.
Millennium Freight Solutions oversees a growing fleet of trucks and trailers as well as a dedicated, professional staff of 14 logistic experts and warehouse and mechanical personnel.
The leading industry sector in Polk County remains Manufacturing with a total employment of 3,030.

Manufacturing contributes the largest share of Gross Regional Product (GDP) for the county at over $392 million in goods and services produced plus the sum of employment earnings, property income, and taxes on production.
Average earnings per job in manufacturing is $70,503.

In partnership with the Cedartown Development Authority and Rockmart Development Authority, the Development Authority of Polk County continues to market approximately 220 acres to potential businesses creating quality jobs and capital investments that will positively impact economic growth for Polk County.

The Development Authority of Polk County (DAPC) is a government entity charged with economic development for the county, supported by the cities, and provides a single point of contact for business and industry looking to relocate or expand within the footprint of the county.

DAPC's mission is to support the creation of jobs and opportunities for the citizens of Polk County through continued growth in new and existing industry and the redevelopment of areas of commercial blight or underuse.
Chris Thomas serves as President/CEO of the Development Authority of Polk County.
source_title: Development Authority of Polk County 2024 Annual Report - Polk County Georgia

facts: Choate Construction is returning to the Vista Metals campus in Adairsville, GA, to construct a massive pit for a Lithium Furnace.
The Lithium Furnace pit is located within a pre-engineered metal framed building and measures 50 feet by 30 feet with a depth of 35 feet.
Construction of the Lithium Furnace pit requires engineered shoring systems, confined-space entrance safety measures, and extra thick concrete slabs and walls.
Additional features for the Lithium Furnace pit include casting pit process piping work, air piping, gas piping, casting pit steam exhaust pits, and miscellaneous trenches.
The project includes multiple ledges, haunches, abutments, embedded steel items, and concrete elevation changes typical with equipment pits.
This expansion adds a 23,000 SF aluminum billet cast house to the Vista Metals Corporation foundry campus in Adairsville, GA.
The facility expansion increases capacity at the foundry to supply raw aluminum billets used by aluminum extruders such as Universal Alloy in Canton, Georgia.
This project marks the third collaboration between Choate Construction and the valued repeat client Vista Metals Corporation.
The casting pit construction consists of a 45-foot deep casting pit, a lube pit, a pump pit, a casting cart pit, and steam exhaust pits.
Foundations added include bases for an aluminum pre-heater, a cooling tower, and two tilting melting furnaces.
Shoring is required for roughly 20 feet by 30 feet to a depth of 45 feet for the casting pit.
A dewatering system is installed during construction, with the water table at the casting pit located at 21 feet below the finish floor.
The casting pit is built from the bottom up upon completion of the dewatering system.
source_title: Vista Metals | Projects | Choate Construction

facts: On March 31, 2025, the Georgian government confirmed that innovative startups in Georgia will be eligible for up to 10 years of tax breaks.
Innovative startups in Georgia will receive 0% corporate income tax for the first 3 years.
The incentive package includes extended tax incentives for a total of 10 years.
Additional support is provided for small and mid-sized companies working on tech-driven products.
Amendments are being made to two prominent legal frameworks: the Law “On Innovations” and the Tax Code of Georgia.
A new legal definition will outline precisely what qualifies a company as an “innovative startup.”
The policy opens eligibility for SMEs working on industry-specific innovations, not just large tech startups.
Georgia’s GII ranking has been improving from 2020 to 2024.

Progress in the GII ranking represents improvements in startup infrastructure, better support for R&D, more IP protection laws like the Georgia Uniform Trade Secrets Act, a shift toward attracting foreign investment and tech talent, and stronger alignment with global innovation policy standards.
Historically, Georgia has had a competitive corporate tax rate of 15%.
Qualified companies will pay 0% corporate tax in Georgia for the first three years.
After the initial three years, companies may continue to receive reduced tax rates or exemptions for up to a decade depending on activities and compliance.
Other likely benefits include VAT exemptions on R&D services, reduced tax on dividend distributions, and simplified reporting for qualified startups.
The government sees tech and entrepreneurship as long-term economic stabilizers and growth engines amid recovery from regional instability and the pandemic.
Georgia is recognized for its ease of doing business and aims to add tax efficiency to that reputation.

Eligibility criteria likely require businesses to be registered and operating in Georgia, develop proprietary technology or software, conduct R&D locally, demonstrate potential for market growth or exports, and maintain transparent accounting and legal records.
Companies must be recognized as Georgian tax residents to qualify for local benefits including the zero-tax regime.
Tax residency requires incorporation in Georgia, management and decision-making based in Georgia, a Georgian bank account, a tax ID, and local filings.
Having a Georgian domain or office lease is not enough to establish tax residency.
Founders must choose the right business structure to affect eligibility for tax incentives, ability to receive investment, IP assignment, and tax residency compliance.
Businesses must document their innovation by providing product development timelines, technical architecture or prototypes, evidence of R&D, and customer use cases or testing feedback.
All IP including code, content, designs, and patents must be transferred to the business entity.
Common IP protection tools include employment or contractor agreements with IP clauses, NDAs with collaborators or vendors, trademark and patent filings, and internal IP assignment documentation.
Businesses need a Georgia tax ID number to submit tax returns and prove eligibility for certification.

Companies must set up accurate and GAAP-aligned bookkeeping, regular Georgia tax return submissions (especially Form 500), clear financial reporting linked to the Georgian entity, and proper categorization of R&D expenses, salaries, and investments.
The Georgia tax return form 500 is the official document used to verify profit/loss and tax liability for the 0% corporate income tax benefit.
Consistent and compliant filing protects against audit risks and builds a paper trail for future grants, partnerships, or funding rounds.
Gegidze is a firm that works with startups, growth-stage companies, and foreign investors to establish a compliant presence in Georgia and navigate the business-friendly tax regime.
The 0% corporate income tax exemption is expected to begin once the new legal framework is fully implemented in 2025.

Foreign-owned startups can apply for Innovation Status as long as the company is legally based in Georgia, meets local tax residency requirements, and proves innovative activity through documentation, IP ownership, and local operations.
source_title: What is Innovation Status in Georgia and How to Get It (Before Everyone Else)

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced on August 16, 2016 that voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. will expand its existing operation in Bartow County.
The expansion project will create 150 new jobs.
The company is investing $50 million in the local community as part of the expansion.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. serves as the supply base for the voestalpine Group, producing ultra-high strength body-in-white components for premium car manufacturers.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that the decision strengthens Georgia’s growing automotive industry and highlights the state’s extensive manufacturing capabilities.
The newly created jobs will range from advanced manufacturing to hot-forming and assembly positions.
The company currently employs approximately 100 Georgians at its facility in Bartow County.
The facility offers extensive training opportunities, including an apprenticeship program with students of the Bartow County College & Career Academy in Cartersville.

Philipp Schulz, managing director of voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc., stated the investment is an important step in the company’s U.S. growth strategy and positions it well in the center of the southern automotive hub.
Philipp Schulz thanked state and local officials as well as the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their assistance in the project.
voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. is a subsidiary company of the Metal Forming Division of the German-based voestalpine Group.
The Metal Forming Division produces highly refined sections, tubes and precision strip steel products, as well as ready-to-install components made of pressed, stamped and roll formed parts.
Bartow County Commissioner Steve Taylor noted that voestalpine was the first company to build in Highland 75 and highlighted its multiple additional phases and continued investment.

Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Senior Project Manager Wylly Harrison and Regional Project Manager Stephanie Scearce represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr stated that global manufacturing companies choosing Georgia testifies to the state’s top-ranked business climate and international reputation.
The voestalpine Group operates globally with around 500 Group companies and locations in more than 50 countries on all five continents.
The voestalpine Group has been listed on the Vienna Stock Exchange since 1995.
The voestalpine Group is a leading partner to the automotive and consumer goods industries in Europe and to the oil and gas industries worldwide.
The voestalpine Group is the world market leader in turnout technology, special rails, tool steel, and special sections.
source_title: Deal: Leading automotive supplier to create 150 jobs in Bartow County | WBHF

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that automotive industry supplier voestalpine Metal Forming, under its automotive body parts unit, will build a manufacturing facility near Cartersville in Bartow County creating 220 jobs.
The company is investing $62 million in this new facility.
This facility will be voestalpine's first in the Southeast.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Georgia offers automotive industry suppliers a great combination of resources necessary to compete worldwide.

Gov. Nathan Deal noted that by choosing Georgia, voestalpine can be assured of a well-trained workforce, a logistics infrastructure growing in global prominence, and a business environment supportive of future growth.
Voestalpine’s Metal Forming Division provides the automotive industry with a complete range of pressed parts in the body-in-white segment.
The division also provides highly innovative structural components to well-respected suppliers.
The company’s customers represent several of the leading manufacturers in the automotive and automotive supply industries.

CEO of voestalpine Metal Forming GmbH and member of the Board of voestalpine AG Herbert Eibensteiner stated that the company has a very high commitment to quality for innovative products and smooth supply chains.

Herbert Eibensteiner noted that Cartersville offers a strategically located and business-friendly environment that supports their mindset and will stimulate future plans for the North American automotive market.

Herbert Eibensteiner confirmed that the state of Georgia, the Georgia Department of Economic Development, and local authorities representing Bartow County and Cartersville beneficially supported their selection process.
Bartow County’s geographic advantages played a significant role in voestalpine’s decision to choose the county for this new manufacturing operation.
The site offers convenient access to I-75, allowing efficient reach to key U.S. customers in the Southeast.
The site allows global connectivity with ease of access to the Port of Savannah.
Voestalpine will be the anchor tenant in the Highland 75 development in Bartow County.
Highland 75 is a GRAD Certified 707-acre master planned industrial/corporate park located between Atlanta and Chattanooga, one mile east of I-75.
Highland 75 is a collaboration of Bartow County, the city of Cartersville, and the Bartow-Cartersville Joint Development Authority.
Bartow County Sole Commissioner Clarence Brown stated that projects like this require the efforts of many involved and committed people.
Clarence Brown noted that years of hard work from the Joint Development Authority and others are now beginning to pay off.
Clarence Brown added that investing in Highland 75 was intended to attract jobs, welcoming voestalpine as the first company in the development for its positive economic impact.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) partnered with the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development to secure this business opportunity for Georgia.
GDEcD project manager Wylly Harrison managed this project on behalf of the state.
The project team collaborated with managing director of Georgia’s European Office in Germany Antje Abshoff.
The project team received assistance from Parker Poe Consulting, LLC and TPA Realty Services.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Cummiskey stated that the project is significant for Georgia as a great intersection of efforts to attract leading manufacturers and international businesses.
Chris Cummiskey added that voestalpine’s decision to locate in Georgia is an important win for the state's high-performing logistics network, workforce, and economic development partners.
The company will receive training assistance from Georgia Quick Start.

Georgia Quick Start is described as the nation’s top-ranked workforce training program that offers tailored, operations-specific training to qualified companies free of charge for expansion or relocation in Georgia.
Future employment opportunities information is available through the Georgia Department of Labor’s Cartersville Career Center.
Voestalpine Metal Forming is part of the voestalpine Group and operates business units including Automotive Body Parts, Tubes & Sections, and Precision Strip.
The voestalpine Group is a steel, processing, and technology group that operates worldwide manufacturing and processing facilities developing high-quality steel products.
With top-quality flat steel products, the Group is one of the leading partners in the automotive, white goods, and energy industries in Europe as well as the oil and gas industries worldwide.
The Group maintains 400 production and sales companies in more than 60 countries on five continents.
The Group has been listed on the Vienna Stock Exchange since 1995.
The Group has approximately 46,500 employees worldwide.
