facts: North Point Volvo Cars operates as a Volvo dealership near Atlanta serving motorists in Alpharetta, GA.
The dealership is located at 1570 Mansell Rd, Alpharetta, GA 30009.
The new vehicle inventory includes Volvo S60 and S90 sedans, as well as the XC40, XC60, and XC90 SUVs.

The new Volvo XC90 features three rows of seating for six or seven passengers, up to 85.7 cubic feet of cargo space, a towing capacity of up to 5,000 pounds when properly equipped, and built-in Google Assistant.
The dealership offers a used vehicle inventory that customers can shop by age, mileage, price, or features.
Certified pre-owned Volvo models must have fewer than 80,000 miles and undergo a thorough 170-point vehicle inspection.
Certified pre-owned vehicles include a CARFAX Vehicle History Report, a limited warranty, and roadside assistance.
The dealership provides financing and leasing options to help customers understand their buying power.
Customers can trade in or sell their current vehicles through the dealership's online service.
The on-site service center offers maintenance for oil changes, brake service, tire rotation, and EV battery maintenance.
The dealership sells replacement parts and accessories for Volvo models.
source_title: North Point Volvo Cars: Atlanta Area Volvo Dealer

facts: Volvo revealed plans to produce a new hybrid SUV at its South Carolina manufacturing facility.
The announcement signals the automaker's commitment to electrification while also maintaining options for drivers not yet fully ready to switch to electric vehicles.
The company continues rolling out new electric models, including the subcompact EX30 and mid-size EX90 SUVs introduced over the past year.
The XC60 remains one of Volvo's top sellers, with U.S. sales jumping 23 percent in the first half of 2025 compared to last year.
Volvo is planning to manufacture the next-generation hybrid model at its Ridgeville plant before 2030.
Volvo designed the vehicle specifically for American customers.
The company expects the new hybrid model to join the XC60, EX90, and Polestar 3 in the production lineup.
The Ridgeville factory is aiming for full utilization of its 150,000-vehicle annual capacity.
The Ridgeville plant is backed by $1.3 billion in investments and upgrades since 2015.
source_title: Volvo announces ambitious new plans in US — here's what's happening

facts: Volvo Cars is making public charging simpler and more convenient for electric vehicle drivers with the introduction of Plug & Charge functionality for all Volvo EX60 and EX90 vehicles in the United States.
Volvo Car USA reported sales of 34,228 vehicles for the second quarter of the year, up 9 percent year over year.
Electrified vehicles accounted for 24.1 percent of total U.S. sales in the second quarter.
Sales of electrified sales declined 17.1 percent compared to the same period last year as the company continues to balance customer demand for the full range of powertrains across the U.S. market.
Volvo Cars and Google aim to create a new generation of contextually aware driving experiences by bringing together their respective technologies and leveraging AI and Google Gemini.
Ordering for the new fully electric Volvo EX60 opened today for U.S. customers.
Customer deliveries for the new fully electric Volvo EX60 are scheduled to start in early summer.
Volvo Car USA reported sales of 22,651 vehicles for the first quarter of the year, down 32 percent year over year.
Electrified vehicles accounted for 25 percent of total U.S. sales in the first quarter.
Sales of electrified sales declined 39 percent compared to the same period last year in the first quarter.
Volvo Cars achieved the highest possible ranking on carmakers’ software defined capabilities by S&P Global Mobility, becoming the only legacy carmaker globally to reach Level 5 SDV capability.
The Volvo XC90 was named a winner in the annual 2026 Parents Best Family Car Awards, taking home honors in Best Luxury Midsize 3-Row SUV and Best Luxury Hybrid / Plug-In Hybrid categories.
source_title: Press & Media | Volvo Cars Media US

facts: Volvo is integrating Luminar Technologies' lidar system on its upcoming electric flagship SUV.
This marks the first time a major carmaker has integrated lidar technology as standard equipment on a vehicle.
The specific sensor being used is Luminar's new Iris lidar system, which allows vehicles to better see their environment.
The Volvo SUV will serve as a successor to the current XC90 model.
The new vehicle is expected to be unveiled next year (2022).
Luminar went public through a SPAC deal in December 2020.
On the announcement day, Luminar shares rose by as much as 13% during intraday trading before closing at $23.95 per share, up 6.8%.
Volvo CEO Hakan Samuelsson stated that Volvo has always been a safety leader and will now define the next level of car safety with this standard hardware.
The standard hardware will allow Volvo to continuously improve safety features over the air and introduce advanced autonomous drive systems.
Lidar (light detection and ranging) systems use light to create high-resolution images for more accurate environmental views than cameras or radar alone.
While lidar has been discussed for self-driving delivery vehicles, consumer adoption was previously limited by cost.
Luminar states its system will initially enhance vehicle safety and will cost approximately $1,000 per vehicle.
Depending on the launch timing, the Volvo SUV could be the first vehicle with lidar as standard equipment.
This partnership represents a major win for Luminar in standardizing lidar technology.
Luminar founder and CEO Austin Russell confirmed the technology is being integrated into Volvo's next-generation EV platform.
The integration suggests Volvo may offer lidar as standard or optional on all of its upcoming EVs.
Volvo has announced plans to transition to a fully electric car company by 2030.
The new vehicles are expected to eventually offer advanced hands-free highway driving with some self-driving capabilities.
Austin Russell identified 2023 as the "first major ramp up" of Luminar's lidar technology for the automotive industry.
Russell projected that in the second half of the decade, millions of vehicles will be on the road equipped with this technology.
source_title: Shares of start-up Luminar surge on lidar deal with Volvo

facts: Novo Energy is the battery cell manufacturing subsidiary of Volvo Cars.
Novo Energy has announced layoffs as its operations are put on hold.
The decision to pause plans was described as necessary during the current situation and not taken lightly.
Volvo Cars continues searching for an external battery technology partner while operations are paused.
Novo Energy was founded with the ambition to produce batteries for Volvo's electric cars in Gothenburg, Sweden.
Novo Energy was established as a joint venture between Volvo Cars and European battery company Northvolt to develop sustainable battery solutions.
Following Northvolt's bankruptcy filing, Volvo Cars took on full ownership of Novo Energy.
Novo Energy was scheduled to begin production of battery cells at the Gothenburg facility this year.
The Gothenburg facility was planned to produce up to 50GWh of battery cells annually.
Until a new technology partner is secured, Novo Energy can no longer proceed with its operations as previously planned.
All positions at Novo Energy have been laid off while Volvo continues its search for a partner.
Novo Energy had a total of 75 staff members who were laid off.
The company stated it is currently not possible to share details on when battery production could start.
It is also not possible to share under what ownership structure future production might occur.
Novo Energy was originally intended to produce cells for Volvo vehicles and potentially other Geely group vehicles at the Gothenburg facility.
The Gothenburg facility was pegged to create 3,000 jobs when Novo was founded.
A report by transport research firm NewAutomotive noted Northvolt’s insolvency as evidence of financial and execution risks in the capital-intensive battery sector.
Europe remains heavily reliant on imports for midstream refining and precursor/anode material production.
Securing reliable sources of critical raw materials such as lithium, cobalt, nickel, and graphite is considered of utmost importance.
China has long held a competitive advantage in EV and energy storage battery manufacturing due to strong national supply chains.
Global trade tensions have led China to restrict exports of some components critical to battery production.
These trade tensions have also impacted the stationary battery energy storage market.
The energy storage technology has benefited from significant private and government investment in Western regions due to its economic and logistical value for achieving net zero.
The quick return on investment (ROI) of energy storage deployment has caused many formerly EV-focused initiatives to switch to storage.
Volvo’s difficulty recruiting a technology partner may stem from an increasing reluctance to invest in the EV sector due to legislative backtracking on ICE vehicle transitions.
All European-founded battery companies have struggled recently due to softened EV demand, competition from China and the US, and a lack of expertise in battery cell manufacturing.
Many battery tech firms are currently occupied with or seeking cell manufacturing supply deals with the storage sector, which offers more certain long-term investment.
source_title: Volvo shutters EV battery cell manufacturer Novo Energy

facts: Volvo Cars announced it will begin producing a new hybrid model in the United States by the end of this decade.
The production shift is part of Volvo's strategy to adjust to President Donald Trump's tariff policies on foreign cars.
The new hybrid model details have not been released but will be designed specifically for the U.S. market.
Producing the new model aims to boost capacity utilization at Volvo's South Carolina plant.
Automakers worldwide are rethinking production strategies in response to Trump's duties on foreign cars and his policy to boost U.S. manufacturing.
In April, Volvo Cars' CEO stated the company would produce more vehicles in the U.S.
In July, Volvo announced plans to start producing its XC60 mid-size SUV at the South Carolina plant starting in late 2026.
The South Carolina plant currently produces only the fully electric EX90 SUV and the Polestar 3.
Production data indicates that current models utilize only a small fraction of the South Carolina plant's annual capacity of 150,000 vehicles.
The announcement coincided with Volvo's 70th anniversary in the United States.
Volvo previously aimed to phase out all non-electric models by 2030 but reversed course last year to keep hybrids in its lineup.

Luis Rezende, president of Volvo Cars' Americas operation, stated: "By adding another model into production here, we'll take a big step toward realizing the full potential of our local manufacturing investments and workforce."
A July trade deal with the European Union reduced U.S. tariffs on European cars to 15%, but the agreement has not yet taken effect.
Vehicles continue to face a 27.5% tariff levy while awaiting the implementation of the EU trade deal.
source_title: Volvo to build new hybrid model in US amid Trump tariff shift

facts: Volvo Cars, majority owned by China’s Geely Holding, received approval from the U.S. government allowing it to continue selling vehicles.

In January 2025, President Joe Biden’s administration finalized rules effectively barring nearly all Chinese cars and trucks from the U.S. market as part of a crackdown on vehicle software and hardware from China.
The rules included a ban on most Chinese-developed and maintained software that took effect in March 2026 for the 2027 model year and covered companies with significant Chinese ownership.
Lawmakers have proposed making the rules even tougher.
Volvo Car USA was required to follow a process with the U.S. Department of Commerce to obtain a specific authorization for the continued import and sale of connected cars in the U.S.
With this specific authorization, Volvo Cars can continue its growth plans in the United States.
Volvo Cars sold 121,600 vehicles in the United States in 2025, down 2.9% from 2024.
In September, Volvo Cars said it would begin producing a new hybrid model in the U.S. by the end of the decade.
The new model will be designed for the U.S. market and help Volvo boost capacity utilization at its South Carolina plant.
In April 2025, Volvo Cars CEO Hakan Samuelsson said the company would produce more vehicles in the U.S.
The group announced in July that it plans to start producing its popular XC60 mid-size SUV in South Carolina in late 2026.
Volvo, long positioned as an EV trailblazer with the aim of phasing out all non-electric models by 2030, last year reversed course and said hybrids would remain part of its lineup.
Volvo Cars currently imports all of its cars into the U.S. from Europe except its electric SUV EX90, which it assembles in South Carolina.
The Swedish automaker used to also import cars from China but halted those after tariffs on Chinese-made cars were put into effect.

Volvo said the Commerce Department gave the company a specific authorization following constructive discussions with government agencies regarding Volvo Cars’ governance, technology and data security.
Sweden’s Polestar, which is also majority-owned by Geely, separately said it continues to work with U.S. authorities to meet the requirements of the announced regulations.
source_title: Volvo Cars wins US approval to keep importing vehicles with ‘connected car’ technology | The Mighty 790 KFGO | KFGO

facts: Swedish giant Volvo is considering Georgia, along with other Southern states, for a possible new manufacturing plant.
It is the second major automotive plant Georgia is pursuing.
Georgia has also been in the mix with other states and potentially European nations for a Jaguar Land Rover factory.
No deals have been signed with Volvo or U.K.-based Jaguar Land Rover.
Decisions on the potential factories could be weeks away.
A person with knowledge of the situation told The Atlanta Journal-Constitution about the status of the pursuit.
A spokeswoman for the Georgia Department of Economic Development had no immediate comment.
Volvo declined to comment on the reports.
source_title: Georgia among states gunning for Volvo factory

facts: Volvo Car USA announced details of its 2026 fully electric lineup for U.S. customers, highlighted by upgrades to the EX90 and new EX30 variants.
The 2026 EX90 features a new 800-volt electrical architecture that delivers faster, more efficient charging.
At compatible 800-volt charging stations, the upgraded EX90 adds up to 155 miles of range in just 10 minutes.
Every 2026 EX90 gains a dual NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Orin-based core computer with 500 TOPS of computing power for advanced safety and driver-assist features.
New safety capabilities include connected safety alerts for slippery roads and hazards, expanded automatic emergency steering in darkness, and Emergency Stop Assist with integrated automatic e-call.
Owners of the 2025 EX90 will receive this computer upgrade at no charge in the coming months.
The 2026 EX90 adds a Single Motor powertrain option alongside Twin Motor and Twin Motor Performance variants, with the latter producing 670-hp.
The 2026 EX90 adds an available electrochromic panoramic roof with adjustable tint and ventilated Nappa leather upholstery in three colors.
Volvo is adding the EX30 Single Motor to the U.S. market in Plus equipment level with a starting MSRP of $38,950.
The EX30 Single Motor offers an EPA-estimated range of up to 261 miles and can charge from 10 to 80 percent in 28 minutes.
The EX30 is designed with a focus on minimizing its carbon footprint across its complete lifecycle, featuring zero tailpipe emissions and recycled/renewable materials throughout the interior.
Volvo is introducing the fully electric EX30 Cross Country for U.S. customers, featuring higher ground clearance, standard all-wheel drive, and available 18-inch all-terrain tires.
The EX30 Cross Country battery can charge from 10 to 80 percent in as little as 27 minutes.
The EX30 Cross Country will be available only as the Twin Motor Ultra model with a starting MSRP of $48,150.
The popular Black Edition styling option is now available on the fully electric EX40 in the U.S.
Volvo Car USA LLC operates a manufacturing campus in Ridgeville, South Carolina.
The Ridgeville operations underscore Volvo's long-term commitment to the United States as a key market.
Volvo Cars' production plants are located in Gothenburg, Ghent (Belgium), South Carolina (US), Chengdu, Daqing and Taizhou (China).
Volvo Car USA maintains offices in California and parts distribution and training centers across the country.
For the full year 2024, Volvo Car Group recorded a core operating profit of SEK 27 billion and revenue of SEK 400.2 billion.
Global sales for Volvo Cars reached a record 763,389 cars in 2024.
As of December 2024, Volvo Cars employed approximately 42,600 full-time employees globally.

links.context: EPA range and fuel economy information

source_title: Volvo Car USA announces major upgrades and new models as part of its electric 2026 lineup | Volvo Cars Media US

facts: Volvo Cars will begin producing a new hybrid model in the United States by the end of this decade.
The new model details have yet to be released but will be designed for the U.S. market.
The new model is intended to help Volvo boost capacity utilization at its South Carolina plant.
Volvo Cars CEO Hakan Samuelsson stated, “It will take time until America is electric everywhere.”
Samuelsson described the larger hybrid model as "more an electric car, but with a Plan B. When the battery is flat, you have a combustion engine kicking in, but normally you'd drive it electric."
Auto makers worldwide are scrambling to rethink production strategies to contend with President Donald Trump's duties on foreign cars, part of his wider policy to boost U.S. manufacturing.
Volvo Cars' CEO announced in April that the company would produce more vehicles in the U.S.
The group announced in July that it plans to start producing its popular XC60 mid-size SUV at the South Carolina plant from late 2026.
The South Carolina factory currently builds only Volvo's fully electric EX90 SUV and the Polestar 3.
Production data shows that the EX90 and Polestar 3 take up only a small fraction of the site's annual capacity of 150,000 vehicles.
The announcement coincided with Volvo's 70th anniversary in the United States.
Volvo, long positioned as an EV trailblazer with the aim of phasing out all non-electric models by 2030, last year reversed course and said hybrids would remain part of its lineup.

Luis Rezende, president of Volvo Cars' Americas operation, stated, "By adding another model into production here, we'll take a big step toward realising the full potential of our local manufacturing investments and workforce."
A trade deal struck in July with the European Union cut U.S. tariffs on European cars to 15%, but the agreement has yet to take effect, with vehicles continuing to face a 27.5% levy.
Samuelsson said the 10% duties eliminated on auto imports from the U.S. into Europe will make selling its U.S.-produced EX90 vehicle abroad more attractive.
Samuelsson added regarding Volvo's operations in Europe, "With this 10% reduction, we have the possibility to grow."
source_title: Volvo Cars to produce new US model amid tariff pressure

facts: Sweden-based carmaker aims to bring its plant in Ridgeville, South Carolina up to full production volumes in the coming years.
The facility has struggled with product delays and lackluster demand for its EVs and sedans.
The facility has capacity to build as many as 150,000 vehicles annually.
source_title: Volvo Car to Offset Tariff With New Model Bound for US Plant (1)

facts: Reported sales of 34,228 vehicles for the second quarter of 2026, up 9 percent year over year.
Sales were driven by the recovery of XC60 and XC90 models.
Electrified vehicles, defined as vehicles with a fully electric or plug-in hybrid powertrain, accounted for 24.1 percent of total U.S. sales in the quarter.
Sales of electrified vehicles declined 17.1 percent compared to the same period last year as the company continues to balance customer demand for the full range of powertrains across the U.S. market.

The overall U.S. automotive market remains impacted by low customer sentiment, increased competition in the SUV segment, and a slower than expected recovery in sales of electric and plug-in hybrid cars post subsidy removal.
Sales increased for two straight months in the second quarter, reflecting the positive impact of actions taken to support retailers in driving business to their stores.
The award-winning XC90 and best-selling XC60 both delivered a sharp double-digit increase for the second quarter compared to the same period last year.

Certified pre-owned sales continued their positive momentum, increasing 38 percent year over year in the second quarter, with fully electric certified pre-owned sales surging 183 percent compared with the second quarter of last year.
Volvo Cars unveiled the all-new EX60 in North America for the first time in May, debuting the highly-anticipated model in New York City and officially opened orders for U.S. customers.

Luis Rezende, President of Volvo Car Americas, stated that the focus remains on meeting the needs of U.S. customers across their electrification journey through a strong portfolio of premium SUVs, positioning Volvo Cars with the right products, offers, and experience to meet the needs of U.S. drivers.
Q2 2026 total sales were 34,228 vehicles compared to 31,395 in Q2 2025.
Electrified vehicle sales in Q2 2026 were 8,262 units, down from 9,970 in Q2 2025.
Fully electric vehicle sales in Q2 2026 were 1,588 units, down from 2,871 in Q2 2025.
Plug-in hybrid vehicle sales in Q2 2026 were 6,674 units, down from 7,099 in Q2 2025.
Mild hybrid vehicle sales in Q2 2026 were 25,966 units, up from 21,425 in Q2 2025.
source_title: Volvo Car USA reports second quarter 2026 sales | Volvo Cars Media US

facts: President Joe Biden visited the Volvo Group Powertrain Operations facility in Hagerstown, Md., for National Manufacturing Day.
During the event, Biden met with Volvo Group executives, toured the facility, and spoke with employees.
Martin Weissburg is chairman of Volvo Group North America and Mack Trucks president.
The Hagerstown site provides advanced transportation technologies to meet customer needs today and in the future.
Over the past 10 years, the Volvo Group has invested nearly $320 million in the Hagerstown site.
The Hagerstown campus produces engines, transmissions, and axles for Mack Trucks, Volvo Trucks, Prevost motorcoaches, and Volvo Buses sold in North America.
The facility recently began production of Modular Power Boxes (MPB).
The MPBs are at the heart of the Group’s new zero-emission battery-electric vehicles.
The zero-emission battery-electric vehicle models include the Volvo VNR Electric and Mack LR Electric.
President Biden toured the MPB subassembly, assembly, and testing areas while on campus.
Marcus Minkkinen is vice president and general manager at the Volvo Group Powertrain Operations in Hagerstown.
The company states that the visit highlights the importance of manufacturing to the U.S. economy and helps encourage people to pursue a career in manufacturing.
The site offers great jobs in a modernized facility that is transforming the transportation industry.
source_title: President Joe Biden visits Volvo plant in Maryland | Trucks, Parts, Service

facts: Volvo Group North America, a part of Sweden’s AB Volvo, is planning to lay off up to 800 workers across three U.S. facilities over the next three months.
The company shared news of the layoffs with employees recently.
These layoffs are part of a larger trend in the auto and truck industry reacting to U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariffs.
According to a Reuters report, Volvo Group North America announced it will lay off 550 to 800 workers.
The layoffs will affect its Mack Trucks plant in Macungie, Pennsylvania.
The layoffs will also affect two other sites in Dublin, Virginia, and Hagerstown, Maryland.
The layoffs are due to concerns over market uncertainty and reduced demand caused by tariffs introduced by Trump.
Volvo Group North America is a division of Sweden-based AB Volvo.
As per its website, the company employs around 20,000 people across North America.
President Trump’s trade policies, including tariffs on imported goods, have disrupted the global trade system that has been in place for over 75 years.
These sudden changes have lowered confidence among both consumers and businesses.
Economists are warning of a potential recession due to these market conditions.
The layoffs at Volvo are part of a broader trend in the vehicle manufacturing sector struggling with the impact of tariffs on imported parts.
Tariffs are increasing production costs for automakers.
A Volvo Group North America spokesperson stated that orders for heavy-duty trucks are falling due to uncertain freight rates, possible new regulations, and tariff effects.
A Volvo Group North America spokesperson said, “We regret having to make this decision, but we must adjust production to match the lower demand for our vehicles.”
source_title: Volvo Group Layoffs: Up to 800 Employees to Lose Jobs in the U.S. – MySandesh

facts: The Energy Department awarded $208 million to Volvo Technology of America to help fast-track the production of heavy-duty electric trucks and related powertrain components.
The funding will assist Volvo Group’s Lehigh Valley Operations truck assembly site in Macungie, Pennsylvania.
The funding will assist Volvo Group’s New River Valley truck location in Dublin, Virginia.
Dublin, Virginia is Volvo’s largest truck manufacturing plant in the world.
Facilities will be upgraded and move toward mixed model assembly, allowing for scalability and flexibility.

Pennsylvania Sen. Bob Casey stated that the upgrades enable a novel manufacturing approach that will significantly increase the production capacity potential of battery electric vehicles (BEV)/fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEV).
A project summary noted that the changes will help improve BEV and FCEV production processes.
The money will help convert the facilities to zero-emission sites, part of Volvo Group’s goal to be 100% fossil-free by 2040.
A Hagerstown, Maryland, plant will get upgrades to support powertrain operations, according to a Volvo spokesperson.

Stephen Roy, chairman of Volvo Group North America and president of Mack Trucks, stated that through facility upgrades and employee training, this grant will help our U.S. plants more efficiently produce the innovative trucks and zero-emission powertrain components essential to this transition.
Funding comes through the Domestic Manufacturing Conversion grant program.
The program will create 295 new union jobs for Volvo and Mack, according to Sen. Casey.
The $2 billion program is available through Sept. 30, 2031.
The program supports domestic production of hybrid, electric and hydrogen fuel cell vehicles, per a federal website.

ZF Axle Drives Marysville was awarded nearly $158 million to convert part of its Marysville, Michigan, facility from internal combustion engine driveline component production to EV components for potential new customers.
Cummins will spend $75 million and receive a matching amount to convert an approximately 360,000-square-foot portion of an engine plant in Columbus, Indiana.
The conversion is for the manufacturing of zero-emission components and electric powertrain systems.
Cummins stated that the $75 million grant is the largest federal grant ever awarded solely to Cummins and is part of appropriations related to the Inflation Reduction Act.
Cummins plans to reach net-zero emissions by 2050.
source_title: Volvo to receive $208M to accelerate heavy-duty electric truck production | WardsAuto

facts: One of High Point’s largest employers announced a $41 million expansion in the city.
Volvo Group North America will construct a 62,000-square-foot addition to its U.S. Uptime Center at 8003 Piedmont Triad Parkway.
The addition will house the global headquarters for Volvo Financial Services.
The facility has a Greensboro mailing address but is located in the city of High Point.
The facility serves as a customer service center for the company’s commercial truck lines and employs about 600 people.
Volvo bought the 122,223-square-foot building and the 10.58 acres it sits on in December for $27.8 million.
The building opened in 2014 and was previously leased by the company.
The facility is part of Volvo Group North America’s corporate campus, most of which is located in Greensboro, just south of Interstate 40.
Once the addition is complete early next year, Volvo Financial Services will relocate its 360 employees from its current leased office in Greensboro about 2 miles away.
The addition will be connected by a glass atrium and two walkways, bringing the total size of the three-story building to about 185,000 square feet.
Volvo Financial Services is the financing arm for customers of Mack Trucks and Volvo Trucks, as well as the company’s buses and construction equipment.
Volvo Financial Services operates in 50 countries and has 1,700 employees.
Volvo Group North America has about 3,100 total employees in the Triad region.
Marcio Pedroso, Volvo Financial Services president, stated that moving Volvo Financial Services allows all Volvo Group entities in the Piedmont to be located on one campus.
Co-location will enable deeper relationships with colleagues and help retain highly skilled, world-class jobs that support the local economy.
source_title: Volvo Group to expand in High Point | News | hpenews.com

facts: Volvo Construction Equipment will begin producing crawler excavators and large wheel loaders in Shippensburg, Pennsylvania.
The Shippensburg site will add capabilities to produce mid- to large-size excavators, thereby increasing its global industrial footprint and improving flexibility.
The total global investment of $261 million aims to strengthen Volvo CE's crawler excavator position across three main production sites in South Korea, Sweden, and the United States.
In addition, the Shippensburg site will add four large wheel loader models to its current wheel loader production.
Production is planned to begin in the first half of 2026.
Approximately $261 million will be invested across three main sites (Shippensburg, Penn., USA; Changwon, South Korea; and a location in Sweden) to expand crawler excavator production.
The investment aims to meet growing customer demands, mitigate supply chain risks, and reduce reliance on long-distance logistics.
Updates will be made to the existing space within the Shippensburg factory to install assembly lines, integrate more automation technologies into the manufacturing process, and train employees.
Melker Jernberg serves as the Head of Volvo CE.
Scott Young serves as the Head of Region North America for Volvo Construction Equipment.
Over 50% of Volvo CE's North American machine supply can be built in Shippensburg as a result of the increased production capacity.
The increase in production capacity will result in shorter lead times while creating opportunities for supplier growth.
Supply chain risks will be mitigated by expanding domestic supplier bases, allowing the company to more nimbly manage economic or regulatory challenges.
Fostering collaboration with local suppliers and customers will better position the company for sustained growth and innovation.
Volvo CE acquired the Shippensburg site in 2007 from Ingersoll Rand.
Volvo CE relocated its regional headquarters to Shippensburg in 2012.
On top of recent investments, Volvo CE will invest approximately $40 million locally over the next five years.
The announcement is described as a win for central Pennsylvania, showing the company's continued commitment to the local community and its economic vitality.
Volvo Construction Equipment is a leading international manufacturer of premium construction equipment with over 10,000 employees.
Volvo CE offers a wide range of products and services in more than 140 countries through its global distribution network.
Volvo CE is part of the Volvo Group, which employs more than 101,000 people and serves customers in almost 190 markets.
In 2024, the Volvo Group's net sales amounted to SEK 527 billion (EUR 46 billion).

links.url: YouTube: Volvo Construction Equipment – North America

source_title: FinancialContent - Volvo Construction Equipment to Expand Production in North America

facts: Volvo Trucks North America will showcase the Class 8 Volvo VNR Electric truck at the Green Transportation Summit & Expo (GTSE) from August 20-22 in Tacoma, Washington.
Volvo Trucks is a Presenting Ride & Drive Sponsor of the 13th annual West Coast expo.
The GTSE ride and drive event takes place on Tuesday, August 20, from 2:00 to 4:30 pm PT at the Tacoma Dome.
Participants must register, complete a waiver, and present a valid commercial driver’s license (CDL) to operate the battery-electric heavy-duty truck on a closed course.
A Volvo VNR Electric will be displayed in the exhibit hall for attendees to examine specifications and speak with a product expert.
The Volvo VNR Electric is engineered to achieve a range of up to 275 miles.
The VNR Electric offers customizable vehicle configurations tailored for local and regional pickup and delivery, drayage hauls, and food-related product distribution.
There are currently over 540 Volvo VNR Electric trucks in customer operations.
The VNR Electric has accumulated over 6.5 million miles of service since its commercial launch in 2021.

Dawn Fenton, vice president of government relations and public affairs for Volvo Group North America, will moderate the kick-off plenary session 'Paving the Way for ZEV (Zero-Emission Vehicles) Trucks in Washington State' on August 21 from 10:15 to 11:30 am PT.

Dawn Fenton leads a panel of real-world practitioners discussing incentive programs and reliable charging infrastructure networks to support zero-emission commercial vehicle adoption in Washington state.
Dawn Fenton serves as board chair for the Powering America’s Commercial Transportation (PACT) coalition, which advocates for ZEV infrastructure for medium- and heavy-duty vehicles.
Dawn Fenton has 30 years of experience in government relations across e-mobility, energy, environment, sustainability, foreign policy, and trade-related issues.

Keith Brandis, vice president of policy and regulatory affairs at Volvo Group Trucks Technology, stated that GTSE is a premier platform for showcasing zero-emission technologies for clean freight transportation.
The Volvo VNR Electric is highlighted as a standout in the zero-emission technology category.
Volvo Group aims to achieve 35% zero-emission vehicle sales by 2030.
Volvo Group targets a 100% CO2 reduction by 2040.

As part of the PACT coalition, Volvo Group contributes to clean transportation efforts by informing industry stakeholders, policymakers, fleet owners, and utility companies about sustainable future achievements.
GTSE is a leading Pacific Northwest resource for fleets, community organizations, industry professionals, policymakers, and thought leaders to advance clean transportation.
The three-day GTSE event includes networking opportunities, information sessions, workshops, and vehicle walkthroughs.
In 2023, GTSE attracted more than 1,000 attendees, 75 exhibitors, and 135 subject matter experts on mobility-related subjects.
source_title: Volvo Trucks Showcasing Sustainability and Freight Efficiency with VNR Electric Ride & Drive at GTSE 2024 - Fleet News Daily  : Fleet News Daily

entity_name: Volvo CE and Portable Electric Partnership
canonical_name: Volvo Construction Equipment and Portable Electric Partnership
facts: Volvo Construction Equipment (Volvo CE) and Portable Electric have formed a partnership to provide sustainable mobile charging solutions for construction sites.
Through the partnership, the companies will offer the Voltstack 30k Level 2 Mobile EV Charger.

The mobile charging station caters to a wide range of electric equipment requirements such as boost charging or providing recharging services whenever needed including on temporary or remote worksites.
Volvo CE's 245 North American dealer locations will enable customers to bundle the Voltstack 30k Level 2 mobile electric charger with the purchase of Volvo electric equipment.
The Voltstack 30k Level 2 e-Charger is equipped with 30 kW power output and 80 kWh battery capacity.
The Voltstack 30k Level 2 e-Charger will play a role in the recharging of Volvo's ECR25 excavator, the L20 and L25 wheel loaders as well as other electric construction equipment.
In addition to providing a variety of charging solutions, the unit provides a source of silent, emission-free power on jobsites.
The Voltstack 30k can also be hybridized with a generator to provide off-grid power through its “Smart Hybrid” feature.
This feature allows the Voltstack 30k to automatically engage a diesel generator when it reaches a state of low charge.

Ray Gallant, VP sustainability and productivity services at Volvo CE, stated they are pleased to partner with Portable Electric to offer customers an efficient, sustainable and off-grid mobile charging solution for their Volvo electric equipment.

Bundling charging infrastructure like the Voltstack 30k Level 2 e-Charger with the purchase of electric excavators or wheel loaders supports customers in reducing the carbon footprint of construction sites and improving the overall environmental impact of their projects, according to Gallant.
Scott Hardy, CEO of Portable Electric, stated they are excited to partner with Volvo CE to provide the Voltstack 30k Level 2 e-Charger to customers across North America.

Hardy added that the charger's silent operation and emission-free power source make it an ideal mobile charging solution for construction sites where noise, air pollution and recharge times are a concern.

Both companies intend to help reduce construction operations' environmental impact and support the transition to a more sustainable future by providing customers with a reliable mobile charging solution.
source_title: Volvo CE-Portable Electric Partnership to Offer Voltstack 30k L2 Mobile Charger From: Volvo Group | For Construction Pros

facts: Global Lithium-ion Battery Recycling Market Size is valued at USD 18.54 Billion in 2025 and is predicted to reach USD 88.17 Billion by the year 2035 at a 17.0% CAGR during the forecast period for 2026 to 2035.
The rise in popularity of electric vehicles is one of the main driving factors for the lithium-ion battery recycling industry.
Incentives and policies that promote the recycling of lithium-ion batteries encourage businesses and consumers to engage in recycling programs, boosting product demand.
Asia Pacific dominated the market and accounted for a revenue share of global revenue in 2025.
One of the significant concerns restraining industry growth is the absence of standardized procedures for collecting, transporting, and recycling lithium-ion batteries.

Lithium-ion battery recycling refers to gathering lithium-ion batteries from various sources, such as automobiles, industrial equipment, consumer goods, and electronic devices, as well as the recovery of metals through recycling procedures.
The global lithium-ion battery recycling market is anticipated to expand due to governmental laws, environmental safety, and public awareness.

The availability of protective layers to reduce the risk of fire and short-circuiting, as well as the attractive design of batteries, are predicted to open up new growth potential in the global market.

Battery recycling businesses will likely have a huge opportunity to invest in and refocus their resources to develop a breakthrough battery recycling technology due to advancements in battery technologies.
The market for lithium-ion battery recycling is constrained by the high capital costs associated with building new recycling facilities and the specialization of supply and collecting chains.

Increased recycling of other chemistries like lead-acid batteries and the lack of an appropriate legislative framework in many developing countries to remove battery materials may hinder the Lithium-ion Battery Recycling market expansion.

Major lithium-ion battery recycling market players include: Accurec Recycling Gmbh, Akkuser Oy, American Battery Technology Company, American Manganese Inc., American Zinc Recycling, Batrec Industrie Ag, Battery Recycling Made Easy (Brme), Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., Limited, Duesenfeld Gmbh, Ecobat, Envirostream Australia Pty Ltd, Euro Dieuze Industrie (E.D.I.), Fortum Corporation, Ganfeng Lithium Co., Ltd, Gem Co., Ltd, Glencore International Ag, Li-Cycle Corp, Lithion Recycling Inc., Metal Conversion Technologies (Mct), Neometals Ltd, Onto Technology Llc, Raw Materials Company (Rmc), Redwood Materials, Inc., Retriev Technologies Inc, Sitrasa, Smc Recycling, Tata Chemicals Limited, Umicore, Urecycle Group Oy.

The market is segmented by source (automotive and non-automotive including industrial, power, marine, consumer electronics), recycling process (hydrometallurgical, pyrometallurgical, physical/mechanical), battery component (active material: cathode/positive, anode/negative; non-active material: electrolyte, separator materials, other material), and battery chemistry (lithium nickel manganese cobalt, lithium iron phosphate, lithium manganese oxide, lithium titanate oxide, lithium nickel cobalt aluminum oxide).

The consumer electronics segment accounts for a major contributor in the Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling Market due to growing demand for smartphones, laptops, and tablets, end-of-life disposal needs, environmental awareness, and valuable metals like lithium and cobalt.

The Lithium Nickel Cobalt Aluminum Oxide (NCA) segment witnessed rapid growth due to rising use in EVs and energy storage, high energy density, sustainability efforts, regulations, and recovery of nickel, cobalt, and lithium.
North America is expected to hold a significant revenue share in the near future, driven by high adoption of photovoltaic systems and growing demand for electric vehicles.
In several nations in North America, the automobile industry is a significant consumer of lithium-ion batteries, driving market revenue growth.
Asia Pacific is projected to grow at a rapid rate due to growing EV popularity and developing economies' consumer understanding of environmental advantages.
In Feb 2022, Glencore and Britishvolt agreed to a strategic agreement to create a world-class battery recycling ecosystem in the UK.

In July 2021, Lohum Cleantech announced plans to increase production and recycling capacities over the next two years, raising recycling capacity from 1,000 tonnes per annum (TPA) to 10,000 TPA and increasing LIB output from 300 MWh to 1 GWh.
Market size value in 2025 is USD 18.54 Billion.
Revenue forecast in 2035 is USD 88.17 Billion.
Growth rate CAGR is 17.0% from 2026 to 2035.
Historic year is 2022 to 2024.
Forecast year is 2026-2035.
Report covers forecast of revenue, company position, competitive market statistics, growth prospects, and trends.
Segments covered: Source, Recycling Process, Battery Component, Battery Chemistry.
Regional scope: North America; Europe; Asia Pacific; Latin America; Middle East & Africa.
Country scope: U.S.; Canada; U.K.; Germany; China; India; Japan; Brazil; Mexico; France; Italy; Spain; South Korea; Southeast Asia.

Competitive landscape includes Ganfeng Lithium Co., Ltd., American Battery Technology Company, Accurec Recycling GmbH, Akkuser Oy, Duesenfeld GmbH, Li-Cycle Corp., Fortum Corporation, Retriev Technologies, Inc., Lithion Recycling, Inc., and Umicore.
Customization scope includes free customization report with procurement, modifications to regional/segment scope, and particular geographic competitive landscape.
Research approach uses secondary research, primary research, data triangulation, hybrid top-down/bottom-up modeling, forecasting, and scenario analysis.

Secondary research sources include government databases, regulatory bodies, international organizations (WHO, OECD, IMF, World Bank), commercial/paid databases, industry associations, company reports, academic papers, patents, and previous market research publications.

Primary interviews involved manufacturers, suppliers, distributors, end-users, industry experts, technology specialists, consultants, regulatory professionals, senior executives, and product managers.
Interview methods included structured/semi-structured questionnaires, telephonic/video interactions, email correspondences, and expert consultation sessions.
Data processing includes standardization of units, cross-verification, normalization, gap resolution, outlier detection, and plausibility checks.

Market size estimation uses bottom-up (company revenues, product sales, installed base, adoption rates, pricing) and top-down (parent market benchmarks, macro indicators, GDP, demographics) approaches with hybrid triangulation.
Forecasting methods include time-series modeling, S-curve/diffusion models, driver-based forecasting, price elasticity models, and market maturity/lifecycle projections.
Scenario analysis includes Base-Case, Optimistic, and Conservative scenarios with sensitivity testing on pricing, demand elasticity, and regional adoption.
source_title: Lithium-ion Battery Recycling Market Latest Report 2026

entity_name: Volvo Group / Volvo Trucks North America New River Valley
canonical_name: Volvo Trucks North America New River Valley
facts: Governor Ralph Northam announced that Volvo Group will invest nearly $400 million to expand its Volvo Trucks North America New River Valley (NRV) assembly operation in Pulaski County.
Major components of the investment include a new 350,000-square-foot building that will ultimately house truck cab welding operations.
The expansion includes improvements to the existing plant's paint operations and overall material/production flow.
Equipment upgrades include installation of several state-of-the-art dynamometers for vehicle testing.
Volvo considered sites in multiple other states for the expansion project.
The project will create 777 new jobs within approximately six years.
Volvo Trucks is the leading private employer in the New River Valley.
Volvo Trucks has been a bedrock of the community for more than forty years and has fueled the regional economy.
The expansion represents one of the largest capital investments in the history of Southwestern Virginia.
Volvo Trucks provides complete transport solutions for customers, offering a full range of medium to heavy duty trucks.
Customer support is secured via a global network of 2,100 dealers and workshops in more than 130 countries.
Volvo trucks are assembled in 15 countries across the globe.
In 2018, more than 127,000 Volvo trucks were delivered worldwide.
Volvo Trucks is part of Volvo Group, one of the world’s leading manufacturers of trucks, buses, construction equipment, and marine and industrial engines.
The Group also provides solutions for financing and service.
Volvo Trucks’ work is based on the core values of quality, safety, and environmental care.
The Volvo Trucks facility in Pulaski County is its largest in the world, with 1.6 million square feet on nearly 300 acres and 3,500 employees.

Secretary of Commerce and Trade Brian Ball stated that Volvo’s historic investment signifies the plant’s future growth and strengthens Virginia’s corporate partnership with Volvo Trucks North America.
The New River Valley is a hub for manufacturers from around the world due to its access to markets, robust workforce, and pro-business climate.
Franky Marchand is Vice President and General Manager of the NRV plant.
The investment will give employees tools to continue providing customers with the highest quality products.

The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Pulaski County, the General Assembly’s Major Employment and Investment (MEI) Project Approval Commission, and Onward New River Valley to secure the expansion for Virginia.
The Volvo Group will be eligible to receive post-performance MEI custom grant payments totaling up to $16.5 million over 10 years.
Grant eligibility is based upon achieving pledged net new job creation and investment commitments.
New job creation will be on top of the baseline employment level of 3,219 in place when project negotiations began in 2018.
The company is also eligible to receive state benefits from the Virginia Enterprise Zone Program, administered by the Virginia Department of Housing and Community Development.
Chair of the Pulaski County Board of Supervisors Andy McCready expressed excitement to partner with the state to support Volvo Trucks and its workforce.
Senator Frank Ruff is Chairman of the MEI Project Approval Commission.
The project will enable the company to add nearly 800 new jobs to its existing workforce.
Delegate Nick Rush is Vice Chair of the MEI Project Approval Commission.
The NRV assembly plant is Volvo Trucks’ largest manufacturing facility in the world.
The company has been able to grow in Pulaski County for decades with support from local, regional, and state economic development partners.
Senator Benton Chafin highlighted the opportunity for hard-working people of Southwest Virginia and the New River Valley.
The investment creates nearly 800 manufacturing jobs.
source_title: Governor Northam Announces Volvo Group to Create 777 New Jobs in Pulaski County Expansion | Virginia Economic Development Partnership

facts: Rep. Peter DeFazio, Chair of the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, visited Volvo Trucks North America’s TEC Equipment dealership location in Coburg to test-drive an electric Volvo Class 8 truck.
DeFazio highlighted his clean energy priorities as he prepares to introduce transformative infrastructure legislation that creates jobs and stimulates the economy.

Chair DeFazio stated: “For years, climate change deniers have argued that big businesses would never choose to invest in clean technologies because it wouldn’t make business sense—but now, big corporations are showing that there is a business case for climate solutions“.
DeFazio is working with President Biden to move the American Jobs Plan through Congress.
The American Jobs Plan aims to make investments that move the country toward cleaner, greener technologies and a zero-pollution transportation sector.
The plan addresses climate change and creates thousands of good-paying jobs while strengthening an economy reeling from the COVID-19 pandemic.
As Chair of the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, DeFazio is expected to be a principal author and negotiator of President Biden’s American Jobs Plan.
The American Jobs Plan is a wide-sweeping plan aimed at fixing and modernizing the nation's outdated infrastructure system.
DeFazio plans to introduce legislative text in the coming weeks laying out transformative investments in roads, bridges, transit and rail.
The legislation includes funding to help build out charging capacity to accommodate the shift to electric vehicles, including passenger vehicles, freight trucks, and buses.
DeFazio has fought to ensure any infrastructure plan invests in clean energy technologies to drastically cut greenhouse gas emissions from the transportation sector.
The transportation sector is currently the number one source of carbon pollution in the U.S.
DeFazio recently held a hearing in his Committee about the private sector’s transition to clean energy.
FedEx’s CEO testified at the hearing about the company’s plans to move to an all-electric fleet.
According to the Business Roundtable, inadequate infrastructure costs U.S. businesses $27 billion per year in extra transportation costs.
According to the American Society of Civil Engineers, between 2016-2025, the economy will lose almost $4 trillion in economic activity if infrastructure is not upgraded.
Failure to upgrade infrastructure will cost 2.5 million jobs.
Chair DeFazio was joined by Dave Thompson, founder and CEO of TEC Equipment.
Alan Zelenka, Assistant Director for Planning and Innovation at the Oregon Department of Energy, attended the event.
Jeff Allen, Executive Director of Forth Mobility, a nonprofit organization advancing electric, shared and smart transportation, was present.
Jonathan Miller, Senior Vice President of Public Affairs at Volvo Group North America, joined DeFazio.
Volvo Group North America is committed to electrification and other efforts to support the decarbonization of transportation and sustainability of its environment.
Volvo launched the Volvo VNR Electric Class 8 truck model.
Jonathan Miller stated: “Last year, the Volvo Group announced plans in line with the Paris Agreement to be fossil-fuel free by 2040.”
The Volvo Group is currently taking orders and delivering on plans to commercially launch battery-electric trucks, buses and construction equipment in the United States and Canada.
source_title: Rep. Peter DeFazio Stresses Importance of Clean Energy in American Jobs Plan | The HeavyQuip Magazine

facts: Governor Terry McAuliffe announced that the Volvo Group will invest $38.1 million at the New River Valley (NRV) assembly facility in Pulaski County.
The NRV assembly facility is where all Volvo Trucks models for North America are assembled.
The investment will allow Volvo to make several plant upgrades and build a 36,000-square-foot Volvo Trucks Customer Experience Center on the NRV grounds.
Volvo will add 32 new jobs at the NRV facility as part of this investment.
Governor McAuliffe stated that growing the advanced manufacturing industry is a key part of efforts to create jobs, diversify, and build a new Virginia economy.

Governor McAuliffe noted that the NRV facility is the largest Volvo truck manufacturing facility in the world and expressed pleasure that the Volvo Group chose Virginia for this significant expansion to ensure its success and longevity in Pulaski County.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Pulaski County to secure the project for Virginia.
Governor McAuliffe approved a $100,000 grant from the Commonwealth's Opportunity Fund to assist Pulaski County with the project.
Governor McAuliffe also approved a $2 million performance-based grant from the Virginia Investment Partnership program, an incentive available to existing Virginia companies.
The company is eligible to receive state benefits from the Virginia Enterprise Zone Program, administered by the Virginia Department of Housing and Community Development.
Additional funding and services to support the company's employee training activities will be provided through the Virginia Jobs Investment Program, administered by VEDP.
source_title: Volvo Group to invest $38.1 million in Pulaski County

facts: Volvo Group North America reached an agreement with the California Air Resources Board (CARB) regarding emissions compliance.
The settlement addresses allegations concerning the accuracy of emissions control descriptions for engines in Volvo trucks manufactured between 2010 and 2016 that were sold in California.
Volvo Group will pay a total of USD 12.5 million in civil penalties as part of the resolution.
Volvo Group will contribute USD 71.0 million to CARB's Air Pollution Control Fund.
Volvo committed to spending an additional USD 108 million on projects aimed at reducing emissions in California, requiring a plan submission for CARB approval within one year.
Volvo agreed to reimburse CARB for costs amounting to USD 5 million associated with the investigation.
The settlement is expected to negatively impact Volvo's operating income for the second quarter of 2026 by approximately USD 196.5 million (around SEK 1.8 billion).
Volvo anticipates a negative operating cash flow impact of USD 89 million (about SEK 0.8 billion) during the same quarter.
The financial ramifications of the settlement will progressively unfold over the next five years.
An internal review by Volvo Group found no evidence of any misconduct, and the settlement does not constitute an admission of liability.
Volvo Group will provide software updates and a partial warranty extension for around 7,200 engines manufactured from 2014 to 2016 in California.
Volvo emphasized that there are no identified performance or safety concerns regarding the engines implicated in this issue.
Volvo Group was founded in 1927 and is headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden.
The company has nearly 100,000 employees serving clients in approximately 180 markets.
In 2025, Volvo Group recorded net sales of SEK 479 billion (approx. EUR 43 billion).
source_title: Volvo Group North America Enters Settlement Over Emissions Compliance with CARB - Third News

entity_name: EV and Automotive Supply Chain News
canonical_name: Hagerty Media EV and Automotive News Roundup
facts: BYD is the world’s largest manufacturer of electric vehicles, producing 1.86 million electric and plug-in hybrids last year.
The Inflation Reduction Act offers a $7500 rebate on EVs but imposes battery material sourcing rules that disqualify EVs produced outside North America from receiving it.
BYD previously planned to display its cars and technology at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas but cancelled the appearance.
A source indicated that selling vehicles with a $7500 disadvantage compared to domestic competitors is a major barrier to U.S. market entry.
BYD already manufactures electric buses at a California factory.
BYD is backed by Warren Buffet’s investment firm and may eventually scale production to three million electric vehicles per year.
Alfa Romeo will offer a single plug-in hybrid powertrain for the 2024 Tonale in the United States market.
The Tonale PHEV combines a 1.3-liter turbocharged four-cylinder engine driving the front wheels with a 90-kW electric motor driving the rear wheels for a total of 285 horsepower.
The Tonale uses a 15.5-kWh battery to achieve a pure electric range of more than 30 miles in the U.S. market.
Alfa Romeo removed a planned 2-liter turbocharged four-cylinder non-hybrid option from the U.S.-spec Tonale lineup, though Canadian and Mexican buyers will retain that choice.
Mazda’s 2024 CX-90 crossover will feature a 3.3-liter turbocharged inline-six engine producing up to 340 hp and 369 lb-ft of torque when using premium fuel.
The CX-90 powertrain incorporates 48-volt hybrid technology, though specific details regarding motor placement and battery type remain undisclosed.
Mazda will offer a plug-in hybrid variant of the CX-90 that likely utilizes a 17.8-kWh battery system to deliver up to 35 miles of electric-only range.
source_title: Volvo splits North American operations, Alfa Tonale pricing revealed, Mazda's most powerful engine yet - Hagerty Media

facts: Volvo Group is planning to lay off as many as 800 workers across three US facilities within the next three months.
Reuters reported the layoffs citing a spokesperson.
The decision comes amidst market uncertainty and demand concerns, which have been exacerbated by the tariffs imposed by US President Donald Trump.
Volvo Group North America confirmed the layoffs are expected to affect the Mack Trucks site in Macungie, Pennsylvania.
The layoffs are also expected to affect two other facilities in Dublin, Virginia, and Hagerstown, Maryland.
Volvo Group North America represented about 29% of total 2024 sales for Sweden-based Volvo Group.
Volvo Group employs more than 19,600 people in North America.
The company operates 16 manufacturing and remanufacturing facilities in the US, Canada and Mexico.
Volvo Group's layoffs are part of a broader response from the car and truck industry struggling with the impact of tariffs on certain parts imposed by the US Government.
Tariffs have raised vehicle manufacturing costs.
The article was originally created and published by Just Auto, a GlobalData owned brand.
source_title: Volvo Group to cut up to 800 jobs at US facilities – report

facts: Volvo CE arrived at CONEXPO 2026 under the theme "Power Your Ambition" with a 55,000-square-foot booth at the Festival Grounds.
The company showcased a next-generation lineup of more than 20 machines, including 14 new or first-look models across electric and conventional power.
The booth featured live demos, hands-on operating experiences, and integrated displays from Volvo Trucks and Volvo Financial Services.

Melker Jernberg, President of Volvo CE, stated that CONEXPO is about listening to customers to support their ambitions and that the company brought machines and services ready to deliver today rather than just showing future concepts.
Approximately 40% of the Volvo Group portfolio has been renewed since the industry last gathered in Las Vegas.
Volvo CE alone has updated more than 35% of its machines using a multi-technology approach that includes battery-electric, internal combustion engine, and grid-connected solutions.
The biggest product updates are across excavators, wheel loaders, and articulated haulers, which together represent around 70% of the global construction market.
Volvo CE experts demonstrated how tools like Load Ticket, Connected Map, and ActiveCare® Direct transform raw data into uptime and safety inside an immersive "site office."
The company highlighted its growing Equipment as a Service (EaaS) model, which offers guaranteed availability and predictable costs through a simple pay-for-use structure.

Scott Young, President of Volvo Construction Equipment North America, noted that combining renewed machines with services and digital tools helps customers work safer, more productively, and with lower total cost of ownership.

The Volvo Group reinforced its long-term commitment to North America through $1.2 billion USD in strategic investments designed to strengthen manufacturing capabilities, regionalize supply chains, and support sustainable technologies.
A centerpiece of this strategy is a $40 million USD investment in the Shippensburg, Pa., facility.
The Shippensburg investment brings production of excavators and four large wheel loader models to the U.S. in early 2026.
This facility expansion is part of a significant Volvo CE investment in the global excavator manufacturing footprint.

Attendees operated compact diesel and mid-size electric excavators, including the 23-ton EC230 Electric excavator, noting that its performance matches expectations for demanding applications without compromise.
Appearances by "Gold Rush" star Tony Beets, YouTuber LetsDig18, and other influencers helped connect Volvo CE innovations to industry professionals.
Melker Jernberg emphasized that meeting customers where they are and leveraging the full strength of the Volvo Group ensures customer success, which in turn benefits communities.

links.context: Learn more about Volvo CE at CONEXPO
Head of Brand, Marketing and Communication contact

source_title: Volvo at CONEXPO | Volvo Construction Equipment Global

facts: Wabash National Corp. announced an alliance with trailer tracking system provider Terion.
The alliance is expected to play a key role in the development of smarter trailers.
Wabash National will factory install Terion’s FleetView Trailer Monitoring System.
Wabash will provide sales, installation and service through its nationwide distribution network.

The companies agreed to co-develop and market advanced trailer management systems that improve trailer utilization and fleet productivity by integrating FleetView with Wabash’s Smart Trailer technology.
Wabash chairman Jerry Ehrlich said the company is expected to begin production of its MBS2 Smart Trailer system next month.
The MBS2 system will have added "brainpower" to handle a number of other trailer sensing and recording technologies.
The future system co-developed with Terion will be branded as RealView.
RealView will store an electronic trailer ID number.
RealView will track trailer mileage.
RealView will sense wheel rotation direction to trigger backup lights and alarms when the trailer is backing.
RealView will sense distance to a dock as the trailer is being backed.
RealView will automatically lift steerable axles when the trailer is being backed up.
RealView will monitor heat build-up at wheel spindles and warn of imminent bearing failure and/or wheel loss.
RealView will monitor brake stroke and warn when it is out of adjustment.
RealView will monitor brake lining wear.
Terion's system will allow fleets to monitor critical trailer systems remotely.
A fleet could potentially manage its trailers entirely from a computer.
Fleets could find the trailer, determine if it's dispatchable (is it empty, are the brakes OK?), handle maintenance on demand, and prevent catastrophic failures such as wheel loss.
Terion and Wabash will offer the RealView product and services as an OEM installation, as well as on a retrofit basis.
The two companies first announced their intent to form an alliance in March.
source_title: Wabash Aligns With Terion, Plans For Smarter Trailers | Heavy Duty Trucking

facts: Wabash (NYSE: WNC) will participate in LINK: The Retail Supply Chain Conference, hosted by the Retail Industry Leaders Association, February 19-22, 2023, in Orlando, Fla.
LINK 2023 is an event for retailers, consumer product manufacturers, and service providers designed to help retail teams shape their supply chains to be stronger, smarter and sustainable.

Wabash will have an information and product exhibit to show its customer-focused solutions, from groundbreaking technology in refrigerated transport to upfitting options and fleet management services.

Tim Griesgraber, vice president, sales and marketing at Wabash, stated the company looks forward to introducing its innovative, end-to-end solutions and approach to strategic partnerships to the broader retail supply chain industry.

At LINK 2023, Wabash will feature future-forward cold chain solutions, sustainability-focused dry freight delivery, and fleet management efficiency that leverages technology to connect across the entire transportation system.

On display will be Wabash’s Acutherm™ Refrigerated Delivery Body with EcoNex™ Technology, part of the company’s Acutherm portfolio of intelligent thermal management solutions, designed to uphold food integrity with built-in sustainability.
Wabash’s proprietary EcoNex Technology is being engineered for improved thermal performance and weight reduction.

The Acutherm Refrigerated Delivery Body with EcoNex Technology allows customers to increase payload while lowering fuel cost and TRU hours, which can help reduce carbon footprint and total cost of ownership.

Wabash’s refrigerated delivery body is being engineered to accommodate customer interest in equipping an all-electric fleet, as the lighter weight composite is designed to offset the additional weight of battery packs.
In pursuit of a zero-emissions solution for medium-duty vehicles, Wabash will show its Dry Freight Body with DuraPlate® Technology on a Class 4 electric chassis.

This product features next-generation panel technology that reduces weight and increases strength through composite technology advancement in core and skin alternatives, with recycling and repurposing in mind.
LINK attendees can discuss their retail logistics challenges with Wabash representatives and see a sampling of the company’s product solutions and fleet services at booth #2331.

Wabash is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana, and designs, manufactures, and services dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.
source_title: Wabash Brings Sustainability-Focused Solutions to LINK: The Retail Supply Chain Conference | WNC Stock News

facts: Wabash National Corp announced it has recently received orders exceeding $100 million from certain of its customers.
Acting CEO Richard E. Dessimoz stated that indications from core customers show their 2002 requirements will exceed orders placed in 2001.
Dessimoz noted that the company does not anticipate industry fundamentals to dramatically improve in the short term.
A number of key customers have performed relatively well during difficult economic times, which appears to be driving demand for new trailers.
Wabash National Corporation designs, manufactures, and markets standard and customized truck trailers under the Wabash and Fruehauf brands.
The company also manufactures composite trailers under the RoadRailer name.
source_title: Wabash Announces $100 Million In Orders | Heavy Duty Trucking

facts: Wabash National Corp. (NYSE: WNC) announced a plan to expand its national footprint in the cargo van and truck body upfitting space with a new facility in Groveport, Ohio.
The new location will allow for greater speed and innovation to meet needs in the areas of cold chain, home delivery and parts and service.
The facility will service all brands of cargo and truck bodies.
The facility will install several products, including hitches, steel flatbed bodies, thermal solutions, liftgates and more.
Vice President of Parts and Services Nick Adler stated the company is building a culture around speed and safety to extend to customers seeking productivity solutions.
With support from supplier partners, Wabash National is expanding capabilities to provide first to final mile upfitting solutions faster and configured to specific customer needs.
The company plans to hold an open house at the new facility in early 2022.
source_title: Wabash National expanding to Ohio - Indianapolis News | Indiana Weather | Indiana Traffic | WISH-TV |

facts: On Dec. 10, 2013, Wabash National Corporation announced its Commercial Trailer Products group produced its 500,000th DuraPlate van trailer.
The 500,000th DuraPlate trailer was built for Crete Carrier Corporation.
Crete Carrier Corporation is one of the largest privately owned trucking companies in the United States.
Tonn Ostergard, chief executive officer and president of Crete Carrier Corporation, stated that the DuraPlate van provides maximum cube space to help serve customers.
Tonn Ostergard noted that the smooth interior walls reduce cargo damage.
Tonn Ostergard added that the durability of the DuraPlate van provides a quality asset with low total cost of ownership and solid residual value at time of sale or trade.
Wabash National offers a range of DuraPlate dry freight vans designed for superior performance.
The DuraPlate HD van includes a high-strength aluminum base plate in the lower sidewall.
DuraPlate XD-35 is the only dry van with a 35,000-pound floor rating.
DuraPlate Pups are specifically designed to reduce total cost of ownership in less-than-truckload applications.

Brent Yeagy, group president of Commercial Trailer Products at Wabash National, stated that reaching the milestone of building half a million DuraPlate dry vans is a significant accomplishment for associates and customers.
Brent Yeagy noted that the achievement further evidences how customers value the durability, longevity and low total cost of ownership provided by the line of American-made DuraPlate vans.
Nearly 20 of the top 50 truckload and less-than-truckload carriers in the United States use Wabash National DuraPlate vans.
The DuraPlate van was introduced in 1996.
The DuraPlate van was designed for a wide range of freight hauls, providing maximum cube capacity, exceptional durability, lightweight performance and resale values among the best in the industry.
DuraPlate composite sidewall panels are produced by Wabash Composites.
DuraPlate composite sidewall panels are constructed of a high-density polyethylene (HDPE) core bonded between two high-strength steel skins.
The composite sidewall panels provide unmatched damage resistance and maximum interior width.
They are the only composite plate sidewall panels manufactured in the United States.
Wabash National is headquartered in Lafayette, Indiana.

Wabash designs, manufactures, and services dry freight and refrigerated trailers, flatbed trailers, tank trailers, dry and refrigerated truck bodies, structural composite panels and products, trailer aerodynamic solutions, and specialty food grade processing equipment.

facts: Meritor announced that various products have been selected as standard position and preferred options on all Wabash National dry and refrigerated van trailer products.

Meritor’s loose axles will be standard equipment designed to reduce weight and offer structural advantages, contributing to improved fleet productivity through increased fuel efficiency and payload capacity.
The Meritor Tire Inflation System (MTIS) has been selected as the preferred automatic tire inflation system.
Meritor air disc brakes, Meritor Trailing-Arm (MTA) suspension, hubs, drums and slack adjusters, and Meritor’s new MTec6™ large-diameter axle are optional in all Wabash configurations.
The MTec6™ large-diameter axle provides a 40-pound weight savings to a standard axle.
Dave Pritchard serves as general manager, Trailer for Meritor.
Dave Pritchard stated that Meritor greatly values Wabash as a long-term customer and appreciates Wabash selecting Meritor for this important business.
For more than 30 years, Meritor has supplied a variety of trailer axle and braking systems to Wabash.
Wabash is one of the industry’s largest manufacturers of trailers for traditional van and vocational configurations across multiple applications.
Meritor has engineered and manufactured groundbreaking trailer suspensions, axles and brakes for over 60 years.
Meritor has built more than 10 million trailer axles that incorporate features to improve quality, extend life and reduce maintenance.
These axles are delivered within standard lead-times.
More than 8 million fleet tires are currently protected by MTIS.
Almost one in every three trailers built today is specified with the tire inflation system.
According to Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) and U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) studies, tire inflation systems extend tire life by 10 percent.
According to FMCSA and EPA studies, tire inflation systems increase fuel economy an average 1.4 percent.
MTIS decreases maintenance costs due to longer intervals between retreads and less time spent on manual pressure checking and filling.

facts: Beckwood Press Co. is a St. Louis-based manufacturer of hydraulic and servo-electric presses and automation systems.
Beckwood Press has been awarded a contract by Wabash National Corp. to engineer and build a custom 3,500-ton hydraulic bulge forming press.
The press is scheduled for delivery in October 2019.
The machine will be used to form stainless steel and aluminum tank heads with diameters up to 156 in.
The equipment supports Wabash National’s tank and silo business located in Lafayette, Ind.
The press measures 285- by 216-in., weighs about 650,000 lbs. when fully assembled, and will be installed in a 6-ft.-deep pit.
The press features 160- by 160-in. bolsters containing machined t-slots for use with a quick die change system.
A fully adjustable 8-point square gib-guidance system will help ensure bed-to-ram parallelism throughout the stroke.
To accommodate Wabash National’s existing tooling, the press will be able to bulge form using either air or water.
Beckwood will design a closed-loop pneumatic control system for the bulge forming operation.
The control system will allow Wabash National to form parts to specific dome heights while using up to 750 PSI of air pressure.
An automated quick die change system with chain-driven die clamps and a bed trolley will be included.
The trolley will transport dies up to 30,000 lbs. across a 30-ft. track via a push-button controls station.
source_title: Beckwood Press to build 3,500-ton bulge forming press for Wabash National

Pat Keslin serves as Chief Financial Officer of Wabash National Corporation.
John Cummings is the Senior Director of FP&A and Investor Relations at Wabash National Corporation.
Mike Pettit transitioned out of Wabash National Corporation as of April 8th after a 14-year tenure.
Consolidated revenue for the first quarter of FY2026 was $303 million, coming in slightly below the low end of prior guidance.

Wabash intends to address its existing ABL facility ahead of September 2026 when it turns current.

New upfit business sites are opening in three major U.S. metroplexes designed to serve the Chicago, Atlanta, and Phoenix areas.
The new upfit locations aim to improve proximity, reduce lead times, and increase win rates by bringing installation and customization capability closer to customers.
Wabash is already supporting major national accounts out of its Atlanta upfit location.
At peak, the additional upfit sites are expected to generate incremental revenue in the range of $10 million-$20 million per site with gross margins approaching 20%.

Recent Section 232 tariffs and pending Antidumping and Countervailing Duties rulings are expected to provide meaningful relief for the domestic industry, with impacts expected in late 2026 into 2027.
