facts: Wabash National is a truck-trailer manufacturer that narrowed its portfolio and shed product lines including aircraft fueling equipment and healthcare containment systems.
The company streamlined from three sometimes-redundant divisions to one under President and CEO Brent Yeagy, who has led since 2018.
Wabash saw record earnings in 2022 under challenging transportation market conditions.

Recognizing the truck market has become less cyclical due to the boom in on-demand e-commerce delivery, Wabash inked longer-term relationships with key suppliers Hydro for aluminum and Ryerson for steel.
The company secured a customer relationship with J.B. Hunt for 15,000 trailers and partnered with technology provider Fernweh for digital service and e-commerce.
Yeagy and Chief Strategy Officer Dustin Smith led an organizational realignment that ushered in a new C-suite of leaders who had been with the company for an average of eight to 10 years.

The company deviated from its previous strategy of becoming a diversified industrial manufacturer to focus on transportation, logistics and distribution, a pivot the team recognized as necessary as early as 2015.
Wabash moved away from strategic business units to a "OneWabash" approach involving hybrid centralization, changing the roles of almost 40 people while only Yeagy's role remained unstructured.

The previous organizational design was rife with waste and duplicity, stacking dollars and people on non-value-added activity, which the reorganization freed up to redeploy for greater value over the next three to four years.
Centralization allowed the company to pull leadership away from market details to see broader opportunities and reallocate resources quickly in an agile fashion.

Dustin Smith was moved from a strategic business unit leader with dedicated cross-functional resources to a strategy role to aggregate manufacturing and focus on future strategic actions, innovation, and value creation.

Under the new model, sales leaders sell the entire portfolio instead of one product, and engineers design products for multiple segments rather than being trapped in specific product areas like flatbeds.
In 2020, Wabash had deployed over $700 million over the previous eight years to add tanks and truck bodies through acquisition without organizing those business units to serve customers.
The company implemented a new commercial strategy called "first to final mile," bundling product services, pricing, and value creation to increase both growth and profitability simultaneously.

Wabash is partnering with impactful suppliers to provide a total enterprise supply chain, creating five-year-plus agreements on the customer side and five- to 10-year agreements on the supplier side that include demand and capacity planning, advanced product development, and engineering.

Approximately 18 to 20% of annual dry van trailer revenue is allocated to long-term-agreement customers, representing $500 to $600 million a year, with expectations for this to grow slightly to maybe 30% of total dry van trailer revenue.
Product innovation focuses on advanced material science for lightweighting, durability/asset-life, and thermal efficiency in full-chain products.
The company is focusing on electrification and autonomy to aid EV technology adoption and become a leader in autonomous adoption.
In the near-term, Wabash is working through which EV providers customers need to do business with for vocational and delivery vehicles to create the right relationships.
Mid-term focus involves adapting truck bodies, trailers, and tankers for near-term regulatory and weight-related innovation to offset battery weight and maintain revenue-carrying capacity.
Long-term focus includes exploring solar technology, scavenging energy off vehicle movement, and advancing hybrid electric technology to increase EV adoption scale rapidly.
Supply chain challenges have shifted from large-scale disruption to a lack of resiliency across the overall supply chain, extending from tier one to tier four.
The efficiency of the pre-pandemic supply chain has been broken, leaving tier three, four, and five suppliers at an economic disadvantage in an inflationary environment.
Wabash is at the tail end of a freight recession that has lasted about 18 to 20 months and should conclude in the first half of 2024, with demand down year over year but not negative.
The company is at its best balance-sheet perspective ever, coming on record profitability and record earnings per share, enabling continued investment in its strategic plan for ‘25 and ‘26.
source_title: Wabash Makes Big Changes That Are Starting to Pay Off | IndustryWeek

facts: Wheelabrator Group will celebrate the 20th anniversary of its LaGrange, Ga., facility in September.

facts: Wheelabrator Group will celebrate its twenty years in operation at its LaGrange, Ga. facility in September 2007.
The LaGrange location opened on Sept. 21, 1987.
The facility houses Wheelabrator Plus, the aftermarket-sales division that provides a full range of services, replacement parts and training.

LaGrange is also home to the Wheelabrator Group North American Research and Development Center along with a one-of-a-kind Customer Demonstration and Testing Facility and the Equipment Modernization Program (EMP) Team.

The EMP Team focuses on providing equipment upgrades that are effective solutions for customers’ requested improvements in productivity and to reduce the costs associated with the operation and maintenance of their existing blasting equipment.
A core group of employees moved to LaGrange from Mishawaka, Ind., in 1987 to start up the facility.

Pierre Tanguay, Vice President North American Supply Chain at Wheelabrator Group, stated that the Georgia town is located southwest of Atlanta on I-85 and was chosen for its proximity to a major interstate and Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport in Atlanta.
The City of LaGrange worked closely with Wheelabrator to train the local workforce.
The LaGrange facility now employs 130 people, with more than 20 additional employees working in field sales and service.
The LaGrange facility will celebrate its 20th anniversary with a luncheon and employee recognition this fall.
Pierre Tanguay stated that Wheelabrator is the global leader in surface preparation technology and has the talent to stay number one.
Wheelabrator will honor another historical milestone in 2008 as the company celebrates its 100th anniversary.
The company was founded in 1908 in Ohio as the Homogeneous Sand Mixing Machine Company.
The company developed the Wheelabrator® airless blast cleaning wheel in 1932, a simple mechanical unit that used controlled centrifugal force instead of compressed air for abrasive blasting.
The Wheelabrator trademark became part of the corporate name spanning from the 1940s to the present day.
Wheelabrator Group is the global leader in providing surface preparation and finishing solutions.

Committed to offering the broadest array of technologies, products, services and technical know-how, Wheelabrator Group works closely with customers to deliver tailored solutions for their specific requirements.
The company operates strategically located and electronically linked offices worldwide to provide customer service and support.

facts: Wheelabrator designs and manufactures a full range of wheel-type shot blast machines, mass finishing equipment dedicated to the demands of the industry, and automated airblast solutions.
Wheelabrator Plus professionals will inspect your current blasting equipment and conduct a thorough assessment.
Surface preparation is a critical process for clean energy applications, whether the focus is on metal fabrication, assembling wind towers or on the metal components.
Given the harsh field conditions, metal components used in manufacturing wind towers require a surface preparation process prior to assembly.
Mechanical pre-treatment of steel can be carried out using centrifugal blast wheels or using blast nozzles.
Wheelabrator Plate and Structural Steel systems meet the needs of Wind Tower manufacturers.
Wheelabrator plate systems are fully-automated and designed to descale and remove rust from plate.
The Wheelabrator horizontal shot blast plate machine models have been specially developed to meet the needs of various industries, including the Wind Power sector.
The machine effectively pre-cleans large plates for wind tower sections and components, eliminating the high labor costs associated with manual air blasting.
Wheelabrator plate machines are available for plate sizes up to 15-feet wide.
Plate machines can easily be integrated into Wheelabrator Preservation Lines for automatic painting.
Depending on customers’ needs, there are three models to choose from.
Each model comes equipped with a pass height range of 20-inches to 48-inches with custom heights available on request.
The plate machines come with EZEFIT® blast wheels that have a history of outstanding performance and reliability while increasing productivity and reducing maintenance.

The plate system blast compartments are constructed of manganese steel and equipped with replaceable cast ship lapped Long-Lyfe® liners in line designed for a heavy-duty continuous operation using steel shot media.
Roll conveyors are available to meet varying part load and length requirements for individual customers.
A combination of brush and blow-off options also contributes to the ease of automatic operations.
The low profile system provides a reduced overall height, allowing for installation in facilities with limited height requirements.
This enables routine maintenance and inspection actions to be performed near floor level; a convenient and efficient solution for any plant site.
Wheelabrator Group is a global provider of surface preparation and finishing solutions.
The company is committed to offering the broadest array of technologies, products, replacement parts, services and technical know-how.
Wheelabrator Group works closely with customers to deliver the best solutions for their specific requirements.
The company’s strategically located and electronically linked offices allow Wheelabrator Group to provide unrivalled customer service and support worldwide.
source_title: Wheelabrator Group | Wind Systems Magazine

facts: WIKA USA, a leading manufacturer of pressure and temperature measurement instrumentation, is located in Lawrenceville, GA.
WIKA USA launched the MH-4 CAN, a cost-effective and technically capable OEM CAN pressure sensor for mobile working machines.
The MH-4 CAN features advanced electronics, robust sensor design, and comprehensive validation testing to ensure performance stability and operational reliability throughout its life cycle.
Chad Reinhard, Senior Product Manager at WIKA USA, stated that the MH-4 CAN is a game-changer for the mobile working machine industry.
According to Chad Reinhard, customers can benefit from exceptional performance, stability, and reliability, along with the ability to customize and adapt the sensor to specific needs.
The MH-4 and MH-4 CAN are ideal for high-volume production as OEM pressure sensors for any kind of mobile working machinery.
WIKA has over 75 years of experience advancing instrumentation for pressure, temperature, level, and flow measurement.
WIKA embraces digitalization, decarbonization, and demographic change while developing stock and custom instrumentation and engineering services.
WIKA is a global leader in lean manufacturing with manufacturing locations around the globe to meet high delivery demands.

facts: JBS USA announced the planned closure of two facilities in Pennsylvania and Tennessee.

JBS USA is implementing transition plans that include opportunities for team members to apply for open roles at other company facilities across the United States.
Transition plans include on-site support and resources to assist team members through the transition.
Transition plans include continued engagement with local stakeholders and workforce partners.
The closures are part of a broader strategy focused on growth, modernization, and long-term competitiveness in the United States.
Over the past year, JBS USA has made significant investments in new facilities and improvements across the United States, including major expansions in Texas, Georgia, and Iowa.
These projects are focused on growing prepared foods and value-added capabilities, modernizing operations, and enhancing the company’s ability to serve customers in the years ahead.

Earlier this year, JBS USA combined its beef and case-ready businesses into a more integrated platform designed to improve efficiency, enhance productivity, and expand value-added capabilities across its network.
Production from the affected facilities will be absorbed into other operations across its network, ensuring continuity of supply and service for customers.
JBS USA remains committed to U.S. agriculture and rural communities, working closely with producers and grower partners across the country.
CEO Wesley Batista Filho said demand for high-quality protein continues to grow and the company is committed to meeting that demand as a reliable partner to customers, producers, and communities.

facts: Offers comfort technology solutions that deliver optimal control over initial softness and support as well as long-term performance.
Provides a series of branded product lines that focus on optimizing specific performance attributes.

Many products provide safety, climate control, energy absorption, antimicrobial agents, flammability compliance, and anti-submarine qualities.
Products are made using sustainable materials.
Manufacturing and engineering expertise enables addressing challenges, making changes, and running production lines more efficiently than other suppliers.

facts: Woodbridge's scientists developed a process to integrate biogenic carbon (biocarbon) in its purest form into the company's high-performing polyurethane seating foam chemistry.
The new product, TrimVisible™ BIO, enables a cradle-to-gate reduction in carbon dioxide (CO2) footprint for seating foam without compromising material properties, performance and comfort.
This technology ultimately contributes to the reduction in CO2 footprint for the lifetime of the vehicle.
The original TrimVisible™ was recognized for its black color and released as a molded seat foam solution that provided a craftsmanship improvement and lower total seat system cost.
TrimVisible™ pioneered the conversion of the molded seat foam market to black, and this global trend continues today.
TrimVisible™ LE was the second generation offering that established a benchmark for low Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) and low odor performance in the industry.
With the introduction of TrimVisible™ BIO, Woodbridge builds upon its market-leading family of TrimVisible™ products.
TrimVisible™ BIO uniquely delivers lightweight performance and carbon-neutral impact, meeting the needs of the evolving automotive market and electric vehicle transition.
Woodbridge partnered with a certified sustainable managed forest to develop a proprietary process to convert residue from lumber processing into a high-purity form of biocarbon.
The Woodbridge process sequesters biocarbon by effectively locking CO2 in a form that can be used to engineer solutions that significantly reduce the CO2 footprint of products.

Woodbridge scientists formulated chemistry to displace conventional urethane chemicals with biocarbon to manufacture TrimVisible™ BIO, a patent-pending technology that offsets the company's Scope 1 CO2 manufacturing emissions to net-zero.

Mike Irwin, Vice President, Advanced Product and Chemical Technology, stated that the launch of TrimVisible™ BIO adds carbon-neutral foam products to their portfolio to help reduce global warming potential.
Irwin noted this will enable support for customers in reaching their supply chain carbon reduction goals while displacing fossil fuel-based chemicals with a completely sustainable alternative.

Woodbridge is developing a comprehensive line of products based on sustainable technologies, including near- and long-term research and development (R&D) projects to advance circularity in the automotive supply chain.
These R&D activities aim to increase environmentally friendly and bio-derived solutions to mitigate global warming potential and eliminate dependence on non-renewable technologies.

John Zianis, President and CEO, stated that Woodbridge is a world-leading seating foam manufacturer focused on bringing customers climate-neutral, renewable, and sustainable technologies as a key part of its forward-looking product strategy.

Zianis added that through R&D activities, they continue to find ways to succeed in replacing fossil-fuel-derived materials with environmentally friendly and sustainable solutions to improve their future competitive position.
Woodbridge has corporate headquarters in Mississauga, Ontario, Canada, and automotive headquarters in Troy, Michigan, USA.
The company has more than 7,500 teammates in over 50 locations across 10 countries.
Woodbridge offers material technologies for applications in automotive, commercial, recreational, packaging, healthcare and building products.
It offers a full complement of services that includes consulting, technical support, chemical R&D and accredited laboratory testing.
source_title: Woodbridge Develops CO2 Neutral Automotive Seat Foam

facts: UAW workers at Woodbridge Corp – Atlanta Foam in Lithonia, Ga., represented by UAW Local 472, initiated a strike action on the morning of October 30.
The strike is over contract disputes concerning wages, benefits, and seniority after protracted negotiations with management yielded no significant progress.
UAW Region 8 Director Tim Smith stated that despite numerous meetings and bargaining sessions, management has yet to make a significant offer on the three critical issues for workers.
The company’s stance left workers with no alternative but to strike, according to UAW Region 8 Director Tim Smith.
The striking workforce is responsible for producing key components such as seating and dashboards for major clients Yamaha and Nissan.
These workers have been operating without a formal contract since the end of September.
On August 22, a significant majority of the workforce, 98%, voted in favor of authorizing the strike.
UAW Local 472 President Rachel Johnson stated that workers are standing together to demand better wages, healthcare, and respect for their work.
This strike follows the notable Stand Up strike where UAW members at the Big Three automakers achieved substantial raises and improved benefits.
The Woodbridge workers are among the latest to engage in strike action during contract negotiations, joining other UAW members who have recently secured significant victories.
source_title: Woodbridge Corp workers strike over wages, benefits, and seniority issues - USA Works

facts: Adient announced the acquisition of a foam production plant in Romulus, MI.
The acquired facility produces foam for seats for multiple automaker customers.
The plant joins Adient’s network of 10 foam plants in the Americas region.
Adient operates a total of 30 foam manufacturing plants globally.
Adient purchased the plant from automotive supplier Woodbridge.
The purchase agreement included the existing building and land, production equipment, inventory, and associated assets.
Employees currently working at the plant will transition to become Adient employees.
The collective bargaining agreement with the UAW will remain in place following the acquisition.

Jim Conklin, executive vice president Americas for Adient, stated that welcoming the Romulus employees adds an important facility to Adient's footprint and positions the acquisition as a positive move for customers due to global scale and expertise.
The acquisition supports Adient’s strategic growth plan and positions the company as a supplier and employer of choice.
Adient is a global leader in automotive seating with more than 65,000 employees across 29 countries.
Adient operates approximately 200 manufacturing and assembly plants worldwide.
Adient produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's expertise spans every step of the automotive seat-making process, from research and design to engineering and manufacturing.
Adient supplies its products into millions of vehicles every year.
source_title: Adient acquires automotive seating foam plant in Romulus, MI

facts: Dublin is the latest Georgia city to win a commitment from a South Korean automotive supplier looking to participate in the state’s burgeoning electric car sector.
Woory Industrial Co. Ltd. is set to invest $18 million and hire 130 people in what will be its first factory in the U.S.
The facility occupies a 76,000-square-foot existing building at 404 Kellam Road just off Interstate 16, about 100 miles west of the massive Hyundai Meta Plant near Savannah.

Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that Woory’s commitment to place an existing manufacturing building back into service helps promote more infill development while creating new career paths for the community, per a release from Gov. Brian Kemp’s office.
Woory has facilities across Asia, Mexico, and in Slovakia, which is home to Kia’s European plant.
The company has been nominated as a supplier to EV makers Tesla, Lucid, Canoo, and Rivian, which is also building a $5 billion plant in Georgia.
It is unclear if the nomination means the company is already selling to all four EV makers.
Woory makes HVAC components like actuators and coolant heaters, along with control systems, for gas-powered cars.
A decade ago, the company started developing electric heaters for EVs, which do not naturally produce heat.
The company’s smart heaters are designed to adjust energy usage based on the car’s battery level.
Experts say that to boost EV range and overcome consumers’ so-called “range anxiety,” car makers need demand efficiency at all points across supply chains.
Woory is also focused on developing new products for hydrogen-powered cars, a clean technology where Hyundai is also placing heavy bets.

Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory, stated: “Currently, we are facing a revolution in the automobile industry for the first time in 100 years. The transition to electric vehicles is the future, and the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets.”

Kim added: “Over the past 10 years, Woory has successfully grown into a specialized EV component company through continuous innovation. Georgia will be a good opportunity for Woory to take another step forward as part of the vanguard of EV development.”
Woory is based in Yongin, just south of Seoul in Gyeonggi province.
It has a U.S. office registered in Warren, Mich.
Jacob Lee, the Georgia Department of Economic Development project manager who got his start with the state in its Korean office and moved here in October, spearheaded the project.
Woory’s Dublin operations are expected to begin in November.
source_title: Dublin Nets Korean Supplier of Heaters for Electric Vehicles  - Global Atlanta

facts: Woory is opening a USD 18 million EV component manufacturing and R&D facility in Georgia.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in November 2023.
The Georgia location will be Woory’s first U.S. manufacturing site.
source_title: Woory to open USD 18 million EV component manufacturing, R&D facility in Georgia - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The facility will create over 130 new jobs.
The project brings an estimated $18 million in investment to Dublin-Laurens County.
Woory Industrial Company was founded in 1989.
The company specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components for the global automotive industry.
Woory is now focusing on developing products for electric and hydrogen-fueled vehicles.
This facility marks Woory's first manufacturing site in the United States.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators.
Products will be supplied to electric and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations at the new facility are set to begin in November 2023.

facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced plans for a new automotive parts manufacturing facility in Dublin.
The investment from Woory Industrial Company will bring one hundred and thirty new jobs to Laurens County.
Woory specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components for the automotive industry.
Woory will be hiring for a variety of positions at the new facility.
The company plans to place an existing manufacturing building back into service.
Miller Soul Food co-owner Shenita Brown says the facility will bring more customers to her business, which has been open since 1955.
Co-owner Rodney Brown believes the investment will increase the tax base and bring in new revenue.
Rodney Brown believes the project opens the door for young adults to take technical classes that can land them a job.
Local business owner Maggie Gilbert states that people need good-paying jobs to support their families.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight issued a statement welcoming Woory and noting its commitment promotes infill development while creating new career paths for the community.
source_title: "More Customers": Business Owners react to new manufacturing facility headed to Dublin

entity_name: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
canonical_name: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
facts: The company operates a facility at 565 Beulah Church Road, Carrollton, GA 30117.
Global headquarters and manufacturing/R&D locations include Japan with a Head Office, Kashiwabara Plant, Suzuka Plant, Kameyama Branch, Saitama R&D Center, and Tochigi R&D Center.
U.S. operations include Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Tech Products of America, Inc. at 2285 Walcutt Road, Columbus, OH 43228.
U.S. operations include AY Manufacturing Ltd. at 5200 Crosswind Drive, Columbus, OH 43228.
U.S. operations include Motherson Yachiyo US Automotive Systems, Inc. at 1177 Kellogg Parkway, Marion, OH 43302.
Mexican operations are located under the entity Motherson Yachiyo Mexico Automotive S.A. de C.V.
Brazilian operations are located under the entity Motherson Yachiyo Automotiva Brasil Industria E Comercio De Pecas Ltda.

Chinese operations include entities in Zhongshan, a Jinhua Branch, and Wuhan Automotive Module Manufacturing Co., Ltd. at 786 ZhuShanHu Avenue, Wuhan Economic & Technological Development Zone, Hubei, P.R.China.
Thai operations are located under Motherson Yachiyo Siam Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. at 304 Industrial Park 214 Moo 7 Tambol Thatoom Amphur, Srimahaphot, Prachinburi 25140.

facts: YMA's plastic fuel tank business was moved to a new plant in the state of Georgia, and YMA has changed its corporate name to Yachiyo Manufacturing of America, LLC.
The facility will take over YMA's fuel tank business, and will manufacture 400,000 plastic fuel tanks per year for Honda's facility in Alabama.
The plant will also have a sunroof production line with an annual capacity of 200,000 units in order to cater to growing demand in North America.
Production of the new plastic fuel tanks will begin at the Company's plant in Georgia, the U.S. in 2015.
The Company shifted production of tanks from its old Alabama plant to the Georgia plant.
The Georgia plant began operations with an annual production capacity of 400,000 units in the fall of 2014.
The Georgia plant has two molding machines.
As the existing molding machines can be used to produce the new fuel tanks, these machines will be partially remodeled.
Planned capital investment for Yachiyo Manufacturing of America, LLC (Georgia, USA) is 766 million yen from April 2015 to March 2016 for increasing production capacity and responding to new business.
Capital investments in the Americas region included building new plant buildings and renewing facilities.
Yachiyo of America Inc. is listed as an R&D facility located in Ohio, U.S.A.
source_title: Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. Business Report FY ended Mar. 2015 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

entity_name: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Tech Products of America, Inc.
canonical_name: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Tech Products of America, Inc.
facts: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Tech Products of America, Inc. is a Tier 1 supplier to the automotive industry since 1997.
The company specializes in designing and producing plastic fuel tanks and sunroofs.
Motherson Yachiyo was established in 1947 by Eiichi Otake in Japan.
Since its establishment, the company has grown its facilities worldwide.
Motherson Yachiyo of America was founded in 1997 to serve a new demographic in the company's global capabilities.
The company aims to become one of the world’s top-level manufacturers of automotive body parts.
The company seeks to supply superior quality while maintaining economical products in direct response to customer needs.
source_title: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Tech Products of America, Inc.

facts: Applicants must complete the employment application in its entirety; resumes will not be accepted in place of a fully completed application.
Applicants must be at least 18 years old.
Candidates must have reliable transportation, applicable insurance coverage, and a valid driver’s license for specific positions.
Applicants must be a US citizen or a non-citizen legally authorized to work in the US for any US employer.
Candidates may be required to take a test specific to a particular job.

Production, Maintenance, and Material Handling positions require the ability to stand or walk for 8 hours per day or more, and associates in those positions may regularly lift and/or move up to 10 pounds or more and occasionally lift and/or move up to, or even in excess of, 40 pounds as part of their essential job duties.

The company considers all applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, and related medical conditions), gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, military/veteran status, age, disability, genetic information, or any other consideration made unlawful by applicable federal, state, or local laws.
source_title: Employment Application - Motherson Yachiyo

entity_name: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
canonical_name: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
facts: The company develops and manufactures fuel tanks and sunroofs.
Honda announced on July 4, 2023, that it will sell its stake in consolidated subsidiary Yachiyo Industry to the Motherson Group.

Honda will first make Yachiyo Industry Kogyo a wholly owned subsidiary through a takeover bid before transferring 81% of voting rights to SMRC Automotive Holdings Netherlands B.V., a group company under Samvardhana Motherson International.
The tender offer for the stake transfer is scheduled to commence around October 2023.
Honda aims to optimize its business portfolio and make the Motherson Group a strategic partner to expand sales channels in existing businesses and expand its plastic products business.

Honda will acquire all shares of Goshi Giken Kogyo held by Yachiyo Kogyo, holding 95% of voting rights, allowing Yachiyo Industry to concentrate management resources on its automotive business while Honda focuses on motorcycle business growth.
The company was delisted on January 10, 2024.
Effective October 1, 2024, the company changed its name to Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Systems Co., Ltd.
After Honda transfers its stock to the Motherson Group, voting rights are forecast to be 81% for the Motherson Group and 19% for Honda.
The product portfolio includes sunroofs, plastic fuel tanks, steel fuel tanks, hydrogen tanks for FCVs, plastic parts, bumpers, instrument panels, and CFRP products.
In October 2014, stamping operations of Yachiyo Manufacturing of Alabama, LLC (U.S.A) were acquired by Unipres.
In October 2014, plastic fuel tank operations were transferred to a new production plant in Georgia.
Following the October 2014 operational changes, the company adopted the new name Yachiyo Manufacturing of America, LLC.
The company was originally established as Ohtake Paint Shop by Eiichi Ohtake in April 1947 at 7, Fujimi-cho, Itabashi-ku, Tokyo, starting with painting machine parts.
In August 1953, Yachiyo Painting Co., Ltd. was established to start a baking finish business and set up the Narimasu Plant in Daiwa-cho, Saitama (currently Wako-shi), which closed in June 1962.
The Suzuka Plant in Suzuka City, Mie, was established in May 1960 as a buffing and painting shop for motorcycle parts.
The company changed its name to Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd. in September 1968.
In August 1972, the Kashiwabara Plant was established in Saitama, starting assembly of Honda's mini-cars on an outsourcing basis, at which point Honda invested and made it an affiliate.
Yachiyo Press Industry Co., Ltd. merged with Shimizu Seisakusho in April 1973.
Goshi Giken Co., Ltd. was established as a joint venture with Honda and Sankei Giken Kougyou Co., Ltd. in November 1974.
Production of buggies for Honda started at the Kashiwabara Plant on an outsourcing basis in February 1976.
The company started producing fuel tanks as automotive parts in February 1977.
The head office was relocated to Toshima Ward in Tokyo in July 1981.
Yokkaichi Factory in Mie Prefecture was established in June 1983 for stamping and welding operations.
Facilities at Yokkaichi Factory were enhanced in August 1985 to produce trucks for Honda on an outsourcing basis.
The Tochigi R&D Center was established in Tochigi Prefecture in January 1986.
Production of sunroofs as part of its automotive-parts range started in June 1986.
Yachiyo of Ontario Mfg., Inc., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in Canada in January 1989.
Contract production of Honda's mini passenger cars started on a subcontracted basis in May 1996.
Yachiyo Industry (U.K.) Ltd. was established in June 1996 and is currently a consolidated subsidiary.
Unipart Yachiyo Technology Ltd., a joint venture with several local companies, was established in the UK in June 1996.
Siam Yachiyo Co., Ltd., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in Thailand in January 1997.
The head office was relocated to Sayama, Saitama Prefecture in May 1997.
Yachiyo of America Inc., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in the U.S.A. in September 1997.
AY Mfg., Ltd., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in the U.S.A. through its local subsidiary in October 1997.
US Yachiyo Inc., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in the U.S.A. in January 1999.
Yachiyo Manufacturing of Alabama Llc was established in the USA in September 2000.
Yachiyo Zhongshan Mfg. Co., Ltd. was established in China in April 2002.

In August 2002, shares of Goshi Giken Co., Ltd. were purchased to make it a subsidiary, bringing Uemura Tech, Shisui Tech, Goshi Tech, Siam Goshi Mfg. Co., Ltd. (Thailand), and Goshi-Thanglong Auto-Parts Co., Ltd (Vietnam) under Yachiyo's subsidiaries.
The company cancelled its registration with the Japan Securities Dealers Association for over-the-counter trading and listed its shares on the JASDAC Stock Exchange in December 2004.
Yachiyo Wuhan Mfg. Co., Ltd., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in China in July 2005.
Equities of AY Mfg. and Ltd. in the United States were acquired in October 2005 to make it a consolidated subsidiary.
Honda Motor Co., Ltd. became the Company's parent company in December 2006 after purchasing shares through a takeover bid.
YG Tech Co., Ltd., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in Mie Prefecture, Japan through a joint-investment pooled by Group companies in April 2008.
Yachiyo India Manufacturing Private Limited, a consolidated subsidiary, was established in India in April 2008.
Goshi India Auto Parts Private Limited, a consolidated subsidiary, started operations in India in December 2008.
Yachiyo de Brasil Industria e Comercio de Pecas Ltda., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in Brazil in September 2009.
Yachiyo Mexico Manufacturing S.A. de C.V., a consolidated subsidiary, was established in Guanajuato, Mexico in February 2012.
The Saitama R&D Center was established in Sayama, Saitama Prefecture in April 2012.
P.T. Yachiyo Trimitra Indonesia was established in May 2012.
In June 2014, Yachiyo of Ontario Manufacturing, Inc. in Canada transferred its sheet-metal business to Matcor-Matsu Group of Companies.
In May 2015, a basic agreement was reached with Topre to start discussions for Yachiyo to transfer stamping operations in Japan to Topre.
In June 2015, all operations with H-One at UYT Ltd. (an affiliate company in the U.K.) were transferred to N Press Assembly Limited.
In January 2016, the transfer of business operations of YG Tech was completed to Topre, ending the Company’s operations in the sheet-metal business.
Yachiyo Industries Yokkaichi Works Co., Ltd. was consigned the vehicle-assembly business and started operations in March 2018.
All stock shares of Yachiyo Industries Yokkaichi Works Co., Ltd. were transferred to Honda Motor Co. in April 2018, ending its consignment of assembling light vehicles.
In July 2023, the company announced it will sell Yachiyo Industry, a consolidated subsidiary, to the Motherson Group based in India.
source_title: Motherson Yachiyo Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. (Formerly Yachiyo Industry Co., Ltd.) - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. announced that production of its all-terrain sport vehicle (ATV) line currently located in Japan will be transferred to the company’s Newnan facility.
Yamaha employs more than 1,000 people at the Newnan facility which consists of three plants.

The Newnan facility manufactures four recreational product lines including golf cars, ATVs, SxS utility vehicles and is the only location in the world where the Yamaha Waverunner personal watercraft is built.
Chromalloy expanded at its Shenandoah Industrial Park facility by adding a gas turbine engine service capability, creating 125 jobs.
West Georgia Technical College began construction on two buildings totaling 83,500 square feet at its new 38.5-acre campus facility near Turkey Creek Road and I-85.

West Georgia Technical College is expected to begin classes in fall 2012 with degree and certification offerings in Allied Health Care including registered nursing, medical assistant, physical therapy assistant, electronic medical records technician, certified first responder and home health aide.
West Georgia Technical College anticipates future construction of seven additional buildings, bringing the campus to 200,000 square feet.
Coweta County Development Authority visited 37 industries in the county to assist companies with expansion projects and help identify resources available to improve operations.
The Coweta County Development Authority works with economic development partners including the Georgia Dept. of Economic Development, the Georgia Dept. of Labor and Georgia Tech.
source_title: 2011 a good year for business in Coweta | The Citizen

canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) SMART Warehouse
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC), based in Newnan, Georgia, broke ground on a new “SMART” Warehouse.
The SMART Warehouse is the first-of-its-kind facility of its type for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The 200,000 square foot facility will utilize state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The warehouse will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.
Mike Chrzanowski, President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated the facility will improve operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines.
The improved logistics will allow Yamaha to get product to dealers and customers faster.
Demand for outdoor recreational products produced at this Georgia factory continues to exceed supply.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage and retrieval system will retrieve and put away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small part automated system will handle 2400 containers per hour, compared to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
Automatic conveyors and tuggers will move parts through the SMART center to factories, enabling a 50 percent reduction in forklifts.
The investment underscores YMMC’s long-term commitment to employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The facility will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs to Coweta County, including roles for robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
SSI SCHAEFER designed the new SMART Warehouse.
The warehouse will include an observation platform for local school students to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS) operates corporate offices in California and two in Georgia, with facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.
YMUS sells products through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers in the United States.

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company (with divisions Bennett Marine in Florida, Kracor Systems in Wisconsin, and Siren Marine, Inc. in Rhode Island), Skeeter Boats in Texas with division G3 Boats in Missouri, and Yamaha Precision Propeller in Indiana.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation Breaks Ground on Innovative SMART Warehouse in Newnan, Georgia

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) and Yamaha Motor Corp., USA (YMUS) celebrated the production of 4 million vehicles with the assembly of a new 2021 Wolverine RMAX2 1000 Side-by-Side at its U.S. manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia.
The milestone celebration took place on August 12, 2021, at the Newnan factory to recognize employees for their dedication to superior manufacturing processes and high-quality vehicle production.
YMMC serves as Yamaha's hub for 4-wheel off-highway vehicle manufacturing, producing all of the company's Side-by-Sides, full-size ATVs, WaveRunners, and Golf Cars for worldwide distribution.
Over the past 20 years, Yamaha has invested more than $380 million into U.S. manufacturing to create a world-class facility in Newnan, according to Taka Imanishi, president of YMMC.
Since its founding in 1988, YMMC has made a significant impact within its community, with the facility covering 280 acres and containing more than 1.3 million square feet of manufacturing space.
Between the YMMC facility and YMUS corporate offices in Marietta and Kennesaw, Georgia, Yamaha now employs over 2,000 people in the state of Georgia alone.
The Georgia employment encompasses more than half of the 4,000+ individuals the company directly employs across the United States.
The city of Newnan named May 18th "Yamaha Day" in 2011 to celebrate the company's positive economic impacts and ongoing job creation.

Yamaha's impact on Newnan has been highlighted through recent disaster relief donations, ongoing community engagement programs, and a season 19 premiere of "Modern Marvels: Machines" on the HISTORY® Channel that went behind-the-scenes at the factory.

Mike Martinez, YMUS vice president of motorsports, stated that Yamaha continues to add personnel and strengthen operations in Georgia as the Side-by-Side line expands with popular new models like the Wolverine RMAX 1000.
Due to increasing demand for outdoor recreation, Yamaha expects continued growth of its operations in Georgia and across the country.
The YMMC facility holds ISO9001 Quality Management System and ISO14001 Environmental Management System certifications for quality, safety, and environmental manufacturing standards.
source_title: Yamaha Celebrates 4 Million Units Assembled in the USA

facts: Yamaha Motor Co. will relocate its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The relocation follows more than 50 years of operations in California.
The move comes after a meeting between Gov. Brian Kemp and Yamaha executives last October at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha is a manufacturer of ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
Yamaha currently employs over 2,300 Georgians across its operations in the state.
The headquarters relocation will begin this year (2026) and is expected to continue through 2028.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated he is honored and proud to welcome Yamaha’s American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.
Kemp noted the move serves as a testament to what Georgia offers job creators from around the world.
Kemp encouraged other California-based companies looking for a better home to keep Georgia on their mind.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986.
The Newnan factory currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The Kennesaw facility was expanded with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from proximity to existing facilities.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Co. to relocate U.S. HQ to Kennesaw - Rough Draft Atlanta

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) broke ground on June 9 at its Newnan, Georgia location for a new SMART warehouse.
The SMART warehouse is the first of its kind for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
SMART stands for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The new facility spans 200,000 square feet.
The warehouse will optimize SMART capabilities to reduce dealer order fulfillment response time by more than 50 percent for ATVs, WaveRunners, side-by-sides, and golf cars.
Mike Chrzanowski, president and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation, stated that demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at the factory continues to exceed supply.
The SMART warehouse will feature an automated storage and retrieval system to significantly improve medium and small parts delivery capabilities.
The new system will reduce forklift usage by 50 percent by utilizing automatic conveyors and tuggers to move parts through the SMART center to factories.
Mike Chrzanowski stated that the warehouse will improve overall operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines, allowing products to reach dealers and customers faster.
The facility represents a significant investment by Yamaha, underscoring a long-term commitment to YMMC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The SMART warehouse will bring higher-paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including roles for robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
source_title: Yamaha Breaks Ground on SMART Warehouse in Georgia | ATV Rider

canonical_name: Layoffs at Three Georgia Businesses (Community Development Institute Head Start, Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation, Americold Logistics)
facts: In the span of two days, more than 200 employees across three different Georgia companies are being let go according to WARN Notices received by the USA Today Network.

Community Development Institute Head Start served as the short-term interim operator of the Savannah River Head Start program while a process by the Office of Head Start identified a replacement recipient.
77 layoffs at Community Development Institute Head Start will be effective Sept. 30 or within 14 days beginning on Oct. 1 when the new administration starts.
The CDI HS layoffs are spread across 11 worksites, with the largest impacts being 15 layoffs at Emanuel Head Start in Swainesboro and 13 at Burke Head Start in Waynesboro.
The CDI HS layoffs are expected to be permanent, and the program location will no longer be operated by CDI HS.
Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation is impacting employees at its Newnan operations due to a change in ownership and control of two distribution centers starting around Oct. 4.
Around 160 employees at Yamaha Motor Manufacturing will be permanently fired during the ownership transition.
A July 31 WARN Notice for Americold Logistics in Atlanta indicates that 12 employees will be impacted by layoffs.
The three Georgia businesses are collectively laying off a total of 249 employees.
source_title: One week, three Georgia businesses, 249 employees laid off. Here's what we know

facts: Yamaha Motor Co. Ltd., which makes ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft and more, is set to move its U.S. headquarters from California to Kennesaw.
The relocation of Yamaha Motor Corp. USA will take place gradually starting this year and continuing through 2028.
The company will mark its half-century anniversary in the United States in 2028.
Yamaha purchased the land for its former U.S. headquarters in Cypress, Calif., in 1978.
The company slowly built up its U.S. business from the 25-acre Cypress site.
The Cypress site will be liquidated under a sale and leaseback transaction once the transition is completed.
The headquarters move culminates a gradual eastward migration consolidating much of Yamaha Motor’s inventory, executive team, and manufacturing in Georgia.
Before the headquarters relocation decision, Yamaha already had about 2,400 employees across its divisions in Georgia.
Yamaha Motor moved its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company later consolidated sales and warehousing functions for its outboard engines in Kennesaw.
In 2023, the Kennesaw operation was expanded with the opening of a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center.
Gov. Brian Kemp visited the Marine Innovation Center for its ribbon-cutting ceremony.
The new center complemented a long-standing parts warehouse on Chastain Road that the company kept after outgrowing it.
The motorsports business was relocated to Georgia in 2019.
This relocation provided proximity to the Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. of America factory in Newnan.
The Newnan factory spans 1.3 million square feet.
The Newnan factory employs 2,000-plus people.
The Newnan factory has produced close to 5 million units over 30-plus years of manufacturing.
Products manufactured at the Newnan facility include ATVs, waverunners, and side-by-sides.
The Newnan unit recently contracted with DHL to manage logistics on its site.
The company transferred 175 employees from its own operations to DHL as part of the logistics contract.
Yamaha Motor USA CEO Mike Chrzanowski worked at the Newnan site for about 20 years before becoming top U.S. executive two years ago.
Mr. Chrzanowski was given the option to relocate to Cypress or stay in Kennesaw.
Mr. Chrzanowski stated, “I chose Kennesaw because this is where all the action is,” during a 2024 celebration of international investment at the Cobb Chamber of Commerce.
The consolidation benefits Georgia amid a tough U.S. operating environment for Yamaha Motor.

According to a news release, Yamaha is “undertaking structural reforms aimed at improving the profitability of its U.S. operations in response to cost increases resulting from U.S. tariffs and changes in the market environment.”
In addition to cutting costs, the company is working on tying profitability less to relentless top-line revenue growth and more to new innovations.

The move supports a thesis by Metro Atlanta Chamber economist Jerry Parrish from early 2025 that pain elsewhere in the U.S. could benefit metro Atlanta as companies cut costs and centralize corporate functions in cities with diverse industries, competitive costs, and strong talent bases.

For its U.S. operations, Yamaha is focused on creating "kando," described as a Japanese word for the simultaneous feelings of deep satisfaction and intense excitement experienced when encountering exceptional value.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Set to Move U.S.  Headquarters to Kennesaw From California - Global Atlanta

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) broke ground on a new, advanced, and innovative “SMART” Warehouse in Newnan, Georgia.
The SMART Warehouse is the first-of-its-kind for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The new 200,000 square foot facility will use state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The warehouse will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.

Groundbreaking attendees included YMMC President Taka Imanishi, YMMC VP Supply Chain Wayne Pierce, YMUS President and CEO Mike Chrzanowski, Newnan Mayor Keith Brady, and Georgia State Senator Matt Brass.
Mike Chrzanowski stated the facility will improve overall operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines to get product to dealers and customers faster.
Demand for all outdoor recreational products produced at the YMMC factory continues to exceed supply.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage system will retrieve and put away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small part automated system will handle 2400 containers per hour, in comparison to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
New systems will enable Yamaha to reduce forklifts by 50 percent using automatic conveyors and tuggers.
The investment underscores the company’s long-term commitment to YMMC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The facility will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County, including robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The warehouse was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.
The facility will include an observation platform for groups such as students from local schools to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.

YMUS products include Motorcycles, Scooters, ATVs, Side-by-Side vehicles, Snowmobiles, WaveRunners, Boats, Outboard Motors, Golf Cars, and Power Assist Bicycles, sold through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers.
YMUS has corporate offices in California and Georgia, facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.
YMUS owns additional U.S.-based subsidiaries including Yamaha Marine Systems Company, Skeeter Boats, and Yamaha Precision Propeller.
source_title: FinancialContent - Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation Breaks Ground on Innovative SMART Warehouse in Newnan, Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp welcomed the announcement that Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has officially decided to move its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company is opting to relocate out of California following roughly 50 years of operation there.
The decision follows a meeting between Governor Kemp and Yamaha executives in October of last year at the Southeast U.S./Japan (SEUS-Japan) annual gathering in Tokyo.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. is a manufacturer of ATVs, boat engines, personal watercraft, and other motorized products.
The company already has a strong presence in Georgia, employing over 2,300 Georgians.
The relocation of the Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S. headquarters will begin in 2026 and is expected to continue through 2028.
Governor Brian Kemp stated: “After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha's American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business.”

Governor Brian Kemp added: “This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we'll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
The Yamaha Motor Company currently employs thousands of people across various facilities in Georgia.
The 1.3-million-square-foot Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan opened in 1986 and currently employs roughly 2,000 Georgians.
Yamaha relocated its marine division to Kennesaw in 1999.
The company expanded the Kennesaw facility with a 75,000-square-foot Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
In 2019, the company moved its motorsports operations to Georgia to benefit from close proximity to existing facilities.
Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. is a world-leading producer of motorcycles, marine products, power products, and robotics.
The company was founded in 1955 and is headquartered in Iwata, Shizuoka, Japan.
source_title: Yamaha to Move HQ to Georgia - Southern Economic Development Council

facts: Operates as a premier recreational vehicle manufacturing facility in Newnan, Georgia.
Employs over 2,000 skilled professionals across six state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities.
Specializes in producing high-quality WaveRunners, side-by-sides, all-terrain vehicles, and golf cars.
Has a remarkable production output exceeding 4 million vehicles to date.
The Newnan facility combines advanced technical processes with diverse expertise from engineers, welders, technicians, and managers working collaboratively.
Uses an integrated approach that ensures precision manufacturing and quality control throughout the production cycle.
Maintains rigorous manufacturing standards while focusing on innovation in recreational vehicle design and assembly.
Serves as a critical component in Yamaha Motor Company's global manufacturing network, contributing to the brand's reputation for reliability and performance in the recreational vehicle market.
Utilizes cutting-edge technology and lean manufacturing principles for comprehensive vehicle assembly processes.
Leverages its strategic Georgia location and substantial workforce to support domestic recreational vehicle production while maintaining quality standards across diverse market segments.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation - Automotive Manufacturer in Newnan, GA | UNITEMFG

facts: On September 24, 2020, Interior Secretary David Bernhardt met with Yamaha Golf-Car Company President Tom McDonald at the Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation facility in Newnan, Georgia.

Mike Chrzanowski, President of Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation, and Ben Speciale, President of Yamaha U.S. Marine Business Unit, signed the President’s Pledge to America’s Workers during the event.
The signing reinforces Yamaha’s commitment to workforce development within its U.S. manufacturing facilities.
Tom McDonald stated that jobs created through Yamaha facilities in Georgia make a positive impact on the state’s economy and contribute to the growth of the outdoor industry overall.
The golf industry supported $191.9 billion in total annual activity in 2016, including 1.9 million jobs and 58.7 billion in wages and benefits.
Yamaha reported record golf participation with rounds played up 14% in June and 19.7% in July of that year.
Yamaha committed to continuing investment in training and best practices necessary to create successful manufacturing career paths within each of its divisions.
The company operates a technician training facility in Kennesaw, Georgia, which supports dealers and distributors across the country.
The President’s Pledge to America’s Workers stems from President Trump’s National Council for the American Worker, established on July 19, 2019.
The council leads a national strategy for training and re-training workers in high-demand industries, such as recreational manufacturing.
More than 430 companies have signed the pledge, contributing to more than 16 million new training opportunities for American students and workers over the next five years.
Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS) manufactures golf cars alongside powersports, marine, and outdoor power equipment products sold through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers.
YMUS maintains two corporate offices in Georgia, with factory operations located in Tennessee and Georgia.
The Yamaha Golf-Car Company is headquartered in Kennesaw, Georgia, with assembly and production of all its vehicles taking place at the Newnan, Georgia facility.
Yamaha was the first to develop Electronic Fuel Injection technology in a golf car, producing the best gas mileage and smallest carbon footprint for the category.
Yamaha introduced the first golf car with fully independent, four-wheel suspension.
Yamaha developed the first gas-powered golf car that is almost as quiet as an electric one.
source_title: Press Release:Interior Secretary David Bernhardt Meets with Yamaha Golf-Car Company President Tom McDonald

facts: U.S. Secretary of the Interior David Bernhardt visited Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation’s (YMMC) Newnan, Georgia facility last week.
Yamaha Motor reinforced its commitment to workforce development within its U.S. manufacturing facilities through the President’s Pledge to America’s Workers.
Ben Speciale, President of Yamaha U.S. Marine Business Unit and chairperson of the NMMA Board of Directors, signed the pledge during the visit.
Mike Chrzanowski, President of YMMC, signed the pledge during the visit.
The Pledge to America’s Workers stems from the President’s National Council for the American Worker, established on July 19, 2018.
The council leads a national strategy for training and re-training workers in high-demand industries, such as recreational manufacturing.
More than 430 companies have signed the pledge, contributing to more than 16 million new training opportunities for American students and workers over the next five years.
The marine industry alone has a total impact of $170 billion on the U.S. economy, which includes more than 691,000 jobs within 35,000 businesses (said Ben Speciale).

Ben Speciale represents six additional Yamaha Marine manufacturing facilities in the U.S., including Yamaha Jet Boat Manufacturing, Skeeter Boats, G3 boats, Bennett Marine, Yamaha Precision Propellers, and Kracor.

Yamaha thanked Secretary Bernhardt for his leadership and support of the Great American Outdoors Act, which will improve infrastructure projects such as boat docks and launch ramps (said Ben Speciale).

Yamaha appreciates Bernhardt’s willingness to open public lands to outdoor recreational activities, which solidifies the future of the industry and its manufacturing and technical jobs (said Ben Speciale).
In recent years, 2,589 students have enrolled in 98 technical school programs spanning 30 states, all supported by Yamaha (said Ben Speciale).
Yamaha has gained 2,116 Yamaha Marine Industry Technical School Certifications (said Ben Speciale).

Manufacturing jobs represent the backbone of the U.S. economy and provide U.S. workers with a solid means for making a living while playing a key role in the industry’s future (said Mike Chrzanowski).
Yamaha promises to support and grow the agile, resilient and diverse workforce of the future by building and expanding pathways that lead to rewarding careers (said Mike Chrzanowski).

facts: Yamaha Motor Co. is officially bringing its U.S. headquarters operations to Kennesaw.
The decision was confirmed by the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
The move reflects a shift in how companies view Georgia, treating it as a place to think, lead, and grow rather than just build.
Kennesaw offers proximity to metro Atlanta’s infrastructure, a deep talent pool, and access to a network of suppliers that already understand advanced manufacturing and mobility.
State leaders say the move strengthens Georgia’s position in the advanced mobility sector.
The advanced mobility category is expanding beyond traditional automotive into everything from powersports to next-generation transportation systems.
Headquarters are following the same path once carved out by production facilities.
The investment adds high-quality jobs, expanded supplier relationships, and a steady pull on local talent who might otherwise look out of state for opportunities.
Georgia is proving it can compete for executive leadership and long-term strategic operations in addition to logistics and manufacturing.
More decisions and innovation regarding the region's business landscape will be guided from Georgia.

facts: Yamaha Motor Co.’s U.S. subsidiary will be moving its headquarters from Cypress, California, to Kennesaw, Georgia over at least a two-year period.
California has been the company’s corporate home for half a century.
Yamaha currently owns and occupies 1270 Chastain Road, a 459K SF office building in Kennesaw, where it opened a 75K SF Marine Innovation Center in 2023.
Yamaha has operated a 1.3M SF Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America factory in Newnan since 1986.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced the move and highlighted it as a signal that companies prefer the state’s business-friendly policies.
Kemp spokesperson Carter Chapman said the state did not provide any grants or Quick Start funds to lure Yamaha to the state.
It is unclear how many jobs will be added to Georgia, but the move will affect around 250 employees in California.
In November, the company leased 25K SF at TownPark Commons, a four-building, 350K SF office park located more than 30 miles north of Downtown Atlanta.
RGRE purchased the office park out of foreclosure nearly a year ago for $42M.
Yamaha is seeking another 50K to 75K SF of office in Kennesaw over the next two years.
Yamaha spokesperson Bob Starr stated the move to Metro Atlanta was part of a structural reform to boost profits as costs and tariff pressures climbed.

The company plans to lease back its sprawling 25-acre headquarters campus at 6555 Katella Ave. in Orange County, California, which includes 279K SF in three buildings, during the transitional period while pursuing future redevelopment entitlements.
Avison Young was tapped to sell Yamaha’s sprawling 25-acre headquarters campus at 6555 Katella Ave. in Orange County, California.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Co. Moving HQ From California To Kennesaw, Georgia

facts: Yamaha Motor Corp. USA is moving its headquarters from Cypress, California to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company has called Cypress home for nearly a half-century.
The company has been moving divisions to Georgia for years.

The corporate exit from California comes as Yamaha undertakes “structural reforms” aimed at improving profitability in response to cost increases from the Trump administration’s tariffs and “changes in the market environment.”
The company’s departure will happen at the end of 2026 and continue until late 2028, according to Yamaha.
Company spokesman Bob Starr said the move would affect about 250 workers at the Cypress facility.
Bob Starr stated, “In terms of efficiency, to have us all together in Georgia, all the functions of the business, it makes a lot of sense.”
Yamaha is the second big company to exit Cypress in recent years.
In 2019, Mitsubishi Motors North America left its Cypress headquarters after 31 years and relocated to Franklin, Tennessee.
The automaker cited lower operating costs in Tennessee for roughly 200 employees at the time of its departure.
Iwata-based parent Yamaha Motor Co. Ltd. will sell the 25-acre office campus on Katella Avenue.
The Cypress campus serves as an administrative hub with corporate functions and financial services for its motorsports operations.
Yamaha's motorsports products include all-terrain vehicles, golf carts, motorcycles, and motorboats.
Yamaha does not perform any manufacturing in Cypress.
Commercial brokerage firm Avison Young has been tapped to market the Yamaha facilities.
The facilities include industrial, flex-warehouse, and office buildings in a combined 278,964 square feet.
The property occupies an entire city block bound by Katella Avenue, Holder Street, and Yamaha Way.
Avison Young says the Yamaha property is one of the largest industrial redevelopment opportunities available in Southern California.
Besides tariffs, Yamaha also said the move is the result of changes in the company’s business focus within the U.S. market.
The move to Georgia follows the relocation of Yamaha's marine business there in 1999 and its motorsports business in 2019.
Alicia Velasco, director of planning and community development for Cypress, said the city regrets Yamaha’s decision to relocate its headquarters to Georgia.
Yamaha was one of the first companies to locate in the Cypress Business Park in 1980 and has been an important part of the business community for more than 40 years.
The city thanks Yamaha for its long-standing partnership and looks forward to working with management to transition to new ownership.
In the past decade, there have been several corporate departures from California for a variety of reasons, though many have cited lower labor costs and taxes.
Other notable departures included Pfizer’s sale of its San Diego campus, Hewlett Packard’s 2020 move to Spring Texas, and Toyota’s 2014 shift to Plano, Texas.
In 2021, electric vehicle maker Tesla moved its headquarters to Austin, and three years ago opened a global engineering headquarters in Palo Alto.
Billionaire founder Elon Musk moved his residence from California to Texas, where there is no state personal income tax.
Tesla’s automaking plants still operate in Northern California.
The Buena Park-based Yamaha Corp. of America, which makes musical instruments and sound gear, is unaffected by the relocation.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Co. leaving longtime Cypress headquarters for Georgia – Daily News

facts: Officials at Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. say they've opened a second assembly plant in Newnan.
The company will hire 100 employees to work on a new utility vehicle.
Yamaha has completed transferring all of its ATV production to the Coweta County plant south of Atlanta.
The plant now employs 1,250 workers.
In addition to the 100 to be hired, an additional 300 workers could be added in years to come.
The facility already produces Yamaha's Grizzly 700 FI all-terrain vehicle and the YFZ450R sport ATV.
The new plant will produce the Viking side-by-side vehicle and other models.

facts: Yazaki plans to invest an additional USD 66 million in Nuevo León, Mexico.
The new capital investment will strengthen the company’s operations in engineering and manufacturing capabilities linked to electrification technologies.

On March 31, 2026, the Secretary General of the Investment Committee of Cambodia met with a delegation from YAZAKI (Cambodia) Products Co., Ltd. to discuss operations in Koh Kong province and production of vehicle wires and components.
Yazaki plans to expand its factory in Cambodia for vehicle wires and components by 2027.
On March 23, Yazaki North America, Inc. announced an agreement to proceed with the construction of a second manufacturing plant in Guatemala.
The new Guatemalan facility will be located in Pajapita, around seven kilometers from its existing plant in Ayutla.
The Guatemala site will produce wiring harnesses for vehicles.
On November 19, the United States activated the Rapid Response Labor Mechanism of the USMCA against automotive supplier Grupo Yazaki in León, Guanajuato.
The U.S. RRM activation was over alleged violations of workers’ rights to freedom of association and collective bargaining.
On October 10, Japanese automotive supplier Yazaki temporarily suspended operations at its León plant after detecting a geological fault beneath the facility.
The suspension aims to protect the safety of over two thousand employees working at the León site.
On April 3, 2025, auto component manufacturer Yazaki reported plans to set up a new manufacturing plant in Chennai, India, in collaboration with Horizon Industrial Parks.
The new Indian facility will be located in Horizon’s Chengalpet Industrial Park and span across 316 thousand square feet.
On January 30, Japanese automotive supplier Yazaki announced the closure of its two manufacturing plants in Colonia and Las Piedras, Uruguay.
The plant closures mark the end of nearly two decades of operations in Uruguay.
Production from the closed Uruguayan plants will be moved to Paraguay and Argentina.
Yazaki produces electrical components for major automakers including Toyota, Renault, Honda, and Volkswagen.
Yazaki Europe Middle East Africa N.V. (Yazaki EMEA), a subsidiary of Yazaki Corporation, completed the acquisition of the Lüdenscheid-based Junker & Halverscheid Group (JuHa) on July 2, 2024.
The integration of JuHa involves around 200 employees being integrated into the Yazaki EMEA operations.
Yazaki EMEA inked an agreement to acquire the Junker & Halverscheid Group, which includes Junker & Halverscheid Formenbau GmbH & Co. KG and JuHa Kunststoffverarbeitung GmbH & Co. KG.
The acquisition of the Junker & Halverscheid Group is subject to approval by competition authorities.

facts: Yazaki Innovations, Inc. (YII), the tech-disrupting subsidiary of Yazaki North America, unveiled a new line of busbars for electric vehicles, mobility, and industrial applications at CES 2025 in Las Vegas, NV on Jan. 7, 2025.
The newly unveiled busbars are engineered with cutting-edge technology to offer exceptional lightweight construction and efficiency.

Swarnjeet Dhaliwal, director of innovation at Yazaki Innovations, stated that the revolutionary line of busbars can be a game changer for EV manufacturers and industrial applications moving away from traditional approaches to moving electrical power.
The busbars are crafted from advanced materials, making them substantially lighter than traditional models.

The lightweight design results in reduced transportation costs, simplified installation with easier handling and faster deployment, and improved maneuverability for complex installations and tight spaces.

The innovative design maximizes current flow while minimizing energy loss, leading to reduced energy consumption, improved system reliability by minimizing heat generation and potential electrical faults, and enhanced system performance enabling higher power delivery with minimal losses.

The busbars are built to withstand demanding applications, offering exceptional corrosion resistance, high mechanical strength to withstand vibrations and mechanical stresses, and superior thermal stability across a wide range of operating temperatures.
YII’s parent company, Yazaki, is a global leader in the research, development, and delivery of vehicle power and data solutions for automotive applications.
Yazaki produces wire harnesses, power distribution & control products, connectors, driver information systems, sensors, and hybrid & electric vehicle products.
Yazaki's products can be found on nearly every vehicle on the road today.
source_title: Yazaki Innovations Showcases Leading-Edge Busbar Technology at CES 2025 - Yazaki

facts: Yazaki North America, Inc. (YNA) has entered into a Service Agreement with NEXCO-West USA (NEXCO), a leading provider of advanced road maintenance and inspection technologies in the United States.
The agreement supports NEXCO’s efforts to enhance road and bridge maintenance through innovative technologies, with a focus on non-destructive evaluation (NDE) solutions.
YNA aims to lay the groundwork for the potential future deployment of digital road maintenance software developed by Yazaki’s AI & Digital Division in the U.S. market.
As the first phase of this collaboration, YNA and NEXCO jointly conducted a bridge inspection project in the State of Minnesota in April 2025.
The bridge inspection project utilized cutting-edge NDE technologies, including Infrared Thermography (IRT) and Ground-Penetrating Rader (GPR), to assess bridge deck conditions.

This partnership provides YNA with an opportunity to support safer and more efficient infrastructure management in the United States, while laying the groundwork to apply its technologies in overseas markets in the future.

Yazaki North America is a leading automotive solutions supplier that designs and manufactures electrical distribution systems, components, electronics, and instrumentation solutions that power and connect modern vehicles worldwide.

facts: Yazaki North America, Inc. launched Component Central, a new online connector search engine that replaces the Connector Connection search capability on its website.

David Conrad, vice president of Information Technology & Global e-Business at Yazaki North America, stated the tool addresses customer needs and lays the foundation for increased e-commerce opportunities in the future.
Rich Tajer, director of Component Business Unit at Yazaki North America, highlighted the tool's additional functionality and ease of use for connector and terminal customers.
Component Central allows customers to pull component specifications, dimensions, and application data by inputting whole or partial part numbers and product type.
Future versions of Component Central will include sample and quote requests, customer design options, and online ordering.
Yazaki Corporation is a global leader in the research and development of vehicle power and data solutions, as well as advanced electronic technologies for vehicle applications.
Yazaki produces electrical distribution systems, fiber optics, advanced networking products, instrumentation, junction blocks, connector systems, and advanced electronics.
Worldwide, Yazaki employs over 115,000 people in 31 countries.
The company's North American headquarters and research center campus is located in Canton, Michigan.
