facts: Yazaki North America, Inc. (YNA) and MOTER Technologies, Inc. signed a strategic financial partnership agreement on May 14, 2025.
YNA made a strategic equity investment in MOTER, with the transaction closing on June 10, 2025.
The partnership will develop and promote AI-based insurance services to help mobility business players analyze and manage complex risks in an accelerating digital transformation environment.
Together, the companies will fuse MOTER’s insurance-software risk-analysis methodologies with Yazaki’s advanced AI to reduce accidents.
They will explore new business opportunities in the U.S. mobility market by working with automakers and mobility service providers.
MOTER deploys sophisticated AI to power real-time insurance software that performs risk analysis and premium calculations, which is installable on in-vehicle devices.
MOTER is developing next-generation insurance products and services specifically tailored for electric and autonomous vehicles.
Yazaki holds a strong track record in components for EVs and hybrid vehicles.
The Yazaki Group operates in three core business areas: Automotive Equipment (wire harnesses), Instrumentation (onboard devices for commercial vehicles), and Environmental Systems.
Yazaki holds a world-leading share in the wire harness market and a top-tier share in Japan for commercial vehicle onboard devices such as digital tachographs and dashcams.
Yazaki North America is headquartered in Canton, Michigan, USA, and engages in technology development, R&D activities, and new business development across North and Central America.

Ko Yazaki, Representative Director & Executive Vice President of Yazaki Corporation, stated the mobility industry is undergoing a once-in-a-century transformation driven by trends such as CASE and MaaS.

Kenichi Fujisawa, President & CEO of Yazaki North America, noted strong synergy between MOTER’s products and Yazaki’s AI-powered traffic accident reduction solutions currently under development within its AI & Digital Division.

Mitsuru Yamaguchi, Representative Director & Executive Vice President of Aioi Nissay Dowa Insurance Co., Ltd., explained that MOTER was established in April 2021 to develop insurance software embeddable in in-vehicle devices and next-gen products for EVs and autonomous vehicles.

Kenji Fujii, Founder & CEO of MOTER Technologies, Inc., highlighted the partnership's goal to develop innovative insurance products by leveraging cutting-edge technologies and data science alongside Yazaki’s automotive expertise.
The strategic financial partnership signing ceremony took place at MOTER’s headquarters in Torrance, California on May 14, 2025.

Attendees at the signing included Chopper Takeda (VP), Tets Hosoda (CFO), Kenji Fujii (CEO of MOTER), Itaru Motobayashi (EVP YNA), Tony Ishibashi (Head of Business Development of YNA), and Shaun Sato (EVP of MOTER).
Yazaki Corporation’s AI & Digital Division leverages cutting-edge technologies such as AI to analyze vast mobility, factory, and energy data.
source_title: Yazaki North America and MOTER Technologies Form  Financial Partnership for Future Strategic Collaboration

facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. announced the 50th anniversary of its Macon Factory on November 9, 2024, with a Family Day event for employees.

Speakers at the anniversary event included Mayor of Macon Lester Miller, Consul General of Japan Mio Maeda, YKK Corporation Chairman Masayuki Sarumaru, President of YKK Corporation of America Jim Reed, and President of YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. John Smith.
John Smith, President of YKK (U.S.A.) Inc., highlighted the pivotal role of the Macon Factory in embodying the company’s philosophy and commitment to the community.

Smith stated that the company expresses deep gratitude not only to its dedicated employees, whose tireless efforts have been instrumental, but also to the city of Macon for their unwavering support over the past fifty years, and is enthusiastic about extending this legacy of excellence and community partnership well into the future.
Japan-based YKK first entered the U.S. market in 1960 as a zipper distributor in New York City near the garment district.
Founder Tadao Yoshida envisioned a vertical integration model controlling the entire production process to ensure superior quality and cost efficiency.
A site selection team explored locations in Tennessee, North and South Carolina, Alabama, and Georgia as the U.S. textile industry shifted southward.
Georgia was selected due to its robust infrastructure, including four interstate highways, the deep-water port of Savannah, and a major international airport.
Cloyd Hall, a Macon native and Special Assistant to then-Governor Jimmy Carter, arranged a meeting that facilitated a connection between Yoshida and Carter.
YKK established its manufacturing facility just south of Macon in the newly developed Ocmulgee East Industrial Park.
Prominent Macon leaders visited YKK’s manufacturing center in Kurobe, Japan, to initiate the partnership.
Under the leadership of Yoshinori Kitano, construction and operation strategies were crafted for the new facility.
Newly hired engineers, including future YKK Corporation of America CEO Alex Gregory, were trained in Japan to transfer specialized manufacturing techniques to Macon.
The Macon plant opened in 1974.
The facility initially produced zippers tailored to the American market for clothing, furniture, and outdoor equipment.
Over the decades, the factory expanded dramatically and now employs over 600 local residents.
The Macon facility diversified into advanced solutions for industries including automotive, government, and medical sectors.
In 1977, YKK played a pivotal role in establishing a sister city relationship between Kurobe, Japan, and Macon, Georgia.
Over the past four decades, more than 1,100 citizens from both cities have visited each other's countries as part of the partnership.
YKK provides substantial sponsorships and active participation for the annual Macon Cherry Blossom Festival.
In 1990, YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. donated $300,000 towards the construction of a dedicated building for festival organizers.
The city of Macon donated $60,000 worth of land to complement the 1990 donation.
Chairman Tadahiro Yoshida personally attended the 1990 groundbreaking ceremony for the festival building.
YKK continues its annual support as a sponsor of the Macon Cherry Blossom Festival.
source_title: YKK USA Celebrates 50 Years of Innovation and Excellence at Macon Factory - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: YKK AP America Inc. is a leading Georgia-based manufacturer of commercial façade systems and residential windows and doors.
YKK AP America Inc. partnered with general contractor JE Dunn to host a topping-out ceremony for their new state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Macon, Georgia.
The topping-out ceremony took place on Friday at the 400,000-square-foot YKK AP Manufacturing Plant.
The ceremony kicked off at 11:00 A.M. and was followed by remarks from key stakeholders and refreshments for attendees.
The event celebrated a construction milestone on the $125 million project.
The manufacturing plant is on track to be finished by October.
The facility will combine two company-owned and operated facilities under one roof.
The new facility will create 100 new jobs.
The expansion will increase YKK AP's Macon workforce to 350 employees.
The facility will be located on Tractor Drive in Macon.

facts: YKK AP America Inc. held a ribbon cutting ceremony to mark the official opening of its first greenfield residential manufacturing facility in Macon-Bibb County, GA.

The ribbon cutting event was held in Macon with Commissioner Pat Wilson of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, Mayor Lester Miller of Macon-Bibb County, Chairman Robby Fountain of the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority, and other local leaders in attendance.

Mayor Lester Miller stated that YKK AP’s expansion into a new, leading-edge facility is a testament to its strong connection with the community and marked the beginning of a new chapter offering more opportunities to enrich the economy, improve the lives of families, and strengthen Macon's position as a hub of innovation and industry.
The facility completed construction in December 2023.
The facility began operations in January 2024.
The facility currently employs more than 250 employees in the Macon community.

Oliver Stepe, President & CEO of YKK AP America Inc., stated that the event marked more than a celebration of the new residential manufacturing facility, symbolized the company's growth and confidence in its business and employees moving forward, and expressed gratitude to the State of Georgia, the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority, and all partners for helping celebrate this important milestone in the company’s journey.
source_title: YKK AP holds ribbon cutting for new residential manufacturing facility in Macon | LBM Journal

facts: YKK AP America Inc. installed a new goods-to-person (GTP) automation system at its residential window plant in Macon, Georgia.
The GTP system was installed at the 400,000-square-foot purpose-built facility’s shipping department to further reduce the manual movement of heavy windows.
Dennis Howard serves as senior manager of transportation and logistics, and Daymon Loyd serves as director of residential manufacturing.
The GTP system improves operations and quality and reduces time to load by 39%.
The system uses autonomous robots to move finished products from the end of the line to material storage units and into a fenced GTP field that houses final products.
YKK AP is making strategic investments in automation to reduce risk and improve safety across its operations.
The specific goal for residential manufacturing was to reduce material handling to improve quality, starting with the shipping department where most material handling occurs.
When the Macon facility opened in early 2024, YKK AP aimed for zero injuries, zero backorders, and 100% on-time delivery.
The facility currently achieves a 97% on-time delivery rate.
To hit 100% on-time delivery, YKK AP invested in the GTP system to close the gap.
The facility handles 80% of its products with the GTP system; the remaining 20% are too large for the material storage units.
Integrating the new autonomous system with YKK AP’s IT infrastructure was identified as the most significant deployment challenge.
Training for the system interface required an aggressive schedule focused on troubleshooting technical issues as they arose.
The GTP system is considered vital for companies seeking to improve safety via automation, provided they remain open to change.
This was the residential manufacturing team’s first implementation of GTP technology, requiring flexibility to adjust original plans to accommodate the technology correctly.
Much of the capital equipment was staged on the manufacturing floor before deployment, involving time and trial and error to reach full operational potential.
source_title: USGlass • September 2025 • Company News

facts: YKK is a Japanese company celebrating 50 years since opening its doors in Macon, Georgia.
YKK first entered the U.S. market in 1960 as a zipper distributor in New York City.
Production started at YKK's textile and metal zipper plant in Macon in 1974 with approximately 50 employees.
YKK founder Tadao Yoshida and a site selection team explored locations in Tennessee, North and South Carolina, Alabama, and Georgia before settling in Georgia.
Georgia was chosen due to its robust infrastructure, including four interstate highways, the deep-water port of Savannah, and a major international airport.
A meeting arranged by Cloyd Hall, a Macon native and special assistant to former Gov. Jimmy Carter, led to a connection between Yoshida and Carter that clinched the decision to settle in Georgia.

YKK officials stated the strategic decision to establish its manufacturing facility just south of Macon in the newly developed Ocmulgee East Industrial Park heralded a deep partnership with the region.
Under the leadership of Yoshinori Kitano, construction and operation strategies were crafted for the Macon facility.
Newly hired engineers, including future YKK Corporation of America CEO Alex Gregory, were trained in Japan to transfer specialized manufacturing techniques to the Macon facility.
The facility initially focused on producing zippers for clothing, furniture, and outdoor equipment tailored to the American market.
Over the decades, the factory has expanded dramatically and now employs over 600 local residents.
YKK has diversified its advanced solutions into industries including automotive, government, and medical sectors.
Marty Timms, YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. vice president of manufacturing, cited the mutual commitment between the company and the Macon community as key to the company's success.
Timms noted that Macon provides tremendous community support and a great workforce to draw from.
Timms highlighted the outstanding industrial authority and chamber of commerce in Macon as always supportive and eager to help with needed resources.
YKK has made significant contributions to local community projects and events for decades, exemplifying its commitment to corporate social responsibility.
In 1977, YKK played a pivotal role in establishing a sister city relationship between Kurobe, Japan, and Macon.
Over the past four decades, the sister city partnership has fostered cultural exchange with more than 1,100 citizens from both cities visiting each other’s countries.
Officials said YKK has been a supporter of the Macon Cherry Blossom Festival through sponsorships and participation in festival activities.
In 1991, YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. donated $300,000 toward the construction of a dedicated building for the festival organizers.
The city of Macon complemented the donation by providing $60,000 worth of land for the festival building.
Chairman Tadahiro Yoshida, son of founder Tadao Yoshida and recipient of the Order of the Rising Sun with Gold and Silver Stars from the Japanese government, was present for the 1991 dedication.
YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. is committed to sustaining its founder's legacy by enhancing Macon's prosperity, enriching Georgia, and advancing the global textile industry.
The company operates under a “Cycle of Goodness” philosophy where no one prospers without rendering benefit to others.
YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. plans to hold a celebration for its employees later in 2024.
source_title: Japanese company YKK celebrates 50 years in Macon, Georgia. | Macon Telegraph

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal spoke during the announcement for the Georgia Ports Authority’s new inland terminal on Monday, Dec. 3, 2018, in Gainesville, Ga.
Cargo moved via the Northeast Georgia Inland Port near Gainesville will reduce Atlanta truck traffic by shifting containers to rail.
The new inland port is set to open in 2021.
The terminal will be operated by the Georgia Ports Authority and served by Norfolk Southern.

On Dec. 3, 2018, Gov. Nathan Deal, representatives of the Georgia Ports Authority, the Greater Hall Chamber, and Norfolk Southern Railroad announced plans to make Hall County the site of the GPA’s next inland port.
The Northeast Georgia Inland Port will be situated in the heart of the manufacturing and logistics corridor along Interstate 85.
The region is important for the production of heavy equipment, food, and forest products.
Besides serving existing port customers, the terminal will act as an economic development tool drawing new investment to Hall and surrounding counties.
The Gainesville terminal will handle both import and export containers.
Norfolk Southern will provide service on a direct rail route to and from the Port of Savannah’s Garden City Terminal.
Jeff Heller is Vice President Intermodal & Automotive at Norfolk Southern.
Savannah is a rapidly growing gateway for global commerce, and Hall County and the surrounding region in Northeast Georgia are key areas of expansion in the state.
Manufacturers and distributors around the globe continue to set their sights on this region for development.
The GPA’s inland port at Gainesville combined with Norfolk Southern’s rail service will provide crucial links in the supply chains of local industries, consumers, and the rest of the world.
It will serve as a catalyst for new opportunities for industrial development.
Improved access to rail will increase logistics options and overall efficiency while reducing congestion on Georgia highways.
Presently, containers moving by truck travel a 600-mile roundtrip to and from the Port of Savannah.
When the new rail yard opens, drivers will be able to make shorter trips from area manufacturers and distribution facilities to the Northeast Georgia site.
Griff Lynch is Executive Director at GPA.
The Gainesville location is part of the Network Georgia initiative, which provides new and existing port customers with additional logistics options.
The new rail hub will allow importers and exporters to move loaded containers to the coast with greater efficiency.
It will provide a ready source of empty containers for Georgia exports.
Jimmy Allgood is GPA Board Chairman.
The new choice in cargo handling will transform the local rail market and help port customers optimize their supply chains.
Inland terminals are bringing ports and producers closer together, providing new economic opportunities.
Georgians will benefit from increased employment options as more companies expand or locate here.
Georgia Ports already support more than 440,000 jobs across every corner of the state.
The terminal is located in the Gateway Industrial Centre on GA 365.
It will be a new 104-acre terminal providing logistics solutions for customers across Northeast Georgia.
The facility’s services will draw from the 1.5-million population in Hall, Gwinnett, and surrounding counties.
Tim Evans is vice president of economic development for the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce.
The new inland port terminal will shorten the supply chain for many manufacturers, processors, and distributors in the region.

Accessing the container port by rail can save time and money because rail deliveries to and from the Port of Savannah can shorten truck delivery times from approximately seven hours to less than 30 minutes.
Direct access to the Port of Savannah, the fastest growing container port in the U.S., is an innovation in logistics infrastructure that provides many existing businesses a competitive advantage.
Auto Metal Direct (AMD), a worldwide distributor of auto body panels and trim for classic cars and trucks, announced its intent to open a new 318,000 square-foot distribution and fulfillment center.
Construction will soon begin on the new home for AMD in the Gateway Industrial Centre.
Upon completion, the $15 million development will bring 40 jobs to Hall County.
Mark Headrick is President of AMD.
Hundreds of containers each year are received by AMD, so GPA services are essential for the maintenance and growth of their business.
The proximity of the new inland port was an influencing factor in the eventual location of their building.
Pat Wilson is Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner.
The GPA continues to be a fantastic economic development partner and catalyst for growth in the state.
Ports have positioned Georgia as a global leader in terms of logistics infrastructure.
Ports have been a critical piece of the puzzle in locating new projects and existing industry expansions across the state.
AMD expansion is a testament to GPA efforts and will further bolster the strength of the workforce in this community.
The Northeast Georgia Inland Port is the second Network Georgia-related announcement this year.
GPA held a grand opening for the Appalachian Regional Port on Aug. 22 just north of Chatsworth, Ga., on a 42-acre site in Murray County.
The Hall County inland terminal is scheduled to be complete in 2021.
At full build-out, it will have the capacity to handle up to 150,000 containers per year.
Phil Sutton is Vice President Administration at Kubota Manufacturing of America Corporation.
GPA has supported Kubota for many years, helping to find solutions to reduce lead times for both in- and outbound shipments.
Using the Port of Savannah provides Kubota a great cost alternative to West Coast ports.
Kubota anticipates several levels of potential cost savings with the new inland port, including reduced costs for chassis and container fees and reduced FTZ administrative fees.
Kubota expects a reduction in lead times due to greater availability of empty containers and shorter transit times to and from the inland port.
There is huge potential to significantly reduce reliance on OTR motor carriers and reduce lead time for inbound parts and outbound finished goods.
Mike Giles is President of the Georgia Poultry Federation.

For many years, Georgia poultry producers have reached customers all over the world through the Port of Savannah, which currently ranks as the number one export facility for poultry products in the nation.

Because poultry producers are relatively close to the port, the Georgia poultry industry enjoys a competitive advantage when delivering chicken products to overseas customers efficiently and cost-effectively.

GPA has invested in infrastructure improving service for poultry shipments through Savannah, and the new inland rail yard continues that investment in future connectivity between the poultry industry and customers worldwide.
Arrik Williams is Business Development Manager at Tatsumi Intermodal USA.
The construction of the inland terminal near their facility would be an immediate enhancement for current and potential new operations.
Tatsumi Intermodal USA’s business model is built around appointing container equipment and negotiating trucking rates between Hall County, Atlanta, Savannah, and Charleston.
Being able to look to Gainesville for these applications will allow better rate negotiation while adding transloading services for customers, simultaneously reducing tractor trailers on interstates.
Brandon Loftus is Director of Operations at ZF Gainesville, LLC.
Continued development of the supply chain for ZF’s business in Gainesville is vital to future growth plans.

ZF utilizes the port regularly for import and export and supports the Gainesville community and State of Georgia which continually supports industry and manufacturing through infrastructure improvements.
This activity should help streamline logistics and support ZF’s assembly and manufacturing processes.
Larry L. Enterline is Chief Executive Officer at FOX Factory.
FOX Factory factored the favorable business climate into their decision to expand Powered Vehicles Group’s operations in Georgia.
Benjamin Sippl is Vice President at Mugele Group.
The terminal will benefit nearby industry and especially local trucking companies.

Due to trucker availability challenges, a rail facility carrying material from the coast will make it easier for manufacturers to get material delivered to assembly lines on time and at a more economical cost.
Rail access could make Mugele more attractive by offering consolidation warehouses of goods nearby a port that can be picked, packed, and shipped to the assembly line on JIT basis.
This reduces exposure to risks during transportation and offers manufacturers flexibility with their own in-house storage capacity.
Georgia’s deepwater ports and inland barge terminals support more than 439,000 jobs throughout the state annually.
They contribute $25 billion in income, $106 billion in revenue, and $2.9 billion in state and local taxes to Georgia’s economy.
The Port of Savannah handled 8.5 percent of U.S. containerized cargo volume and 10 percent of all U.S. containerized exports in FY2017.
source_title: Georgia announces new inland terminal location - Georgia Ports Authority

facts: Dongwon Tech Corporation announced on November 18, 2020, that it will invest $700,000 to open an advanced manufacturing facility and sales office in Hall County.
The project is expected to deliver 40 jobs to the Gainesville area.
The new facility will be located at 2175 Centennial Drive in Gainesville and will span 20,000 square feet.
Job positions at the Hall County facility will include roles in production, management, sales, and administration.
Dongwon Tech Corporation is a U.S. subsidiary of Yuchang Air Con Co., Ltd., which is headquartered in Anyang, Gyeonggi-do, Korea.
The company produces heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) ducts, CR/DR systems, and HVAC items that help save energy and meet customer-specific needs.
The Gainesville facility will primarily produce rectangular air ducts for SK Innovation and another local HVAC company located less than an hour away in Commerce, Georgia.
Dongwon Tech is planning to invest in the production of spiral ducts to capture a broader market in the coming years.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the project, citing Georgia as the No. 1 State for Business eight years in a row.

CheolWoong Heo, CEO of Dongwon Tech Corporation, stated that Georgia's large Korean-American population and existing Korean conglomerates made it easier for the company to enter during early investment stages.
Tim Evans, Vice President of the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce, noted that Dongwon Tech chose Gainesville-Hall County for its new North American headquarters and manufacturing center.

Yoonie Kim, Director of Korean Investment at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce division, represented GDEcD on this project in partnership with the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Dongwon Tech's investment moves Georgia another step closer to building the battery supply chain necessary to support the electrification of the automotive industry.

Wilson added that Dongwon Tech will supply SK Innovation in Commerce, Georgia, as well as HVAC customers throughout the Southeastern U.S., highlighting the state's competitive logistics network and business climate.
The company's main products are all kinds of ducts for HVAC, and it also supplies and installs various HVAC equipment with its Energy Saving Technology.
source_title: Dongwon Tech to invest $700K in Gainesville plant

facts: Adient PLC is a global leader in automotive seating and an automotive seating manufacturer.
Adient's stock price touched a 52-week low of $20.16 amid market challenges.
Adient experienced a 47.15% stock decline over the past year.
Adient faces automotive industry headwinds including supply chain disruptions and shifting consumer demands.
Adient is facing operational challenges due to slower vehicle launches by its OEM customers.
Adient is facing an unfavorable product mix.
Adient is experiencing decreased demand in the electric vehicle market.
Adient is undergoing a gradual shift away from its lower-margin metals business.
Adient significantly reduced its revenue guidance for fiscal year 2024.
Adient made further revenue guidance adjustments in the third fiscal quarter due to the production environment and foreign exchange rates.
Deutsche Bank resumed coverage on Adient, assigning a Hold rating with a price target of $24.00.
Adient revised its target for an 8% EBITDA margin to be achieved by the end of fiscal year 2027, representing a 200 basis points increase from the anticipated margin for fiscal year 2024.
Adient is pursuing strategic shifts including growth in the Chinese market, restructuring in Europe, and a full exit from the lower-margin metals business to reach its EBITDA margin target.
Adient has a market capitalization of $1.77 billion.
Adient's price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio stands at 26.51, adjusting to 12.66 over the last twelve months as of Q3 2024.
Adient reported revenue of $14.86 billion, experiencing a contraction of 3.01% over the last twelve months.
Adient's management has been actively buying back shares, signaling confidence in the company's value proposition.
Adient is noted for high shareholder yield but does not pay a dividend.
Adient is expected to be profitable this year according to analyst predictions.
source_title: Adient stock hits 52-week low at $20.16 amid market challenges By Investing.com

facts: Adient is a global leader in automotive seating with approximately 72,000 employees across 31 countries.
Adient operates more than 200 manufacturing and assembly plants worldwide.
The company produces and delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's product range spans from complete seating systems to individual components.
The company's expertise covers every step of the automotive seat-making process.
Adient's integrated in-house skills enable it to take products from research and design to engineering and manufacturing.
Adient supplies its products into more than 20 million vehicles every year.
The company expects increased earnings, margin, and free cash flow in fiscal year 2023.
Adient's Board of Directors approved a $600M share repurchase program.
Douglas Del Grosso serves as Adient's president and chief executive officer.
Jeff Stafeil serves as Adient's executive vice president and chief financial officer.
Adient cites risks related to the Ukraine conflict and COVID lockdowns in China impacting regional economies and supply chains.
The company notes potential work stoppages due to supply chain disruptions and similar events.
Adient highlights risks regarding the availability of raw materials and component products, including semiconductors required by customers for vehicle manufacturing.
The company faces uncertainty regarding its ability to execute its turnaround plan and meet debt service requirements.
Adient notes that global climate change and related emphasis on ESG matters may impact consumer demand and market shifts.

Executive Vice President and CFO Jerome Dorlack and VP, Treasurer, Investor Relations & Corporate Communications Mark Oswald will participate in a fireside chat at 12:40 p.m. ET.
A live webcast of the fireside chat will be available on the investor section of the Adient website.
Adient employs over 70,000 people across 30 countries.
Adient operates more than 200 manufacturing/assembly plants worldwide.
Adient provides automotive seating solutions and serves all major OEMs.
Adient manages the seat-making process from design to manufacturing.

Adient delivers its products into millions of vehicles every year.
On Feb. 8, 2023, ADNT stock declined 3.23%.
