facts: Rivian is increasing initial production capacity at its Georgia manufacturing plant to 300k vehicles annually, a 50% jump from the original 200k estimate, to support its new midsize R2 platform.
Vertical construction for Rivian's Georgia plant is expected to begin this spring, with vehicle production starting in late 2028.
Rivian announced a partnership with Uber to build up to 50,000 robotaxis at the Georgia plant.

Chinese supplier Yanfeng Automotive Interiors will lay off 153 workers at its Chattanooga, Tennessee, plant on June 29, ending a relationship dating to 2017 when it began supplying dashboards and door panels for VW’s Atlas SUV.
Although the Yanfeng Chattanooga plant was expected to employ about 325 people at full production after a $55M investment, volume never reached that level.
VW spokesperson Michael Lowder noted that the automaker periodically changes suppliers for specific components.
VW spokesperson Michael Lowder confirmed that seating supplier Adient will stop supplying VW around July, resulting in the closure of Adient’s Athens, Tennessee, plant and 210 additional layoffs.
VW recently ended ID.4 production in Chattanooga to focus on the gas-powered Atlas family.

General Motors is investing roughly $1.4B across three US plants and one Canadian plant that produce gas engines, transmissions, and metal castings, reinforcing its combustion-engine capacity as EV demand softens.
The GM investment includes $300M at Romulus, Michigan, for transmission production.
The GM investment includes $150M at a Saginaw, Michigan, casting plant for engine parts.
The GM investment includes $40M at a Toledo, Ohio, transmission plant.
The GM investment includes $505M at an Ontario facility for the next-generation V-8 engine.

Several major automakers booked roughly $2.3B in expected refunds from US tariff payments in Q1 following a February Supreme Court ruling that struck down some Trump administration tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).
Ford recorded $1.3B in tariff refunds; GM, $500M; Stellantis, $467M; and Mercedes-Benz, an unspecified amount.
These refund bookings were made as accounting estimates, not cash received.
Up to $166B in reimbursements is due to importers who paid IEEPA tariffs, with companies acknowledging uncertainty over timing.
President Trump warned he would “remember” companies that opt for refunds.
GM said tariffs will still cut $2.5B to $3.5B from its 2026 profits, while Ford put its net tariff cost at $1B.
Foreign automakers have warned the Trump administration that they could pull their cheapest car models from the US market if USMCA is not renewed or is significantly weakened.

The companies told Trump’s economic advisers that they may not be able to build and sell affordable cars for the US market without meaningfully reduced tariffs on cars and parts made in North America.
The auto industry has been pressing for an extension of USMCA, calling it crucial to American auto production.
Trump’s 25% Section 232 tariff on Mexican and Canadian automotive exports replaced the duty-free trade under the agreement.
The three nations face a July 1 deadline for the USMCA trade deal review.
The Atlantic Council laid out three scenarios for USMCA’s July review: clean renewal with targeted modifications, no consensus leading to bilateral deals, or no consensus and no replacement.

Each USMCA scenario carries distinct supply chain consequences: the bilateral scenario forces manufacturers to operate under dual compliance regimes, while the no-deal scenario reverts trade to pre-NAFTA tariff levels.

The Atlantic Council analysis recommends supply chain teams treat the period before July as a planning window: rethink production footprints, strengthen US hubs, shift from just-in-time to inventory buffers, invest in dual sourcing, monitor supplier solvency, and run bill-of-materials audits to identify components that could lose preferred status.
Companies with integrated North American platforms face the greatest exposure, particularly if Canadian upstream metals and subassemblies lose preferential access.

Chinese automakers SAIC Motor and Hongqi are exploring production in Spain to bypass EU tariffs, with SAIC planning an EV factory for its MG brand and Hongqi negotiating to build vehicles at Stellantis’ Zaragoza plant through Leapmotor.

Volkswagen CEO Oliver Blume told analysts that VW is exploring whether to bring its China-developed cars to Europe and to share its underused European factories with Chinese partners including SAIC, FAW, JAC, and Xpeng.

Toyota Industries President Koichi Ito said Japanese suppliers are increasingly receiving two weeks’ notice that components will not be available, as the Iran conflict ripples through the auto industry’s supply base.

Toyota supplier Denso projected $3.1B in operating profit for the fiscal year through March 2027, well below the $4B analyst estimate, and has factored in roughly $282M in profit hit from supply chain uncertainty.
Toyoda Gosei warned that raw material disruptions could emerge as early as June, particularly thinners used in automotive paint, and has assumed 200k fewer vehicles in its production planning.
Toyota Boshoku is now seeking short-term supply assurances from individual suppliers, as long-term commitments have become difficult to secure.
Toyota Boshoku executives note that everything from door trims to seat urethane traces back to naphtha-derived resins.
source_title: China's automotive path to the EU through Spain

facts: Based in Plymouth, MI, Adient plc is the world’s largest manufacturer of automotive seating.
The company accounts for one-third of the global market and provides components for 25 million vehicles.
Adient was founded as a spin-off from Johnson Controls in 2016.
Today, the company works with all major automakers and vehicle classes.
Adient operates some 200 assembly plants in 30 countries.
One of those factories is a 125,000-square-foot plant in West Point, GA.
At the West Point factory, seats, door panels and other interior parts are assembled for the Kia Sorento.
Adient began building the Georgia plant in 2009 while it was still part of Johnson Controls.
Todd Emmons is an engineer in the automotive business unit for Adient.
Subassemblies for some 500 cars a day move through the West Point facility.
The plant completes 55 jobs an hour, with a goal to step that up to 60 jobs an hour.
John Maicki is president of DR Storage Systems.
Adient required durable, ergonomically sound assembly line technology capable of accommodating various product sizes and being moved, adjusted, and reused as production demands changed.
The company previously used tubular pipe structures on assembly lines in some plants but found them ineffective for the Georgia facility due to customization and mobility limitations.
Adient selected UNEX Manufacturing’s Flow Cell workstations after conducting a thorough analysis, obtaining vendor quotes, and developing prototypes.
Each workstation at the West Point plant has 84 inches of pick space.
UNEX’s Flow Bed carton flow accommodates seats, door handles, power controls, air bags, brackets, buttons, and other materials.
The Georgia plant operates on a just-in-time basis to deliver products exactly when the nearby Kia facility is ready.
Reconfiguring the Flow Cells at the West Point plant is three times faster than reconfiguring other types of rack products.
Adient plans to soon add new car parts to the production line for other types of vehicles.
Adient has ordered 41 Flow Cells and assembled them using UNEX instructions in less than half an hour each.
John Maicki first recommended the Flow Cell product to a customer in 2003.
Customers are constantly reconfiguring products and updating standards, requiring plants to remain flexible and efficient.

links.context: More information on Flow Cell products

source_title: Flow Racks Put Automotive Supplier in the Driver’s Seat | ASSEMBLY

facts: Yanfeng entered into an agreement with Adient to acquire Adient Asia’s 49.99 percent share in the Yanfeng Adient Seating Co, Ltd (YFAS) joint venture in China.
As part of this agreement, Yanfeng takes full ownership of the joint venture and all 16,000 employees.
The joint venture includes 55 seat manufacturing facilities.
The joint venture includes a full-service technical center and JARC (headliner company).
Current and sourced future programs will transition to Yanfeng.
YFAS was established in 1997.
YFAS has developed independent and complete R&D and manufacturing systems in automotive seating with a wide range of OEM customers.
YFAS products cover complete seats, frames, foam, and headliners.
Gerald Jia, CEO of Yanfeng, stated that making YFAS a wholly-owned entity enhances capabilities and product offering as a full-service supplier through cross-business collaboration.
The three seating technical centers in Shanghai, Hefei, and Chongqing will join Yanfeng’s existing global innovation and engineering network.
This integration will offer product and innovation synergies including Interiors, Seating, Safety, and Cockpit Electronics.
YFAS’ manufacturing locations in China, along with Yanfeng's existing seat manufacturing bases in Thailand, Serbia, and Mexico, will be consolidated to form a complete seat supply capacity.
The consolidation aims to make the business fully globalized.
The transaction includes equity share and controlling interest changes to other joint ventures held by Yanfeng and Adient in China.
Yanfeng will continue its current JV relationship (Keiper) with Adient in mechanisms.
Keiper is a consolidated entity under Yanfeng, focusing on seat mechanism components.
The transaction is subject to regulatory approval.
The transaction is expected to be completed in six months.
During the transition period, Yanfeng will maintain close cooperation with Adient to ensure smooth business transition and continuity of program execution and delivery.
Yanfeng remains committed to offering one-stop Smart Cabin solutions to global customers.
Yanfeng is a leading global automotive supplier, focusing on interior, exterior, seating, cockpit electronics, and passive safety.
Yanfeng has more than 240 locations and approximately 55,000 employees worldwide.
The technical team of 4,200 experts is in 8 R&D centers and other regional offices.
Yanfeng has complete capabilities including engineering and software development, styling, and test validation.
Yanfeng focuses on Smart Cabin and lightweight technology to support automakers with future mobility and cabin solutions.
source_title: | PressReleasePoint

facts: The ADIENT plant in Lučenec was established in 2005.
It currently employs more than 800 people.
Due to its complexity, it is one of the largest plants of the Adient company and is continuously modernized according to Industry 4.0 standards.
The facility focuses on the production of headrests and foam parts for car seats.
Its products are supplied to 21 car manufacturers worldwide.
The product portfolio ranges from standard items to luxury components.
The company plays a significant role in implementing RFID technology into its production process.
The contactless RFID identification technology allows customers to track individual operations throughout the entire production process up to the final product.
RFID technology is also used for the inspection of final packaging and shipping.
source_title: Adient - BARTECH SLOVAKIA

facts: The global automotive pneumatic comfort seat system market is expected to see huge growth by 2030.
By functions, the pneumatic massage segment is expected to register significant CAGR during the forecast period.

Increase in demand for premium automobiles in developing countries and surge in need for comfortable commercial vehicle seating technology drive the growth of the global automotive pneumatic comfort seat system market.
High costs and malfunctioning issues of pneumatic seating solutions hinder the market growth.
Retrofitting of existing automotive vehicles and adoption of lightweight and durable seating solutions create new opportunities in the coming years.

Based on vehicle type, the passenger car segment held the largest share in 2020, contributing to more than half of the total share, and is projected to maintain its leadership status during the forecast period.
The heavy commercial vehicle segment is projected to manifest the highest CAGR of 7.2% from 2021 to 2030.

Based on region, North America accounted for the largest share in 2020, contributing to around one-third of the total share of the market, and is estimated to maintain its leadership status during the forecast period.
Asia-Pacific is projected to register the fastest CAGR of 7.5% from 2021 to 2030.
The sale of the automotive pneumatic comfort seat system is directly associated with automotive production and sales activities across the globe.

The spread of the COVID-19 pandemic has negatively impacted the growth of the automotive pneumatic comfort seat system market, owing to commute restrictions and poor financial performance of the market players in 2020.

The COVID-19 crisis is causing uncertainty in the automotive pneumatic comfort seat system market by delaying supply chains, hampering business growth, generating uncertain demand scenarios, and increasing panic among the customer segments.

Based on propulsion type, the ICE vehicles segment contributed to the highest share in 2020, accounting for more than four-fifths of the total share of the global automotive pneumatic comfort seat system market, and is projected to maintain its dominant share during the forecast period.
The electric and hybrid vehicles segment is expected to portray the highest CAGR of 8.8% from 2021 to 2030.
Asia-Pacific and Europe are the leading consumers of automotive pneumatic comfort seat systems, and this trend is projected to continue during the forecast period.

India is developing at a significant rate, as a result of government initiatives, such as self-reliant India, to boost its manufacturing activities and enhance the outlook for automotive manufacturers.

Key players profiled in the report include Continental AG, Magna International Inc., Robert Bosch GmbH, Toyota Boshoku, Tangtraning Seating Technology Inc., Adient PLC, Kongsberg Automotive, Faurecia, Grammer AG, TS Tech, Lear Corporation, Leggett And Platt, Alfmeier, Genthner.
source_title: Automotive Pneumatic Comfort Seat System Market to See Huge Growth by 2030 | Adient PLC, Magna International Inc. - EIN Presswire

facts: Adient plc is a global leader in the automotive seating supply industry with leading market positions in the Americas, Europe and China.
Adient maintains longstanding relationships with the largest global automotive original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).

Adient's proprietary technologies extend into virtually every area of automotive seating solutions, including complete seating systems, frames, mechanisms, foam, head restraints, armrests and trim covers.
Adient operates more than 200 wholly- and majority-owned manufacturing or assembly facilities, with operations in 29 countries.
Adient has partially-owned affiliates in China, Asia, Europe and North America.
Adient's business model focuses on developing and maintaining long-term customer relationships to grow with leading global OEMs.
Adient works closely with customers as vehicle platforms are developed, creating close ties with key decision makers at OEM customers.
Adient has more than 70,000 employees operating in more than 200 manufacturing and assembly plants in 29 countries worldwide.
Adient intends to maintain high capacity utilization and increase efficiency through continued use of standardized manufacturing processes.
Adient employs a Sales & Operational Planning (S&OP) process to ensure appropriate service levels, minimal inventory and optimal factory utilization.
Adient works with OEMs to develop complete seating solutions to meet consumer expectations for performance, safety and comfort.
Adient's customer teams lead the new business acquisition process to ensure alignment with product, process and manufacturing strategies.
Management expects to increase investment in innovation for product and process technologies.
Adient utilizes a global Core Product Portfolio (CPP) strategy for part and design reuse across all product applications.
Through the CPP strategy, Adient provides high quality products with market competitive cost and mass (low weight to improve fuel economy).

Adient's worldwide engineering network includes ten core development centers located in Plymouth (USA), Trencin (Slovakia), Burscheid (Germany), Yokohama (Japan), Solingen (Germany), Chongqing (China), Kaiserslautern (Germany), Ceska Lipa (Czech Republic), Ansan (South Korea) and Pune (India).
Adient leverages low cost country development centers in India, China, Czech Republic and Slovakia.
Adient is a leading supplier of "just-in-time" seating in China, operating through wholly owned entities and 6 joint ventures with 36 manufacturing locations in 22 cities.
Adient has relationships with global OEM customers to expand business with regional customers growing globally and new automotive market entrants.
Adient is able to leverage its position as the market leader in Europe, North America and China to grow in other markets such as Southeast Asia.

Adient designs and manufactures a full range of seating systems and components for passenger cars, commercial vehicles and light trucks, including vans, pick-up trucks and sport/crossover utility vehicles.

Adient is a supplier to all global OEMs and has longstanding relationships with premier automotive manufacturers including BMW, Mercedes-Benz Group, Ford Motor Company, General Motors Company, Honda Motor Company, Hyundai Motor Company, Jaguar Land Rover, Kia Corporation, Mazda Motor Corporation, Mitsubishi Motor Corporation, Nissan Motor Corporation, Renault Group, Stellantis N.V., Suzuki Motor Corporation, Toyota Motor Corporation, Volkswagen Group and Volvo Car Group.

Adient supplies most growing regional OEMs such as Beijing Automotive Group CO., Ltd., Changan Automobile (Group) Co., Ltd., FAW Group Corporation, Proton Holdings Berhad, Ashok Leyland, Tata Motors Limited and Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd.
Adient also supplies newer auto manufacturers such as NIO and Xpeng Motors.
Adient has more than 6 joint venture partnerships with key OEMs including Guangzhou Automobile Group Co., Ltd., Beijing Automobile Group Co., Ltd. and FAW Group Corporation.
The Automotive Seating industry provides OEMs with complete seats on a "just-in-time" or "in-sequence" basis, assembled to specific order and delivered directly to an automotive assembly line.

Adient is a global leader in complete seat assembly and one of the largest in all major seating components, operating plants that produce seating foam, metal structures, seat covers and seat mechanisms.

Demand for automotive parts in the OEM market is generally a function of new vehicle production driven by macro-economic factors such as credit availability, interest rates, fuel prices, consumer confidence, employment and other trends.
Most OEMs have adopted global vehicle platforms to increase standardization, reduce per unit cost and increase capital efficiency and profitability.
Three seating sourcing patterns exist among OEMs: core seat structures, component sourcing, and engineering "in-sourcing".

As a supplier with global scale and strong design, engineering and lean manufacturing capabilities, Adient is positioned to accommodate core seat structures, component sourcing, and engineering in-sourcing patterns.
New safety and environmental regulations and rapid customer preference changes are requiring suppliers to respond faster with new designs and product innovations.
Electric vehicles (EVs) continue to be a focus in the global automotive industry driven by product offerings from legacy manufacturers and new entrants.
The rollout of EV platforms varies across regions with factors such as pricing, affordability, government incentives, infrastructure and overall consumer acceptance influencing adoption pace.
While seating systems are not largely impacted by the shift to EVs, key attributes of seat design are evolving as the market pivots toward EVs.

This movement provides Adient opportunities to provide value added solutions through its Evolution of Seating Systems Sustainability (ES3) and capture market share through new entrants based on its existing leading market position.

As the global automotive industry incorporates Advanced Driver Assist Systems (ADAS) and Automated Driving Systems (ADS), Adient is poised to capitalize on greater seating content accompanying these innovations.
Adient has developed an interiors concept for autonomous driving addressing major seating and interior trends expected to drive the future automotive industry.
Adient will continue to partner with OEMs and other customers in the development of ADAS and ADS concepts.
Adient faces competition from traditional seating suppliers, component specialists, and competitors partnered with an OEM through ownership or interlocking business relationships.
Independent principal competitors include Lear Corporation, Toyota Boshoku Corporation, Forvia SE and Magna International Inc.
Raw materials used by Adient include steel, aluminum, polyurethane chemicals, fabrics, leather, vinyl and polypropylene.
The automotive industry experienced significant volatility in commodity prices continuing into fiscal 2023, which may continue as demand increases and/or supply remains constrained.
Price volatility has resulted in an overall increase of input costs for Adient that may not be, or may only be partially, offset through customer negotiations.
During fiscal 2024, commodity prices and availability could fluctuate throughout the year and significantly affect Adient's results of operations.
Adient embeds sustainability into its company by integrating ESG principles into product development initiatives, manufacturing processes, procurement practices and corporate governance activities.
In fiscal year 2021, Adient set a goal of reducing its scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions 75% by 2030 (with 2019 as the base year).
In 2023, Adient expanded its goal with a longer term target of achieving carbon neutrality at its manufacturing locations for scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.

Recent sustainability examples include replacing company pool vehicles with EVs as leases end, recovering waste heat from air compressors, adding occupant sensors to plant floors, optimizing foam mold temperature machines, replacing cardboard boxes with reusable packaging, and collecting rainwater for sanitary applications.
Adient has set a goal to attribute 100% of the electricity needed worldwide to renewable sources by 2035.
Several of Adient’s facilities already generate renewable electricity on-site via solar panel installations, and more than 60 Adient locations now consume electricity from a renewable source.

Sustainability has been part of product development for more than three decades, with vehicle electrification emphasizing the need for lighter, slimmer seating products made of environmentally friendly materials.

Adient developed Soft Back Panel and Soft Side Valance components that integrate up to 70% recycled polyethylene terephthalate (PET) while improving knee clearance and reducing seat weight by as much as 2 kg.
Adient developed an innovative, EV-ready concept seat called the Pure Essential that combines comfort with a sleek appearance while reducing cost, complexity, weight and carbon footprint.

The Pure Essential seat promotes product circularity by using just two primary eco-friendly material types: green steel for the seat structure and recyclable polyester for comfort features and trim covers.
Adient is working with suppliers to reduce scope 3 (value chain) emissions 35% by 2030 (with 2019 as the base year).
In fiscal year 2024, Adient plans to expand the use of its proprietary Product Carbon Footprint Tool to impact designer, engineer and customer decisions.
Adient updated its Ethics Policy in 2023 to address evolving laws and regulations including those regarding ESG.
In 2023, Adient initiated ESG-related due diligence questionnaires for top suppliers to promote transparency in supply chain human rights policies and practices.
Adient supports women-, minority- and veteran-owned businesses by spending more than $1 billion with diverse suppliers every year.
Adient's Executive Vice President, Chief Legal and Human Resources Officer, and Corporate Secretary oversees global talent processes to attract, develop and retain employees.
As of September 30, 2023, Adient's workforce composition consists of approximately 45% working in the Americas, 42% in EMEA and 13% in Asia-Pacific.
Approximately 41% of the global workforce is female and 49% of employees in the U.S. have identified themselves as an ethnic minority.
Adient maintains a health and safety management system certified to the ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety standard.
Globally, 100% of Adient’s facilities are internally audited and compliant, and 60% are also third-party audited and certified.
Adient President and CEO Doug Del Grosso signed the "CEO Action for Diversity & Inclusion" pledge.
Adient is a member of the Center for Automotive Diversity, Inclusion and Advancement (CADIA).
In fiscal 2022, Adient published a Human Rights Policy Statement and a Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DE&I) Commitment Statement.
source_title: SEC-Show

facts: Zaheer Faruqi launched Axis Aviation, a business providing spare aircraft parts, from his two-car garage in Peachtree City on September 1, 2001.
The American aviation industry halted following the September 11 terrorist attacks, creating immediate hurdles for the new U.S.-based business without a domestic customer base.
In late 2002, Faruqi partnered with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) to guide his international expansion.
Aventure Aviation now operates locations in China, Pakistan, Turkey, England, Canada, and the U.S.
Faruqi earned degrees in administration management and aircraft maintenance engineering from Northrop University in Inglewood, California, and holds an FAA airframe and power plant license.
Before founding his company, Faruqi worked for Avatar Alliance, a now-defunct aviation parts company in Fairburn, Georgia.
The company's vision was to act as the "AutoZone of aviation" by sourcing and sending spare parts to keep commercial aircraft flying.
Faruqi signed his first consignment deal in September 2003.
Following then-President Georgia W. Bush's "Axis of Evil" remark, Faruqi rebranded Axis Aviation to Aventure Aviation.
GDEcD facilitated an international trade trip to Toronto and Montreal that led to a deal with Air Canada Jazz, which became the company's best international customer.
Aventure opened its first international office in Toronto in 2005 to service its growing customer base.
Sixty percent of Aventure Aviation's revenue now comes from international customers.
A GDEcD representative suggested expanding to Beijing, provided translated Mandarin business cards and brochures, arranged an interpreter, and set up meetings.
Taube Ponce, GDEcD’s senior international trade manager, described Aventure as a "model client" for smartly using free economic development resources.
In 2003, the company moved from its garage to a shared space six miles away in Peachtree City, with Georgia company Hipage handling shipping and receiving.
Approximately five years later, Aventure constructed a new global headquarters featuring a 16,000-square-foot purpose-built facility with office and warehouse space near Atlanta.
The company established a military division in 2010 to open new markets.
In 2014, Aventure acquired its first aircraft, a Boeing 767, which it dismantled to harvest and sell parts.
Due to airline cost-sensitivity, the business model shifted from accepting consignment parts to purchasing retired airplanes directly.
In June 2025, Aventure Aviation completed its largest aircraft deal by purchasing 12 Boeing 737NGs from Alaska Airlines.
Six weeks after the June 2025 purchase, the company opened a 70,000-square-foot warehouse as the first phase of its new headquarters in Peachtree City.
Construction of a second phase, including a 54,000-square-foot office and an additional 36,000 square feet of warehouse space, is slated to begin later this year.
Aventure Aviation currently employs 70 people worldwide, with 31 based internationally.
The company received the 2025 Exporter of the Year award for Georgia and the Southeast Region from the Small Business Administration.
Aventure was honored with a 2022 Exporter of the Year Award from the Export-Import Bank of the United States.
The company achieved the 2021 U.S. President’s “E” Award for Export Excellence.
Faruqi completed Aventure's first corporate acquisition of Atlanta Aviation International, an FAA-certified repair station based in Atlanta.
Faruqi's three sons, Talha, Hamzah, and Osman, are owners of the company.
source_title: How Aventure Aviation Became a Global Aviation Brand

facts: Archer Aviation's Midnight eVTOL air taxi is set to be manufactured in Covington by carmaker Stellantis (merger of Fiat Chrysler and Peugeot).
source_title: Covington, Georgia - Wikipedia

facts: Archer Aviation has begun construction on its Georgia-based manufacturing facility.
The company closed the local incentive transaction for the facility.
The planned facility is approximately 350,000 sqft.
The facility is located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
The facility will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
Archer stated this type of high-volume manufacturing facility is believed to be a first for the eVTOL industry.
The factory can be expanded by an additional 800,000 sqft.
The expansion could support long-term production targets of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer recently announced it had expanded its relationship with automaker Stellantis, which owns brands like Jeep, Ram and Maserati.
Under the partnership, Archer and Stellantis will join forces to manufacture Archer’s production aircraft, Midnight.
Adam Goldstein serves as Archer’s founder and CEO.
Goldstein stated they are working closely with Stellantis to ramp up high-volume manufacturing capabilities at the new Covington, GA facility.
Goldstein emphasized that scale is key to unlocking the promise of what the eVTOL aircraft industry can bring.
Goldstein highlighted the support provided by the State of Georgia and Newton County to this first of its kind facility in a new industry.
Stellantis will contribute advanced manufacturing technology, expertise, personnel and capital to Archer.
The partnership is intended to enable the rapid scaling of aircraft production.
The partnership allows Archer to strengthen its path to commercialization by avoiding millions of dollars of spending during the ramp up phase.
Stellantis is planned to mass produce Archer’s eVTOL aircraft as its exclusive contract manufacturer.
source_title: Archer begins construction on Covington manufacturing facility | Revolution.aero

facts: Archer Aviation is an eVTOL air taxi company that has been compared by short seller Grizzly Research to troubled truck start-up Nikola, being called the “Nikola of the Skies.”
Grizzly Research issued a report questioning Archer's future, citing weak orders, a quiet factory, and reliance on publicity rather than progress.
Archer values its order book at $6 billion, but Grizzly claims many of the orders are linked to partners with weak financials or limited aviation experience.
An order tied to Air Chateau is considered unlikely to be real because the group has only ever bought one helicopter in the past.
Another order tied to Future Flight Global comes from a small firm with no track record.
A deal with KakaoMobility in Korea has already fallen apart but still shows up in Archer's order pipeline.
Archer often highlights its work with the U.S. Air Force, yet only a small part of the full contract has been awarded so far.
Grizzly argues that Archer’s Midnight aircraft is flawed and unlikely to be cleared for flight by regulators due to design hurdles that cannot be fixed with minor changes.
Archer maintains that certification for its Midnight aircraft is on track, contrasting with Grizzly's claims.
Grizzly says that Archer’s Covington, Georgia factory shows little sign of mass production.
Investigators visited the Covington, Georgia site over the summer and reported empty lots and no sign of scaled output.
Archer has stated that its Covington, Georgia site can support up to 650 aircraft per year.
Grizzly argues that Archer's target of 650 aircraft per year at the Georgia facility is unrealistic.
The report claims that Archer leans heavily on events and photos to shape its story rather than demonstrating progress.
One example cited is a “Launch Edition” in Abu Dhabi where the aircraft shown was not new but a reused unit meant for display.
Grizzly states that Archer has even “delivered” the same aircraft more than once, raising questions about its disclosure practices.
Rival Joby Aviation (JOBY) is making faster gains across certification and commercial preparation compared to Archer’s reliance on future promises.
Archer continues to promote its path toward both urban flight and defense applications.
Grizzly frames Archer’s move into defense as a late attempt to shift focus, but notes investors will have to decide if that pivot has real depth.
ACHR shares have dropped 11% year-to-date and more than 8% in the last five trading sessions.
Wall Street analysts maintain a “Strong Buy” consensus on Archer Aviation based on seven recent ratings.
The average 12-month price target for Archer Aviation is $13.14, implying a 52.44% upside from the current price.
source_title: Grizzly Research Says Archer Aviation Is “Nikola of the Skies,” Questions $6 Billion Order Book - TipRanks.com

facts: Stellantis and Archer Aviation Inc., a leader in electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, have announced an additional $55 million investment from Stellantis.
The investment is made under the companies’ strategic funding agreement following Archer’s recent transition flight test milestone achievement.
This latest investment builds on Stellantis’ previous purchase of 8.3 million shares of Archer’s stock in March.
Throughout 2023, Stellantis has invested $110 million in Archer through a combination of stock purchases and investments under their strategic funding agreement.
Archer remains on track to complete the construction of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Georgia later this year.
The first phase of the build-out includes a 350,000-square-foot facility on a 100-acre site.
This facility is designed to support the production of up to 650 aircraft annually, potentially making it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aircraft industry.
Archer aims to leverage Stellantis’ expertise as its contract manufacturer to support its planned commercial ramp-up.

Carlos Tavares, Stellantis CEO, expressed his enthusiasm for the partnership: “Few things rival the excitement of seeing a dream take flight. I applaud the innovation, expertise, and hard work of the engineering and manufacturing teams from Stellantis and Archer. With this additional investment in Archer, we remain on course for a future where freedom of mobility extends beyond today’s roads.”

Archer CEO Adam Goldstein highlighted the importance of Stellantis’ support: “The commitment by Stellantis to Archer has been unrivaled, from its foresight to provide the manufacturing expertise and capital needed to accelerate Archer’s business objectives, to the strategic vision and steadfast support from Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares. Together, we’re working to redefine urban transportation, opening new worlds of opportunity for citizens across the globe by providing more efficient access to people, places, and events across the regions they live in.”
Stellantis has been a strategic partner to Archer since 2020 through various collaboration initiatives and as an investor since 2021.
Archer has leveraged Stellantis’ manufacturing, supply chain, and design expertise in its efforts to design, develop, and commercialize its eVTOL aircraft.

Archer’s goal is to transform urban travel by replacing 60-90-minute car commutes with estimated 10-20-minute electric air taxi flights that are safe, sustainable, low-noise, and cost-competitive with ground transportation.
Archer’s Midnight is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
This investment news comes at a controversial time, as Stellantis continues to downsize its North American automotive operations.
Carlos Tavares has repeatedly stated that he views Stellantis as not an automaker but a mobility company.
Stellantis has been clear about its intention to cut an additional $200 million from its North American operations, with more layoffs rumored to be coming later this month.
source_title: Stellantis Invest An Additional $55 Million Into Archer Aviation - MoparInsiders

facts: Archer Aviation has successfully completed several flight tests of its flagship Midnight eVTOL aircraft.
Archer has obtained certification from the Federal Aviation Administration to begin operating a commercial airline.
On July 2, Archer announced that Chrysler and Fiat parent Stellantis (NYSE:STLA), a key strategic investor, has invested an additional $55 million into the company.
Stellantis previously invested $110 million during a funding round last year.
On July 12, Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) signed a memorandum of understanding with Archer Aviation.
Archer already has such a partnership with United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), another major U.S. carrier.
A recent press release indicates that Archer's flagship manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia is nearing completion.
source_title: Pounce on Archer Aviation Stock Pullback as the Shorts Pile In | InvestorPlace

facts: Archer Aviation unveiled a proposed air taxi network for New York City in partnership with United Airlines that would allow passengers to add an Archer ride to traditional airline tickets.
The proposed NYC network will feature nine core nodes connecting Manhattan to JFK, LaGuardia, Newark, Downtown Skyport, east and west side heliports, Teterboro, Westchester, and Long Island Republic.
Archer's aircraft is a five-seat electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) vehicle named Midnight.
The company is waiting on Federal Aviation Administration approval for the Midnight aircraft before it can begin testing planned routes.
Archer has only flown the Midnight autonomously without humans inside, whereas competitors Joby Aviation and Beta Technologies have both conducted piloted flights.
CEO Adam Goldstein expressed hope that Archer will achieve necessary FAA certifications in 2026 and plans to provide an update on piloted flights at its next earnings call.
Archer went public in 2021 via a special-purpose acquisition merger and has raised $3.36 billion to date through public and private fundraises.
United Airlines will assist with aircraft storage, maintenance, charging setup, and vertiport construction at airports for the NYC network.
Archer partnered with fixed-base operators Atlantic Aviation, Signature, and Modern Aviation to operate Manhattan helipads, provide passenger access, and help establish charging infrastructure.

CCO Nikhil Goel stated that New York is the No. 1 helicopter commuting city in the world outside of São Paulo, making it compelling for replacing helicopters with Archer aircraft due to existing routes, familiar air traffic patterns, and available FBO/landing facilities.
Archer plans to initially deploy up to five aircraft to NYC and other cities to practice running routes before a full launch.
The company's long-term goal is to operate hundreds of aircraft across several cities over the next 10 to 20 years.
Archer began constructing Midnight aircraft at its production facility in Georgia last month.
The Georgia production facility was built in partnership with strategic investor Stellantis.
Archer plans to build 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Archer also plans to launch air taxi services in San Francisco and Miami, though timelines remain dependent on FAA certification.
The company has not yet selected its first U.S. launch city.
Archer plans to launch an air taxi service in Abu Dhabi later this year due to less strict regulations there.
source_title: Archer Aviation wants to help New Yorkers skip airport traffic with electric air taxis | TechCrunch

facts: The multi-rotor electric vertical take-off and landing vehicle was first announced in 2021.
Commercial launch is on track for this year, contingent on FAA approval.
Archer will serve as the official air taxi provider of the Olympic Games and Team USA at LA 2028.
The long fuselage has room for up to four passengers, their carry-on luggage, and a pilot.
It is powered by six independent proprietary battery packs, each supporting a pair of Archer custom electric engines.
The battery packs feature proven safe and reliable lithium-ion cells.
The battery system can be charged in around 10–12 minutes between flights.
The aircraft is designed to cruise at 240 km/h (150 mph).
It has a range of 160 km (100 miles).
It operates at approximately 100 times quieter than conventional helicopters.
Operations require dedicated airports for eVTOL aircraft called vertiports.
These vertiports would also serve as charging stations.
The aircraft is aimed at fleet operators such as air taxi services, corporate fleets, and government programs rather than individual buyers.
The latest catalog price is US$5 million.
source_title: Archer Midnight eVTOL Air Taxi Could Replace Helicopters for 20–50 Mile City Flights -

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) unveiled a multi-pronged update tied to its third-quarter results, including a $650 million equity raise and a deal to acquire control of Los Angeles’ Hawthorne Airport for $126 million.
The company signed definitive agreements to gain control of the 80-acre Hawthorne Airport (Jack Northrop Field), located just under three miles from LAX, for $126 million.
Archer plans to use Hawthorne Airport to anchor its Los Angeles air-taxi network and serve as an AI operations testbed for AI-powered traffic and ground-ops management.
The airport acquisition transaction remains subject to closing conditions and city approval.
Archer will assume the master ground lease for the facility rather than buying the land outright, with the lease running through 2055.
The company has listed additional capital projects at the site as part of an “airport of the future” build-out.
Archer filed a 424B5 prospectus supplement for 81,250,000 new shares priced at $8.00 per share, generating gross proceeds of $650 million.
Following the issuance, total Class A shares outstanding will be approximately 732.5 million.
A separate prospectus covers up to approximately $27.6 million of additional shares to settle a contractual payment in stock.
Archer reported a Q3 2025 GAAP net loss of $129.9 million and an adjusted EBITDA loss of $116.1 million.
The company held $1.64 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments at the end of the quarter.
Archer guided for a Q4 adjusted EBITDA loss between $110 million and $140 million.
Its Midnight eVTOL completed a 55-mile piloted flight lasting 31 minutes at speeds exceeding 126 mph.
The Midnight aircraft also successfully completed a 10,000-foot altitude flight.
Archer closed its acquisition of the Lilium patent portfolio, bringing its global IP assets to over 1,000.
Hawthorne Airport's proximity to LAX, SoFi Stadium, the Intuit Dome, The Forum, and Downtown LA positions it as a strategic hub for short-hop urban missions ahead of the LA28 Olympic Games.
Industry coverage indicates the airport will serve as the central node of Archer’s LA network and a staging area for 2028 operations, pending approvals.
Archer cites lease renewal risk and capital expenditure obligations to transform the site as part of its master lease assumption.
The company flagged execution and certification risks in its prospectus filings.
Archer states it is largely through Phase 3 of the FAA type-certification program and is now focusing on Phase 4 implementation and formal TIA testing.
The company estimates receiving approximately 15% of compliance verification documents for the final certification phase.
Archer holds a Part 135 air carrier certificate.
The company is currently producing six Midnight aircraft across U.S. facilities for testing, certification, and non-U.S. launch-edition programs.
Archer highlighted test and demonstration flights in the UAE alongside new airline partnerships with Korean Air and a JAL/Soracle JV in Japan, though some agreements remain conditional.
JPMorgan cut its price target for ACHR to $8, assigning a Neutral rating following the Q3 results.
Needham reiterated a Buy rating but lowered its price target to $10.
ARK Invest disclosed purchasing over 3 million ACHR shares on November 6 across multiple funds.
Post-raise liquidity stands at greater than $2 billion, according to company statements.
The Lilium patent acquisition added approximately 300 assets to Archer's intellectual property portfolio.

Archer continues developing a Los Angeles network blueprint with ties to the LA28 Olympics and maintains partnerships with airlines like United Airlines to integrate urban air mobility into existing networks.
source_title: Archer Aviation (ACHR) Raises $650M, Moves to Buy Hawthorne Airport for $126M; Shares Slip After Q3 Update — Nov. 7, 2025

facts: Archer Aviation provided an update on the construction progress of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, GA, reaffirming an expected 2024 completion date.
In preparation for the facility’s completion, Archer showcased its Midnight aircraft at Atlantic Aviation’s new terminal at DeKalb–Peachtree Municipal Airport.

Guests at the showcase included government officials from Georgia’s Office of the Governor, Georgia’s Congressional Offices, the City of Atlanta, Georgia’s State Legislature, the Federal Aviation Administration, and other local governments.
Archer completed the first build phase earlier this year, inclusive of grading and foundation work.
The facility is nearing completion of phase two, which involves the erection of the exterior walls and roof.
Phase three (interior fit out) and phase four (equipment load in and power on) are on track to be completed in the coming months.
Once complete, the facility will be nearly 400,000 square feet.
The facility will support Archer’s planned production of up to 650 Midnight aircraft annually.
This production volume would make it one of the largest manufacturing facilities by volume in the aviation industry.
Archer’s buildout is taking place in close partnership with Stellantis.
Archer and Stellantis are continuing to progress their plans to mass-produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft together.

Adam Goldstein, CEO and Founder of Archer, stated that thanks to the hard work of the Archer and Stellantis teams, they are tracking towards completing this manufacturing facility by the end of the year.
Once complete, Archer will focus on ramping production up to 650 aircraft a year.
Archer aims to be the first in the eVTOL industry to achieve scaled manufacturing.
Midnight is Archer’s electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
The Midnight aircraft is built around Archer’s proprietary electric powertrain.
The Midnight is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation.
The Midnight travels at speeds up to 150mph, turning hour-long ground commutes into minutes in the air.
The Midnight is designed for back-to-back flights of 20-50 miles with minimal charge time in between.
The Midnight is 100x quieter than a helicopter at cruising altitudes, nearly inaudible from the streets below.
The Midnight features redundant systems across the aircraft allowing Archer to target similar levels of safety as commercial airliners.
The Midnight is designed to carry a pilot, up to four passengers, and carry-on luggage.
source_title: Archer Nears Completion of eVTOL Manufacturing Facility - Advanced Air Mobility Intl

facts: Hawthorne City Council voted 5–0 on Nov. 12 to approve consent on the master lease assignment for Hawthorne Municipal Airport, clearing the way for Archer Aviation Inc. to take operational control.
The $126 million agreement is a pivotal step in Archer's plans to establish an electric air taxi network across Los Angeles and the surrounding region.
Planned routes will connect key destinations including LAX, VNY, BUR airports, Hollywood, Orange County, Long Beach, SoFi Stadium, Rams (Woodland Hills), and Santa Monica.
The 80-acre city-owned airport, known as Jack Northrop Field, will support Archer’s role as the official air taxi provider for the 2028 Olympics announced by LA28 in May.
By assuming the remaining 30 years of a master lease held by Hawthorne Airport LLC (HAL), along with its subleases, Archer will control nearly all non-runway land through 2055.

The lease setup previously prompted monopoly-related litigation addressed by the 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in an Aug. 4, 2022 memorandum, which dismissed the case on procedural grounds for being filed after the statute of limitations.
SEC filings show Archer has signed agreements with HAL, 395 Park Place LLC (“395”), and Advanced Air LLC, linking the transaction to new hangar construction already under environmental review.
A Mitigated Negative Declaration (MND) prepared by consultant Watearth lists 395 as developer of 13 hangars in a document dated Sept. 19, 2025 describing the Hawthorne Airport Hangars Project.
The city posted its public notice for the hangar project on Nov. 5, setting a Dec. 9 hearing date for possible MND adoption without identifying the applicant.
Local community group Hawthorne Quiet Skies expressed frustration with the lack of transparency, stating the acquisition was never mentioned at recent Airport Communities Network meetings.
City officials did not respond to multiple phone and email requests for comment, and Archer also did not reply before publication.
The proposed project represents only the first phase of airport work, separate from a larger redevelopment described in Archer’s Q3 2025 shareholder letter.
CEQA documents note geotechnical investigations by Bay City Geology in August 2024 for the narrow-scope 13-hangar project.

Hawthorne Quiet Skies warned of potential environmental impacts from what it called the “first ever eVTOL hub,” stating residents are entering this new era unprepared with no clear pathway to success.
Concerns were raised during LAX/Community Noise Roundtable's Nov. 12 meeting, where Chair Denny Schneider suggested gathering more information for a future agenda on shared South Bay airspace.
In April, Watearth initiated consultation with relevant tribes under Assembly Bill 52 as part of the CEQA process for the proposed hangars.

Hereditary Chief Andrew Salas of the Gabrieleño Band of Mission Indians – Kizh Nation responded in letters dated July 8 and July 17, 2025, warning of a high likelihood that prehistoric cultural resources, including burials, remain beneath portions of the airport.
Salas stated that any excavation, grading, or trenching near the airport carries a high likelihood of intersecting unrecorded tribal cultural resources protected under CEQA.
The MND incorporates several of the tribe’s recommendations, including a Kizh Nation approved monitor during ground-disturbing work and protocols for handling inadvertent discoveries.

Kizh leaders disputed the city's characterization of the consultation in the weeks leading up to the December vote, with Salas claiming key recommendations were brushed aside and interaction treated as a formality.
Archer must navigate federal aviation regulations and certification requirements beyond environmental and cultural reviews.
The deal positions Hawthorne to become a proving ground for electric air taxis.
Archer’s CEO Adam Goldstein told investors during the company’s Q3 2025 call that proving electric air taxis work in Los Angeles would allow scaling the product across the U.S. and the world.
A Georgia Institute of Technology press release on Oct. 17, 2025, cited hurdles in certification, air-traffic integration, and public acceptance for scaling air mobility.

Dr. Laurie Garrow, a Georgia Tech professor who co-directs the Center for Urban and Regional Air Mobility, stated that safe corridors need to be defined for aircraft to operate alongside traditional air traffic and that aviation certification is a rigorous process requiring time to earn public trust.

On Oct. 23, the National Transportation Safety Board posted on X that an unmanned Air Vev Inc. Zero eVTOL crashed in a field and was consumed by fire, raising safety concerns for nearby residential areas.
Archer’s shareholder letter explicitly stated plans for the airport to serve as a testbed for AI-powered aviation.

At the Nov. 15 meeting, Archer representative Eric Lentell clarified that FAA restrictions limit test flight locations and that testing is conducted at a remote site in Salinas, California, not Hawthorne.
Hawthorne Quiet Skies expressed support for California innovators but emphasized the need for collaboration and proposed establishing a new Hawthorne Airport noise working group led by Archer.
Under the agreement terms, Archer and the sellers can walk away if closing is not finalized by December 30, 2025.
source_title: iSpyVNY - Archer Aviation Takes Control of Hawthorne Airport: Buzz, Backlash & Battle Ahead

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced plans on November 14, 2022, to locate its manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia, at a site adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
The selected site is located in Newton County.
Archer plans to initially build a 350,000 square-foot facility on a 96-acre site.
The initial facility will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
Construction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024, with initial production expected to begin in the second half of 2024.
The facility can be expanded by an additional 550,000 square feet.
The expansion is estimated to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer intends to invest $118 million over the next 10 years.
Archer will receive an incentive package totaling 33% of its capital investment commitment.
The incentive package includes land conveyance, tax incentives, and a Georgia REBA grant.
Archer has entered into a letter of intent with Synovus Bank to obtain financing for the project.
Synovus Bank is the largest bank headquartered in Georgia.
Archer completed a comprehensive survey of potential manufacturing sites across the United States before selecting Covington.
Key site selection considerations included local labor market talent availability, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics.
The planned eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs over the long term.
Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated that Georgia was selected after many months of discussion and evaluation.
Adam Goldstein noted that Georgia has played a vital role in the U.S. aerospace industry.
Archer's eVTOL technology is intended to transform how urban and rural communities live and commute.
The factory is expected to create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Adam Goldstein expressed gratitude to Governor Brian Kemp, Bob Kosek, Sara Gershon, Serra Hall, Frank Turner, and their teams for their support.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that nurturing innovation is a state priority as they work with businesses to define the future.
Governor Kemp noted that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Governor Kemp highlighted that Archer will utilize Georgia's Quick Start workforce training program to help fill over 1,000 long-term jobs in Covington.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies.
The state has a dozen high schools offering aerospace training, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.
The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, has supported innovative aerospace companies for over 10 years.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer's corporate team is based in Santa Clara, California.
source_title: Archer

facts: The State of Georgia maintains full-time representation in a dozen international markets.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development and its economic development partners capitalized on the 2024 SelectUSA Investment Summit to demonstrate why Georgia stands out to international companies seeking to locate or expand in the U.S.
Georgia has emerged as a leader in the e-mobility sector, with more than $27.3 billion in investments since 2018.
The e-mobility sector has created over 32,200 new jobs.
Major Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) making significant investments in Georgia include Kia, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), Rivian, and Archer Aviation.
Battery manufacturers at the forefront of battery innovation in Georgia include SK Battery America and LG Energy Solution.
Recyclers and fuel cell manufacturers advancing recycling and hydrogen fuel technologies in Georgia include Aurubis AG, Ascend Elements, and Plug Power.
Georgia’s traditional automotive sector exports reached $4.1 billion in 2023.
The Georgia Ports Authority’s Brunswick facility is the second-busiest automotive Roll-on/Roll-off port in the U.S.
Georgia’s strong foundations in mobility are anchored by its aerospace and automotive sectors.
The state’s workforce pipeline for these industries is supported by institutions like the University System of Georgia and the Technical College System of Georgia.
The Georgia Center of Innovation supports these sectors with industry-specific expertise and strategic partnerships.
For over a decade, Georgia has established itself as a prime location for global business growth, offering diverse opportunities across key industries.
Georgia’s e-mobility sector has attracted over $27.3 billion in investments and created 32,200 jobs.
With a strong infrastructure, skilled workforce, and supportive business environment, Georgia provides an ideal setting for companies to thrive.

facts: US operations for the Midnight aircraft are expected to begin this year under the White House’s eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (eIPP) and in preparation for the LA28 Olympic Games.

Archer expects Midnight operations in American cities to begin this year as part of the White House’s eIPP and in preparation to serve as the Official Air Taxi Provider of the LA28 Olympic Games, in coordination with the DOT and FAA.
Archer was recently selected as an air taxi partner in 3 winning applications encompassing 8 states, including Florida, Texas, and New York.
In April, Archer became the first eVTOL company to close Phase 3 of the FAA’s 4-phase Type Certification process for the Midnight aircraft.
Archer has been driving progress in Phase 4 of the FAA certification process, where Midnight's compliance with FAA airworthiness requirements will be demonstrated through formal testing and analysis.
Archer’s flight test program expanded this quarter, with piloted VTOL and CTOL flights across its expanded fleet occurring nearly every day, often multiple times a day.
Archer took over operations of Hawthorne Airport in LA to develop the site into an innovation hub for next-generation aviation technologies and anchor planned LA air taxi operations.
Hawthorne Airport is located near LAX and several major LA sports and entertainment venues.
Archer continues work alongside Anduril on a dual-use, hybrid, autonomous aircraft, aiming to deliver one of the most sophisticated vertical lift platforms in this category.
Archer anticipates beginning to win phased government awards for its dual-use aircraft program this year.
NVIDIA is integrating its IGX Thor platform to power safety-capable onboard compute and autonomy-ready flight systems for Archer's AI stack.
Starlink will deliver high-speed, low-latency LEO satellite connectivity for Midnight aircraft.
Palantir was downselected as a finalist for the FAA's SMART AI project, which is instrumental to the DOT's ~$20B air traffic control modernization effort.
Archer ended Q1 2026 with $1,775.9 million of cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments on its balance sheet and an additional $7.3 million of restricted cash.

Q1 2026 cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments decreased by $188.8 million from Q4 2025, primarily due to $149.1 million used in operating activities and $32.6 million used for property and equipment purchases.
Q1 2026 Revenue increased by $1.3 million from Q4 2025 to $1.6 million as operations expanded at the Hawthorne Airport in LA.

Q1 2026 Operating Expenses increased by $21.5 million from Q4 2025, driven by continued investment in expanding flight testing, certification efforts, and production activities for the Midnight aircraft, along with design and development efforts for the hybrid aircraft.

Q1 2026 Net Loss increased by $28.8 million from Q4 2025, primarily driven by the $21.5 million increase in operating expenses, a $7.3 million decrease in non-cash gain within other income (expense), net, and a $1.2 million decrease in interest income, net, offset by a $1.3 million increase in revenue generated.
Q1 2026 Adjusted EBITDA was a loss of $172.5 million, which falls within the guidance range of $160 million to $180 million.
Archer’s financial estimates for Q2 2026 project an Adjusted EBITDA loss of $170 million to $200 million.
Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated that defense and AI software efforts are advancing quickly, opening up a bigger future for the company beyond air taxis.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc.'s facility in Covington received a certificate of occupancy last week, signaling completion of construction for its high-volume manufacturing facility adjacent to Covington Municipal Airport.
The company held a commemorative ribbon-cutting ceremony Tuesday for the approximately 400,000-square-foot facility, known as ARC.
The ARC facility will manufacture Archer's Midnight eVTOL (electric vertical take-off and landing) aircraft.
Archer is currently working to complete the tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line.
Production at the facility is expected to begin in early 2025.
Archer aims to ramp up production to two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
Archer is finalizing an agreement with partner Stellantis, targeting a manufacturing capacity of 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, and experienced personnel to the project.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested cities around the world.
Archer has invested more than $118 million in the manufacturing facility located on 96 acres at Covington Municipal Airport.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp praised Archer Aviation's decision to locate its manufacturing facility in Georgia.

Governor Kemp stated that the announcement marks the next step in a journey reaffirming Georgia's commitment to working with innovative companies to create quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.

Congressman Mike Collins of Georgia, who serves on the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, highlighted Georgia's leadership in innovation, advanced manufacturing, and job creation due to the facility.
Congressman Collins added that the project will drive progress, deliver economic benefits for Georgia, and create opportunity for everyone involved with and around the facility.
Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated that the facility's completion is a testament to the industry shifting from R&D into commercialization.
Goldstein noted that the team is focused on the start of production planned for early next year, followed by execution and scaling.
Goldstein expressed pride in the Archer team and partners for completing construction fast and on budget.

Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer Arnaud Deboeuf stated that supporting Archer's electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.
Deboeuf congratulated the Archer team on the milestone, calling it a significant step in taking clean mobility to the sky.
Archer's goal is to transform urban travel by replacing 60-90 minute car commutes with estimated 10-20 minute electric aircraft flights.
The Midnight aircraft is designed to be safe, sustainable, low noise, and cost competitive with ground transportation.
The Midnight is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
The aircraft travels at speeds up to 150 mph, turning hour-long ground commutes into minutes in the air.
The aircraft is designed for back-to-back flights of 20-50 miles with minimal charge time in between.
The aircraft operates at 100x quieter than a helicopter at cruising altitudes, making it nearly inaudible from the streets below.
The aircraft features redundant systems allowing Archer to target similar levels of safety as commercial airliners.
The aircraft is designed to carry a pilot, up to four passengers, and carry-on luggage.
