facts: Robert Bosch GmbH (“Bosch”) is a German engineering and technology group headquartered in Gerlingen, near Stuttgart.

Founded in 1886, Bosch has grown into a global supplier of technology and services spanning mobility (automotive and software-driven mobility), industrial technology, consumer goods, and energy/building technology.
Bosch is privately held and reports figures across multiple pages and reports.
Bosch reported €90.3 billion in sales revenue in 2024 with €3.1 billion EBIT from operations.
Bosch employed 417,859 associates worldwide as of Dec. 31, 2024.
For 2025 preliminary figures, Bosch lists €91.0 billion in sales revenue, €1.7 billion EBIT from operations, and 412,400 associates worldwide as of Dec. 31, 2025.
In 2024, Bosch reported €7.8 billion in R&D spending, with 86,840 R&D associates across 136 locations.
Bosch reports having subsidiaries and regional companies in some 60 countries (preliminary as of Dec. 31, 2025).
Bosch reports 490 subsidiaries and regional companies (preliminary as of Dec. 31, 2025).
source_title: Bosch Statistics (2026): Revenue, Employees, R&D Spend, Subsidiaries & Key Facts

facts: Growth markets such as electromobility developed much more slowly than expected, adversely affecting Bosch Group’s business development.
A lack of sales in electromobility areas and accompanying underutilized capacity negatively impacted results.
Continued high upfront expenditure for future technologies and provisions for necessary strategic adjustments had a negative impact on result.
By 2030, the company aims to be among the leading suppliers in its areas of business in key markets.
The company is striving for average annual growth of between 6 and 8 percent, with a margin of at least 7 percent.
Bosch systematically implemented its Strategy 2030 and reached important milestones in the past fiscal year.
Bosch plans to acquire the HVAC business of Johnson Controls and Hitachi for around 8 billion U.S. dollars to expand presence in growth markets such as the U.S. and Asia.
Electromobility, hydrogen, and sustainable technologies remain a growth business and the focus of Bosch's innovations.
Bosch is currently testing a cryogenic pump in the U.S. that compresses up to 600 kilograms of liquid hydrogen per hour.
The tested cryogenic pump will allow trucks to fill up with enough hydrogen for the next 1,000 kilometers in just 10 minutes.
Bosch expects to generate sales of more than 6 billion euros with software and services by the beginning of the next decade, two-thirds of this in the Mobility business sector.
AI has been playing an important role in assisted and automated driving at Bosch for years.
Bosch developed a system solution called Vehicle Motion Management that enables brake-by-wire braking systems, in which an electronic brake pedal operates without a mechanical connection.
Bosch aims to achieve its target margin of 7 percent by 2026.
The Mobility business sector reported sales revenue of 55.9 billion euros, roughly reaching the previous year's level despite a declining market.
North America sales revenue grew by 5 percent to 16 billion euros.
As of December 31, 2024, the Bosch Group employed approximately 417,900 associates worldwide, nearly 3 percent lower than the previous year.
In Germany, headcount fell by around 3 percent to just over 129,800.
source_title: The 2024 business year: Bosch held back by market developments - Bosch Media Service

facts: Bridgestone operates 30+ manufacturing facilities across the Americas in the United States.
The company employs Operations Technicians, Engineers, Maintenance Technicians, Analysts, Researchers, and other roles across its U.S. facilities.
The Bandag plant in Griffin, Georgia has been producing tread rubber and retreading materials since 1976.
Tread rubber and retreading materials from the Griffin, Georgia plant are shipped to Bandag retread dealers for end-user tire production.
The Firestone Airide plant and distribution center in Williamsburg, Kentucky produces and distributes air springs for automotive, heavy-duty truck and trailer, and seat/cab spring applications.
The Williamsburg, Kentucky location recently underwent a major automotive expansion to meet growing demand for advanced air suspension technology in electric vehicles.
A distribution center expansion at the Williamsburg, Kentucky location was implemented to enhance logistics operations and provide scalability for future growth.
The major investment in production and logistics capacity at the Williamsburg, Kentucky facility increases the workforce by 250 employees.
The Firestone Airide plant and distribution center in Dyersburg, Tennessee produces stamped metal plates for air suspension systems and heavy-duty products.
The Dyersburg, Tennessee facility finishes and crimps air springs for automotive aftermarket (Ride-Rite), heavy duty, and off-highway applications.
The Dyersburg, Tennessee location recently completed a major 80,000 square foot expansion to meet growing demand for advanced air suspension technology.
A distribution center expansion at the Dyersburg, Tennessee facility was implemented to enhance logistics operations and provide scalability for future growth.
The investment in production and logistics capacity at the Dyersburg, Tennessee facility increases the workforce by approximately 40 teammates.
In 2023, the Dyersburg, Tennessee plant received recertification for the Volunteer STAR award, which is Tennessee’s highest honor for workplace safety and health.
The Passenger/Light Truck Radial Tire plant in Graniteville, South Carolina (Aiken County) opened in 1998.
The Graniteville, South Carolina facility spans 2.7 million square feet and employs more than 1,700 teammates as Maintenance Technicians, Mechanical Engineers, Business Analysts, and other roles.
The Wilson, North Carolina manufacturing facility produces passenger radial, light truck radial tread, and run flat tires.
The Wilson, North Carolina facility spans 2.67 million square feet and employs more than 2,000 teammates.
The Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations plant in Warren County, Tennessee produces truck and bus radial tires, including premium steer and wide base models.

The Warren County, Tennessee facility spans nearly 2 million square feet and employs more than 1,000 teammates as Operating Technicians, Safety and Health Managers, Electrical Engineers, and Production Analysts.
Production at the Trenton, South Carolina Aiken County Off-Road Tire Plant began in 2013.
The Trenton, South Carolina facility spans 1.5 million square feet and manufactures large off-the-road radial tires for mining and aggregate customers.
Firestone Fibers & Textiles has owned and operated the Kings Mountain, North Carolina plant since 1992.
The Kings Mountain, North Carolina plant produces tire cord and industrial fabric for Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations and Firestone Airide, as well as for external customers.
The Bandag plant in Oxford, North Carolina has been in operation for 50 years producing tread rubber and retreading materials for Bandag dealers.
The Oxford, North Carolina plant location has received numerous safety awards and certifications since its groundbreaking in 1967.
The Gastonia, North Carolina plant has been owned and operated by Firestone Fibers & Textiles since 1935.
The Gastonia, North Carolina plant produces reinforcement materials for Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations and Firestone Industrial Products, as well as for external customers.
source_title: Bridgestone Manufacturing | BeBridgestone

facts: BATO conducts tire research and develops, manufactures and markets tires for a wide variety of things that move on wheels.
Offers a wide range of Bridgestone, Firestone and associate brand tires, maintaining wholesale and original equipment sales operations across a broad line of products.
Product lines include passenger, light truck, commercial truck and bus, agricultural, motorcycle, kart and off-the-road tires.

Through its Bridgestone Bandag Tire Solutions unit, retread customers have access to industry-leading research and development, manufacturing, marketing and sales expertise, providing them with a total tire solution.
The BATO companies employ approximately 24,000 employees throughout North America.

Within the BATO business unit are seven North American tire manufacturing facilities located in Aiken County, S.C.; Akron, Ohio; Bloomington/Normal, Ill.; Des Moines, Iowa; LaVergne, Tenn.; Warren County, Tenn.; and Wilson, N.C.

BATO includes international manufacturing and sales business units located in Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Mexico and Venezuela, with additional sales representation throughout Latin America and the Caribbean.
Sister company Bridgestone Canada Inc. is engaged in the manufacture and sale of tires and retread in Canada.
BATO is the first tire company to have all of its plants ISO 14001:2015 certified, which is the international standard for environmental management systems.
As a premium, technology-driven consumer tire brand, the Bridgestone brand is the choice for many of the world’s most prestigious cars.

BATO focuses on the strength of technology in its consumer product developments and continues its research and development, production, and advertising and marketing in order to introduce new products and new versions of popular current offerings.
Firestone traces its roots to Harvey Firestone in 1900.
BATO continues its commitment to research and development, production, and advertising and marketing to support the Firestone brand.
Firestone tires are used as original equipment on a wide range of models produced by some of the world’s leading auto manufacturers.
Over the past century, Firestone tires have appeared and won in nearly every form of motorsport competition.
The Firestone brand focuses on tradition while striving to provide the most innovative products.
BCS manufactures, markets and sells medium and heavy duty truck tires for the original equipment and replacement markets in the United States and in Canada.
Bridgestone, Firestone brand truck tires are available through more than 2,500 dealers and truck stops across the U.S. and Canada.
Around the world, top truck fleets and original equipment manufacturers rely on Bridgestone and Firestone brand tires for long tread life and low cost per mile.
Through its Bandag brand, Bandag retreading dealers have access to industry-leading research and development, manufacturing, marketing and sales expertise.
This combination of new and retread product offerings provides trucking customers with total tire solutions.

In the trucking business, where tires are often the second highest non-payroll cost for a business, BATO medium commercial truck tires are among the top in the industry in technical innovation and performance.
BATO truck tires are known for extremely long tread life and low cost per mile.
GCR Tires & Service is commercial tire and service provider supporting the trucking, off-the-road, agriculture and mining industries.
Staffed by a team of proactive tire advisors, GCR leverages best-in-class tools and technologies to help customers maximize their tire investment.
Bridgestone Americas Off the Road Tires was formed in February 1994 to market off the road tires used in heavy duty applications for construction, mining, aggregates and industrial operations.

Since its inception of the business unit, off the road tire sales revenue has tripled and Bridgestone Americas Off the Road Tires market share has grown to nearly 40 percent of the domestic replacement and original equipment markets.
Today, Bridgestone Americas Off the Road Tires is a significant supplier to most original equipment manufacturers as well as most major global mining and construction companies.

Offers the industry’s most comprehensive selection of radial and bias off the road products; a dedicated sales and engineering staff; sales and distribution through 1,000 dealers as well as the company’s commercial outlets; and the proprietary software, TreadStat™ Tire and Rim Management System.
BAAG is the industry leader in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of market-leading Firestone brand agricultural tires.

BAAG’s Des Moines, Iowa, facility is located in the heart of America’s farm belt and supplies Firestone agricultural tires to half of the North American original equipment market and about 40 percent of the replacement market.
BAAG also has approximately 50 percent of the market in harvesting and planting machinery.
The BAAG testing facility, located in Columbiana, Ohio, is the only facility in the world solely dedicated to the development and testing of agricultural tires.
In addition to agricultural tires, BAAG markets and sells forestry, ATV and construction tires.
BATB is a global force in the sale and distribution of inner tubes for all applications from small lawn and garden tubes to large farming and forestry tubes.
BATB has been located in Russellville, Ark., since October 1964 and is the only manufacturer of inner tubes in the United States and Canada.
Currently BATB has more than 30 percent of the global market for inner tubes, clearly positioning BATB as the world leader in the inner tube industry.
Along with inner tubes, BATB produces other rubber products used in retreading applications as well as custom mixed rubber used in the manufacturing of tires.
BATO’s legacy of motorsports accomplishments began more than nine decades ago when Harvey Firestone showcased his tires in the 1909 Indianapolis 300, the brand’s first documented race victory.
In 1911, a Firestone-equipped driver won the first Indianapolis 500-Mile Race®.
Today, the tradition continues. Firestone brand racing tires have won more Indy 500 races than all other tire brands combined.
Further, BATO’s tire brands have appeared in thousands of competitions around the globe, recording a remarkable variety of wins and honors.

Great drivers in Formula One, the IZOD IndyCar® Series, MotoGP and many other forms of racing have relied on tires provided by the global Bridgestone Group of companies to ensure drivers the consistency and durability they need to win.
BATO’s involvement with motorsports provides more than thrills. The motorsport community’s use of Firestone tires is a testament to the quality of the products the company produces.
Through thousands of miles of experience on the race track, the company’s engineers have paved the way for better street tires.
BATO adapts the technology developed and tested for racing tires to make higher-performing and longer-lasting consumer tires.
BSCA is a wholly owned subsidiary of Bridgestone Americas, Inc.
BSCA manufactures tires in Canada and markets tires and retread in Canada.
In Canada, Bridgestone, Firestone and associate brands are distributed and sold through BSCA.
These products include passenger, light truck, medium truck, forestry, agricultural and small, medium and large off road tires.
BSCA also wholesales retread.
Product is sold by BSCA through its network of independent dealers and through its retail division GCR Tires & Service.
BSCA operates a tire plant in Joliette, Quebec, Ontario.
It is also engaged in the sale of building products in Canada through its division, Firestone Building Products Canada.
BSCA employs approximately 1,550 employees.
source_title: Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations | BATO

facts: Bridgestone is making a US$60 million investment in Abilene, Texas.
The investment will support the rapid growth of the retread tyre business.
The expansion provides room for additional equipment.
The expansion increases production capacity.
The expansion increases days of operation up to seven days a week.
The plant is adding 50,000 sq ft to an existing 200,000 sq-ft facility.
Bridgestone recently celebrated the expansion of its Bridgestone Bandag facility with an official groundbreaking ceremony.
The expansion includes an immediate increase in operational activity by running on six and seven-day production schedules.
The expansion includes the construction of new mixing operations at the facility.
The mixing operations are expected to be completed and in operation by January 2025.
The investment in the facility and the additional days of operations increase the plant’s output by 16%.
Bandag is an industry-leading supplier of retread tyres for fleets.
Retread tyres provide fleets with economic benefits.
Retread tyres are an environmentally responsible way to extend the life of tyres in any fleet.
Since 1957, Bandag has kept approximately 300 million tyres out of the waste system.
Keeping these tyres out of the waste system helps reduce material emissions, water, and energy use associated with new tire production.
The Bandag retreading process has saved four billion gallons of oil since it began 50 years ago.
The Bandag plant in Abilene celebrated 50 years of operations in 2021.
The plant continues to produce pre-cured truck tread, rubber cement, and solvent packaging.
Bridgestone says its investment will build upon the plant’s existing 200,000 sq-ft blueprint.
The investment will provide capacity needed for future production.
source_title: Bridgestone breaks ground on US$60 mn expansion of tyre plant in Texas – Rubber Journal Asia -News on  Rubber machinery ,  Manufacturers ,  Rubber chemical producers and  Rubber processors

facts: Bridgestone Bandag broke ground on the expansion of its retread tire plant in Abilene, Texas.
The project marks a $60 million capital investment into Abilene.
The expansion will add 50,000 square feet to their existing 200,000 square-foot facility.

Misty Mayo, President and Chief Executive Officer of the DCOA, stated that Bridgestone Bandag has continued to invest in Abilene for over 50 years and prioritizes building long-term partnerships with local existing companies.
The expansion is expected to retain 179 full-time employees and create an additional 25 full-time positions for Abilene.
Over the next 10 years, the $60 million project is estimated to add over $21 million in taxes for the City of Abilene, Taylor County, and Abilene Independent School District.
Abilene Mayor Weldon Hurt stated that the project is a tremendous opportunity for the community and expressed pride in Bridgestone Bandag's continued growth and investment.
Over the next 10 years, the total estimated indirect and direct economic impact of retaining and expanding the company is $1.81 billion.
Sam Vinson, DCOA Board Chair, noted that the expansion brings immense value because Bridgestone Bandag chose to stay, grow, and prosper in Abilene, Texas.
As a leading manufacturer of tire retreads, the expansion will focus on developing an automated operation for rubber mixing and production.

Billy Fralick, Plant Manager at the Abilene Plant, stated the expansion will allow growth of production capabilities to meet customer needs and thanked the community for support over the last 52 years.

The plant expansion supports Bridgestone’s E8 Commitment, which consists of eight values starting with “E” (Energy, Ecology, Efficiency, Extension, Economy, Emotion, Ease, and Empowerment) to realize a sustainable society.

Chris Daniel, Executive Director of Bandag Manufacturing at Bridgestone Americas, stated the expansion is driven by growing demand for products and the team’s commitment to delivering innovation and superior quality globally.
The company plays a vital role in the transformation of mobility by building greener retreads and helping maximize fuel efficiency for fleets.

The project taps into Abilene’s skilled labor force in manufacturing, supported by four universities, a technical college, a community college, and state-of-the-art training facilities for high school and college students.
Abilene boasts robust infrastructure and a strategic location in the south-central United States, securing it as a premier location for manufacturing.
source_title: TX: Bridgestone Bandag Breaks Ground on $60 Million Expansion in Abilene, Creating 25 New Jobs | Trade and Industry Development

facts: On 23 September 2022, Bridgestone Americas announced an investment of $60 million to expand its Bridgestone Bandag, LLC manufacturing plant in Abilene, Texas.
The expansion adds a 50,000-square-foot footprint to the existing Abilene facility.
The expansion targets growing demand for tread rubber products driven by the rapid growth of the retread business.
Operational changes include an immediate increase in activity by running on six and seven-day production schedules.
New mixing operations are expected to be completed and operational by January 2025.
The investment and additional operating days will increase the plant's output by 16 percent.

Barry Owens, senior vice president, Bridgestone Americas Manufacturing Group, said the company celebrates growth and commitment with new investment and physical expansion driven by growing demand for products and the team's commitment to delivering innovation and superior quality for customers here and around the world.
Owens noted that the company and Abilene team play a vital role in the transformation of mobility by building greener retreads while helping maximize fuel efficiency for fleets.
Bandag is an industry-leading supplier of retread tires for fleets, providing economic benefits and extending tire life as an environmentally responsible practice.
Since 1957, Bandag has kept approximately 300 million tires out of the waste system, reducing material emissions, water use, and energy use associated with new tire production.
The Bandag retreading process has saved four billion gallons of oil over its 50-year history.

The Abilene plant expansion supports Bridgestone's E8 Commitment, which consists of eight values starting with the letter "E" (Energy, Ecology, Efficiency, Extension, Economy, Emotion, Ease, and Empowerment) to realize a sustainable society.
Expanding Bandag operations specifically aligns with the company's "Efficiency," "Ecology," and "Energy" commitments.
The Abilene plant celebrated 50 years of operations in 2021 and continues producing high-quality pre-cured truck tread, rubber cement, and solvent packaging.
Bridgestone's investment builds upon the plant's existing 200,000-square-foot blueprint to provide capacity for future production.
Bridgestone Americas is the U.S.-based subsidiary of Bridgestone Corporation, headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, and employs more than 50,000 people across its worldwide operations.
source_title: Bridgestone Invests $60 Million to Expand Abilene Bandag Retread Tire Plant

facts: Bridgestone Americas has named Bridget Neal as president of its off-the-road (OTR) tire business.
The appointment is effective August 14.
Neal succeeds Taylor Cole, who recently was named president of Firestone Building Products LLC.

Kurt Danielson, president of the commercial group for U.S. and Canada at BATO, stated that Neal has developed extensive knowledge of the commercial tire business across her 15-year career with Bridgestone.
Neal has held leadership roles in supply and demand planning, logistics, marketing, and other critical business areas.
As president, Neal will lead all aspects of the Bridgestone OTR tire business in North America, including strategic planning, product and technology introductions, sales, and engineering.
She will be responsible for aligning the company’s go-to-market strategies with the broader commercial portfolio.
Neal will help drive coordination for the Bridgestone OTR business globally.
Neal will continue to advance efforts to develop technology solutions that track tire performance and help OTR customers improve productivity and profitability.
Neal joined Bridgestone in August 2002 as a supply planning and sourcing engineer.

During her tenure, she held roles including commercial supply chain manager; manager of distribution programs for Bridgestone Bandag retread systems; account executive for OE commercial truck and bus tires; and general manager of original equipment and government sales for the truck and bus business.
She was promoted to director of OTR tire sales in 2015.
Neal earned a bachelor’s degree in industrial and systems engineering from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
source_title: Bridgestone Americas Names New President Of Off-the-Road Tire Business

facts: Operates a facility named Carcoustics USA South Inc.

facts: Georgia offers automotive industry suppliers like Carcoustics resources necessary to compete worldwide, according to Deal.
By choosing Georgia, Carcoustics will benefit from a well-trained workforce, a thriving automotive industry, and the nation’s top business environment.
Deal expressed encouragement at seeing another German company locate in Georgia, citing successful efforts to build international relationships.
A new 130-square-foot manufacturing facility is being built at 4620 Thompson Mill Road to manufacture solution-oriented acoustic and thermally effective components for the automotive industry.

Stefan Vallentin, project manager at Carcoustics, stated the new plant location in Georgia offers great international transport connections and is logistically positioned between BMW in South Carolina and Mercedes in Alabama.
Potential customers for the facility include Automotive Tier I/Tier II suppliers, Volkswagen, Mercedes Vans, Volvo, and Asian OEMs like Nissan, Honda, Toyota, Kia, and Hyundai.
The location provides new possibilities for Carcoustics' growth strategy, particularly in the U.S. market.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson expressed excitement over continued economic development opportunities in Gwinnett County.
Carcoustics is joining a growing number of German automotive manufacturers that have established operations in Georgia.
The international Carcoustics Group model integrates a central research center with a manufacturing structure designed for proximity to major markets to enable rapid global product availability.

Gwinnett County Board of Commissioners Chair Charlotte Nash welcomed Carcoustics to Gwinnett County, noting it joins a strong base of international companies that find the county's talent, resources, and location ideal.

Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Director of International Investment Nico Wijnberg represented the Global Commerce division in partnership with Partnership Gwinnett, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Buford City Manager Bryan Kerlin called the project a great win for the City of Buford and welcomed Carcoustics as one of its newest businesses.
Bryan Kerlin expressed confidence that Carcoustics will find the necessary support and resources in Buford to remain competitive globally.
Partnership Gwinnett project manager Deven Cason expressed excitement over Carcoustics choosing to locate in Gwinnett County.
The new Buford facility is strategically located to serve Carcoustics' rapidly growing global customer base.

Carcoustics will join nearly 100 other German companies operating in Gwinnett County, alongside 500 internationally owned and operated firms, reinforcing the county's status as a leader in international business.

The establishment is operated by and rolled up under the parent organization Carcoustics.
CARCOUSTICS USA SOUTH INC. has accumulated 24 OSHA violations across 2 inspections over a 6-year recorded history, with $69,969 in total assessed penalties.
The facility sits in the 92nd percentile for violations within its industry-state peer group of 156 employers, while its inspection frequency runs at the 51st percentile.

No severe injury reports (hospitalization, amputation, or loss of an eye) are on file under 29 CFR 1904.39 for the establishment.

facts: Georgia offers incentives designed to encourage the adoption of electric and alternative-fuel vehicles.
These state incentive programs can save buyers thousands of dollars on the purchase of an EV, in addition to federal incentives.

The federal Clean Vehicle Credit, established under the Inflation Reduction Act, offers up to $7,500 for qualifying new battery electric vehicles and plug-in hybrids that meet domestic manufacturing requirements and critical mineral sourcing thresholds.

A used EV tax credit of up to $4,000 (or 30% of the sale price, whichever is less) is available for qualifying used electric vehicles purchased from a licensed dealer, provided the vehicle is at least two model years old and priced at $25,000 or less.
Starting in 2024, buyers can transfer federal EV tax credits to dealers at the point of sale to reduce the purchase price immediately instead of waiting for a tax return refund.
Businesses can claim a commercial clean vehicle credit of up to $7,500 for qualifying light-duty EVs and up to $40,000 for heavier commercial EVs used for business purposes.
Federal new EV tax credit income limits require modified AGI to be below $300,000 for married filing jointly, $225,000 for head of household, or $150,000 for single filers.
Federal used EV tax credit income limits require modified AGI to be below $150,000 for married filing jointly, $112,500 for head of household, or $75,000 for single filers.
Federal tax credits for SUVs, vans, and pickups require the vehicle's MSRP to not exceed $80,000.
Federal tax credits for all other vehicles, such as sedans and hatchbacks, require the vehicle's MSRP to not exceed $55,000.
Federal EV tax credit eligibility depends on where the vehicle was assembled, where the battery components were manufactured, and where the critical minerals were sourced.
The IRS maintains an updated list of qualifying vehicles online for buyers to verify before purchasing.

Popular qualifying vehicle models include select US-assembled Tesla models, Chevrolet Bolt/Equinox EV/Blazer EV, Ford F-150 Lightning/Mustang Mach-E, Rivian R1T/R1S, Hyundai and Kia US-assembled models, BMW select models, and Chrysler/Stellantis Jeep and Ram plug-in hybrids.
The list of eligible vehicles for federal tax credits is updated quarterly, so buyers must verify qualification status before purchasing.
To claim the federal EV tax credit, buyers must verify vehicle eligibility by checking IRS and Department of Energy websites for the specific make, model, and trim.
The new vehicle federal tax credit requires the EV to be purchased or leased from a licensed dealer rather than a private party.
Dealers are required to submit a time-of-sale report to the IRS confirming the vehicle qualifies and the applicable credit amount.
Buyers can claim the federal credit by filing IRS Form 8936 with their tax return or by electing an immediate point-of-sale transfer to the dealer.
Georgia state EV incentive programs may require separate applications through the state's energy office or DMV.
Many Georgia state incentive programs have limited funding and operate on a first-come, first-served basis.
Level 1 EV charging uses a standard 120V household outlet, adds approximately 3-5 miles of range per hour, has no installation cost, and is best suited for plug-in hybrids or low-mileage drivers.
Level 2 EV charging requires a 240V outlet or hardwired installation, adds 20-40 miles of range per hour, and typically costs $500-$2,000 to install depending on home electrical capacity.
The federal Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Credit covers 30% of home charger installation costs, up to $1,000 for residential installations.
Many electric utility companies in Georgia offer time-of-use rates that make overnight EV charging significantly cheaper.
Some Georgia utility companies also offer rebates specifically for EV charger installation.

links.url: Federal and State EV Tax Credits Guide

source_title: Georgia EV Incentives 2026 — Tax Credits, Rebates & Savings

facts: Club Car is an American manufacturer of electric and gas-powered golf carts and small utility vehicles for personal and commercial use.
Founded in 1958, the company is headquartered in Evans, Georgia.
It is one of the largest employers in Columbia County.
The company was acquired by Platinum Equity in 2021.
Prior to acquisition, it operated as a business unit in Ingersoll Rand corporation's Industrial Technologies division.
Club Car was founded in 1958 in Houston, Texas, under the name Landreath Machine.
In 1962, entrepreneur Bill Stevens Jr. purchased the company and relocated it to Augusta, Georgia, renaming it Club Car.
The company has maintained its global headquarters in Augusta since 1962.
In 1978, a group of eight former EZGO executives acquired Club Car, positioning it as a local competitor in the golf cart manufacturing industry.
William Dolan, co-founder of EZGO, was among the new leadership.
Club Car was acquired by Ingersoll Rand in 1995 and became part of its Infrastructure division.
Club Car was later merged with Bobcat to form a Compact Vehicles Technology division that expanded offerings into utility and commercial vehicles.
In 2017, Ingersoll Rand purchased GPS Industries, which provided fleet management systems for Club Car since 2009.
In 2021, Platinum Equity acquired Club Car from Ingersoll Rand in an all-cash transaction valued at $1.68 billion.
Nearly a year later, Club Car announced and completed the acquisition of Garia, a Danish low-speed electric vehicle manufacturer, including its subsidiary Melex from Poland.
The Garia acquisition marked the company's first acquisition under Platinum Equity.
The deal extended the company's reach into street-legal Neighborhood Electric Vehicles (NEVs) and lightweight utility vehicles.
In addition to its global corporate headquarters, Club Car operates two other Georgia-based facilities: a distribution center and a production facility.
The company also operates facilities in Mielec, Poland and JiaXing, China.
In June 2025, Craig Scanlon was named President and CEO of Club Car.
Club Car produces vehicles for personal use, business and commercial applications, and golf operations.
The company's first product was a three-wheeled golf cart introduced in 1958.
It is regarded as an industry leader involved in many innovations, including producing one of the first street-legal golf carts.
The DS line of golf carts began in 1980, named after designer Dom Saporito, and remained a hallmark until 2004 when the Precedent line was introduced.
The company revealed the Precedent i2 to critical acclaim, and again in 2008 with an all-new drive system.
A partnership with GPSI in 2008 produced the world's first connected golf cart (Precedent i3), featuring a system to connect an entire golf fleet to the clubhouse and options like score tracking.
The Precedent i2, i3, and 4Fun were used by golf courses, while the i2 was available for personal use.
The Club Car Tempo vehicle launched in 2018, replacing the Precedent.
In 2017, the company launched the first of its Onward series of personal transportation vehicles, introducing online customization for accessories and color.
The initial Onward launch featured 2-passenger, 4-passenger, and 4-passenger lifted vehicles, with 6-passenger and lithium-ion options added later.
In 2025, Club Car announced its most advanced personal vehicle yet, the Onward LSV (Low-Speed Vehicle).

The Onward LSV standard equipment includes a seven-inch touchscreen display, four-wheel brakes, an Occupant Protection System with reinforced frame, seatbelts, side mirrors, turn signals, DOT-approved glass windshield, back-up camera, and an Acoustic Vehicle Alerting System (AVAS).
Advanced testing for the Onward LSV included automotive style brake performance tests and roof crush tests.
Beginning in 2008 and 2009, Club Car entered the utility vehicles market with its XRT line of personal utility vehicles.
The XRT line ranged from the XRT800 4x2 UTV to XRT1550 4x4 UTV, with the ability to add work attachments.
Club Car offers street-legal golf carts with automotive features such as seat belts, turn signals, windshields, and more.
These vehicles, UTVs, and LSVs were manufactured and branded for other companies, making Club Car the OEM.
In 1985, the company created vehicles targeted at business applications with its Carryall II, aimed at manufacturing facilities, college campuses, and golf course operations.
In 2014, the company re-launched its commercial utility vehicle line with improved efficiency and a new line of accessories.
Two new types of vehicles were developed in 2009 and 2010: street-legal low-speed vehicles (LSV) and vehicles for transporting multiple people at resorts, venues, and campuses.
In April 2024, Club Car celebrated a new manufacturing facility in Appling, Georgia, with Gov. Brian Kemp in attendance.
Club Car opened a second factory in Poland to cope with increased demand in April 2024.
source_title: Club Car - Wikipedia

Club Car operates as a manufacturer of golf carts and utility vehicles.
They specialize in personal, utility, and golf vehicles for applications including personal transportation, commercial use, and golf course operations.

The company raised an undisclosed amount of funding from Kelso & Company.

facts: Club Car celebrated the opening of its new Appling manufacturing facility with tours and a ribbon cutting ceremony on April 18.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp joined state and local elected officials and area business leaders to tour the new manufacturing facility, learn about the Club Car Urban vehicle and participate in a ribbon cutting ceremony.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that Club Car is bringing jobs into Georgia in the growing e-mobility sector and leading the way in compact utility EV manufacturing.
Gov. Brian Kemp noted that Club Car's growth and investment are a testament to the work being done to continue to make Georgia the number one state to do business.
The Club Car Appling Manufacturing Facility is a 105,000-square-foot flexible production and warehouse storage space.
Club Car opened the new facility to offer customers a better experience as demand in the United States for the Club Car Urban vehicle increases.
Club Car has committed nearly $8 million in capital investments into the facility over two years.
The new facility currently operates a state-of-the-art production line and can produce up to 20 vehicles per day.

Mark Wagner, Club Car President and CEO, stated that the company is excited to share how its innovative vehicles are impacting commercial fleet electrification, mini-mobility and last mile delivery with the Governor and First Lady.
Mark Wagner noted that Club Car has been a Georgia based company for more than sixty years and is proud of the quality, safety and reliability built into every product.
Since 1958, the Club Car product portfolio has grown to include much more than golf cars, now encompassing vehicles for commercial and consumer markets.
The Club Car Vehicle Group includes luxury electric vehicle brand Garia and lightweight electric utility vehicle brand Melex.
Garia and Melex product portfolios share similar characteristics of reliability, thoughtful design and proven performance.
Club Car Vehicle Group stated it is proud to be at the forefront of environmentally responsible Zero Emission Vehicle (ZEV) technologies.

The Club Car Urban is described as a workhorse that has become a leading choice as an environmentally friendly, street-legal alternative and addition to college campuses, businesses, farms and industrial sites around the world.
Applications for the Club Car Urban include facilities maintenance, moving equipment, last-mile delivery of packaged goods or food services.
The Club Car Urban features flexible configuration options and accessories that make it easy for users to take advantage of its truck-like payload capacity to handle most tasks.
The Club Car Urban can be equipped in different electric lithium options to provide extended range and improved performance in every climate.

facts: Constellium Automotive USA is significantly reducing its workforce, with dozens of employees set to lose their jobs later this summer.

A notice filed with the Georgia Department of Labor indicates that Constellium Automotive USA, located on Constellium Drive in White, plans to eliminate 57 positions from its workforce of 99 employees.
The filing classifies the action as a permanent reduction in force.

The layoffs will affect workers across multiple departments and job classifications, including operators, material handlers, maintenance technicians, operations supervisors, shift supervisors, quality management personnel, and environmental health and safety staff.
According to the WARN notice, employee separations are scheduled to begin on August 1.
Constellium Automotive USA has operated in the Highland 75 Industrial Park since opening its facility in 2017.
The plant manufactures lightweight aluminum structural components and crash management systems used by automotive manufacturers throughout the Southeast and across North America.
The company is part of Constellium N.V., an international aluminum producer with headquarters in France and the Netherlands.
Constellium focuses on the design, engineering, manufacturing, and recycling of aluminum products serving the aerospace, automotive, and packaging sectors.
source_title: White Plant to Layoff Massive Workforce - Coosa Valley News

facts: Constellium Automotive USA LLC filed a single WARN Act notice with an effective date of June 2026.
The layoff affects 57 employees in Georgia.
The affected location is listed as Unknown, GA.
The notice was classified as a layoff.

Under the WARN Act (Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act), employers with 100 or more employees are required to provide 60 days advance notice before conducting plant closures or mass layoffs.
These notices are filed with state labor departments and are public record.

facts: Constellium stands out as a leading aluminum supplier with the unique ability to deliver both advanced rolled products and extrusion-based components for the automotive industry.

As a trusted partner and Automotive News Top Supplier, Constellium helps empower the world’s leading automakers with advanced aluminum solutions to shape the next generation of vehicles: lighter, cleaner, more sustainable, and built for performance.
25% of Constellium's 2025 revenue came from the automotive market.
Constellium has an annual capacity of 350K+ mt for automotive rolled products.
Constellium has an annual capacity of 100K+ mt for automotive extrusions.
Constellium supplies a complete range of aluminum body sheet materials tailored for inner panels, outer skins, and structural parts.

The company's automotive body sheet products are engineered to support the automotive industry’s shift toward lighter, more efficient vehicles – without compromising strength, safety, or visual quality.
Constellium is a trusted supplier of advanced aluminum extrusion-based automotive components, engineered for superior safety and structural integrity.
The company provides customized high-strength, and lightweight solutions that meet the rigorous demands of automakers, while enhancing vehicle performance and sustainability.
Constellium brings deep expertise and a forward-thinking approach to specialty automotive applications, including enabling efficient thermal management with high-performance heat exchanger alloys.
The company also elevates vehicle interiors and exteriors through precision-finished decorative surfaces.
Aluminum’s thermal conductivity is about five times that of steel, making it the superior choice for thermal applications in battery enclosures.
For battery enclosures, Constellium uses aluminum sheets and extrusions to provide a strong, crash- and intrusion-resistant exterior to protect the batteries that power EVs.

Constellium's technical teams are working closely with automakers to design modular battery enclosures that can be easily configured to fit various shapes and sizes, optimizing both design flexibility and manufacturing costs.

facts: German auto supplier Continental announced a four-billion-euro ($4.6-billion) deal to sell its ContiTech division to a US investment group.
The sale is the latest move in a major restructuring as Continental struggles amid a crisis in the wider European auto sector.
Continental has been slimming down its operations to focus on its traditional tire business.
ContiTech manufactures plastic and rubber products for industrial clients.
In addition to the four-billion-euro sale price, Continental could receive up to 250 million euros more in coming years depending on the division's performance.
The planned sale of ContiTech completes Continental's strategic realignment.
The deal is expected to be finalized by the end of 2026.
Some funds from the sale will be used to pay down debt, while around 2.5 billion euros will be distributed to shareholders.
Last year, Continental spun off a division focused on car components including braking systems and sensors as a separate entity called Aumovio.
The group has announced thousands of job cuts in recent years, alongside other auto suppliers and manufacturers in Germany.
The broader automotive sector is facing fierce Chinese competition, weak demand in key markets, and a troubled shift to electric vehicles.
source_title: Struggling German Auto Supplier Continental to Sell Unit

facts: Continental AG issued a formal press release stating that it would discontinue operations at its textile manufacturing facility, Aldora Mills, in Barnesville, Georgia, by the end of 2026.
The facility employs approximately 230 people.
The plant produces “textile reinforcement materials”, including tire-cord fabric, hose yarn, and knitted fabric used by Continental’s Tires and ContiTech business units.
The plant was acquired by Continental in 1988 and has roots going back long before that as a cotton mill and tire-cord fabric producer.

According to the company’s release, the closure decision followed a “comprehensive business review” aimed at safeguarding profitability, competitiveness and business performance in the Americas region.
Continental stated the plant “has not been cost competitive for many years despite continuous turnaround efforts.”
Changes in global market dynamics and supply chains were cited as additional pressures making this decision necessary.
The plant’s closure means the loss of a major local employer in Barnesville-Lamar County, with the layoffs timed by end-2026.

For the region, such an announcement brings significant economic and social consequences including job loss, decreased local spending, ripple effects for suppliers, and the need for workforce transition support.

The site had long been part of the local industrial base, operating since the early 20th century originally as a cotton mill, then shifting to tire-cord fabrics in the 1920s, sold to General Tire & Rubber in 1930, which later became part of Continental.
Continental emphasizes its commitment to support affected employees during the transition through career counselling and access to employment resources in collaboration with local partners.
The company reiterated that the U.S. remains a key market for Continental, noting that it has invested about US$1.5 billion in its U.S. tire manufacturing operations over the last decade.
The official end of operations at the Aldora Mills plant is slated for late 2026.

Over the coming months, Continental will work with local authorities, workforce organizations and employees to execute the transition plan including job counselling, potential redeployment, and managing the winding-down of the facility.
Local leaders will likely face the challenge of attracting new investment or repurposing the site to mitigate job losses and fill the economic void.

The closure of such a long-standing manufacturing facility is part of a broader trend of global supply-chain shifts, automation, cost pressures, and strategic consolidation in manufacturing industries.

For Barnesville and the surrounding region which includes counties like Upson, Meriwether, Lamar, etc., the news underscores the importance of economic diversification and support for workforce transition.
source_title: Continental AG’s Closure of the Aldora Mills Plant in Barnesville, Georgia – 360News.com

facts: Aruna Anand has been named the new president and CEO of Continental’s Automotive group sector in North America.
Anand will assume responsibility as president and CEO effective Dec. 1.
She will retain her current role as head of the Architecture and Networking business area for the North American region of Continental Automotive.
In this role, Anand is responsible for all global architecture and networking business activities that support customers based out of North America.
Anand joined the company in 1997 and has held numerous leadership positions across different business areas of the Continental Automotive group sector.
She previously served as head of Software for Gasoline Engine and Transmissions Systems for the Electronic Controls business unit within the company’s then Powertrain division.
She also led the Wireless Product Group Engineering within the Connected Car Networking business unit of Continental’s Vehicle Networking and Information business area.
Prior to her current appointment, Anand was responsible for leading Continental Engineering Services, an independently operating business unit that offers comprehensive engineering services.
Nikolai Setzer, CEO of Continental AG, stated that Anand brings an outstanding combination of experience and talent to the President and CEO Automotive North America position.
Setzer described Anand as a respected leader and technical expert in areas that are the focus of Continental's future growth.
Setzer noted that Anand’s dedication to customers and employees will help guide the success of Continental in the important North American market.
Anand earned her master’s degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Oakland University.
She earned a bachelor’s degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from Anna University in Chennai, India.
In 2020, Anand was recognized by Automotive News as one of the 100 Leading Women in the North American Automotive industry.
In 2018, Anand was honored with the prestigious Automotive News Rising Star award.
She was recently recognized at the Women of Color Awards Gala and received the Professional Achievement in Industry award.

Anand stated that it is an honor to be selected as Automotive President and CEO for North America and looks forward to leading her team toward a bright future and providing customers with leading technological solutions.
source_title: Aruna Anand Appointed President & CEO of Continental Automotive North America

facts: Section 179 is a provision of the US tax code that allows businesses to deduct (write-off) the full purchase price of qualifying vehicles and equipment in the year of purchase, rather than depreciating them over several years.
For taxable years beginning in 2026, the Section 179 maximum deduction is $2,560,000.
The deduction begins to phase out dollar-for-dollar when total qualifying purchases exceed $4,090,000, and is fully phased out at $6,650,000.
Section 179 is generally limited to taxable income from the active conduct of your trade or business.
Amounts elected but unable to be used due to the business-income limit may be carried forward to future years.
Vehicles must be used more than 50% for business to qualify for the deduction.
Business use includes traveling between job sites, transporting goods and equipment, and business-related travel away from the regular work location.
Commuting and personal use are not deductible, even if some business activity occurs in the vehicle during those times.
A separate 'heavy SUV' cap limits expensing to up to $32,000 for certain heavy SUVs (generally >6,000 and ≤14,000 lbs GVWR) primarily designed to carry passengers in 2026.
Some vehicles are excluded from the heavy SUV cap.
Bonus depreciation allows businesses to claim an additional first-year depreciation deduction on eligible property, including some vehicles.
For 2026, the bonus depreciation rate is 100% for qualified property acquired after Jan. 19, 2025.

Heavy SUVs are subject to a separate Section 179 SUV limitation, and passenger automobiles (generally vehicles at or below 6,000 lbs GVWR) are subject to annual depreciation limits under Section 280F.
The IRS uses Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR), not curb weight, to determine the 6,000-lb threshold for eligibility.
GVWR is the maximum operating weight specified by the manufacturer and can typically be found inside the driver’s side door jamb.
Eligible vehicles include heavy SUVs, pickups, and vans over 6,000 lbs GVWR with more than 50% business use.

The list of qualifying vehicles includes models from Audi, Bentley, BMW, Buick, Cadillac, Chevrolet, Chrysler, Dodge, Ford, GMC, Honda, Infiniti, Jeep, Land Rover, Lexus, Lincoln, Mercedes-Benz, Nissan, Porsche, Rivian, Rolls-Royce, Tesla, and Toyota.
Rivian R1T and R1S have a GVWR of 8,532 lbs for model years 2022–2026.
Tesla Model X Long Range has a GVWR of 6,130 lbs (model years 2021–2026) and the Plaid trim has a GVWR of 6,561 lbs (model years 2021–2026).
Vehicle weights listed are estimates for standard configurations and can vary by model year, trim package, engine size, towing package, and other factors.
Actual GVWR should be verified on the driver's side door label as multiple weight classifications may apply.
Used vehicles can qualify for Section 179 provided they are 'new to you', meet general requirements like >50% business use, and are acquired by purchase for trade or business use.
Vehicles acquired by gift or inheritance do not qualify, and property acquired from a related person generally does not qualify either.
If bonus depreciation is claimed on a used vehicle, additional eligibility rules apply.
Taxpayers who claim Section 179 or bonus depreciation on a vehicle generally cannot use the standard mileage rate method for that same vehicle.
To calculate business-use percentage, divide total miles driven for business by total miles driven (including personal/commuting) for the year.
Detailed records of vehicle usage, including mileage logs, receipts, invoices, and related expenses, must be kept for at least three years after filing.
Depending on your state, state-specific rules and regulations may apply to Section 179 deductions; businesses should contact their Department of Revenue or a tax professional.
Crest Capital offers an exclusive financing program for business-use SUVs and company cars over 6,000 lbs GVWR to help maximize Section 179 deductions while maintaining working capital.
The program allows financing under a company's name to separate personal and business liabilities and potentially enhance the company's credit profile without blanket liens.
The premium financing program is reserved exclusively for established Crest Capital customers with excellent payment history and outstanding credit profiles.
New customers can access premium offerings by first financing equipment, trucks, vans, or specialty vehicles through standard programs to build a proven payment history.
Financing eligible vehicles towards the end of the calendar year minimizes monthly payments while still allowing for the deduction.
The list of qualifying vehicles was last reviewed in January 2026 and is updated periodically.

Information on vehicle weights is sourced from official manufacturer websites, government databases, and automotive review sites but may not be accurate; Crest Capital assumes no liability for errors or inaccuracies.

links.url: IRS inflation adjustments for tax year 2026

source_title: Section 179 Deduction: Vehicles Over 6,000 lbs (2026 Updated List)

facts: Continental Tire the Americas, LLC is a leading manufacturer of tires and rubber products.
The company provides high-quality solutions for various vehicles.
Continental Tire the Americas was established as a subsidiary of Continental AG.
The company maintains a strong presence in the North American market with a focus on innovation and sustainability.
Continental Tire produces a wide range of tires for passenger cars, trucks, and specialty vehicles.
The company emphasizes research and development to ensure its products meet high safety and performance standards.
Continental Tire is dedicated to environmental responsibility and actively works to reduce its carbon footprint through sustainable manufacturing practices and eco-friendly materials.
In addition to tire production, Continental Tire the Americas offers comprehensive services including tire management and support for fleet operators.
Continental Tire's vision is to be the most trusted partner in mobility by providing innovative solutions that enhance the driving experience.
The company focuses on customer satisfaction across all aspects of its business.
Continental Tire the Americas is headquartered in Fort Mill, South Carolina.
The company employs a diverse workforce committed to fostering a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.
Continental Tire invests in employee development and community engagement.
The company leverages advanced technologies and strategic partnerships to navigate the evolving landscape of the automotive industry.

facts: Continental is one of the world’s largest tire manufacturers, delivering top performance for more than 150 years.
Continental produces tires at 19 locations in 16 countries globally.
Continental operates manufacturing facilities in Clinton (MS, USA), Mount Vernon (IL, USA), Plymouth (IN, USA), and Sumter (SC, USA).
A modern car tire typically consists of 25 components and 12 different rubber compounds.
Continental covers around 25 million test kilometers annually with new tire models during development and quality control.
In 2024, Continental conducted more digital tire simulations and virtual calculations than physical tests for the first time.
Virtual simulations save an average of 400 km in real-world test driving per test program.
Since March 2025, seven self-driving autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) have been transporting tires within the ContiLifeCycle plant in Hanover-Stöcken.
Continental uses materials like recycled PET bottles in ContiRe.Tex technology and silica from rice husks to increase renewable and recycled raw material shares.
Continental has over 2,000 R&D employees around the world.
source_title: Tire Manufacturing

facts: Continental Tire the Americas, LLC, d.b.a. General Tire, is an American manufacturer of tires for motor vehicles, and semi trucks.

Founded in 1915 in Akron, Ohio, by William Francis O'Neil, Winfred E. Fouse, Charles J. Jahant, Robert Iredell, and H.B. Pushee as The General Tire & Rubber Company using funding from Michael O'Neil's department store.
Initial capitalization included $200,000 USD borrowed from the store.
Early products included the General Jumbo premium replacement tire for Ford Model Ts, a low-pressure General Balloon Jumbo tire, and Dual 90 tires.
By 1930, the company had 14 retail stores and about 1.8% of the tire market.
During the depression, The General Tire & Rubber Company bought out Yale Tire and Rubber Company and India Tire and Rubber Company, increasing market share to 2.7% by 1933.

In 1984, the company diversified into a conglomerate (GenCorp, Inc.) with holdings in tire manufacturing, rubber compounds (DiversiTech General), rocketry and aeronautics (Aerojet), and broadcasting (RKO General).
The compounds division was spun off and became OMNOVA Solutions Inc.
The rocketry and aeronautics business was kept and expanded, eventually becoming Aerojet Rocketdyne Holdings, Inc.
The company's tire division was sold to Germany's Continental AG in 1987, becoming Continental General Tire Corp. before its re–incorporation again to its current name.
Headquarters moved to Fort Mill, South Carolina in 2008 and remains there as of the page's publication.
General Tire is the official tire supplier of the ARCA Menards Series and NASCAR Canada Series.
General Tire is an official partner of Major League Fishing and Jeep Jamboree USA.
source_title: General Tire - Wikipedia

facts: Continental Tire the Americas, LLC has one WARN filing on record in Georgia affecting 235 workers.
The affected facility is located at 160 Aldora St, Barnesville, Georgia.
The WARN notice type is classified as a closure.
The mass layoff/plant closing effective date is March 31, 2026.
The WARN notice was filed on January 30, 2026.
Employers with 100 or more employees must give 60 days written notice before mass layoffs or plant closings under the WARN Act.
Workers who received less than 60 days notice may be entitled to back pay and benefits for each day of the shortfall.
Affected workers are advised to file for unemployment insurance with their state workforce agency as soon as possible.
Affected workers can review COBRA options to continue health coverage for up to 18 months.

facts: Continental Tire the Americas LLC manufactures passenger, light truck and commercial tires at facilities in Barnesville, Georgia.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC also operates manufacturing facilities in Mt. Vernon and Sumter, South Carolina.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC is based in Fort Mill, South Carolina.
Continental Tire the Americas LLC is the North American subsidiary of the German-based tire and automotive component manufacturer Continental AG.
The company faces $341,866 in proposed penalties from OSHA for safety violations at its Illinois tire plant.

OSHA cited the company for deficient safety procedures regarding energy control, combustible dust preventative engineering, housekeeping, personal protective equipment, emergency egress routes, and hazard communication.

In November 2021, two consecutive incidents at the Illinois plant resulted in one employee suffering an amputation of three fingers, another requiring hospitalization for severe burns, and a third sustaining a concussion from a mixer explosion.
OSHA issued citations for two repeated violations, 16 serious violations, and five other than serious violations following its investigation.
OSHA Area Director Aaron Priddy stated that Continental Tire the Americas must review company safety procedures and employee training to ensure worker safety.
source_title: OSHA fines Continental Tire for multiple safety violations at Illinois tire plant - Rubber World - The Technical Service Magazine for the Rubber Industry

facts: Continental Tires has its U.S. headquarters in Fort Mill.
Jochen Etzel, chief executive officer of Continental Tire the Americas, LLC, announced his retirement effective May 1, 2025.
Tansu Işık will succeed Etzel as the new head of Continental’s Business Area Replacement Tires the Americas.
Işık will report directly to Christian Koetz, executive board member of Continental and head of Continental Tires.
Etzel has served in this capacity since 2011.
Etzel played an important role in uniting the various Continental tire activities in the region and further strengthening their competitive position.
Etzel started his career as a lawyer in Continental’s corporate law department.

He then took over a financial manager position responsible for developing the company’s tire business in regions outside of Europe before leading Continental’s corporate Mergers and Acquisitions team.

Christian Koetz, executive board member of Continental and head of Continental Tires, stated that Etzel’s impact on the tire business has been immeasurable and thanked him for his leadership, dedication, and contributions throughout his tenure.

Işık brings more than 20 years of international experience in a wide variety of markets and functions within Continental’s tires business, including marketing, supply chain, strategy, and key account management.
Işık began his career as a business development specialist in Istanbul, Turkey.
source_title: Continental Tires The Americas CEO Announces Retirement - Integrated Media Publishing

facts: A new automotive supplier manufacturing facility for Daesol Ausys Georgia will create more than 140 new jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daesol Ausys Georgia will establish a new manufacturing facility in West Point.
The facility will create more than 140 new jobs and $72 million in investment in Harris County.
“West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right, and we are excited that Daesol Ausys will join other innovators in Harris County,” said Governor Brian Kemp.

“Our state’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for the jobs of the future.” (Governor Brian Kemp)
Daesol Ausys was established in Korea in 2017.
The company specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
It is a key supplier for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
“We’re committed to delivering world-class services that exceed our clients’ requirements and expectations,” said Min Ho Kwon, CEO of Daesol Ausys.
“We are very excited about this project in the Northwest Harris Business Park and look forward to continuing the partnership with the State of Georgia and Harris County.” (Min Ho Kwon)
Daesol Ausys’ new facility will produce luggage boards and luggage covers.
Operations are expected to begin in December 2024.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.

“Thanks to the collective efforts of all involved, Daesol Ausys’ decision to locate in the Northwest Harris Business Park represents a commitment to Harris County,” said Robert Grant, Chairman of the Harris County Commission.

“West Point and Harris County have a wonderful relationship with Daesol Ausys’ sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials, and we have been working closely with company officials to get this new venture in West Point.” (Robert Grant)

“We extend a gracious welcome to Daesol Ausys and look forward to a long and mutually beneficial partnership with our newest corporate citizen,” said Craig Greenhaw, Chairman of the Harris County Development Authority.
“West Point and Harris County continue to work well together, and this is yet another successful venture that brings jobs and capital investment into the region.” (Craig Greenhaw)

Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Harris County Commission, Harris County Development Authority, City of West Point, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

“Economic development is a team sport that is driven by the local level. Congratulations to Harris County and our community partners for helping bring Daesol Ausys to West Georgia,” said GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson.

“The automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state. We’re excited to see the continuation of this growth and look forward to the opportunities that Daesol Ausys will provide to the people of Harris County.” (Pat Wilson)

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Daesol Ausys Georgia is a leading manufacturer of automotive components and a key supplier of luggage boards and luggage covers.
The company is committed to exceeding customer expectations with award-winning products and services through innovation and efficiency.
The company has a long history as one of the top suppliers to Kia and Hyundai worldwide.
source_title: New Georgia automotive facility will create 140 new jobs

facts: Three Hyundai suppliers plan to add production plants in Georgia for a combined investment of nearly $180 million.
Automotive parts supplier NVH Korea will invest over $72 million in a new production facility in Locust Grove, Georgia.
NVH Korea will produce the EV battery system components that will protect, connect and monitor the battery cells’ performance.
The new facility is expected to create 160 jobs and operations are anticipated to begin in Q2 of 2024.
The Locust Grove plant will be the company’s second Georgia facility.
The company’s subsidiary, AFS America, has a facility in Columbus that produces floor and carbo mats and cargo trays.
Another South Korea-based supplier, Daesol Ausys, will establish a $72 million facility in West Point.
The new plant will create 140 jobs in production, quality control, maintenance, human resources and management.
Production is expected to begin in December 2024.
The company will produce luggage boards and covers and is also a supplier for General Motors and Kia, the latter of which also has a production facility in West Point.
In July, Kia announced it was investing $200 million to expand the plant to produce its new electric SUV model beginning in Q2 of next year.
Car seat maker Das Corp. will establish a $35 million plant in Metter.
The new facility will produce automotive seat structures when operations begin in the second half of 2024.
The investment will create 300 jobs.
The three factories follow Hyundai to the state after the automaker announced a $5.5 billion manufacturing campus in Ellabell last year, the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.
Hyundai has also been investing heavily in itself regarding its upcoming $5.5 billion EV metaplant.
In late May, Hyundai and LG Energy Solution struck a joint venture deal to build a $4.3 billion battery cell plant, which will be adjacent to the automaker’s EV campus.
At the beginning of September, the two companies announced an additional $2 billion investment toward the upcoming plant.
The two South Korea-based companies will each take a 50% stake in the venture and facility.
Hyundai will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant to be sent to its U.S. manufacturing sites.
The total $6.3 billion investment will create 8,500 jobs, according to a state official.
source_title: Hyundai’s Georgia EV supplier network adds 3 more manufacturers | Supply Chain Dive

facts: An automotive supplier, Daesol Ausys Georgia, is investing $72 million in Harris County.
The supplier is establishing a new manufacturing facility in West Point, creating more than 140 job opportunities for residents.
The Daesol Ausys company was founded in Korea in 2017.
The company designs and manufactures motor and electric vehicle interior components.
The facility coming to West Point, Georgia will produce luggage boards and covers.
Governor Brian Kemp stated in a press release that West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right and expressed excitement that Daesol Ausys will join other innovators in Harris County.
Operations for the facility are planned to start in December 2024.
Positions will be open in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.

Craig Greeenhaw, Chairman of the Harris County Development Authority, stated in a press release that they extend a gracious welcome to Daesol Ausys and look forward to a long and mutually beneficial partnership with the company.
Greeenhaw noted that West Point and Harris County continue to work well together and that the venture brings jobs and capital investment into the region.
source_title: Gov. Kemp announces new manufacturing facility to be opened in West Point

facts: South Korean company Daesol Ausys unveiled plans to establish a $72 million factory in Georgia.
The facility will be focused on producing electric vehicle (EV) components, specifically luggage boards and compartment covers.
The company intends to employ over 140 workers at the new plant.
The factory will be constructed in West Point, Georgia.
This will mark the third factory within the same West Point industrial park connected to the company.
Daehan Solution Co. started a factory in the park in 2008 for sound insulation parts.
Daesol Material established a factory in the park in 2017 to create automotive fiber padding.
Daesol Ausys is a key supplier to Hyundai, Kia, and General Motors.
The initiative reflects a growing trend of EV suppliers choosing Georgia for their facilities.
Hyundai previously announced a $5.5 billion plant in the state to assemble EVs and batteries near Savannah.
Hyundai plans to commence vehicle production at its Georgia plant in 2025.
The wave of investment is anticipated to create nearly 5,000 jobs in the region.
Georgia’s automotive industry already employs more than 50,000 individuals.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp emphasized that the state's automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for future jobs.
Daesol Ausys aims to begin production in December 2024.
The state of Georgia is actively supporting the venture by providing training for workers.
The company has received financial incentives including property tax breaks worth around $5.7 million.
The company has also received potential state income tax credits totaling $875,000.

The investment adds to Georgia's increasing prominence in the electric vehicle supply chain and reinforces the region's position as a vital player in the automotive industry's electrification efforts.
source_title: Daesol Ausys Invests USD 72 mn in Georgia Plant for EV Parts

facts: Doowon Climate Control America, Inc. is a Korean automotive air-conditioning manufacturer.
Doowon Climate Control America, Inc. announced a $30 million factory investment on Tuesday.
The new plant will be located in Candler.
The facility will produce automotive air conditioning (A/C) components.
The project promises 200 jobs.
The investment is part of a fast-growing list of parts suppliers moving to Southeast Georgia.
The supplier's move is intended to support Hyundai Motor Group's future electric vehicle factory in Ellabell.
source_title: Hyundai supplier promises 200 jobs at new Candler A/C plant - Statesboro Herald

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on Thursday, September 7th, 2023, that DAS Corp. (DAS), an automotive parts company, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The facility will create 300 new jobs and generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
This initiative will also involve collaborative indirect investments.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.

Governor Kemp noted that Georgia's logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners in all corners of the state and to markets across the world.
Established in Korea in 1987, DAS specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.

Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS, stated that the strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in their decision-making process.

Sean Kim added that Metter was deemed an ideal location for future expansion prospects in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub thanks to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter and will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations at the new facility are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will be recruiting for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.

Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated that the impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving the socioeconomic wellbeing of citizens.
Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing Candler County as their new home and stated they will provide a boost to spur more economic development.

Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the Authority is proud to support the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state and work together to drive economic activity and jobs.

Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network as a result of years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.

In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21, and suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
source_title: DAS Corp. to Bring 300 Automotive Jobs to Metter - LaGrange CEO
