facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a leading U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider.
Stryten Energy will showcase its energy storage technology at the inaugural Battery Council International (BCI) Flow Batteries North America event.
The BCI Flow Batteries North America conference is dedicated exclusively to advancing flow battery innovation and deployment.
The conference will take place October 27-29 at the Renaissance Chicago Downtown Hotel.
Stryten Energy is leading the advancement of domestically manufactured energy storage with a battery-first portfolio designed to optimize power across all energy sources.
As the premier Diamond Sponsor, Stryten Energy will spotlight its leadership in domestic manufacturing and the advancement of sustainable energy storage technologies.
Stryten Energy CEO and President Mike Judd will deliver the event’s welcome address on Tuesday, October 28.
Mike Judd will share how North America-manufactured solutions are transforming grid reliability and reshaping the future of energy storage.

Mike Judd will address how now is the right time for the entire industry to deploy utility-scale flow batteries across multiple applications to serve increased energy demand and improve U.S. energy security and independence.
Scott Childers, Vice President of Essential Power at Stryten Energy, stated that Stryten Energy provides solutions that strengthen energy resilience.
Scott Childers reiterated that the company is leading the advancement of domestically manufactured energy storage with a battery-first portfolio designed to optimize power across all energy sources.
Attendees can meet with the Stryten team to explore real-world applications of Stryten Energy’s next-generation battery energy storage systems (BESS).
Stryten Energy’s BESS are used in data centers, microgrids, military operations and industrial applications.
Stryten’s innovations are playing a key role in securing and modernizing the grid.

On Wednesday, October 29 at 11:35 a.m., Scott Childers will join Nate Kirchhofer (Cofounder & CEO at BioZen Batteries, Inc.), John Miller (Materials Science Engineer at Entex), and Russ Weed (President at CleanTech Strategies) in a Flow Battery Industry Group member panel.
The panel will discuss the critical need for advancing flow battery technology as the grid evolves to accommodate renewable energy integration.
Storion Energy is a joint venture between an affiliate of Stryten Energy LLC and Largo Clean Energy Corp.
Storion Energy will present its Vanadium Redox Flow Battery (VRFB) component technology, designed for scalable, long-duration storage needs.

Stryten Energy helps solve the world’s most pressing energy challenges with a broad range of energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten Energy partners with some of the world’s most recognized companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten powers applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains and trucks.
Stryten’s stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.
Stryten has more than a century of expertise in the industry.
source_title: Stryten Energy Powers Resilience at BCI Flow Batteries North America With Advanced Energy Storage Solutions

facts: Textron Specialized Vehicles, Inc. is located in Augusta, Georgia.
The company manufactures Bad Boy XTO and Bone Collector XTO model off-road utility vehicles.
The recalled four-wheeled vehicles are battery-powered and have a bench seat for a driver and one passenger and a two-passenger rear-facing bench seat on the back.
Recalled vehicles were sold in camouflage, black, white, forest green, flame red, and patriot blue with the brand and model name printed on the side and front panels.
The recalled vehicles have serial numbers ranging from 8000020 through 8004934 located on the steering column connected to the wheel.
The recalled utility vehicles were sold at Bad Boy dealers nationwide from November 2010 through June 2013.
Recalled vehicles were priced between $13,200 and $14,200.
The recalled utility vehicles were manufactured in the United States.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) and Textron Specialized Vehicles announced a recall of about 1,100 Bad Boy off-road utility vehicles due to the lack of seat belts posing a risk of serious injury or death from rollovers.
A 14-year-old male passenger died in Toombs County, Georgia in 2014 as a result of a vehicle rollover.
An adult male operator suffered a serious leg injury in a separate rollover incident involving the vehicles.
Consumers are instructed to immediately stop using the recalled vehicles and contact Textron Specialized Vehicles or an authorized dealer for a free installation of seat belts.
Textron Specialized Vehicles is contacting all known purchasers directly regarding the recall remedy.
source_title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Recalls Bad Boy Off-Road Utility Vehicles Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death; One Death and One Injury Reported | CPSC.gov

facts: The fuel tank cap does not fit correctly, causing a fuel leak around the filler neck, posing a fire hazard.
About 10,300 utility vehicles are affected by the recall.
An additional approximately 1,000 units were sold in Canada.
The recall involves model years 2022 and 2023 Prowler Pro, Prowler Pro Crew, Tracker 800SX, and Tracker 800SX Crew utility vehicles.
The recalled vehicles were sold in various colors including green, gray, red, fossil, and timber.
"Prowler" or "Tracker" is stamped on the side of the cargo box.
The 10th digit of the vehicle identification number (VIN) is printed on the operator side of the frame under the cargo box.
A VIN 10th digit of "N" indicates a model year 2022 vehicle.
A VIN 10th digit of "P" indicates a model year 2023 vehicle.
Customers should immediately stop using the recalled utility vehicles.
Customers should contact an authorized dealer for a free inspection and repair.
Textron Specialized Vehicles is contacting all known purchasers directly regarding the recall.
The firm has received 48 reports of incidents involving fuel leakage around the cap.
No injuries have been reported related to the fuel leakage issue.
source_title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Recalls Prowler Pro and Tracker Utility Vehicles (UTVs) Due to Fire Hazard | CPSC.gov

canonical_name: Czech Automotive Trade Mission to South Carolina and Georgia
facts: A trade mission from the Czech automotive industry to the United States was led by the Embassy of the Czech Republic in Washington, DC, and CzechInvest on October 17-20, 2016.

The purpose of the visit was to gather information about the US automotive industry, compare education and training levels for automotive professions against their actual job application, make business and academic contacts, and find opportunities for Czech automotive exporters.
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Czech Republic financially supported the visit as part of its economic diplomacy efforts.

In South Carolina, the trade mission visited the Clemson University International Center for Automotive Research (CU-ICAR), an advanced-technology research, innovation, and educational campus advancing the automotive sector.
Czech university representatives expressed interest in Clemson’s student project Deep Orange, which has been running for eight years.
The Deep Orange initiative gives students the opportunity to work with a chosen car manufacturer to create a prototype vehicle within a two-year program.

The delegation held meetings with companies in Charleston, Atlanta, and Gainesville, focusing on foreign firms’ conditions for success on the American market and understanding differences between European and American automotive industries.
The delegation met with ALBAform Inc., which is owned by its Czech parent company ALBA-METAL.
ALBAform has been designing, developing, and manufacturing precision metal components focused on the automotive industry in Georgia since 2011.
ALBAform operates as a family-run branch that has grown alongside its volume of customer orders and shared its experiences with the delegation.

Ambassador of the Czech Republic Petr Gandalovic noted that Honorary Consuls often have local contacts hard to obtain from Washington and appreciated the assistance and organizational support of Honorary Consul for North and South Carolina Petr Vasicko and Honorary Consul for Georgia George Novak.
Head of the Economic Section at the Czech Embassy Tomas Hart stated that the mission took the first step in making new contacts and is now evaluating next steps.
Tomas Hart noted that from ALBAform’s example, it is evident that small and middle-sized companies are able to take on the American market.
source_title: Czech Auto Experts and Managers Visit South Carolina and Georgia | Embassy of the Czech Republic in Washington, D.C.

facts: Aspen Aerogels ended 2025 with $158.6 million in cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash.
Aspen expects to receive approximately $37.6 million from General Motors in Q1 2026 related to a commercial settlement for prior EV capacity adjustments.
Aspen recorded a $291.2 million impairment charge in 2025 related to its previously planned Statesboro, Georgia plant.
Aspen executed a non-binding letter of intent to sell assets from its demobilized Statesboro, Georgia facility and expects to complete the transaction during 2026.
Aspen confirmed its Q4 2025 EV customer award relates to Volvo Cars, expanding its European OEM relationships, with additional awards anticipated in 2026.
Aspen was awarded a North Sea subsea pipeline project with expected delivery in the third quarter of 2026.
Aspen initiated a strategic review to evaluate opportunities to strengthen its long-term competitive position and optimize its capital structure.
Aspen engaged Piper Sandler & Co. as its exclusive financial advisor for the strategic review.
President and CEO Don Young stated the review aims to align the company's strategy, capital allocation, and asset base with value creation.
CFO Grant Thoele noted that North American EV production levels decreased in response to evolving regulatory frameworks and end-market demand during 2025.
Aspen reduced its fixed cost structure by more than $75 million in response to market conditions.
Aspen anticipates continued softness in thermal barrier volumes as EV demand finds a floor at lower production levels, while expecting topline growth from Energy Industrial project momentum.
Aspen's PyroThin® products enable solutions to thermal runaway challenges within the electric vehicle market.
Thermal barrier segment revenue for Q4 2025 was $16.1 million, reflecting a significant reduction in customer demand following changes in regulatory frameworks and incentive programs.
Full year 2025 thermal barrier segment revenue was $168.9 million, compared to $306.8 million in the prior year.
Aspen's Q1 2026 revenue is expected to range between $35 million and $40 million.
Aspen's FY 2026 capital expenditures are expected to be less than $10 million.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year

facts: Decostar Industries, Inc. is a manufacturer and supplier of automotive parts based in Carrollton, Ga.
The company will pay $38,500 to settle a religious discrimination lawsuit filed by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC).
An employee made numerous requests to be excused from mandatory overtime on Saturdays due to religious beliefs prohibiting work during the Sabbath.
Decostar initially approved the accommodation request until January 2014, when a new supervisor took over and repeatedly denied ongoing accommodation requests.
The company discharged the employee on Oct. 27, 2014, after she refused to violate her religious beliefs.
A consent decree settling the lawsuit requires Decostar to adopt and implement a new policy for employees to request religious accommodations.
The decree mandates that the company provide annual equal employment opportunity training to its managers.
The two-year decree requires Decostar to post a notice about the lawsuit to employees and provide periodic reporting to the EEOC regarding disability discrimination complaints.
Antonette Sewell, regional attorney for the EEOC's Atlanta District Office, stated that forcing workers to choose between livelihood and religion is unconscionable and unlawful.
source_title: Decostar Industries to Pay $38,500 to Settle EEOC Religious Discrimination Suit | U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission

facts: Canton's economy has diversified from cotton textile manufacturing to include healthcare, education, technology, advanced manufacturing, corporate and regional headquarters, government services, and retail trade.
The City of Canton, along with economic development partners, offers exceptional incentives to relocating and expanding businesses.
Businesses that locate within the Central Business District may qualify for an 80% reduction on all new water and sewer tap fees.
Businesses that locate within the Central City Opportunity Zone may become eligible to receive $3,500 per created job when two or more jobs are created.

The Downtown Development Revolving Loan Fund (DDRLF) is available through the Georgia Department of Community Affairs (DCA) and offers below-market rate financing to fund capital projects in core historic downtown areas and adjacent historic neighborhoods where it will spur commercial redevelopment.
The Canton Main Street Program in partnership with Canton Tourism administers a grant program for downtown property owners seeking to improve the exterior of their buildings.
Property owners can apply for up to $2,500 in matching grant funds that can be used for a variety of exterior work through the Façade Improvement Program.

The City offers a 100% Freeport Inventory Tax Exemption covering raw materials and goods in process of manufacture, finished goods produced in Georgia within the last 12 months, and finished goods stored in Georgia within the last 12 months destined for shipment out-of-state.
Businesses that locate within Riverstone may qualify for a 30% discount on new sewer tap fees.

Businesses that locate within the River Mill Livable Centers Initiative (LCI) Enterprise Zone may qualify for abatement of occupational tax, inspection fees, and other fees based on the project’s economic impact.
Property tax abatement is provided for a certain period of time based on the value of investment into existing property improvements in the River Mill LCI Enterprise Zone.
Eligible businesses in the River Mill LCI Enterprise Zone must create and maintain at least five new full-time jobs.
Businesses that locate within the Technology Ridge Opportunity Zone may become eligible to receive $3,500 per created job when two or more jobs are created.
The Canton Campus of Chattahoochee Technical College offers popular business courses to students, including Business Management, Accounting, and Marketing Management.
These programs prepare students for entry into management or supervisory occupations in a variety of businesses and industries.

The Cherokee Office of Economic Development (COED) works each day to drive economic development in Cherokee County by promoting the community’s business, academic and lifestyle strengths to continually attract dynamic companies and professionals to the county.

The Georgia Department of Labor provides services including administration of unemployment insurance, employment service, provision of workforce information to the public and private sectors, and oversight of child labor issues.

The Georgia Small Business Development Center (SBDC) provides tools, training and resources to help small businesses grow and succeed, with a mission to enhance the economic well-being of Georgians by providing educational services for small business owners and aspiring entrepreneurs.
The Go Build Georgia Foundation is the private arm of the public/private partnership of the Go Build Georgia program undertaken by the Governor’s Office of Workforce Development.
Go Build Georgia educates children on the value of learning a trade.

facts: Maxxis will open a new distribution facility just northwest of Indianapolis, Indiana later this year as part of a major expansion of its U.S. warehouse capacity.
The facility will feature more than 350,000 square feet of warehouse space, including 10,000 square feet of office space and two 2,500-square-foot administrative warehouse offices.
Maxxis is one of the world’s top ten tire manufacturers and a Taiwan-based company that has operated in the U.S. since 1985.
The company offers automotive, light truck, bicycle, motorcycle, and ATV tires as both original equipment and aftermarket products.

In addition to the Indiana facility scheduled to open in August, Maxxis operates U.S. distribution centers in Rancho Cucamonga, California; Grapevine, Texas; and its corporate headquarters in Suwanee, Georgia.
The new facility is located in the Park130 business park in Whitestown, Indiana.
Maxxis management cited Indiana’s excellent transportation infrastructure, friendly business environment, and proximity to major automakers as reasons for choosing the state for the new facility.
Management noted that Whitestown is a rapidly growing area with easy access to both major interstates and rail.
The new center is located near I-65, just minutes from I-465, and less than a five-hour drive from all of the Midwest’s auto manufacturing hubs.

Molly Whitehead, executive director of the Boone County Economic Development Corporation, stated that Maxxis will be a valuable asset to Boone County and expressed excitement for a productive relationship in the years to come.
Maxxis will recruit for warehouse manager and warehouse supervisor positions in the Indianapolis area.

For over 50 years, Maxxis International has delivered high-quality tires to original equipment and aftermarket customers across Asia, Europe, and North America, with products distributed in approximately 180 countries.
Maxxis International employs more than 30,000 people around the world.
source_title: Maxxis Tires to open in Whitestown - Town of Whitestown

facts: Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. submitted a notification of proposed production activity to the FTZ Board on behalf of its Covington, Georgia facility.
The notification conforming to FTZ Board regulations was received on August 10, 2017.
The Nisshinbo facility is located within Site 33 of Foreign-Trade Zone (FTZ) 26 in Covington, Georgia.
The facility will be used to assemble and produce automotive brake pads.
FTZ activity would be limited to specific foreign-status materials and components and specific finished products as described in the submitted notification.
Production under FTZ procedures could exempt Nisshinbo from customs duty payments on foreign-status components used in export production.

For domestic sales of foreign-status materials/components, Nisshinbo would be able to choose duty rates during customs entry procedures that apply to brake linings and pads, and brake disc pads, with duty rates ranging from free to 2.5%.
Nisshinbo would be able to avoid duty on foreign-status components which become scrap or waste.
Customs duties could possibly be deferred or reduced on foreign-status production equipment.

Components and materials sourced from abroad include natural graphite powder, natural graphite, kaolinite clay, magnesium oxide, slaked lime, mica powder, vermiculite, synthetic zeolite, antimony trisulfide, carbon black, carbon fiber, crystalline silica, quartz, spherical silica powder, zinc powder, white fused alumina, aluminum oxide, black iron oxide, zirconium oxide, antimony trioxide, calcium fluoride, zinc sulfide, barium sulfate, calcium carbonate, rubber with calcium carbonate, sepiolite, calcium silicate, potassium titanate, salts of inorganic acids, calcium hydride, dicumyl peroxide with calcium carbonate, stearic acid from saturated acyclic monocarboxylic acids (zinc stearic acid), paints and varnishes based on synthetic polymers in a non-aqueous medium, hematite, artificial graphite plates, rods, powder and other forms for manufacturing pitch cokes, artificial graphite powder, silicone rubber coated cashew particle, phenolic resin, synthetic-amorphous silica, black silicon carbide, silicone rubber, cashew dust from cashew nut shell, friction dust from manufacturing grinding process, cellulose fiber, acrylonitrile/butadiene, tire rubber crumb, rubber powder, styrene-butadiene rubber, rock wool fiber, brake linings not mounted, ceramic fiber, microblast of other mineral substances, glass chopped strand, chopped stainless steel wire fiber, copper powder, copper fiber, bronze fiber, aluminum grain, alumina powder, atomized tin powder, tin sulfide powder, zirconium silicate, zircon flour, and backing plates/disc pads.
Backing plates and disc pads have duty rate ranges from free to 6.5%.
Public comment on the proposed production activity was invited, with a closing period for receipt of submissions on October 10, 2017.
A copy of the notification will be available for public inspection at the Office of the Executive Secretary, Foreign-Trade Zones Board in Washington, DC.
source_title:
Federal Register
       :: 
      Foreign-Trade Zone (FTZ) 26-Atlanta, Georgia; Notification of Proposed Production Activity; Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. (Automotive Brake Linings, Pads, and Disc Pads Assembly and Production); Covington, Georgia

canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Transportation Technologies Office
facts: Improving batteries for electric drive vehicles, including hybrid electric (HEV) and plug-in electric vehicles (PEV), plays a key role in the country's transportation future.
Transitioning to a light-duty fleet of HEVs and PEVs could reduce U.S. foreign oil dependence by 30-60%, depending on the exact mix of technologies.
Further improvements in batteries could make electric drive vehicles more affordable and convenient to consumers.
Some heavy-duty manufacturers are pursuing hybridization of medium and heavy-duty vehicles to improve fuel economy and reduce idling.

The Transportation Technologies Office (TTO) focuses on reducing the cost, volume, and weight of batteries, while simultaneously improving vehicle battery performance (power, energy, and durability) and tolerance to abuse conditions.
Reaching TTO's goals in battery areas and commercializing advanced energy storage technologies will allow more people to purchase and use electric drive vehicles.
TTO pursues Exploratory Battery Materials Research, which addresses fundamental issues of materials and electrochemical interactions associated with lithium and beyond-lithium batteries.

This exploratory research attempts to develop new and promising materials, use advanced material models to predict battery failure modes, and employ scientific diagnostic tools to understand why materials and systems fail.
Building on these findings, the exploratory research works to develop ways to mitigate those failures.
TTO pursues Applied Battery Research, which focuses on optimizing next-generation, high-energy lithium-ion electrochemistries that incorporate new battery materials.
This applied research emphasizes identifying, diagnosing, and mitigating issues that negatively impact the performance and lifespan of cells using advanced materials.

TTO pursues Advanced Battery Development, System Analysis, and Testing, which focuses on developing robust battery cells and modules to significantly reduce cost, increase lifespan, and improve performance.
This development research aims to ensure battery systems meet specific goals for particular vehicle applications.
The DOE's battery research builds upon decades of work conducted by the department in batteries and energy storage.
Research supported by TTO led to modern nickel metal hydride batteries, which were used in nearly all first-generation hybrid electric vehicles.
The office's research also helped develop the lithium-ion battery technology used in the Chevrolet Volt, the first commercially available plug-in hybrid electric vehicle.
This lithium-ion battery technology is now being used in a variety of hybrid and plug-in electric vehicles coming to market, including the Ford Focus EV.
The batteries subprogram works extensively with national laboratories and universities.
Within the DOE, the office collaborates with the Office of Science and ARPA-e (Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy).
Across the federal government, the subprogram works with the Interagency Advanced Power Group.
Across the federal government, the subprogram works with the Environmental Protection Agency.
Across the federal government, the subprogram works with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.
Across the federal government, the subprogram works with the National Science Foundation.
Across the federal government, the subprogram works with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (Department of Transportation).
Across the federal government, the subprogram works with the U.S. Army Tank, Automotive Research and Development and Engineering Center (Department of Defense).
The subprogram collaborates on international research with the International Energy Agency's Hybrid & Electric Vehicle Technology Collaboration Programme.
The subprogram collaborates on international research with the Clean Energy Ministerial's Electric Vehicle Initiative.
The subprogram collaborates on international research through the Clean Energy Research Center bilateral agreement between the U.S. and China.
Much of the subprogram's research is conducted in sync with industry partners through the U.S. DRIVE Partnership, focusing on light-duty vehicles.
Much of the subprogram's research is conducted in sync with industry partners through the 21st Century Truck Partnership, focusing on heavy-duty vehicles.

Much of the subprogram's research is conducted in sync with industry partners through the United States Advanced Battery Consortium (USABC), a partnership between the DOE, Chrysler, Ford, and General Motors to develop and demonstrate advanced battery technologies for hybrid and electric vehicles, as well as benchmark test emerging technologies.

TTO's Batteries and Energy Storage subprogram aims to research new battery chemistry and cell technologies that can reduce the cost of electric vehicle batteries to less than $100/kWh—ultimately $80/kWh.
TTO's Batteries and Energy Storage subprogram aims to research new battery chemistry and cell technologies that can increase the range of electric vehicles to 300 miles.
TTO's Batteries and Energy Storage subprogram aims to research new battery chemistry and cell technologies that can decrease charge time to 15 minutes or less.
source_title: Batteries | Department of Energy

entity_name: North Georgia Mountains EV Charging Infrastructure
canonical_name: North Georgia Mountains EV Charging Infrastructure
facts: The North Georgia region does not have as many chargers as the state’s major metropolitan areas.
EV drivers can power up at public and private charging stations at hotels, resorts, and attractions throughout the area.
Charging stations are located in the small towns of Blue Ridge, Ellijay, Young Harris, and Dillard.
Drivers should keep their EV’s battery fully charged and not dip below 20 percent.
Remote mountain roads have longer stretches between charging stations.
Hampton Inn Blue Ridge offers guests access to four EV chargers.
Blue Ridge Inn Bed & Breakfast is equipped with one EV charger, which is a Tesla plug-in exclusively for guests.
Gilmer Chamber & Welcome Center has three EV chargers.
Brasstown Valley Resort & Spa has two 16kW Tesla chargers.
Asiano, a restaurant in downtown Hiawassee, has a dual charging station for EVs.
Julep Farms offers one of two Gen-3 Tesla wall connectors available for guest use.
Clayton has four public charging station ports for EV vehicles.
source_title: Best Electric Vehicle Road Trips in the North Georgia Mountains | Explore Georgia

The announcement occurred earlier this week relative to April 8, 2021.
On Wednesday, the West Point Development Authority approved a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for the expansion.

facts: Inalfa Roof Systems is a supplier of vehicle roofing systems.
Inalfa Roof Systems will open a manufacturing plant in Cherokee County, Geogia.
The plant represents a $17.1 million investment.
The plant will create 300 jobs for Georgians.
Governor Nathan Deal stated that Inalfa joins the dynamic auto industry Georgia has cultivated for more than 100 years.

Governor Nathan Deal noted that Georgia's central location in the Southeast, deep workforce resources and logistics infrastructure have attracted well over 300 companies and continue to give the state a competitive advantage in this manufacturing sector.
Inalfa Roof Systems supplies sunroofs and open roofing systems Hyundai Kia, Ford, General Motors, Volvo, Chrysler Group and BMW Group.

Danny Colaris, vice president of North American Operations for Inalfa Roof Systems, stated that after a four-year search for a location in the South, the company found its ideal site in Cherokee County.

Danny Colaris noted that state and local economic development teams, as well as Georgia Power, have all been extremely helpful in introducing the company to this strategic location and the advantages of doing business in Georgia.
This facility will be Inalfa's fourth plant in the U.S.
This facility will be Inalfa's first plant in the Southeast.
Inalfa has its global headquarters based in Venray, The Netherlands.
Inalfa has a global workforce of 3,000 employees.
Inalfa’s North American headquarters is located in Michigan.
Inalfa operates a total of four plants in Michigan and Mexico.
Worldwide, Inalfa Roof Systems also has facilities in Slovakia, South Korea, China, Brazil and Japan.
Inalfa is currently also expanding its footprint in Poland.
Chris Cummiskey, Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated that investment by international companies creates jobs and contributes to the state’s economy.
Chris Cummiskey noted that commitments from top-notch businesses like Inalfa help other investors understand how Georgia can help them become successful in the U.S. market.

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Inalfa Roof Systems will open a manufacturing plant in Cherokee County.
The plant represents a $17.1 million investment and 300 jobs for Georgians.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Inalfa joins the dynamic auto industry Georgia has cultivated for more than 100 years.

The facility is strategically located off I-75 at Cherokee 75 Corporate Park in close proximity to automotive manufacturers throughout the Southeast.

This facility will be Inalfa's fourth in the United States and first in the Southeast.

The new investment and jobs created will have a significant impact on Cherokee and the northwest Atlanta region.
Wylly Harrison is the project manager for the Georgia Department of Economic Development, which partnered with the Cherokee Office of Economic Development on the project location.
Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development Chris Cummiskey stated that investment by international companies creates jobs and contributes to the state’s economy.
Chris Cummiskey noted that commitments from top-notch businesses like Inalfa help other investors understand how Georgia can help them become successful in the U.S. market.

Inalfa Roof Systems is a reliable engineering and assembly partner that leverages its global footprint to maintain relationships with customers and suppliers.
Inalfa stands for innovation, technological competence, and quality.
Due to its global presence, Inalfa is always in close proximity to its customers' production lines.

facts: Walt Farrell has been named president and chief executive officer of Atlanta Gas Light and Chattanooga Gas, effective March 31, 2025.
Farrell will serve on the Southern Company Gas Management Council.
He will oversee all aspects of the companies' southern operations, providing clean, safe, reliable, and affordable natural gas to approximately 1.8 million customers in Georgia and Tennessee.
Farrell currently serves as vice president of economic development for Georgia Power, where he leads a team dedicated to recruiting new businesses and fostering job and investment growth in Georgia.
His team supports regional economic development initiatives and pioneers innovations spanning marketing, data analysis, site analysis technologies, and engineering.

In 2024, under Farrell's leadership, Georgia Power was named a "Top Utility in Economic Development" by Site Selection magazine for the 26th consecutive year and by Business Facilities magazine for the third consecutive year.

Southern Company Gas Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer James Y. (Jim) Kerr II stated that Farrell brings critical experience and proven leadership in economic and workforce development at a crucial time for navigating natural gas's role in serving increased energy demand in Georgia and Tennessee.
In his prior role as manager of statewide economic development for Georgia Power, Farrell oversaw business recruitment and attraction efforts to bolster new business growth in the state.
Before joining Georgia Power in 2015, Farrell served as a director of the advanced manufacturing team at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).

At GDEcD, he was responsible for recruiting domestic and international companies to Georgia, leading tax incentive negotiations on Georgia's behalf, and directing the Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development Program, the state's industrial site certification program.
Prior to his work with GDEcD, Farrell worked in community and economic development for both Electric Cities of Georgia and MEAG Power.
Farrell currently sits on the board of directors for the Georgia Lottery Corporation, Georgia 4-H Foundation, CareerRise, and the Georgia Chamber of Commerce.
He also serves on the board of advisors for GDEcD, is a working partner for the Georgia Allies, and is a proud member of the Georgia Economic Developers Association.
Farrell is a Georgia Trend magazine "40 Under 40" recipient and a graduate of the Leadership Georgia Class of 2015 and the Young Game Changers Class of 2017.
Farrell holds a Master of Business Administration from Auburn University and a Bachelor of Science in marketing from Clemson University.

Southern Company Gas is a wholly owned subsidiary of Atlanta-based Southern Company (NYSE:SO) and serves approximately 4.4 million natural gas utility customers through regulated distribution companies in four states.

Southern Company Gas also serves more than 600,000 retail customers through its companies that market natural gas, with nonutility businesses including investments in interstate pipelines, asset management for natural gas wholesale customers, and ownership/operation of natural gas storage facilities.
Atlanta Gas Light provides natural gas delivery service to approximately 1.7 million customers in Georgia and has been in operation since 1856.

Chattanooga Gas provides retail natural gas sales and transportation services to approximately 71,000 customers in Hamilton and Bradley counties in southeast Tennessee, including the communities of Chattanooga, Cleveland, Red Bank, East Ridge, Lookout Mountain, and Signal Mountain.
source_title: Walt Farrell Named President and Chief Executive Officer of Atla

facts: JTEKT Corporation was established in January 2006 through the merger of Koyo Seiko Co., Ltd. and Toyoda Machine Works, Ltd.
JTEKT supplies innovative products such as steering systems, driveline components, bearings, machine tools, electronic control systems, and home accessory equipment.
JTEKT is dedicated to 'Monozukuri' (the art, science, and craft of making things) and focuses on safety, environmental sustainability, and comfort.
The Nagoya headquarters serves as the central hub for global operations, strategic planning, corporate governance, R&D direction, and administrative functions.
The Nagoya headquarters is located in the JR Gate Tower, a modern skyscraper complex directly connected to Nagoya Station.
JTEKT supports global customers through an extensive network of manufacturing plants, R&D centers, and sales offices across Asia, Europe, North America (USA, Mexico, Canada), and South America.
JTEKT has a facility located at 7 Research Dr, Greenville, SC 29607, USA.
The Greenville, SC location supports and expands JTEKT's business with major automotive OEMs and industrial customers throughout North America.
JTEKT maintains an office at Address: 3-5-8 Minami-semba, Chuo-ku, Osaka 542-8502, Japan to maintain a strong corporate presence in the Kansai region.
As of April 2025, JTEKT North America Corporation has a defined leadership structure.
JTEKT North America Corporation has been backed by several prominent investors over the years.

JTEKT announced significant changes to its Board of Directors and executive officer lineup in February 2024, effective April 1, 2024, following the Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders in June 2024.

facts: JTEKT North America (JTEKT), a subsidiary of global automotive and industrial supplier JTEKT Corporation, completed a $5.8 million expansion of its headquarters on the campus of the Clemson University International Center for Automotive Research (CU-ICAR).
The expansion project began in 2017 to accommodate new jobs JTEKT has and will continue to create in Greenville, totaling 100 positions, all of which will be filled by 2020.
JTEKT manufactures steering systems, driveline components, bearings technologies, and precision machine tools, all critical to the state’s automotive industry.
The company employs more than 6,000 associates at 24 sites across North America, including four manufacturing facilities located in South Carolina.
In 2015, JTEKT strengthened its presence in the state by announcing the consolidation of a portion of its headquarters to CU-ICAR in Greenville.
To expand that operation, the company added 19,000 square feet of office and lab space at CU-ICAR’s One Research Drive building.
This complements the 135,000 square feet of space JTEKT already occupies between two buildings on the campus.
The new space will provide work areas for associates who have relocated to Greenville, as well as the new positions currently being filled.
JTEKT is actively hiring for the new positions created by the expansion.

JTEKT Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer Mike Davidson stated that Greenville is an excellent location for their growing headquarters due to its central geography relative to their manufacturing footprint and the availability of talented professionals in business and technical fields.

Mike Davidson noted the expansion facilitates co-location of vital functions within the organization and the continuation of research and development to better serve customers and allow associates to thrive in an innovative environment.

South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster stated that JTEKT's expansion and creation of 100 new jobs make a statement that South Carolina remains an ideal destination for automobile manufacturers and suppliers.
South Carolina Secretary of Commerce Bobby Hitt stated that the state’s robust automotive supply chain continues to grow and thrive, citing JTEKT’s expansion as a perfect example of that growth.

Greenville County Council chairman and director of Greenville Area Development Corporation H.G. “Butch” Kirven Jr. stated that JTEKT’s decision to expand operations in Greenville is a testament to the dedicated workforce and strong pro-business environment.
H.G. “Butch” Kirven Jr. described JTEKT North America as a great corporate citizen and a prime example of the growing family of leading international companies located in Greenville.
source_title: JTEKT North America expands Greenville County headquarters

facts: On January 4, 2024, the Development Authority of Lumpkin County (DALC) and the Dahlonega Downtown Development Authority (DDA) hosted the Leadership Lumpkin County Economic Development Day at the JTEKT North America plant in Lumpkin County.
The event brought together 28 participants to cover economic development fundamentals and community profiles for Lumpkin County and the City of Dahlonega.

LLC Chair Deb Thompson stated that Lumpkin Leadership is an amazing program that offers even ‘nuggets’ a new perspective and a deeper understanding of our community, and called Economic Development Day key to that increased understanding.

A MythBusters Panel featured Lumpkin County Chairman Chris Dockery, Community and Economic Development Director Rebecca Mincey, Main Street Manager Ariel Alexander, and City of Dahlonega Councilman Ryan Reagin.

A Workforce Development Panel included Bobbi Larson (Director of Economic Development and Community Engagement at the University of North Georgia), Kerri Whitmire (Director of Student Services at the Lumpkin County Board of Education), and Troy Lindsey (Dawson Campus Dean at Lanier Technical College).
These workforce partners collaborate with DALC, local businesses, and community partners to prepare students for careers in Lumpkin County and the region.

Participants toured two local manufacturers, JTEKT North America and Big Creek Distilling Company, which contribute to Lumpkin County through capital investment, corporate responsibility, and workforce development.
The event highlighted the diversity of industries and collaborative efforts involved in bringing new businesses to the community to support the local economic engine.

source_title: DALC hosts Leadership Lumpkin County Economic Development Day – Development Authority of Lumpkin County

facts: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG, a Textron Inc. company, announced it has received an "A-" Climate Change rating from CDP.
Kautex announced its sustainability roadmap in November 2021, committing to target an 80 percent reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions and a 30 percent reduction in Scope 3 emissions by 2030.
The company aspires to be net-zero by 2050 in all three scopes.
In 2022, Kautex challenged its 30-plus plants worldwide to reduce their Scope 1 and 2 emissions through practical steps like energy audits and strategic investments in renewable energy.
In 2022, all the Company's facilities on mainland Europe, China, Mexico and in Lavonia, GA are sourcing renewable energy – a significant step in reducing Scope 2 emissions.

The company joined CDP as a Supply Chain Corporate member and is challenging its suppliers to join CDP to disclose their own environmental impact and take concrete steps to reduce their impact over time.

Sylvain Genesi, Kautex's vice president, Global Supply Chain and Sustainability, stated that the company took significant steps to improve its own Scope 1 and 2 emissions and drive its suppliers to do the same.

Sophie Louis, global director, Energy and Sustainability, noted that Kautex's sustainability commitments are a commitment to customers and that as a Tier One supplier, their emissions directly impact the overall emissions of the OEM.
To assist customers with product life cycle assessment, Kautex developed an application to help customers estimate the carbon impact of their Kautex-designed products.

As a Tier One automotive supplier with more than 30 plants in 14 countries, Kautex designs, develops and manufactures traditional and hybrid fuel systems, advanced cleaning solutions for assisted and autonomous driving, engine camshafts and plastic industrial packaging solutions.
Kautex is expanding its portfolio to offer smart products and data-driven services to customers, including smart fuel systems, thermoplastic composite and composite-metal hybrid battery systems.
From a lightweight battery system to a hybrid fuel system to autonomous drive vehicle cleaning systems, Kautex is committed to pioneering solutions for the era of new mobility.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.
source_title: TXT Press Release: Kautex Textron GmbH & Co. KG Receives A- Clima...

facts: The facility is America’s largest car manufacturing plant, with nearly everything powered by AI.
Drones are used to buzz and scan stacked pallets to inventory parts.
Robotic arms swivel through their programmed arcs.
Cameras direct sanding tools to paint blemishes.
Uncrewed taxis move vehicles and parts to their next manufacturing destination.
A four-legged Boston Dynamics AI dog named Spot walks through the facility and scans components to ensure they are snapped into place.
The facility, called Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, is roughly equivalent to 278 football fields and has a nearly $7.6 billion price tag.
The HMGMA facility celebrated its grand opening in March.
Unlike most US automakers that are retrofitting decade-old plants with AI and robotics, Hyundai built HMGMA from the ground up with AI, Nvidia chips, and robotics at its core.
Each vehicle built in the plant runs through at least 23 AI-based or robotic systems before it is finalized.
Industry analysts stated that Hyundai’s AI-first approach could be a boon for navigating policy changes and customer trends that impact the carmaker’s bottom line.
The technology can help improve quality control measures and reduce manufacturing costs in ways that traditional manufacturing cannot match.
Erin Keating, an executive analyst at Cox Automotive, stated that Hyundai’s integration of humanoid robots sets a new benchmark for smart manufacturing.
Keating noted potential cost savings and the ability for Hyundai and others to respond faster to any industrial disruption.
At the center of Hyundai’s Georgia operation is an integrated digital command hub acting as a digital twin that mirrors the physical plant in real time.
Sensors collect data from across the production floor, and an AI algorithm creates a virtual replica of each step in the real-life manufacturing process.
Miles Johnson stated that AI can play a significant role in predicting optimized outcomes and identifying root causes of production issues.
If a quality issue arises in production, the AI system can search past data it has been trained on to identify the likely cause and suggest a corrective action plan.
Keating said Hyundai’s AI can detect defects in real time, analyze root causes, and suggest corrective actions that can significantly reduce rework, scrap, and warranty claims.
The brand’s two retro-modern electric vehicles, the Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9 SUVs, are built with these highly technical processes.
The plant will eventually serve Hyundai’s other brands as well, with Kia production set to begin in 2026 and Genesis vehicles planned for an unspecified future date.
In a press release, Hyundai stated that the AI and robotics systems will assist humans, who will remain in charge of on-the-ground robotics maintenance, data analysis, and parts installation.
Hundreds of roles now involve programming the factory’s 850 robots, troubleshooting AI, or interpreting production data rather than performing repetitive physical tasks.
The company aims to hire 8,500 employees at HMGMA by 2031 and has dubbed the project the largest single business investment in Georgia’s history.
In March, Hyundai said it had already hired 1,000 full-time staff members at the plant.
The company built a nearby Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia to train a pipeline of college-level talent for the plant.
The training program, coordinated with three local technical colleges, is expected to open this year.
Hyundai’s massive AI investment faces significant challenges in the current automotive market.
Both the Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9 were positioned to benefit from then-President Joe Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act.
The cars are both eligible for the $7,500 tax credits for EVs assembled in American plants.
Americans’ uptake of EVs has underperformed analysts’ predictions since Hyundai broke ground on HMGMA in October 2022.
EV adoption is facing more federal pushback as subsidies for EVs are set to end in September.
Hyundai is betting that its EVs, including the three-row Ioniq 9 which launched in May 2025, will attract buyers without the need for tax incentives.
Some industry analysts say Hyundai’s AI-powered manufacturing can still work even if it currently relies on EV sales.

AI’s advantages, like real-time quality control and faster problem solving, could prove crucial as automakers face pressure from increasingly cheap Chinese vehicles and ongoing supply chain volatility.
Morningstar analyst David Whiston said that companies adopting AI for manufacturing and logistics are better positioned to manage costs and respond to disruptions.

Keating said automakers leveraging AI for smart factories, autonomous logistics, and predictive analytics will be better positioned to scale production efficiently and meet regulatory and consumer demands faster.
Keating stated that AI and robotics are becoming non-negotiable for competitiveness.
source_title: Hyundai built its newest factory around AI-powered technology like robotics and digital twins - B17 News

facts: Macon-Bibb County is preparing for a major manufacturing expansion that state and local leaders say will bring a $125 million investment and at least 500 new jobs to Middle Georgia.
Georgia-based Unified Legacy plans to build a new manufacturing facility off Barnes Ferry Road, near Hawkinsville Road, in Macon.
Construction is expected to begin in 2026, following an announcement from Gov. Brian Kemp’s office.
Unified Legacy manufactures products and components for the defense, aerospace, data center, and industrial markets.
The company already operates United Defense, a manufacturing facility in Byron that opened in 2022.
Gov. Brian Kemp said Georgia’s manufacturing sector continues to grow because companies benefit from the state’s workforce, infrastructure, and partnership approach.
Unified Legacy CEO Eric Williams said the Macon expansion reflects confidence in the region and will allow the company to handle larger and more complex projects as demand grows.
The company expects to hire workers in manufacturing, skilled trades, engineering, logistics, quality control, and administrative positions.
CEO Eric Williams stated that the goal is to create careers people can be proud of while building a workforce capable of meeting higher standards.
Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller welcomed the announcement, saying economic development and workforce development remain top priorities for the community.
source_title: New Manufacturing Plant Promises $125 Million Investment and 500 Jobs in Macon – 360News.com

facts: Mack Trucks is investing $14.5 million to expand its Roanoke Valley Operations (RVO) manufacturing facility in Virginia.
The expansion prepares the plant for higher demand for the Mack MD Series and Mack MD Electric vehicles.
Stephen Roy, global president of Mack Trucks, stated that Mack is committed to making industrial and product investments to be a North American market leader.
Roy said the RVO plant expansion will help grow in a strategic market segment and support sustainability goals.
Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin announced the $14.5 million investment during an event with state and local officials at RVO.
Gov. Youngkin approved a $255,000 grant from the Commonwealth’s Opportunity Fund for the project.
The project will result in 51 jobs.
Roanoke County offered a package of incentives totaling $842,420.
Gov. Youngkin said Mack Trucks’ expansion strengthens Virginia’s manufacturing industry ecosystem, which is a core focus of the Commonwealth’s economic development strategy.
Youngkin noted that Mack's initial investment in a new Roanoke County operation four years ago has yielded a second major investment, demonstrating confidence in Virginia.
Mack’s investment will fund equipment, tooling, and a 72,000 square-foot building expansion.
The facility expansion increases the total size of RVO to 352,000 square feet.
Construction on the expansion is scheduled to begin in April 2024.
The expansion project is expected to be completed in Q4 2025.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Roanoke County Economic Development and the Roanoke Regional Partnership to secure the project.
The Virginia Jobs Investment Program will support employee training activities for the project.
Mack is eligible to receive state benefits from the Major Business Facility Job Tax Credit for new full-time jobs created as a result of the project.
All Mack medium-duty models are produced at RVO.
Mack launched the diesel-powered Mack MD Series in 2020.
Mack launched the Mack MD Electric in March 2023.
Adding the new model to the product lineup increased the need for more space at the facility.
Roy stated that customer demand for the Mack MD and MD Electric continues to grow beyond expectations.
The investment and expansion will position Mack Trucks for the future amid growing demand.
The diesel-powered Mack MD and MD Electric are available in Class 6 and Class 7 ratings.
The Class 6 model has a Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) of 25,995 pounds.
The Class 7 model has a GVWR of 33,000 pounds.
Both models are exempt from the 12 percent Federal Excise Tax (FET).
The MD and MD Electric were developed specifically for medium-duty applications with a focus on reliability, durability, and productivity.
The trucks will serve trucking applications requiring dry van/refrigerated, stake/flatbed, and dump vocations.
The MD6 model does not require a Commercial Driver’s License (CDL) to operate for non-hazardous payloads.
Mack Trucks has provided purpose-built transportation solutions for 125 years.
Mack is one of North America’s largest producers of heavy-duty trucks.
Mack trucks are sold and serviced through an extensive distribution network in more than 45 countries.
Mack trucks, diesel engines, and transmissions sold in North America are assembled in the United States.
Mack manufacturing locations are certified to ISO 9001 (quality), ISO 14001 (environmental management), ISO 45001 (health and safety), and ISO 50001 (energy management).
Mack is a sponsor of Share the Road, an American Trucking Associations public information campaign aimed at enhancing roadway safety.
Mack Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, which offers trucks, buses, construction equipment, power solutions, financing, and services.
The Volvo Group was founded in 1927, is headquartered in Gothenburg, Sweden, employs about 100,000 people, and serves customers in almost 180 markets.
In 2025, the Volvo Group's net sales amounted to about $49 billion.
source_title: Mack Invests $14.5 Million to Expand Roanoke Valley Operations

facts: Mack Trucks announced it has achieved a milestone of more than 200,000 Class 8 trucks built with its proprietary telematics gateway since 2014.
Connected Mack trucks are supported around the clock by Mack OneCall agents at the company's 24/7 Uptime Center.
Jonathan Randall serves as president of Mack Trucks North America.
Connected vehicles include diagnostics through Mack GuardDog Connect, remote software updates via Mack Over The Air, and fleet monitoring services through the Mack Connect portal.
The proprietary telematics gateway has been standard on all Mack trucks with Mack engines since late 2014.

Mack GuardDog Connect monitors critical fault codes, immediately notifies customers of issues requiring attention, categorizes alerts by severity, and provides detailed repair information for informed service decisions.
Mack Over The Air (OTA) provides remote software update capability that has demonstrated a reduction of unplanned downtime by more than 24%.
The Mack Connect portal provides real-time visibility into vehicle location, fuel economy, and safety metrics to enable fleet managers to optimize operations.

The connected vehicle portal supports proactive maintenance services through Mack's service contracts using real-time vehicle data to anticipate maintenance needs based on actual operating conditions.
Patrick Brown serves as director of strategy at Mack Trucks.
Mack Trucks has provided purpose-built transportation solutions for more than a century and is one of North America’s largest producers of heavy-duty trucks.
Mack trucks are sold and serviced through an extensive distribution network in more than 45 countries.
Mack trucks, diesel engines, and transmissions sold in North America are assembled in the United States.
Mack manufacturing locations are certified to ISO 9001 for quality, ISO 14001 for environmental management systems, and ISO 45001 for health and safety management systems.
Mack is a sponsor of Share the Road, an American Trucking Associations public information campaign aimed at enhancing roadway safety.
Mack Trucks is part of the Volvo Group, which employs about 104,000 people and serves customers in almost 190 markets.
In 2023, the Volvo Group's net sales amounted to about $52 billion.
source_title: Mack Trucks Reaches Major Milestone with 200,000 Connected Vehicles

MAC will add an additional $35 million for equipment and building improvements at its North American Headquarters over the next three years.
The expansion promises 67 new jobs.
With this expansion, MAC's total investment in Opelika will exceed $190 million.
When the expansion is complete, MAC will employ nearly 700 people.
Opelika Mayor Gary Fuller stated that Mando's continued confidence and additional investment are extremely honored by the city.
Mayor Fuller noted that MAC is one of Opelika's largest manufacturing employers and appreciated the employment opportunities for citizens.
The Mando America plant in Opelika produces brake corner modules, brake calipers, suspension modules, and steering systems.
Mando America is headquartered in Korea.
The company operates 17 production plants and 8 R&D centers across 10 countries.

facts: Mando, a part of Halla Group, has opened a new auto parts facility in Meriwether, Georgia, US marking its second plant in the state.
The new plant can accommodate 170,000 rack type-electronic power steering (R-EPS) systems and 600,000 electronic stability controls (ESC).
It also comprises a steel-casting facility capable of producing 18,000 tons of goods per year.
Mando plans to expand the plant's ESC capacity to one million units over the next four years.
The facility will make electronic components that are used in half-a-million vehicles annually.
A Mando source said: "The latest plant will permit the car parts manufacturer to better cope with moves by automakers to make more upscale, sophisticated cars."
Apart from the US, Mando's operations are also spread across Poland, India, Brazil, Turkey and China.

facts: HL Mando said on the 6th it will push in earnest to commercialize its artificial intelligence (AI)-based electric fire prevention solution "e-HAECHIE."
HAECHIE secured stability and reliability through proof of concept (PoC) at 17 factories in Korea.
Mass production of the HAECHIE system will begin in June.
Hyundai Motor Group is the first customer for the HAECHIE system.
The HAECHIE system will be applied at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Georgia.
An HL Mando official stated that remote handling is essential because the plant is an unmanned automated facility.
HL Mando's HAECHIE was selected for its integrated disaster-prevention process covering arc detection, monitoring, judgment, and control.
The mass-production version of HAECHIE has evolved into a "smart disaster-prevention consultant."
While the early model was a simple arc detection system, the mass-production version introduces AI to analyze arc characteristics and patterns and even assess risk levels.
Depending on the assessed risk level, the system can perform essential preemptive measures such as inspection, equipment replacement, and changes to operating conditions.
The system incorporates infrared sensors and thermal imaging cameras to track and detect even minute temperature changes.
HL Mando will unveil the mass-production product at the 2026 International Electric Power Exhibition held at COEX in Seoul from the 6th to the 8th.
source_title: HL Mando deploys AI fire-prevention system at Hyundai’s US Metaplant - CHOSUNBIZ

facts: General Motors presented HL Mando with a 2024 Supplier of the Year Award at GM's 33rd annual Supplier of the Year event in Phoenix, Arizona, on April 8, 2025.
HL Mando has received the Supplier of the Year award for five consecutive years and 11 times in total.
HL Mando was recognized in the Brake Apply & Controls category for its Motor on Caliper (MoC) product.
The Motor on Caliper (MoC) product is manufactured at HL Mando's Alabama plant.
General Motors praised HL Mando's localization efforts and technological innovation.
HL Mando's North American localization journey began in 1996.
HL Mando established its first R&D center, Mando Engineering & Sales America (MESA), in Michigan to lay the groundwork for North American expansion.
HL Mando launched its first manufacturing facility, Mando Corporation America (MCA) in Alabama, and won its first Supplier of the Year award in 2003.
Following the completion of the Alabama plant in 2004, a second production facility was added in 2012 to meet growing demand for casting components, which became the MCA Georgia Plant.
In 2013, HL Mando expanded its R&D capabilities by establishing a second R&D center, HL Mechatronics (HM), in Michigan.
MESA focuses on development activities up to mass production, while HM specializes in future-oriented automotive technologies.
HL Mando has recently invested approximately USD 700 million in its North American operations.

Jeff Morrison, GM's global chief procurement officer, stated that GM recognizes top global suppliers annually to spotlight innovation and resiliency, and together they are bringing advanced technology and a broad portfolio of vehicles to market for GM customers.

GM's Supplier of the Year awards recognize global suppliers for execution across safety, innovation, and resilience, with selections based on 2024 performance and alignment to GM's core values and goals.

facts: HL Mando America Corp. was recognized for its support of Auburn University, the Samuel Ginn College of Engineering and the Department of Mechanical Engineering.

HL Mando committed $200,000 to support up to four graduate students participating in an accelerated bachelor’s and master’s degree program in mechanical engineering at Auburn who have a special interest in automotive technologies.
HL Mando is based in Seoul, South Korea, and operates as an electric vehicle and autonomous driving solutions company specializing in core components for vehicle safety.

HL Mando is a global leader in key enabling technologies for future mobility including redundancy concept and by-wire technology and strengthens eco-friendly vehicle solutions such as e-Drive and hydrogen fuel cell converters.
HL Mando America is HL Mando’s US-based subsidiary with manufacturing operations located in Opelika and Hogansville, Georgia.
HL Mando America produces brake calipers, electric steering systems and suspension systems.
HL Mando America executives, including CEO Bruce Kim and Head of Americas Region Yun Lee, attended a reception on Monday, Aug. 5 hosted by Mario Eden, dean of engineering.
During the reception, HL Mando presented the college with a ceremonial check.

Yun Lee stated that they consider it a blessing to have an esteemed institution such as Auburn University in their vicinity and noted great experiences partnering with the Department of Mechanical Engineering and the Office of International Programs.
Yun Lee expressed hope that their contributions will help prepare tomorrow’s leaders in the automotive industry, both locally and globally.
HL Mando has established a long-standing relationship with Song-Yul Choe, the William B. and Elizabeth Reed Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering.
Song-Yul Choe’s research interests include modeling/control of power sources, piezoelectric devices, high powered connectors, solenoid valves and tires.
Mario Eden expressed gratitude for the ongoing support of partners at HL Mando, noting that industry leader support is vital to preparing graduate students to enter the workforce as leaders.
source_title: HL Mando America Corp. recognized for support of Auburn mechanical engineering

facts: MAT Augusta is developing a phased fleet operations hub to support high-utilization electric and future autonomous transportation fleets with charging, staging, cleaning, light maintenance, dispatch support, route readiness, and local field rescue.
The infrastructure is platform-agnostic, designed for EV fleets today and autonomous deployments tomorrow.
The project focuses on the local execution layer that large platforms and fleet owners still need.
Development is built around phased site control, power planning, partner capital, and recurring fleet services.

The concept has evolved into a vehicle-agnostic EV/AV fleet operations platform to create a local hub where mobility platforms, fleet operators, charging partners, energy partners, and regional stakeholders can each participate in a practical transportation infrastructure model.
Managed EV charging, secure parking, public/private site separation, and staged fleet positioning will be provided for daily operations.
Cleaning and readiness services will cover cabin reset, exterior cleaning, vehicle condition checks, lost-item process, and route-ready preparation.
Light maintenance will include tire pressure, wipers, washer fluid, filters, basic inspections, minor service coordination, and dealer/OEM referral for major repairs.
Dispatch support will encompass route readiness, check-in/check-out workflows, driver/operator support, reporting, and local operating coordination.
Field rescue services will involve tow coordination, incident response workflows, mobile support relationships, and local issue resolution for fleet partners.
Future AV support capabilities will include secure depot planning, sensor/camera cleaning protocols, readiness logs, field support, and autonomous fleet support capability.
MAT's role is to control the local opportunity, organize the site, operate the depot layer, and align capital-heavy partners where they already know how to make money.
For fleet operators, MAT can provide a predictable place to charge, stage, clean, inspect, and prepare revenue-generating vehicles.
For mobility and AV platforms, MAT can become a local execution partner for secondary-market deployment, reducing the need to build every local support function from scratch.
The site will create a mix of public-facing charging, private fleet charging, and high-utilization demand for charging partners.
Long-term fleet load will support evaluation of solar, battery storage, demand management, and energy-resilience structures for energy partners.

Augusta is large enough to support transportation demand but more manageable than a major metro, featuring clear movement patterns, major institutional anchors, and airport-adjacent infrastructure potential.
Augusta Regional Airport creates a logical location for transportation staging, public charging, fleet access, and visitor-facing mobility services.
Demand anchors include Fort Eisenhower, the medical district, universities, downtown, hospitality corridors, and regional events creating recurring transportation needs.
Masters Week surge in Augusta creates a unique stress test for transportation readiness, staging, and fleet coordination.
MAT is being designed as a practical model for markets that need EV/AV fleet infrastructure but may not be first-wave mega-city deployments.
The project is being evaluated around utility load, phased growth, public/private charging structure, and long-term infrastructure control.
If proven in Augusta, the model could be repeated in other airport-adjacent secondary markets with similar transportation gaps.

MAT Augusta is focused on establishing the project foundation before major capital deployment, covering site control, power feasibility, fleet economics, partner requirements, and phased development structure.
Site planning involves evaluating long-term ground lease potential, access, public/private separation, security, stormwater, signage, and expansion rights.
Power and charging feasibility efforts include coordinating load planning, phased electrical service, charger mix, metering, managed charging, and future energy resilience.
Partner requirements involve engaging mobility, fleet, charging, energy, and local service partners to design around real operating standards.
Financial discipline focuses on vehicle-month economics, contribution margin, partner-funded infrastructure, and repeatable development value.

MAT Augusta welcomes conversations with transportation fleet operators, mobility platforms, charging infrastructure companies, energy partners, airport/regional stakeholders, and local service vendors.

The VPC facilities serve as the first stop for new imported Mercedes-Benz vehicles destined for Mercedes-Benz dealerships throughout the United States.
The facility is located in Glynn County on the south side of Colonels Island.
The Brunswick VPC is strategically located to process vehicles headed for Mercedes-Benz dealerships throughout the Southern region.
The VPC has the ability to perform vehicle inspections, factory campaigns, full paint, body and mechanical repairs.
Mercedes-Benz USA operates two other VPCs in the United States: Baltimore, Maryland and Long Beach, California.

facts: German luxury automobile maker Mercedes-Benz said Tuesday that it's moving its U.S. headquarters from New Jersey to Atlanta, in part to be closer to its manufacturing facility in Alabama.
Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal said the company accepted an incentive package from the state but said he couldn't discuss the details yet.
The Fulton County Development Authority met briefly Tuesday to discuss its own incentive package for Mercedes' estimated $93 million facility but provided no details.

The decision comes after weeks of lobbying — some of it public — by New Jersey officials who sought to keep the company in Montvale, at a campus that's about a five-minute drive from BMW's North American headquarters.
About 1,000 jobs are to be moved starting in July.
The company said it will move first to a temporary facility in Atlanta before moving into a new space in about two years.
Deal and Georgia economic development officials would not discuss the location Mercedes is pursuing in metro Atlanta.
Mercedes said it would announce more details later this month.

Mercedes-Benz USA President and CEO Stephen Cannon said in a statement that the company will benefit by being closer to its growing base of customers in the Southeast as well as its port in Brunswick, Georgia, and its manufacturing facility in Alabama.
Cannon praised New Jersey and the company said some operational areas will remain in Montvale and Robbinsville.

Cannon stated in a statement that while the state worked tirelessly with them during their evaluation, it became apparent that to achieve sustained, profitable growth and efficiencies for the decades ahead, their headquarters would have to be located elsewhere.
Cannon also said the quality of life, schools and cultural options in Atlanta were reasons to move there.

Other recent Georgia projects in the automotive industry include the construction of a new U.S. headquarters for Porsche near Hartsfield-Jackson Airport and Kia's first U.S. manufacturing facility about 75 miles southwest of Atlanta.
Executives have cited access to the world's busiest airport for employee travel and to the state's ports for shipping.
Deal credited the state's access to that infrastructure and an insistence on cooperation between economic development, utility and education officials for the results.
Deal said the most memorable portion of the state's pursuit was being told that the automaker was seriously considering Georgia for its U.S. headquarters.
Deal said Mercedes-Benz approached Georgia officials several months ago.
Deal said "The prestige associated with that name is exciting to me."
New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie had a series of meetings and calls with Cannon to try to keep the company in New Jersey, said Michael Drewniak, a spokesman for the governor.

Drewniak stated that in each conversation Mercedes USA made it clear that the cost of doing business and the tax environment is just too high in New Jersey to be competitive with a state like Georgia.
source_title: Mercedes Announces Decision To Move U.S. Headquarters To GA | Manufacturing.net

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Mercedes-Benz will establish Atlanta as its headquarters in North America by centralizing and uniting key corporate functions.
The company will move up to 500 jobs to its existing facility known as “1MB” in Fulton County.
Mercedes-Benz will make a multi-million dollar investment in a future state-of-the-art Research & Development (R&D) facility located near the 1MB site.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia continues to lead in the future of mobility and technical innovation by attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that Mercedes-Benz, which already has close ties to Georgia, is doubling down on its presence in the state.
Mercedes-Benz opened its “1MB” facility in Sandy Springs in 2018.
The existing “1MB” facility currently supports approximately 800 jobs in Georgia.
Jason Hoff, CEO of Mercedes-Benz North America, thanked the State of Georgia for supporting the deepening of the company's roots in the Atlanta area.
Jason Hoff stated that the expansion strengthens the company's position for continued growth and reinforces its commitment to the U.S. market.
Jason Hoff noted that consolidating teams will enable greater agility, faster speed to market, and improved customer experience.
The “1MB” facility in Sandy Springs will house existing sales teams, financial services teams, and corporate functions.
The new state-of-the-art Research & Development hub will be located near Sandy Springs.
Mercedes-Benz anticipates completing the move to metro Atlanta by August 2026.
Mayor Rusty Paul of Sandy Springs expressed excitement over Mercedes-Benz's expansion in the city.
Mayor Rusty Paul noted that since establishing their headquarters in Sandy Springs in 2018, Mercedes-Benz has been an outstanding corporate partner.
Mayor Rusty Paul stated that the expansion highlights the success of Sandy Springs' recent infrastructure and capital investments.
Mayor Rusty Paul described the expansion as a wonderful opportunity and significant milestone for the city's continued development.
Chairman Robb Pitts of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners stated that Mercedes-Benz's investment and growth reaffirm the strength and vitality of the community.
Chairman Robb Pitts noted that the expansion proves Fulton County remains a destination for corporate solutions with an accessible, vibrant, and growing community.

Katie Kirkpatrick, President & CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated that the expansion testifies to Mercedes-Benz's commitment to excellence and metro Atlanta's strength as an innovation and talent hub.

Katie Kirkpatrick noted that when the 1MB facility opened in 2018, it quickly became an integral part of the local business landscape, driving economic growth and elevating the region's global presence.
Katie Kirkpatrick expressed pride in seeing the partnership deepen as Mercedes-Benz continues to invest in the region's future success.
Assistant Director of Statewide Projects Elizabeth McLean represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project.
The GDEcD partnered with the City of Sandy Springs, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power for this competitive project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that since relocating Mercedes-Benz USA to Sandy Springs in 2018, the company has become an integral part of the local business community.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Mercedes-Benz's continued growth and community involvement exemplify why the state recruits industry leaders like them.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the expansion and R&D commitment will strengthen the company's long-term success and highlight talent and partnerships fostered by the University System of Georgia.
source_title: Mercedes-Benz Establishing North American Headquarters, New Research & Development Hub in Metro Atlanta - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: Mercedes-Benz is establishing Atlanta as its North American headquarters.

The company will add 500 jobs to its existing facility.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated: “Georgia continues to lead the way in the future of mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz that are driving the automotive industry forward.”

Gov. Brian Kemp stated: “We’re excited that a job creator that already has close ties to Georgia is doubling down on that choice and growing their presence here in the best state for business and opportunity.”
The facility known as “1MB” currently houses 829 employees.
The employee count at 1MB will increase with new hires and the new R&D hub.
1MB will be known as Mercedes-Benz USA (MBUSA) and will be responsible for the distribution, development, and marketing of all Mercedes-Benz products in the United States.
The new R&D hub will bring engineering and technical talent to Georgia.
The R&D hub will create opportunities to collaborate with Atlanta’s tech ecosystem.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: “Since the strategic decision to relocate Mercedes-Benz USA to Sandy Springs in 2018, we have watched Mercedes-Benz become an integral part of our business community.”

The office located in Sandy Springs aims to complete the transition in August 2026.
The transition involves consolidating all of Mercedes-Benz's North American operations.
Mayor Rusty Paul of the City of Sandy Springs stated: “We’re excited to see Mercedes-Benz expanding in Sandy Springs.”
Mayor Rusty Paul stated: “Since establishing their headquarters here in 2018, they have been outstanding corporate partners.”
Mayor Rusty Paul stated: “Their decision to grow in Sandy Springs highlights the success of the city’s recent infrastructure and capital investments, which are now clearly paying dividends.”
Mayor Rusty Paul stated: “This expansion represents a wonderful opportunity and a significant milestone for our continued development.”
