facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, a division of Panasonic Corporation of North America, collaborates with Totem USA to bring Totem’s first UL-certified eBike “Zen Rider” to the U.S. market.
In 2019, Panasonic established the industry’s first e-Bike safety standard development organization and has led safety for science companies.
The Totem USA eBike “Zen Rider” is powered by Panasonic systems, providing consumers an affordable, family-friendly option to meet rising eBike market demands.
NPD research indicates a 240% growth rate for electric bicycles within the 12 months leading up to July 12, 2021.

Derek Arciniaga, senior manager of business development at Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America, announced Totem USA’s first eBike with a UL certified Panasonic system to expand mobility transformation in the US.
The Zen Rider features a low step-through mounting, making its design and motor/battery system especially suited for urban areas.
Panasonic’s second UL 2849 motor & battery system includes a 2-Axle motor + battery eBike system.
The system provides 75 Nm of max torque and a 404 Wh battery.
The system offers pedal assistance up to 15mph.
The collaboration between Totem USA and Panasonic is ready to design, develop, and produce custom eBikes for companies interested in UL certified eBikes.
The latest Panasonic drive system is suitable for two-wheel and three-wheel city tour or cargo eBike variants.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive collaborate with Totem USA to design First UL-Certified E-Bike

facts: The 2026 Tesla Model 3 is an electric compact sedan recognized as one of the first and most recognizable players in its segment.
Core strengths include quick acceleration, long-range estimates, and impressively fast charging times.
The vehicle recently received suspension and interior quality updates to maintain its position at the top of its class.
Design features a clean exterior and interior with few hard buttons or controls, following a minimalist approach.
Traditional driver-centric instruments like the speedometer are positioned on a single center-mounted display.
Apple CarPlay and Android Auto connectivity are absent from the vehicle.
For 2026, Tesla resurrected the base Model 3 trim with mild adjustments to the trim roster.
The former Model 3 Long Range RWD and AWD trims are now named Premium RWD and AWD.
The Performance trim remains the top of the lineup.

Base trim cost-cutting measures include a manually adjustable steering wheel, textile seat inserts instead of full faux-leather, front-seat-only heating, no ventilation for any position, removal of the rear infotainment screen and premium audio, and adaptive cruise control lacking lane-keeping functionality.
Pricing starts at $38,630 and goes up to $56,630 depending on trim and options.
The rear-wheel-drive, single-motor Model 3 Premium delivers 363 miles of EPA-estimated range.
Moving to the AWD variant requires an upcharge and reduces range slightly to 346 miles.
All Model 3s come standard with heated and ventilated front seats, navigation, and Tesla's Autopilot driver-assist system.
Available EV motors include a 286-hp single motor, a 425-hp dual motor, and a 510-hp dual motor.
Drivetrain options are rear-wheel-drive and all-wheel-drive.
The battery is carried under the floor, resulting in a low center of gravity that improves handling stability.
Steering offers three different settings to adjust the level of effort.
The Performance trim includes a sport-tuned suspension with adaptive dampers, upgraded brakes, and staggered wheels with grippier tires.
C/D 0-60 mph test times are 2.8 seconds for the Performance, 4.6 seconds for the Premium Long Range RWD, and 5.4 seconds for the Base RWD.
C/D real-world highway range tests yielded 310 miles for the Premium Long Range RWD, 270 miles for the Base RWD, and 260 miles for the Dual Motor Performance.
EPA EV range estimates range from 309 to 363 miles.
Charging options include Tesla's Supercharger network, adapters for public charging stations, and home-charging stations.
On a DC fast charger, a dual-motor Performance model replenished its battery from 10 percent to 90 percent in 39 minutes.
The base Model 3 has a maximum charging rate of 225 kW, while the rest of the lineup charges at up to 250 kW.
C/D 75-mph highway fuel-economy tests yielded 128 MPGe for the Premium Long Range RWD and 91 MPGe for the Dual Motor Performance.
EPA city ratings range from 120 to 145 MPGe.
EPA highway ratings range from 107 to 128 MPGe.
Various versions are rated between 113 MPGe and 138 MPGe by the EPA.
The Model 3 comes standard with aluminum wheels covered by plastic aerodynamic hubcaps that surprisingly improve driving range.
Interior sound levels measure 29 dBA at idle, 71 dBA at full throttle, and 69 dBA during 70-mph cruising.
Front row features a flat floor, minimalistic design cues, and a monolithic touchscreen with supportive front seats.
Rear seats are positioned low and cramped, making them less accommodating for adults.
Cargo capacity includes 3 cubic feet in the front trunk and 21 cubic feet in the rear trunk, expandable by folding the 60/40 rear seat.
Infotainment features a 15.4-inch touchscreen with Bluetooth and USB connectivity.
Audio systems include a standard 9-speaker stereo and an optional 17-speaker Performance stereo.
The vehicle is not available with AM radio or SiriusXM satellite radio but includes internet-streaming radio, Netflix, YouTube, and arcade games on the central display.
Premium and Performance trims feature an 8.0-inch touchscreen mounted to the rear of the center console for rear-seat climate and infotainment control.
Autopilot driver-assist system features smooth operation and capabilities like automatic lane changes.
Standard safety features include adaptive cruise control, automated emergency braking, and lane-departure warning.
Tesla's warranty coverage does not include corrosion protection.
The Long Range model includes an additional 20,000 miles of coverage for its battery and drive components.
Limited warranty covers four years or 50,000 miles.
Powertrain warranty covers eight years or 100,000 miles.
Complimentary maintenance is not included.

The 2026 Base Model features a rear-motor, RWD layout, 5-passenger capacity, 4-door sedan body, base price of $38,380, 286 hp permanent-magnet synchronous AC motor, 322 lb-ft torque, 70 kWh liquid-cooled lithium-ion battery pack, peak charge rate of 11.5 kW AC/225 kW DC, direct-drive transmission, multilink suspension F/R, 12.6-in front/13.2-in rear vented disc brakes, Hankook Ventus S1 AS tires (235/45R-18), wheelbase of 113.2 in, length of 185.8 in, width of 72.8 in, height of 56.7 in, passenger volume of 56/41 ft3, trunk volume of 21 ft3, front trunk volume of 3 ft3, and curb weight of 3721 lb.

C/D test results for the Base Model show 0-60 mph in 5.4 sec, 0-100 mph in 12.0 sec, 1/4-mile in 13.7 sec @ 107 mph, top speed of 126 mph, braking 70-0 mph in 166 ft, braking 100-0 mph in 339 ft, and skidpad grip of 0.88 g.

Fuel economy and charging data for the Base Model shows observed 109 MPGe, 75-mph highway range of 270 mi, average DC fast-charge rate of 79 kW (10-90%), DC fast-charge time of 44 min (10-90%), EPA combined/city/highway ratings of 139/147/130 MPGe, and range of 321 mi.

The 2024 Model 3 Performance features a front and rear motor AWD layout, 5-passenger capacity, 4-door sedan body, base price of $56,630, combined power of 510 hp, combined torque of 554 lb-ft, 79.7 kWh liquid-cooled lithium-ion battery pack, 11.5 kW onboard charger, peak DC fast-charge rate of 250 kW, direct-drive transmission F/R, multilink suspension F/R, Pirelli P Zero PZ4 Elect tires (F: 235/35ZR-20, R: 275/30R-20), wheelbase of 113.2 in, length of 185.8 in, width of 72.8 in, height of 56.7 in, passenger volume of 56/41 ft3, trunk volume of 3/21 ft3, and curb weight of 4046 lb.

C/D test results for the Performance model show 0-60 mph in 2.8 sec, 0-100 mph in 6.8 sec, 1/4-mile in 11.0 sec @ 125 mph, 130 mph in 12.3 sec, 150 mph in 18.3 sec, top speed of 164 mph, braking 70-0 mph in 163 ft, braking 100-0 mph in 338 ft, and skidpad grip of 0.92 g.

Fuel economy and charging data for the Performance model shows observed 91 MPGe, 75-mph highway range of 260 mi, average DC fast-charge rate of 98 kW (10-90%), DC fast-charge time of 39 min (10-90%), EPA combined/city/highway ratings of 112/117/107 MPGe, and range of 303 mi.
source_title: 2026 Tesla Model 3 Review, Pricing, and Specs

facts: Kevin Ostertag was appointed President of Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC (PASA), effective April 1, 2026.
Kevin Ostertag succeeds Scott Kirchner, who has been serving in a dual role as both President of PASA and Global Chief Operating Officer (COO) of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd (PAS).
Scott Kirchner stated that Kevin brings a deep understanding of the business, a strong track record of success, and trusted relationships established across the organization regionally and globally.

Since 2021, Kevin Ostertag has served as Chief Financial Officer of PASA, where he played a key role in strengthening performance, driving efficiencies, and strategically positioning the organization for diversified growth.

Kevin Ostertag stated he is honored to step into the role at a pivotal moment in the company’s journey and looks forward to upholding Scott Kirchner's vision to further strengthen PASA's position as a leader in the mobility industry.

Scott Kirchner served as President of PASA since 2017, during which time the company underwent a significant transformation to position it for accelerated growth while remaining rooted in its cultural values and commitment to delivering high-quality, safe technologies to the world’s leading OEMs.
Scott Kirchner will continue to serve as Global COO of PAS after stepping down as President of PASA.
In April 2027, Panasonic Automotive will change its company name and brand to Mobitera to further position the organization for accelerated growth and optimize its overall business portfolio.
Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC is a premier supplier of automotive and mobility technologies for the world’s leading OEMs.
PASA's North American operations are headquartered in Peachtree City, GA, with locations across the region strategically positioned to serve customers.
PASA strives to create a safe, comfortable, eco-friendly in-vehicle experience through its wide-range of high-quality solutions.
source_title: Kevin Ostertag Named President of Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC | http://lifestyle.lmcordoba.com.ar/

facts: UK-based holographic technology startup Envisics reached an agreement with Panasonic Automotive Systems to jointly develop and commercialize a new generation of head-up displays for cars, trucks, and SUVs.
Panasonic Automotive Systems is a Tier 1 automotive supplier and a division of Panasonic Corporation of North America.
The head-up displays are units integrated into the dash of a vehicle that project images onto the windshield to aid drivers with navigation and provide other alerts.
The Panasonic HUDs will use Envisics holographic technology.
The deal was announced ahead of the virtual 2021 CES tech trade show.
The announcement follows Envisics' $50 million Series B funding round, which brought the company a valuation of more than $250 million.
Investors in Envisics' funding round included Hyundai Mobis, GM Ventures, SAIC Ventures, and Van Tuyl Companies.
Envisics' technology will be integrated into the upcoming Cadillac Lyriq electric vehicle.
Envisics is solely focused on the automotive application of holography.
Envisics' first generation holographic technology is already integrated in more than 150,000 Jaguar Land Rover vehicles.
The new agreement aims to combine Panasonic's expertise in optical design and its global reach as a Tier 1 supplier with Envisics' technology to bring holography into the mainstream.
Mass production of vehicles using the combined technology is slated for 2023.
The head-up displays will feature high resolution, wide color gamut, and large images that can be overlaid upon reality.
The technology can project information at multiple distances simultaneously.
Potential applications include night vision by overlaying enhanced information upon a dark road to clarify the path and obstacles.
Short-term applications will include augmented reality features like navigation, lane highlighting, and safety applications.
Future applications for autonomous driving could unlock opportunities in entertainment and video conferencing.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Systems: Startup partners with Panasonic to provide a better in-car experience with holographic technology, ETAuto

facts: On May 17, 2019, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. plans to create 100 jobs and invest at least $100 million, with potential up to $330 million, in an expansion at its existing facility in Monroe, Georgia.
Governor Kemp stated that Hitachi has been a great partner to the Walton County community by investing in employees and creating jobs for more than twenty years.
Governor Kemp expressed confidence that the investment will positively impact the local community and encourage continued success in Georgia’s strong automotive industry.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. is a group company of Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd., which is headquartered in Tokyo.

The company manufactures and markets engine powertrain systems, electric powertrain systems, and integrated vehicle control systems for major automotive original equipment manufacturers (OEM) worldwide.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. President and CEO Paul Carroll stated the company has been operating in Georgia for over twenty-two years.
The Georgia factory supplies advanced technology products for major automotive OEMs including Ford Motor Company, General Motors, Nissan, Subaru, and Honda.
The company cited its partnership with the State of Georgia, Walton County, and the City of Monroe as a reason for continuing to invest in the state.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. opened its first facility in Georgia in 1997.
The company currently employs 900 Georgians at its existing facility.
The expansion is intended to increase production capacity in response to the growth of the automotive industry.
Walton County Commission Chairman Kevin Little noted that Hitachi has been a great corporate partner and has become the county's largest employer.
Mayor John Howard of Monroe stated that Hitachi continues to invest in high technology and top-quality jobs, with community support described as second to none.

Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Sr. Project Manager Tammy Caudell represented the Global Commerce Division alongside the Development Authority of Walton County, Walton EMC, and the Georgia Department of Community Affairs.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that companies like Hitachi, which create jobs and take an active role in the community, elevate the state's economic development efforts.
Commissioner Wilson noted that companies like Hitachi Automotive Systems have driven significant success and interest from Japanese companies in Georgia.
Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd. is a wholly owned subsidiary of Hitachi, Ltd., headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.
The parent company is engaged in the development, manufacture, sales, and services of automotive components, transportation related components, industrial machines and systems.
source_title: Hitachi Automotive Systems America expanding in Monroe, GA - Location Georgia

facts: October 2023 marked a significant achievement for Perkins as their renowned 2.8 and 3.6-liter engines surpassed the production of 100,000 units.
Since their inception in May 2019, these engines have powered an array of machinery, ranging from excavators to tractors and from dumpers to pumping equipment.
The journey of these engines, in sync with both original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and end-users, reflects Perkins’ unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence.
The 2.8 and 3.6-liter engines are integrated into over 80 different machine models.
These engines offer high power density and torque despite their compact size.
These models have shown up to an 8 percent improvement in fuel economy during operation.
The 3.6-liter model delivers power up to 100 kW (134 hp) with up to 550 Nm of torque.
Features ensuring ease of maintenance include single side servicing, hydraulic lash adjusters, and long-life belts.
The 2.8 and 3.6-liter engines employ a DPF regeneration process that remains undetected by the operator to ensure uninterrupted operation.
Matt Coleman, product manager of the range, stated that they have met high expectations for the 2.8 and 3.6-liter engines.

Jaz Gill, vice president of global sales, marketing, service, and parts, stated that the addition of the 2.8 and 3.6-liter engines to their range of sustainable power solutions embodied that sentiment.
source_title: Perkins Achieves a Milestone: 100,000 Engines Released in October - News Caterpillar

facts: Peterson Spring is liquidating assets from several of its plants through an auction.
The auction includes lots 600-700 located at the Peterson Spring Georgia facility.
Other auction locations include Peterson Spring Greenville in Greenville, IL; Peterson Spring Three Rivers North in Three Rivers, MI; and Peterson Spring Canada Ltd. in Kingsville, Ontario, Canada.

Assets for sale include vehicles such as pickups, box trucks, over 35 trailers, snorkel lifts, electric- and gas-powered welders, forklifts, material handling equipment, transits, rigging equipment, and assorted tools and shop equipment.
Industrial machinery available includes 4-Slide Machines, Spring Coilers, Presses, Cut-To-Length Lines, Lathes, Grinders, Furnaces, Milling Machines, Plant Support Equipment, and Office Furniture.
The auction is being conducted as a webcast via the Bidspotter platform.
The auction date and time is January 20, 2022 at 10:00 AM CDT.
An earlier notice on the page states the auction ends July 17, 2018.
Inspection for the assets is scheduled for Wednesday, January 19, 2022 from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM CDT by appointment only.
A buyer's premium of 18% applies to online purchases and 15% applies to on-site purchases.
Accepted payment methods include cash, cashier's check, company check with a bank letter of guarantee, or wire transfer; credit cards are not accepted.
All assets are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" without warranty or guarantee from the auctioneer.
Buyers are solely responsible for removal, shipping, and providing necessary insurance certificates prior to picking up lots.
Disputes arising from the agreement will be settled by arbitration in Illinois under American Arbitration Association rules.

facts: Peterson Spring Georgia is a leading privately held spring manufacturer in North America.
The company provides designing, prototyping, testing, and manufacturing services for springs, snap-rings, wire forms, clips, clamps, stampings, and subassemblies.
The Athens, Georgia location employs approximately 108 people.
The facility generates annual revenue of approximately USD 500,000 to USD 999,999.
The company was established in 1914, with additional records indicating a founding year of 1932.
Peterson Spring operates branches in multiple locations across the United States.
Cary Foster serves as the plant manager for the Athens location.
Nine additional contacts are associated with the company, including Gary Strunk, Les A. Hanson, Hans J. Guenther, David Bruce, Leslie Neal, Michael Lims, Bob Jaros, Dave Wegner, and Dave Wehrs.
The business is categorized under the Spring Supplier and Manufacturing industries.
The facility operates from Monday through Thursday, 7:30 AM to 4:30 PM, and is closed on Fridays through Sundays.

facts: Investment Date was April 2019.
Exit Date was September 2023.
Fund involved was Fund I.
Sector is Spring Manufacturing, Wire Forming.
Headquarters is located in Southfield, MI.
Footprint includes 10 facilities across the US, Canada, Mexico and the UK*.
Revenue at acquisition was ~$130 million*.
End Markets include Industrial, Racing, Automotive Aftermarket, Truck & Trailer, Automotive OEM.
Peterson American Corporation is a designer and manufacturer of highly engineered springs, wire forms, retaining rings, clamps, stampings, and a variety of related sub-assemblies.
The company serves a diverse set of end markets that span automotive OEM, industrial, motorsport/racing, truck & trailer, heavy equipment, and consumer products.
source_title: Peterson American Corporation - MiddleGround Capital

facts: Peterson American Corporation's mission is to work with customers to provide the best products available to the world market through practical, innovative thinking.
The company maintains an equitable relationship with its customers, employees, shareholders, and communities.
Peterson Spring's performance culture emphasizes discipline, hard work, integrity, operational excellence, innovation, and quality/precision.
The company's history begins in the 1880s with the immigration of founder August Christian Peterson, a Norwegian blacksmith.
August studied blacksmithing and rose through spring manufacturing ranks in the early 1900s before managing plants across the Midwest.
In 1914, August moved his family to Detroit to form a company targeting demand from the railroad industry and the budding automobile industry.
August worked with his eldest son Alfred, who left school after eighth grade to work in steel mills' spring plants.
Other sons Sigurd, Conrad, and Harold joined later; Sigurd made significant contributions as a mechanic and developed innovative machines.
The company expanded overseas during the 1960s and 1970s through the purchase of foreign firms and joint ventures.
In 1978, the company split into two halves, with one renamed Peterson American Corporation.
The departed half's assets were repurchased in 1991, bringing the entire family of companies back under the PAC umbrella.
Peterson Spring is a founding member of the Spring Makers Institute (SMI) and has been an active member since its inception in 1933.
Former employees Bud and Pete Peterson, grandson and great-grandson of founder August Christian Peterson, both served two-year terms as SMI president.
Bud received the SMI's Golden Coil Award in 1989 for contributions of lasting importance to the spring industry.
The company operates 13 facilities worldwide, all TS/ISO/QS certified.
Decades of innovating thinking honed in the high-performance automotive market provide knowledge for thriving in fields implementing springs.
Peterson's metallurgists, engineers, and designers create springs used daily in products ranging from lawnmowers to military applications.
source_title: About Us - Peterson Spring

Is the largest privately held spring manufacturer in North America.

facts: The Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority helps companies evaluate why a move to the “Top of Georgia” is the right one.
Top of Georgia serves as a nonprofit fundraising arm that enhances the marketing efforts of the publicly funded Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority.
Top of Georgia offers private individuals and companies a way to contribute to the economic well being of Northwest Georgia.
Top of Georgia contracts with the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority for marketing initiatives including print materials.
Top of Georgia funds advertising directed toward site selectors, project managers, and target industry executives.
Top of Georgia sponsors meetings at which site selectors, project managers, and target industry executives are present.
Top of Georgia covers attendance at state, regional, national and international trade shows for economic development purposes.

Top of Georgia facilitates one-on-one visits with site selectors, project managers, and target industry executives who have expressed interest in bringing or expanding their business in Northwest Georgia.
Ashleigh Givens serves as the Director of Marketing/Project Manager for the Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority.
The Northwest Georgia Joint Development Authority Board of Directors includes representatives from Catoosa County, Chattooga County, Dade County, and Walker County.
Michael Signiski serves as Secretary on the board representing Catoosa County.
Rick Partain and Dwayne Morgan serve as Members on the board representing Catoosa County.
David Tidmore serves as Treasurer on the board representing Chattooga County.
Allen (Butch) Eleam serves as a Member on the board representing Chattooga County.
Evan Stone serves as Vice Chairman on the board representing Dade County.
Stacey Prater and Shannon Henry serve as Members on the board representing Dade County.
Chip Catlett serves as Chairman on the board representing Walker County.
Daryl Massey and Bobby Teems serve as Members on the board representing Walker County.
Steven Henry serves as Chairman of Catoosa County Commissioners.
Jeff Long, Chuck Harris, Richard Tharpe, and Charlie Stephens serve as District 1 through District 4 Commissioners for Catoosa County.
Andy Allen serves as the Sole Commissioner for Chattooga County.
Don Townsend serves as Board Chairman & County Executive for Dade County.
Bill Pullen, Phillip Hartline, Bob Woods, and Melissa Bradford serve as District 1 through District 4 Commissioners for Dade County.
Angie Teems serves as Chairwoman for Walker County.
Robert Blakemore, Mark Askew, Brian Hart, and Gene Wilson serve as District 1 through District 4 Commissioners for Walker County.

facts: Piolax is known worldwide for its degree of excellence in automobile parts and is committed to growing along with its customers.
Due to a commitment to supply quality products to the automotive industries of the world, Piolax decided to establish a location in the United States.
In August of 1988, Piolax US began production in Canton, Georgia.
Piolax is a leading supplier of plastic and metal products.

facts: Pirelli will start Cyber™ Tyre production in Georgia.
Investment and output expansion details will be communicated in the coming months following the finalization of the development plans.
The Georgia plant is already dedicated to producing technologically advanced products for the U.S. market, including the High-Value segment and Motorsport.
Pirelli participated in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, a flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce, where Cyber™ Tyre was showcased as a core innovation shaping smart mobility.
Cyber™ Tyre is the world's first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires.
The system processes sensor data through Pirelli's proprietary software and algorithms.
Cyber™ Tyre communicates in real time with vehicle electronics to enable new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems.
These functionalities enhance the driving experience, increase safety levels, and support connected infrastructure.
Cyber™ Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth during the SelectUSA summit.
Pirelli has been established in Georgia for more than 20 years.
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber™ Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the country.
The production start reflects Pirelli's commitment to bringing advanced technologies closer to the market and strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process at its Rome, Georgia plant.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group and is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
The new process enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
MIRS is a highly digitalized system that enables a direct link between product design and its industrial application.
This development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli's production capacity in Rome as part of the Group's industrial development plans.
The Rome, Georgia plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility hosts a dedicated R&D center to strengthen technological development and product innovation.
The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC®-certified natural rubber.
source_title: PIRELLI WILL START CYBER™ TYRE PRODUCTION IN GEORGIA UNDERLINING THE STRATEGIC IMPORTANCE OF THE U.S. MARKET PR Newswire

Some Pirelli tires are marketed with a 75,000-mile warranty.
Pirelli prohibits the repair of products with a speed rating of 'V' or higher.
Pirelli recommends following USTMA industry guidelines for tire repairs.
Tires equipped with Pirelli's seal inside technology contain an internal gel that prevents standard patching after punctures, necessitating full replacement.
Pirelli does not offer a standard road hazard warranty, requiring customers to purchase separate warranties through authorized dealers for coverage of damage like nails or punctures.
Warranty claims for premature wear are handled via prorated refunds based on remaining tread depth down to 2/32, with no mileage warranty provided for sport tires.

The Rome, Georgia facility is used to better serve the North American OEM and Replacement markets.
Scott Griffin serves as VP of Pirelli Motorcycle Tire North America.

Scott Griffin stated that the AMA invited bids from all tire companies for its forthcoming road-racing series and Dunlop was chosen, adding that Pirelli respects the AMA’s transparent bidding process and congratulates Dunlop on the technical and logistical challenge ahead.
Dunlop was selected by the AMA to supply spec tires for its upcoming road-racing series.
Pirelli will apply assets from its ongoing development of high-performance tires in World Superbike and World Supersport to other avenues in the North American market.

facts: Pirelli Tire North America Inc. announced that it is partnering with the Berry College Longleaf Pine Project, a collaboration between the college and The Nature Conservancy in Georgia to restore rare and essential longleaf pine habitat.
The restoration site is located just north of Rome, Ga., a few miles away from Pirelli’s U.S. factory.
The mountain containing the habitat is part of the Berry College campus and is home to one of the few remaining relict stands of Mountain Longleaf Pine.
Longleaf pine forests once occupied more than 92 million acres throughout the southeastern U.S. and now cover only about 2% of the original range.
One of the key pillars of Pirelli’s global sustainability strategy is to create value for the local communities in which the company operates.

facts: Pirelli announced plans to start production of ‘cyber tire’ connected tires at its plant in Rome, Georgia.
The announcement was made in a 6 May statement dated 7 May 2026.
The Georgia facility already manufactures “the most technologically advanced products for the US market.”
The plant will be further expanded to include production of tires equipped with cyber tire technology.
Pirelli stated the move highlights the strategic importance of the US for its global growth roadmap and strengthens its integrated industrial and technological presence in the country.

No details regarding planned investment levels or additional production capacity were disclosed, with further details to be communicated "in the coming months following the finalisation of the development plans."

The announcement coincided with Pirelli’s participation in the SelectUSA Investment Summit in Washington, where the company showcased cyber tire as “a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.”
According to Pirelli, the cyber tire is “the world’s first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires.”
The system processes data in real time and is claimed to enable “new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems to enhance the driving experience and increase safety levels.”
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, commented on the decision.
Zanardo described the start of cyber tire production in Georgia as “a significant milestone for Pirelli in this country.”
He added it reflects their commitment to bringing advanced technologies closer to the market, further strengthening their industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the US.
As part of the development, Pirelli is introducing the latest version of its MIRS (modular integrated robotised system) manufacturing process at the Rome plant.

The company described MIRS as “the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group,” noting the technology will be exclusive to the Georgia facility.
According to Pirelli, the ‘highly digitalised system’ enhances “robotised production capabilities,” improving productivity, flexibility, and quality.
The process enables “a direct link between product design and its industrial application.”
This will lay the groundwork for growth in Pirelli’s production capacity in Rome and further strengthen its position in the US, described as “one of its most important and strategic markets.”
Operational since 2002, the facility in Georgia has a production capacity of 400,000 units per year.
The facility manufactures 'high-value tires for the North American market.'
The operations also include an R&D centre focused on technological development and product innovation.
source_title: Pirelli to bring ‘cyber tire’ production to US plant | European Rubber Journal

The company sells and distributes products to clients in the original equipment, replacement, export and domestic markets.
It also offers a range of tires for motorsport competitions.
Pirelli Tire North America is a part of Pirelli & C. S.p.A., which is one of the largest tire manufacturers in the world.

Pirelli & C. S.p.A. is an operational holding company for a group involved in the design, development, production and marketing of tires for cars, light vehicles and motorcycles, buses, trucks, and agricultural and earth moving machinery.
The facility is located at 2 Industrial Blvd SE Rome, GA 30161.
Regular operating hours are Monday through Friday.
Also known as Pirelli Tire North America and Pirelli Tire LLC.

facts: Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA) maintains its Rome, Georgia headquarters, which houses a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and a research and development center.
PTNA operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico.
The company manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for domestic car and motorcycle applications.
Pirelli has commercial presence in over 160 countries and operates 19 factories across 13 countries.
Motorsport serves as an essential part of Pirelli's research and development program, functioning as an open-air testing laboratory to develop Ultra High Performance tires for the road.

Pirelli USA renewed its agreement with Porsche Club of America for three years, adding support for Werks Reunion Amelia Island alongside five national programs: PCA Club Racing, PCA High Performance Driver Education (HPDE), PCA Autocross, Porsche Parade, and PCA Treffen.
Pirelli will supply tires and provide trackside support for the renewed Porsche Club of America partnership.
For 2019, Pirelli supports the Air-Cooled Cup USA championship for two years, featuring seven rounds starting in April at Portland International Raceway in Oregon.
Pirelli's partnership with the Trans Am Series was extended for five years through 2025, marking its tenure as headline sponsor since 2017, with 12 races scheduled for the 2019 season.
Pirelli has equipped the Intercontinental GT Challenge since 2014 and continues to provide P Zero slick tires for high-performance prestige brands.
The 2019 Intercontinental GT Challenge begins in February at Liqui-Moly Bathurst 12 Hours in Australia and visits Laguna Seca track in California one month later for the California 8 Hours.
Key parts of the Pirelli USA program include P Zero-equipped single-make championships from Ferrari (Ferrari Challenge) and Lamborghini (Lamborghini Super Trofeo).
Pirelli supports the Challenge Xtreme Elite rally series in Canada for another three years, with its first event in February in Quebec.
In Mexico, Pirelli supports the Formula 4 grassroots motorsport series running on P Zero tires.
Pirelli was founded in 1872 and maintains headquarters in Milan, Italy, focusing on the high-end, premium product segment where it is a world leader.
The company is currently the exclusive supplier to the Formula 1 championship and the Superbike World Championship.
PTNA maintains sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City, along with a prestige flagship store in Los Angeles.

facts: Korean tire manufacturer Kumho is expected to bring more than 400 jobs to Bibb County.
Local officials promised to waive permits and fees related to the plant’s construction as part of the deal.
Economic development officials compiled a nearly $12.5 million pot of money to help pay for site acquisition and preparation, infrastructure improvements, and other startup costs.
Macon generally does not forego permit fees as policy, so it will contribute cash to the pile of incentives and in-kind work instead.
The incentive pool includes contributions from state and federal grants, the Bibb County Commission, the Macon Water Authority, plus other public and private entities.
The project requires about $280,000 to be paid to Macon’s Bureau of Inspection and Fees as well as the Macon-Bibb County Planning & Zoning Commission.

Chip Cherry, president of both the Macon Economic Development Commission and the Greater Macon Chamber of Commerce, stated that competition for the plant was intense and necessitated 'extraordinary incentives' including the agreement to waive permit fees.
The project was one of the state’s largest when negotiations began last year.
Through sales tax revenue on equipment and energy used, Kumho’s presence in Bibb County will directly repay Macon within four to five years.
Development officials estimate a net economic benefit of $7.6 million for the region over the next 20 years from the company’s facility investment and high-paying skilled jobs.
The city's contribution will be paid from Macon’s unallocated reserve account, causing the fund balance to dip down to $90,000.
Human Resources Director Ben Hubbard stated that about $500,000 in salary savings from vacant positions is being transferred into the reserve fund to replenish it.
Appropriations Chairman Mike Cranford called Kumho a 'wonderful project' and a unique opportunity for the city after his committee unanimously approved the payment.
source_title: City's Reserves Might Help Fund Tire Plant - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Pirelli Tire North America has unveiled the P Zero Trofeo Track, a Department of Transportation (DOT)-marked, track-focused tire engineered for the United States market.
The new tire is designed to excel in dry conditions and uses ultra high-performance road tire technology purpose-built for the circuit.
The P Zero Trofeo Track is produced at Pirelli’s Rome, Ga. facility.
The tire is developed for club racers, amateur motorsports enthusiasts, and serious track-day drivers.
It offers sharp handling, grip, and consistency while maintaining DOT compliance for sanctioned racing events.
Claudio Zanardo serves as CEO and president of Pirelli North America.
The tire is designed, engineered, and manufactured at Pirelli's American facility in Rome, Ga.
Pirelli views the tire as a symbol of its continued investment in U.S. motorsport and delivering locally developed, locally made products for this market.
Other features include a slick-like tread pattern, bold groove designs for DOT compliance, racing-derived compound, and a fitment range of 17-inch to 21-inch for performance coverage.
The P Zero Trofeo Track is DOT-marked for compliance with U.S. motorsport regulations and is specifically marked "For Competition Use Only."
It is designed with P-metric and Light Load sizes in accordance with Tire and Rim Association standards.
The tire is exclusively targeted for the U.S. market.
source_title: Pirelli Unveils P Zero Trofeo Track | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. has officially opened its new fine dining “Restaurant 356” in Atlanta, Georgia.
The restaurant sits on the complex of the company’s new $100 million Porsche Experience Center and headquarters.
Restaurant 356 offers panoramic views of a 1.6-mile driver development track.
Andre Oosthuizen, vice president of marketing for Porsche Cars North America, Inc., stated that Atlanta is known for its diverse tapestry and international cuisine.
At Restaurant 356, guests will enjoy elegant dishes that honor Atlanta’s global flavors while culminating a unique Porsche experience.
The restaurant is located on the second level of the Porsche Experience Center.
From the restaurant, guests can view aircraft taking off and landing at Runway 826 Right at the Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
Innovative dishes are served inside a dining room featuring contemporary decor.
The seasonal menu showcases refined world cuisine made with locally farmed and foraged ingredients.
A vast wine selection pays tribute to the great winegrowing regions of the world.
Classically-focused cocktails are available for those favoring tradition.
Executive Chef Kyle Forson, former executive sous chef at The Spence, leads the restaurant's culinary approach.
Chef Forson focuses on carefully hand-crafted dishes that let the quality of seasonal ingredients shine through.
The menu reflects influences from Forson’s Polish heritage and experiences with French, Californian, and Southern fare.
No single type of cuisine will dominate the menu.
Chef Forson stated he wants guests to enjoy food that enhances the Porsche experience and fuses it with a sense of place.
Restaurant 356 is open to the public Tuesday through Saturday for lunch and dinner, and on Sunday for brunch and dinner.
The restaurant provides catering services and operates as an event venue.

In addition to Restaurant 356 and the driver development track it overlooks, the Porsche Experience Center features a classic car gallery, restoration center, human performance center, and driving simulator lab.
A state-of-the-art business center within the complex includes 13,000 square feet of conference and event space.
The complex was specifically designed as a public destination with an estimated 30,000 guests expected to visit each year.
source_title: Restaurant 356: Porsche opens fine dining restaurant in Atlanta

facts: Porsche Cars North America Inc. hosted the grand opening of its new headquarters near the Atlanta airport, kicking off an initiative to rev up the area.
The $100 million “Experience Center” sits on 27 acres near the international terminal at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport and serves to reinforce the sports car’s brand.
The facility is complete with a 1.6-mile test track, a classic car gallery, a simulator lab, and amenities for business conferences.
About 30,000 people are expected to visit each year, with many trying out driving experiences that start at $300.
The test track runs through the building's courtyard and is centered on helping drivers understand their cars to enhance performance rather than just speed.

Instructors lead drivers through six stations on the test track, including a handling circuit with hairpin turns, an off-road course for Porsche SUVs, a low-friction area, and a kick plate that simulates slippage due to ice or wet leaves.
Georgia’s leaders have been jubilant over Porsche’s economic impact and the visible seal of approval it provides for other international companies.
The headquarters is Porsche AG’s largest investment in a facility outside of Germany.
Porsche elected to stay in Atlanta after scouting more than 70 other destinations around the U.S., despite already having its North American office based in Sandy Springs.

Porsche AG CEO Matthias Müller, Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal, Atlanta Mayor Kasim Reed, and North America President and CEO Detlev von Platen attended the press conference to praise the company and its home.
Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Porsche’s investment represents confidence in the future success of the state, economy, and workforce.
Gov. Deal noted that the facility at One Porsche Drive is innovative, forward-thinking, and performance-driven, and that travelers on I-75 have witnessed the area's progress.
The opening coincided with Porsche eclipsing sales of 5,000 vehicles for the first time ever.
Porsche's recent sales growth was mostly driven by the new Macan small SUV.
All other Porsche models sold in the U.S., including the Boxster/Cayman, 911/918, Cayenne, and Panamera, saw declines in April compared to the same month last year.
Year-to-date sales for Porsche were up 17 percent at the time of the opening.
The opening occurred on the same day Mercedes-Benz USA CEO Stephen Cannon was slated to speak to the Atlanta Press Club about relocating that German auto maker’s headquarters to Atlanta.
source_title: Porsche's $100 Million Headquarters and Test Track Takes Off - Global Atlanta

facts: Porsche Financial Services, Inc. (PFS) is located in Atlanta, Georgia.
PFS launched an auto lease Asset Backed Securities (ABS) offering in the United States with a principal amount of $810 million.
This marks the fourth bond issuance by PFS within a year.
PFS is a wholly owned subsidiary of Dr. Ing. h.c. F. Porsche AG, a German luxury car manufacturer.
The securities issued in this Rule 144A transaction retained a 'AAA' rating from rating agencies and achieved competitive pricing.
The transaction was divided into five tranches, including one with a floating rate, and was backed by auto lease contracts for Porsche vehicles.
The deal saw strong subscription levels across all tranches from a diverse investor base that included investment funds, asset managers of financial institutions, trusts, banks, and corporates.
Barclays Capital Inc. led the transaction with support from CIBC World Markets Corp., Mizuho, US Bancorp, and Wells Fargo Securities as book runners.
Tobias Hausladen serves as Treasurer & Chief Financial Officer at PFS.

Tobias Hausladen stated that PILOT 2025-1 is the first lease transaction to include battery-electric vehicles (BEVs), enabling PFS to securitize the entire range of model lines within the Porsche portfolio.
PFS was founded in 1991 and provides leasing and financing products for Porsche customers and dealers in the U.S.
In 2012, PFS expanded operations to become the captive finance provider for Volkswagen Group's exclusive brands including Bentley, Lamborghini, and Bugatti.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) was established in 1984 and has been headquartered in Atlanta since 1998.
PCNA is the sole U.S. importer of models such as the Porsche 911 and Taycan.
PCNA operates two Porsche Experience Centers in North America, one in Atlanta and another near Los Angeles, featuring driver development tracks and business centers.
PCNA supports over 200 independently owned Porsche dealerships across the U.S., along with several studios and satellite stores.
These dealerships provide parts, service, marketing, and training to ensure customers receive top-notch experiences aligned with Porsche's legacy of vehicle performance excellence.
Porsche has a racing heritage with over 30,000 motorsport victories to date.
source_title: Porsche Financial Services Issues First Prime Auto Lease ABS Transaction Of 2025 - DriveSpark News

facts: On Feb. 24, 2025, Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) announced the addition of the Macan Electric to driving programs at the Porsche Experience Centers in Atlanta and Los Angeles.
The Macan Electric joins existing PEC driving programs that feature vehicles such as the iconic 911 and versatile Cayenne.

Michelle Rainey, Director of Porsche Experience Centers, stated that introducing new cars is special and highlighted the program's ability to demonstrate vehicle performance through high-speed laps, acceleration runs, and low grip training.
In Atlanta, customers can choose from three Drive experiences: Macan Electric, Macan Turbo Electric, and a comparison of the Macan Turbo Electric and Macan GTS.
In Los Angeles, PECLA offers three options including the Macan Turbo Electric drive experience and two comparison programs against the Taycan Turbo or internal combustion engine Macan GTS.

Drive programs last 90 minutes and include a safety orientation with a Porsche Drive Instructor followed by track-based driver development modules such as handling circuit, kick plate, and low friction circle.
PEC Atlanta and PEC Los Angeles are celebrating 10 years in the United States as fully immersive brand destinations.

The facilities feature off-track elements including ever-changing displays of curated vehicles, simulators, unique dining experiences, retail stores for enthusiasts, meeting spaces, and iconic architecture.
Drive programs begin at $600 per experience.
source_title: Porsche Experience Centers welcome the new Macan Electric to the track Intrado

facts: The Porsche Experience Center in Atlanta, Georgia, has been hosting customers and fans since June 2015.
The facility is undergoing significant expansion, including the addition of a second track.
Opening April 1st, 2023, the center includes a professional 1.3-mile driving track, a Porsche museum, restaurants, retail shops, a heritage gallery, and professional driving courses.
Construction has begun on a second track that effectively doubles the length of the existing driver development course.
The new section can be driven on its own or in combination with the existing track.
The project is the cornerstone of a multi-million dollar investment in further developing the headquarters campus of Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA).
The expansion is part of a 33-acre site development next to the current headquarters’ 27-acre site, which was first inaugurated in 2015.
Construction will be ongoing through 2023 and include a new Porsche Classic Factory Restoration facility, a parking deck, and the already-opened Porsche Service Center South Atlanta.
Kjell Gruner, President and CEO of PCNA, stated that the physical connection a driver enjoys with sports cars is core to the Porsche brand.
Gruner noted that Experience Centers in Atlanta and LA serve as destinations for anyone to experience Porsche sports cars and learn about the brand.
The Atlanta center has hosted more than 6,000 visitors a month in normal times.
The new track will feature a 1.3-mile handling circuit with elements inspired by the Corkscrew at Laguna Seca, with a rise of 30 feet and drop of 25 feet through approaching and descending turns.
The track will include the Carousel from Germany’s Nurburgring-Nordschleife and the twisty Tail of the Dragon in the Smoky Mountains.

Three new driving modules will be added: a low-friction, wetted 60-meter asphalt circle; an ice-hill with a wet and slick surface along with an incline and decline to demonstrate traction control; and a dynamic area comprising 135 feet by 555 feet of asphalt to demonstrate handling capabilities.
The expansion will operate independently from the existing South track, but the two layouts can be combined for an overall main handling circuit of 2.3 miles.
Up to 2.9 miles of track surface can be utilized when all layouts are combined.
The track expansion is expected to be ready for operation in the first quarter of 2023.
Porsche Experience Center Atlanta is located at One Porsche Drive Atlanta, GA 30354.

source_title: Porsche Enthusiasts Reving Up For A Second Track Coming To The Porsche Experience Center In Atlanta, Georgia - Car Collectors Club

facts: Series production of the first purely electric Porsche is set to begin next year.
The vehicle previously operating under the working title “Mission E” has now been given its official name: Taycan.
The name can be roughly translated as “lively young horse,” referencing the imagery at the heart of the Porsche crest, which has featured a leaping steed since 1952.

“Our new electric sports car is strong and dependable; it’s a vehicle that can consistently cover long distances and that epitomizes freedom,” Oliver Blume, Chairman of the Executive Board of Porsche AG, explains.
Porsche announced the name for its first purely electric model as part of the “70 years of Porsche Sports Cars” ceremony.

Two permanently activated synchronous motors (PSM) generating a system output of over 600 horsepower accelerate the electric sports car to 60 miles per hour in well under 3.5 seconds and to 124 miles per hour (200 km/h) on the track in less than twelve seconds.

The Porsche Taycan will also offer a consistent level of power that is unprecedented among electric vehicles: multiple launches in a row will be possible without a loss in performance, and the vehicle’s maximum range will be over 300 miles as measured in accordance with the New European Driving Cycle (NEDC).
Porsche plans to invest more than six billion Euros in electromobility by 2022, doubling the investment that the company had originally planned.
Of the additional three billion Euros, some 500 million Euros will be used for the development of Taycan variants and derivatives.
Around one billion Euros from the additional investment will be allocated to the electrification and hybridization of Porsche's existing product range.
Several hundred million Euros will fund the expansion of production sites.
Around 700 million Euros will be dedicated to new technologies, charging infrastructure, and smart mobility.

At the Porsche headquarters in Stuttgart-Zuffenhausen, a new paint shop, dedicated assembly area for the Taycan, and a conveyor bridge for transporting the painted bodies and drive units to the final assembly area all are currently being built.
The existing engine plant is being expanded to manufacture electric motors, and a new body shop will also be developed.
Investments are also planned for the Weissach Development Center.
Production of the Taycan is creating around 1,200 new jobs in Zuffenhausen alone.
Established in 1984, Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is the exclusive U.S. importer of Porsche 911, 718 Boxster and 718 Cayman; Macan and Cayenne; and Panamera.

Headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia since 1998, PCNA is also home to the first Porsche Experience Center in North America featuring a module-based 1.6 mile driver development track, business center, and fine dining restaurant, 356.

The company operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles that features a 53-acre complex with a driver development track containing eight educational modules totaling 4.1 miles, a business center, and Restaurant 917.
PCNA employs over 300 people who provide parts, service, marketing, and training for 189 dealers.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.

facts: Goodie Nation announced the Goodie Nation Product Strategy Sprint Series in collaboration with Porsche Cars North America, Inc. on Friday, November 19th, 2021.
The initiative is a 90-day virtual program where management and related functional experts from Porsche will advise 20 founders around creating enterprise products.
Joey Womack, CEO of Goodie Nation, stated the goal is to uplift the next generation of social entrepreneurs and diverse founders as they design product experiences.
Womack expressed thrill at having Porsche's dynamic support in guiding founders through developing what's next.
The Sprint Series targets startup companies at the beginning of their journey or seeking to reach the next level in their product roadmap.

Eligible startup spaces for participation include Customer Experience, Future of Work, Logistics & Mobility, Supply Chain & Manufacturing, Big Data/Analytics & Security, and Climate Tech & Sustainability.

Participants will experience virtual lunches with Porsche, weekly accountability group huddles, office hours, storytelling help from Goodie Nation, and invitations to appear on Goodie Nation podcast episodes.
Kjell Gruner, President and CEO of Porsche Cars North America, Inc., stated that innovation is a fundamental part of the Porsche DNA.
Gruner expressed excitement to work with Goodie Nation and the select group of founders as they take bold steps to grow their businesses.
Together, Porsche and Goodie Nation hope to inspire participants to embody the same entrepreneurial spirit that Porsche was founded upon as they take their companies to the next level.
Previous Goodie Nation mentorship programs have included collaborations with companies such as Verizon, AARP, and Deloitte.
Womack added that Goodie Nation works to bring relationships of expertise to founders to accelerate business.
Womack noted that these intensives provide tremendous value, insights, learnings, and connections for founders.
Applications for the program are due by December 10.
source_title: Goodie Nation Collaborates with Porsche Cars North America - The ATL Airport Chamber

canonical_name: Porsche Taycan Turbo GT Lap Record Event at Road Atlanta
facts: The Taycan Turbo GT set a new benchmark lap time for a production EV at Road Atlanta.
Professional race car driver and Porsche Cars North America brand ambassador Patrick Long completed the record lap in 1:27.15 minutes.
The run utilized available Pirelli P Zero Trofeo RS NF0 summer performance tires developed and homologated specifically for the Taycan Turbo GT.
The vehicle was equipped with the optional Weissach Package.
The Taycan Turbo GT beat the previous record set by the Taycan Turbo S in 2020 by 6.73 seconds.
A Taycan Turbo S previously completed the lap in 1:33.88 minutes on the 2.54-mile track at the end of 2020.
The record was set on May 28, 2025 under continuously changing weather conditions.
At the time of the run, temperatures were in the mid 70s with moderate cloud cover and winds of 3-5 mph from the North.
Persistent morning rain left track conditions patchy damp in some spots but mostly dry during the record run due to a break in clouds and a consistent breeze.
Patrick Long stated after his first lap: “The new Taycan was so easy to get up to speed, and the brakes were amazing, there was absolutely no compromise in feel or power.”
After the record run, Long noted he expected steering feel, connection, and feedback but did not expect how powerful the Active Ride suspension would be in counteracting pitch, roll, and squat.
Long added that Road Atlanta puts a lot of load into a car, and the hydraulics kept up without issues to ensure tires were always at their best.

To ensure safety, the team experimented with traction and stability control settings, including Sport PSM on and off, finding the car worked fine in Sport mode with seamless operation from the driver's seat.
Long described Attack Mode as intense, stating he had never felt such a smooth but intense burst of usable power in anything, including race cars.
The record-setting vehicle was a stock production 2025 Porsche Taycan Turbo GT equipped with the Weissach Package and Pirelli Trofeo RS tires.
The car featured an exterior color of Purple Sky and the GT Interior Package with accents in GT Silver.
The vehicle was also optioned with the Illuminated Light Strip.
Starting tire pressures were set to 37psi for both front and rear tires.
The starting battery State of Charge was 97%.
Track Endurance Mode was enabled prior to the run to reduce the starting temperature of the battery to 68 degrees F.
Two attempts were made to break the record, with the second run being the faster one.
source_title: Taycan Turbo GT sets new production EV lap record at Road Atlanta - EIN Presswire

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. is located at One Porsche Drive Atlanta GA 30354.
The page details recall campaign ANB8 for certain 2021 Taycan, Taycan 4S, Taycan Turbo S, Taycan 4 Cross Turismo, and Taycan 4S Cross Turismo vehicles.
A retaining ring on top of the suspension strut may come loose due to an incorrectly machined groove, causing air loss from the pneumatic spring.
The defect can cause a drop in ride height, loss of ride comfort, and adversely affect vehicle handling capabilities, increasing the risk of a crash.
There are 28 potentially affected vehicles in the U.S., manufactured between July 27, 2021 and February 3, 2022.
Porsche became aware of the issue in November 2022 through investigation of field cases with dislodged suspension struts.
The sub-supplier for the front pneumatic spring strut is Vibracoustic GmbH & Co KG, located at Hörstener Str. 45-47 Hamburg, Germany.
Porsche decided to recall the subject vehicles on December 7, 2022, to prevent negative effects on handling capabilities.
Dealers will inspect the suspension strut and replace it if necessary, free of charge to customers.
Owner notification letters are expected to be mailed February 10, 2023.
There were 4 warranty claims reported from April 2022 to October 2022.
Vehicles built after September 17, 2021, were produced with parts outside the identified incorrectly machined batches and are within specification.
The supplier has implemented several process improvements to avoid similar manufacturing deviations in the future.

PCNA serves as the exclusive U.S. importer and distributor of Porsche sports cars, Cayenne SUVs, and Panamera four-door sports sedans.
The company was established in 1984.
PCNA is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
PCNA employs approximately 220 people who provide parts, service, marketing, and training for 192 dealers.
In September 2012, PCNA reported U.S. sales of 2,736 vehicles, a 26.08 percent increase over the 2,170 vehicles sold in September 2011.

facts: Coca-Cola Co.’s home base in Atlanta and Fulton County continues to be a Southern corporate and cultural icon.
John Pemberton was motivated to concoct the Coca-Cola drink in 1886 in part because of Prohibition legislation passed by the city and county.
Coca-Cola Co. Chairman and CEO Muhtar Kent said, “Simply put, there would be no Coca-Cola without Atlanta … and Atlantans.”
Coca-Cola is investing more than $250 million in a strategic expansion of its metro-area production and supply system facilities to meet the needs of its Freestyle fountain dispensing systems.
The Coca-Cola investment will create 100 new jobs by the end of 2012 at Coke and its primary supplier.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development and the Development Authority of Fulton County assisted Coca-Cola in a $100-million plant expansion to manufacture ingredients used in those machines.
Fulton County comprises Atlanta and 13 other municipalities within its boundaries.
Ken Dobson serves as the economic development administrator for Fulton County.
Progress Partners of North Fulton Atlanta is a coalition launched last year to support regional economic development.
The Development Authority of Fulton County encompasses 534.5 square miles of unincorporated areas and municipalities, including the City of Atlanta.

The Development Authority of Fulton County has issued more than $16 billion in bonds and helped well over 300 businesses and institutions build, upgrade, renovate, expand or relocate in Fulton County.
Fulton County is interweaving asset-based, knowledge-based, and technology-based economic development approaches into one integrated process.
The county's economic development strategy starts with engaging institutional and corporate leaders as champions and forming an economic development advisory group.

Six topical committees will feed into the advisory group, focusing on real estate development, technology-based entrepreneurship, sustainable development, schools and work-force education, development finance, and international trade and commerce.
Metro Atlanta hosts more than 60 foreign governments' consulates and trade offices, many located in Fulton County.
In May, Fulton County formalized an agreement with the State of Bahia, Brazil, to promote cooperation and joint initiatives between the two governments.
Fulton County Chairman John Eaves and fellow county commissioners formed the Fulton County Economic Development Division in December 2011 with a county-wide focus.
Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport is the state's largest employer at 58,000 employees.
The new Maynard H. Jackson Jr. International Terminal at the airport is expected to create another 1,500 direct and indirect jobs.
The airport's total economic impact was assessed at $32.6 billion in 2009.
Delta Airlines, the airport's largest tenant, employs 30,000 people.
The world's busiest airport saw 92 million passengers pass through in 2011 and hosts 950,000 takeoffs and landings a year.
The airport just opened its new $1.4-billion Maynard Jackson International Terminal and is seeing increased cargo activity.
Louis Miller serves as the aviation general manager at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.
Porsche Cars North America decided to locate its headquarters at the former Ford Motor Co. plant site at the end of one of the airport runways.
The Porsche headquarters complex is designed by HOK, with development partner Jacoby pursuing related development around the site.
Tim Quinn, vice president for after sales at Porsche Cars North America, stated that Atlanta has turned out to be almost the ideal place for their headquarters.
The decision to stay in Atlanta was the result of a thorough evaluation process that spanned metro Atlanta and several out-of-state sites.
The new Porsche headquarters will house PCNA, Porsche Financial Services, Inc., and Porsche Business Services, Inc., both currently located in Lisle, Illinois.
A total workforce of 400 will be deployed at a technical service and training center, as well as a customer experience center that will feature a handling road course.

Partners in attracting Porsche included the Georgia Dept. of Economic Development, Atlanta Development Authority, the Fulton County Development Authority, the City of Hapeville, Georgia Power, Jacoby Development, and Cushman & Wakefield.
Porsche plans to move into its new headquarters during the second half of 2013.
Stan Conway is senior vice president and director of development for Majestic Realty Co., overseeing 11 million sq. ft. in Dallas and Atlanta.

Majestic Realty has built some 9 million sq. ft. in South Fulton along South Fulton Parkway in the past decade, including a build-to-suit for Kraft and a facility in Union City for biotech manufacturer Dendreon.
County officials were instrumental in getting Majestic Realty going on South Fulton Parkway, balancing resident needs with industry needs and understanding logistics business drivers.
County taxes in Fulton County have remained quite low relative to the rest of the country, especially among major logistics centers.
The Fulton Industrial Boulevard corridor in unincorporated South Fulton comprises more than 4,500 acres of industrial and commercial property.
The State of Georgia granted the Fulton Industrial Boulevard corridor Opportunity Zone status through 2020, featuring tax incentives for creating as few as two jobs.
Moses Brown has lived in the Atlanta area since 1971 and recently retired from Reed Elsevier Group.
Brown was part of the Equifax team that decided in 1987 to move some operations to Alpharetta, building a data center there in 1989 and a second facility in 1991.
Equifax spun off Choicepoint, which later became part of Reed Elsevier’s LexisNexis Risk Solutions business, still operating in Alpharetta alongside other corporate residents.

Brown serves as vice chairman of the North Fulton Community Improvement District, a self-taxing district that has invested more than $13.6 million in $75 million worth of new interchange, roadway, and bridge work.
The infrastructure improvements in the North Fulton Community Improvement District have sped up commutes for many of the 77,000 employees who work in the district.
Majestic Realty recently acquired developer CRB, which had successful projects in Johns Creek and mid-Fulton.
Parkway South is a plan by CRB for some 2,500 acres along South Fulton Parkway that could be a potential hit for the industrial sector.

facts: Automotive components are in high demand as the auto industry continues to expand, buoyed by the production of electric, hybrid and internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles.
Industrial Info is tracking $8.8 billion worth of capital-spending projects in the U.S. that are geared toward auto component manufacturing.
Honda Motor Company is investing approximately $700 million to retool its automotive plants in Ohio.

Honda's $250 million project at its Marysville Auto Plant (MAP) entails performing upgrades and modifications to existing production equipment at the 3.6 million-square-foot plant, to assemble battery cases and modules that will be installed in electric vehicles (EVs).
The project will make MAP Honda's first plant transition to EV production.
Construction kicked off in mid-2023 and is expected to be completed in mid-2025.
Stellantis NV is retooling its KTP transmission plant in Kokomo, Indiana, to manufacture electric drive modules (EDM)--a single unit that combines an EV's motor, gearbox, and power electronics.
Stellantis aims for 50% of passenger car and light-duty truck sales in the U.S. to be EVs by 2030.
The project is expected to wrap up next year.

Ford Motor Company is investing $160 million to install new equipment and supporting equipment and systems at its Sharonville Transmission Plant in Ohio, to continue assembling 10R 10-speed automatic transmissions and current gear families used in rear-wheel-drive vehicles.
Construction kicked off this summer and is expected to be completed in January 2025.
Ford said in August that it plans to cut its annual mix of capital expenditures devoted to EVs from 40% to 30%, amid challenges facing the U.S. EV market.
Magna International Incorporated is renovating a former animal shelter and pet adoption center in Auburn Hills, Michigan, to become the exclusive supplier of seating for General Motors' EVs.
The $100 million project is expected to wrap up next month.
Sewon America Incorporated, with Evans General Contractors, is building a grassroot automotive component plant in Rincon, Georgia, to produce EV body parts.
Sewon America is a subsidiary of SEWON Group (South Korea).
The project is expected to wrap up around the end of the year.
One of the plants expected to receive the parts is Hyundai Motor Group's EV assembly plant under construction in nearby Ellabell, which is expected to begin production in 2025.
Hyundai and GM recently signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) to 'explore future collaboration across key strategic areas,' including vehicle production and clean-energy technologies.
Robert Bosch GmbH, through its subsidiary ZF Transmissions Gray Court LLC, is performing a $500 million expansion of its Gray Court Automatic Transmissions plant in South Carolina.
The project entails adding a 436,000-square-foot building to boost production at the existing 975,000-square-foot plant.
The project is expected to wrap up in mid-2025.
Industrial Info Resources (IIR) has been tracking over 200,000 current and future projects worth $17.8 Trillion (USD).

facts: Sewon America first announced an investment of more than $300 million in February 2023 to construct its new facility.
The facility is located at Grande View Industrial Park on Old Augusta Road in Rincon, Effingham County, with a dedicated address of 1000 Sewon EV Road.
In June 2023, a delegation from South Korea visited the site and signed a memorandum of understanding with Savannah Technical College to create specialized training for future workers.

Lucas Jeung Gi Lee, President/CEO of Sewon America, stated that Effingham County was chosen for its existing infrastructure, proximity to the new Hyundai Metaplant, and ability to hire high-quality workers.
Proprietary machinery, including state-of-the-art robotics for making electric vehicle parts, is being assembled in South Korea.
The new equipment will enable the production of lighter aluminum components instead of steel, reducing final vehicle weight and spark output during welding for safer manufacturing.
The equipment and a team of assemblers from South Korea are scheduled to be sent to the U.S. for installation at the Rincon facility in February.
Sewon America Director of Human Resources Robert Vazquez visited multiple plants in South Korea and described the company culture as high quality and professional.
The first cohort of students completed a week-long training program at Savannah Technical College in January.
Standout students from each cohort will be selected for additional opportunities, with thirty high achievers primed for leadership through further training at Sewon’s LaGrange facility.
The hiring process has begun for 50 technical positions required for the production of the first round of parts.

Vazquez noted that because this type of manufacturing is new to the area, they are seeking candidates with great learning capacity and commitment to quality across HR, IT, safety, quality, production line, engineering, and maintenance roles.
Once the facility is complete, Sewon America-Rincon will employ 740 people at its Effingham County location.
The total number of jobs created by Sewon in Georgia will exceed 1,600.

To date, Sewon has donated $40,000 to the Effingham County School District, Savannah Technical College, City of Rincon, and Effingham County Board of Commissioners to support infrastructure and educational outcomes.
The company is partnering with the student mentorship program within the Effingham County School District to build a talent pipeline starting in middle school.
Sewon plans to deliver high-quality products to the new Hyundai Metaplant as its first electric vehicles roll off the production line.
The company hopes to deliver its first shipment in October 2024 and be fully operational in 2025.
Due to the rapid construction timeline of the Hyundai Metaplant, no groundbreaking ceremony was held for Sewon's project, but a grand opening will be held once production begins.
Effingham County Industrial Development Authority CEO Brandt Herndon stated that the partnership with Sewon will bring a prosperous era for the county and its residents.
source_title: Sewon Drives Positive Growth: An Update on Rincon’s Largest Economic Development Project | Effingham County Development Authority

facts: Shiroki North America is a top-tier automotive supplier and manufacturer.
Shiroki produces seat tracks, door frames, window regulators, and other parts for Toyota, Nissan, Subaru, Mitsubishi, as well as others.
In addition to manufactured parts, Shiroki creates the actual fixtures and jigs required to create the components they manufacture for their clients.
Shiroki worked with MISUMI to standardize its components and designs to streamline its design process.
Standardizing parts and processes generated both cost and time savings for the shop floor.
Brandon Johnson serves as the Assistant General Manager of Engineering and Maintenance for Shiroki North America.
Once engineers design a part to precise dimensions and specifications, that part is standardized to help save time and control costs.
MISUMI assigns a unique product number to standardized parts, which greatly facilitates re-ordering.
Shiroki took advantage of MISUMI’s CAD configurator to generate standard CAD parts for its designs.
Using the CAD configurator reduced the need for time- and labor-intensive fabrication of custom parts, whether in-house or by an outside supplier.
MISUMI’s CAD support offers automatic download of native CAD files, meaning engineers do not have to spend time drawing components or pay an outside supplier to draw them.
As a result of standardizing by using MISUMI’s products, standard parts configurator, and downloadable files, Shiroki saw direct results to its bottom line.
MISUMI enabled Shiroki to build a sizable machine development group and actually manufacture the machines within its own company rather than undertaking the time, hassle, and expense of outsourcing.
This initiative led Shiroki North America to achieve the largest cost savings in its history.
source_title: Shiroki North America Saves Costs with Standard Part Configurator

facts: About 300 Koreans were arrested at a U.S. battery factory construction site.
Domestic corporations are rechecking their investment strategies in the U.S. market to brace for uncertainty following the incident.

LG Energy Solution, Hyundai Motor, Samsung Electronics, Samsung SDI, SK hynix, SK On, CJ CheilJedang, and LS Cable & System are pursuing the construction and expansion of factories in the United States.
LG Energy Solution and Hyundai Motor invested about $6.3 billion (8.8 trillion won) at the HL-GA plant.
Key personnel are all leaving the United States, and construction of the HL-GA plant is expected to be delayed by at least two to three months.
LG Energy Solution is building factories in four areas, including Queen Creek, Arizona; Lansing, Michigan; and Fayette County, Ohio.
Samsung SDI is building joint-venture plants in Indiana with Stellantis and General Motors (GM), respectively.
The combined investment in the two Samsung SDI plants amounts to $7.4 billion (10.3 trillion won).
SK On is also building plants in Georgia, Kentucky, and Tennessee.
Samsung Electronics is constructing a foundry (semiconductor contract manufacturing) plant in Texas with a total investment of more than $37 billion (51.6 trillion won).
SK hynix is preparing to build a back-end semiconductor plant in Indiana for next-generation high bandwidth memory (HBM) production by investing $3.87 billion (5.4 trillion won).

Hanwha decided to make an additional $5 billion (7 trillion won) investment in the Philadelphia Naval Shipyard as part of the Korea-U.S. shipbuilding industry cooperation project "MASGA" (Make American Shipbuilding Great Again).
Including the battery industry, the scale of investment that domestic corporations are pouring across the United States in semiconductors, shipbuilding, and automobiles exceeds 200 trillion won.
An official in the battery industry said they are analyzing the impact that the visa issue will have on future business in the United States and are checking existing investment plans.
The incident is prompting louder calls for improvements to the visa system, with the government launching a working group to resolve visa issues between Korea and the United States.
The government plans to discuss concrete measures, including expanding employment visa quotas.

Presidential chief of staff Kang Hoon-sik emphasized that although it takes time to fundamentally overhaul the problem, discussions must proceed quickly to remove distrust so corporations can invest in and work in the United States safely.
source_title: Korean corporations reassess US investments after mass detentions at battery plant - CHOSUNBIZ
