canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and SK On EV Battery Manufacturing Facility
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and SK On selected a site in Bartow County for a new EV battery manufacturing facility.
The new facility will supply Hyundai Motor Group’s plants in the U.S.
Stakeholders estimate the project will create more than 3,500 new jobs through approximately $4-5 billion of investment in Bartow County.
Hyundai Motor Group and SK On recently signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) regarding the partnership for the new EV battery facility in the U.S.
Details of the HMG and SK On partnership are still in development.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Hyundai Motor Group and SK On are valued partners and key players in Georgia’s ever-growing automotive industry.
SK On was established in 2021 as the lithium-ion battery subsidiary branch of SK Innovation.
SK On currently employs more than 2,000 Georgians at its SK Battery America facility in Commerce.
In July, SK Battery America announced it would partner with the Work for Warriors Georgia program to hire veterans, servicemembers, and their families at its Georgia operations.
The new facility's development is subject to execution of relevant agreements and final board decisions by HMG and SK On.
The selected site is Bartow Centre, a zoned manufacturing and industrial site located on Highway 411.
The project aims to begin operations in 2025.
Bartow County Sole Commissioner Steve Taylor expressed pleasure with the decision and thanked the owners of the former Bartow Centre Industrial property.
The project will establish Bartow in Georgia’s EV ecosystem and leverage the county's strong history and talent base in advanced manufacturing, the automotive industry, and innovation.
Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project.
The GDEcD partnered with Georgia EMC, Development Authority of Bartow County, the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development, and Georgia Quick Start on this competitive project.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that HMG and SK have been pioneering partners for Georgia for decades, with HMG being a major driver for Georgia’s automotive renaissance and SK On being the first Korean manufacturer to locate in the state.
The partnership aims to create a fully integrated supply chain for automotive OEMs while connecting battery manufacturers with recyclers to close the loop on battery manufacturing.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric transportation industry due to its prime location, major ports system, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and pro-business climate.
Excluding this announcement, EV-related projects announced in Georgia since 2020 total approximately $17 billion in investment and more than 22,800 new jobs.
Hyundai Motor Group separately announced in May plans to invest $5.54 billion in Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, an EV manufacturing facility in Bryan County.
Hyundai Motor Group broke ground on the Metaplant America facility in October with Governor Kemp and other state, local, and federal officials.
source_title: Hyundai, SK On Select Bartow County for Build EV Battery Facility in Georgia - The EV Report

facts: The U.S. Labor Department said on Monday that SK Battery America faces $77,200 in fines for five serious safety violations.
Workers at the company's Georgia plant suffered potentially permanent respiratory damage from an October 2023 lithium battery fire.
This is the second violation notice issued by the department against SK Battery America in recent months.
In January, OSHA said SK Battery exposed employees at a U.S. battery plant to unsafe levels of nickel and other metals.
The company faces $75,000 in fines over six serious violations from the January citation.
SK Battery supplies Volkswagen and Ford Motor.
The company employs 3,100 workers at its Commerce, Georgia plant.
OSHA Area Office Director Joshua Turner stated that on multiple occasions in less than a year, they found SK Battery America failing to meet required federal safety standards for workers.
OSHA emphasized that when employers fail to provide safe and healthful workplaces, it will hold them accountable.
SK Battery America stated it is working diligently to assess the issues raised and resolve any questions with the ultimate goal of employee safety.
The company stated that it has comprehensive safety protocols and training programs in place.
SK Battery America is a subsidiary of SK On, a global EV battery manufacturer.
SK On is a unit of SK Innovation, which is part of SK Group, the second-largest conglomerate in South Korea.
SK Battery America did not immediately comment on Monday regarding the new citation.
The company has challenged the findings from the January citation before the independent Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission.
OSHA said following the October 2023 fire that SK Battery failed to train its employees on how to protect themselves adequately in the toxic atmosphere that left multiple workers injured.
OSHA said SK exposed workers to inhalation hazards, including hydrofluoric acid vapors produced in lithium battery fires, by failing to establish a complete emergency response plan.
OSHA said SK Battery did not ensure its staffing agency Moveret Inc., which supplied workers at the site, made their employees aware of the hazards associated with lithium battery fires.
OSHA said in January that SK Battery exposed employees working with cobalt, nickel and manganese to respiratory hazards by failing to complete a workplace hazard assessment.
The company failed to ensure employees were given clean, disinfected and sanitary respirators.
The company failed to store respirators properly.
source_title: US says SK Battery faces $77,200 in fines over employee health hazards

facts: SK Battery America has invested $2.6 billion in Jackson County to build two manufacturing facilities to supply electric vehicles.
The facilities will supply batteries for the Ford F-150 Lightning and the Volkswagen ID.4.
In January, the company announced it had exceeded its hiring goal of 2,600 employees at the end of 2022.
Mass production began in 2022.
The company has reached its target for growth, which was exceeded, and is now up to about 3,000 employees.
SK Battery helps keep the tax base balanced between residential, commercial, and industrial sectors in Jackson County.
Jackson County has a more diverse industrial and commercial landscape than ever before due to SK Battery.
Businesses that have located in or close to Jackson County are suppliers of SK Battery.
Continued interest from businesses in Jackson County is driven by SK Battery.
SK Battery provides good-paying advanced manufacturing jobs for local citizens.
The county's unemployment rate has hovered around 2% due to this growth.
SK Battery utilizes a regional workforce, pulling employees from surrounding counties.
Daytime population increases from SK Battery employees are helping increase sales tax collections in the county.
Commerce city is drawing attention specifically because of SK Battery.
Commerce has strategically developed industrial areas away from its central business district and housing to accommodate growth related to SK Battery.
source_title: Jackson County: Tidal Wave of Growth - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: On February 14, SK Battery America announced it was laying off 958 workers at its plant in Commerce, Georgia, representing 37% of the facility's total workforce.
The Commerce, Georgia plant manufactured lithium-ion battery cells for the Ford F-150 Lightning.
Ford cancelled the electric F-150 program after Congress eliminated the $7,500 EV tax credit in a reconciliation bill last fall.
The SK Battery plant is located in Commerce, Jackson County, Georgia, a rural community of about 72,000 people, and was the largest private employer in the county.
Laid-off workers were drawing $22-$28/hour wages, a significant step up from previous employment.
Many laid-off workers had bought homes or cars in anticipation of stable long-term employment.
The layoff was announced with only 60 days notice, leaving little time to adjust.
Georgia's state gas tax is $0.29/gallon, which is below the national average.
Georgia has pipeline access through the Colonial Pipeline, which runs from Houston to New Jersey and passes directly through the state.
Georgia's current average gas price is $3.41/gallon, up $0.23 since February 28.
At current crude price trends, Georgia's average gas price is projected to cross $3.50 within the next 7-10 days.
Workers in Jackson County commute approximately 25 miles each way on rural roads with no public transit alternative, making $3.50 gas a direct cost burden.
The Democratic incumbent in Georgia's Senate race had a narrow path dependent on running up margins in Atlanta's suburbs while keeping losses in rural areas manageable.

In suburban areas, educated voters tend to care about climate policy and supported the EV tax credit, but the political framing that Democrats killed good Georgia jobs by protecting EV credits is factually backwards since the credits were eliminated by Republican-aligned legislation.

The combination of EV job losses and high gas prices creates a narrative for opposing campaigns that Washington made a bet on electric vehicles, killed affordable gas cars, and now residents cannot afford to fill up their current vehicles.
Georgia exemplifies a broader pattern across competitive Senate races where rural economies are heavily impacted by energy costs compared to insulated suburban or biotech hubs.
In North Carolina, the Research Triangle's biotech economy is insulated from energy costs in ways that rural western North Carolina is not.
Maine has the highest percentage of homes heated by oil of any state in the country, making its energy cost story particularly acute.
Suburban Phoenix voters in Arizona are among the most car-dependent in the country and have swung competitive for Democrats in recent cycles.
The historical threshold above which the incumbent party's losses in midterm elections become severe is a $3.75/gallon national average gas price.
At the time of writing, the national average gas price was $3.31, leaving it 44 cents away from the $3.75 threshold.

If a Hormuz disruption lasts five weeks and Goldman's $100/barrel scenario plays out, national gas prices could cross $3.75 before Memorial Day, giving the energy price story five months of impact before the election.
If a ceasefire materializes within two weeks and prices retreat to $3.00-$3.10 by summer, the energy price story would fade and elections would revert to previous dynamics.
Georgia is identified as the state where job destruction from the energy transition and price pain from energy disruption are most visibly converging.
source_title: Georgia's EV Layoffs + $3.50 Gas = A Senate Race That Just Changed

facts: The future of the automotive industry is electric, with rapid growth in companies directly supporting the EV market.
Kentucky has positioned itself as a national leader within the EV market in the United States.
Gov. Andy Beshear stated that the EV and battery infrastructure being created will make Kentucky an industry leader and attract quality companies and jobs for decades to come.
Beshear claimed the Commonwealth has resources making it the perfect location for growth within the EV and energy storage industries.
Beshear stated Kentucky will be the nation’s top producer of electric-vehicle batteries.
Within the past 14 months, Kentucky announced its two largest economic development projects in its history.
These announcements established Kentucky as the new EV battery capital in the United States.
In August, Gov. Beshear joined state and local officials and representatives from Japan-based Envision AESC to break ground on a $2 billion gigafactory in Bowling Green’s Kentucky Transpark.
The 30 GWh plant will create 2,000 skilled jobs in South Central Kentucky.
The plant will produce battery cells and modules that will power up to 300,000 next-generation EVs by 2027.
The batteries will be manufactured for multiple global automotive manufacturers.
Jeff Deaton, managing director of Envision AESC U.S., said the decision to invest in Kentucky was an obvious choice due to the readiness of the skilled workforce required for a project of this scale.
Deaton noted that batteries are fast becoming a strategically important industry powering the United States’ move to electric vehicles and Kentucky’s economy and jobs.
In September 2021, Gov. Beshear and executives from Ford Motor Co. and SK On announced the BlueOvalSK Battery Park in Hardin County.
The $5.8 billion project is set to create 5,000 jobs in the heart of the state.
The facility will be home to two battery plants to power the next generation of Ford and Lincoln electric vehicles.
In August 2022, Ascend Elements announced an investment of $310 million to create 250 full-time jobs in Christian County.
Ascend Elements is a producer of advanced, sustainable battery materials made from recycled lithium-ion batteries.

Thanks to more than $480 million in federal grants awarded by the U.S. Department of Energy, the Ascend Elements project now includes a nearly $1 billion investment and will create more than 400 full-time jobs.
The expanded project is known as Apex 1 and will establish the company’s largest U.S. operation.
CEO Mike O’Kronley stated that clean energy and climate technology industries are bringing good jobs to communities across the country.
O’Kronley noted they are building a domestic source of sustainable lithium-ion cathode material for EV batteries that does not exist anywhere in the United States.
The location will house the Hydro-to-Cathode process, a closed-loop direct precursor synthesis manufacturing system that generates no toxic waste and minimal carbon emissions.
The operation will produce enough recycled battery precursor and sustainable cathode active materials to equip up to 250,000 electric vehicles annually.
Ascend Elements’ Kentucky operation will serve a range of EV-related customers and build on partnerships with American Honda Motor Co., EcoPro Group, and SK Battery America.
In January 2022, Piston Automotive LLC increased the scope of a Jefferson County expansion to include 117 full-time jobs with a $26.3 million-plus investment.
Piston Automotive LLC is a provider of automotive assemblies, including advanced electric- and hybrid-vehicle battery systems.
The expansion includes a new production line and increased capabilities to support growing market demand for the EV industry in Kentucky and throughout the region.
Kentucky leverages its central geographic location, logistics strength, and skilled workforce as a premier destination for the EV industry.
source_title: Supplying success: Why Kentucky's EV & battery industry momentum is here to stay - Building Kentucky

facts: SK On, a major South Korean battery manufacturer, has laid off nearly 1,000 workers at its US battery plant in Georgia amid slowing electric vehicle (EV) demand.

SK Battery America, SK On's US unit, dismissed 968 employees at its battery manufacturing facility in Commerce, Georgia, according to a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) notice posted on a Georgia state website.
The layoffs reportedly account for about 37 per cent of the plant's total workforce of around 2,500 employees.
The company said the workforce reduction was part of restructuring efforts prompted by slowing EV sales and changing market conditions.
The decision was also made to adjust operations while maintaining its commitment to the state of Georgia and efforts to build a strong U.S. supply chain for advanced battery manufacturing.
The Georgia plant has been supplying EV batteries to automakers including Germany's Volkswagen and South Korea's Hyundai Motor.
It also supplied batteries for Ford Motor's F-150 Lightning electric pickup truck, though profitability was affected after Ford canceled production plans for the model.
SK On is currently building a second battery plant in Georgia to supply batteries to Hyundai Motor, with production scheduled to begin in the first half of this year.
Another plant in Tennessee is expected to begin production in 2028.
In October last year, Governor Brian Kemp of the state of Georgia met with the head of South Korean battery maker SK On to discuss ways to enhance cooperation and strengthen business ties.
Kemp and his delegation visited SK On's headquarters in central Seoul earlier in the day and had talks with Lee Seok-hee, chief executive officer (CEO) of the Korean battery manufacturer.
The two previously met in June 2024 during Kemp's visit to Seoul.
SK On operates a 22 gigawatt-hour battery plant in Commerce, Georgia through its U.S. unit, SK Battery America.
SK On is building another facility jointly with Hyundai Motor Group in Bartow County.
source_title: SK On lays off nearly 1,000 workers at US battery plant amid EV slowdown - www.lokmattimes.com

facts: SKBA is a security facility that strictly controls outsiders' access and serves as an intensive force of advanced battery technology.
The factory requires workers to wear dustproof suits, masks, and gloves, tolerating even minor dust.
Production involves applying cathode and anode materials evenly on metal foil, drying them, and cutting them to specific thickness and density according to customer requests.
SK-on's core Z-stacking method completes pouch-type battery cells by cross-laying electrode plates like folding paper.
Robot arms and artificial intelligence (AI) inspection systems are used to ensure accurate interlocking of electrode plates and separators for stable performance.
Each cell is assembled with surgical precision.
Real-time production yields and targets are updated in minutes and seconds on large monitors installed for each line.
Monitor screens show yield data at levels where almost no defective products are found, such as 98 percent and 99 percent.
Achieving results required training over 3,000 unskilled local workers in Georgia, which was difficult for middle-class U.S. workers mainly from the service industry to transition to manufacturing.
SKBA introduced a two-week "boot camp" training system covering basic safety education and basic manufacturing training before entering the production line.
Many applicants left during the first month of boot camp due to high initial dropout rates, requiring additional hiring.
Choi Yoon-sang, head of SKBA, noted that Americans were not used to working weekends if necessary without being late.
A strikeout system allowing workers to be fired after three instances of lateness was introduced to inject Korean-style production DNA.

Production equipment and process technology originally designed for Korean body types were modified to suit Americans, including raising machine screen eye levels and enlarging buttons for larger hands.
Non-technical improvements minimizing human errors from operator manipulation are considered equally important to machine and technology improvements for maximizing yield.
Climate challenges include annual typhoons causing factory difficulties in controlling humidity.
Battery quality can be affected if humidity increases by only 1-2 percent.
Flexibility to respond appropriately through comprehensive judgment is essential when external variables like humidity cannot be eliminated.
Efforts to improve quality led to favorable reviews from local automakers and positive regional perceptions, creating a virtuous cycle.
SKBA is working on more than 100 production improvement projects to further boost yields in the second half of this year.
A "language service team" will be organized to solve language problems and facilitate communication between engineers and workers to harmonize culturally.
The good performance of SK-on's North American production base is expected to provide an opportunity for SK Group to rebound in its overall performance.
The facility has begun deriving tangible indicators and numbers in the battery business sector, positioning it as a future growth driver rather than a concern for the group.
source_title: Commerce City, an hour and a half drive from Atlanta, Georgia, United States. When you enter a small.. - MK

facts: SK On Co., a major South Korean battery manufacturer, laid off nearly 1,000 workers at its U.S. battery plant in Georgia amid slowing electric vehicle demand.

SK Battery America Inc., SK On's U.S. unit, dismissed 968 employees at its battery manufacturing facility in Commerce, Georgia, according to a Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) notice posted on a Georgia state website.
The layoffs account for about 37 percent of the plant's total workforce of around 2,500 employees.
The company said the workforce reduction was part of restructuring efforts prompted by slowing EV sales and changing market conditions.
The decision was made to adjust operations while maintaining its commitment to the state of Georgia and efforts to build a strong U.S. supply chain for advanced battery manufacturing.
The Georgia plant has been supplying EV batteries to automakers including Germany's Volkswagen and South Korea's Hyundai Motor.
It also supplied batteries for Ford Motor's F-150 Lightning electric pickup truck, though profitability was affected after Ford canceled production plans for the model.
SK On is currently building a second battery plant in Georgia to supply batteries to Hyundai Motor.
Production at the second Georgia plant is scheduled to begin in the first half of this year.
Another SK On plant in Tennessee is expected to begin production in 2028.

facts: At the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) Grand Opening on March 26, President and Chief Executive Officer of Hyundai Motor Company José Muñoz shared that Hyundai planned to increase direct jobs to 14,000.
His statement appeared to imply that the job commitment update correlated to the announcement that the metaplant would increase production from 300,000 units to 500,000.

Assistant Manager of Public Relations Bianca Johnson wrote to the Savannah Morning News (SMN) via email on April 3 to correct media statements regarding jobs numbers and a second EV battery plant announcement.
Hyundai has clarified that it has entered into two joint venture battery plant projects: one on the Bryan County Megasite in partnership with LG Solutions, referred to as HL-GA Battery Company.
The second joint venture is with SK On Battery of America for a site in Bartow County slated to open by the end of 2025.
SK On already owns and operates two EV battery plants in Commerce, Georgia.
A Hyundai press release from March 24 stated that it would create 14,000 direct jobs across the entire U.S., not just Georgia.

Some of the additional jobs may eventually include roles related to HMGMA's increased production, though when it plans to meet those goals and how many additional jobs may be added remain in development at this time.

HMGMA Head of Communications Joe LaMuraglia noted that the company is still in start up mode and will soon begin hiring Meta Pros (its name for its employees) for the second shift to work toward increasing unit production.
A corporate Hyundai press release dated March 26 stated that the Group's investment in Georgia related to HMGMA and joint battery ventures with LG Energy Solution and SK is $12.6 billion.
This represents the largest single investment in the state's history and includes a total of 8,500 jobs at HMGMA by 2031.
SK On's job numbers do not factor into the 8,500 jobs slated for Bryan County megasite as described by Hyundai's economic development agreement with Georgia.
HMGMA alone is not responsible for all 8,500 jobs; on-site affiliates are also expected to factor into the job commitment total.

While HMGMA does not oversee hiring at its affiliate organizations, the economic agreement indicates that HMGMA will be responsible for compiling and reporting the annual job numbers for the entire megasite.

Savannah Economic Development Authority (SEDA) President Trip Tollison confirmed that starting in 2031, those numbers are to be reported to the Savannah/ Interstate-16 Corridor Joint Development Authority (Savannah JDA) and Georgia Department of Economic Development under a process called 'project verification.'

HMGMA Chief Administrative Officer Brent Stubbs and other officials had stated at the grand opening that the plant itself will hire roughly 2,600 employees, though it could potentially be more as a result of the increased production announcement.
On-site affiliate HL-GA Battery Company has committed to creating 2,000 jobs.
Hyundai Mobis has projected hiring another 1,578 according to the Savannah JDA's Announced HMGMA Suppliers webpage.
LaMuraglia said that HMGMA does not know the hiring projections for either Hyundai Glovis or Hyundai Transys.
The two affiliates (Glovis and Transys) should account for roughly 620 to 2,320 jobs that would round out the total jobs commitment for the megasite.
Neither Transys or Glovis representatives responded to inquiries made prior to publication.
A breakdown of the numbers shows that 6,800 total jobs on the megasite with an average annual salary of $58,105 is 80% of the full 8,500 commitment.
The 8,500 job threshold must be reached by 2031 in order to comply with the economic development agreement terms.
Hyundai's latest U.S. investments also aim to expand partnerships with U.S. companies working on autonomous driving, robotics, AI, and logistics.
Johnson shared that the investment includes improvements to Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama and Kia Georgia.
Portions of the $21 billion will also go toward the construction of a Louisiana steel mill, where Hyundai plans to annually produce 2.7 million tons of steel.
In addition to Hyundai's 14,000 direct jobs, the March 26 press release notes that all of the automaker's investments would result in about 100,000 direct and indirect jobs.
LaMuraglia said the number is likely based on a Center for Automotive Research (CAR) May 2023 report.
The CAR report reads, 'for every 1 Hyundai U.S. job it will generate 7.3 other jobs.'

The CAR report says 'spin-off or expenditure-induced employment—the jobs supported by the HMA [Hyundai Motor America] employees and suppliers' workers who spend their earnings in the economy' account for indirect jobs.

The idea behind the job multiplier is that nearly every sector, from the service industry and public safety to education and healthcare, would need to increase to meet demands and needs of the new manufacturing workers and the communities in which they will live.
For example, St. Joseph's/Candler just opened a new care facility in Ellabell across I-16 from the metaplant to accommodate its employees and their families.

Also related, last summer Augusta University's Medical College of Georgia opened a four-year Savannah location at Georgia Southern University's Armstrong Campus to help address the healthcare shortage in the region as well as throughout the state.
source_title: Hyundai's metaplant jobs commitment clarified following grand opening confusion

facts: SK Battery America has exceeded its hiring goal of 2,600 employees two years ahead of schedule for its two lithium-ion battery manufacturing facilities in Commerce, Georgia.
The company met its hiring goal at the end of 2022 and is further expanding its workforce to approximately 3,000 over the next year to meet growing demand for electric vehicles.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that SK Battery America and the State of Georgia share a common trait of never resting on their laurels and always working to reach the next milestone.
Governor Brian Kemp thanked SK Battery America for going above and beyond in delivering on its commitments.

Governor Brian Kemp noted SK Battery America is one of the first companies to put Georgia on the e-mobility map, with a relationship that continues to bring opportunity to communities across the state.
SK Battery America announced its first project in Georgia in 2018, with an initial planned investment of $1.6 billion.

Since 2019, the company has invested $2.6 billion to build two battery manufacturing facilities in Jackson County to supply electric vehicles, including the Ford F-150 Lightning and the Volkswagen ID.4.
SK Battery America started mass production in early 2022.
SK Battery America CEO Timothy Jeong credited the state’s Quick Start program for helping attract and train workers with a speed and scale difficult for any company to achieve on its own.
More than 300,000 new electric vehicles a year will be on American roads because of the work being done at the Georgia facilities.
SK Battery America has worked with Georgia Quick Start, the state’s top-ranked job training program through the Technical College System of Georgia, since announcing its first project in 2018.

In July 2022, SK Battery America announced a partnership with the Georgia National Guard’s Work for Warriors Georgia program to focus on hiring veterans, servicemembers, retirees, reservists, and their families.
The company has held numerous career fairs, visited schools, and held community events in recent months to attract talent.
To attract the highest-quality workforce, SK Battery America has consistently improved wages, benefits, and its overall work environment.
Tom Crow, Chairman of the Jackson County Board of Commissioners, appreciated SK Battery America’s commitment to provide high quality jobs within the county.
Tom Crow expressed gratitude for SK Battery America’s substantial financial investment and looked forward to seeing the benefits the operation will continue to bring to the community.
The company is still hiring for jobs in production, quality, utility, logistics, ERT, maintenance, and other areas.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson praised SK Battery America’s commitment to providing high-skilled, high-paying jobs for Georgians and meeting standing commitments and aggressive hiring needs.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson commended SK Battery America’s dedication to the state, its adopted community, and the people of Georgia.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson noted SK Battery America’s out-front role in the development of the electric mobility ecosystem in the state and participation in every opportunity presented.
SK On is the parent company of SK Battery America and a global leading electric vehicle battery developer, manufacturer, and solutions provider.
As SK On’s U.S. production base, SK Battery America produces one of the world’s safest, fastest charging and longest-lasting batteries.
The batteries produced at the Georgia facilities are currently installed in the Ford F-150 Lightning and Volkswagen ID.4.
The combined capacity of the Georgia facilities will make 22 GWh of battery cells a year.
This production capacity is enough to power the equivalent of about 300,000 EVs annually.
Since 2019, SK Battery America has invested a total of $2.6 billion to build EV battery plants in Commerce, Georgia.
source_title: SK Battery America Exceeds Hiring Goal, On Track to Reach 3,000 Workers - AllOnGeorgia

facts: SK Battery America has announced a significant workforce reduction affecting 958 employees at its Georgia manufacturing facility.
This marks one of the largest single-site layoffs in the electric vehicle battery manufacturing sector this year.

A typical severance formula might provide 1-2 weeks of base pay per year of employment, though actual packages can vary significantly based on company policy, role level, and individual circumstances.

Affected employees may receive additional benefits beyond base severance pay, potentially including continuation of health insurance coverage, outplacement services to assist with job search, and in some cases, extended vesting of certain benefits.

Under the Older Workers Benefit Protection Act (OWBPA), employees age 40 and older typically receive special protections when signing severance agreements that include releases of age discrimination claims.

Individual terminations usually come with a 21-day review period, while group layoffs of this scale may trigger a 45-day review period and require the employer to provide information about the decisional unit and selection criteria.
Employees in this protected age group typically also receive a 7-day revocation period after signing, during which they can change their minds.
Severance agreements commonly include restrictive covenants such as non-disparagement, confidentiality, and release of claims provisions.
Some manufacturing employers also include non-compete clauses, though enforceability varies by state and circumstance.
Georgia courts may enforce reasonable non-compete agreements, so affected workers should carefully review any restrictions on future employment.
Management severance is typically 2 weeks per year, while union severance is per CBA terms (typically 1 week/year).
Minimum management severance is 4 weeks, with a maximum of 26 weeks for management; union minimums are per contract.
A COBRA subsidy for 3 months is typical, and union employees may have extended coverage per CBA.
Unvested RSUs are forfeited for management, while pensions follow plan terms.
Career transition services are provided for management roles.
Limited enforcement applies to non-solicitation for client-facing roles.
The layoff affects 958 workers at SK Battery America's Georgia manufacturing plant, representing a substantial portion of the facility's workforce.
The reduction was reported in March 2026 and appears to impact production and operations staff at the battery manufacturing site.

Given the single-site nature of this reduction and the headcount exceeding 100 employees, the layoff likely falls under WARN Act coverage, which typically requires employers to provide 60 days advance written notice to affected workers.
The electric vehicle battery manufacturing sector has experienced volatility as automakers adjust production schedules, manage supply chain challenges, and respond to shifting market demand.
SK Battery America's workforce reduction may reflect broader adjustments in the EV supply chain ecosystem.

Under the federal WARN Act, employers with 100 or more full-time employees must generally provide 60 days advance notice before a plant closing or mass layoff affecting 50 or more employees at a single site.
Failure to provide adequate notice may entitle affected workers to back pay and benefits for the notice period not provided.
Georgia does not have a state-specific WARN Act equivalent, so affected employees would rely on federal WARN protections.

For employees over 40 in group layoffs, the Older Workers Benefit Protection Act (OWBPA) generally provides 45 days to review a severance agreement (or 21 days for individual terminations) and 7 days to revoke after signing.
For others, employer-imposed deadlines are often negotiable — there's typically no legal requirement to sign by a specific date.
Severance agreements typically include a release of claims, non-disparagement clauses, and sometimes non-compete provisions.
Understanding exactly what you're agreeing to before signing is critical, as these limits what you can do after signing.
Employees should gather and carefully review any termination letters, severance offer documents, WARN Act notices, and written communications about the layoff.
If over 40 and part of a group layoff, employees must confirm they received the required disclosure information about the decisional unit and selection criteria.
Employees age 40 and older typically have 21-45 days to review severance agreements depending on whether it is classified as a group termination.
Severance packages vary by role, tenure, location, and circumstances.
SimpleSeverance provides negotiation guidance and educational information, not legal advice.
source_title: SK Battery America Layoffs: 958 Georgia Workers Affected | SimpleSeverance

facts: SK Battery America operates a plant in Jackson County near Commerce, Georgia.
Many storefronts in downtown Commerce were vacant when SKBA broke ground on its factory.
Since the factory began commercial operations, a Korean restaurant opened followed by other businesses gradually filling empty spaces.
A city official stated that SKBA has acted as a catalyst for revitalizing downtown Commerce.
Misti Martin, deputy commissioner of global commerce at the Georgia Department of Economic Development, said SKBA is one of the largest employers in northeast Georgia.
Many local residents who used to commute long distances for work can now have good jobs close to home.
Taylor Young from SKBA’s human resources department noted the company offers relatively high wages compared to other local employers.
Many high school graduates choose to work at SKBA.
Engineer Christopher Chastain reported his income rose significantly after switching jobs and built real engineering expertise over four years despite starting with no experience.
Chastain’s wife joined the quality control department three months ago, making multiple family members work at the same facility.
Paula Byars has worked in SKBA’s HR department for nearly three years after learning about the job through her cousin’s spouse in the electrode division.
SKBA has helped improve income levels for many people in the community.
source_title: SK Battery America helps revitalize small Georgia city | The DONG-A ILBO

facts: Georgia is making huge investments in its electric vehicle (EV) future.
According to the U.S. Electric Vehicle Manufacturing Investments and Jobs report, Georgia is one of 10 states accounting for 86% of announced EV manufacturing investments.
According to the Georgia Department of Economic Development, the Peach State is now sixth in the nation for public EV chargers.
Ongoing new projects from Hyundai Motor Group, Rivian, Inc., and SK Investments will increase clean energy jobs and inject a wave of economic benefits into the state’s economy.

Kristi Brigman, deputy commissioner of global commerce at the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated that Georgia has attracted approximately $22.7 billion in investment and 29,000 new jobs related to e-mobility over the last three years.
The state’s e-mobility ecosystem includes industry leaders like SK Battery America, Rivian, and Hyundai Motor Group as well as more than 40 other suppliers, recyclers, and manufacturers.
Hyundai will open its first full EV and battery manufacturing facility in Georgia in 2025.
The Bryan County site is expected to produce 300,000 vehicles yearly.
The Hyundai facility is focused on sustainability for the company and the community.
In Commerce, north of Atlanta, SK Battery is investing $940 million to expand its plant’s capabilities.
The SK Battery expansion will create another 600 new jobs.
This expansion adds to the more than 2,000 new jobs expected due to SK Battery’s statewide $1.67 billion new facility investment.
Rivian will invest $5 billion in a sprawling site across Walton and Morgan counties east of Atlanta for its new electric adventure vehicles.
The Rivian campus will produce nearly half a million cars a year.
The Rivian plant will likely add more than 7,500 new jobs to the local economy.
Kristi Brigman stated that generational projects like Rivian create the jobs of the future.
Brigman noted that when combined with workforce training programs like Quick Start, these projects create opportunities for Georgians to upskill and compete for higher-paying opportunities.
Brigman explained that manufacturing has evolved into high-tech, clean-room operations that require technical skills.

Brigman stated that these projects are transformational for the local economy by diversifying the industry mix and providing a new tax base that contributes to local schools, infrastructure, and other community improvements.
Georgia’s Department of Education is working with all of the company’s leadership to add Electric Vehicle Career Pathway courses to prepare students for clean energy careers.
Brigman stated that Georgia’s success is rooted in its business-friendly environment, robust logistics infrastructure, and focus on building long-term relationships between partners.
Brigman noted that decades of state leadership have invested in assets like the ports, which continue to expand.
The state works with local developers, partners in the university and technical college systems, and the business community to develop solutions to the most pressing challenges companies face today.

State initiatives include speed-to-market solutions like the development-ready site certification program Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development, and customized workforce training through Georgia Quick Start.
According to the Georgia Department of Economic Development, more than 4,000 EVs were sold in Georgia in 2021, representing a nearly 30% annual growth.
As of September 2022, more than 42,500 EVs are registered statewide.
Those EV registration numbers are expected to expand exponentially as the state continues to build EV infrastructure and new clean energy job opportunities multiply.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated during his inaugural address for his second term that this is a unique moment of opportunity for the state and for thousands of hardworking Georgians who will benefit from great jobs and incredible innovative companies for generations to come.
source_title: Just Peachy: Electric Vehicles Investment Is Booming In Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) and SK On have selected a site in Bartow County for a new electric vehicle (EV) battery manufacturing facility.
The new facility will supply Hyundai Motor Group’s plants in the U.S.
Stakeholders estimate the project will create more than 3,500 new jobs through approximately $4-5 billion of investment in Bartow County.
Hyundai Motor Group and SK On recently signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) regarding the partnership for a new EV battery facility in the U.S.
Details of the partnership are still in development.
SK On was established in 2021 as the lithium-ion battery subsidiary branch of SK Innovation.
SK On currently employs more than 2,000 Georgians at its SK Battery America facility in Commerce.
In July, SK Battery America announced it would partner with the Work for Warriors Georgia program to hire veterans, servicemembers, and their families at their Georgia operations.
The new facility is subject to execution of relevant agreements and HMG and SK On’s final board decisions.
The facility will be located at Bartow Centre, a zoned manufacturing and industrial site located on Highway 411.
The project is aiming to begin operations in 2025.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that SK and HMG share his administration's goal of bringing jobs and opportunity to overlooked communities, and expressed pride in their investment in Georgia, calling it the No. 1 state for business.

Bartow County Sole Commissioner Steve Taylor stated that the project will have regional benefit for job seekers, establishes Bartow in Georgia’s EV ecosystem significantly, and noted Bartow’s strong history and talent base built upon advanced manufacturing, the automotive industry, and innovation.
Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with Georgia EMC, Development Authority of Bartow County, the Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development, and Georgia Quick Start on the project.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that HMG and SK have been pioneering partners for Georgia for decades, highlighted Georgia’s prime location, major ports system, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and pro-business climate as attractive factors, and expressed excitement for creating a fully integrated supply chain while connecting battery manufacturers with recyclers to close the loop on battery manufacturing.
Excluding this announcement, EV-related projects announced in the state since 2020 total approximately $17 billion in investment and more than 22,800 new jobs in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group separately announced in May its plans to invest $5.54 billion in Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, an EV manufacturing facility in Bryan County.
Hyundai Motor Group broke ground on the Metaplant America facility in October with Governor Kemp and other state, local, and federal officials.

facts: Headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten Energy is a leading U.S.-based manufacturer with more than 14 gigawatt-hours of energy storage production capacity annually.
Stryten provides advanced lead, lithium and vanadium battery solutions, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.
Founded on 130 years of battery expertise, Stryten Energy employs more than 2,500 people across 10 U.S. manufacturing plants and four R&D centers.
Stryten offers a complete offering of advanced lead, lithium and vanadium batteries to allow customers to select the right chemistry or combination for their energy needs.
Stryten’s premium solutions are delivered through five focused divisions: Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation power, Military and Government, and Energy Components.
Energy Components uses a proven plastic injection molding methodology to produce critical automotive, motive and essential power battery components.
In August 2023, Stryten Energy installed a VRFB system with Snapping Shoals EMC, an electric cooperative in the Atlanta metro area.
The VRFB system was specifically designed and sized to conduct long-duration testing that is six hours or more.
This project is the first VRFB energy storage system manufactured and installed in Georgia.

Mike Judd, CEO and President of Stryten Energy, stated the demonstration project is an important step to proving the capabilities of VRFB technology in utility use cases such as energy cost control, peak shaving and avoiding curtailment, and that the partnership with Snapping Shoals provides the ideal platform to test the system and show the potential impact of Georgia companies working together to bring clean energy to the state.
A lead battery energy storage system (BESS) is installed at VICARS Community Center, connecting the BESS to a new microgrid, solar array and the legacy power grid.

This resiliency hub will provide the community access to reliable power for their essential devices, continue to receive information as emergency situations develop, store medications sensitive to temperature, and safely gather in the aftermath of an emergency or severe weather event.
The VICARS Community Center project went live in July 2024.
Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens, Representative Nikema Williams and EPA Administrator Michael Regan joined Pastor Kevin Early for the ribbon-cutting at Vicars Community Center.

Stryten Energy has received a contract award from the Defense Innovation Unit’s (DIU) FAStBat project to develop and evaluate a prototype 6T lithium-ion battery and potentially extend the technology to other cross-service platforms.
The FAStBat program seeks to support the domestic growth of manufacturing capacity to underpin the United States defense industrial base.
Stryten is investing in domestic lithium battery assembly to support its next-generation lithium battery production for military, essential power and industrial applications.
Stryten plans to source 100% domestically made lithium cells as the U.S. lithium battery supply chain and manufacturing capabilities mature.
Production of the domestically sourced lithium batteries will launch in early 2025 and will initially support Stryten’s military and industrial customers.
Stryten has been awarded Phase One MAKE IT Prize funding to build a domestic vanadium electrolyte manufacturing plant to support long-duration energy storage.
The Securing America’s Vanadium Electrolyte Supply (SAVES) project will deploy Stryten’s proprietary reactor design to provide domestically produced, low-cost electrolyte.

Stryten Energy recently announced a partnership with Dragonfly Energy to distribute a mix of existing Battle Born Batteries brand of lithium-ion batteries for automotive aftermarket applications and jointly develop new battery models specifically tailored to Stryten’s target markets.

Mike Judd, CEO and President, stated the addition of Dragonfly Energy’s Battle Born Batteries provides customers access to a well-known brand of quality lithium batteries, gives Stryten a complete lithium battery portfolio across multiple industries, and noted that Dragonfly Energy’s mission to produce American-made lithium batteries complements Stryten’s vision for a domestic supply chain and circular economy.
Stryten Energy commemorated the opening of its expanded, state-of-the-art battery testing laboratory in August 2024.

The lab will support Stryten Energy’s engineering, research and development, and quality assurance departments, as well as manufacturing facilities, in new product development, technology evaluations, warranty investigations and root cause analysis.

A proclamation from Lieutenant Governor Burt Jones recognized the celebration, stating Stryten Energy is forging a brighter future for economic development in Georgia and fulfilling a shared commitment to innovation and growth, harnessing collective talents for the prosperity of the state of Georgia.
Brian Berland (Senior Director of VRFB Products) and Scott Childers (Vice President of Essential Power) gave a tour of the Stryten HQ microgrid to students from the Georgia Institute of Technology.
At Stryten Energy, each lead battery produced in its U.S. manufacturing plants is made with 90 percent recycled material.
For every battery produced, Stryten recycles the equivalent of that battery through its North American-based recycling partners.
Stryten Energy’s corporate headquarters is equipped with a fully functional microgrid originally designed in 2008-2009 to support the 24×7 operations of a data center.
Stryten will replace the old array with a new system, adding a lead BESS for peak shaving during high demand use periods and leveling total power consumption from the grid each day.
The new HQ BESS will be used for complete access to control the system for future research and development needs and will serve as an onsite testbed for energy storage technologies.
The page notes that the battery industry provides nearly 45% of the world’s rechargeable energy storage and contributes billions of dollars to the domestic economy.
source_title: Essential Energy Tour - Stryten Energy | Battery Council International

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
On January 4, 2023, the company announced executive leadership changes to support its long-term growth strategy.

Dan Autey joined as Executive Vice President, Commercial, on January 3, 2023, responsible for leading commercial strategies across the business.
Tim Vargo stated that Mike Judd's CEO appointment is the culmination of a succession plan developed over several years to achieve increased profitability and growth.
Mike Judd noted that the new leadership team will help meet increased demand for sustainable, clean energy storage solutions.
Dan Autey brings automotive and industrial battery expertise to strengthen customer service capabilities.
Petar Oklobdzija brings tenure in operations and finance in the automotive and battery industries to accelerate growth into new and existing energy storage markets.
Over the past year, Stryten Energy integrated three strategic acquisitions that added advanced lithium-ion and vanadium redox flow battery technologies to its energy storage chemistry portfolio.
The company established a vertically integrated supply chain for engineered energy components to address growing market capacity needs.
Stryten Energy is making ongoing capital investments in its manufacturing plants to meet increased demand for domestically produced, clean, and sustainable energy storage solutions.
The company serves the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
Stryten Energy's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
The company's solutions power applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
The company debuted Reluctance, an innovative mobile microgrid example of a resilient energy ecosystem, at CES 2025 in Las Vegas.
Reluctance is a modified concept Jeep that operates as a hybrid diesel-electric vehicle.

The vehicle is enabled by proprietary software that efficiently manages a variety of energy sources, both carbon-based and renewable, and effectively stores that power for use on demand during prolonged power outages.
Mike Judd, President and CEO of Stryten Energy, stated that Reluctance challenges the status quo with an alternative approach to energy resiliency.
Judd emphasized the need to capture all energy produced from renewables and hydrocarbons in stationary energy storage solutions and mobile systems like Reluctance.
The company utilizes a technology-agnostic Earth to Energy™ model that focuses on building a sustainable, domestic supply chain for each battery chemistry in its product portfolio.
Stryten uses the circular economy of lead batteries as the model for lithium and vanadium energy storage solutions.
The company is creating an energy ecosystem of the future with a battery-first design that provides grid stability and drives efficient use of power generated from renewable and carbon-based sources.

This approach enables long-duration energy storage that can be scaled in size and duration to meet the needs of utilities, commercial and industrial, military bases, and emergency response applications.
Stryten Energy's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
The company powers applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.
Stryten Energy will host two panel discussions at CES with industry experts and government officials to explore grid modernization, battery supply chains, and energy storage systems.

The first panel, Earth to Energy: Reimagining the Future of Energy Resilience, will examine how the U.S. energy industry is keeping up with skyrocketing demand and what the future of the battery supply chain should look like to ensure energy security and independence.
The Earth to Energy panel is scheduled for Tuesday at 2 pm in the Stryten booth.

Panelists for the Earth to Energy session include Eric Hsieh, Deputy Assistant Secretary for Energy Storage at the U.S. Department of Energy Office of Electricity; Dr. Vick Singh, Senior Vice President of Technology of Dragonfly Energy; and Scott Childers, Vice President of Essential Power of Stryten Energy.

The second panel, Scaling Energy: Myths, Microgrids and The Race for Resilience, will focus on current and future power demands on U.S. grid infrastructure and what the energy ecosystem should look like to solve resilience challenges.
The Scaling Energy panel is scheduled for Wednesday at 11 am in the Stryten booth.

Panelists for the Scaling Energy session include Dr. Erik Spoerke, Materials Scientist and Battery Materials Lead at DOE Sandia National Laboratories; Dr. Vick Singh, Senior Vice President of Technology of Dragonfly Energy; and Scott Childers, Vice President of Essential Power of Stryten Energy.
Mike Judd stated that batteries are critical to achieving energy resilience and that no single technology can do it all.
Judd noted that Stryten Energy's domestic, vertically integrated supply chain is the foundation for the energy ecosystem of the future that will set the U.S. on the path to energy independence.
The company partners with some of the world's most recognized companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten Energy is backed by more than a century of expertise in delivering top-performing energy solutions.
source_title: Energy, Automobile, EV, Renewable News | Stryten Energy Debuts Trailblazing Mobile Microgrid Solution to Advance U.S. Energy Resilience at CES 2025

facts: Stryten Energy LLC, headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, announced on January 19, 2022 that it purchased the assets of Storion Energy Inc.
The acquisition brings advanced vanadium redox flow battery (VRFB) technology to Stryten's Essential Power portfolio for applications requiring more than four hours of storage capacity.
VRFB technology is ideal for utility-scale storage, microgrids, military bases, telecommunications, uninterruptible power supply (UPS), railway, and renewable markets.

Storion’s proprietary VRFB design solves the energy-to-power tradeoff by allowing batteries to operate for many hours without sacrificing power or release speed through the addition of more electrolytes.

Key features of the technology include domestically sourced vanadium that is easily recycled, increased power density, reduced battery downtime, potential limitless cycle life with proper maintenance, high-temperature ratings, thermal safety, and scalability.
The acquisition adds a flow battery technology research and development facility to complement Stryten’s four North American R&D centers focused on advanced lead and lithium energy storage solutions.
Stryten leverages its team of 2,500 employees and more than two million square feet of manufacturing space to establish reliable U.S.-engineered and produced long-duration batteries.

Tim Vargo, Chief Executive Officer of Stryten Energy, stated the acquisition positions the company for success in the rapidly growing long-duration energy storage market while expanding capabilities to solve current and future energy challenges.

Mike Judd, President and Chief Operating Officer of Stryten Energy, noted the combination of Stryten’s industrial resources with Storion’s technology will support essential power, motive power, transportation, military, and government customers.

Mohan Misra, Founder, Chairman and CEO of Storion Energy, highlighted that leveraging Stryten’s U.S.-based manufacturing footprint and distribution network will accelerate commercial deployment of VRFB technology.

The new Essential Power division provides advanced lead and lithium battery technologies, services, chargers, and racking systems for backup power in military, government, telecommunications, UPS, railway, utility, microgrid, and renewable sectors.

Storion Energy Inc. originated from a decade of fuel cell research and an ARPA-E-funded redox flow battery development program that began in 2010, with the company formally formed in 2015 as a commercial spinoff from ITN Energy Systems.

The technology aims to stabilize inconsistent power from renewable sources, manage peak load demand and frequency regulation, and support the decarbonization of the electric grid toward President Biden’s goal of net-zero carbon emissions by 2035.
source_title: Stryten Energy Enters The Long-Duration Energy Storage Market With Acquisition of Storion Energy’s Vanadium Redox Flow Battery Technology - CleanTechnica

Under the new agreement, Continental Battery Co. will gain access to Stryten’s portfolio of automotive batteries.
Stryten’s battery portfolio includes a large range of options from standard flooded batteries to advanced technologies such as enhanced flooded batteries (EFB).
Bill Nonnamaker, vice president, transportation sales for Stryten Energy, stated they are looking forward to the partnership to supply high-quality and top-performing automotive batteries.

At Stryten Energy, our commitment to supporting military veterans remains unwavering, according to CEO Mike Judd.
Stryten Energy provided a generous financial donation for the fifth year in a row to support wounded U.S. combat veterans participating in Rivers of Recovery programs.

The donation directly supports combat veterans suffering post-traumatic stress, mild traumatic brain injury, or other psychological wounds of war by allowing them to go through RoR’s outdoor rehabilitation program at no cost.
As part of the sponsorship, veteran employees from Stryten Energy will have the opportunity to participate in several fly-fishing trips as volunteers alongside RoR guides.
For the second consecutive year, Stryten Energy facilitated an additional generous donation on top of its annual donation after winning a charity cornhole tournament for the second year in a row.
Amy Simon is the Executive Director of Rivers of Recovery.
Thanks to Stryten’s extraordinary contribution for 2025, RoR is poised to dramatically expand its impact this year.

Stryten's 2024 donation funded a groundbreaking research partnership with Cornell University on outdoor therapy effectiveness and enabled program expansions across California, Arkansas, Connecticut, Wisconsin, New York, and Idaho.
Stryten's support also helped conduct river trips, launch all-women veterans’ retreats in Wisconsin, and support a landmark Catalina Island experience that served 40 California veterans.
RoR plans to expand its program in 2025 by adding additional trips to California, Arkansas, Connecticut, Wisconsin, New York, and Idaho.

RoR is advancing a pioneering collaboration with Cornell University on outdoor therapy research, enhancing the evidence base for nature-based interventions in veteran rehabilitation and suicide prevention.
RoR is developing innovative technology to measure program outcomes related to resilience and protective factors against suicide.
RoR is exploring the return to states that previously operated successful programs, including Oregon, Washington, and Florida.
RoR is evaluating opportunities to establish new programs in states like Arizona.
RoR is expanding its women veterans initiative by building on the success of women-led Wisconsin retreats and looking at other locations.
In 2024, RoR hosted its first women’s event in Arkansas.
RoR's 2024 success included eight hunting trips across Illinois, New York, and California, including a Catalina Island experience, Stockton archery group, and a second New York turkey hunt.
Stryten is the platinum sponsor for RoR’s Stars and Stripes charity fishing tournament to be held June 5-6, 2025, in Newburg, MD.
Rivers of Recovery was founded in 2008 by Dan T. Cook to help combat veterans overcome the invisible scars of war.

Rivers of Recovery utilizes a medically designed curriculum coupled with outdoor recreational activities to treat combat veterans suffering with Post Traumatic Stress (PTS), mild Traumatic Brain Injury (mTBI), stress, anxiety, and depression.
source_title: Energy, Automobile, EV, Renewable News | Support from Stryten Energy Enables Rivers of Recovery to Expand Veteran Rehabilitation Programs

facts: Stryten Energy has been named a 2024 Gold Military Friendly® Employer for the second year.

The recognition from militaryfriendly.com acknowledges companies that create sustainable career paths, community outreach, brand enthusiasm, and enduring partnerships benefiting the military community.
Stryten Energy hires military members across every level of the company, from executive management to the plant floor, with many having progressed through internal promotions.
Military veterans make up approximately six percent of Stryten Energy's workforce.

Mike Judd, President and Chief Executive Officer at Stryten Energy, stated that the company values the service of veterans and provides opportunities for them to translate military skills into careers in the battery business.
The designation announcement was made on Veterans Day.
Institutions earning the Military Friendly® Employer designation are evaluated using public data sources and responses from a proprietary survey.
Over one thousand companies participated in the 2024 Military Friendly® survey.
Methodology, criteria, and weightings were determined by VIQTORY with input from the Military Friendly® Advisory Council of independent military recruitment leaders.

Final ratings are determined by combining an organization's survey score with assessments of thresholds for Applicant, New Hire Retention, Employee Turnover, and Promotion & Advancement of veterans and military employees.

Kayla Lopez, Senior Director of Military Partnerships at Military Friendly®, stated that designated organizations have invested in initiatives benefiting service members, military spouses, and veterans.
Stryten Energy currently has job openings for military members across numerous U.S. locations.

Stryten Energy sponsors Rivers of Recovery, a national nonprofit that uses outdoor-based activities to treat symptoms of post-traumatic stress, depression, and anxiety in combat veterans and active-duty military.
Stryten Energy provides energy storage solutions and components across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
The company is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten Energy's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
The company powers applications ranging from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.
Stryten Energy is backed by more than a century of industry expertise.
