facts: Georgia Institute of Technology and Stryten Energy LLC announced the successful installation of Stryten Energy’s Lead Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) at the Carbon Neutral Energy Solutions Laboratory (CNES).
The CNES building is located in the North Avenue Research Area of the Georgia Tech campus.

The CNES houses the Strategic Energy Institute (SEI), an interdisciplinary research institute focused on energy research, and multiple research groups dedicated to renewable energy and energy infrastructure-related topics.
The installation aims to create a living-learning lab on campus that supports research and real-world applications of medium-duration energy storage solutions.
Lead BESS was selected for this initial installation due to its cost-effectiveness, high discharge rates, and recyclability.
Extensive research demonstrates the lead BESS's reliable performance.
The BESS is a dynamic storage system that integrates renewable energy sources into the existing power mix.
The system provides stable and dependable backup power and reduces grid dependency during peak hours.

With its additional components and software, the system is capable of bi-directional charging, allowing current to flow into the battery for charging and out of the battery to power the grid or microgrid.

Christine Conwell, SEI’s interim executive director, stated that Georgia Tech’s strategic plan envisions its campus as a dynamic laboratory and experimental test bed where sustainable practices are seamlessly integrated into operations.

Conwell noted that enduring partnerships with organizations like Stryten are creating mini ecosystems that yield valuable situational data to help chart a path for innovative energy research well beyond the campus.
Scott Childers, vice president of essential power at Stryten Energy, said large utility-scale implementations were clearly the focus when solar and other renewables hit the market years ago.
Childers stated that with the introduction of this BESS powered by lead batteries, behind-the-meter applications are getting their day in the sun.
Childers expressed excitement about deploying this unit in commercial and industrial microgrids and paired with EV charging stations to help the U.S. achieve its energy goals.
Childers called Georgia Tech a tremendous partner and highlighted demonstrating the advantages of lead BESS from cost savings, technology, environmental, and safety perspectives.
Richard Simmons, SEI’s director of research and studies, called the Stryten lead BESS system an enabling piece of the Distributed Energy Resources (DER) puzzle.
At the CNES lab, Georgia Tech researchers can now control charging and discharging cycles for the battery in coordination with the existing Solar PV array and the new EV charging test bed.
This research tool will allow the time-shifting of peak solar input by several hours to meet late afternoon building loads.
The system will store renewable energy for the overnight charging of campus vehicles.
DERs are a crucial area of research, particularly understanding their impact on the grid, contribution to system reliability, and effect on energy costs.
This research is especially important in the context of the ongoing transition to clean energy.
Simmons expressed hope that the lead BESS will be one of several living lab battery pilots at Georgia Tech.
Along with regional partners, researchers are exploring similar R&D and testing projects involving flow batteries that can facilitate longer-duration storage.
Researchers are also exploring lithium-ion BESS that may integrate second-life EV battery modules for grid resilience.
These efforts aim to drive advancements in sustainable energy research.
Georgia Tech is one of the top public research universities in the U.S., developing leaders who advance technology and improve the human condition.

Georgia Tech’s leading researchers work across the energy value chain in basic and applied science in EVs, photovoltaics, hydrogen, carbon capture, industrial decarbonization, grid security and resilience, and related social sciences.
Georgia Tech is consistently ranked among the top universities in the nation for graduating underrepresented minorities in engineering, physical sciences, and energy-related fields.
U.S. News & World Report recently ranked Georgia Tech as the No. 1 public university and No. 3 overall in energy and fuels research.
Georgia Tech serves as a regional resource to help communities understand how they can transition to a clean energy economy.
Georgia Tech is the leader in achieving regional impact through education and contributions to communities.

Stryten Energy helps solve the world’s most pressing energy challenges with a broad range of energy storage solutions across the essential power, motive power, transportation, military, and government sectors.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten partners with some of the world’s most recognized companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.
Stryten powers everything from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains, and trucks.
Stryten’s stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
Stryten is an industry leader backed by more than a century of expertise.
source_title: Georgia Tech and Stryten Energy Unveil Installation of Lead Battery Energy Storage System for Advanced Research | The AI Journal

facts: Dragonfly Energy Holdings Corp. signed a $30 million licensing agreement to license its Battle Born Batteries® lithium-ion battery brand to Stryten Energy for global distribution.

The agreement grants Stryten Energy exclusive rights to market and distribute Battle Born Batteries in B2B end markets where Dragonfly Energy currently has no presence, including military, automotive, marine, power sports, lawn and garden, and golf cart applications.
Dragonfly Energy will receive an initial upfront licensing fee of $5 million.
Dragonfly Energy is entitled to mid-single digit royalty payments based on Stryten Energy’s product volume, with a tiered structure reaching up to an additional $25 million.
Upon reaching the additional $25 million threshold, Stryten Energy will be required to pay a nominal annual license fee.
Additional fees for battery design and contract manufacturing services outside the licensing agreement are expected to provide additional revenue to Dragonfly Energy.
The partnership focuses on battery design, contract manufacturing, and licensing of Battle Born Batteries products for new B2B end markets.
Dragonfly Energy will continue its independent operations in existing markets including recreational vehicles (RV), off-grid properties, heavy-duty trucking, stationary storage, and oil and gas.

Stryten Energy is a leading U.S.-based manufacturer of energy storage solutions providing advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium batteries to customers across transportation, industrial, military, and government sectors.
Stryten Energy has a workforce of over 2,500 employees.
Stryten Energy operates ten manufacturing plants.
Stryten Energy is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.

Dragonfly Energy will initially contract manufacture all batteries distributed by Stryten Energy at its facility in Northern Nevada until Stryten Energy establishes its own domestic manufacturing footprint.
The partnership encompasses a mix of existing Battle Born Batteries products with a strong focus on developing new battery models specifically tailored to Stryten Energy's target markets.
Dragonfly Energy will continue to grow its technical support team alongside increasing demand for Battle Born Batteries products.
Dragonfly Energy specializes in cell manufacturing, battery pack assembly, and full system integration.
Hundreds of thousands of reliable Battle Born Batteries packs have been deployed in the field through top-tier OEMs and a diverse retail customer base.

Dragonfly Energy's patented dry electrode manufacturing process delivers chemistry-agnostic power solutions for applications including energy storage systems, electric vehicles, and consumer electronics.
Dragonfly Energy's overarching mission includes the future deployment of its proprietary, nonflammable, all-solid-state battery cells.
Stryten Energy's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
The licensing agreement was announced on July 30, 2024.

Dr. Denis Phares, chief executive officer of Dragonfly Energy, stated that the partnership aims to make lithium batteries more accessible to consumers nationwide alongside other leading brands’ components at major retailers.

Mike Judd, chief executive officer of Stryten Energy, stated that the partnership will allow Stryten Energy to expand its transportation product portfolio and provide customers access to the Battle Born Batteries brand of quality lithium batteries.

Tyler Bourns, chief marketing officer at Dragonfly Energy, stated that leveraging Stryten Energy's vast retail and distribution customer network is a major step forward in making Battle Born Batteries a household name across North America.
source_title: Dragonfly Energy Enters into $30M Licensing Agreement for Battle Born Batteries Brand with Stryten Energy, a Leading North American Battery Manufacturer - EIN Presswire

facts: Stryten Energy showcased breakthrough battery technologies at AAPEX 2025.
The AAPEX event took place from November 4-6 at the Venetian Expo.
Stryten featured its Enhanced Flooded Battery (EFB) technology and the Battle Born® powered by Stryten Energy brand of lithium batteries.
Vehicle computerization and electrical demands are increasing, making battery selection more critical than ever.
EFB technology delivers reliable performance for stop-start and hybrid vehicle applications.
Dan Autey serves as Executive Vice President, Commercial, for Stryten Energy.

According to Dan Autey, vehicle electronics perform well with a Stryten Energy EFB due to its exceptional cycling performance, superior start-stop capability, and outstanding durability in extreme hot and cold conditions.
According to Dan Autey, EFBs deliver greater dynamic charge acceptance, enabling faster recharging compared to conventional flooded batteries.
Battle Born powered by Stryten Energy Batteries are premium LiFePO₄ lithium solutions designed for off-grid living, marine, and powersports applications.
Stryten Energy markets and distributes Battle Born Batteries across most business-to-business markets through an exclusive licensing agreement.
The partnership with Dragonfly Energy (NASDAQ: DFLI) enables Stryten to offer customers a comprehensive portfolio of battery technologies.
A Jeep Gladiator outfitted with an EFB and three Battle Born Batteries was displayed at Stryten's booth.
The booth featured an electric people mover showcasing the new 48v, 105Ah Battle Born by Stryten Energy lithium Golf Cart Battery.
Stryten Energy provides energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
Melissa Floyd holds the title of Vice President, Corporate Communications & Digital Marketing at Stryten Energy LLC.
Stryten Energy's exhibition booth was located at Booth #4216 in the Underhood section of the Venetian Expo.
source_title: Stryten Energy Showcases New 48V 105Ah Golf Cart Battery at AAPEX | DFLI Stock News

facts: Stryten Energy LLC announced the launch of expanded functionality in the Monitor module of its inCOMMAND™ energy performance management software on March 20, 2023.
inCOMMAND, formerly known as GNB Cloud, is an energy performance management software tool that enables users to design and deploy web-connected battery and charger solutions.
The software includes modules for executing power studies, modeling battery and charger solutions, monitoring systems, and managing asset configuration.
The enhanced Monitor module provides remote management capabilities for motive power systems at scale.
Multi-site customers can view battery health data organized by region or location and drill down to the individual asset level.
Dealers can manage rental and service customers from a single dashboard within the software.
The system uses self-adaptive IoT technology enabled by smart gateways to automatically update location tracking for batteries equipped with PowerLoggers as they move between customer sites.
The enhanced Monitor module tracks fleet energy usage, allowing customers to monitor site efficiency, discover optimization opportunities, and reduce costs across corporate fleets.
The software enables new monetization opportunities for dealers, such as a “Power by the Hour” model that bills customers only for battery use instead of charging expensive monthly fees.
Battery licenses are issued upfront with a one-time payment that guarantees data access for the life of the battery and includes multi-user access capability.

Matt Gould, Vice President of Sales and Service, Industrial at Stryten Energy, stated that managing IoT at scale with mobile assets requires smart solutions to track asset locations and configurations across an enterprise to help dealers scale service businesses efficiently and maximize customer value.

Gould added that inCOMMAND offers a 360-degree approach to energy and motive power fleet operation, with the Monitor module supplying necessary data to improve ongoing battery management for dealers and customers.
Stryten Energy provides energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military, and Government sectors.
The company is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Stryten Energy's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers, and energy performance management software.
The company has over a century of industry expertise and partners with major companies to meet the growing demand for reliable and sustainable energy storage capacity.

facts: Stryten Energy's stored energy solutions are built in the U.S. and power applications ranging from forklifts and distribution centers to cars, trains, trucks, microgrids, and telecom networks.
Stryten Energy manufactures advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium batteries and intelligent chargers, and provides cloud-based software for smart fleet design.
Stryten Energy installed a lead Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) at the VICARS Community Center in Southwest Atlanta to provide power resiliency.

Stryten Energy introduced the E-Series AGM160/190 multi-terminal batteries for telecommunications and utility applications, featuring spill-proof designs and suitability for harsh environments and long-duration discharge.
Stryten Energy developed a mobile microgrid solution designed for remote power and disaster relief.
At CES 2026, Stryten Energy convened experts to discuss how batteries can address energy demand challenges in hyperscale data centers driven by AI adoption.
At NAATBatt 2026, Stryten Energy highlighted its domestically sourced and manufactured battery energy storage solutions to meet growing energy demand.
At RE+ 2025, industry experts discussed technical and economic hurdles in scaling solar-plus-storage systems, including software interoperability and supply chain constraints.

At CES 2026, Stryten Energy unveiled lead, lithium, and vanadium redox flow battery technologies for grid resilience and renewable integration, with scalable BESS solutions supporting data centers, manufacturing, and EV charging.
On October 27, 2025, Stryten Energy announced it is delivering battery-first technologies to BCI Flow Batteries North America for commercial, industrial, and utility-scale applications.
Stryten Energy installed a lead BESS at the Georgia Institute of Technology to support the university's carbon-neutral initiatives alongside existing solar infrastructure.
On September 4, 2025, Stryten Energy announced it would feature BESS solutions at RE+ 2025 to deliver reliable power and business continuity during outages and peak demand.
Scott Childers serves as Vice President of Essential Power at Stryten Energy, while Dr. Vick Singh is Chief Operating Officer at Dragonfly Energy; both discussed energy resilience strategies.
On April 23, 2025, Georgia Tech and Stryten Energy announced the successful installation of a lead battery energy storage system for advanced research in Atlanta.
Stryten Energy highlighted the development of a domestic lithium supply chain, noting that lithium batteries offer higher energy density, efficiency, cycle life, and calendar life.
Stryten Energy partnered with Storion Energy to address cost barriers for vanadium redox flow battery long-duration energy storage, offering domestic BESS and vanadium electrolyte.
Stryten Energy formed a joint venture with Largo to launch Storion Energy in Alpharetta, Georgia, aiming to deliver price-competitive vanadium electrolyte for LDES via a leasing model.
Stryten Energy noted that the Inflation Reduction Act's Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (AMPC) provides incentives for domestic clean energy manufacturing investment.
Stryten Energy cited U.S. power demand projections reaching 4,185 billion kWh in 2025, up from 4,067 billion kWh in 2022.
On February 12, 2025, Stryten Energy highlighted its technology-agnostic battery-first approach at NAATBatt 2025 in Orlando, Florida, to address increasing U.S. energy demand.
At CES 2025, Stryten Energy discussed energy resilience as a key innovation area for meeting U.S. energy security and independence goals.

facts: Flow batteries leverage two specialized liquids to generate electricity when they meet each other, separated only by a thin membrane.
The basic flow battery technology dates back to the 1970s, but earlier iterations were bedeviled by corrosion among other issues.
Flow batteries emerged as an application for wind and solar industries in the early 2000s to provide long duration storage solutions for intermittent energy resources.
Georgia is now home to its first ever utility-scale flow battery, which will serve as a model for similar facilities across the country.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp is one of 19 Republican governors who signed their states up to the “Anti-ESG Alliance.”
Snapping Shoals EMC hosts Georgia’s first-ever flow battery and provides electric service to approximately 100,000 homes and businesses in an eight-county area.
The eight-county service area includes large portions of Newton, Henry, and Rockdale counties, as well as parts of DeKalb, Butts, Walton, Jasper, and Morgan counties.

Snapping Shoals EMC belongs to a sprawling network of member-owned utilities established under the Rural Electric Cooperative umbrella during the Great Depression for federally funded electrification opportunities.
Electric cooperatives have a community benefit mission in their charters that enables them to explore new clean power and energy storage technologies.
Stryten Energy, a Georgia-based company, supplied the new battery for the Snapping Shoals project.

Stryten Energy’s partnership with Snapping Shoals will focus on demonstrating the VRFB (Vanadium Redox Flow Battery) energy storage capabilities and evaluating additional use cases for the long-duration energy storage technology.
The Snapping Shoals project represents the first VRFB energy storage system manufactured and installed in the state of Georgia.
Shaun Mock, CEO and President of Snapping Shoals, stated that the cooperative sees Stryten Energy as a natural partner in harnessing the power of VRFB for long-duration energy storage.
Mock added that they are confident VRFB will be a game-changer in how they reliably and affordably store clean energy for their 100,000+ residential and business customers.

Stryten CEO and President Mike Judd explained that long-duration energy storage technologies are needed to support widescale power generation from renewable energy sources and that VRFB is ideally suited for this application.
The Snapping Shoals project will demonstrate the ability of flow battery technology to manage key grid tasks, including cost control, shaving peak demand, and avoiding curtailments.
Stryten noted that Georgia has more than 3.6 gigawatts (GW) of solar, wind, and storage capacity according to the Department of Energy.
There is almost 1.1 GW of additional planned clean energy capacity in the works in Georgia, which will power more than 155,000 homes.
Small-scale flow batteries for home energy storage are already inching into the residential market.
Researchers at Georgia Tech have developed a new space-saving design for flow batteries that could help accelerate the trend toward residential adoption.
Vanadium, a hard silvery-gray transition metal, lends itself to simplicity in flow battery design because it can exist in four different states of oxidation.
Stryten’s vanadium flow battery can operate for more than 20 years without losing storage capacity.
The U.S. Department of Energy’s Pacific Northwest National Laboratory is researching a “sugar battery” that uses a simple sugar derived from starch to enhance flow battery solutions.

In June, Energy Secretary Jennifer Granholm visited Georgia and stated that since the start of the Biden Administration, DOE has tracked more than $100 billion in new battery supply chain investments.
These DOE-tracked battery supply chain investments include a whopping 19 facilities in Georgia, adding up to over $26 billion and over 23,000 jobs.
In the same period, DOE has tracked more than $5 billion in new solar manufacturing investments, including over $2.8 billion in Georgia.
These new solar manufacturing facilities will add over 4,100 new jobs to the state of Georgia.
source_title: The Flow Battery Code Is Starting To Crack: Red State Edition - CleanTechnica

facts: Stryten Energy is presenting a portfolio of solutions at CES 2026, located at booth 6929 in the West Hall of the Las Vegas Convention Centre.
The company's portfolio includes advanced lead, lithium, and vanadium technologies designed to ensure customers have the right chemistry for their specific application.
Stryten offers modular lead battery energy storage system (BESS) solutions that can be scaled from microgrids to large industrial sites.
These lead BESS solutions can integrate with other energy sources to deliver safe and dependable power for peak shaving and as a safeguard against power outages.
A significant domestic circular economy for lead batteries already exists, making lead one of the most sustainable storage options available.
Stryten also provides lithium and vanadium flow battery solutions.
The vanadium flow battery technology offers safe, scalable, long-duration energy storage with 5-24+ hours of resilience for data centers and microgrids.
Vanadium redox flow battery components are available through Stryten Energy’s joint venture, Storion Energy.
Storion Energy offers domestically sourced vanadium electrolyte and high-performance stack power assemblies.
Mike Judd serves as the CEO of Stryten Energy.
CEO Mike Judd stated that Stryten is driving energy transformation with advanced battery solutions built in the US to strengthen communities, secure supply chains, and power America’s energy future.
According to Battery Council International (BCI), US electricity demand is projected to quadruple by 2030, primarily driven by AI adoption and business sector electrification.
Batteries can support surging electricity demand by storing excess energy, balancing supply and demand, and providing reliable backup power.
Domestic battery production strengthens energy security by reducing reliance on foreign supply chains to ensure a resilient grid.
source_title: Stryten Energy showcasing advanced battery energy storage solutions at CES 2026

facts: SungEel HiTech has announced the opening of its first US battery recycling plant.
The South Korean firm will invest more than US$ 37 million in the new site.
The new site will be operated by its subsidiary SungEel Recycling Park Georgia, LLC.
The new battery recycling facility will be built in Stephens County, Texas.
The project will create a total of 104 jobs.
Operations at the new site are expected to begin in early 2024.
This expansion follows SungEel’s decision to acquire Australian firm Envirostream earlier this year.

Suk Jae Yim, representative of SungEel’s US venture, stated that their entry into Georgia is the last piece of the puzzle to help Georgia build a sustainable ecosystem for its electric vehicle supply chain.
SungEel possesses more than 10 years of patented technology in complete recycling technology.
The company's technology allows it to recover 95% of battery metals, including nickel, cobalt, and lithium.
SungEel treats both end-of-life batteries and manufacturing scrap.

Pat Wilson, Stephens County Development Authority commissioner, stated that creating the jobs of the future and protecting resources means preparing key industries, such as the automotive industry, for the next technological revolution.
Pat Wilson met with SungEel in Korea during the week of the article's publication.
source_title: Korean battery recycler invests millions in US hub • Recycling International

facts: SungEel HiTech is a public company based in Gunsan si, South Korea, founded in 2000.
It operates as a lithium-ion battery recycling solution provider.
The company has 42 active competitors, including 19 funded and 6 that have exited.
Top competitors include Aqua Metals, Redwood Materials, and Mangrove Lithium.
SungEel HiTech recycles lithium-ion secondary batteries and extracts materials such as cobalt (Co), nickel (Ni), and others.
It develops a Continuous Cooling Crystallization technology for producing nickel sulfate.
Employee count is 40 as on May 31, 2026.
Legal entity SungEel HiTech Co., Ltd. was incorporated on Mar 29, 2017, with revenue of $50M - $100M (as on Jun 30, 2025).
SungEel HiTech got listed on Jul 28, 2022.
SungEel HiTech has 2 institutional investors.
Ranked 12th among 42 active competitors.
Envirostream strengthened its relationship with SungEel HiTech on Jun 13, 2024.
Emerson was chosen as the automation partner for Korea’s largest EV lithium-ion battery recycler on Dec 18, 2023.
SungEel HiTech and Samsung planned battery recycling in Germany on Sep 11, 2023.
EcoGraf successfully tested anode recycling to partner with SungEel HiTech on Mar 22, 2023.
SK Innovation and SungEel HiTech formed a battery-recycling joint venture on Dec 13, 2022.
Korean battery recycler brought $37 million funding to Stephens County on Aug 19, 2022.
Envirostream Australia confirmed an offtake deal with the Korean battery recycler on Nov 06, 2019.

SungEel HiTech describes itself as a global leader in lithium-ion battery recycling and a battery material re-supplier.
Since its establishment in 2000, SungEel HiTech has focused on closed-loop systems in the lithium-ion battery industry.
The company extracts valuable materials from end-of-life or spent batteries and their scraps by segregating and refining them to supply to battery material manufacturers and other customers.

facts: The powder coating industry in Georgia is composed of a diverse range of companies that offer tailor-made services to various industries.
Companies specialize in powder coating, an advanced method of applying a protective or decorative finish to products like metal hardware, automotive components, and furniture.
These companies employ high-performance machinery and techniques to ensure smooth, durable, and uniform finishes.
Widespread demand exists for sturdy, long-lasting products across local, regional, and national scales.
Advancements in powder coating technologies and eco-friendly solutions forecast a promising future for the industry.
Advanced Metal Components, Inc. is headquartered in Swainsboro, Georgia, was founded in 1977, and has a headcount of 51-200 employees.
Advanced Metal Components specializes in precision sheet metal contract manufacturing, metal fabrication, powder coating, and assembly services for customers across the eastern United States.
Advanced Metal Components offers expertise in complex weldments, NEMA rated enclosures, motor mounts, and brackets.
Advanced Metal Components' latest funding type is Debt Financing.
K&K Manufacturing, Inc. is headquartered in Milner, Georgia, was founded in 1990, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
K&K Manufacturing specializes in custom fabrication and manufacturing of truck bodies, trailers, and related products.
K&K Manufacturing offers laser cutting, press brake forming, powder coating, and media blasting services.
Raypaul Manufacturing is headquartered in Lavonia, Georgia, was founded in 1972, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Raypaul Manufacturing operates a fabrication and assembly warehouse offering powder coating, industrial finishing, curing ovens, bake ovens, turnkey systems, and paint booths.
Exquisite Powder Coating is headquartered in Canton, Georgia, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Exquisite Powder Coating offers decorative and protective coating solutions for industrial, residential, and commercial clients.
Lithia Powder Coating, INC. is based in Lithia Springs, Georgia (headquartered in Atlanta), was founded in 1975, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Lithia Powder Coating specializes in powder coating services for various industries.
Powder Coat America is headquartered in Lavonia, Georgia, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Powder Coat America offers metal fabrication services and a variety of colors and finishes for automotive, commercial, and residential coatings.
Secoa Technology, LLC is headquartered in Dalton, Georgia, was founded in 1964, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Secoa Technology supplies high-performance coatings and plating for various industries and provides engineering solutions for finishing needs.
Secoa Technology's capabilities cover very small to very large parts with throughput volumes ranging from a few pieces at once to thousands of parts per day.
Georgia Powder Coating Inc is headquartered in Gainesville, Georgia, was founded in 1999, and has a headcount of 51-200 employees.

Georgia Powder Coating Inc is a commercial and architectural high specification powder coating service provider serving government agencies, architectural companies, and industrial operations near Gainesville and beyond.
Southern Fluid Systems, INC. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, was founded in 1971, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Southern Fluid Systems offers fluid handling solutions including pumps, valves, hoses, fittings, and custom solutions for various industries.
TCI Powder Coatings is headquartered in Ellaville, Georgia, was founded in 1987, and has a headcount of 51-200 employees.
TCI Powder Coatings offers innovative powder coatings and architectural products for home appliances, furniture, fixtures, heavy-duty equipment, automotive, and general industrial products.
Elixir Extrusions, LLC is headquartered in Douglas, Georgia, was founded in 1984, and has a headcount of 201-500 employees.

Elixir Extrusions produces custom aluminum extrusions from the 6000 series of alloys (6061, 6063, 6005, 6463, etc.) for automotive, aerospace, military, firearms, architectural, lighting, consumer durables, and electrical industries.
Elixir Extrusions is an ISO-9001 Registered company offering full fabrication capabilities, powder-coat painting, and anodized finishing.
Elixir Extrusions operates its own fleet of trucks for regional delivery or uses local and national LTL carriers for nationwide delivery.
Powder Coating (powdercoatit.us) is headquartered in Griffin, Georgia, has a headcount of 1-10 employees, and has been in business since 1994.
Powder Coating focuses on unmatched service, shorter turnaround times, and top-quality products.
Barton Associates is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, has a headcount of 501-1000 employees, and is a Veteran-Owned company with over 46 years of experience as a paint finishing systems integrator.
Savannah Powder Coat is headquartered in Rincon, Georgia, has a headcount of 1-10 employees, and offers expert powder coating and sandblasting services at reasonable rates for Southeast Georgia.
Innovative Computer Corp is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, and has a headcount of 51-200 employees.
Innovative Computer Corp specializes in finishing system solutions from pre-treatment to final product assembly with a focus on waste reduction and compliance.

Innovative Computer Corp's customer base includes agriculture, military, and heavy equipment industries, and they are known for comprehensive training, hands-on support, and partnerships with leading coating manufacturers.

Omni Manufacturing specializes in metal stamping and offers welding, staking, assembling, nylon and PVC coatings, powder coating, and tool room services.

Fussell Sheet Metal Works is a precision contract and custom metal fabricator producing high-quality parts and assemblies using state-of-the-art technology.
Fussell Sheet Metal Works offers services from prototype to production, including laser cutting, CNC punching, CNC bending, powder coating, silk screening, and engineering services.
Engineered Fabrication Inc is headquartered in Watkinsville, Georgia, was founded in 1985, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Engineered Fabrication Inc offers machining, fabrication, sheet metal waterjet, stainless steel fabrication, painting & powder coating, and bead blasting services.
Miller's Powder Coatings is headquartered in Lilburn, Georgia, was founded in 1985, and has a headcount of 11-50 employees.
Miller's Powder Coatings offers powder coating for items ranging from cars/frames to nuts/bolts and provides sandblasting services with a booth capable of handling entire cars or small parts.
Miller's Powder Coatings has over 25 years of experience.

The company delivers color cards, primers, and powder coatings across architectural, automotive, furniture, industrial, and other markets.
The company provides technical support services including expert support, troubleshooting, testing, research, equipment and defect analysis, corrective action plans, and training.

facts: For nearly 40 years, TCI has been manufacturing industrial coatings with a strong commitment to people, products, and service.
The company aspires to be the premium brand supplier for OEM coatings through superior product innovation and performance.
TCI was founded in Ellaville, Georgia, initially operating out of a modest plant and a portable building that served as lab and office space.
TCI has expanded into an international company with multiple manufacturing locations.
TCI operates a state-of-the-art product development center.
TCI employs a staff of several hundred employees focused on servicing customers.
TCI's highly trained technical service and sales teams extend across North America, providing expert advice to customers ranging from large multinational corporations to independent custom coaters.
TCI maintains an expansive network of distribution centers composed of certified or approved external providers.
The distribution network services customers throughout the United States, Canada, and Mexico, ensuring stock powders and products are readily available.
TCI is owned by RPM International Inc., a multinational company with subsidiaries that are world leaders in specialty coatings, sealants, building materials, and related services.

facts: TCI Powder Coatings is located in Ellaville, GA.
TCI Powder Coatings is a subsidiary of RPM International Inc.
Thomas G. Whalen was named Director of Marketing and Segment Strategy at TCI Powder Coatings.
Whalen brings over 25 years of experience in the industrial coatings market to this position.
For the past two years prior to this appointment, Whalen served as Global Business Director, Consumer Electronics, and Performance Glass Coatings at PPG Industries.
Prior to PPG, Whalen was Vice President of Liquid and Mirror Coatings Units at Spraylat, which was acquired by PPG.
Before joining Spraylat, Whalen worked at Valspar with increasing management experience, including Global Segment Director.
Whalen started his coatings career in 1986 at Moline Paint in sales and held senior positions including International Business Director.
source_title: TCI Powder Coatings Names Director of Marketing and Segment Strategy | 2015-08-23  | PCI Magazine

facts: TCI Powder Coatings, a subsidiary of RPM International Inc., announced the opening of its new Product Development Laboratory in Americus, Georgia.
Planning for the laboratory began in the spring of 2016, with an official opening in January 2017.
The lab's primary functions include developing innovative products, improving current technologies, and producing rapid color matches.
Additional lab functions encompass analytical work, corrosion testing, environmental research and testing, manufacturing, and customer support.

The facility features state-of-the-art color matching equipment and software combined with an upgraded database of improved pigments to enable unparalleled color matching capabilities and quick turnaround times.
A styling room is available for critical color matching and styling work.
The lab design includes spaces specifically created for customer collaboration and employee training.
The laboratory is equipped with FTIR and DSC capabilities.
TCI uses this infrastructure to support customers in key market segments with technical support, collaboration, and expertise while delivering high-quality coating solutions.
Staff working within the lab include product formulators, chemists, segment technology leaders, color-matching specialists, and analytical chemists.
source_title: TCI Powder Coatings Opens Product Development Laboratory | 2017-06-04  | PCI Magazine

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that TEKLAS, a Turkish advanced research and development, manufacturing company and supplier of electric vehicle (EV) parts, will invest $6.5 million to open its first North American facility and headquarters in Gordon County.
The project will deliver 120 jobs to Calhoun.

Governor Kemp cited Georgia's highly skilled workforce, top-notch logistics network, and pro-business environment as key factors for TEKLAS joining the state's growing electric vehicle parts industry.
TEKLAS was founded in Turkey in 1971.

The company designs, develops, and manufactures fluid circulation systems for cooling and heating applications, including hoses and pipes made from rubber, plastic, and metal for the global automotive industry.
TEKLAS utilizes highly innovative and automated manufacturing processes that employ advanced water molding technology.
TEKLAS USA will feature assembly lines designed to serve prominent original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in the industry.
Operations at the new facility are scheduled to begin in the spring of 2021.
TEKLAS is headquartered in Turkey and maintains existing locations in Bulgaria, Serbia, Mexico, and China, along with sales offices in Germany and France.
Regional Director Arcan Ergur stated the company is excited to open a new plant in Calhoun, Georgia.
Regional Director Arcan Ergur stated that the new Calhoun location will help the company be closer to its main customers.
The 200,000-square-foot facility and headquarters will be located at 320 South Industrial Boulevard in Calhoun, Georgia.
TEKLAS will hire for positions across a variety of skill levels, including advanced manufacturing, management, and supervision roles.
Larry Roye, chairman of the Development Authority of Gordon County, applauded the state and local partners for helping encourage TEKLAS to choose Calhoun.

Gordon County Chamber of Commerce President and CEO Kathy Johnson commended TEKLAS for its research, advancements in EV manufacturing, and contribution to expanding the local automotive manufacturing base.
GDEcD Project Manager Fernanda Kirchner represented the Global Commerce division on this competitive project in partnership with the Economic Development Authority of Gordon County and Georgia Power.
The Center of Innovation for Logistics, a division of GDEcD, provided freight analysis to support the project and company planning.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted TEKLAS's decision to establish its North American headquarters and first U.S. advanced manufacturing facility in Calhoun as a successful example of Georgia's efforts to attract and grow future jobs.

Commissioner Wilson stated that Georgia will work to foster an ecosystem of manufacturers and suppliers on the leading edge of the automotive industry's transition to electric vehicles and e-mobility.
Commissioner Wilson welcomed TEKLAS to the diverse family of EV suppliers in Georgia.
source_title: Electric Vehicle Manufacturer to Open North American Headquarters in Georgia, Create Jobs in Calhoun - AllOnGeorgia

facts: Beginning this August, Richmond County high school students seeking an alternative path to completing their secondary education will have one.
Reaching Potential Through Manufacturing (RPM) is a partnership between Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. (TSV) and the Richmond County School System.
The RPM program offers students the ability to make up lost ground toward a high school diploma while earning income and valuable work experience in a manufacturing facility.
The cooperative education program combines an instructional day with a four-hour shift at a manufacturing facility that produces components and subassemblies for products manufactured by TSV.
TSV constructs vehicles under the E-Z-GO®, Cushman®, Bad Boy®, TUG™, and Douglas™ brands.
The program will draw students from all Richmond County high schools.
Applicants for the RPM program are selected based on need and suitability.
Depending on their specific needs, students will have a choice between attending classes at their home high school and taking coursework at the RPM facility.
Classroom and educational space is available on site at the RPM facility.
Besides traditional academic classes, RPM students will also receive training in life skills and tutoring resources as needed.

Kevin Holleran, president of TSV, stated that RPM will give students a unique educational path to reach their goal while providing training in life skills and vocational work experience they can leverage to start careers afterward.
Dr. Angela Pringle, superintendent of Richmond County Schools, said the partnership will provide an unprecedented experience for students who enroll in the manufacturing career pathway.
RPM is modeled after “12 For Life,” a highly successful program of Southwire Company LLC and the Carroll County School System.
Since its inception eight years ago, more than 870 students have graduated from the 12 For Life program.
Sister programs for 12 For Life have been created in Monroe, Georgia and Florence, Alabama.
Georgia 12 For Life has received national attention and acclaim.
Southwire and Carroll County Schools officials provided valuable knowledge and assistance in the creation of RPM.
The Richmond County School System is home to over 32,000 students in 56 schools.
The Richmond County School System is the 10th largest school district in Georgia.
The Richmond County School System is the third largest employer in Augusta-Richmond County with over 4,000 employees.
RCSS boasts having the oldest public school in the south, and the fifth oldest public high school in America.
Several of its schools are on the National Register of Historic Places.
Although steeped in history, Richmond County strives to move education forward and to provide students with opportunities to succeed.
Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. is a leading global manufacturer of golf cars, utility and personal transportation vehicles, and ground support equipment.
TSV's vehicles are found in environments ranging from golf courses to factories, airports to planned communities, and theme parks to hunting preserves.

Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services.

Textron is known around the world for its powerful brands such as Bell Helicopter, Cessna, Beechcraft, Hawker, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO, Greenlee, Textron Systems, and TRU Simulation + Training.
source_title: RPM To Provide New Path To Graduation For Richmond County High Schoolers | Georgia Public Broadcasting

facts: Textron’s Arctic Cat division will lay off 385 employees at its Thief River Falls, Minnesota, facility starting May 8, 2025.
The decision was driven by “changing business needs that require the company to reduce our workforce at the location permanently.”
Textron Aviation laid off around 800 workers on July 14, 2020.
Most of those workers were employed at facilities in Wichita, Kansas.
The count does not include the 70 employees laid off in June, 2020.

Preparing and responding to the “evolving global economic uncertainty and existing market conditions,” the company has laid off approximatel 800 employees, with the majority being employed in Wichita, Kansas.
According to an SEC filing, the company plans to eliminate 1,950 positions in three business segments: Textron Aviation, Textron Specialized Vehicles, and Tru Simulation + Training.”
In the finest corporate-speak, “Textron Aviation remains focused on continuing to support our customers with best-in-class aircraft and services during this difficult time.
Workforce reductions affect approximately 250 salaried exempt and salaried nonexempt employees across the company’s workforce at most of its U.S. facilities as of June 26, 2020.
Bargained employees are not impacted by today’s announcement.

A previous filing with the SEC (Securities and Exchange Commission) indicated that the company planned to cut 2,000 positions as part of a restructuring plan to address economic challenges due to the pandemic.
Textron Aviation will lay off 875 professional employees, mostly engineers and support personnel, at its Wichita headquarters effective December 31, 2019.
This follows a previous offer of voluntary retirement for selected employees.
It is likely that the decision was conditioned on the completion of development on the new Cessna Citation Longitude business jet, which was FAA certified on September 2019.
The company has also suspended development on the Cessna Citation Hemisphere due to engine-related issues.

According to a company spokesperson, “Textron Aviation remains focused on our long-term strategic workforce plan and will continue hiring as needed for skilled advanced aviation manufacturing positions such as composites, tooling, A&P mechanics, avionics technicians, and other direct manufacturing and customer support jobs.”
In an SEC filing, the company also announced that a number of facilities would be closed.

Providence, Rhode Island-based Textron, the manufacturer of such iconic brands such as Bell Helicopters, Cessna Aircraft, Beechcraft Aircraft, and E-Z-GO small vehicles, has announced that it plans to proceed with a restructuring of its Specialized Vehicles operation within its industrial group.
The restructuring will result in the closure of one Augusta, Georgia facility and laying off approximately 400 employees.
The decision was driven by the under-performing business segment.

According to an Securities and Exchange Commission filing, “Management conducted a strategic review of the Textron Specialized Vehicles businesses, which included an assessment of the acquired dealer network and go-to-market strategy for the Textron Off Road and Arctic Cat brands, as well as cost reduction initiatives throughout the Textron Specialized Vehicles businesses.

The restructuring plan will result in the impairment of intangible assets, primarily related to product rationalization, the elimination of approximately 400 positions, representing approximately 10% of Textron Specialized Vehicles’ workforce, and closure of several factory-direct turf-care branch locations and a manufacturing facility.
The restructuring actions under this plan are expected to result in improved operating results for these businesses.”
Textron announced plans to separate its Industrial segment from its core aerospace and defence operations.
The separation aims to sharpen strategic focus and unlock long-term value.
Textron is exploring several options for the separation, including a potential sale or the creation of an independent, publicly listed entity.
The move will result in a streamlined “New Textron” focused exclusively on aerospace and defence.
New Textron will be built around key businesses: Textron Aviation, Bell, and Textron Systems.
Leadership concluded that the two divisions operate in fundamentally different markets with distinct investment needs and growth trajectories.
Separating the divisions is expected to give each business greater operational clarity, improved agility, and the ability to pursue tailored capital allocation strategies.
This includes more targeted investment in product development and enhanced flexibility to pursue both organic growth and acquisitions.
New Textron is expected to emerge as a leading pure-play aerospace and defence company with projected 2026 revenues exceeding US$12 billion.
New Textron will have a backlog of US$19 billion.
New Textron's portfolio will span general aviation through the Cessna and Beechcraft brands, rotorcraft via Bell, and a range of defence and aerospace solutions from Textron Systems.
The company anticipates stronger revenue growth and improved operating margins following the separation.
New Textron will maintain a disciplined approach to capital allocation, including continued investment in research and development.
The Industrial business will reposition itself as a global mobility-focused company with expected revenues of more than $3 billion.
The Industrial segment comprises Kautex, which specialises in automotive systems such as fuel tanks and battery enclosures.
The Industrial segment also includes Textron Specialized Vehicles, known for brands including E-Z-GO and Jacobsen.
As a standalone entity, the Industrial business is expected to pursue strategies better aligned with its market dynamics and customer base.
Textron aims to complete the separation within 12 to 18 months, subject to regulatory approvals and board sign-off.
In the meantime, the Industrial segment will continue operating under its current strategy, with ongoing investment in growth, efficiency and innovation.
source_title: Textron to split Industrial arm in strategic overhaul

facts: In 1954, in a one-room machine shop in Augusta, Georgia, the first Textron Specialized Vehicles products were born.
Two brothers started the company with a belief that they could build a better golf car that met customers' needs better than any other vehicle on the market.
The Textron Specialized Vehicles product line includes golf cars, ground support equipment for the aviation industry, industrial utility vehicles, and recreational side-by-side UTVs.

The company has become a world leader in transportation and a leading global manufacturer of golf cars, side-by-sides, utility vehicles, personal transportation vehicles and aviation ground support equipment.
The company builds thousands of vehicles each year, many featuring game-changing technology and innovations to improve vehicle efficiency and enhance the user experience.
Textron Specialized Vehicles includes brands that are leaders in their respective industries: Cushman®, E-Z-GO®, Arctic Cat®, Jacobsen®, Ransomes®, TUG™, Douglas™, Premier™, and Safeaero™.
Since 1960, the company has been part of Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT), a global multi-industry company headquartered in Providence, RI.
Textron Inc. is a Fortune 500 company with more than 35,000 employees worldwide and operations in 25 countries.
Textron's notable brands include E-Z-GO, Cessna, Beechcraft, Hawker, Bell, and Kautex, among others.
The company maintains a single belief to build vehicles and services that exceed customers' expectations better than any other offering on the market.
The company operates in the Motor Vehicle Manufacturing industry.
The company size is 1,001-5,000 employees.

facts: Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc., a Textron Inc. company, announced its new line of Cushman Shuttle personnel carriers powered by the company's exclusive 72-volt AC electric drivetrain.
The new product line includes the Cushman Shuttle 4, Shuttle 6, and Shuttle 8 models.
Textron Specialized Vehicles is the leader in AC electric drivetrains in the golf-car industry under its E-Z-GO brand.
The 72-volt AC drivetrain technology delivers improved energy efficiency, increased range, longer battery life, and remarkable hill-climbing capabilities compared to DC electric power.
Cushman vehicles are designed and manufactured in Augusta, Georgia.

Kevin Holleran, president and CEO of Textron Specialized Vehicles, stated that the new Shuttles leverage proven energy savings, power, and range benefits to move customers' employees and guests more efficiently and cost-effectively.

Cushman Shuttle personnel carriers are commonly used for transporting people and cargo in applications including golf courses, resorts, airports, educational facilities, and college and commercial campuses.
Standard features on the Cushman Shuttles include headlights, a brush guard, an hour meter, and a USB port.
Optional equipment for the Shuttles includes a limited-slip differential, windshields, and canopies.
The Cushman Shuttle 4 seats up to four people and includes a stake-side cargo bed for equipment, luggage, or personal belongings.
The Cushman Shuttle 6 seats up to six people.
The Cushman Shuttle 8 seats up to eight people.
Since 1901, Cushman has provided light-transportation solutions for turf, commercial, and industrial applications.

Cushman's product line includes more than 15 different vehicle models, ranging from rugged Hauler utility vehicles for outdoor hauling and towing to the fully electric Titan material-handling vehicle for indoor environments with a 3,000-pound load capacity.

Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. is a leading global manufacturer of golf cars, utility and personal transportation vehicles, snowmobiles, side-by-sides, all-terrain vehicles, professional turf-care equipment, and ground support equipment.
Textron Specialized Vehicles markets products under the E-Z-GO, Cushman, Arctic Cat, Textron Off Road, Jacobsen, Dixie Chopper, Ransomes, TUG, Douglas, Premier, and Safeaero brands.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company that leverages a global network of aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses to provide innovative solutions and services.

facts: Augusta, Georgia-based Textron Specialized Vehicles has recalled about 34,000 E-Z-GO RXV Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTV) or golf carts due to injury and crash hazards.
The PTV's steering knuckles and A-arms can break during or after an impact and cause loss of steering control, posing crash and injury hazards.
The company received 335 reports of incidents of the steering knuckles and A-arms on the PTVs breaking, including one incident that resulted in two reports of abrasion injuries.
The recall includes model year 2022 and 2023 Freedom and Valor E-Z-GO RXV personal transportation vehicles.
Recalled units have serial numbers within the range of 5656874 through 5656886 and 5659215 through 5700741.
The company has asked its customers to immediately stop using the recalled PTVs and contact Textron Specialized Vehicles for a free repair.
The recalled golf carts were sold at dealerships nationwide and online from January 2022 through September 2022.
The recalled golf carts were sold for between $10,000 and $17,000.
source_title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Recalls 34K Golf Carts

facts: ADVICS Manufacturing Ohio, Inc. is an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) supplier of disc brake calipers and control systems to the automotive industry.
In 1989 ADVICS Manufacturing Ohio, Inc. was established when 50 Team Members began producing brake components for one vehicle model.

Today, over 900 experienced and dedicated people produce disc brake calipers, electronic stability control units, and electric parking brakes for over 20 different vehicles for many of the world’s leading auto manufacturers.
The company’s vision is to improve the quality of life through the evolution of vehicle safety.
Toyota, Chrysler, Ford, Mazda, and Nissan (and their customers) trust ADVICS to produce only the highest-quality, safety-sensitive products.
The company is continuing to grow by adding more Team Members and producing new, technology-leading brake system products designed by its parent company.
Management and Team Members are active in the local community, sponsoring events like the Lebanon Carriage Parade & Festival and contributing to United Way and other charities.
Team Members volunteer time for 3rd Friday and Food Drives, and contribute time, donations, and expertise as coaches and volunteers.
The greater Lebanon area has been a great home for ADVICS-Ohio and the company is committed to giving back.
ADVICS Manufacturing Ohio, Inc. is located at 1650 Kingsview Drive, Lebanon, OH 45036.
The company offers competitive compensation.
The company provides flexible benefits with low premiums covering Medical, Dental & Vision.
The company offers a 401(k) plan and gainsharing.
The company provides paid vacation and extensive holidays.
Prospective new Team Members are informed that the work environment is fast-paced and dynamic, where value is placed on their ideas and commitment to provide the highest-quality products.

facts: The ADVICS name stands for Advanced Intelligent Chassis Systems.
ADVICS operates with the combined experience of AISIN, DENSO and SUMITOMO ELECTRIC, three of the world’s premier automotive suppliers.
ADVICS has earned its reputation as a world-class manufacturer of the market’s most trusted brake systems.
ADVICS is a supplier of ultra-premium OE brake system components that are engineered and manufactured with industry-leading technology and innovation for the aftermarket.
The ADVICS journey represents the very best of Japanese quality, global expertise and environmental sustainability for the road ahead.

ADVICS advanced technology yields brake pads, cylinders, boosters, rotors, calipers, and brake fluid that provide optimal performance and ultimate durability for the most comfortable and reliable brakes on the road.
ADVICS dedication to the art of braking technology enables it to design peak performance systems while strengthening its foundation as a leader in the U.S. aftermarket.
Today, ADVICS is a leader in ultra-premium aftermarket braking systems and components for Asian and domestic vehicles, in North America and around the globe.
ADVICS maintains a relentless commitment to innovation, safety and comfort as an OE and aftermarket manufacturer focused on braking performance and customer safety.
