Johnson Matthey Process Technologies employs 100 people in Chatham County (CH) producing catalysts.
Kraton employs 180 people in Chatham County (CH) manufacturing chemicals.
There are 9 other manufacturers with various products employing between 100 and 249 people each.
Walmart retail operations employ between 2,750 and 5,000 people.
UTC Overseas employs between 500 and 1,000 people providing logistics solutions.
Spirit Construction Services employs between 500 and 1,000 people as general contractors.
Ft. Stewart/Hunter Army Airfield has 6,700 civilian personnel on bases.
Georgia Southern University employs 2,781 people in education.
Savannah College of Art & Design employs 2,320 people in education.
YMCA of Coastal Georgia employs 1,521 people as a civic association.
source_title: Major Employers - Savannah Chamber

facts: ALBAform is a second-generation family business with 30+ years of experience and manufacturing locations in the U.S. and the Czech Republic.
ALBAform’s expertise is in forming, welding, and assembling metal components for the automotive, agriculture, and e-mobility sectors.
A facility tour at ALBAform's Flowery Branch, GA location was scheduled for May 17, 2023 @ 10:00 am – 12:30 pm EDT.
Monika Vintrlikova, Senior Executive and Honorary Consul for the Czech Republic, along with the ALBAform team, hosted a tour of their Flowery Branch, GA facility for Next Generation Manufacturing.
The Flowery Branch facility encompasses 120,000 square feet of production, distribution, and storage space.
The facility employs 145 employees.
Operations include 20 high-tech CNC bending machines.
Operations include 16 high-tech welding robots.
The facility produces 1.2 million seat structures per year.
The facility processes 110 truckloads of steel per year.
ALBAform holds Georga MADE certification.
ALBAform holds ISO and IATF certifications.
The Flowery Branch location is home to Altanta Cylinders, subsidiary of Cylinders Holdings.
ALBAform is Georgia’s newest Czech company to invest and establish operations in the state.
Event registration cost $35 and included a facility tour, discussion with leadership, lunch, and networking.
Registration is open to executives, directors, and senior managers of manufacturing companies.
Refunds are provided for cancellations made up to 72 hours in advance.
source_title: ALBAform Facility Tour – Next Generation Manufacturing

facts: The $800 million Anovion project has been delayed until 2026.
Construction for the project is expected to begin sometime in 2025.
Anovion was initially expected to begin construction in 2024.
Chief Commercial Officer Patrick Donnelly explained that the delay is due to legislation related to the Inflation Reduction Act.
Development Authority of Bainbridge and Decatur County executive director Rick McCaskill stated that Anovion will begin construction in the first half of next year.
There was an expectation that a tax credit for refraining from using Chinese synthetic graphite would take effect in 2025.
Chief Commercial Officer Patrick Donnelly said Anovion plans to operate a small-scale version of the original plan in 2026.
The full-scale projected project is expected to be up and running in 2027.
The project is planned for Bainbridge, Georgia.
source_title: Anovion still plans to build in Bainbridge but the project delayed to 2026

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Anovion Technologies will build a new manufacturing facility in Bainbridge.
Anovion is a supplier of premium synthetic graphite anode materials for lithium-ion batteries.
The project is expected to create more than 400 jobs and over $800 million in investment in Decatur County.
Governor Kemp stated Georgia is fast becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Governor Kemp noted that job creators are recognizing every corner of the state has the resources and workforce needed for success.
Eric Stopka, Chief Executive Officer of Anovion, said the company is opening its first large-scale expansion manufacturing facility in Georgia.

Stopka stated Georgia's strong technical workforce and top-tier schools will help advance research partnerships and contribute to a highly skilled talent pipeline for Anovion's innovation and climate technology jobs.
Stopka noted the region's growing availability of clean energy will result in increased sustainability for Anovion's manufacturing process.
The facility will be 1,500,000-square-foot with proprietary furnace technologies located at Downrange Industrial Park in Bainbridge.
Operations are expected to begin late 2025.
The facility will produce synthetic graphite for use in batteries that power EVs, electric storage systems, electronics, military applications, and other products.
Graphite is the largest battery material used in electric vehicles by mass, more than copper, nickel, manganese, cobalt, and lithium.
Anovion plans to hire for technical, production, engineering roles as well as administrative and management positions.
Mayor Edward Reynolds said Anovion's decision to locate in Bainbridge is a huge win due to the $800 million investment and creation of over 400 high-paying quality jobs.
Chairman Pete Stephens from Decatur County Board Of Commissioners added that the new green industry jobs will provide quality employment opportunities for decades to come.
Project Manager Taylor Walden represented GDEcD's Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was supported by City Of Bainbridge, Development Authority Of Bainbridge And Decatur County, University System Of Georgia, Georgia EMC, And Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated much of the critical supply chain for e-mobility and manufacturing is currently sourced outside of the U.S., and Anovion fills that need by delivering another critical component to Georgia.
Georgia has announced 40 EV related projects with more than $21 billion investment since 2020, making it a hub for the electric mobility industry.
The new project represents another step for Decatur County towards diversifying its industry mix while providing quality employment opportunities.
source_title: Georgia Chron - Georgia: Gov. Kemp: Anovion Technologies to Create Over 400 Jobs in Bainbridge, Invest 0M in Manufacturing Facility - 10227039

facts: Anovion chose southwest Georgia to invest an initial $800 million for its 1.5-million-sq.-ft. plant.

Site selection factors included proximity to existing and planned low- and carbon-free energy sources, short supply chains, access to existing rail infrastructure, a highly valuable skilled workforce, and a business-friendly environment.
Decatur County is seen as an ideal fit for Anovion's new expansion by company officials.

CEO Eric Stopka stated Anovion conducted a thorough search to address energy, transportation, logistics, and human capital needs to produce the highest quality and sustainable synthetic graphite anode material.
CEO Eric Stopka noted that existing infrastructure makes southwest Georgia attractive and proximity to other battery and electric vehicle manufacturing plants will help reduce their carbon footprint.
Anovion began commercial production of lithium-ion battery materials in early 2021.
Anovion is among a limited number of graphite anode producers in America qualified for EV applications.

In October 2022, Anovion was awarded a $117 million grant from the U.S. Department of Energy under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to scale production capacity and construct a large-scale factory in the Southeast.
The Georgia facility will be the first of its scale for Anovion.
Anovion views the Georgia plant as a crucial first step to reshoring production of synthetic graphite anode, which is favored by EV and battery manufacturers for consistency and performance.
The new Georgia plant is expected to initially produce 40,000 metric tons per year once fully operational.
The facility will create hundreds of high-quality local clean energy jobs for the surrounding region.
Gov. Brian Kemp welcomed Anovion to Georgia, calling it the No. 1 state for business and fast becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Gov. Brian Kemp stated that job creators recognize every corner of Georgia has the resources and workforce needed for success.
Gov. Brian Kemp expressed looking forward to celebrating their partnership with Georgians in Decatur County after meeting the company's team personally.
Anovion plans to use innovative, sustainable, and technologically advanced manufacturing processes at the new Decatur County facility.
The facility will rely on the region's rapidly decarbonizing energy mix to power manufacturing.
The facility will introduce proprietary furnace technologies that substantially reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
These furnace technologies provide a clear roadmap for Anovion to achieve a carbon-neutral footprint over time.

Executive Chairman Chip Dunn stated their long-term strategic vision is to focus on growth and innovation, positioning Anovion as a leader in the U.S. energy transition and securing a domestic source for critical materials for U.S. lithium-ion battery production.
Chip Dunn expressed excitement to bring their full arsenal of innovation to the Georgia facility to execute their long-term business strategy and combat the climate crisis.
In addition to Georgia, Anovion is expanding its Anovion Center of Excellence.
The company is also expanding a hydro-powered sister manufacturing facility in Sanborn, N.Y., near Niagara Falls.
Sanborn, N.Y. is one of the only qualified U.S. sources of battery-grade synthetic graphite to commercially ship product today.
source_title: Decatur County, Ga., Chosen for Lithium-Ion Battery Materials Factory : CEG

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp, in conjunction with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), today announced that the state earned the No. 1 spot in Site Selection’s annual Prosperity Cup rankings for successful investment recruitment in 2023.
Criteria for this national award include the total values of new and expanded facilities in the state, business-climate attractiveness, and workforce readiness.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that companies choosing Georgia get access to the global market through logistics assets like Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport and some of the fastest-growing ports in the nation.
In 2023, Georgia attracted 318 new and expanded facilities to secure the No. 1 ranking.
The award includes an expanded scope for the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), which is the largest project in state history.
HMGMA announced an additional 400 new jobs and $2 billion in investment last year.
HMGMA’s total investment is now $7.59 billion and will net 8,500 new jobs for Georgians.
Anovion Technologies selected southwest Georgia for its $800 million, 400-job synthetic graphite manufacturing facility in May.
Anovion Technologies broke ground at the site in June of last year.
Meissner Corporation plans to create more than 1,700 new jobs at a new campus in northeast Georgia that will more than double the company’s manufacturing footprint in the U.S.

Site Selection’s Mark Arend stated that the Prosperity Cup recognizes state-level economic development competitiveness and that Georgia’s consistent delivery of location attributes demonstrates it competes to win.
On the heels of Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America’s groundbreaking in October 2022, Georgia welcomed more than 10 Hyundai and automotive suppliers in 2023 reaching middle Georgia and beyond.
These suppliers will ultimately create over 4,300 new jobs and bring $1.2 billion in combined investment.
Kia’s facility in West Point announced an expansion during 2023.
Toyota’s campus in Pendergrass announced an expansion during 2023.
Kia and Toyota expansions created a combined 440 new jobs and $286 million in investment.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia’s economic development team is truly collaborative, including sister state agencies, utility partners, local economic developers, and many others who work to help companies and communities find the right match for mutual success.

Wilson noted that decades of partnerships, intentional economic development from the community level to the state level, combined with consistent pro-business policies, have led to these positive results.

links.context: Full article on Georgia's selection and 2024 Prosperity Cup information

source_title: Georgia Scores Top Spot in Competitive Economic Rankings - Rome CEO

facts: Ascend Elements is a clean technology company that manufactures advanced battery materials from recycled lithium-ion batteries.
It supports domestic supply chain security in North America and Europe.
source_title: Ascend Elements Email Format 2026 - Find the Right Pattern | Clay

facts: Ascend Elements, a U.S.-based engineered materials and lithium-ion battery recycling company, secured $300 million in equity and debt financing.
The funding includes $200 million in Series C equity investments from an international group of strategic and financial investors.
The funding round was led by Fifth Wall Climate and joined by SK ecoplant, the environmental unit of South Korean conglomerate SK Group.
Other new investors include Oman Investment Authority, Lithium Americas Corporation, GLy Capital Management’s New Mobility Fund, Mirae Asset Capital & LS and Shinhan GIB.

Previous investors participating in the round include Hitachi Ventures, Jaguar Land Rover’s InMotion Ventures, TDK Ventures, Orbia Ventures, At One Ventures, TRUMPF Venture, and Doral Energy-Tech Ventures.
The funding is in addition to two recently awarded grants totaling $480 million from the Department of Energy.
Ascend Elements plans to invest up to $1 billion to build a sustainable lithium-ion battery materials facility in Hopkinsville, Ky.
The first-of-its-kind manufacturing facility, named “Apex 1”, will produce enough lithium-ion battery pCAM and sustainable CAM to equip up to 250,000 electric vehicles per year.

The funding will accelerate commercialization of Ascend Elements’ proprietary Hydro-to-Cathode™ direct precursor synthesis process, establishing a closed-loop, EV battery materials supply chain in North America.
Ascend Elements is already generating revenue and processing end-of-life batteries and manufacturing scrap at its “Base 1” facility in Georgia.
The Georgia Base 1 facility will have 30,000 metric tons of EV battery recycling capacity by the end of 2022.
The Georgia facility will ultimately feed Apex 1 in Kentucky.
Ascend Elements expects to recycle more than 150,000 metric tons of lithium-ion batteries per year globally by 2026.

The company transforms recycled batteries into high-performance, customized, EV battery cathode active material precursor (pCAM) and cathode active materials (CAM) that meet or exceed top-tier battery manufacturer requirements.
CEO Mike O’Kronley stated the company’s patented closed-loop process goes beyond simple recycling to produce sustainable, high-performance cathode active materials for new EV batteries.

Fifth Wall Partner Peter Gajdoš said securing recyclability of critical metals is essential to achieve energy independence from fossil fuels and supports creating sustainable batteries for automotive and stationary storage.

SK ecoplant CEO Park Kyung-il stated the investment continues their strategic partnership and lays the foundation for SK ecoplant as the first shareholder to dominate the global battery recycling industry.

Oman Investment Authority Director Ibrahim Al Eisri stated the partnership aligns with OIA’s strategy and Oman’s Vision 2040 priorities to transform the lithium-ion battery recycling industry globally.
source_title: Ascend Elements secures $300 million in funding, began building plant for EV sustainable battery materials

facts: Canadian private equity firm Kinterra Capital has acquired the rights to a planned €1.63 billion precursor cathode active material plant near Opole in Poland.
The acquisition rescues a project that had been thrown into doubt after its previous developer, US firm Ascend Elements, filed for bankruptcy in April 2026.
The planned facility is scheduled for completion in 2031.
The facility will produce pCAM — a key component in the lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles, smartphones and other consumer electronics.
The deal was finalised in May.
The deal includes intellectual property rights for lithium-ion battery processing, lithium recovery and pCAM production.
The deal includes rights to a Polish government subsidy of 1.22 billion zloty (€285 million).
The subsidy is one of the largest state grants ever awarded in Poland.
The subsidy is backed by an EU programme supporting the transition to a net-zero economy.
Kinterra also signed a conditional agreement to purchase the land plot for the factory.
Ascend Elements had announced the project in May 2025.
Ascend Elements cited Poland’s strategic position as Europe’s largest lithium-ion battery producer and its importance in reducing European dependence on Asian suppliers.
The company subsequently cited “insurmountable” financial challenges in launching US bankruptcy proceedings, casting doubt over the future of the grant and the facility.
Kinterra Capital manages approximately $1.5 billion in assets focused on critical minerals and infrastructure.
Kinterra said the project offered the strategic location, infrastructure access, public administration support and industrial base it requires.
Graeme Weeks, Kinterra’s global head of project execution, said: “This project has the key advantages we are looking for.”

Laura Fernandez, a Kinterra partner, said the investment “addresses the most important challenges facing European industry today,” citing supply chain security and European independence from Asian suppliers.
Poland is home to Europe’s largest EV battery plant, operated by LG Energy Solution in Wrocław.
The LG Energy Solution plant accounted for approximately half of Europe’s EV battery production capacity in 2024.
Poland’s position faces a potential challenge from forthcoming EU regulations incorporating battery carbon footprint requirements.
These measures could penalise Poland given that coal still accounts for around half of its electricity production.
source_title: Canadian Private Equity Firm Kinterra Capital Rescues Poland’s €1.63 Billion Battery Materials Plant After Ascend Elements Bankruptcy – MINEX Forum

facts: TDK Ventures was founded in 2019 with a mission to build a sustainable future and help usher in the energy transformation needed.
In early 2020, TDK Ventures invested in Ascend Elements (then known as Battery Resourcers) for their lithium-ion battery recycling technology.
The United States has vowed a 50% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 and launched multiple government initiatives to fund progress.
The European Union announced a benchmark of zero carbon emissions by 2035 and launched multiple government initiatives to fund progress.
BMW, Volvo, and Jaguar/Land Rover have all committed to going 100% electric in the next 10 years.
GM, Ford, Toyota, Nissan, and others are declaring targets of up to 1/3rd of the market share along the same timeline.
Lithium-ion batteries are projected to see a demand of 9.3 TWh of energy in 2030.
Industry and government are rushing to address technical gaps with more than $500 bn in promised funds and a pledged 4.5 TWh of increased capacity by that timeline.
Lithium-ion batteries require raw materials including cobalt, nickel, and lithium carbonate, which are processed into cathode components.
Sourcing these materials presents a critical supply chain problem due to expensive and volatile pricing from unsustainable mining practices and sociopolitical relations.
Lithium is often found raw as lithium carbonate, which must be chemically refined into lithium hydroxide for use in batteries.
Cathode production costs alone make up 40–50% of the price for each battery.
Ascend Elements developed a novel process to convert used Li-ion batteries and related manufacturing scrap into EV-ready grade cathode active materials, cathode precursors, and anode materials.
The technology allows battery manufacturers and OEMs to skip refining and cathode/anode production steps from virgin materials.
Introducing recycled materials has the potential for a 59% reduction in unit cost.
There is an estimated 93% reduction in carbon footprint impact due to reduced processing requirements compared to using virgin materials.
Ascend commenced construction of two recycling facilities, including a 154,000 sq. ft. plant in Covington, Georgia which will be capable of processing up to 30,000 metric tons per year.
Ascend is also constructing a 500,000 sq. ft. facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky capable of equipping over 250,000 electric vehicles per year with recycled batteries.
Value extraction from end-of-life lithium-ion batteries has been shown to result in more than 10x more revenue and almost 3x the margin.
Testing has demonstrated that their recycled cathodes outperform those produced by traditional means.
Ascend has established a recycling process that is material agnostic, ensuring relevance as future manufacturing methods develop.
Prior to 2021, Ascend had already established partnerships with both the US Department of Energy (DOE) and the National Science Foundation.
In 2022, the DOE followed up with $480 million to directly support Ascend production and operation.
Industry partners include Honda, Hitachi, SK Ecoplant, Jaguar Land Rover, EcoPro, and many other industrial leaders and battery manufacturers.

The Battery Business Unit Manager at Jaguar Land Rover stated they considered 14 recyclers for partners but chose Ascend due to high-value products, technological sophistication, and ease of doing business.
The same manager noted that Ascend's unique selling point is that their leading technology actually works and gives OEMs options to choose from.
Ascend has quadrupled its team size since the partnership with TDK Ventures.
There is a projected $6.5 billion market for the battery recycling market by 2025, representing an 87% CAGR.
Ascend is positioned to be the leading player in North America's battery recycling sector.

links.context: Article about recycled cathodes outperforming traditional means

source_title: Why We Invested in Ascend Elements to Accelerate Our Vision for Sustainable Energy Transformation | TDK Ventures

facts: Ascend Elements, a lithium-ion battery recycling company based in Westborough, Massachusetts, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.

On April 9, President and CEO Linh Austin stated in a LinkedIn post that the Chapter 11 filing aims to place the company on firmer financial footing, maximize stakeholder value, and allow operations to continue under existing management.
Linh Austin was appointed CEO in March 2025 and has acknowledged the company's history of fiscal and operational challenges since taking the role.

The company demonstrated commercial-scale production at its Base 1 facility in Covington, Georgia, but CEO Linh Austin stated that financial difficulties were too great to overcome without restructuring despite assembling an experienced team.
Ascend's Base 1 plant in Covington, Georgia, began operations in 2022 and has a capacity to process 30,000 metric tons of end-of-life lithium-ion batteries and manufacturing scrap annually.
Since late 2022, Ascend has been constructing its Apex 1 facility on a 140-acre site in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
The Base 1 and Apex 1 projects together represent more than $1 billion in investment.
Ascend secured a grant offer of up to $320 million from Poland’s Ministry of Economic Development and Technology for a precursor cathode active material (pCAM) plant in Europe called Apex 2.

Founded in 2015, Ascend has scaled its patented Hydro-to-Cathode synthesis process to recover and manufacture NMC (nickel, manganese, cobalt) precursor cathode active material (pCAM) and cathode active material (CAM).
Ascend also produces lithium carbonate and various sulfate products from black mass.
To date, Ascend has raised over $1.1 billion from investors and received grants from the U.S. Department of Energy under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.

Ascend previously returned a $164 million DOE grant for CAM infrastructure at Apex 1 due to changing market conditions and experienced a partial cut of a remaining $316 million DOE award in late 2025.
Ascend has pursued alternative funding sources to bridge the financial gap left by the returned and reduced DOE grants.

In 2025, Ascend reported strong momentum, including a take-or-pay lithium carbonate offtake agreement with Trafigura, successful commercial-scale production in Georgia, and a multiyear supply contract with a leading automaker.
Ascend's Apex 2 project was recognized as Manufacturing Investment of the Year by the Polish Investment and Trade Agency in early 2026.
CEO Linh Austin emphasized that the Chapter 11 filing is a restructuring step rather than a relinquishment of the company's mission.

Ascend remains committed to building a domestic, circular supply chain for critical battery materials and fulfilling existing offtake and customer agreements during the court-supervised restructuring process.
source_title: Ascend Elements Files Chapter 11 to Bolster Finances - Battery-Tech Network

facts: The Ministry of Economic Development and Technology in Poland has extended a grant of up to USD $320 million (1.22 billion PLN) to Ascend Elements.
The grant is intended to facilitate the establishment of a sustainable precursor cathode active material (pCAM) manufacturing facility in Poland.
This subsidy represents one of the largest grants ever provided by the Republic of Poland.
The grant aligns with the European Union’s Temporary Crisis and Transition Framework (TCTF), which aims to support Europe’s shift towards a net-zero economy.
pCAM is a crucial material in the production of lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles and other advanced industrial applications.
Ascend Elements has pinpointed a location in Poland for the construction of its first advanced pCAM manufacturing plant in Europe.
CEO Linh Austin met with Poland’s Secretary of State Michał Jaros to formalize the grant offer.
The meeting marks a significant advancement in Ascend Elements’ European expansion plans.
Pawel Pudlowski, Ph.D., Vice President of the Management Board at the Polish Investment and Trade Agency (PAIH), stated that the project is among the largest U.S. investments in recent years.
The Polish facility is expected to enhance Poland’s position within the global battery supply chain and promote regional development.
The project will include advancements in technology and collaborations with local educational institutions.
Ascend Elements intends to commercialize its Hydro-to-Cathode® technology at the new Polish facility.
The technology facilitates the production of sustainable nickel, manganese, and cobalt (NMC) pCAM from recycled lithium-ion battery materials.
The process is more efficient and environmentally friendly compared to traditional methods, resulting in reduced carbon emissions and particulate matter.
A recent life cycle assessment highlighted that the Hydro-to-Cathode® process generates 49% lower carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) emissions and 26% lower particulate matter (PM 2.5) emissions.
The company aims to achieve further emission reductions by 2030 through additional decarbonization efforts.
Ascend Elements is expanding its presence in the United States alongside its European operations.
The company operates a major battery recycling facility in Covington, Georgia.
A new lithium recovery line at the Covington facility is expected to produce up to 3,000 metric tons of Lithium Carbonate annually.
Ascend Elements is developing an advanced Hydro-to-Cathode® pCAM manufacturing facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
The Hopkinsville facility is projected to become North America’s largest NMC pCAM producer by late 2026.
The Polish grant underscores Ascend Elements’ commitment to building a sustainable and circular battery materials supply chain.
The investment supports both European and global efforts to meet strategic net-zero targets.
source_title: Poland Grants $320 Million to Support Ascend Elements’ Battery Plant - Battery-Tech Network

facts: Westborough, Massachusetts-based lithium-ion battery (LIB) recycler Ascend Elements has announced that it has raised $542 million in new equity investments.
The financing includes $460 million in Series D investments and $82 million of additional investments from earlier this year.
Ascend’s Series D round was led by Decarbonization Partners, Singapore-based investment firm Temasek, and Qatar Investment Authority (QIA).

Other investors include Tenaska; Alliance Resource Partners; PULSE-CMA CGM Energy Fund; BHP Ventures; Fifth Wall; Hitachi Ventures; Mirae Asset; At One Ventures; Agave Partners; and Alumni Ventures, among others.
Ascend claims the $542 million equity financing is one of the largest cleantech equity private placements in the U.S. this year to date.
The financing ranks as one of the top 10 equity private placements in the U.S. this year.
Ascend CEO Mike O’Kronley states the company's sustainable lithium-ion battery materials will power EV batteries and accelerate the global transition to zero carbon emissions.
O’Kronley adds that the investment supports North America’s critical EV battery infrastructure and aims to bring good manufacturing jobs back to the United States.

Dr. Meghan Sharp, global head of Decarbonization Partners, identifies Ascend Elements as a leader in the fast-growing sustainable battery materials space vital to accelerating transport electrification.
Sharp notes that Decarbonization Partners is collaborating on this investment alongside their JV partner, Temasek.
The funding will advance the construction of Ascend's Apex 1 facility in Hopkinsville, Kentucky.

The Apex 1 facility will manufacture sustainable cathode precursor (pCAM) and cathode active material (CAM), which are engineered materials made to precise microstructure specifications for use in EV batteries.
Most of the world’s pCAM and CAM are made in China from primary metals.
Ascend aims to commercialize an efficient method to make sustainable pCAM and CAM from black mass—the traditional output of LIB recycling facilities.

The company notes its patented Hydro-to-Cathode direct precursor synthesis process eliminates several intermediary steps in the traditional cathode manufacturing process and provides significant economic and carbon-reduction benefits.

Ascend points to several peer-reviewed studies showing its recycled battery materials perform as well as similar materials made from virgin sources while reducing carbon emissions by up to 93 percent.

Mohammed Al-Sowaidi, chief investment officer, Americas at QIA, states the investment aligns with QIA’s strategy of supporting innovative companies shaping the future of the global economy and will be a major investment in their North American portfolio.

Over the last 12 months, Ascend says it has achieved several significant milestones, including signing its first commercial scale pCAM contract, starting construction of its Apex 1 facility, and securing two U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) grants.

In June, the company signed a $1 billion contract to supply sustainable pCAM starting in the fourth quarter of 2024 with options to expand the multiyear contract to a larger quantity with a value of up to $5 billion.
In October 2022, the company began construction of its Apex 1 facility on a 140-acre site in southwest Kentucky.
When complete, the 1 million-square-foot facility will produce enough pCAM for 750,000 EVs per year.
Ascend was awarded two DOE grants totaling $480 million, part of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law in October 2022.
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC acted as sole placement agent on the Series D transaction.
source_title: Ascend Elements raises $542M to accelerate production of LIB materials - Recycling Today

facts: Georgia’s largest economic development project to date demonstrates how longstanding relationships can lead to the greatest success stories.
Over two decades ago, the Kia Georgia automobile manufacturing plant opened in West Point, Georgia.
The Kia plant delivered thousands of high-quality jobs to a rural community and attracted numerous suppliers to the region.
Over a five-year period, Kia’s investment revitalized the community and helped the company reach new goals.
Kia has announced new product lines, including most recently the highly anticipated Kia EV9 SUV starting 2024.
Essential factors for the location’s success are partnership and workforce, which Georgia has in abundance.
These are exhibited through the state-of-the-art 70,000-square-foot Kia Georgia Training Center.
The training center was developed to provide advanced industrial training for employees in collaboration with the Technical College System of Georgia.

This approach played a major role in a speedy six-month negotiation period leading to Hyundai Motor Group’s plan for the $5.54 billion electric vehicle Metaplant at the Bryan County Megasite near Savannah, Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) has remained on the fast track, breaking ground on the facility five months after its announcement.
This new OEM is already anchoring a supplier network worth an estimated $2 billion that extends across coastal Georgia.
More suppliers are expected to locate in other corners of the state.
In Commerce, Georgia, SK Battery America’s (SKBA) 2018-announced facility is also thriving.
As of January 2023, batteries were being shipped to customers from the SKBA facility.
The company was two years ahead of schedule to meet its hiring goal of 2,600 employees.
Over the next year, SKBA plans to further expand its workforce to handle growing market demand at its two existing lithium-ion (Li-ion) battery manufacturing facilities.
Following SK Battery America’s opening, Li-ion battery recycling and engineered materials startup Ascend Elements announced a new facility in Georgia in January 2022.
Within months, Ascend Elements confirmed a partnership with SKBA.
When Ascend Elements celebrated its grand opening only a year later, it had already attained a full 50 percent of its hiring goal ahead of schedule.
This led the company to announce a workforce expansion beyond its original plans.
Global leader Aurubis will focus on recycling copper and other precious metals for sustainable technologies such as electric vehicles, batteries, and solar panels.
An existing advantage in the solar manufacturing industry is cementing Georgia’s place on the cutting-edge of the generational transformation of clean energy.
Qcells North America, an affiliate of Korean-based Hanwha, began solar panel production in northeast Georgia in 2019.
The company has seen accelerated growth since then, announcing major expansions of the original facility and an additional $2 billion facility in a nearby county.
Suppliers have also begun to locate in the area.
The state is adapting for a seamless transition to sustainable technology by fostering fully integrated ecosystems through its support for organic partnerships.
Growth during the past five years has been rapid, and Georgia has continued to meet company needs thanks to years of foresight and investment in speed-to-market solutions for businesses.
Solutions range from megasites capable of supporting OEMs and their suppliers.
Pre-certified “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” sites fast-track construction timelines.

The state develops workforce pipelines and training through the Technical College System of Georgia, College and Career Academies, tapping into the large population of veterans, and through more traditional methods.
The growth of e-mobility extends to the aerospace industry, which has traditionally been drawn to Georgia for the highly qualified talent produced at its research universities.

Archer Aviation has announced a new state-of-the-art electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft (eVTOL) manufacturing facility located alongside one of Georgia’s nine regional commercial airports in Covington, Georgia.

Universities like the Georgia Institute of Technology and the University of Georgia draw companies to the Atlanta region, where creating research and development (R&D) space can provide opportunities for collaboration and innovation.
Anduril Industries acquired then Marietta-based Area-I as a way to enter Georgia’s aerospace market.
The company reinforced the public-private partnerships that had been developed through years of working with the Georgia Center of Innovation’s aerospace industry team.
Anduril elected to transform two buildings near Georgia Tech’s Tech Square in Midtown into mixed-use office, R&D, manufacturing, and production space.
Georgia’s technology and software development talent is as diverse as it is well-educated.
Cisco and Micron Technology each cited diversity as part of their decisions to locate in Atlanta.
Georgia took steps early to expand internet access across the state to allow companies and employees more choices for where they can live and work.

The State of Georgia’s marketing arm, the Georgia Department of Economic Development, works with communities to identify strategic markets for growth and hone their existing resources for interested companies.

The Technical College System of Georgia and Georgia Quick Start prepare the workforce with both customized training as well as holistic, concept-based manufacturing training to educate employees on the “bigger picture” of their role within a system.
This training provides employers with capable, informed team members who are ready to perform at a high level and lead with confidence.
This broader education also equips Georgians with transferrable skills that make them more capable of crossing industries.
Further, Quick Start’s training programs identify opportunities to upskill workers across industries, leading to a nimbler workforce.
Companies prioritizing logistics are not limited to Atlanta and the world’s busiest commercial airport.
Coastal communities offer access to both the fastest-growing port in the nation in Savannah and the Port of Brunswick, which is the second-busiest port in the U.S. for total Ro/Ro cargo.
Communities located more centrally in the state are connected through an inland port system that boosts the use of rail cargo transportation.
source_title: In Georgia, Collaboration Equals Solutions - Expansion Solutions

facts: Local and state economic development officials gathered on a Thursday in a former cornfield for the groundbreaking ceremony of the $1 billion Ascend Elements facility.
The plant will produce lithium-ion materials for electric vehicle batteries at Commerce Park II, which features CSX rail access on John Rivers Road.
Gov. Andy Beshear stated the facility will be Ascend Element’s largest U.S. facility and the largest economic development project in Christian County and Western Kentucky.

The privately held Massachusetts company initially announced a $310 million project that would employ 250 workers, with future expansion potentially reaching a $1 billion investment and 400 or more employees.
On Wednesday prior to the ceremony, the U.S. Department of Energy approved a $480 million grant for the Hopkinsville facility, elevating the project's total planned investment to $1 billion.

CEO Michael O’Kronley stated the federal grant is part of an initiative under President Biden’s leadership funded through the $1.2 trillion Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to incentivize domestic production of critical battery materials and reduce U.S. reliance on foreign-made batteries, most of which currently come from China.
The company utilizes a patented process called Hyrdo-to-Cathode that relies on recycled lithium-ion battery materials.
The 450,000-square-foot facility will be named Apex 1 and will produce enough material for up to 250,000 batteries a year.
Hopkinsville was selected from six site finalists in the eastern U.S., with access to CSX rail cited as a key advantage.

O’Kronley credited Mayor Wendell Lynch, Judge-Executive Steve Tribble, and Carter Hendricks (executive director of the South Western Kentucky Economic Development Council) for their recruitment efforts after about a year of working with local and state officials.
Ascend Elements will partner with the local school system and Hopkinsville Community College to train potential employees.
The company estimates the region will experience $4.4 billion in economic impact from the plant's development.

John Crenshaw, chairman of the Hopkinsville Industrial Foundation, highlighted workforce benefits and thanked the federal government for its greener planet policy goal, expressing hope that the project will be environmentally beneficial.
The industrial foundation secured land options from the Garnett farm family to develop Commerce Park II.

The site was previously considered in October 2021 for a 500,000-square-foot beef slaughter facility by Wisconsin-based American Foods Group, which withdrew after local opposition over environmental concerns.
Beshear asserted that the Ascend Elements plant will not generate any toxic waste.
Approximately 20 people, including Beshear and O’Kronley, participated in the groundbreaking by tossing dirt into the air to mark the start of Apex 1.
Ascend Elements has projected the plant will be in operation in 2024.
source_title: Ground broken, potential praised for $1B EV battery plant in Hopkinsville

facts: Ascend Elements is creating a domestic supply of critical minerals and engineered lithium-ion battery materials through “urban mining” and the innovative Hydro-to-Cathode® direct precursor synthesis technology.
The company is working toward higher rates of Li-ion battery recycling by improving recycling economics.
Ascend Elements returns 100% of critical battery active materials back into the domestic supply chain.
The company preserves and uplifts critical materials for the batteries that power the energy transition.
Ascend Elements transforms lithium-ion battery material production by establishing an efficient supply chain using recycled feedstock.
Its patented Hydro-to-Cathode® direct precursor synthesis technology produces new cathode materials from spent lithium-ion cells more efficiently than traditional methods.
The company's approach results in reduced cost, improved performance, and lowered process emissions.
Ascend Elements launches a truly closed-loop circular battery economy through its operations.
Linh Austin was appointed President & CEO in 2025 after previously serving on the company’s Board of Directors.
Linh Austin has over 30 years of experience directing U.S. and international oil & gas businesses, hydrogen companies, P&L management, mergers & acquisitions, strategy, and EPC.
Linh Austin held senior-level roles at McDermott, BP, and ARCO.
Eric Gratz Ph.D. is the Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer at Ascend Elements.
Eric Gratz served as CEO of Battery Resourcers from 2016 to 2020.
Eric Gratz holds a Ph.D. in Materials Engineering from Boston University.
Eric Gratz was a post-doctoral fellow at Worcester Polytechnic Institute in the lab of Prof. Yan Wang.
Eric Gratz co-developed the innovative cathode synthesis technology that became Ascend Elements’ Hydro-to-Cathode™ direct precursor synthesis technology.
Eric Gratz frequently speaks at lithium-ion battery industry events and serves on the technology committee of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Li-Bridge public-private program.
Ahmed Allouache serves as Chief Financial Officer of Ascend Elements.
Ahmed Allouache brings more than 30 years of finance leadership experience across U.S. and international manufacturing and services, including private‑equity–backed companies.
Ahmed Allouache has deep expertise in financial operations, treasury and working capital management, and leading acquisitions and integrations.
Ahmed Allouache is a Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA) graduate and is fluent in English, French, and Arabic.
Barbara B. Knight was appointed Chief Administrative Officer in August 2025.
Barbara B. Knight oversees the company’s marketing, communications, human resources, and IT functions.
Barbara B. Knight previously served as Head of Global Business Lines Communications & Marketing at McDermott International for seven years in Dubai, U.A.E.
Barbara B. Knight served in the White House as Deputy Press Secretary to First Lady Laura Bush and later as an advisor to former President George H.W. Bush and former First Lady Barbara Bush.
Barbara B. Knight holds a degree from the University of Texas at Austin and is a graduate of the Corporate Affairs and Reputation Program at the University of Oxford’s Saïd Business School.
Tomasz Poznar, Ph.D. joined Ascend Elements in 2021 and currently serves as Chief Commercial Officer.
Tomasz Poznar has diverse battery industry experience in strategic planning, products, and new business development.
Tomasz Poznar's previous employers include A123 Systems, EnerDel, Delco Remy, and Volvo.
Tomasz Poznar holds a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering from Wroclaw University of Science and Technology in Poland.
Deacon Powell joined Ascend Elements as General Counsel in July 2025.
Deacon Powell is a civil law and common law qualified attorney with deep expertise in construction law and over 25 years of experience in the energy industry.
Deacon Powell has overseen legal, compliance, contracts, and risk management functions for many in-house clients across the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and the United States.

Deacon Powell's previous employers include a NYSE-traded engineering and construction firm with multi-billion-dollar operations globally and a cybersecurity venture between a major U.S. defense contractor and the national oil company of Saudi Arabia.
Faysal Sohail serves as Chairman of the Board and Director for Ascend Elements, representing Presidio Partners.
Kojo Ako-Asare serves as a Director for Ascend Elements, representing Decarbonization Partners.
Robin Duggan serves as a Director for Ascend Elements, representing Just Climate.
Ascend Elements Corporate Headquarters is located at 133 Flanders Road, Westborough, MA 01581-1031.
Ascend Elements Base 1 is located at 9176 Industrial Drive Northeast, Covington, GA 30014.
Ascend Elements R&D Center is located at 133 Flanders Road, Westborough, MA 01581-1031.
Ascend Elements Apex 1 is located at 6505 John Rivers Rd., Hopkinsville, KY 42240-7417.
source_title: About Ascend Elements | Manufacturing advanced battery materials from discarded EV batteries

facts: Aspen Aerogels Inc. has canceled plans for a proposed 500,000-square-foot manufacturing plant in Statesboro, Georgia.
The canceled project was expected to create over 250 jobs.
The facility was projected to cost $325 million.
The plant was slated to produce thermal barriers for electric vehicle batteries.
The project was planned for a 90-acre site in Bulloch County, southeast Georgia.
Turner Construction Co. was brought on board to manage the construction of the plant.
On February 12, Aspen Aerogels announced it would cancel the Statesboro project and shift focus to maximizing output at its existing facility in East Providence, Rhode Island.
The CEO stated that streamlining operations in Rhode Island became a priority due to growing uncertainties in the electric vehicle market.
The cancellation was primarily influenced by slower-than-expected demand for electric vehicles and rolled-back EV promotion policies from the Biden administration.

A recent memo from the Federal Highway Administration announced the suspension of a $5 billion program aimed at funding EV chargers nationwide, which contributed to the company reevaluating its expansion plans.
Aspen is planning to relocate some equipment from the canceled Statesboro site to further enhance its Rhode Island facility.
The cancellation aligns with a broader industry trend, as other corporations like Freyr Battery have also put their Georgia expansion plans on hold.
Local authorities in Statesboro and Bulloch County expressed disappointment over the cancellation but remain determined to pursue other economic opportunities in the area.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels Cancels Construction Project in Georgia

facts: Over 100 new jobs will soon be coming to Bulloch County.
Aspen Aerogels will invest more than $70 million in Statesboro.
Aspen Aerogels manufactures energy infrastructure insulation used in refineries, lLNG facilities, and power plants.
The company will build its manufacturing plant in the Gateway Industrial Center across from Great Dane.
Benjy Thompson, CEO of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, stated that the advanced manufacturing company fits into the existing industry in Bulloch County.

Benjy Thompson noted that the project brings a significant amount of jobs and a high level of capital investment, which is a trend for advanced manufacturing projects, and that they pay a very good wage.
Construction on the new plant will start early next year.
Production at the new plant is scheduled to begin in late 2017.
source_title: Over 100 jobs coming to Statesboro

facts: Aspen Aerogels will pay $741,000 to the City of Statesboro for grants the city obtained in a partnership to provide infrastructure to the industrial park.
The City of Statesboro voted Tuesday morning to formally terminate an agreement with Aspen Aerogels, Inc. so that the city can be paid back for grants obtained to pay for infrastructure.

In 2022, the State of Georgia and the Bulloch County Development Authority announced that Aspen Aerogels would invest $325 million in a manufacturing facility in Bulloch County, which was expected to create 250 jobs.
Hiring was slated to begin in the second half of 2022 and production was supposed to begin in late 2023.
In May 2023, Statesboro and Bulloch County were awarded $750,000 in EIP grant funds to assist with utility infrastructure for the Aspen Aerogels site.
The basis for the $750,000 EIP grant funding was that the project was slated to be the largest economic development project in Bulloch County history.
The funding came from the Georgia Department of Community Affairs, was obtained by the local governments, and guaranteed by the private entity.

Development Authority of Bulloch County Vice Chair Billy Allen said the decision to build here was a testament to the region’s “commitment to innovation, particularly in the electric mobility ecosystem.”
At the start of 2025, the facility was still not operational as construction was not complete.
In February 2025, Aspen Aerogels stopped construction and signaled it would be backing out of the site.
As part of the claw back agreement, Aspen Aerogels must repay the $741,000 the City has drawn in grant funds to date.
Upon receipt of funds, the City will forward the funds to the Georgia Department of Community Affairs.
This action will relieve all parties of future obligations under the grant.

City Manager Charles Penny said $11,000 will also be paid for the grant preparation, which includes consultants, but there is no line item for that within the agreement approved by council on Tuesday.
The total cost of the infrastructure installation was $1.7 million, city officials said during the council meeting Tuesday.
The Development Authority of Bulloch County also provided the 90-acre site to Aspen Aerogels for free.
The company was also provided a partial 10-year waiver of property taxes with the exception of fire protection and school taxes.
Councilwoman Shari Barr said the money is getting paid back but the infrastructure is still there for the next business to use.
City Manager Charles Penny gave a ‘shoutout’ to the Development Authority, noting they worked hard to protect the community despite being criticized.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels to Pay Back $741K for Grants Taken by City of Statesboro • The Georgia Virtue

facts: Aspen Aerogels shares surged more than 13% Wednesday following the announcement of a major funding development and strong preliminary Q3 2024 results.
The company revealed it had secured a conditional commitment from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) for a loan of up to $670.6 million.
The funding, part of the Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing loan program, will finance the construction of Aspen's second aerogel manufacturing facility in Register, Georgia.
According to Aspen, the new plant will provide a revenue capacity between $1.2 billion and $1.6 billion.
Production at the new facility will be driven by PyroThin thermal barriers designed to enhance battery safety for electric vehicles (EVs).

Aspen CEO Donald Young emphasized that the proposed loan would fully fund the required CAPEX for the second aerogel manufacturing facility and play a key role in scaling PyroThin supply for the Thermal Barrier business.
Aspen's PyroThin product has attracted six major OEM contracts in the U.S. and Europe.
The PyroThin product contributed to a 277% year-over-year growth in thermal barrier revenues.
Aspen provided preliminary Q3 financial results reporting $117 million in revenue and $25 million in adjusted EBITDA.
The company posted a net loss of $13 million, impacted by a one-time $27.5 million charge from the extinguishment of a convertible note.
Chief Financial Officer Ricardo Rodriguez highlighted that the proposed loan and its terms will provide an opportunity to optimize capital structure if finalized.
The Register Plant project is expected to create 255 permanent jobs.
The project aligns with the DOE's goal of supporting domestic manufacturing for zero-emissions vehicles.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels stock jumps on DOE loan commitment for new plant

facts: Aspen Aerogels is adding a new location soon in Right to Work Bulloch County, Georgia.
The company is investing a total of $325 million toward the project.
The new location will create over 250 new jobs.
The facility will be Aspen's second aerogel manufacturing plant.
The City of Statesboro and Bulloch County region offer access to efficient ports, a strong available workforce, and secure low-cost utilities and critical raw materials.
Aspen Aerogels provides innovative, sustainable solutions for the electric vehicle and energy storage markets.
Georgia employees will collaborate with Aspen’s network of world-class scientists, engineers, and additional skilled workforce to solve challenges in the changing mobility landscape.
The Development Authority of Bulloch County welcomed Aspen Aerogels to the Statesboro-Bulloch County community.
Aspen's decision to locate in the region is seen as a testament to the region’s commitment to innovation, particularly in the electric mobility ecosystem.
Georgia Southern University and Ogeechee Technical College will provide critical education resources and support to help Aspen flourish.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels Plans Major Investment in RTW Georgia | NILRR

The company secured a conditional commitment from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) for a loan of up to $670.6 million.

facts: Aspen Aerogels announced plans to build a new plant in Statesboro/Bulloch County, Georgia.
The company will invest $70 million in the new facility.
The plant is expected to create 100 new jobs.
The facility will be located at the Gateway Industrial Park.
The new plant will be situated near Great Dane.
Aspen Aerogels manufactures specific insulation for refineries, petroleum plants, and other uses.
The deal took 11 months to finalize after the company's first visit to Statesboro last December.
The company visited the area about half a dozen times during the negotiation process.
Economic Development Director Benjy Thompson stated that adding jobs from several small to medium plants helps broaden and stabilize the local economy.
Thompson noted that the new industry brings diversity to the community and attracts people with different skills to fill those jobs.
Aspen Aerogels has plans to expand the facility, though no timeline or details were provided.
Hiring procedures and opening dates have not yet been announced.
source_title: New jobs coming to Statesboro with Aspen Aerogels plant

facts: Aspen Aerogels Inc released its 8-K filing on February 12, 2024, announcing financial results for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2023.
The company is a leader in aerogel technology for sustainability and electrification solutions.
Aspen Aerogels Inc offers high-performance aerogel insulation used across various industries, including energy infrastructure and electric vehicles.
The company operates primarily in the U.S. and serves international markets.
Fiscal year 2023 revenue was $238.7 million, compared to $180.4 million in fiscal year 2022.
The thermal barriers segment saw a 110% year-over-year increase in revenue during 2023.
Fiscal year 2023 net loss was $45.8 million, down from $82.7 million in fiscal year 2022.
Fiscal year 2023 gross margin was 24%, up from 3% in fiscal year 2022.
Fiscal year 2023 Adjusted EBITDA was ($22.9 million), compared to ($60.6 million) in fiscal year 2022.
Net loss per share improved from $2.10 in 2022 to $0.66 in 2023.
Capital expenditures were significantly reduced, and the company ended the year with a healthy cash balance.
Fourth quarter 2023 revenue was $84.2 million, compared to $59.6 million in the same period of 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 net loss was $0.5 million, down from $9.6 million in Q4 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 gross margin was 35%, up from 23% in Q4 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 Adjusted EBITDA was $9.1 million, compared to ($4.4 million) in Q4 2022.
The company's long-term strategy focuses on scaling to profitability and capitalizing on growing demand for its products in the electrification and sustainability sectors.
Aspen Aerogels Inc anticipates fiscal year 2024 revenues to exceed $350 million.
The company projects fiscal year 2024 Adjusted EBITDA to be at least $30 million.
Fiscal year 2024 net loss is projected to be under $23 million, with net loss per share under $0.30.
Capital expenditures for 2024 are expected to be around $50 million, excluding investments in Plant II.
Don Young, Aspen’s President and CEO, stated that diversifying the EV Thermal Barrier customer base will drive growth beyond 2024 and validate recent investments in this segment.
Ricardo C. Rodriguez, Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, emphasized the company's focus on sustained profitability and efficiently managing fixed costs and capital investments.
source_title: Aspen Aerogels Inc Reports Significant Year-Over-Year Revenue Gr

facts: Aspen Aerogels reported $37.9 million in revenue for fiscal Q1 2026 (period ended March 31, 2026), down 8% sequentially from fiscal Q4 2025.
Revenue breakdown included $21.6 million from Energy Industrial and $16.3 million from thermal barrier.
GAAP net loss was negative $23.7 million compared to negative $72.9 million in fiscal Q4 2025.
Adjusted EBITDA was negative $12.7 million, improving 29% sequentially from negative $18 million in fiscal Q4 2025 despite lower revenue.
Company-wide gross margin was 11%, with segment gross margins of 15% for Energy Industrial and 6% for thermal barrier.

Temporary operational disruption at the East Providence facility due to an explosion caused plant shutdown and near-term cost pressures from repair, expedited freight, and elevated inventory across facilities.
Aspen Aerogels received $37.6 million in claim proceeds from General Motors (GM).
$3.5 million of the GM claim was recognized as revenue in fiscal Q1 2026, with $4.9 million per quarter to be recognized through 2027 under ASC 606.
Cash position ended fiscal Q1 2026 at $175.6 million, an increase from $158.6 million at year-end 2025, supported by GM proceeds and an $8 million working capital benefit.
Term loan balance was $86 million, with a sole covenant requiring cash equal to at least 100% of the term loan balance after fiscal Q1 2026.
Fiscal Q1 2026 capital expenditures totaled $1 million, with full-year guidance remaining under $10 million for CapEx and $26 million in scheduled debt payments.
Fiscal Q2 2026 revenue guidance is between $40 million and $48 million, representing a 5%-28% sequential increase.
Adjusted EBITDA guidance for Q2 2026 is negative $10 million to negative $4 million, pending production and supply outcomes.
Management expects approximately 20% segment growth in Energy Industrial in 2026, driven by subsea, LNG, and maintenance turnaround opportunities.
European thermal barrier revenue tripled year over year for the quarter, with management forecasting $10 million to $15 million in European thermal barrier revenue for 2026.
General Motors EV market share averaged 14.1%, with Cadillac EV sales representing over 30% of segment sales in fiscal Q1 2026.
Management cited lower GM production relative to sales, resulting in destocking of finished vehicle inventory.
PyroThin is Aspen Aerogels' proprietary thermal barrier designed for lithium-ion battery fire protection in electric vehicles and energy storage systems.
Battery electric vehicles now account for more than 20% of new vehicle registrations in Europe, supporting early stages of production ramp-up among OEMs with whom Aspen has design awards.
Across European awards, Aspen supports programs incorporating battery cells from a diversified global supply base including European, Korean, Japanese, and leading Chinese manufacturers.

Aspen is advancing growth opportunities in battery energy storage systems (BESS), actively engaged in qualifications and commercial discussions with developers for grid infrastructure and data centers.
Management expects sequential revenue growth each quarter through 2026, targeting adjusted EBITDA breakeven at $50 million in quarterly revenue during the second half.
Guidance assumes the East Providence staged restart proceeds as planned, with delays likely to increase near-term cash outflows and pressure gross profit.
Sale of Plant 2 assets is expected in fiscal Q4 2026 and will be applied directly for debt reduction.
Financial framework enables management to reduce breakeven revenue levels from $330 million in 2024 to $175 million by end of 2027.

An operational disruption occurred at the East Providence facility on April 8, involving an explosion in a high temperature oven that resulted in plant damage confined to a specific area and temporary cessation of operations.
Staged restart of the East Providence facility is planned for May, conditional on safety reviews and agency coordination.

Aspen is working closely with its external manufacturing facility (EMF) to enhance capabilities for Energy, Industrial, and Thermal Barrier segments to support short- and long-term supply flexibility.
Energy Industrial revenues declined 15% sequentially, impacted by delivery delays in the Middle East and conflict in Iran creating logistics and inventory challenges.
Q2 guidance assumes GM production at an annualized rate of approximately 55,000 to 65,000 vehicles in the quarter, up from Q1 where GM sourced the equivalent of 43,000 vehicles annualized.
Current IHS forecast has GM producing nearly 100,000 vehicles for 2026, pointing to more production weighted to the second half of the year.
Elevated costs are expected in Q2 and potentially Q3 due to expedited freight, repair costs, and inventory build across EP and EMF.
Restructuring actions were designed to achieve EBITDA breakeven at $50 million of quarterly revenue, with management expecting to reach it in the second half of 2026.
Aspen aims to scale its Energy Industrial business to a $200 million high-margin business without the need for incremental capital investment.

GM has maintained its full line of EV nameplates and remains dedicated to long-term EV success, including the Cadillac division where EV sales represented 28% of total sales in 2025 and over 30% in Q1 2026.
Aspen's EU thermal barrier revenue in Q1 increased more than threefold versus the prior year quarter, with momentum expected to translate into 2026 revenue in the $10 million to $15 million range.
Management initiated a strategic review in Q4 last year to evaluate strategic options and align growth strategy and capital allocation priorities with maximizing long-term shareholder value.
Aspen expects to reach EBITDA breakeven in the second half of 2026, assuming success of ongoing production and supply mitigation efforts.
CapEx and scheduled debt payments are expected to total less than $12 million in Q2, with working capital variability depending on inventory production and sales locations.
Total cash outflows for Q2 could be $20 million to $30 million, including $12 million of CapEx and scheduled debt payments, highly dependent on production and supply mitigation efforts.
Proceeds from the potential sale of Plant 2 assets are most likely a Q4 event rather than Q3 and would be applied directly to reduce term debt dollar-for-dollar.
Aspen's financial framework supports flexibility to further delever the business and opportunistically invest in strategic growth initiatives while maintaining balance sheet strength.

source_title: Aspen Aerogels (ASPN) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript | The Motley Fool

facts: Aspen Aerogels is leveraging its aerogel technology platform to become a fast-growing supplier of critical parts and thermal barriers to EV OEMs.
The company is experiencing rapid adoption of its PyroThin thermal barriers in the electric vehicle market to address thermal runaway and fire safety in lithium-ion battery packs.
Aspen's value proposition for PyroThin is highest in NMC and mixed silicon graphite anode chemistry applications, particularly for pouch or prismatic cell form factors.
The company has started supplying production parts for PyroThin thermal barriers in the fourth quarter of 2021.
Aspen Aerogels generated PyroThin revenue from 10 customers in 2021 and is currently providing prototype materials to potential longer-term customers as they develop battery platforms.
The company has disclosed GM and Toyota as customers in its presentation.
Aspen Aerogels plans to build Plant 2, a new silica aerogel manufacturing facility, in Statesboro, Georgia, near the port city of Savannah.
Plant 2 is positioned central to a growing automotive and battery ecosystem in the Southeast U.S.
The first phase of Plant 2 will have approximately $650 million of revenue capacity with a startup date in late 2023.
Upon Phase 1 startup, Aspen's combined plants will have approximately $900 million of revenue capacity, sufficient to service industrial business and approximately 2.2 million electric vehicles.
The company projects gross margins of approximately 35% and EBITDA margins of approximately 25% at full capacity utilization for the two-plant operation.
Aspen plans to construct necessary infrastructure during Phase 1 to support a second phase that will add another $700 million of revenue capacity in approximately 15 months from kickoff.
Upon completion of both phases, Plant 2 and Plant 1 will have an overall revenue capacity of approximately $1.6 billion.
The full Plant 2 project is projected to require capital of approximately $575 million for Phase 1 with full infrastructure and approximately $125 million for Phase 2.
Capex per dollar of revenue for the full Plant 2 project is less than $0.55, which is lower than the prior projection of $0.65 per dollar of revenue capacity.
Aspen Aerogels expects to incur between $250 million and $275 million of capital expenditures in 2022, principally for the Plant 2 project.
The company reiterated a target to double revenue from 2021 to 2023 and upgraded its target to triple revenue from 2023 to 2025 to approximately $720 million.

Aspen Aerogels received a recent $150 million investment from Koch Strategic Platforms (KSP), comprised of convertible notes and common equity, following a $75 million KSP equity investment in June 2021.
The company is leveraging resources at Koch Industries for the planning, engineering, and execution of the Plant 2 capital project to de-risk scaling its business.
Aspen Aerogels projects content per vehicle (CPV) for PyroThin to settle in the range of $300 to $325, with PyroThin content making up around 60% of that total.
The company is investing in carbon aerogel battery materials, specifically focusing on silicon-rich anode materials, and plans to share key performance and cost metrics in 2022.
Ricardo Rodriguez, Aspen's chief strategy officer with a deep automotive and tier 1 background, will assume the role of CFO upon John Fairbanks' retirement on April 1, 2022.
Aspen Aerogels ended 2021 with more than $75 million in cash and received a $150 million investment from KSP to provide a financial foundation for raising additional capital over the next year.
The company's 2022 revenue outlook is set between $145 million and $155 million, representing growth of between 19% and 27% compared to 2021.
Aspen Aerogels estimates that awarded business from two North American and Asia-based automotive OEMs represents 100% of the 2023 PyroThin thermal barrier revenue target.
The company anticipates converting highly active prototyping and quoting work into additional program awards for battery platforms from other automotive OEMs.
Aspen Aerogels plans to publish its first ESG report this spring, followed by a more in-depth report recapping 2022 progress and defining future ESG positioning.
