source_title: Aspen Aerogels (ASPN) Q4 2021 Earnings Call Transcript | The Motley Fool

facts: Aspen Aerogels conditionally received a $670 million Department of Energy Loan Program Office (LPO) loan in October dedicated specifically to its planned manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia.
In a February earnings call, Aspen Aerogels told investors it had withdrawn from the negotiation process for the DOE loan.
The company will instead focus on expanding production at an existing factory in Rhode Island, as well as in China and Mexico.
Aspen plans to increase annual production capacity by $200 million in China by 2026.

CFO Ricardo Rodriguez stated that Aspen's manufacturing partnerships in China are "capable of delivering increased aerogel production capacity" to replace the Georgia plant, adding there are "literally no limits" to how much capacity can be increased there.
The earnings call occurred after the Trump administration's first 10% tariff increase on China but before a second tariff that took effect recently.
Rodriguez stated that OEM customers are not worried about the strategic shift, saying, "They don't care where it's made."
To qualify for various Inflation Reduction Act tax credits, EV batteries must meet a threshold of components manufactured in the United States, though the fate of those tax credits remains in flux.
Aspen officially opened two assembly plants outside Monterrey, Mexico in February: one to create aerogel rolls and a second to complete thermal barrier assembly.
The Mexican factories are currently owned and managed by a consulting company, but Aspen has notified them of its intent to purchase the "automated fabrication facility."
Due to the Georgia plant cancellation and a "reduction of various positions" in the last quarter, Aspen aims to reduce fixed costs by over $35 million per year.

Rodriguez noted that working with external manufacturing, including in China, has allowed Aspen to "pick up a couple of points of margin" in other business areas, calling it a "no-brainer" for the EV thermal barrier business.
As of the end of 2024, Aspen had paid approximately $323.6 million for engineering, design, and construction costs for the Georgia facility.
Some of the Georgia factory's assets will be moved to the Rhode Island facility to increase its throughput.
The company is also evaluating sending some equipment to an external manufacturing facility.
The canceled Georgia project was expected to come online in 2027 and was originally designed to supply thermal barriers for more than 200 million EV batteries each year.
The project would have created more than 500 construction jobs and 255 full-time operations jobs in a swing state that clinched Trump's election victory.
Aspen Aerogels makes thermal barriers for electric vehicle batteries, including for General Motors.

In late January, the president addressed the World Economic Forum, telling delegates that under his second administration there would be "no better place on Earth to create jobs, build factories, or grow a company" than the United States.

The Trump administration has paused funding disbursements related to the Inflation Reduction Act, and GOP lawmakers have stated plans to cut the law, though 21 House Republicans recently issued a letter supporting clean energy tax credits within it.
The Trump administration has discouraged EV adoption, including by halting a $5-billion federal EV charger program.
Rodriguez told investors that Aspen is concerned about the relatively slow rate of EV adoption in the U.S., noting China is at 50% penetration while North America and Europe remain at 10% to 15%.
Most of Aspen's thermal barrier customers are global OEMs who are mostly "staying the course" but remaining cautious about capital deployment during Trump's second term.
Rodriguez remarked that "Four years within a 20-year transition of electrification is not a ton."
Aspen Aerogels is at least the second loan recipient to walk back domestic manufacturing plans, following Kore Power which scrapped an $850 million LPO-backed battery plant in Buckeye, Arizona.
Kore Power stated it still plans to make batteries domestically but will do so faster and more cheaply via a retrofit rather than a greenfield plant.
Aspen Aerogels did not cite political factors among its reasons for the Georgia project cancellation and declined to provide additional comment on the decision.

links.context: Contact for Statesboro Mayor Jonathan McCollar media interviews

source_title: ICYMI: Latitude Media: Why Aspen Aerogels canceled its LPO loan — and its Georgia factory | Climate Power

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy is giving conditional approval to Aspen Aerogels Georgia for a possible $671 million loan for a new manufacturing facility in Register.
The company will make aerogel thermal barriers, which are protective layers for electric vehicle batteries.
The plant will initially create 550 construction jobs and add 225 permanent, full-time jobs.
Operating at full capacity, the Georgia factory is expected to supply thermal barriers for over 2 million vehicle batteries annually.
The incremental fuel savings is equivalent to removing over 5,000 internal combustion engine vehicles from the road each year.
The facility enables a reduction of over 8,000 tons of carbon dioxide equivalent annually.

The project will significantly expand EV thermal barrier manufacturing capacity in North America, helping to reinforce the domestic EV supply chain while delivering new economic opportunities for workers in Georgia.

The loan would be part of the Advances Technology Vehicles Manufacturing Program, which supports manufacturing of advanced technology vehicles, qualifying components, and materials that improve fuel economy.
Aspen currently has multi-year contracts with General Motors, Toyota, Scania, Automotive Cells Company, and Audi.
Register is located in the southeastern corner of Georgia.
The addition of the manufacturing plant is part of the President’s Justice40 Initiative, which targets what the administration defines as disadvantaged communities.

The Aspen Aerogels facility will be located in a disadvantaged community identified by the Climate and Economic Justice Screening Tool, which faces one of the highest rates of expected agricultural losses and barriers to transportation in the country.
Aspen is seeking to work with local and federal partners on public electric transportation solutions to help its employees commute to the facility.
The loan is contingent on the department and the company meeting certain conditions.
The project will boost the Biden administration's goals for electric vehicle adoption.
source_title: Georgia town of 157 lands $671M Energy Department loan for manufacturer | Just The News

facts: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. announced a definitive loan agreement with General Motors Holdings LLC for a secured lending commitment of $100 million.
The $100 million loan proceeds will be available to Aspen to draw on a periodic basis from January 2023 through September 2023.
The loan carries a maturity date of March 31, 2025 and has no prepayment penalty.
The financing is in connection with the construction and equipping of Aspen's second aerogel manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia.
The new Georgia facility is designed to triple Aspen's nameplate aerogel capacity.
Phase I of Plant II at the Georgia facility is expected to be commissioned in the first half of 2024.
Donald R. Young, Aspen's President and CEO, stated that GM's investment will support the construction of the new state-of-the-art aerogel manufacturing facility in Georgia.
Aspen has been a key supplier to General Motors for over two decades.
Earlier this year, General Motors named Aspen an Overdrive Award winner as part of GM's 30th Annual Supplier of the Year awards.
Aspen signed GM's ESG Supplier Pledge, outlining a shared vision for a collaborative supply chain that minimizes environmental impact and enhances long-term sustainable electrification.
In November 2022, Aspen was awarded a Silver EcoVadis Medal for its overall sustainability scorecard, placing it in the 87th percentile of companies assessed by EcoVadis.
Aspen's PyroThin® products are designed to enable solutions to thermal runaway challenges within the electric vehicle market.
Aspen Battery Materials, the company's carbon aerogel initiative, seeks to increase the performance of lithium-ion battery cells to help EV manufacturers extend driving range and reduce costs.
Aspen currently manufactures its products at its East Providence, R.I. facilities, which house three existing production lines.
Aspen also operates a Mexico parts fabrication facility for thermal barrier assembly operations.
The company's Aerogel Technology Platform™ is leveraged to bring sustainability solutions into large markets including e-mobility and clean energy.

links.context: View original content to download multimedia

source_title: Aspen Aerogels, Inc. - Aspen Aerogels, Inc. Announces $100 Million Secured Loan Agreement with General Motors

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Company, Inc. is based in Mansfield, Georgia.
The company was founded in 1971.
Beaver Manufacturing engages in the manufacture and supply of reinforcing yarns for industrial hoses used in automotive and various industrial applications.
Healthcare Interactive (HCIactive) secured a contract to provide population health management, including corporate wellness services, to Beaver Manufacturing Company, Inc.
Implementation of the wellness program is slated to begin as early as April 2016 for the company's 2016 enrollment period.

The wellness program features include smoking cessation modules, onsite and telephonic RN health coaching, incentive management with premium deduction rewards for participation, a technology platform integrating all program features, and actionable analytics with real-time reporting.
Steve Briley serves as Beaver’s Vice President of Finance.

Steve Briley stated that offering these wellness services to employees is a worthwhile investment to create a workplace environment that supports their wellness goals and motivates them to be the healthiest they can be.

A study published in the Annals of Internal Medicine and covered by CNN indicates that more than 80% of large employers are now using some form of financial incentive to increase physical activity among employees.
source_title: Healthcare Interactive Awarded Population Health and Wellness Contract from Beaver Manufacturing

facts: Beaver Manufacturing Co. Inc. is located in Mansfield, Ga.
Beaver Manufacturing Co. Inc. earned the Parker-Hannifin Hose Products Division's Fiscal 2020 Gold Link Strategic Supplier Award.
The award recognition was reported on March 02, 2021.
source_title: Parker-Hannifin hose business honors Beaver Manufacturing with supplier award - Rubber News

facts: Blue Bird Corporation conducts its primary bus assembly at the Fort Valley, Georgia facility, operational as the company headquarters since 1946.
The Fort Valley plant produces Type C and Type D school buses encompassing diesel, propane, natural gas, and electric variants.
In May 2023, Blue Bird inaugurated an Electric Vehicle Build-up Center within the Fort Valley complex.
The EV Build-up Center facilitates a six-fold expansion in electric bus production capacity to a targeted 5,000 units per year on a long-term basis.
By August 2024, the company had delivered its 2,000th electric school bus from this facility.
The Fort Valley operations employ over 1,000 personnel.
Blue Bird's total workforce exceeds 1,500 across Georgia-based sites.
Corporate administrative functions are managed from Macon, Georgia, approximately 25 miles northeast of the manufacturing site.
In October 2025, partner IMMI opened an ancillary manufacturing site for school bus seating in Macon, spanning 75,000 square feet and focusing on compartmentalized seats with three-point belts.
Blue Bird's joint venture Micro Bird established a new U.S. assembly site in 2025 to double output and add over 350 jobs, tied to New York State development announcements from late 2024.
A planned 600,000 square foot facility aims to accommodate projected growth in alternative-fuel demand without specified timelines or locations as of mid-2025.
Blue Bird utilizes a make-to-order production approach at its Fort Valley facility, featuring dedicated lines for chassis fabrication and body construction.
In 2019, the company commissioned a 60,000-square-foot advanced paint facility equipped with robotic systems to automate and standardize painting.
The EV Build-up Center handles final integration for electric models including the Vision and All American series, incorporating power systems like the Accelera by Cummins PowerDrive 7000.
Subsidiary Blue Bird South in Georgia focuses on prefabricating bus materials, including sheet metal components, to support main-line assembly.
The company implemented a multi-source strategy for procuring critical components during fiscal 2022 to counter disruptions from global events and semiconductor shortages.
In March 2022, Blue Bird appointed an executive vice president to lead global supply chain efforts and develop a stable supplier network.
Logistics optimization includes a partnership with Uber Freight that yielded over $3 million in savings by fiscal 2023 through consolidated shipping and carrier management.
Blue Bird launched the Vision Electric school bus in the early 2020s with up to 130 miles of range per charge and 6-8 hour overnight recharging capability.
In July 2023, Blue Bird collaborated with Accelera by Cummins to introduce a next-generation electric platform prioritizing enhanced performance for zero-emission student transport.
By March 2025, the firm debuted a standardized electric battery pack featuring the Accelera BP97E to optimize fleet integration and scalability.
The Vision Electric Type C battery-electric school bus entered limited production in 2018 with full-scale manufacturing commencing in 2019.
The Vision Electric utilizes a 196 kWh lithium-ion battery pack, delivering up to 140 miles of range on a single charge under typical school routes.
The electric bus supports regenerative braking and thermal management for battery longevity.
Powertrains for the electric models are sourced from Cummins (now Accelera).
The Vision Electric accommodates 72 passengers and features quiet operation aiding driver focus and pedestrian safety alerts for low-speed maneuvers.
The All American Electric provides a Type D transit-style alternative with similar zero-emission capabilities, integrated into Blue Bird's electrification strategy since the 2020s.
In July 2024, Blue Bird was awarded an $80 million grant to expand EV production capacity.
A 2023 repower program allows for converting recent gasoline and propane Vision buses to electric.
Approximately 20% of Blue Bird's sales involve propane-fueled buses, with over 1.3 million students daily riding propane models across more than 1,100 U.S. districts as of 2025.
CNG-powered options were expanded in 2019 with ultra-low NOx engines certified 90% cleaner than federal diesel standards.
Blue Bird maintains a dominant position in the North American school bus market, commanding approximately 33% share as of early 2025.
The company projects overall school bus deliveries to expand at a 6% compound annual growth rate through 2030 to 41,700 units annually.
In December 2023, Blue Bird formed the Clean Bus Solutions joint venture with Generate Capital to market electrification-as-a-service models and mitigate upfront costs for districts.
Over 20,000 cumulative low-emission units have been sold by the company.
Fiscal 2024 net sales reached $1.35 billion, an 18.92% increase from $1.13 billion the prior year.
Fiscal 2025 third-quarter net sales hit a record $398 million, up 20% year-over-year, supported by 2,467 buses sold representing a 14.7% rise in units and 5.4% increase in average sales price.
Adjusted EBITDA for the third quarter stood at $58 million, yielding a 14.7% margin.
The company raised full-year guidance to $1.45 billion in net revenue and $205-215 million in adjusted EBITDA, alongside a $100 million share repurchase authorization.
The company holds $173 million in cash offsetting $91.5 million in debt by quarter-end.
source_title: Blue Bird Corporation — Grokipedia

facts: Macon, Georgia-based school bus builder Blue Bird Corp has entered the truck segment.
Blue Bird Corp announced its Class 5-6 EV stripped chassis at ACT Expo in Long Beach, California.
The chassis powertrain is provided by Colorado-based Lightning eMotors Inc.
The chassis is suitable for last-mile delivery step vans, motorhomes, and other specialty vehicles.
Production of the chassis will begin in late 2023 at Blue Bird’s manufacturing plant in Fort Valley, Georgia.
The chassis features a modular design that allows for multiple battery configurations ranging from 70kWh up to 225kWh.
At its high-end configuration, the chassis supports a maximum vehicle range of 175 miles (280km) on a single charge.
The chassis is offered in three wheelbase options of 178”, 190”, and 208”.
The chassis supports a maximum GVWR of 26,000 lbs (11.8 tonnes).
source_title: Blue Bird Corp moves into truck segment with unveiling of Class 5-6 chassis at ACT Expo

facts: ADOMANI, Inc. signed an exclusive three-year supply agreement with Blue Bird Corporation for zero-emission electric drivetrains.
The agreement covers Blue Bird's Type C and D school bus product lines.
Blue Bird will purchase all of its production requirements for these drivetrains exclusively from ADOMANI, with no minimum purchase requirements.
Blue Bird's new electric-powered school buses are expected to be in production in 2018.
Jim Reynolds serves as President and CEO of ADOMANI.

ADOMANI will provide drivetrains leveraging state-of-the-art components, advanced battery technology, and its engineering team to help Blue Bird provide a safe, reliable, cost-effective zero-emissions transportation solution.
The timing for the agreement aligns with increasing availability of funding from government programs, incentives, and the unprecedented multibillion-dollar Volkswagen environmental mitigation trust.
Phil Horlock serves as President and CEO of Blue Bird Corporation.
Blue Bird unveiled its all-new electric bus chassis at the Student Transportation News conference in Reno in July.
Blue Bird has been rigorously testing its electric-powered school bus and demonstrated its on-road capabilities to potential customers.
Blue Bird is celebrating its 90th anniversary in 2017.
ADOMANI designs advanced zero-emission electric and hybrid drivetrain systems for integration into new school buses and medium to heavy-duty commercial fleet vehicles.
ADOMANI recently opened a new headquarters location in Corona, California, and maintains an administrative office in Silicon Valley.
Blue Bird has sold more than 550,000 buses since its formation in 1927 and currently has approximately 180,000 buses in operation today.
Blue Bird manufactures school buses at two facilities located in Fort Valley, Georgia.
Blue Bird's Micro Bird joint venture operates a manufacturing facility in Drummondville, Quebec, Canada.
Service and after-market parts for Blue Bird are distributed from its parts distribution center located in Delaware, Ohio.
Blue Bird is the market leader in alternative fuel applications with its propane-powered and compressed natural gas-powered school buses.
source_title:
ADOMANI and Blue Bird Enter into Exclusive Electric Drivetrain Supply Agreement for Type C and D School Bus Product Lines |
                                    Newswire

facts: Georgia plans to announce next week it has landed a factory from well-financed electric vehicle startup Rivian.
Officials hope the deal will make the state a leader in the next generation of the automotive industry.
Gov. Brian Kemp and Rivian officials are expected to hold a ceremony next Thursday to outline the project.
Rivian has been in talks to develop a site along I-20 straddling Walton and Morgan counties about an hour’s drive east of Atlanta, between the cities of Social Circle and Rutledge.
The deal would allow the company to ultimately produce 2,000,000 vehicles per year.
The plant is expected to be built on a site that was previously used as a manufacturing facility for automotive automation robot workers.
source_title: Rivian EV plant seen headed to Georgia on site east of Atlanta

facts: Central Georgia is an eleven-county region anchored by both the Macon and Warner Robins metropolitan areas.
The economy in Central Georgia tends to cluster around five areas: aerospace, healthcare and hospitals, kaolin, warehousing and distribution, and heritage and ecotourism.
In August 2017, Canadian-based Irving Consumer Products announced plans to build a manufacturing plant in Macon that would create additional jobs.

The Central Georgia Business and Technology Park in Thomaston is a 240-acre facility that houses tenants such as Southern Company, Solutions Pest & Lawn, Criterion Technologies, and Chief Manufacturing.

Houston County contains Georgia's largest industrial complex at Warner Robins Air Force Base and has more than 3,000 acres of land for industrial development with one of the lowest property tax rates in Central Georgia.
Blue Bird Corporation is listed as a major employer in the region.
The Macon Economic Development Commission recruits new businesses and industries to assist in business growth and development.
The region is only hours away from the Port of Savannah, a major U.S. seaport.
Major freeways and highways in the region include Interstate 16, Interstate 75, Interstate 475, US23, US41, US80, US129, US341, and US441.
source_title: Central Georgia - Wikipedia

facts: The Georgia Center of Innovation, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), hosted hundreds of attendees at the March 16 Georgia Logistics Summit.
Experts offered insights on the digitalization of the logistics industry and harnessing needed talent.

Created in 2009 to bring together all aspects of the logistics industry, the Georgia Logistics Summit has grown to become one of the Southeast’s most important logistics and supply chain-centered events.
The total sales impact of Georgia’s logistics industries jumped to $83.4 billion in 2021, equaling approximately 7% of the state’s total sales, an increase from $60.7 billion (or 6%) in 2018.
Georgia outpaced the nation in warehousing services growth at 92.8% growth since 2018.
In 2018, one out of every 14 Georgia jobs owed its existence to logistics; today, that number has grown to one in every nine Georgia jobs.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that long-term investments have made Georgia’s logistics networks a gateway to the entire United States and driven record-breaking economic investment and trade year after year.

Commissioner Wilson noted the summit was a sold-out program of hundreds of industry practitioners from business, government, and education who gathered in Macon to discuss best practices and position businesses for success.
Two featured panels at the Summit were “Inside the Mind – Awakening Your Talent Search” and a keynote panel titled “A Vision for Supply Chain Digitalization in 2030.”
Panelists included business leaders from Amazon, RaceTrac, Blue Bird Corporation, and Accenture who shared experiences on how technology is shaping logistics and fulfillment operations.
The role of data and analytics in supply chain transformation was explored, including ways companies are approaching changes to make supply chains future-proof.

Panelists emphasized that while digitalization offers tools, logistics remains a “people business” and stressed redundancy of operations as a key need for supply chain resiliency amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

Panelists noted digitalization is increasingly utilized for data harvesting and demand signaling, with companies focusing on vertical integration through leading-edge technology and automation to streamline logistics sequences.

It was noted that 50% of existing repetitive tasks can be automated through existing technology, but companies must also leverage human creativity, decision-making, problem-solving, and leadership skills.
Amazon representatives focused on delivery speed and responsible resource use at the summit.

Sean Holloway, General Manager of Sav 3 at Amazon in Macon, highlighted Amazon's electric delivery van fleet order with American startup Rivian to accommodate increasing demand for “last mile” deliveries.
Holloway stated that companies will increasingly focus on being good stewards and adopting green technology moving into the future.
The summit featured Macon Mayor Lester Miller and Irving Consumer Products, who highlighted Macon as a prime community for logistics success.

Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT) Planning Director Jannine Miller provided updates on strategic investments to maintain and improve Georgia’s top-ranked interstate highway system and state roads and bridges.
Miller briefed attendees on GDOT’s strategic investments in local projects to help trucks move more efficiently, including updates on long-term projects like the I-75/I-16 Interchange in Macon.
Center of Innovation Executive Director David Nuckolls stated that digitalization changes how business is done and why the Center fills a critical need for Georgia businesses.
Nuckolls added that through events like the annual Georgia Logistics Summit, they continue to showcase industry best practices and make Georgia’s logistics industry more competitive.

Educating the needed workforce was the focus of the event’s first panel, with representatives from secondary and higher education, along with the Technical College System of Georgia, taking a proactive approach to aligning education with industry needs.
New career pathways in warehousing and distribution are preparing students for direct entry into the industry upon graduation, and in some cases while still in school.
The availability and skill of Georgia’s large veteran population was discussed, including opportunities through the Georgia Veterans Education Career Resource (VECTR Center).
The VECTR Center provides unique, accelerated programs in high-demand strategic industries that abbreviate post-secondary certificate and degree processes by recognizing veterans' military training.

Panelists focused on developing students’ core skills, creating future leaders, and building partnerships with local schools and industrial/economic development authorities to foster industry-educator conversations.
Georgia is home to 85% of third-party logistics companies and is ranked No. 1 for infrastructure and access to global markets by Area Development.
The state is home to Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport, the busiest and most efficient airport in the world, and the Port of Savannah, the fastest-growing seaport in the U.S.

Georgia offers robust rail and highway infrastructure, including more rail miles than any other Southeast state at 5,000 miles, 1,200 miles of interstate highways (I-75, I-85, I-95, and I-20), and 20,000 miles of federal and state highways.
Many leading logistics companies have operations or headquarters in Georgia, including Amazon, The Coca-Cola Company, Delta Air Lines, The Home Depot, Norfolk Southern, and UPS.
Additional information on the 2022 Georgia Logistics Summit and next year's plans is available through an official summit website.

links.context: Additional information on the 2022 Georgia Logistics Summit and next year's plans

source_title: Georgia Center of Innovation Hosts 2022 Logistics Summit In Macon, Georgia | AM 1180 Radio

facts: Bonnell Aluminum temporarily shut down operations at its Newnan, Georgia plant following an explosion on Wednesday morning that injured five employees.
The company stated it was too early to speculate on the cause of the accident but is working closely with local fire officials in an investigation.
The explosion occurred in the casting area of the plant.
Bonnell produces aluminum extrusions for the building and construction markets, the automotive market, and specialty applications like refrigerators and air conditioners.

A prolonged outage at the plant could reduce the amount of primary and scrap aluminum consumed by the company, potentially contributing further to a global glut that has sent prices tumbling about 30 percent from their 2014 peak.
Three-month aluminum on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.7 percent at $1,635.50 per tonne as of 12:36 p.m. EDT (1636 GMT).
One injured employee was airlifted to a hospital in Atlanta, two were transported to a local hospital, and two were treated at the scene.
The explosion occurred at approximately 9:15 a.m. EDT (1315 GMT).
A spokeswoman for the Newnan police stated it was not a situation involving hazardous materials.
Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corp.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum shuts production at Georgia plant after blast

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation.
President Brook Hamilton will retire in July 2026 after more than two decades of leadership and service at Bonnell Aluminum.
Carl Czarnik has been promoted to Senior Vice President and General Manager of Bonnell, effective June 15, 2026.
Mr. Hamilton led Bonnell since 2013, following a career with the company that began in 2005.
Mr. Czarnik joined Bonnell in 2019 as Vice President of Operations.
Prior to joining Bonnell, Mr. Czarnik served as Vice President of Operations at Gerdau Long Steel and Vice President of Human Resources for Gerdau North America.
Dr. Arijit (Bapi) DasGupta serves as president and CEO of Tredegar Corporation.

Bonnell Aluminum manufactures custom-fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions serving the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, and electrical and distribution markets.
Branded product lines include TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum and Futura Transitions by Bonnell Aluminum.
The company operates manufacturing facilities in Newnan GA, Carthage TN, Niles MI, Elkhart IN, and Clearfield UT.
source_title: Tredegar Announces Bonnell Aluminum Leadership Changes

facts: Bonnell Aluminum has its headquarters in Newnan, Georgia.
The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Carthage, Tennessee; Niles, Michigan; and Elkhart, Indiana.
Bonnell Aluminum employs a team of more than 1,300 people.
The company specializes in the production of mill finish, anodized, painted, fabricated, and thermal-barrier assemblies of customized aluminum extrusions.
Product applications include construction, pleasure crafts, heat sinks for LED lighting, photovoltaic panels, automotive engineering, walkways, windows, and shower enclosures.
The company's work is featured in projects such as the Georgia Aquarium, Luxor’s pyramid-styled hotel & casino in Las Vegas, and the Philadelphia Eagles football stadium.
William Bonnell acquired his first extrusion press in 1939, making his firm the first in the industry to have its own in-house extrusion capability.
During World War II, scientists working on the Manhattan Project turned to William Bonnell for help developing a method of extruding radioactive uranium.
In the early 1950s, William Bonnell served as superintendent and general manager of Trimedge, Inc., which later became known as Trimedge of Georgia, Inc.
In 1955, he bought out patents and trademarks affiliated with Trimedge to form the William L. Bonnell Company.
The company continued to thrive as a wholly owned subsidiary of Ethyl Corporation starting in 1965.
Ethyl Corporation opened the Tennessee facility in 1969 and later acquired an aluminum window fabricator formerly known as Capitol Products.

By the late 1980s, Ethyl consolidated its aluminum, plastics, and energy holdings into Tredegar Corporation, making Bonnell Aluminum a core business unit of that international, publicly-traded corporation.
Duncan Crowdis serves as the President of Bonnell Aluminum.
Imports from China, benefiting from heavy government subsidies, grew to capture as much as 20% of America’s market.
Bonnell Aluminum joined forces with other dedicated extruders to file petitions with the U.S. Department of Commerce citing unfair trade practices involving China.

Working with support from the U.S. Trade Commission, the team successfully maneuvered through bureaucratic processes culminating in an ultimate decision by the Department of Commerce to apply duties to extruded product imports from China.
Following these duties, Chinese imports were reduced to a trickle compared to their volume in 2009.
To diversify and gain fabrication capabilities, Bonnell strategically acquired Indiana-based extruder AACOA.
The company sees particular optimism for opportunities in the automotive industry due to a push for vehicle lightweighting.

Federal guidelines are prompting new fuel efficiency rates to be implemented by 2025, making aluminum vital as it is one-third the weight of steel but can offer appropriate strength for many applications.
Transportation and future vehicle direction represent a significant growth area for the extrusion industry, including Bonnell Aluminum.
Bonnell Aluminum works in conjunction with the Aluminum Extruder’s Council, a U.S. industry association, to educate designers on the benefits of using aluminum and aluminum extrusions.
The company's culture emphasizes high integrity, long-term client relationships, and empowering employees to serve customers without personal agendas.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum | The Construction Source

facts: Bonnell Aluminum Corporate is a company that manufactures aluminum extrusion products.
The company offers aluminum profiles, channels, beams, bars, rods, and other products.
The company caters to building and construction, automotive, energy, furniture sectors, etc.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum Company Profile - Office Locations, Competitors, Financials, Employees, Key People, News | Craft.co

facts: Bonnell Aluminum, a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE: TG), announced that its TSLOTS line of aluminum structural framing systems is expanding operations into the Midwest region of the United States.

The TSLOTS aluminum framing system allows manufacturers to create light, durable, attractive, strong, versatile, and functional structures using custom T-slotted aluminum extrusions and connecting hardware.
A new leased facility operated in conjunction with Bonnell Aluminum’s existing Niles, Michigan location provides 12,550 square feet of warehouse space for TSLOTS inventory.
The additional storage capacity will improve customer service levels for the Eastern and Midwestern United States, including reduced shipping costs and shorter lead-times.
Steve Silvester, TSLOTS Sales Manager, stated that over the years as TSLOTS has grown, customers in the Midwest and East have been asking for a location closer to theirs.
Mr. Silvester added that providing materials that ship from the Midwest will allow customers to gain competitive benefits in terms of costs and lead-time.
TSLOTS is a full-service operation providing project design assistance, fabrication, custom packaging, and full assembly.
source_title: TSLOTS is Expanding Operations | Bonnell Aluminum

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a subsidiary of Tredger Corporation.

Bonnell Aluminum collaborated with the United States Department of Labor and the Aluminum Extruders Council (AEC) to introduce an apprenticeship programme designed to upskill potential aluminium extrusion workers.
The programme has national accreditation and offers potential funding from the DOL.
The programme is available to existing employees who want to focus on a specific trade career path.
The initial phase of the programme came in 2023 with an agenda to recruit and groom industrial maintenance technicians in a two-year apprenticeship regime.
A second phase of the programme was announced specifically designed to train aluminium extrusion press operators and die shop technicians, and is set to roll out in 2025.
Bonnell Aluminum plans to add skilled trades such as CNC operation, robotics, and welding to its upskilling initiative shortly.
Carl Czarnik serves as the Vice President of Operations for Bonnell Aluminum.

Carl Czarnik explained that the AEC Apprentice Programme is beneficial to both participants and the company, helping identify, develop, and retain talent while allowing apprentices to learn a skilled trade at no cost.

Bonnell Aluminum is one of the top manufacturers of custom-fabricated and finished aluminium extrusions serving the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, and electrical and distribution markets.
The company's branded products include TSLOTS and Futura Transitions.
The company has manufacturing facilities in Newnan (Georgia), Carthage (Tunisia), Niles (Michigan), Elkhart (Indiana), and Clearfield (Utah).
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum apprenticeship proves crucial in carving potential workers' future

facts: Bonnell Aluminum is a manufacturer of custom fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions.
The company has over 65 years in the industry.
Its production facilities serve many of North America’s largest and most respected manufacturing companies through design solutions and unmatched extrusion capabilities and services.
Bonnell Aluminum operates five manufacturing plants.
Approximately 80% of its focus is in custom manufacturing.
The company places high value on delivering products on time to customers.
Poor shipment visibility and delays caused by global material shortages previously made it difficult for Bonnell to keep up with growing customer expectations.
Plant managers previously had to combine onsite data using spreadsheets with a homegrown ERP reporting system, leading to inconsistent data and decisions across locations.
Michael Brown serves as the Vice President of Information Technology at Bonnell Aluminum.
Bonnell chose Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP and Fusion ERP Analytics as its preferred solution to achieve visibility across finance, sales, and supply chain data.
The company deployed Fusion Analytics across finance and HR departments in all locations simultaneously to improve decision-making.
Bonnell plans to roll out Oracle Fusion SCM Analytics next to complete the vision of full data integration across all plants.
With a single view of data across all locations, Bonnell Aluminum optimizes purchasing and makes timely inventory decisions based on customer needs.
Oracle Fusion Analytics provides visibility into inventory, production floor, and back office operations for stakeholders ranging from plant management to key executives.
The company uses enhanced analysis of its Fusion Cloud data across ERP, HCM, and SCM to identify problem areas in production that contribute to process inefficiencies and delayed orders.
Managers can better optimize labor across sites and direct spending based on real-time insights.
Fusion Analytics helps Bonnell connect cross-departmental data, allowing finance and procurement teams to find savings with suppliers and negotiate better prices during sourcing.
The implementation aims to drive cultural change across the organization with a more data-driven approach.
source_title: Bonnell Aluminum unifies cross-departmental data with Oracle Analytics

facts: Boogook Georgia, LLC is a Korean automotive supplier known for its expertise in fluid-carrying systems for vehicles.
The company announced its entry into the United States market with the establishment of its first facility in Macon, Georgia.
The manufacturer will be located on Weaver Road in Macon in the building that was once home to Brown and Williamson Tobacco.
At the time, over 3,000 people worked in the B&W building when it was Macon's largest employer.
Upon its opening in 2024, Boogook Georgia is projected to generate 80 full-time jobs, with further job growth anticipated in the future.

These employment opportunities will provide the local community with professional roles in the automotive sector, spanning management, manufacturing, assembly, quality assurance, and delivery functions.
source_title: From tobacco titan to auto innovator: Korean company breathes new life into historic Macon building

facts: Boogook Industries Co., Ltd. is a global automotive part manufacturer specialized in fluid-carrying systems in vehicles.
The company has supplied its products and services to various customers including Hyundai Motor Corps, Kia Motor Corps, and other major global automobile manufacturers as a TIER-1 supplier.
source_title: Boogook Industries, Co., Ltd. | The Org

facts: Manufactures motor vehicle exhaust systems & parts.

Produces commercial physical research laboratory equipment including traveling water screens, stop logs, water trash racks, fixed screens, intake structures, steel exhaust stacks, concrete exhaust stacks, flare or vent stacks, and exhaust inlet stacks.
Estimated shipping rates from Ann Arbor, Michigan are $80 for a pallet, $610 for a partial dry van, and $700 for a 48' or 53' dry van truckload.
The company's office is currently closed.
source_title: Bosal Industries Georgia Inc | Ann Arbor, MI - Macrae's Blue Book

facts: Freightliner Trucks is an American semi truck manufacturer headquartered in Portland, Oregon.
The company is owned by Daimler Truck subsidiary Daimler Truck North America (DTNA).
Vehicles are currently manufactured in Cleveland, North Carolina, and Mount Holly, North Carolina.
In 1979, a new plant was constructed in Mount Holly, North Carolina, and a parts manufacturing plant in Gastonia, North Carolina, both located in the Charlotte metropolitan area.
In 1989, Freightliner acquired a standing plant in Cleveland, North Carolina, near Statesville, that had previously produced transit buses for German manufacturer MAN.
Under its Freightliner Custom Chassis subsidiary, the company produces bare chassis and cutaway chassis for multiple types of vehicles.
In 2012, parent company DTNA announced plans to expand its workforce at its Cleveland, NC facility due to increased demand for the Cascadia model.
As of August 2014, Freightliner employed 2600 workers at its Cleveland facility and 8000 employees across North Carolina.
In September 2019, parent company Daimler announced it would halt internal combustion engine development initiatives as part of its efforts to embrace electric vehicles.
As of May 2022, the MT-series step van (called MT50e) is available as an all-electric vehicle.
The eCascadia, an all-electric semi-truck by Freightliner, was handed to customers for field tests in 2019 and officially premiered in May 2022.
An all-electric variant of the M2 box truck has undergone field tests.
Freightliner introduced natural gas versions of the Business Class M2 at its Mount Holly facility in 2009.
In 2013, Freightliner expanded its alternative-fuel lineup by adding a natural-gas version of the Cascadia.
source_title: Freightliner Trucks - Wikipedia

facts: DTNA delivered the first vehicle in its Freightliner Electric Innovation Fleet – a Freightliner eM2 – to Penske Truck Leasing this week, fulfilling its promise to put an electric commercial truck in customer hands in 2018.
Roger Nielsen, president and CEO of DTNA, ceremonially handed over the eM2 key to Brian Hard, president and CEO of Penske Truck Leasing, during an event in Carson, California.

Nielsen stated that increased hauling demands, regulatory pressures, and ongoing concerns over energy resource depletion make it more important than ever for DTNA to rigorously test and research electric vehicle solutions together with customers.
Nielsen noted that electric commercial vehicles present a real opportunity to advance the ideal of emissions-free mobility while improving customers’ real cost of ownership (RCO).
The introduction of the eM2 into Penske’s fleet marks a first in DTNA’s co-creation approach with customers as it co-develops technology to shape the future of transportation.

Hard stated that Penske is honored to be the first company to put this new medium-duty electric truck into service and thanked Nielsen and his team for their outstanding collaboration and spirit of co-creation over the past nine months.
Hard emphasized Penske’s commitment to providing the most effective vehicle technologies to customers and driving innovation and sustainability when it comes to mobility.
As a first step in infrastructure deployment, Penske Truck Leasing will install 20 high-power charging stations across five of its California locations starting this month.
Next year, Penske will put an additional nine medium-duty electric eM2 trucks and 10 heavy-duty eCascadia electric trucks into targeted service in California and the Pacific Northwest.
Penske will place the electric vehicles into service within its expansive logistics, truck leasing, and truck rental fleets.
Judy Mitchell, a governing board member at the South Coast Air Quality Management District (SCAQMD), participated in the ceremony; SCAQMD focuses on improving air quality in the South Coast Basin.
The Freightliner Electric Innovation Fleet is partially funded with a nearly $16 million grant from SCAQMD.
The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and the ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach also contributed to the grant.

Mitchell stated that SCAQMD is using every tool in its toolbox to bring cleaner and more efficient technology to the marketplace to reduce harmful mobile and stationary source emissions to the region.
Mitchell expressed pride in being part of this innovative and ground-breaking project and looked forward to seeing positive results from these efforts in the coming year.

Following the handover ceremony, the keys to the eM2 were turned over to Santa Claus for its first holiday delivery of native plant seedlings to help restore communities suffering from devastating wildfire damage.
DTNA and Penske Truck Leasing made a joint $50,000 donation to TreePeople, a local non-profit organization that plants and cares for trees throughout Los Angeles County and nearby mountain forests.

Cindy Montañez, CEO of TreePeople, stated that trees make Los Angeles livable and thanked DTNA and Penske for their generosity in helping create climate-resilient neighborhoods lined with trees that produce healthy, clean air.

Earlier this year, DTNA formed the Freightliner Electric Vehicle Council composed of 30 customers with strong use-cases for electric trucks, including Penske Truck Leasing, to further drive its sustainable transportation program.
The company is working with council members to ensure a holistic approach to launching electric trucks.

Members of the customer council benefit from co-development of deployment strategies for battery electric vehicles, including applicable use cases, current legislation and requirements for facilities, charging infrastructure, and service support.
The Freightliner eM2 truck is an electrified solution for local distribution, pickup and delivery, food and beverage delivery, and last-mile logistics applications.
The Freightliner eCascadia is a Class 8 tractor designed for local and regional distribution and drayage.
Both the eM2 and eCascadia trucks enter series production in 2021.

The eCascadia and eM2 are part of Daimler Trucks’ global electrified truck initiative, joining the company’s Thomas Built Buses all-electric Saf-T-Liner eC2 school bus, the FUSO eCanter, and the Mercedes-Benz eActros.
source_title: Daimler Trucks North America Delivers First Battery-Electric Commercial Truck To Penske Truck Leasing

facts: Workers at Daimler Truck North America voted by 96 percent on March 8 to authorize a strike when the current contract expires on April 26 at 12:01 a.m.
The strike authorization vote covers workers at six facilities in North Carolina, Georgia, and Tennessee.
The company employs nearly 7,000 United Auto Workers members at facilities throughout the South.
Daimler Truck manufactures Freightliner trucks, heavy-duty Western Star trucks, and Thomas Built Buses.
Daimler Truck is the largest manufacturer of commercial vehicles in the world.
The firm has over 100,000 employees across 35 countries.
Daimler Truck was spun off from German auto giant Daimler AG (subsequently renamed the Mercedes-Benz Group) in 2021.
Mercedes remains the largest shareholder of Daimler Truck.
In 2023, Daimler Truck made a record €5.5 billion ($6 billion) in earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT), with the figure jumping 38 percent compared to a year earlier.
The company announced that it expected to exceed earnings forecasts for 2024.
Daimler Truck raised its dividend by nearly 50 percent, from €1.30 to €1.90 a share.
The dividend increase translates to approximately a $1.5 billion windfall for the company’s biggest investors.
Truck assemblers at plants in North Carolina start at just $19.25 an hour.
Workers cite issues motivating the strike authorization vote including insurance, benefits, cost of living, raises, and health and safety issues.
Workers were forced to accept massive concessions following the 2008-09 recession that have yet to be restored while inflation continued to erode their standard of living.
A Freightliner worker in Mount Holly, North Carolina, stated there is a 100 percent chance of striking based on the mood of most UAW workers.
The strike authorization vote marks the beginning of the first major battle by a section of autoworkers in 2024.
It is the first major autoworker battle since the UAW’s “stand-up strikes” at the Big Three automakers last year.
During the Big Three strikes, no more than 30 percent of UAW members were on strike at their largest point.
Ford Motor Co. made $10.41 billion in profits in 2023, including $1.1 billion in the fourth quarter during the “stand up” strike period.
General Motors profits rose to $10.1 billion in 2023, an increase of 1.9 percent compared to the previous year.
Stellantis reported increased profits in 2023 at a total of $20 billion, an 11 percent increase over the previous year.
UAW President Shawn Fain worked closely with the White House and President Biden during contract negotiations to contain and suppress autoworker strikes.
Fain and the UAW Executive Board have endorsed President Biden’s reelection campaign.
The Big Three contracts provided a 25 percent wage increase over the contract period, which is far below the 40 percent or more demanded by autoworkers.
By the end of the Big Three contracts in 2028, top pay will still be less than what it was in 2007 when adjusted for inflation.
The COLA formula in the Big Three contracts does not keep pace with real inflation.
The contracts shortened the “progression” period from eight to three years rather than abolishing tiers as workers demanded.
Company-paid pensions and retiree health benefits were not restored under the Big Three contracts.
The UAW bureaucracy shut down limited “stand-up strikes” before workers voted on or saw the contracts.
Fain falsely claimed that all temporary workers would eventually be rolled over into full-time positions.
Stellantis carried out mass firings of up to 2,300 temporary workers and eliminated shifts at factories in Toledo and Detroit.
Company executives at Ford, GM, and Stellantis planned to make up for pay increases by laying off tens of thousands of workers as part of the transition to electric vehicles.
In October, workers overwhelmingly rejected a Mack Trucks contract presented to them by 73 percent after opposition was organized by the Mack Trucks Workers Rank-and-File Committee.
The UAW subsequently called a strike over the Mack Trucks deal and presented a virtually identical contract within a few weeks.
The UAW released a video titled “Not Like It Used to Be” during the run-up to the Daimler strike authorization vote.
The UAW video attempts to foment anti-Mexican sentiment by pointing to plants Daimler has built in Mexico and stating, “We’re just fighting to keep jobs here, in America.”
In 2022, the UAW made an unsuccessful effort to convince workers to agree to reopen the current contract, implement changes demanded by the company, and extend the agreement by three years.
The International Workers Alliance of Rank-and-File Committees (IWA-RFC) is building a global movement with member committees among autoworkers at Ford, GM, and Stellantis.
source_title: Workers at Daimler Truck North America vote overwhelmingly to authorize strike  - World Socialist Web Site

facts: Daimler Truck North America sales declined by 20% in Q2.
Daimler Truck North America orders fell by more than 50% in Q2.
Executives stated customers are grappling with a dynamic market environment during the company’s earnings call held Aug. 1.
The manufacturer lowered its 2025 sales guidance for North America.
Daimler Truck now expects to sell between 135,000 to 155,000 units in North America in 2025.
This revised forecast is down from the 155,000 to 175,000 units previously forecasted as of May.
The earlier forecast was 180,000 to 200,000 units as of March.

Daimler Truck CEO Karin Rådström said during the Aug. 1 earnings call: “I think our customers are probably mostly concerned with what happens with the American economy because that drives the need for transport or not.”
Rådström noted that continued uncertainty makes it difficult for customers to predict freight volume development and gear investments accordingly.
Daimler is scaling back production in North America due to a lack of demand, projecting a 20% drop in truck volumes this quarter compared to Q2.
In recent weeks, the truck maker reduced its headcount by 2,000 people in the U.S. and Mexico.
Executives confirmed the headcount reduction was a mix of white- and blue-collar workers.
The company builds Western Star and Freightliner trucks.
Daimler Truck has manufacturing facilities in Oregon, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Mexico.
CFO Eva Scherer stated that every week counts when it comes to orders.
Orders picked up in July compared to June.
The company expects or hopes that the July ordering momentum continues as the base for its full-year guidance.
Daimler Truck North America saw an improvement in orders in May versus April before another backslide in June.
The segment reported 13,842 incoming orders in Q2.
This compares to 29,604 orders during the same period last year, according to Daimler’s earnings presentation.
Scherer described the environment as very volatile and said it is too early to call the July increase a trend.
The company will keep monitoring the ordering activity closely.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America orders fall by more than 50% in Q2 | Trucking Dive

facts: Autoworkers in the South are currently engaged in a historic, high-stakes labor struggle against the multinational corporation Daimler Truck North America.
The labor contract between DTNA and seven thousand United Auto Workers (UAW) members is set to expire at midnight on April 26.
UAW members build heavy trucks and buses at plants in North Carolina, Georgia, and Tennessee.
Daimler Truck posts multibillion-dollar profits.
DTNA workers report being beaten down by stagnant wages, the rising cost of living due to inflation, and an increasingly demoralizing work environment.
UAW members are fighting for wage increases, improved health insurance coverage, and an end to divisive tiered employment structures, among other demands.
If their demands are not met, autoworkers say they are ready to strike.
source_title: Daimler Truck Workers Are Strike-Ready in the Anti-Union South

facts: Daimler Trucks North America (DTNA) announced the addition of two new parts distribution centers to its supply chain network.
The new facilities are located in Grimes, Iowa and Whitestown, Indiana.
The new facilities will hold more than $15 million of inventory.
The new facilities will hold over 20,000 parts.
The facility in Grimes is scheduled to be completed January 2018.
The Whitestown location is scheduled to be fully operational at the same time as the Grimes facility completion.
Jay A. Johnson serves as the general manager of Supply Chain Network for DTNA.
DTNA’s supply chain system has been architected to be anticipatory in nature, with the goal of having parts at service locations when customers need them.
DTNA utilizes Dedicated Delivery and Variable Cut-Off processes to enable same-day part delivery to service locations if a part is not in stock.
Daimler Trucks North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.
DTNA manufactures, sells, and services commercial vehicles under the Freightliner, Western Star, Detroit, and Thomas Built Buses nameplates.
DTNA is a Daimler company.
source_title: Daimler Trucks North America Expands its Parts Distribution Network - Thomas Built Buses

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) is expanding its all-electric RIZON brand in British Columbia and Quebec in 2026.
RIZON trucks (specifically Class 4 and 5) are designed for last-mile urban delivery, reefers, and flatbeds.
Early adopters in 2024 requested more capacity, prompting chassis upgrades for the 2026 model year.
The latest 2026 RIZON models feature higher payload capacity to make switching from diesel to electric financially viable for fleets moving heavy pallets.

Fleet owners in Canada can utilize the federal iMHZEV program (Incentives for Medium- and Heavy-Duty Zero-Emission Vehicles) to offset purchase prices, often stacking it with provincial rebates in BC and Quebec.
RIZON is technically based on the proven eCanter platform from Mitsubishi Fuso, a Daimler subsidiary.
In April 2026, Mitsubishi Fuso will merge with Hino Motors (part of the Toyota Group) to form a new global holding company called ARCHION.
The combined focus of Archion is explicitly on electrification, hydrogen, and autonomous driving.
For Canadian RIZON customers, the merger ensures long-term parts availability and innovation dominance backed by two of the world's largest truck manufacturers (Daimler & Toyota Group).
RIZON trucks have a range of up to 250 kilometres on a single charge, depending on the battery pack configuration.
RIZON supports both Level 2 AC and DC Fast Charging, offering flexibility for overnight depot charging or quick top-ups.
RIZON comes standard with Daimler’s active safety suite, including Active Brake Assist and Lane Departure Warning.
With federal incentives still in place and improved payload specs for 2026, RIZON is positioning itself as the leader in zero-emission city logistics.
source_title: Daimler’s "RIZON" Expands in Canada: More Payload and a New Giant in the Background - Truckstop Canada

facts: Daimler Truck North America LLC’s Mexican division delivered the initial two battery electric Freightliner eCascadias to Transportes Esteban.
The Freightliner eCascadias were manufactured in Portland, Oregon.
Transportes Esteban is a prominent cargo transportation company with 22 years of industry expertise.
The electric Class 8 truck is slated for regular operations within Daimler Truck Mexico’s truck manufacturing plant (TMP) in Santiago.
The Santiago truck manufacturing plant has been designated as the site for Mexico’s second commercial vehicle charging facility specifically tailored for heavy-duty truck electric charging.
DTNA’s Mexican branch drew insights from the pioneering “Electric Island” at DTNA headquarters in Portland.
Marcela Barreiro serves as President & CEO of Daimler Truck Mexico.
Marcela Barreiro highlighted the commencement of operations for the first all-electric Freightliner unit in Mexico and the inauguration of the second Electric Island in Mexico.
The second “Electric Island” charging station is situated within the Daimler Truck Santiago TMP for use by the Freightliner eCascadia as part of daily logistics operations.
This investment aligns with a broader project to curtail the plant’s emissions while leveraging DTNA’s advanced technology.
The second “Electric Island” installation features a Detroit eFill charger with a charging capacity of up to 120 kW.
The charger is capable of charging 80% of the eCascadia’s battery capacity in 220 minutes.

The Detroit eFill chargers incorporate intelligent charging management software that optimizes energy consumption and integrates vehicle information, stations, and the charging process through a telematics platform.
Since its production debut in 2022, the battery-electric Freightliner eCascadia has been distributed to over 50 fleets in North America.
The Freightliner eCascadia has accumulated more than 3.5 million electric miles in real-world customer applications to date.
Daimler Truck Mexico is taking a significant stride in the transition to zero-emission mobility with this delivery and future plans.
source_title: Daimler Truck Mexico Delivers First Electric Freightliner and Unveils Second Charging Station

facts: Daimler Truck North America is laying off 2,000 workers in the U.S. and Mexico.
The company attributes the workforce reduction to navigating a challenging economic environment.
source_title: Daimler Truck North America to lay off 2,000 workers in U.S., Mexico - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Daimler Truck North America renewed its Gold Level Partnership with the Women In Trucking Association to support gender diversity in transportation and logistics.
Daimler Truck North America has been an essential partner of the Women In Trucking Association since 2012.
The partnership includes sponsorship of the annual Influential Woman in Trucking Award for female industry leaders.
Kary Schaefer, general manager of Product Strategy and Market Development for Daimler Truck North America, serves on the Women In Trucking Association board of directors.
Ellen Voie serves as president and CEO of the Women In Trucking Association.

The Women In Trucking Association was founded in 2007 and serves over 7,000 corporate and individual members across the United States, Canada, Mexico, Japan, Australia, Sweden, South Africa, and New Zealand.
The association released the 2022 WIT Index to benchmark the percentage of women in critical transportation roles.
The percentage of professional female drivers increased to 13.7% according to recent data.
The Women In Trucking Association participated in White House and FMCSA roundtables and events.
The association launched the Professional Driver Hub, an online resource for driver success.
The 2021 Accelerate! Conference and Exhibition had over 800 registered attendees.
The Women In Trucking Association is a nonprofit encouraging women's employment in trucking, with 17 percent of its members being men.

Gold Level Partners supporting the association include Amazon, Arrow Truck Sales, Daimler Truck North America, DAT Solutions, FedEx Freight, Great Dane, J.B. Hunt Transport, Michelin North America, PACCAR, Penske Transportation Solutions, Ryder System, Walmart, and WM.
Daimler Truck North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.

Daimler Truck North America designs, engineers, manufactures, and markets medium- and heavy-duty trucks, school buses, vehicle chassis, and associated technologies and components under the Freightliner, Western Star, Thomas Built Buses, Freightliner Custom Chassis Corp, and Detroit brands.
Daimler Truck North America is a subsidiary of Daimler Truck Holding AG (DTG), one of the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturers.
source_title: Women In Trucking Association Announces Continued Partnership with Daimler Truck North America

facts: Daimler Truck North America (DTNA) announced Angela Lentz has been appointed chief people officer (CPO) for DTNA and its affiliated companies in the United States, Canada, Mexico and Australia.
Lentz joins DTNA’s Operating Committee (OpCom), along with the Daimler Truck AG Human Resources OpCom.

In her role as CPO, Lentz will be responsible for all aspects of Human Resources, including performance management, learning and development, labor relations, corporate security and corporate real estate.
Lentz began her career at DTNA in 1997 as a buyer in the purchasing department.
She became director of project controlling in 2015.
She subsequently served as director of procurement for mechatronics and Mexico truck and bus.
In 2021, she took on the role of general manager of aftermarket and strategic value chain and technology.

John O’Leary, president and CEO of Daimler Truck North America, stated that Lentz's broad-based experience ranges from finance to production to aftermarket, with extensive leadership experience and a drive to get things across the finish line.
O’Leary noted Lentz's dedication to assembling a team that celebrates diversity.
DTNA has over 26,000 talented employees who design, build, sell and service commercial vehicles.
Lentz succeeds Eileen Frack, who will retire after 18 years at DTNA.
Both executive moves will take effect on Jan. 1, 2023.
Lentz will be located full-time in Portland, Oregon.
Daimler Truck North America LLC is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.

DTNA designs, engineers, manufactures and markets medium- and heavy-duty trucks, school buses, vehicle chassis and their associated technologies and components under the Freightliner, Western Star, Thomas Built Buses, Freightliner Custom Chassis Corp and Detroit brands.
DTNA is a subsidiary of Daimler Truck Holding AG (DTG), one of the world’s leading commercial vehicle manufacturers.
Angela Lentz earned a bachelor’s degree in Business Management and Communications from Concordia College.
source_title: Angela Lentz named Chief People Officer for Daimler Truck North America | Daimler Truck North America

facts: Since 1968, Dekalb Tool and Die has been an excellent source for tool and die design, die making, tool and die repair and maintenance as well as metal stamping production in Georgia and throughout the South Eastern United States.
Dekalb Tool and Die has the experience and equipment necessary to satisfy all our customer's tooling needs.
source_title: Tool and Die Maker Metal Stamping Manufacturer Dekalb Tool Die Design Repair Atlanta Georgia

facts: According to the National Association of Manufacturers, the manufacturing sector contributes to close to 10% of Georgia’s economic activity.
Major brands present in Georgia include Keystone Foods, Coca-Cola, Gulfstream, Lockheed Martin, Blue Bird, Kia, Mohawk Industries, GE Power Electronics, and Southwire.
Machine shops in Georgia provide repair, prototyping, and OEM manufacturing services to these sectors.
Services prevalent among Georgia machine shops include CNC machining, manual turning, milling, Swiss-style machining, reverse engineering, fabrication, assembly, heat treatment, and EDM.

Xometry, Inc. offers an online CNC machining service through a verified network of 5,000+ vendors, providing rapid prototyping, small-batch machining, and high-volume manufacturing via AS9100D machine shops.

Prince Precision Products runs several 3- and 4-axis CNC lathes, offers manual machining for small batches, specializes in sheet metal fabrication, serves Southeast industrial clients, and provides walk-in service to the general public.

Torco specializes in mid to high-level production quantities of precision-turned parts for OEMs in automotive, aerospace, and related sectors, offering CNC/EDM machining, Swiss-screw machining, grinding, thread rolling, broaching, and assembly.
Southern Machine & Fabrication provides bespoke machining for one-off items and CNC machining for large-volume runs, operating since 1975 in 80,000 square feet of fabrication and manufacturing space.

DeKalb Tool & Die serves aerospace to pharmaceutical clients with metal stamping and CNC milling, has been in business near Atlanta since 1968, and creates dies for progressive, draw, and single-hit stamping.
Guthrie Machine offers Swiss-screw machining, CNC milling, and turning for small- to medium-sized production runs from a new factory near Buford in the Atlanta suburbs.

Aerospace Fabrications of Georgia focuses on CNC machined components and fabrications for aerospace clients, manufactures for over thirty distinct aircraft programs, and operates more than a dozen CNC machining facilities.

Hye Precision Products offers CNC/manual milling, turning, grinding, heat treatment, CMM inspection, and develops equipment for aerospace/industrial industries, with past projects including alignment and positioning fixtures and hexapod positioners.

West Cobb Engineering & Tool produces aircraft parts using CNC machining centers capable of handling components up to 204 x 114 x 55 inches, fabricating common metals and uncommon alloys like Invar 36 and 421.
Cobb Tool offers toolroom CNC and manual machining for one-off and small-quantity runs alongside high-production CNC machining, with expertise in welding and assembly fixture design and construction.

Win-Tech is a Kennesaw-based aerospace machine shop with AS9100 certification that provides manual machining for single and low-volume components and CNC machining and turning for high-volume precision parts.
Techwood Precision Engineering runs CNC milling and turning facilities alongside traditional milling, focusing on reverse engineering production equipment to increase performance above OEM offerings.

C & S Machine serves walk-in customers to heavy industrial agricultural clients with over a half-dozen CNC mills and lathes, offering stainless steel welding, waterjet cutting, and plasma/oxy-fuel cutting.

Lingerfelt Machine specializes in micro-machining for aerospace, medical, electronics, and military sectors, with CNC milling and turning capabilities and endmill sizes as small as 0.002 inches in diameter.

Gentry Machine produces parts for sectors from bakeries to foundries using a fleet of CNC machines, specializes in creating precise unique gears, has been in business since the 1950s, and is owned by an owner over 70 years old who recently received a BSME from Auburn University.
source_title: Top 15 Machine Shops in Georgia (GA) in 2026 | Linquip

facts: DENSO celebrated the opening of its new thermal assembly plant and warehouse facility in Urbandale, Iowa.
The new facility is named DENSO Manufacturing Michigan Iowa Plant (DMIA).
The facility represents a $500,000 investment.
The facility created seven new jobs.
The company expects to expand the workforce to 20 employees within two years.
The company expects to nearly double its production within two years.
The Urbandale facility assembles cooling modules for the agricultural and construction industries.
The plant handles pass-through products, including radiator assemblies and cooling modules for the construction, agriculture, and electric power industries.
The plant is localizing products that were previously assembled in the United Kingdom for U.S. customers.
This opening is part of DENSO’s recent expansion initiative to invest nearly $2 billion in North America.
More than $750 million of DENSO’s expansion investment is allocated to the United States.
The expansion aims to strengthen and expand DENSO’s product development and manufacturing capabilities across the region.
DENSO executives and local city officials celebrated DMIA’s grand opening with a ribbon cutting ceremony.
Urbandale Mayor Bob Andeweg attended the celebration.
DENSO International America President and CEO Howard Sugi attended the celebration.
DENSO Manufacturing Michigan President John Kumagai attended the celebration.
DENSO Marston President David Hanson from the U.K. attended the celebration.
DMIA Plant Manager Paolo Vercelli attended the celebration.
Howard Sugi stated that while the facility extends existing thermal operations in Michigan and the U.K., it is very much an Iowa facility.
DENSO has been operating in Iowa since 1974, when it opened its first facility in Cedar Falls to support heavy-duty, off-highway engine management and others.
Howard Sugi expressed excitement about opportunities for DENSO’s heavy-duty business in Iowa in the years ahead.
Paolo Vercelli credited the Greater Des Moines Partnership, the city of Urbandale, and the Urbandale Development Association for their strong support.
Vercelli also thanked the Urbandale Chamber of Commerce for its warm welcome and assistance.
DENSO’s motto is “Protecting lives, preserving the planet and preparing a bright future for generations to come.”
source_title: DENSO Manufacturing Michigan Iowa Plant Celebrates Opening Of Urbandale Facility

facts: Christine Gray and Haley Penney led a project to establish and standardize lactation rooms across DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee, Inc. (DMTN) campus facilities in Maryville, Tennessee.
The initiative increased access to comfortable and secure spaces for working mothers at the DMTN location.
Gray serves as an electrical controls engineer and Penney works as a quality engineer at DMTN.
Gray and Penney earned the 2025 CADIA Impact Award in the Systemic Change category from CADIA, an organization dedicated to driving opportunities for all in the automotive industry.
The award was accepted by Gray on behalf of herself and Penney during a May 21, 2025 award ceremony in Troy, Michigan.
In December 2022, Gray and Penney began collaborating with DENSO Women’s Associate Resource Group members and site leaders to evaluate how to better support new mothers.
They identified existing spaces to convert into lactation rooms and developed common standards to provide private, comfortable, and safe experiences for team members.
A key feature of the initiative was a two-in-one design concept that separated milk storage and retrieval spaces to maintain privacy and avoid disruptions during pumping.
DMTN currently operates four lactation rooms across its campus, with two additional rooms expected to open in the fall following the May 2025 announcement.
Once complete, there will be at least one lactation room in each building housing manufacturing or office spaces on the DMTN campus.

Gray and Penney are also active in Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) activities aimed at encouraging local students to explore technical fields through their DENSO Women’s Associate Resource Group roles.
CADIA presents the Systematic Change Award annually to individuals, teams, or organizations that break down systemic bias and address organizational barriers to inclusion and equity in the workplace.

Chuntao Ye, president of DMTN and leader of the North America Peace of Mind Business Group at DENSO, stated that DENSO strives to be a place where all can succeed and that Christine and Haley embody these values.
Victoria White of DENSO Manufacturing Athens Tennessee, Inc. won a CADIA Impact award for Creating Inclusive Cultures in 2022.
Former DENSO Manufacturing Michigan, Inc. team member Christina Khim earned runner-up honors in the same category in 2023.
DENSO is globally headquartered in Kariya, Japan, and operates as a $47.9 billion leading mobility supplier developing advanced technology and components for nearly every vehicle make and model.
The company invests in around 180 facilities worldwide to produce cutting-edge electrification, powertrain, thermal, and mobility electronics products.
DENSO employs 158,000 global employees and spent around 8.6 percent of its global consolidated sales on research and development in the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.

In North America, DENSO is headquartered in Southfield, Michigan, employs 27,000+ team members across nearly 50 sites in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and generated $12.5 billion in consolidated sales in the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.
source_title: DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee, Inc., Team Members Earn CADIA Impact Award for Initiative Supporting New Mothers |  News |  News |  DENSO US/Canada Website

facts: Tennessee Gov. Bill Haslam and Economic and Community Development Commissioner Randy Boyd along with DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee officials recently announced the company will expand its automotive components and systems manufacturing plant in Maryville.
The expansion makes the Maryville campus one of the largest operating campuses in DENSO Corporation’s worldwide operations.
DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee has a workforce of more than 3,100 employees.
DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee is Blount County’s largest employer.
The company will create 500 new jobs.
The company will invest $400 million to expand its manufacturing capabilities and construct a new warehouse.
Due to expanded production needs at two of the company’s manufacturing plants, the existing warehouse will be converted to manufacturing space.
A new consolidated warehouse will be built on the company’s current campus.
Governor Haslam stated the announcement is another indication of the momentum Tennessee has in the automotive manufacturing sector.
Commissioner Randy Boyd noted this is the second expansion DENSO has made at one of its Tennessee facilities in less than a year since he became commissioner.

DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee President Jack Helmboldt, who is also a DENSO Corporation global executive director, thanked skilled associates, Governor Haslam, Commissioner Boyd, state and local leaders, the Blount Chamber Partnership, and TVA for their support.
DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee is a key supplier of advanced technology to the automotive industry.
The Maryville campus produces starters, alternators, instrument clusters, and a wide range of electronic components and systems.
The Maryville campus consists of four manufacturing plants on 194 acres.
The Maryville campus has undergone eight major expansions.
U.S. Sen. Lamar Alexander noted the auto industry has spread into 80 of Tennessee's 95 counties to become the main contributor to raising family incomes.

Blount County Mayor Ed Mitchell praised the collaborative efforts of the Blount Partnership, the state of Tennessee, and the cities of Alcoa and Maryville in producing a win-win environment for the regional community.
Maryville City Mayor Tom Taylor highlighted DENSO's impact on the community with jobs and its way of giving back.

DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee donated a separate gift of $50,000 to East Tennessee Children’s Hospital.
Alcoa City Mayor Don Mull welcomed the opportunity to further the relationship with DENSO for the next 25 years.
