facts: Michelin North America retread business celebrated a decade of business with Lehigh Technologies, a Georgia-based company that manufactures Micronized Rubber Powders.
The partnership with Lehigh Technologies began early in the specialty chemical company’s history to manufacture Micronized Rubber Powder (MRP) for reusing end-of-life tire materials.
Lehigh Technologies was acquired by the Michelin Group in October 2017.
Lehigh is a key element of Michelin’s ambition to make tires with 80 percent sustainable materials by 2048.

Gary Scheide, responsible for materials manufacturing for Michelin North America, stated that Michelin and its customers will benefit from the closed-loop approach where tires are recycled into tires with Lehigh Technologies.
Gary Scheide described the Lehigh team as a great partner and reliable supplier to Michelin’s plants in North America.
Lehigh’s proprietary technology converts rubber materials into a high technology powder incorporated in new retread compounds for higher performance levels.

MRP replaces oil- and rubber-based feedstocks in industrial and consumer applications including high-performance tires, plastics, consumer goods, coatings, sealants, construction materials, and asphalt.
Lehigh technical experts collaborate with customers to optimize products for each application.
Michelin North American retread facilities operate as zero-waste operations due to the closed-loop model.
This circular economy approach reduces carbon dioxide load and delivers economic advantages to the company.
Lehigh is now part of Michelin’s High Technology Materials Business Unit.
The acquisition supports Michelin’s Sustainable Mobility vision and its ambition to recycle 100 percent of tires by 2048.
Michelin plans to be the global leader in using MRP in new tires.
Working with Lehigh ensures supply chain efficiencies are maximized and material specifications exactly fit retread compound performance requirements.

Christophe Rahier, director of the High Technology Materials Business Line for the Michelin Group, noted that markets must improve sustainability profiles and reduce carbon-based material consumption.

Christophe Rahier stated Lehigh’s products enable rubber powders from end-of-life tires to deliver cost savings, performance, and sustainability to customers in tire, asphalt, construction, plastics, and coatings markets.
Michelin is committed to growth plans for Lehigh’s PolyDyne™ and MicroDyne™ products across multiple global high-performance markets.
The value proposition for MRP has been proven in the tire industry, prompting Michelin to support wide adoption of the technology for sustainability and environmental performance leadership.
For Europe, Michelin is working with Lehigh to build the first MRP plant outside the U.S., a 10,000-metric-ton facility in Navarra, Spain.
The Spanish MRP plant will be commissioned in summer 2018.
source_title: Michelin Celebrates 10 Years of a Closed-Loop Process Using Micronized Rubber Powder in North America :: Michelin North America, Inc.

facts: Lehigh Technologies is an Atlanta-based subsidiary of Michelin North America, Inc.
Lehigh’s Rubber Modified Asphalt (RMA), made with recycled tire rubber powder, is being used to resurface 6.3 miles of Lawrenceville Highway between Tucker and Lilburn, Georgia.
The paving project began in September and was in its final stages as of the article's publication date.
RMA is produced using Lehigh’s MicroDyneTM rubber powder plus proprietary additive RheopaveTM.
This product blend has been used to produce more than 1,500 miles of road in recent years.
Kara Fulcher, Director of Sustainability and Government Affairs for Michelin North America, Inc., states that RMA uses end-of-life tires already available in the North American market.
The recycled material replaces a polymer derived from the oil-refining process.
RMA serves as a locally sourced replacement for an ingredient generally imported from Asia, providing a resilient response to a long supply chain.
Kara Fulcher states that Michelin and Lehigh are committed to taking end-of-life tires and giving them new life in other products through innovation.
RMA can modify liquid bitumen at an asphalt terminal or be directly added at an asphalt contractor’s site via the “Dry Process,” which was used for this project.

Jason Stravinski, President and CEO of Lehigh Technologies, states that Rubber Modified Asphalt produced via the Dry Process provides a paving solution equally effective as oil-derived polymer modified asphalt, yet cheaper and more environmentally friendly.
The repaved route covers 6.3 miles of Lawrenceville Highway (State Road 8) from the DeKalb County line to Ronald Reagan Parkway.
Lehigh’s headquarters is located in Tucker, Georgia, a suburb of Atlanta.
In addition to road surfacing, Lehigh’s micronized rubber powders are used in new tires, as well as a variety of rubber goods, roofing, plastics, industrial coatings, and automotive components.
Lehigh Technologies uses cryogenic turbo mill technology to convert end-of-life tires and other post-industrial rubber into micron-scale powders for consumer and industrial applications.
Lehigh's customers include more than 60 leading tire, asphalt, and plastics companies around the world.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies helps turn Georgia’s Lawrenceville Highway into Easy Street :: Michelin North America, Inc.

facts: Lehigh Technologies has been owned by Michelin since October 2017.
Lehigh Technologies is now part of Michelin’s High Technology Materials business unit.
Lehigh Spain is setting up a Micronised Rubber Powders (MRP) production facility in Murrillo del Fruto, Spain.
The new Spanish MRP production facility will begin operations in summer 2018.
The Spanish facility is the product of a joint venture project between Lehigh Technologies and Hera Holding.
Lehigh Technologies was founded in 2003.
Lehigh Technologies is the market leader for Micronised Rubber Powders (MRP).
MRP reduces feedstock costs by up to 50 percent while delivering performance across a wide range of applications.
MRP replaces oil- and rubber-based feedstocks in high performance tyres, plastics, consumer goods, coatings, sealants, construction materials, and asphalt.
Lehigh technical experts collaborate with customers to optimize products for each application.
The Lehigh facility in Tucker, Georgia (USA) has an annual capacity of 54,000 tonnes.
The Lehigh facility in Tucker, Georgia is the world’s largest MRP manufacturing plant.
Lehigh’s Application & Development Center is located in Tucker, Georgia.
The Tucker, Georgia center serves as an innovation hub where Michelin conducts research and formulates MRPs in collaboration with customers.
Michelin has developed five MRP product ranges: PolyDyne, MicroDyne, EkoDyne, Rheopave, and Zenoflex.
The range of solutions in core markets continues to expand.
source_title: Lehigh Technologies: European MRP production beginning this summer - Tyrepress

canonical_name: Systems, Methods, and Apparatuses for Manufacturing Micronized Powder
facts: The systems relate to producing a predetermined particle size range of micronized powder from materials of heterogeneous size and composition.
Micronized powders may be used in the food industry, pharmaceutical industry, plastics industry, and rubber industry.
Recycling vulcanized rubber (e.g., used tires) into micronized rubber powder (MRP) addresses environmental disposal problems.
MRP can serve as a filler in new rubber mixes, such as tire tread compounds for vehicle tires.
MRP can be used as a filler for polyolefins in plastic formulations and in asphalt manufacturing.
In these applications, MRP replaces virgin compound materials and comprises vulcanized elastomer particles of 2 mm or less in diameter conforming to ASTM D-5603-01 standards.
Traditional MRP production methods include cryogenic grinding, chemical processes, and other methods, which are often expensive and inefficient.
The disclosed systems allow any vulcanized rubber/elastomer as infeed material regardless of supplementary materials like steel plates and fibrous materials from tires.
A vibrating screener is used to remove fibrous materials from MRP by retaining the fibers while allowing the powder to pass through.
A warming apparatus regulates operating efficiency using recirculation of MRP as part of a proportional integral derivative (PID) loop.
Reintroducing MRP to the warming apparatus increases surface area contact, promoting rapid heat transfer between particles and the apparatus.
An accumulator, such as a storage silo, decouples the grinding process from the screening process to allow both to operate at optimal rates.

Operating efficiency of the warming apparatus can be determined by measuring motor amperage, temperature at a predetermined point, current capacity, volumetric flow rate, or using a theoretical model.
If operating efficiency falls below a predefined threshold, MRP is recirculated through the warming apparatus until efficiency improves.
A vibrating screener embodiment uses mesh sizes of 20 or 25 and operates for at least 1 minute to retain ≥99% of fibrous material while passing ≥99% of MRP.
The overall process includes pre-grinding processing, cryogenic freezing, grinding, warming, ferrous metal and fiber removal, accumulation, screening, and storage.
Pre-grinding processing modifies infeed material by roughly grinding large irregular rubber pieces and may remove extraneous materials like steel plates from tires.
Cryogenic freezing significantly reduces infeed temperature, often using liquid nitrogen introduced via a rotating auger, to make rubber/elastomer brittle for uniform grinding.
A conical-shaped impact mill grinds the cryogenically-frozen infeed by pulling it toward a high-speed rotor where particles bounce until broken into MRP.
MRP exiting the grinder has a broad particle size distribution (e.g., ≥50 mesh and ≤140 mesh, or diameter ≤2 mm, or ≥40 mesh and ≤200 mesh) and remains at cryogenic temperatures of −80° F to −100° F.

Warming prevents condensation that could turn MRP into a spongy solid, potentially recirculating it until reaching >80° F, above the dew point, a specific flow rate like 6,000 pounds per hour, or 90% capacity.
Accumulation occurs in silos, storage bins, drums, flexible intermediate bulk containers, or intermediate bulk containers to decouple grinding and screening flow rates.
A screening apparatus sorts MRP by particle size into predefined narrow ranges using multiple screens of varying mesh sizes.
Screened MRP is stored or packaged in bags, silos, trucks, etc., for later use in applications like asphalt or tire manufacturing.
One warming apparatus embodiment uses an 18″×20″ Thomas Conveyor piped with 2″ insulated pipe attached to a HEAT thermal fluid heat transfer system.
source_title:
US10675634B2 - Systems, methods, and apparatuses for manufacturing micronized powder 
        - Google Patents

facts: LINDE + WIEMANN combines profiling, stamping, joining and gluing technology to drive innovations in structural assemblies for the automotive industry.
The company aims to increase vehicle safety and reduce vehicle weight through its products.
Its German site in Hagenbach produces ready-to-install assemblies of high-strength steel.
LINDE + WIEMANN supplies these assemblies to global vehicle manufacturers and automotive suppliers.
Automated 3D measurement technology solutions from ZEISS support the tier-1 supplier throughout the entire manufacturing process.
Three integrated ScanBox systems scan measuring data and submit it directly to upstream production using ZEISS PiWeb.
Deviations are detected quickly so appropriate corrective measures can be initiated.
As a close partner of the automotive industry, LINDE + WIEMANN has faced increasing customer demands and ever more complex geometries for many years.
This development requires constant adaptation of production and quality assurance processes.
The automotive supplier regularly shares its measuring data with customers.
The parallel operation of the ScanBox systems accelerates the measuring procedures significantly.
Quality management receives valid information on part quality during ongoing production.

Peter Kluge, Head of Quality Management of LINDE + WIEMANN, explains that they pass on measuring results to the manufacturing department promptly so decisive measures can be taken directly and unnecessary quality costs avoided.
ZEISS optical measuring machines offer significant advantages for automotive suppliers in terms of accuracy.
ATOS sensors digitize the entire surface of the structural components to be inspected within seconds via fringe projection.
Every measurement identifies possible deviations between the actual 3D coordinates and the CAD data, which are displayed through a color comparison in the ZEISS INSPECT software.
Michael Kray, Quality Manager at LINDE + WIEMANN, explains that their parts are very complex but optical 3D metrology from ZEISS makes measuring them easy and efficient.
The company benefits from the flexibility of the automated systems.
In everyday production, the low set-up time of the ScanBox system enables the inspection of other structurally relevant components within just a few minutes.
source_title: LINDE + WIEMANN | Quality assurance with automated component testing

facts: Lund International is a premier manufacturer and marketer of automotive aftermarket accessories primarily for light and heavy trucks, sport utility vehicles (SUVs), and crossovers.

The company's product portfolio includes running boards, hood shields, side window deflectors, visors, bug shields, tonneau covers, folding bed covers, grille guards & bull bars, guide lights, cargo bed extenders, fender flares, and ABS plastic parts.
In 1998, Lund International acquired AVS, taking it to new levels of success and innovation; AVS became synonymous with Ventvisor® side window deflectors and hood protectors.
Lund International is one of the world's leading automotive accessories providers offering highly functional, protective, and stylish products from the industry's most recognized brands.
The company operates within the Transportation Equipment Manufacturing industry with a reported revenue range of $100 to $500 million (USD).
Lund International is located in Buford, GA.
Lund International launched a new look and new capabilities for its consumer and commercial website on May 17, 2005.

facts: Lyle Industries is a premier supplier of automotive textile carpet for molded flooring, interior trim, and floor mats.
The company serves OEM, Aftermarket, and Accessory markets for tufting, precision cutting, and engineered finishing solutions.
Lyle Industries has 34.5 million square yards of annual tufting capacity for automotive and specialty markets.
Production utilizes multiple gauge configurations including 1/10 Gauge, 5/64 Gauge, loop machines, high-speed creel, and beam systems tuned to OEM specifications.
The company uses Lectra CNC and press-based cutting systems, specifically the Bruno FH350 Ton Press, Bruno FH350T Press, and Freeman Schwabe 300 Ton Press, for dimensional accuracy.
Manufacturing processes include advanced latex and polyethylene coating tailored to exact performance requirements.
The company implements thermal bonding, moisture barriers, and specialty finishes configured to application and market specifications.
Products are engineered to meet durability and compliance standards for passenger cars, light trucks, and heavy transport vehicles.
Lyle Industries is certified to ISO 9001:2015 with operations structured around documented quality systems and traceability.
The company actively evaluates material inputs and production methods to reduce waste, improve recyclability, and align with OEM sustainability requirements.
Lyle Industries sponsors charities supporting healthcare, youth vocation, and community improvement in its operating regions.
The company operates as a Georgia employer committed to safe working conditions, fair labor practices, and contributing to the Dalton region's manufacturing workforce.
Lyle Industries was founded in 1981 by Lamar and Loutrelle Lyle.
The company is headquartered in Dalton, Georgia.
The leadership team brings decades of automotive textile manufacturing expertise and deep industry relationships.
source_title: Lyle Industries — Automotive Textile Carpet Manufacturing | Dalton, GA

facts: Magna is a mobility company that innovates like a start-up and thinks like a technology company.
The company employs a team of 171,000 dynamic, entrepreneurial-minded employees.
Magna has a presence spanning 343 manufacturing operations and 88 product development, engineering and sales centers in 29 countries.
Cosma provides a comprehensive range of body, chassis and engineering solutions to global customers.
Through robust product engineering, outstanding tooling capabilities and diverse process expertise, Cosma brings lightweight and innovative products to market.
The Thinning Analysis Tech position maintains a continuing Thinning Analysis capability within KAMTEK for safe metal formability.
The role ensures all Draw Dies in the Die Shop are Safe & Robust for production.
The position alerts and forecasts any inconsistencies in Draw Dies to Die Shop, Press, & Assembly so they will be monitored until fixed.
The role protects Kamtek & the Customer from an unacceptable product.

Essential functions include utilizing various test and measuring systems, including Thinning Analysis, Circle Grid Analysis, Stretch Analysis, Radius/Over Draw Analysis, to assess material formability.
The position provides technical and problem solving support to Tool and Die and Press Shop personnel on issues related to formability.
The role maintains a database of formability test and problem solving results for reference and training purposes.
Required skills include understanding of draw and trim operations and functions of a Draw Die.
Candidates must have experience using XL & Power Point Software.
A High School Diploma or GED equivalent is required.
Sheet metal stamping and/or fabrication experience with a sound background in problem solving and root-cause analysis is desired.
Minimum 3 years of computer skills in XL & PowerPoint Software are required.
Site benefits include an onsite cafeteria with multiple fresh-cooked options and salad bar.
The company hosts annual events such as Summer cookouts and Christmas party.
Employees receive an annual uniform allowance and an annual shoe allowance.
Hourly employees are eligible for perfect attendance rewards.
Benefits include Medical Dental Day, education assistance, and 12 Paid Holidays.
Health Insurance includes telemedicine, FSA, HSA, and other benefits.
The company offers Dental Insurance and Vision Insurance.
A 401(k) with match is provided.
An Employee Profit Sharing Plan is available.
An Employee Assistance Program is offered.
Tuition Reimbursement is provided.

Magna is an equal opportunity employer that hires on the basis of experience and qualifications regardless of color, ancestry, religion, gender, origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability or gender identity.
The company discourages sending applications via email to comply with GDPR requirements and local Data Privacy Law.
Worker Type is Regular / Permanent.
The position belongs to the Cosma International group.

Healthcare Strength benefits include medical, dental, vision, and pharmacy coverage alongside disability and life insurance, plus mental‑health support via an Employee & Family Assistance Program and wellness programs.
Voluntary options such as pet and auto/home insurance further expand the health-related offering.
Retirement Support features a 401(k) with company match and profit sharing, with corporate filings confirming the U.S. 401(k) plan and employer contributions.
Other long‑term levers like equity programs and performance bonuses add to wealth‑building potential.
Wellbeing & Lifestyle Benefits highlight a total‑rewards commitment and programs such as donation‑matching and an employee disaster relief fund.
Work-life and development elements include PTO, parental leave, flexible work, and tuition reimbursement for many roles.
Employees engage in a combination of remote and on-site work, with the majority of roles being hybrid with flexibility.

facts: Global auto supplier MollerTech officially launched work on a $46.3 million manufacturing facility near Woodstock, Alabama on Tuesday, November 9, 2016.
The project will create more than 220 jobs and spur growth in the rural region.
Greg Canfield, secretary of the Alabama Department of Commerce, stated that Alabama’s rural areas are now better positioned to compete for new capital investment and jobs.
Canfield noted that conventional wisdom previously held large companies would not go to rural settings, but this is no longer true.
The facility is located on a 20-acre site near Woodstock, which has a population of 900.
Bulldozers have flattened a hillside location in the Scott G. Davis Industrial Park for the factory.
The factory will be 150,000 square feet and become MollerTech’s flagship U.S. manufacturing facility.
The new facility joins MollerTech locations in Georgia and Michigan.
Steve Jordan, president of MollerTech’s North American operation, stated the company is already eager to hold a ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Bibb County facility.
Jordan told reporters that the company is a family company that likes to work with local communities.
The facility was formally announced just three weeks prior to the groundbreaking ceremony.
The facility is expected to be in full production by 2019.
It will supply interior parts for next-generation sports utility vehicles produced at Mercedes-Benz’s Alabama assembly plant in neighboring Tuscaloosa County.
MollerTech is the first automotive supplier to locate in Bibb County.
The selected Woodstock area is just minutes away from the Mercedes mothership in Vance.
Bibb County Commissioner Ricky Hubbard stated the project's impact will be felt for many years in Bibb County.
Secretary Canfield said recent project activity underscores that rural communities across Alabama are receiving increased consideration for economic development projects.

The Alabama Department of Commerce and its allies have been working strategically to generate more economic development action in the state’s rural areas, which historically faced disadvantages like small labor pools and inferior transportation networks.
Canfield stated they will continue to focus on growth, job opportunities, and capital investment in rural Alabama.
Beginning last year, companies locating a qualified project in one of Alabama’s 25 “targeted” counties (fewer than 25,000 residents) became eligible for enhanced job creation and investment credits.
As part of its project agreement, MollerTech was granted an enhanced job credit valued in total at $2.7 million.
The company will receive assistance from AIDT, the state’s primary workforce development agency, in assembling its employee base.
Two Rivers Lumber Co. received enhanced job credits for a $65 million sawmill project in rural Marengo County.
AIDT is part of the Alabama Department of Commerce and will assist with pre-screening and training the company’s Alabama workforce.
Canfield stated they have the workforce capability and that AIDT has the talent and expertise to assemble a workforce with the skills employers need.
An updated version of the Accelerate Alabama strategic economic development growth plan is in the works, focusing on facilitating rural economic development.
Canfield noted significant economic development activity is taking place in rural areas, and the Accelerate Alabama 2.0 strategy is focused on developing opportunities there.

facts: MollerTech USA, LLC hosted the Bibb County Chamber of Commerce monthly coffee group on Thursday, January 3 to kick off the 2019 New Year.
A group of about 30 or more interested business, political and civic leaders attended the event.
Tiffany Overton serves as Human Resources Manager at MollerTech USA.
Tammy Lauderdale serves as Human Resources Administrator at MollerTech USA.
The company manufactures trim parts for automobiles.
The primary customer for the Bibb County plant is the nearby Mercedes plant, which takes all of their three-shift production capacity.
MollerTech is a German company that also operates plants in two other U.S. states and produces parts for other auto makers.
The Bibb County plant started production in October 2018.
The facility began with 20 employees and has already grown to a staff of 165.
MollerTech currently has 90 open jobs to fill at the Bibb County location.
The plant is presently making 72 different trim parts for Mercedes vehicles.
MollerTech plans to double the size of its Bibb County plant and its production capacity.
Uncrated machinery is already on the factory floor, ready to be installed and set up to add to their capacity.
The facility features clean conditions with wide, well-marked walkways and emphasizes safety for workers and visitors.
The production area and office area are separated by glass so workers are always in view of management and there is frequent contact between office personnel and production personnel.
HR Administrator Tammy Lauderdale knows every plant worker on a first-name basis.
Every plant management-level worker maintains contact with the production staff.
MollerTech is described as a good place to work.
Charles Allen and Valerie Cook of the Bibb County Chamber of Commerce visited the facility to learn about the manufacturing process.
source_title: MollerTech USA Growth Continues In Woodstock | The Bibb Voice

facts: Japan’s Nidec Corporation opened new offices in Serbia and is setting up two new factories in Novi Sad to bring the company to the forefront of the global electric car market.
CEO Shigenobu Nagamori aims for “domination” in the global e-motor business within the next five years.
By 2025, Nidec plans to supply five million electric motors to the global automotive industry, targeting the line where electric cars become the majority in the industry.

Reports from November 2020 indicated about one million of these motors were destined for Europe, prompting additional offices and production facilities in Serbia’s Novi Sad, located about 90 km northwest of Belgrade.
Nidec initially set the start of construction for September 2021 and hoped to reach full capacity by mid-2022, with the foundation laid in January.
Nidec Electric Motor Serbia’s principal business is manufacturing and selling automotive motors and related products.
Jun Seki, President and Chief Operating Officer, represented Nidec Corporation at the press event following the new offices’ opening.
Nidec declined to disclose specific figures regarding clients and capacity due to confidentiality policies.
The company established Nidec Elesys Europe LLC in Novi Sad to manufacture and sell automotive inverters and electronic control units (ECUs).
The Elesys site will cover 36,000 square meters once completed on the same schedule as the e-motor facility.
Nidec intends to employ 200 people at the Elesys facility.
The larger e-drive business factory will cover almost 60,000 square meters and will employ 1,000 people.
Nidec chose Serbia to recruit locally from a workforce abundant in science and engineering who are fluent in English.
Serbia’s status as a non-EU member was another factor for establishing operations there, with COO Jun Seki noting that being first in a pre-accession market provides a strategic headstart.
The company claims “tremendous support” through the Western Balkans Cooperation Initiative promoted by the Japanese government.
Upon opening the new offices, Nidec entered a strategic alliance with the University of Novi Sad to actively engage in technological exchanges via industry-academia partnerships.

The new facilities will consolidate the Nidec Group’s production activities in the East European region and allow the Automotive Motor & Electronic Control Business Unit to launch multiple future businesses.
This strategy mirrors a successful cluster of twelve businesses Nidec established in China’s Kudong region.

The Serbian business base will supply products to the European market while eventually designing and developing products locally, creating synergies by sharing production infrastructure and back-office operations across multiple firms at the same site.
Nidec plans to invest up to ten billion US dollars in the automotive segment over the next five years, announced in November 2020.
CEO Nagamori intends to “dominate the market for electric vehicle engines,” targeting a quarter of the market by 2025 and between 40 and 45 percent by 2030.
The company estimates electric cars will reach price parity with combustion or hybrid vehicles within the next five years.

Nidec works with three client types: Type A (outsources motor business, e.g., Foxconn for their EV platform MIH), Type B (understands outsourcing benefits, e.g., Stellantis), and Type C (OEMs developing motors in-house).
COO Seki stated it is only “a matter of time” until OEMs will source components from suppliers like Nidec once they scale up to be cost-competitive.
Nidec holds a large contract for hybrid vehicles from an unnamed manufacturer.
The company forecasts growing demand for motors in the compact EV segment, citing Chinese Mini EV sales of around 35,000 units a month as evidence of high market potential.
An update in December 2021 confirmed construction started in December 2021 (delayed from September) but maintained the mid-2022 completion schedule for the 59,760-square-meter production facility.
A July 2022 update reported ground was broken for the Nidec Elesys plant, which will produce inverters and ECUs in an area of around 36,000 square meters, with completion planned for early 2023.

An October 2022 update confirmed construction of the electric motor factory was completed, with series production scheduled to start in 2023 and a first-phase employment target of around 1,000 people.

facts: Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. (NAMI) and Business Developement Center (MI) are part of the Nisshinbo Group, consisting of 59 subsidiaries and 40 affiliates worldwide.
The Nisshinbo Group was established in 1907, when it was producing yarns and threads.
In the 1940s, Nisshinbo was called upon to manufacture automotive friction materials and developed a process using woven fabric embedded with friction material.
Nisshinbo's brakes and friction materials serve diverse applications in the global transportation market.
Nisshinbo continues the evolution with a library of more than one thousand different recipes, each specific to customer applications.
North American operations are dedicated facilities serving OEM transportation manufacturers in the NAFTA market.
NBDC, located in Sterling Heights, Michigan, provides team management, engineering, prototyping, and manufacturing capabilities.
NAMI is Nisshinbo's state-of-the-art facility located in Covington, Georgia.
Together these facilities produce high-quality friction materials and logistical support to serve transportation manufacturers.

facts: Nisshinbo Micro Devices Inc. is an analog solution provider formed by the integration of former New Japan Radio Co., Ltd. and former RICOH Electronic Devices Co., Ltd.
Former New Japan Radio Co., Ltd. is known as an Op-amp supplier.
Former RICOH Electronic Devices Co., Ltd. is known as a Power management IC supplier.
The company offers the NB7401 Series, a 2 to 4 serial cell Li-ion or Li-polymer battery protection IC for secondary protection.
The company offers the NC2650 Series, an ultra-low quiescent current (IQVIN = 85nA) 1A step-down switching regulator.
Nisshinbo Micro Devices Inc. achieved an industry-leading ultra-low quiescent current (Iq) of 85 nA with the new release of the NC2650 Series.
The company offers the NB7130 Series, a 1-cell Li-ion battery protection IC for low-side FETs.
The company offers the NT9000/NT9001/NT9002/NT9003 diode for 1W/1mW class rectifier.
source_title: Nisshinbo Micro Devices Inc. - Official Website | Nisshinbo Micro Devices

facts: Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal has announced yet another Japanese auto supplier expansion that will net 100 new jobs for Georgia.
The company is a more than two-decade corporate citizen of the state.
Brake pad maker Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing Inc. is spending $72 million to expand a factory in Covington, east of Atlanta.
The company says its pads, also known as brake shoes, are better for the environment than competitors.

Environmental concerns have been key for Japanese investors of late, with multiple companies having flocked to an industrial park in Griffin offering a sylvan setting and buildings and landscapes designed to mitigate negative impacts.

Nisshinbo’s announcement comes on the heels of building products supplier Nichiha’s August commitment to invest $120 million and hire 74 new workers, doubling down on its initial investment 20 years earlier.
Nisshinbo celebrated its 20th anniversary in Covington last October.
NAMI CEO Shigeru Matsuki said the Tokyo-based company has been happy with its Newton County operation.
Mr. Matsuki said, “We hope to grow here with the people of Covington for the next 20 years and beyond.”

The company’s pledge to expand also comes ahead of the Southern Automotive Conference that will come to Atlanta in early October, which will have international pavilions dedicated to Japan along with Canada and the United Kingdom.
More than 500 Japanese affiliated companies employ some 30,000 workers in Georgia.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Joseph Huntemann, who speaks Japanese, worked as project manager on the Nisshinbo investment.
The state has had an office in Tokyo for more than 40 years.
State leaders including GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson visited Nisshinbo on a Georgia trade mission to the country last year.
source_title: Another Japanese Automotive Expansion, Another 100 Jobs for Georgia - Global Atlanta

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. (NAMI) will create 100 jobs and invest $72 million in an expansion of its Covington facility.
The facility expansion is located in Newton County, Georgia.
NAMI is a leading manufacturer and supplier of environmentally friendly brake pads.
Governor Nathan Deal stated that Georgia provides the business climate, skilled workforce, and thriving automotive sector necessary for manufacturers like NAMI to enjoy long-term growth.

The Covington facility expansion allows NAMI to continue benefiting from close proximity to top vehicle manufacturers while reaching customers across the nation and around the world using Georgia’s logistics infrastructure.

Governor Nathan Deal noted that NAMI’s commitment directly reflects the strength of Georgia’s manufacturing environment for global industry leaders and appreciated NAMI’s investment over the last 20 years.
NAMI has maintained a presence in Newton County for more than two decades.
Along with its business development center in Sterling Heights, Michigan, NAMI’s Newton County facility produces high-quality friction materials for automotive manufacturers.
The Newton County facility develops environmentally friendly, copper-free brake pads.
New jobs at the expanded facility will include positions in production and administration.
Select Newton Vice President of Economic Development David Bernd stated that Nisshinbo has been an indispensable partner in Covington for 20 years.
David Bernd highlighted that the project represents more than $70 million in Foreign Direct Investment for 100 well-paying advanced manufacturing careers.
source_title: Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. to create 100 jobs in Covington, GA - Location Georgia

facts: Nivel Parts & Manufacturing Co., LLC announced that it has acquired System 3 Off-Road, a leading UTV tire and wheel supplier.

The purchase brings together Nivel’s manufacturing, sourcing, and distribution capabilities with the highly respected System 3 brand of tires and wheels, as well as extensive design and engineering capabilities.
System 3 products are distributed in partnership with Tucker Powersports.
Nivel intends to continue growing the business with a seamless transition of System 3’s talent, vendor relationships and distribution partnerships.
Craig Petersen, founder and former owner of System 3 Off-Road, stated that they are very excited to join the Nivel team and believe the transition is a great fit for System 3.
Craig Petersen noted that Nivel’s resources will position System 3 to further accelerate product innovation, brand strategy and strengthen distribution relationships.
Nivel recognizes the growth in the UTV market and understands the importance of a well-rounded product portfolio appealing to all UTV segments.
Through the acquisition of System 3, Nivel expands into the UTV Tire & Wheel market with a well-recognized brand built on providing an excellent customer experience and high-quality products.
Nivel CEO Brett Hankey commented that they are thrilled to welcome System 3 into Nivel’s family of brands.
Brett Hankey highlighted that the System 3 team has proven their ability to leverage their experience, design talent, and engineering skills to bring best-in-class products to the market.
Brett Hankey expressed anticipation to continue building on this success and delivering must-have products to UTV enthusiast customers.

Nivel was founded in 1968 as a global manufacturer, distributor, and seller of aftermarket parts and accessories for vehicles in the Personal Transportation Vehicle (PTV), Utility Terrain Vehicle (UTV), Heavy Duty, and Automotive sectors.
Nivel is a trusted business partner to thousands of dealers, distributors, and consumers in the U.S. and international markets.
Nivel owns the most valuable brands in the aftermarket niche vehicle parts and accessories categories: MadJax, Red Dot, GTW, Jake’s, Reliance, Seizmik, Highlifter, and Falcon Ridge.
System 3 Off-Road designs, develops, and manufactures high-quality radial tires and cast aluminum wheels for the UTV market.
The System 3 brand has quickly become recognized as an industry leader in tire and wheel products for ATV/UTV replacement and high-performance applications.
System 3 has raised the bar in product innovation, design and performance, earning preferred shelf space at dealerships throughout the US and Canada.
source_title: Nivel Parts & Manufacturing Acquires System 3 Off-Road

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering, Inc. is a privately held corporation located in Rome, Georgia.

The company delivers specialized off-the-road (OTR) tires, wheels, mounted tire/wheel assemblies, and rubber tracks to Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEM), resellers, wholesale distributors, and aftermarket customers.
OTR Wheel Engineering specializes in manufacturing, distributing, and engineering off-the-road tire and wheel products for its customers.
The company can uniquely manufacture orders in-house or outsource them to various domestic and global suppliers.
OTR’s manufacturing, sourcing, and integration capabilities make it a competitive and cost-efficient single-source provider for a range of off-the-road tire and wheel products.
OTR operates as a one-stop shop for all tire, wheel, and assembly components.
The company offers Just in Time (JIT) delivery programs designed to reduce on-site material inventory.
OTR is capable of global integration, including importing and exporting.
The company maintains sales and service facilities throughout the U.S., Canada, Europe, South Africa, and China, with additional affiliates in Australia and South America.
In 2013, OTR Wheel Engineering was ranked on Inc. 500's list of the 5000 fastest-growing companies.
The company has been in business since 1987.
Languages spoken within the company include English, French, Spanish, and Mandarin Chinese.
Accepted credit cards for transactions include Visa, MasterCard, American Express, and Discover.

facts: OTR Wheel Engineering is collaborating with Taylor Machine Works (TMW) to launch a port wheel inspection program.
The collaboration facilitates on-site wheel inspection at major U.S. ports and provides critical safety enhancement.
OTR is expanding this program to its customer base and partners in Europe.
Through the expansion, OTR will leverage its footprint and staff to replicate the same process being implemented in the U.S. globally.
The program offers proper de-mounting and installation of OEM-approved wheels.
Michael Stoeckel, Global Vice President, Sales & Marketing at OTR, describes the program as game-changing and notes it provides critical service to ports across the U.S. and soon globally.
Charles Jackson, senior manager and sponsor of OTR’s program with TMW, explains that they inspect, diagnose, and confirm the condition of the wheel, its duty cycle, and the application.
If a wheel has a stress crack, it can be visually seen or detected using a proprietary process.
If a wheel is approaching end of life, OTR will inform the port staff accordingly.
OTR recommends an OEM-approved replacement wheel allocated from its in-house manufactured stock to ensure safe and continuous vehicle operation at the ports.
OTR will exhibit at Bauma from October 24-28, 2022, at Booth 430 in Hall A6, where a 10-bar OTR port wheel will be displayed.
Staff will be on-site at Bauma to explain how the program can be implemented in additional locations worldwide.

OTR Wheel Engineering, Inc. is a market-leading global enterprise specializing in innovative off-the-road tire and wheel solutions for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and aftermarket customers.
OTR provides value-added services including tire mounting, sequencing, warehousing, and subassembly for strategic OEM and aftermarket partners.
OTR serves the Construction, Lawn & Garden, Powersports, Agriculture, Forestry, Mining, Material Handling, and Specialty Vehicle markets.
OTR’s infrastructure comprises 34 manufacturing facilities and warehouse locations throughout the U.S., Canada, Europe, and Asia to support localization and velocity in the supply chain.
source_title: OTR Wheel Engineering Announces Port Wheel Inspection Program with Taylor Machine Works (TMW) - EIN Presswire

The company supplies off-the-road (OTR) tires, wheels, and rubber tracks for equipment or projects.

OTR Wheel Engineering operates as a diversified integrator, engineering firm, manufacturer, and distributor with a global footprint for specialized tires, wheels, steel castings, rubber tracks, and other fabricated goods.

facts: Paccar executives expressed optimism that U.S. planned tariffs on new trucks and truck parts will boost sales and provide market certainty.
CEO Preston Feight applauded Commerce and the White House for a clear document outlining tariff plans during an Oct. 21 earnings call with analysts.
Paccar reported third-quarter net income of $590 million ($1.12 per share) and revenue of $6.67 billion (all USD).
The White House clarified that 25% tariffs on Classes 3-8 trucks and truck parts will begin on Nov. 1.
Trucks qualifying for preferential treatment under the USMCA will only be subject to tariffs on the value of non-U.S. content.
Paccar produces more than 90% of its Kenworth and Peterbilt trucks sold in the United States at facilities in Texas, Ohio, and Washington.
Feight stated the tariffs will benefit customers by reducing tariff costs and bringing clarity to the market.
Paccar expects tariff impacts to peak during the current quarter, with uncertainty moderating after an adjustment period for truck parts manufactured in Mexico and Canada.
Feight said the situation should improve Paccar's competitive position heading into next year.
Paccar develops integrated powertrains and manufactures trucks in Europe under the DAF brand nameplate.
CFO Brice Poplawski noted that bonus depreciation measures for new equipment in the recently signed "Big, Beautiful Bill" will help spur new truck demand during the fourth quarter.
Feight indicated that tariff clarity will allow Paccar to shift discussions away from price increases back to standard truck pricing.
Paccar estimates total U.S. and Canadian Class 8 sales for the current year in a range of 230,000-245,000 trucks.
Paccar projects next year's (2026) Class 8 sales to range between 230,000-270,000 trucks.
Feight noted the 2026 total could end up higher than current year projections if clarity is realized around tariffs, emissions policy, and potential freight market improvements.
source_title: Paccar expects boost after seeing U.S. truck tariff details - Truck News

facts: FPC stands out as one of the world’s largest producers of acoustical underlayment for laminate flooring in the USA.
As of 2025, it supplies a wide range of high-performance solutions for both residential and commercial markets, with a strong focus on noise reduction and easy installation.
In late 2024, FPC invested $15 million into a state-of-the-art IXPE (Irradiated Cross-linked Polyethylene) manufacturing plant in Trion, Georgia.
The facility covers 100,000 sq. ft. and can expand up to 300,000 sq. ft., located on a 20-acre site.
This strategic location near America’s flooring manufacturing corridor cuts logistics time and costs, providing faster delivery for “Made in USA” underlayment—reducing reliance on Asian imports.
The new plant is projected to create about 100 new jobs by the end of 2025.
FPC’s top products include Eco Ultimate Silencer™, Eco Silencer HD FOF, and Silencer™ LVT.
These product lines support all installation types: floating, glue-down, and nail-down.
The underlayments are compatible with laminate, hardwood, engineered, bamboo, WPC, SPC, EVP, and luxury vinyl flooring.
All FPC underlayments are GREENGUARD Gold Certified for low emissions.
Wide North American distribution includes OEMs, flooring distributors, and major multi-store retailers.
FPC rejoined the North American Laminate Flooring Association (NALFA) in 2021 as an associate member.
The Georgia facility was built with support from state incentives and new infrastructure.
Current capacity allows for both present and future growth as market needs evolve.
The new plant can also manufacture foams for automotive, medical, and industrial use.
FPC produces items like shoe insoles, non-skid pads, seat cushions, mouse pads, and laundry press pads, serving a worldwide client base.
FPC meets the rising demand for underlayments that are both U.S.-made and environmentally certified.
Its premium products are known for versatility, great sound absorption, and broad flooring compatibility.

facts: Rivian Automotive (RIVN) has long been positioned as a disruptor in the electric vehicle (EV) market.
Recent strategic moves are centered on the R2 launch, Georgia plant expansion, and cost-optimized production.
These moves signal a pivotal shift toward mass-market accessibility and profitability.
Rivian's R2 involves supplier renegotiations.
The R2 will utilize next-generation LG 4695 battery cells.
Rivian temporarily paused operations at its Normal, Illinois plant.
Once operational, the company plans to leverage shared fixed costs with R1 and electric delivery van production.
This scalability is essential for achieving economies of scale and reducing per-unit costs.
Rivian announced a Georgia plant expansion.
The facility will integrate localized battery production in Arizona.
This move reduces reliance on imported components.
The expansion aligns with U.S. trade policies favoring domestic manufacturing.
A Volkswagen collaboration provides $1.5 billion in funding to Rivian.
The partnership provides access to global supply chains.
The partnership ties Rivian's fortunes to Volkswagen's strategic priorities.
Rivian's Q2 2025 financials reveal a company at a crossroads, driven by supply chain bottlenecks and regulatory credit reductions.
Market sentiment has been volatile, but analysts remain divided.
Key near-term risks include production delays from the Illinois plant's retooling and Georgia construction timelines.
Any slippage could delay R2 deliveries and strain liquidity.
Rivian faces competition with established brand loyalty and charging networks.
Regulatory shifts in the U.S. pose a key risk.
Rivian's focus on practicality includes NACS compatibility.
The R2 represents a high-risk opportunity for long-term investors.
A “Buy” recommendation is justified if the company executes on milestones while navigating near-term risks.
Near-term risks are significant, but long-term valuation potential exists provided they remain vigilant about execution risks.
source_title: Rivian's Strategic Growth Catalysts: R2 Launch, Georgia Expansion, and Long-Term Valuation Potential

facts: Rivian has officially launched its new manufacturing site in Georgia.
The facility is located near Social Circle.
The project represents a multi-billion-dollar investment.
The site is expected to create 7,500 permanent jobs by 2030.
The build-out phase will create 2,000 construction jobs.
Customer vehicle production is scheduled to begin in 2028.
The site will be dedicated to Rivian’s next generation of all-electric models, including the midsize, five-seater R2 SUV and the R3 crossover.
The company plans to develop the plant in two phases.
Each phase is designed to deliver 200,000 units of annual capacity.
The total planned annual capacity is up to 400,000 vehicles.
The site covers nearly 2,000 acres.
The facility will use modern construction techniques and advanced environmental management.
Plans include recreational trails for employees and customers, including a dedicated Rivian experience trail.

Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated: “We are cementing Rivian’s future at our Georgia plant, helping ensure America maintains its technology leadership and excellence in automobile manufacturing.”

Scaringe added: “Our Georgia facility will support our global expansion and provide the scale necessary to get millions of future drivers in our incredible all-electric vehicles, both in the United States and overseas.”
An analysis by IMPLAN, cited by Rivian, estimates the investment will generate nearly 8,000 indirect jobs.
The total number of positions supported by the project is more than 15,000.
The IMPLAN report estimates over $1 billion in annual labour income will be created for suppliers, vendors, and small businesses in the region.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated: “Today is another milestone in bringing quality, good-paying jobs to Georgians in this part of the state.”

Kemp added: “With today’s groundbreaking, this innovative company is further delivering on its commitment to the people of Georgia, and the thousands of hardworking Americans who will work at this facility will see generational benefits for their families.”

Jerry Silvio, Chairman of the Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton & Walton Counties, stated: “The Rivian project is exactly the kind of opportunity the JDA envisioned 25 years ago: high-tech, good-paying jobs in the communities we call home.”
Silvio added: “With Rivian here, our region continues to be a hub for innovation and high-tech manufacturing.”
At the beginning of the year, Rivian secured a $6.6 bn loan from the US Department of Energy (DOE) to set up the new production facility in Georgia.
The DOE funds will be paid out in two tranches.
Excluding capitalised interest, Rivian will receive around 3.4 billion dollars in the first phase and 2.6 billion in the second.
Construction of a previously planned new plant in Stanton Springs North was suspended in 2024.
The company recorded high losses in 2023 and again in the first quarter of last year.
Rivian previously relocated production of the upcoming R2 to its operating factory in Normal, Illinois.
Once the Georgia site is operational, both the midsize SUV (R2) and the midsize crossover R3 will roll off the production line in Georgia.
source_title: Rivian breaks ground on Georgia EV plant - electrive.com

facts: Rivian officially launched its R2 SUV in the U.S. on June 9.
Rivian's stock trades nearly 80% below its IPO price of $78.
When Rivian went public in 2021, it only sold three electric vehicles: the R1T pickup, R1S SUV, and custom electric delivery vans for Amazon (and later other companies).
The launch editions of the R1T and R1S started at $75,000 and $77,500, respectively.
Subsequent versions of the R1T and R1S started at $85,000 to $95,000.
High prices limited the mainstream appeal of the R1T and R1S.
Rivian's own supply chain constraints throttled its annual production.
Annual production dropped from 57,232 vehicles in 2023 to 42,284 vehicles in 2025.
The launch version of the R2 starts at $57,990.
Rivian plans to roll out an even cheaper version with a starting price of around $45,000 by the end of 2027.
The R2 costs less to manufacture than the R1T and R1S.
Rising sales of the R2 should boost Rivian's gross margins.
Rivian expects the R2 to boost its annual deliveries to 62,000-67,000 vehicles this year.
Analysts expect Rivian's revenue to more than triple from 2025 to 2028 if delivery efforts pay off.
Rivian's stock trades at just 3 times this year's sales.

The automaker will soon begin vertical construction on its planned $5 billion EV factory.
The Joint Development Authority (JDA) of Jasper, Morgan, Newton and Walton counties approved multiple resolutions earlier this week to finalize the lease agreement.
JDA will issue bonds related to the project’s property tax financing.

facts: Opponents are trying to derail a $5 billion, 7,500-job electric truck plant in Georgia.
A state meeting was held to gather suggestions on how to design the plant to mitigate any impact on the environment.
The state assumed oversight over the Rivian Automotive project after opponents overwhelmed Morgan County planning and zoning officials.
The plant was announced by the company and Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp in December.
The plant is the biggest single industrial project in state history.
The first meeting of one of the oversight committees was held Monday in the city of Monroe.
The Irvine, California-based electric vehicle manufacturer announced last year that it would build the facility on a 2,000-acre (809-hectare) site in Morgan and Walton counties.
The site is located about 45 miles (72 kilometers) east of Atlanta along Interstate 20.
The plant plans to produce up to 400,000 vehicles a year.
Rivian also operates a plant in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian hopes to break ground as early as this summer and begin production in 2024.
The state panel overseeing the project was led by John Eunice, deputy director for the state Environmental Protection Division.
A hostile crowd gathered at Athens Technical College in Monroe for the meeting.
Opposition to the plant has been heavy from Rutledge-area residents who say it will spoil their rural quality of life.
Residents criticized the meeting as a sham, stating it is impossible to make meaningful suggestions without a plant design and claiming the state is only working to get the plant built.
Pam Jones stated she saw her governor on TV announce the Rivian, 2,000-acre plant coming to Rutledge, Georgia as a done deal.
Many speakers voiced concerns about possible well-water contamination, light pollution, and the disruption of wildlife habitats and farmland for heavy industry.
Edwin Snell of Oconee County criticized officials for sitting on Rivian's side of the table instead of asking Rivian and Gov. Kemp to explain how the environmental project is a disaster.
A Rivian executive was present via video conference but did not speak during the hearing.
A Rivian spokesperson said the meeting was a valuable opportunity for the company to gather input.
The company is committed to sharing details of their plans for the site once they are complete and meet its own high design and environmental standards.
The plant has become a subject of contention in Georgia’s Republican primary for governor.
Former U.S. Sen. David Perdue attacked Gov. Kemp for agreeing to the Rivian location without support from neighbors.
John Eunice stated he does not know when Rivian will file for environmental permits needed to build the facility.
The state Environmental Protection Division will take public comment on the permits.
Monday’s meeting was the first of four planned for the site design and environmental committee.
The state plans four meetings each with three other committees tasked with examining quality of life, workforce, and local business engagement issues.
source_title: Rivian electric car plant blasted by foes at Georgia meeting | AP News

facts: Rivian Automotive has applied for a federal loan to enable the construction of its electric vehicle factory in Georgia, according to a filing by the U.S. Department of Energy.
The U.S. Department of Energy has not made a final decision on the loan application, and the filing did not disclose the requested amount or terms.
The Georgia facility is scheduled to begin partial operations in the third quarter of 2027.
The first production capacity block at the Georgia facility is scheduled to reach full operation in 2028.
Rivian had previously paused construction of its planned $5 billion factory in Georgia to prioritize speeding up the production of vehicles on the R2 midsize platform and conserving cash.
Aiming to save $2.25 billion and speed up production, Rivian shifted the manufacturing of its R2 midsize SUV (set to compete with Tesla's Model Y) to its Illinois factory.
Production at the Illinois factory for the R2 midsize SUV is slated to begin in 2026.
In 2022, Rivian secured $1.5 billion in state and local incentives for the Georgia facility.
Rivian lowered its annual production forecast and missed third-quarter deliveries expectations due to a parts shortage and a slowdown in EV demand.
At the end of June, Rivian had cash and cash equivalents of $5.76 billion and a long-term debt of $5.53 billion.
In June, Volkswagen said it would invest $5 billion in Rivian as part of a new joint venture to share EV architecture and software.
Rivian received an $827 million incentive package from the State of Illinois to expand operations at its Normal facility, where it also makes electric delivery vans for Amazon.com.
source_title: Rivian applies for federal loan to restart Georgia EV factory construction, ETManufacturing

facts: Seohan Auto Georgia will create at least 180 new jobs and invest more than $72 million in a new manufacturing facility in Liberty County, Ga.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced the project on Tuesday.
Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation is a Tier-1 automotive parts supplier for Hyundai Motor Group America based in South Korea.
The company has production facilities around the world, including multiple facilities in the United States.

Jung Kee Koo, CEO of Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation, stated that sustained growth of the EV market over the past few years suggests accelerated changes to the automotive market are inevitable and believes Georgia will be the center of the EV industry and a new frontier for Seohan's future.
The new facility is scheduled to begin production in late 2024.
The facility will be located at the Dunwoody Court Site in the Tradeport East Business Center, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified site.
The new manufacturing facility will produce H/shafts, axles, and brake systems for OEMs, including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.

Allen Brown, Chairman of Liberty County Development Authority, stated that Seohan and its affiliated enterprises are responsible, thorough, and progressive organizations striving to advance innovative technology.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Liberty County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the ongoing transition to e-mobility and sustainable technology creates a window of opportunity for companies and states remaining on the forefront.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in Bryan County on October 25, 2022, marking their first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Offsite suppliers connected to the Hyundai Metaplant project were predicted to invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around Georgia.
Projects announced to date for the Hyundai ecosystem have already exceeded the predicted investment by nearly $1 billion, according to the release.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced more than $21 billion in investment and 27,800 jobs in Georgia.
source_title: Seohan Auto Georgia Announces 180 New Jobs in Liberty Co.

facts: Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Seoyon E-HWA will create 740 new direct and indirect jobs.
Seoyon E-HWA will invest almost $76 million in a new manufacturing facility in Chatham County.
Seoyon E-HWA is a global auto parts supplier that manufactures both interior and exterior components.
Governor Brian P. Kemp referenced his announcement of the largest economic development project in Georgia history from the previous year.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia aims to be the e-mobility capital of the nation and remains the No. 1 state for business.
Seoyon E-Hwa was established in 1972.
Seoyon E-Hwa operates 32 corporations across North America, South America, Europe, and Asia.
Seoyon E-Hwa supplies door trim, headlining, seats, C/PAD, bumpers, and other parts to Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, Mercedes-Benz, Ford, Volkswagen, and others.
Seoyon’s existing LaGrange facility in Georgia will continue to support 630 full-time jobs.
Seoyon E-Hwa Chairman Ryu Yang-seok stated the company manufactures automobiles in Korea, Europe, and India.
Seoyon E-Hwa will supply electric vehicle parts at its new Chatham County, Georgia facility.
The company hopes its new Savannah facility will make Savannah a central city for automobile development and production.
Seoyon E-Hwa plans to continuously cooperate with the local government regarding its Savannah expansion.
Seoyon’s new manufacturing facility will be located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
The Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified site.
The new facility will produce automotive interior parts including door trims, headlining, and tailgate trims.
Production at the new facility is expected to begin in October 2024.
Approximately 500 of the 740 announced jobs will be permanent roles in human resources, development, quality, and production.

Savannah Economic Development Authority President and CEO Trip Tollison noted the new facility's location is ideally positioned near Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, the Port of Savannah, and Interstates I-16 and I-95.
The new facility will provide well-paying jobs for the Savannah region.
Seoyon currently uses the Port of Savannah to supply its existing manufacturing facility in West Georgia.
The Georgia Ports Authority supports Seoyon's growth, noting positive ripple effects from the Hyundai announcement on the regional economy.
GDEcD Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the agency on this project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Savannah Economic Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia Power.
This will be Seoyon’s second facility in Georgia.
The new facility will nearly double the number of jobs Seoyon supports in Georgia.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Hyundai’s new EV facility is drawing an experienced supplier network to the region.
The growth is expected to add to the state’s industry expertise and shape a larger skilled workforce.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced over $21 billion in investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, more than 35 EV-related projects have announced the creation of 26,700 jobs in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground on October 25 for its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility in neighboring Bryan County.
Offsite suppliers connected to the Hyundai project have already exceeded the predicted $1 billion in investment by more than $600 million.
source_title: GA: Auto Parts Manufacturer Seoyon E-HWA to Create 740 Jobs With Chatham County Facility | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Seoyon E-HWA will invest $76 million in a new plant near Savannah to manufacture interior and exterior parts for Hyundai Motor Group.
The new Georgia facility will produce door trim, headlining, and tailgate trim.
Seoyon E-HWA plans to begin production at the new Georgia plant in October 2024.
The new plant will hire more than 500 workers.
The facility is located near Savannah to supply parts for Hyundai's upcoming assembly plant in nearby Ellabell.
Seoyon E-HWA already operates a plant with 630 employees in LaGrange, Georgia.
The existing LaGrange plant is located near Hyundai's assembly plant in Montgomery, Alabama, and Kia's plant in West Point.
Seoyon E-Hwa operates 32 manufacturing units worldwide.
Seoyon E-HWA is the fourth Hyundai supplier to announce plans for a southeast Georgia plant since Hyundai announced its $5.5 billion electric vehicle and battery assembly plant in May.
Hyundai's planned EV and battery plant could grow to employ 8,100 workers and is scheduled to begin turning out vehicles in 2025.
Hyundai Mobis previously announced plans for a $926 million powertrain plant in Richmond Hill that will employ 1,500 workers.
Joon Georgia announced plans for a $317 million plant near Statesboro that will employ 630 workers.
Ecoplastic announced plans for a $205 million plant employing 456 workers in Register.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp has proclaimed his goal for his second term is to make Georgia the leading state for electric-powered vehicles.
Trip Tollison, president and CEO of the Savannah Economic Development Authority, described the new jobs as "well paying" but did not disclose specific wage details.
Seoyon E-HWA currently imports parts through Savannah's port to supply its existing LaGrange plant.
The state of Georgia will pay to train workers for the new facility, though the total incentive package from state and local governments was not immediately clear at the time of the announcement.
Seoyon E-HWA could qualify for more than $3.1 million in state income tax credits, calculated at $1,250 per job over five years, contingent on workers earning at least $31,300 annually.
source_title: Hyundai supplier plans $76M Georgia plant, hiring 500, ETHRWorld

The company is likely advancing digital transformation, expanding global production capacity, and developing innovative components for electric and autonomous vehicles.
Seoyon E Hwa published its first Sustainability Report in 2024.

facts: Seoyon E-HWA, a global auto parts supplier, plans to invest almost $76 million in a new manufacturing facility in Chatham County.
On Monday, Seoyon E-HWA broke ground on its new facility in Chatham County.
The groundbreaking comes less than three months after the company announced plans to hire 740 workers and invest almost $76 million at the new manufacturing facility.
The new facility will be a supplier to the new Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan County.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated in February that he promised it was just the start in bringing even more jobs and opportunity to hardworking Georgians after announcing the largest economic development project in Georgia history last year.
Seoyon’s facility is located at the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center.
Established in 1972, Seoyon E-Hwa supplies door trim, headlining, seats, C/PAD, bumpers, and other parts to Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, Mercedes-Benz, Ford, Volkswagen and other car manufactures.
The new facility will produce automotive interior parts such as door trims, headlining, and tailgate trims.

Seoyon E-Hwa Chairman Ryu Yang-seok said the establishment of Seoyon E-Hwa Savannah is hoped to provide new jobs in the region and contribute to making Savannah a central city for automobile development and production.
Production at the new facility is expected to begin in October 2024.
About 500 of the announced jobs will be permanent roles in human resources, development, quality and production personnel.
