facts: SKF USA’s Aeroengine Plant in Falconer is receiving a mini-grant from the Jamestown BPU’s Retool WNY program.
The grant supports efforts to right-size the company and create efficiencies amid seven consecutive quarters of organic sales losses.
The plant is investing in additional equipment and automation tools to ramp up production of ceramic rolling elements for the electrified aircraft market, part of the emerging clean energy sector.
Ceramic rollers and balls are 40 percent lighter than their equivalent steel rolling elements.
The lighter-weight components help reduce fuel usage and enable growth in the electrified aircraft market.
SKF is adding a robotic arm to two existing loading and unloading machines to improve production line efficiency and increase output to meet rising demand for lighter-weight rollers.
Reinvesting and right-sizing efforts are ongoing throughout the entire SKF company.
SKF reported first quarter financial results showing net sales of SEK 23,966,000, a decrease from SEK 24,699,000 in the first quarter of 2024.
Organic growth was -3.5%, an improvement over the -7% organic growth recorded in the first quarter of 2024.
Adjusted operating profit was SEK 3,233,000, a decrease from SEk 3,303,000 in the first quarter of 2024.
Adjusted operating margins improved by .1% to 13.5%.
Net cash flow from operating activities decreased from SEK 1,781,000 in the first quarter of 2024 to SEK 977,000 in the first quarter of 2025.

Rickard Gustafson, SKF president and CEO, stated during a conference call that the company operates in continued weak demand with tough market conditions and volatile geopolitics, but sees signs of markets bottoming out, especially in Europe.
Unlike Cummins officials who withdrew second quarter or full year financial projections, SKF expects continued volatility and plans for another quarter with negative volumes.
SKF expects organic sales to weaken somewhat in the second quarter of 2025 compared to the same period in 2024.
SKF is conducting an organizational review to rightsize its organization to better withstand future turbulent markets.
Staff cuts, including in Europe, will be presented in conjunction with the 2025 second quarter financial statements.
SKF owns the SKF Aeroengine North America plant in Falconer after acquiring MRC Bearings in 1986.
The Falconer plant is part of SKF’s aerospace division.

Gustafson declined to give a specific number for staff reductions due to ongoing discussions by country, but noted roughly 13,000 employees are classified as staff globally as the base for the reduction.
Sales declines are not currently due to tariffs, though that effect is expected later once more is known.

Tariffs remain a consideration for SKF officials, with Gustafson noting it is hard to predict impacts given recent pauses and discussions between China and the U.S. about potentially relaxing some tariffs.
source_title: Local SKF USA Plant Making Investments | News, Sports, Jobs - Post Journal

facts: SKF USA plans to close its plant in Seneca during the next 18 months.
The closure will eliminate 170 jobs in the small town north of Topeka.
The plant is one of the main employers in Seneca, which has a population of about 2,000 people.
SKF USA is owned by Sweden-based SKF Group.
The company stated it is consolidating its industrial seals manufacturing work.
The work being done in Seneca will be transferred to a plant in Salt Lake City.
Topeka Mayor Joe Mitchell called the decision a "huge blow" to the city.
Mayor Mitchell said he's hopeful the town will find another manufacturer to replace SKF.
source_title: SKF USA to Close Factory, Eliminate 170 Jobs | Industrial Equipment News

facts: SKF announced its latest wheel end and driveline solutions will be on display at booth No. 4619 during AAPEX show in Las Vegas from Nov. 1-3.
SKF will showcase innovations through detailed cutaways of hub bearings, u-joints, seals and more.
A custom 2013 Dodge Ram 1500 Longhorn equipped with SKF hub bearing units will be on display at the booth.
The booth will showcase SKF’s updated u-joint product line featuring broader coverage and an expanded range of its high-performance Brute Force line.
SKF Universal Joints are available in greasable and non-greasable versions and meet or exceed OE specifications.
Each universal joint part is precision-manufactured to provide reliable performance and maximum service life.

The expanded universal joint product line provides complete coverage for vehicles including cars; light, medium- and heavy-duty trucks; buses; RVs; construction and off-highway equipment; agricultural and industrial equipment; and marine applications up to the 2015 model year.
Brian Jungmann, automotive communications manager at SKF USA Inc., stated SKF is looking forward to returning to AAPEX to feature innovative solutions for the automotive aftermarket.
Jungmann noted SKF will share industry-leading expertise in bearing and seal technology and highlight high-performance solutions customers expect from SKF.
SKF will highlight its hub bearing kits at the show, which are designed to save time by providing everything an automotive technician needs to replace a press-in style hub bearing.
Each SKF hub bearing kit includes a cartridge-style wheel bearing, c-clip, axle nut, seals and a premium hub flange.

SKF says its hub bearing kits reduce the chance of customer returns by providing quality parts designed to fit and work properly together, eliminating the hassle of sourcing separate parts from separate suppliers.

links.context: Information about expanded line of SKF universal joints, hub bearing kits and other vehicle aftermarket solutions

source_title: SKF To Showcase Its Latest Vehicle Solutions At AAPEX 2016

facts: SKF announced a reconfiguration of its operations in Mexico as part of the separation of its automotive business.
The company aims to strengthen efficiency and competitiveness across the region.
SKF will shift production from Monterrey to facilities in Puebla and La Silla, consolidating capabilities in strategic locations.
The Monterrey plant operated as a shared facility between SKF’s Industrial and Automotive divisions.
The Monterrey plant was originally designed to support projected growth in electric vehicles across the Americas.
Slower-than-expected momentum in the EV segment, combined with the business separation, prompted a reassessment of installed capacity.
Under the new configuration, SKF will optimize its manufacturing footprint and concentrate operations to improve overall operational performance.
The company will retain the flexibility to respond to a potential rebound in automotive electrification.
The adjustment includes the elimination of approximately 390 positions in Monterrey.
The adjustment includes the creation of around 100 new jobs in Puebla and La Silla.
These locations will centralize operations, reinforcing their role within SKF’s regional manufacturing strategy.
This decision is part of a broader consolidation process aimed at aligning production with current market conditions.
The move strengthens SKF’s presence in Mexico as a key node within its manufacturing network in the Americas.
The restructuring is expected to cost approximately 500 million Swedish kronor.
The restructuring costs will be recorded in the second quarter of 2026.
The restructuring costs have already been incorporated into the company’s annual financial guidance.
Mexico remains a strategic destination for advanced manufacturing, supported by infrastructure and talent that enable companies to reorganize and sustain competitiveness.
source_title: SKF relocates automotive operations from Monterrey to Puebla and La Silla

facts: The Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority board of directors has initiated an executive search for a new president.
The Chason Group, an executive search firm specializing in economic development organizations, chambers of commerce, foundations and non-profits, has been engaged to assist.
The process is expected to take 90 days with the goal of having a new professional in the office this spring.

Chair Guy Cochran stated that the board is taking a very methodical and transparent approach to finding the next economic development professional to lead the authority, emphasizing that finding the right person is more important than time constraints.
The authority is the key organization in Dublin and Laurens County that focuses on marketing the community for new investments, expansion of existing businesses and maintenance of current employers.
The executive search has been broken into three phases, with the first phase considered a listening segment completed in December.

Many community, non-profit, government and business representatives were interviewed by The Chason Group to garner feedback on how the authority should shape the position description for the next president as well as what skill sets are most important.
The current phase involves identifying candidates that fit the traits identified in phase one.

A position announcement has been posted with the International Economic Development Council, Georgia Economic Developers Association, Southern Economic Developers Council and various other social media sites that correlate with economic development.
The Chason Group team is also reaching out directly to candidates in the industry who fit the profile.
The final phase of the executive search will initiate the narrowing of the candidate pool and interview process.
A detailed application has been developed and will be provided to the candidates who meet a pre-determined set of criteria.
After review and vetting, the authority anticipates interviewing the leading three to six candidates.
The Chason Group will serve as the contact for candidates.
Scott Beasley serves as vice chairman of the authority.
James Malone serves as treasurer of the authority.
Other members of the authority include Jimmy Allgood, Roger Folsom (immediate past chair), Phillip Gay, and Keith Griffin.
Members of the authority are appointed by the City of Dublin’s mayor and council and the Laurens County Board of Commissioners.

links.context: More information about the executive search

source_title: The Chason Group to find New President for Dublin-Laurens Development Authority | Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority

facts: SRG Global Inc. is a privately held manufacturer and supplier of chrome plated plastic parts for the automotive and commercial truck industries.
The company has manufacturing operations across North America, Europe and Asia.
SRG Global's Advanced Development Center in Taylor, Michigan, is a dedicated facility for developing new plastics coating technology.
SRG Global was founded in 2009 and is headquartered in Troy, Michigan.
The company reports revenue of $1 billion+.
Revenues were $380 MM in 2024, $250 MM in 2023, $90 MM in 2022, $120 MM in 2021, and $65 MM in 2020.
Gross profit was $190 MM in 2024, $125 MM in 2023, $45 MM in 2022, $60 MM in 2021, and $32.50 MM in 2020.
Net income (loss) was $43 MM in 2024, $25 MM in 2023, $9 MM in 2022, $12 MM in 2021, and $6.50 MM in 2020.
Total assets were $850 MM in 2024, $720 MM in 2023, $680 MM in 2022, $650 MM in 2021, and $600 MM in 2020.
Total liabilities were $450 MM in 2024, $400 MM in 2023, $380 MM in 2022, $350 MM in 2021, and $320 MM in 2020.
Total equity was $400 MM in 2024, $320 MM in 2023, $300 MM in 2022, $300 MM in 2021, and $280 MM in 2020.
The company employs between 1,000 and 5,000 people.
Key executives include Clyde Newcomer (Chief Chemist), Merritt Gaunt (President), Michael Lee (President), and Elk Krausz (Tools Engineer).
On March 15, 2024, NorthBridge PE completed an acquisition of SRG Global for $120M, taking a 100% stake and taking the company private.
The target company valuation was listed as $1.20 MM at the time of the March 2024 deal.
A secondary transaction scheduled for September 1, 2023, was canceled.
The canceled September 2023 secondary transaction involved a majority stake and techniques including a secondary sale and management buy-out (MBO), with 65% total acquired.
source_title: SRG Global, Inc. Company Profile: Financials, Valuation, and Growth | PrivCo

facts: SRG Global is one of the world’s largest manufacturers of chrome-plated plastic parts for the automotive and commercial truck industries.
Merritt Gaunt was named president of SRG Global, directing the company’s global operations to advance its vision, culture, and strategy for long-term sustainable growth.
Gaunt takes over for Dave Prater, who announced his retirement earlier in 2019.
Prior to this appointment, Gaunt served as vice president of Africa, Middle East, India and Asia (AMEIA) for Guardian Glass, a Guardian company.
Gaunt joined Guardian Industries in 2013 as managing director of Corporate Development.
He was later named vice president of Operations for Guardian Glass before assuming the AMEIA role.

Ron Vaupel, president and CEO of Guardian Industries, stated that Gaunt will apply Market-Based Management® to achieve superior results for the company, its customers, employees, and strategic partners.
Vaupel noted that this is a time of change and opportunity for SRG Global and the automotive industry.
Gaunt has more than 16 years of operations, finance, marketing, and business development experience.
He previously held various positions at Koch Industries in the Koch Equity Development Group.
In 2006, Gaunt joined the strategy and business development group of Georgia-Pacific.
In 2007, he was named vice president of Finance and Business Development for Georgia-Pacific’s European operations.
In 2010, he took responsibility for Georgia-Pacific’s consumer brand paper towel business.
He was subsequently named vice president and general manager for the skincare category and new category development at Georgia-Pacific before joining Guardian.
Both Guardian Industries and Georgia-Pacific are wholly owned subsidiaries of Koch Industries.
Gaunt earned Bachelor of Science degrees in entrepreneurship and accounting from Wichita State University, with minors in physics and French.
SRG Global is headquartered in Troy, Michigan, USA.
The company operates a Research and Development Center in Taylor, Michigan, USA.
SRG Global maintains a manufacturing presence in major world regions including North America, Western and Central Europe, and Asia.
The company engineers solutions for greater surface durability, structural integrity, enhanced functionality, vehicle efficiency, and design flexibility.
SRG Global develops innovative exterior and interior products and processes for the automotive and commercial truck industries.
source_title: Merritt Gaunt Named President of SRG Global®, Inc. | glassonweb.com

facts: QTS Atlanta 1 Campus features electric vehicle (EV) charging stations available on-site.
QTS Suwanee 1 Campus offers EV charging spots for visitors and employees.
Georgia Power’s 2025 Integrated Resource Plan includes planned battery storage installations as part of its renewable energy expansion.
Georgia Power plans to expand total renewable resources to about 11,000 megawatts by 2035, including utility-scale solar and rooftop solar programs.
QTS signed a 20-year agreement with Georgia Power to procure nearly 350 megawatts of renewable energy for its Atlanta-Metro and Suwanee campuses.
Switch partnered with Georgia Power to use 100% renewable energy from local sources for its Keep Campus in Atlanta.
QTS Fayetteville campus ("Project Excalibur") aims to operate on 100% carbon-free electricity once completed.
Georgia’s primary utility, Georgia Power, offers access to a mix of natural gas, nuclear, coal, and renewable energy.
Surging demand from sectors like data centers is expected to add 8,200 megawatts of new load over six years.
Georgia Power delayed previously scheduled retirements of Plant Bowen and Plant Scherer coal plants into the 2030s to maintain grid reliability amid surging power demand.
Georgia offers sales and use tax exemptions for high-tech data centers meeting investment and job creation thresholds, available through December 31, 2031.
To qualify for tax exemptions in counties over 50,000 people, facilities must meet a $250 million investment and 25 new jobs threshold.
For counties with 30,000 to 50,000 people, the tax exemption requirement is $150 million and 10 jobs.
For counties under 30,000, the tax exemption requirement is $100 million and 5 jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp vetoed a two-year pause on new data center tax exemptions in 2024, warning it would hurt investment.
House Bill 696, known as the "Switch Bill," provides a 0% sales and use tax rate for Switch’s Keep Campus in Atlanta.
source_title: Data Centers in Georgia for Colocation (Updated 2025)

facts: Thailand has secured more than $4.1 billion in electric vehicle supply chain investments across 198 projects.
The investments expand battery production, charging infrastructure, and vehicle manufacturing.
The investments strengthen Thailand's position as Southeast Asia's automotive hub.
According to the Board of Investment (BOI), Thailand has gained over $4.1 billion (137 billion baht) worth of investments from its EV supply chain.
The 198 approved projects encompass vehicle manufacturing, batteries, charging stations, and essential parts.
The investments occur as global car manufacturers continue to produce more in Southeast Asia by diversifying supply chains and adding electric vehicle manufacturing capacity.

Narit Therdsteerasukdi, Secretary-General of the BOI, stated that Thailand’s EV strategy caters to hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs), plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs), and battery electric vehicles (BEVs).
Thailand’s EV strategy does not focus on a single technology type.

At the International Electric Vehicle Technology Conference (iEVTech) in Bangkok, Narit stated the strategy is designed to benefit both incumbent auto brands and new entrants while developing Thailand’s automotive supply chain.
BEV-related projects total about $1.18 billion (39.5 billion baht) and include 18 projects.
The projected annual production capacity for BEV projects is over 370,000 units.
Investments in HEV and PHEV projects total about $1.18 billion (39.3 billion baht) across 14 projects.
Battery and energy storage system manufacturing accounts for $1 billion (33.5 billion baht) through 57 projects.
Another $373 million (12.5 billion baht) has been committed to 49 projects producing components such as drive motors, battery management systems, and power control units.
Charging infrastructure projects represent $292 million (9.8 billion baht) across 42 projects.
These charging projects support the rollout of more than 22,900 charging stations, including over 10,000 DC fast chargers.
Mercedes-Benz started local battery electric vehicle production in Thailand in 2022.
BYD, Great Wall Motor, SAIC Motor, and Aion opened assembly operations in Thailand in 2024.
Changan Auto and EV Primus began production in Thailand in 2025.
Hyundai Mobility and Omoda & Jaecoo are scheduled to begin production in Thailand in 2026.
The BOI stated that these manufacturing projects have created more than 16,000 jobs.
The BOI has organized 18 supplier matchmaking sessions bringing together multinationals and Thailand's component suppliers.
Over 800 local suppliers have attended the matchmaking events.
The events resulted in over 1,200 deals.
According to the BOI, the collaboration will lead to a procurement value of approximately $1.79 billion (60 billion baht).
Thailand created the BOI in 1966 to encourage investments and develop its industries.
source_title: Thailand Attracts $4.1 Billion in EV Supply Chain Investments Across 198 Projects - Responsibleus

entity_name: HL-GA Battery Co. Savannah EV Battery Plant
facts: Construction at Hyundai’s EV manufacturing campus near Savannah, Georgia, was halted two months prior by a dramatic immigration raid before machinery and workers returned.
On September 4, 2025, U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detained approximately 300 South Korean nationals at the HL-GA Battery Co. construction site.
The detained workers were found to be in the U.S. on expired visas or visa waivers that specifically prohibited employment.
The immigration raid abruptly shut down work on the battery facility, which sits adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group’s new EV assembly plant.
HL-GA Battery Co. announced on November 14, 2025, that construction had resumed using a mix of new hires and returning South Korean specialists.
The company expressed gratitude to the U.S. and South Korean governments, as well as Georgia officials, for their collaboration in supporting a smooth and timely return.
HL-GA Battery Co. stated it remains on track to start production in the first half of next year (2026).
The company continues to actively hire local positions to operate the facility.
Mary Beth Kennedy, spokesperson for HL-GA Battery, confirmed that some returning workers were among those detained in September but declined to specify how many had returned.
LG Energy Solution confirmed that 'subject matter experts' were back at the HL-GA Battery work site to restart machinery installation and get the project back on schedule.
LG Energy Solution did not disclose exactly how many of the 317 South Korean nationals detained by ICE had returned.
Hyundai Motor Co. CEO José Muñoz predicted in September that the immigration action would delay the battery factory’s opening by two to three months.
The manufacturing campus includes the new Hyundai EV assembly plant and six affiliated supplier facilities.
The campus is expected to produce up to 500,000 vehicles annually once fully operational.
The battery plant is designed to supply batteries not just for the Savannah assembly line but also for other North American EV and hybrid projects.
Governor Brian Kemp and other Georgia leaders campaigned vigorously to attract the investment, touting it as a transformative moment for the region.

According to The New York Times, most of the detained workers were in the country on short-term B-1 or H-1B visas, which allow business visits and certain skilled labor but have strict conditions and expiration dates.
HL-GA Battery Co. and its partners worked closely with both the U.S. and South Korean governments to facilitate the return of essential personnel.

An HL-GA Battery spokesperson told Fox News: 'We appreciate the efforts of our partners and government officials in ensuring business continuity and the creation of high-paying, hard-working jobs for Georgians.'
The company reiterated in November that it remains on track to start production in the first half of 2026.
The facility aims to ramp up toward full-scale battery production in 2026 while blending international expertise with homegrown local skills.
Korean attorney Hee Kuk, based in Atlanta, told The New York Times that navigating visa categories and labor rules creates anxiety for foreign-born workers and employers.
Much of the specialized knowledge required to install and calibrate advanced battery equipment was developed by LG Energy Solution in Korea.
Production at Georgia’s EV hub is slated to begin in the first half of 2026.
source_title: Hyundai Battery Plant Restarts After Immigration Raid - Grand Pinnacle Tribune

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced an additional $2 billion investment for Hyundai Metaplant on Thursday.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solutions will invest the additional $2 billion in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at the Metaplant in Bryan County.
The added investment will create 400 more jobs at the facility.
The facility is expected to create 8,500 new jobs for the region in the coming years.
With the announcement, the electric vehicle manufacturing facility and the battery joint venture represent about $7.59 billion in total investment.
The project will create 8,500 new jobs for the region over the next eight years.
Once fully operational, the facility will be able to support the annual production of 300,000 electric vehicle units.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated that in a single year, they broke ground on the largest project in state history, landed multiple suppliers across the state for Hyundai's Metaplant, and welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated that they are building on that success as they continue to make Georgia the e-mobility capital of the nation.
St. Joseph's/Candler is opening a site at Hyundai Metaplant.
source_title: Georgia: Extra $2 billion investment coming to Hyundai Metaplant - WJCL

facts: Volkswagen, Ford and others rolled back spending on new battery plants and curbed production of some models, notably the F-150 Lightning pickup.
Volkswagen idled workers at its largest EV plant in Europe.
EV startups Enovate, Levdeo, Singulato and WM Motor filed for bankruptcy in China during Q4.
The Chinese market has 110 other EV brands to evaluate.
Share prices of electric spinoffs like Volvo’s Polestar declined, while erstwhile highflyers Vinfast, Lucid, Rivian and Fisker also saw declines.
Fisker's share price is down 90% in value from its 2023 peak.
EV startup Arrival filed for bankruptcy before selling its first vehicle after being valued as much as $15 billion.
Ampere’s IPO was shelved, with Adamas battery capacity data showing Renault-Dacia EV brands were not making headway in their home base.
Renault's idea that its EV unit would fetch more than its parent’s $10 billion valuation was considered fanciful.
Tesla shares doubled in value in 2023.
BYD surpassed Tesla in terms of sales last year (2023).
Over 19.2 million EVs, including plug-in and conventional hybrids, were registered globally last year, which is 4.6 million more than in 2022.
The global EV industry grew at 32% on an annual basis.
In 2023, EVs represented 23% of all passenger vehicle sales around the world.
December was a record month in terms of total battery capacity (GWh) deployed onto roads globally in newly sold EVs, growing by 12% over the previous record month of November.
For the 2023 calendar year, 689.2 GWh was added to the electric car parc, an expansion of just under 200 GWh or 40% compared to 2022.
Some 37% of all power-hours deployed over the past decade were rolled onto roads in 2023 alone.
More gigawatt-hours were deployed in 2023 than in the eight years from 2014 to 2021 combined.
The GWhs deployed in December 2023 alone were more than the entire 2018 calendar year.
Germany, the fourth largest EV market worldwide, saw passenger EV battery capacity deployment drop 46% year-over-year in December.
Germany's decline was mainly due to the axing of government subsidies a full year early.
Germany's December 2022 outcome was distorted by an exceptional month where more than 7.3 GWh were added to autobahns on the back of buoyant Tesla Model Y and 3 sales.
The Asia Pacific region saw combined battery capacity of 48.8 GWh deployed in December, a new record for the region and up 45% over the same month the year before.
In the Americas, 12.6 GWh were deployed onto roads in newly sold passenger EVs in December, a 34% year-over-year gain marking a new all-time monthly high.
The US and Canada make up more than 96% of the Americas regional market.
In Europe, total battery capacity deployment shrank by 39% in December 2023 compared to the same month a year earlier.
Historically, December has been Europe's strongest month, but its best month came as far back as March when 16.1 GWh hit roads versus 15.9 GWh in December 2023.

In France, the third largest EV market in the region in GWh terms, December battery capacity deployment rocketed by 47%, smashing all records and surpassing the UK only for the third time ever on a monthly basis.
On an annual basis in 2023, Europe powered ahead 32% year-on-year with 156.2 GWh deployed.
Asia Pacific saw 40% growth in 2023 with 405.6 GWh deployed.
The Americas saw 49% growth in 2023 where 117.0 GWh was deployed.
Across Europe and the UK, December was the 2023 high mark for EV adoption with more than one-third of passenger vehicles leaving showrooms as full electric or hybrid vehicles.
Germany accounts for a fifth of the European regional market, and December was its second best month of the year for EVs relative to gas-powered passenger vehicles.
The only market in Europe that contracted on an annual basis in 2023 was Norway, with buyers accumulating 24% less battery power than the year before.
84% of all vehicles sold in Norway last year were EVs, which is on par with 2022.
More than half of cars sold in Finland, Sweden, Denmark and Netherlands in 2023 were EVs, with Belgium reaching a similar level by the end of the year.
In Iceland, EV penetration rates ran north of 90% since August and hit over 99% of sales in December last year, matching the previous year.
source_title: What electric car slowdown? Global EV industry is breaking records - Adamas Intelligence

entity_name: U.S. Battery and EV Supply Chain
canonical_name: U.S. Battery and EV Supply Chain
facts: Automakers with U.S. operations plan to significantly ramp up production of electric vehicles this decade and commit huge capital outlays.
An electric vehicle (EV) can use hundreds to thousands of individual lithium-ion batteries.
There is a wave of new investment in gigafactories devoted to the production of lithium-ion batteries.
Planned investment in U.S. gigafactories exceeds $40 billion, targeting parts of the U.S. to build out a key part of the domestic supply chain for batteries and EVs.
Gigafactories are enormous facilities covering hundreds of thousands of square feet that produce millions of cells per year.
The annual capacity of battery factories is measured in gigawatt hours (GWh), with gigafactories exceeding 1 GWh annually.
A typical household in the U.S. consumes 30 kilowatt hours (kWh) of electricity daily.
An EV battery pack usually has a capacity of 50–100 kilowatt hours (kWh).
A gigafactory with 1 GWh capacity operating at full capacity could theoretically produce enough batteries each year to power 10,000 to 20,000 EVs.

The U.S. experienced an initial wave of investment in lithium-ion battery factories after the Great Recession, driven in part by $2.2 billion of funding allocated in the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009.
Six new facilities worth more than $5 billion were announced from 2018 to 2020.
Since the start of 2021, more than 15 new facilities or expansions have been disclosed in the U.S., reflecting a potential investment of at least $40 billion.
Several plants have also been announced in Canada.
All but one of the newly announced gigafactories exceeds 10 GWh of capacity, with the largest exceeding 40 GWh.
U.S. lithium-ion battery capacity is expected to grow more than fivefold from 2021 to 2026 according to Benchmark Mineral Intelligence.
By 2031, U.S. battery capacity is expected to expand another 86 percent.
The U.S. share of global battery capacity is forecasted to advance from 5.5 percent in 2021 to almost 11 percent by 2031.
China’s production capacity is expected to rise about 486 percent from 2021 to 2031.
EV sales in the U.S. totaled more than 466,000 units in 2021, double the level of 2020.
Outside the U.S., EV sales have doubled each year since 2010 and reached almost 4.2 million units in 2021.
Ford plans to spend $50 billion through 2026 to expand its EV production.
GM plans to invest $35 billion through 2025 to expand its EV production.
Ford targets 2 million global EV sales by 2026.
GM targets 1 million global EV sales by mid-decade.
Other companies with a footprint in the U.S. are also preparing plans to scale up production in the region.
Auto production for these companies is largely concentrated in the Midwest and the South.
Due to the high costs of transporting large quantities of lithium-ion batteries, most newly announced gigafactories will be located in the same geographical region, referred to as the “Battery Belt”.
Many of these investments are partnerships between automakers and battery producers.
The largest investment recently announced is a $5.8 billion joint project by Ford and SK Innovation for two 43 gigawatt-hour-capacity facilities in Kentucky.
These partnerships allow battery companies to secure demand while allowing automakers to obtain supplies they need to produce EVs.
The battery supply chain begins with the mining of key raw materials including lithium, nickel and graphite, and subsequent refining of those minerals.
U.S. capacity in raw material mining and refining is limited, though interest is growing.
A few new U.S. facilities to produce battery materials have been announced over the past year, the largest of which is a $3.5 billion operation in Nevada.
There are plans for several recycling plants and growing interest in lithium mining and refining upstream.
The U.S. government hopes to further boost investor interest through numerous provisions contained in the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022.
The Inflation Reduction Act offers direct subsidies to defray the costs of producing critical minerals, battery components and battery cells.

The tax credit available to buyers of EVs is worth up to $7,500 per car and includes requirements for the percentage of both the value of battery components and the value of critical minerals that must be sourced from domestic producers or free-trade partners.
The subsidy contains a prohibition on the use of critical minerals, battery materials and other components from “foreign entities of concern,” including any output produced within China.
The critical minerals portion of the ban takes effect at the start of 2025.
The components prohibition begins at the start of 2024.
These provisions create potentially powerful incentives to boost investment in other parts of the U.S. supply chain, though it is too early to know how successful they will be.
source_title: Automakers’ bold plans for electric vehicles spur U.S. battery boom - Dallasfed.org

facts: Kia officially announced at the New York Auto Show that its newest EV, the Telluride-sized EV9, will be built at its plant in West Point, Georgia.
Hyundai plans to build 300,000 electric vehicles at their Bryan County facility.
The announcement ratifies statements and actions of the Georgia Department of Economic Development regarding strategic courtship of EV and supplier manufacturers.

At a legislative study committee meeting in August 2022, Commissioner Pat Wilson testified that Georgia’s strategy to attract EV and supplier manufacturers aimed to retain existing auto manufacturing jobs during the industry's transition to new technologies.
Georgia had 55,000 auto and supplier jobs tied to traditional internal combustion engine (ICE) vehicles prior to the transition.
World’s largest automakers are shifting R&D efforts toward EVs and away from ICE, creating an opportunity to capture plant and equipment investment while retaining current jobs.
Hyundai Motor Group has begun production of its electric GV70 model for the Genesis luxury brand alongside other ICE vehicles at its Montgomery, Alabama plant.
Hyundai selected Bryan County for its first dedicated EV plant and third Southeastern factory, forming a critical mass capable of producing over one million vehicles per year.
Commissioner Wilson testified that Hyundai expects to help co-locate more than 3 dozen new suppliers to support manufacturing efforts in Bryan County.
Hyundai’s official target from the Bryan County selection announcement was $1 billion in additional capital investment from suppliers.
As of March 2023, announced projects totaled $1.8 billion, with more supplier location decisions still pending.
Much of the costs and value added for EV manufacturing will come from battery unit production.
The Bryan County factory will include an on-site battery production facility.
SK Innovation has been producing EV batteries in Northeast Georgia for several years.
SK Innovation currently employs 2,000 Georgians near Commerce with a goal to reach 3,000 employees by year-end 2023.
A subsidiary of SK Innovation announced an additional battery manufacturing facility northwest of Atlanta in Bartow County.
The Bartow County facility represents $4.5 billion in new investment with a goal of employing 3,500 Georgians.
SK Innovation is establishing an IT office in Roswell to create 200 high-tech jobs.
American companies are diversifying supply chains away from China due to economic and national security concerns following the pandemic, the war in Ukraine, and tensions over Taiwan.
Korea is highlighted as a long-standing US ally with aligned national security interests, increasing its importance as an Asian trading partner for Georgia.
Georgia Ports remain a major economic driver for the state with a balanced mix of two-way trade and are among the ports with the highest exports compared to imports.
Yoonie Kim serves as the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s point person on Korean investment.

Governor Brian Kemp gave Yoonie Kim a name-check during his State of the State address, followed by a standing ovation from legislators for landing billions in investment and thousands of jobs while maintaining the current employment base.
Georgia previously lost a Ford plant in Hapeville and GM plants at Lakewood and Doraville.
Kia put Georgia back on the automotive manufacturing map by building an SUV there beginning in 2009.
Kia’s decision to build a dedicated EV model at the same West Point plant a decade and a half later supports Georgia’s growing manufacturing and trading future.
source_title: Kia Production Announcement Brings GA’s EV Efforts Full Circle - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: A research team at Georgia Institute of Technology has discovered that fast charging can improve the performance of zinc-ion batteries.
The discovery challenges conventional assumptions about battery degradation.
The team was led by Hailong Chen, an associate professor in Georgia Tech’s George W. Woodruff School of Mechanical Engineering.
Higher charging rates suppress the formation of dendrites—needle-like metal spikes that can short-circuit batteries.
Faster charging allows zinc to stack into smooth, dense layers instead of accelerating dendrite formation.
Hailong Chen stated: “We found that using faster charging actually suppressed dendrite formation instead of accelerating it.”
Zinc-ion batteries are considered a potential alternative to lithium-ion batteries because zinc is more abundant, less expensive, and safer.
Dendrite formation has historically limited the practical applications of zinc-ion batteries.
The findings were published in Nature Communications.
Fast charging may prevent dendrite-related failure modes and extend the battery’s lifespan.
Chen’s team developed a custom experimental tool to observe zinc’s behavior in real time under different charging speeds.
Hailong Chen noted: “We weren’t just seeing whether the battery worked or not,” adding, “We were watching the structure of the material evolve as it charged.”
The team identified patterns in dendrite suppression that had previously gone unnoticed in laboratory tests.
While the zinc anode showed improved performance, the cathode still requires optimization to match the anode’s durability.
The team is currently experimenting with zinc blends to create a more robust full battery system.
Researchers see potential for zinc-ion batteries in large-scale applications such as home solar energy storage and grid stabilization.
Hailong Chen stated: “You can imagine these zinc-ion batteries being used to store solar energy in homes, or for grid stabilization. Anywhere you need reliable, affordable backup power.”
The team estimates that zinc-ion batteries could be commercially available within five years.
Zinc-ion batteries are positioned as an alternative amid fluctuating lithium prices and supply chain pressures.
source_title: Fast Charging Strengthens Zinc-Ion Batteries, Georgia Tech Study Finds

facts: Max Nagle, Head of Marketing and Communications at the SER Group, visited St Ambrose Preparatory School in Hale Barns, Altrincham on Friday 20th June to speak to pupils about battery recycling.
The visit aimed to explain why batteries cannot simply be thrown away and how materials inside them can be recovered and given a new life.
Cellcycle's visit formed part of its ongoing commitment to building broader public understanding of battery recycling and its place in the UK’s transition to net zero.
The UK disposes of 600 million batteries every year.
Most disposed batteries end up in landfill, where chemicals can leach into soil and water.
Batteries range from household AA cells to lithium-ion packs inside phones, laptops, and electric vehicles.
Materials inside batteries include zinc, manganese, lithium, cobalt, nickel, copper, and graphite, which are finite resources that are recoverable.
Recovered materials have a second use in new batteries, paint, or pharmaceutical applications.
Building a domestic recycling capability to handle energy transition volumes is a skills and awareness challenge as well as a technical one.
The session covered the full lifecycle of a battery from raw materials to end-of-life disposal.
Risks of incorrect disposal include leaking toxic chemicals into soil/water and degraded batteries posing a fire hazard in refuse vehicles.
Pupils were introduced to the Cellcycle process: collection, safe transport, shredding, material recovery, and re-entry into new products.
Responsible disposal is straightforward via collection points at supermarkets, libraries, and some schools.
A quiz/challenge highlighted that if 129 pupils each recycled one battery in a week, 129 batteries would be diverted from landfill before the following Friday.
Mrs. L. Ellis stated Cell Cycle delivered an informative assembly that was fun, interactive, and helped pupils learn about battery contents, recycling processes, and importance.
Max Nagle noted the engagement showed young people are seriously thinking about sustainability connected to daily technology, which is what the industry needs from the next generation.
Cellcycle holds Environment Agency-approved treatment facilities with a combined permitted capacity of 80,000 tonnes per year.
Cellcycle's LithiumCycle™ process was developed through a Knowledge Transfer Partnership (KTP) with Coventry University.
The LithiumCycle™ process applies a bio-based refining route to recover cathode active material at a purity and yield viable for direct re-entry into battery manufacturing.
A patent for Cellcycle's novel direct recycling process has been filed.
Cellcycle is the battery recycling division of the SER Group.
source_title: We Visits St Ambrose Preparatory School to Talk Battery Recycling

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in Georgia will manufacture EVs for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands.
The plant has a production capacity of 300,000 units with a start of production targeted for the first half of 2025.
Hyundai Motor Group announced a US-only $5.54 billion (Rs 45,943 crore) investment to establish itself as a global top-tier electric vehicle OEM.
On October 25, Hyundai held a groundbreaking ceremony for the EV-only plant in Bryan County, Georgia.
Approximately $1 billion (Rs 8,293 crore) is anticipated to be invested by non-affiliated HMGMA suppliers in other locations.

Attendees at the groundbreaking included Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, U.S. Senators Raphael Warnock and Jon Ossoff, U.S. Representative Buddy Carter, U.S. Department of Commerce’s Don Graves, Chairman Chung Eui-sun, Hyundai Motor President Jae-Hoon Jang, and Global Chief Operating Officer Jose Munoz.

Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung stated that Hyundai and Georgia share qualities of respect for history, ingenuity, creativity, and determination to improve the next generation's world.
Chung noted that Hyundai's EVs are recognized as best-in-class and the company aims to become a global leader in electrification, safety, quality, and sustainability.
Chung added that HMGMA will help the company evolve beyond an automaker into a leading global mobility solutions provider.
HMGMA will be built on a 11.83 million square metre site capable of mass-producing 300,000 EVs annually.
Construction is scheduled to begin in the first half of next year.
The plant will use a flexible manufacturing system to produce multiple EV types for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis brands to meet local demand.
The Electric-Global Modular Platform (E-GMP) will underpin EVs ranging from hatchbacks to full-size SUVs across the three brands.
The three Hyundai Motor Group US production bases are adjacent, enabling economies of scale in parts procurement and supply chain management.
HMGMA is located approximately 420km from Kia Georgia and about 510km from Hyundai Motor America's manufacturing subsidiary in Alabama.
HMGMA will utilize a manufacturing innovation platform developed at Hyundai's Singapore Global Innovation Center (HMGICS).
The HMGICS platform features a demand-oriented AI-based intelligent control system, eco-friendly low-carbon methods for carbon neutrality and 100% renewable energy use, and human-friendly facilities.
Hyundai Motor Group plans to sell 3.23 million EVs globally by 2030, targeting a 12% market share.
The company aims to sell 840,000 EVs in the US by 2030.
In the first three quarters of this year, Hyundai Motor and Kia sold 47,095 EVs in the US, a 212.0% year-over-year increase.
Hyundai sold 22,418 Ioniq 5 units (up 142.3%), while Kia sold 24,677 EVs including 17,564 EV6 units, resulting in a 322.2% sales increase.
The proportion of EVs in Hyundai Motor Group's total US sales rose from 1.3% in January-September last year to 4.3% in the first three quarters of this year.
Hyundai expects US EV sales to grow explosively once HMGMA begins full-scale operations.
The company plans to establish a stable local procurement system for both finished EVs and batteries.
Hyundai plans to form a strategic alliance with a global battery company to build a battery cell factory near HMGMA.
The joint battery plant will develop and mass-produce batteries optimized for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EVs.
Hyundai is accelerating its electrification transition by building EV production bases in both Korea and the US.
Hyundai Motor will build a new EV plant at its Ulsan driving test site, while Kia will build a PBV EV plant in Autoland Hwaseong.
Both Korean plants will begin mass production in 2025, aligning with HMGMA's timeline.
By 2030, Hyundai (including Genesis) will have an 18-EV lineup and Kia will have 13 EVs, producing 1.44 million EVs annually in Korea.
Last year, Hyundai Motor and Kia's domestic production increased 12% compared to 2004 levels, with finished car exports increasing by 79% over the same period.
source_title: Hyundai breaks ground at new EV and battery plant in the US | Autocar Professional

facts: American battery materials firm Ascend Elements has been awarded grants from the U.S. Department of Energy's Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (BIL) Battery Materials Processing and Battery Manufacturing Initiative.
The awards total $480 million in matching funds.
The funding will support construction of the 'Apex' manufacturing facility in southwest Kentucky.
The project establishes lithium-ion battery supply chain infrastructure that previously did not exist in the United States.
The planned facility will utilize the company's patented 'Hydro-to-Cathode' technology.
The technology produces sustainable cathode precursors (pCAM) and cathode active materials (CAM) from spent lithium-ion batteries.
CEO Mike O'Kronley stated the awards are transformational for the U.S. lithium-ion battery industry.
The Apex facility will be the first and largest battery material production facility of its kind in the U.S.
The facility aims to improve security of supply of critical materials for the domestic electric vehicle industry.
The project aims to improve the United States' competitiveness in the lithium-ion battery industry on a global scale.
CEO Mike O'Kronley emphasized that the importance of a closed loop lithium-ion battery supply chain cannot be understated.
The grant accelerates achievement of that closed-loop supply chain.
CEO Mike O'Kronley noted the $480 million DOE grant was over 2x the next largest grant.
Construction at the 140-acre site in Hopkinsville is expected to begin this month.
Once completed, Apex will produce enough material annually to power over 250,000 electric vehicles.
The project will generate approximately $4.4 billion in total economic impact during its construction phase and the first ten years of operation.
The project will create over 400 jobs in the Hopkinsville area.
The Apex project will seek to establish community benefits programs including job training and workforce development, affordable childcare, and affordable transportation initiatives.
source_title: Ascend Elements bags $480 million US DoE grant to make sustainable battery CAM -

facts: The White House unveiled the nation’s first ever national EV charging standards earlier this week.
The new standards fall under the federal government’s National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) program.
NEVI aims to make recharging an EV battery as simple, seamless and standardized as filling up a gas tank.
The new standards apply to all federally funded public EV charging stations, including those funded through NEVI.
NEVI is administered by the Federal Highway Administration, a branch of the US Department of Transportation.
NEVI is a $5 billion federal funding program established through the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
The program provides funding to states to strategically deploy EV charging infrastructure and establish an interconnected network for data collection, access, and reliability.
The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law passed the House with all Democratic Representatives voting in favor and 13 Republican votes.
In the Senate, 19 Republican Senators voted in favor and 30 voted against, with the bill passing with all Democrats voting in favor.
President Biden signed the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law into law on November 15, 2021.
The new standards cover more than just the charging plug, applying to EV charging from hardware to traffic control, connectivity and mapping apps.
EV charging stations have already begun appearing at gas stations, particularly those with convenience stores and restrooms.
The Pilot Company is on board with its network of 500 Pilot and Flying J travel centers in partnership with General Motors and EVgo.
TravelCenters of America has committed 200 of its Travel Centers and Petro locations to the initiative.
bp finalized an acquisition deal for TravelCenters of America on February 15, the same day as the NEVI announcement.
bp has partnered with Hertz in support of NEVI to build EV charging hubs in cities focusing on rideshare vehicles, rental vehicles, taxis and general public charging.
FreeWire Technologies manufactured a modular EV charging station installed at a Phillips 66 station in Houston.
FreeWire’s setup integrates energy storage into an all-in-one package that eliminates the need to install new high-voltage lines.
FreeWire has prepped a NEVI guide to smooth the process for funding applicants and maintains relationships with gas station stakeholders.
Last September, FreeWire announced a deal with Chevron and Texaco branded stations.
Phillips 66 had more than 2,500 locations in the US at the time of reporting.
Chevron and Texaco had about 8,000 locations between them at the time of reporting.
FreeWire’s modular setup provides quick installation and more flexibility in site selection compared to conventional EV charging stations.

A White House fact sheet stated that FreeWire announced a new 66,000 square foot global headquarters in Newark, CA, representing a $20 million investment focusing on R&D and manufacturing of ultrafast EV charging equipment.
The same White House fact sheet noted FreeWire will create more than 200 well-paying engineering and manufacturing jobs in electrification and clean energy across the community.
State-level transportation agencies in all 50 states are participating in the NEVI program.
Florida Governor Ron DiSantis’s state is receiving $198 million in NEVI funding over the next five years to install more EV infrastructure, despite his public attacks on corporate ESG investing.
Texas is set to receive $408 million in NEVI funding for EV driving infrastructure, even as Republican US Senator Ted Cruz and Governor Gregg Abbott criticize “woke capitalism.”
Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin turned down Ford’s new LFP battery factory, costing his state thousands of jobs.
Virginia will receive $100 million in NEVI funding to build out its EV charging station network.
Florida Governor Ron DiSantis, Texas Senator Ted Cruz, Texas Governor Gregg Abbott, and Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin are all targeting the Oval Office during the 2024 election cycle.
source_title: EV Charging Station Sneak Attack On Gas Stations

facts: The National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) program is set to accelerate its rollout of EV chargers across the United States in 2025.
The NEVI program was established under the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
Its goal is to create a national network of fast chargers for electric vehicles across the country.
The program aims to facilitate the widespread adoption of EVs by alleviating range anxiety among potential users.
By 2025, the program is expected to have significantly increased the number of charging stations available to the public.
The NEVI program's updated strategy will focus on improving installation timelines and enhancing funding accessibility for local governments and private firms.
This initiative follows a comprehensive review of the program's initial rollout phases, which faced delays due to regulatory hurdles and funding allocations.
As part of these changes, NEVI plans to invest heavily in upgrading existing infrastructure to support a wider array of vehicle types.
Many projects face delays due to complex local permitting processes.
Despite federal support, not all regions have the financial resources to match funding requirements, which may lead to uneven charger distribution.
Ongoing global supply chain issues continue to impact the availability of necessary components for EV charger installations.
A well-distributed charging network will bolster consumer confidence, thus driving EV sales upwards.
According to a recent report from Statista, increased charger availability is projected to boost EV market share to over 20% by 2025, compared to just 4% in 2020.
Achieving these ambitious goals requires navigating regulatory and funding challenges that could slow deployment.
Ongoing collaboration between federal, state, and local entities, alongside private investment, will be essential to ensuring the successful implementation of the NEVI program.
source_title: NEVI EV Charger Rollout Sped Up in 2025: Overcoming Roadblocks for a G

facts: Electric vehicle maker Rivian is preparing to resume work on its paused $5-billion manufacturing plant in Social Circle, Ga.
The manufacturer scheduled a ceremonial groundbreaking for September.
Construction is on track to begin next year, according to company representative Peebles Squire.
The startup will still use its originally selected contractor, Clayco, for the job.
The plan calls for a 16-million-sq-ft manufacturing plant on an 1,800-acre campus.
It will be built in two phases, each adding production capacity for 200,000 vehicles.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp (R) originally announced Rivian’s plan to build the plant in 2021.
In 2023, the company said it selected Chicago-based Clayco to build the facility, along with Jacobs as engineer and Skidmore, Owings & Merrill as design architect.
Last year, shortly after Rivian revealed a $5.4-billion loss over its prior fiscal year, the company paused the project.
The company decided to produce an upcoming SUV at its existing plant in Normal, Ill., instead of at the Georgia facility.
A few months later, Rivian announced it would build a $1.5-billion expansion on the Illinois plant.
In January, Rivian closed a loan agreement with the U.S. Dept. of Energy’s Loan Programs Office for up to $6.6 billion to support the Georgia project.
The financing is on top of an incentive package from Georgia worth $1.5 billion.
Under terms of an economic development agreement with the state, Rivian must invest at least $5 billion and create 7,500 full-time jobs by the end of 2030.
Last month, Rivian announced it would open an East Coast headquarters office in Atlanta, in addition to the manufacturing campus about 40 miles east of the city.
That office is scheduled to open this year, with an expansion to follow.
Rivian’s Georgia manufacturing plant is set to begin production in 2028.
source_title: Rivian to Resume Construction of $5B Georgia EV Plant in 2026 | Engineering News-Record

facts: Rivian confirmed plans to build a $5 billion factory in Decatur, Georgia.
The factory will employ 7,500 people.
The announcement comes amid controversy about the company’s environmental record, groundwater quality, traffic, and urban sprawl.
Rivian is pledging to abide by local laws while keeping factory operations as low-pollution as possible.
The company planned to begin construction on the plant in the summer of 2022.
The new factory will span nearly two thousand acres in Morgan and Walton counties east of Atlanta.
Rivian hopes to start building electric cars and trucks as early as 2024.
Opponents raised concerns about potential groundwater contamination, light pollution, and disruption to farmland and wildlife habitat.
Rivian executives were present at the hearing by video conference but did not address any of the concerns.
The proposed plant became a flashpoint in the Republican primary for governor.
Former U.S. Senator David Perdue publicly attacked Gov. Kemp for accepting Rivian’s proposal.
A Rivian executive says the plant will create jobs for around 7,500 people.
The factory will occupy nearly two million square feet of land east of Atlanta.
State officials have declined to reveal the exact cost of the factory.
Rivian plans to start production by 2024, with a goal of producing up to 400,000 cars a year.
The company already has an existing plant in Normal, Illinois, but the planned Georgia plant will be five times larger.
The new plant will be located on 2,000 acres, which means it will be five times the size of the Illinois factory.
When deciding where to build its factory, Rivian considered factors including the availability of clean energy, the availability of 2,000 acres of land, and the availability of potential employees.
The facility in Normal, Illinois, should be able to produce more than one hundred thousand electric vehicles per year.
The Illinois facility will also include a training center and waste and recycling facilities.
Rivian currently builds electric pickup trucks and SUVs at a former Mitsubishi plant in Normal, Illinois.
Rivian has plans to expand into other U.S. states, including California.
The new Georgia factory would be responsible for producing battery cells.
The company has nearly seven thousand pre-orders for its R1T pickup truck and R1S SUV.
The company is spending billions on new factories to produce electric vehicles.
Rivian plant is expected to begin construction in the summer of 2022 and develop the adjacent 2,200 acres over several years.
The government of Morgan County has given preliminary approval to the project.
Local residents are trying to block future construction.
Some opponents organized into a Facebook group called Our Communities Oppose the Rivian Plant that has over 2,000 members.
Rivian recently announced plans to build a manufacturing plant near Atlanta, Georgia.
The factory is expected to cost $5 billion and produce 400,000 electric vehicles a year.
The company is required to create 7,500 jobs by 2028.
Each job must pay at least $56,000 a year plus benefits.
Some Democrats have expressed opposition to the plant, including former U.S. senator David Perdue, who is running against Kemp in the Republican primary.
Perdue has also raised the issue of liberal billionaire George Soros, who owns 2% of Rivian.
Gov. Kemp has said he supports the plant and reiterated his support in the most recent primary debate.
Rivian has been receiving property tax breaks worth $700 million.
Rivian plans to make $300 million in payments in lieu of taxes over a 25-year period.
Rivian’s incentive package has become the largest in American history.
Rivian is a startup manufacturer of electric trucks and commercial delivery vans that is headquartered in Irvine, California.
The company already has a plant in Illinois and plans to break ground in Georgia this summer.
Rivian’s preorder holders have been angry before about price hikes when told vehicles would cost as much as $20,000 more, which sparked a social media uproar.
Rivian walked back the price increase and apologized to customers.
The company raised the entry price of the R1T and R1S pickups by about $5,000 and $6,000.
Rivian is an electric vehicle company founded in 2009 by engineer RJ Scaringe.
It was originally named Mainstream Motors and briefly known as Avera Automotive.
At first, the company planned to build a mid-engine hybrid sports car, but eventually shifted to focusing on electric adventure vehicles.
Amazon, Tesla, and Toyota, among others, have invested in the company.
The first Rivian truck will roll off the production line in September 2021.
The company will go public on the Nasdaq stock exchange in December 2021.
Currently, the company has raised US$1.3 billion from investors, including T Rowe Price, Ford, Amazon, and Pario Ventures.
source_title: How Electric Truck Became Lightning Georgia? [Answered 2026] | Prettymotors

facts: Rivian’s long-delayed Georgia plant broke ground in September 2025.
The project is anchored by the largest incentive package in Georgia state history.
Most of the $1.5 billion deal money is contingent on Rivian hitting aggressive job and investment targets.
The contract requires meeting at least 80% of commitments by the end of 2028 or face clawbacks.
The plant is not scheduled to build its first saleable vehicle until the fourth quarter of 2028.
Georgia agreed to roughly $1.5 billion in incentives to land Rivian’s second US assembly plant.
The facility will be a 2,000-acre campus near Social Circle, about 45 miles east of Atlanta.
In exchange, Rivian committed to invest at least $5 billion and create 7,500 full-time jobs at an average salary of $56,000.

The incentive package bundles property tax abatements, job tax credits, state-funded land acquisition and site preparation, road and interchange work, a rail spur, and a tailored Quick Start workforce training center.
State officials have described roughly a quarter of the package value as direct state incentives, with a further share delivered as physical site work.
Rivian is required to maintain its job and investment levels through 2047, a 25-year window during which the state can seek repayment if performance slips.
The original economic development agreement set December 31, 2028 as the deadline to hit 80% of the $5 billion investment and 7,500-job commitments.
Rivian’s current timeline has groundwork starting in 2026 and first saleable vehicles rolling off the line in Q4 2028.
Hitting 80% of the job commitment requires about 6,000 workers in a factory that has not yet shipped a vehicle.
More recent statements from the state and Rivian reference 7,500 jobs “by 2030,” suggesting the milestone may have been adjusted or renegotiated.
Clawbacks are a mechanism to recover incentives already received but no longer qualified for, triggered if performance falls below 80%.
The agreement calls for pro-rata repayment, meaning the state recovers a proportional share of credits and abatements tied to missed targets.
Job tax credits and property tax abatements only flow as Rivian actually hires and invests, making the structure deliberately back-loaded to protect taxpayers.
Georgia has already spent roughly $175 million on buying and grading land, improving roads, building a new I-20 interchange, and constructing a rail spur.
The state took direct ownership of the site in 2022 to bypass local zoning resistance, making the infrastructure investment sunk regardless of future performance.
In Q1 2026, Rivian reported revenue of $1.38 billion (up 11% year over year) and a net loss of $416 million (narrower than the prior year's $541 million).
The company is targeting its first year of gross operating profit in 2026.

Rivian began producing the R2 midsize SUV at its existing Illinois plant in April 2026, a decision that saved an estimated $2.25 billion in capital but shifts Georgia’s near-term job creation reliance to the later R3 ramp.
Rivian renegotiated its Department of Energy loan down from $6.57 billion to $4.5 billion, consolidating it into a single tranche to be drawn in early 2027.
For Georgia, this project serves as a test case for megadeal industrial policy amid slowing US EV sales growth.
Scale is critical for Rivian's profitability, and the company cannot reach required R2 and R3 volumes without this plant.
The incentive structure ties both parties together: Georgia needs delivery to justify the spend, while Rivian needs Georgia’s capacity to survive.

Key developments to track include whether the 80% milestone is officially 2028 or 2030, DOE loan draw timing in early 2027, construction hitting the 2026 groundwork timeline, R2 sales momentum as a demand signal for the R3, and potential legislative or watchdog moves to revisit deal terms.
The “$1.5 billion at risk” framing overstates contingent exposure and understates the already committed ~$175 million in sunk site and infrastructure costs.
Recent “by 2030” language suggests the original 2028 clawback deadline is being quietly stretched, as enforcing it literally would be nearly impossible given the production timeline.

Both Georgia and Rivian are highly entangled due to a prior pause, federal loan, state-owned land, and a 25-year maintenance clause, making it unlikely either side will let a calendar technicality derail the deal.
source_title: Rivian's $1.5B Georgia Incentives Come With a Catch: Hit the Targets by 2028

facts: Battery company SK Battery America Inc. laid off nearly 1,000 workers at a manufacturing plant northeast of Atlanta on Friday.
The layoffs occur amid automakers’ changing electrification plans and uncertain consumer demand for EVs.
Friday marked the last working day for 958 plant employees, about 37% of its workforce.
A Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) notice detailing the layoffs was filed by human resources chief Chuck Moore.
Impacted workers will be paid through May 6.
The plant will continue to employ about 1,600 workers.
SK opened the $2.6 billion battery plant in Commerce, Georgia, in January 2022.
The Korean company notably supplied the Ford F-150 Lightning electric pickup truck.
Ford announced plans to cancel the fully electric version of the truck in December.
source_title: SK lays off nearly 1,000 workers at Georgia plant amid cooling automaker EV plans – WABE

facts: Working with Glovis Americas, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) put an initial 21 Class 8 XCIENT trucks on the road to transport vehicle parts from suppliers to the factory on a daily basis.
With the trucks connecting suppliers and HMGMA’s on-site consolidation centre, the firms plan to expand the logistics to a broader network of suppliers.
To support the deployment, Hyundai and Glovis’ joint venture, HTWO Logistics, will install a hydrogen refuelling station at the Megasite.
Hydrogen production capacity at the site is expected to be announced later.
Set up earlier this year, the joint venture is slated as a brand that will enable “each stage of the entire hydrogen value chain, from production and storage to transportation and utilisation.”
“HMGMA’s deployment of XCIENT for logistics and operations is one of the main initiatives of HTWO,” said Hyundai’s North America commercial vehicle and hydrogen business boss, Jim Park.
“This is delivering on our strategy and vision to showcase a closed-loop hydrogen logistics ecosystem and improve the environmental sustainability of manufacturing operations.”
Hyundai and Glovis have been working together on heavy-duty hydrogen trucking for several years.
Most recently, the pair joined arms in a $53m project in the San Francisco Bay Area to deploy 30 XCIENT trucks.
Launched in 2020, the XCIENT truck has been deployed in the US, Switzerland, Germany, Israel, Korea and New Zealand.
Featuring two 90kW fuel cells, it has a driving range of over 450 miles per tank of hydrogen.
source_title: Hyundai Deploys Hydrogen Trucks for Georgia EV Plant Logistics - World-Energy

facts: Georgia was awarded a Gold Shovel by economic development business publication Area Development for achieving significant success in attracting new business investment and job creation in 2023.
The award announcement was made on Friday, June 28th, 2024, by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that thousands of jobs were created across Georgia thanks to economic development teams in all four corners of the state.
Kemp noted that these efforts helped Georgia land record-breaking investments and hundreds of thousands of jobs for its communities.
Kemp attributed Georgia's continued No. 1 ranking for business to a pro-business approach, an unmatched workforce, world-renowned logistics, and letting the market work.

Area Development’s annual Gold and Silver Shovel Awards recognize state economic development agencies that demonstrate excellence in creating a business-friendly environment, fostering innovation, and implementing strategies that successfully attracted high-quality investment projects.
All states were invited to submit information to Area Development on major groundbreakings, investments, and business expansions occurring in 2023.
Area Development Editor Andy Greiner said the awards highlight the exceptional efforts of state economic development teams and their unwavering commitment to building strong economies.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted advanced manufacturing, aerospace, e-mobility, life sciences, and fintech as industries instrumental in achieving a Shovel Award again this year.
Wilson reflected on back-to-back record-breaking years of investment and job creation in Georgia.
Wilson expressed anticipation for celebrating upcoming milestones with partners who make these opportunities possible.

Wilson congratulated Governor Kemp, the state legislature, the state economic development team, and all statewide partners for their dedication to sowing opportunities that communities will reap for decades to come.

links.context: 2024 Gold and Silver Shovel Awards and detailed profiles of winning states

source_title: Georgia Recognized for New Business Investment, Job Creation - Rome CEO

facts: Kuntz Electroplating Inc. (KEI) will receive a $3-million Government of Canada investment.
The investment aims to strengthen KEI's manufacturing capabilities in the automotive and Electric Vehicle (EV) industry.
The project will create 45 new jobs in the automotive and EV industry.
KEI is one of Kitchener's longest-running family-owned businesses.
KEI is one of North America's leading manufacturers of electroplated and powder-coated exterior components for the automotive and motorcycle industries.
The investment will help KEI modify its existing production lines and add new equipment to meet growing demand from the EV market.
The project will help KEI enter the clean technology sector.
KEI will fulfill larger volume orders and serve new clients by offering more environmentally sustainable solutions, including in the EV sector.
The investment will strengthen the manufacturing sector in Kitchener and position KEI for long-term growth.
Growing demand for greener alternatives gives Ontario's manufacturers immense potential to harness expertise and new technologies to support the rapidly growing Electric Vehicle (EV) market.
Ontario's manufacturing sector employs thousands of workers and is a significant contributor to Canada's economy.
The Honourable Filomena Tassi, Minister responsible for the Federal Economic Development Agency for Southern Ontario (FedDev Ontario), announced the investment.
Valerie Bradford, Member of Parliament for Kitchener South-Hespeler, joined the announcement.
Tim Louis, Member of Parliament for Kitchener-Conestoga, joined the announcement.
Minister Tassi met with Jan Liggett, Mayor of Cambridge; Susan Foxton, Mayor of North Dumfries; and Dorothy McCabe, Mayor of the City of Waterloo, to discuss regional economic growth opportunities.
source_title: Canada Invests in Kitchener-Based Company's Cleaner Electric Vehicle Manufacturing | Mirage News

facts: Following a $217 million expansion, EV6 joins the EV9 as the plant’s second electric vehicle.
The plant is currently assembling five overall vehicles in West Point, Georgia.
On Thursday (Nov. 21, 2024), it was announced at the 2024 Los Angeles Auto Show that the all-electric 2025 EV6 SUV will be assembled at the plant.
Kia Georgia celebrated 15 years of manufacturing in Georgia on Saturday (Nov. 20, 2024).
Production at Kia Georgia began on Nov. 16, 2009.
Since beginning production, Kia Georgia has assembled more than 4.5 million vehicles.
Kia Georgia has helped create more than 14,000 jobs in the West Point, Georgia region.
Eligible Kia owners will receive $500 in assistance for the purchase or lease of select new Kia vehicles between October 4, 2024, and January 2, 2025.
Kia America and Kia Georgia, Inc., donated $250,000 to the American Red Cross to aid in relief efforts.
On November 28, 2017, Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) delivered a significant financial contribution to The Ray, Georgia’s living laboratory for transportation technology on Interstate 85.
The $26,000 gift was the second installment of a three-year commitment for the WheelRight® tire safety station.
The WheelRight® tire safety station was installed in December of 2016 at The Ray.
source_title: Uncategorized Archives - Kia Georgia

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), in cooperation with Glovis America, announced it has deployed Hyundai XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
Initially, a total of 21 XCIENT trucks will be in operation at the facility.

Oscar Kwon, HMGMA CEO, stated that the company is committed to creating sustainable transportation solutions and that the zero-emission hydrogen fuel-cell trucks offer a cleaner alternative to traditional freight logistics methods.
The deployment represents a significant step in HMGMA’s efforts to reduce its carbon footprint and promote sustainable practices in daily logistics operations.
The use of zero-emissions hydrogen fuel cell technology demonstrates Hyundai Motor Group’s commitment to responsible environmental stewardship.
The Hyundai XCIENT hydrogen fuel-cell Class 8 heavy-duty trucks will transport vehicle parts from HMGMA suppliers across the region to the Megasite on a daily basis.
During the initial rollout, the XCIENT trucks will transport parts between suppliers and the on-site Consolidation Center before expanding to a broader network of suppliers.
The 21 XCIENT vehicles will represent more than one-third of Glovis America’s truck fleet at HMGMA.

Earlier this year at CES, Hyundai Motor Group announced HTWO, its new hydrogen value chain business brand encompassing businesses and affiliates across production, storage, transportation, and utilization stages.
HTWO represents “Hydrogen” and “Humanity,” the two main pillars of Hyundai’s hydrogen business.

HMGMA’s deployment of XCIENT trucks for logistics is one of the main initiatives of HTWO, delivering on its strategy to showcase a closed-loop hydrogen logistics ecosystem and improve manufacturing environmental sustainability.
Jim Park serves as SVP of commercial vehicle and hydrogen business development at Hyundai Motor North America.
HTWO Logistics, a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Company and Glovis America, will install a mobile hydrogen refueling station at the Megasite for efficient refueling.
A hydrogen production and refueling station is being developed for the Megasite and will be announced at a later date.
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated electric vehicle mass-production plant.
The facility is located in Bryan County, Georgia.
The new plant will feature a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem to realize customer value.
The Georgia facility will become an intelligent manufacturing plant where order collection, procurement, logistics, and production processes are optimized utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system is designed to create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with approximately 250,000 employees worldwide and mobility brands including Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Deploys XCIENT Hydrogen Fuel Cell Trucks for HMGMA Clean Logistics - HMGMA
